GA 202155337R

The utility model claims an adult middle concentration oxygen mask; the double chin support is suitable for more face size; the soft sealing ring improves the comfort and sealing effect, reduces the possibility of pressure sore; opening design, preventing carbon dioxide aggregation; at the same time, it is good for protecting environment and saving resource.

PT P
PN CN112779185-A
TI Purifying Staphylococcus aureus from Proteus comprises e.g. dipping cotton swab into aztreonam drug sensitive paper into physiological saline, coating on blood agar plate, picking Proteus and Staphylococcus aureus, and incubating.
AU SUN Y
   YE M
   FANG M
AE SUN Y (SUNY-Individual)
GA 202155007Q
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying Staphylococcus aureus from Proteus comprises (i) taking 2 ml of 0.45-0.9% physiological saline, (ii) adding at least one aztreonam drug sensitive paper into physiological saline, mixing uniformly to obtain aztreonam medicine liquid, (iii) dipping cotton swab into aztreonam medicine liquid, (iv) uniformly coating on the blood agar plate, and then allowing to stand for 5-10 minutes to obtain blood agar plate containing aztreonam medicine, (v) picking mixture of Proteus and Staphylococcus aureus, inoculating into blood agar plate containing aztreonam medicine, then drawing lines in 3 or 4 areas, (vi) adding blood agar plate into 35 degrees C bacteria culture box with or without carbon dioxide and incubate for 16-24 hours to isolate Staphylococcus aureus.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying Staphylococcus aureus from Proteus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes as long as there is Staphylococcus aureus in the mixture, a large number of single pure colonies can be accurately separated, and it is not contaminated by Proteus and can be directly used for drug sensitivity tests, and greatly shortens the time period.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN112760288-A; CN112760288-B
TI Special culture medium useful for culturing lung cancer organoids, comprises basic medium and specific supplemental factors.
AU YU Y
   ZHU Y
   LI G
   HUANG M
AE ACCURATE INT BIOTECHNOLOGY GUANGZHOU CO (ACCU-Non-standard)
GA 2021541962
AB    NOVELTY - Special culture medium comprises basic medium and specific supplemental factors. The specific supplementary factors include: GSK429286A, Y27632, epidermal growth factor (EGF), bone morphogenetic protein 4 (BMP4), human fibroblast growth factor 4 (FGF4), insulin-transferrin-selenium, B27, all-trans retinoic acid, keratinocyte growth factor (KGF), DAPT, GlutaMAX (RTM: Supplement), R-Spondin-1, CHIR99021, 8-Br-cAMP, bovine serum albumin (BSA) and phosphodiesterase (PDEs) inhibitor.
   USE - The special culture medium is useful for culturing lung cancer organoids (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium realizes rapid growth of lung cancer organoids, and is cultured stably for a long period of time. The formed tumor organoid cell spheres are regular in spherical shape and relatively uniform in size, and retain heterogeneity of the patient's tumor tissue in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for culturing lung cancer organoids, involves pretreating the separated lung cancer tissue and digesting twice to collect the cell pellet, lyzing cell pellet, mixing with matrigel glue, after the mixed glue has solidified, adding the above-mentioned medium, and cultivating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 6-14 days; and
   (2) lung cancer organoid, obtained by the culture method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The specific supplementary factors include: 2-20 mu M, preferably 5-15 mu M GSK429286A, 2-20 mu M, preferably 5-12.5 mu M Y27632, 10-500 ng/ml, preferably 50-200 ng/ml EGF, 10-500 ng/ml, preferably 50-200 ng/ml BMP4, 50-1000 ng/ml, preferably 200-600 ng/ml human FGF4, 0.5-2 x, preferably 0.75-1.5 x insulin-transferrin-selenium, 0.5-3 x, preferably 0.75-1 x B27, 0.1-1 mu M, preferably 0.4-0.6 mu M all-trans retinoic acid, 10-500 ng/ml, preferably 50-200 ng/ml KGF, 2-20 mu M, preferably 5-15 mM DAPT, 0.5-2 x, preferably 0.75-1.25 x GlutaMAX (RTM: Supplement), 10-1000 ng/ml, preferably 300-800 ng/ml R-Spondin-1, 1-20 mu M, preferably 5-10 mu M CHIR99021, 10-200 nM, preferably 50-150 nM 8-Br-cAMP, 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.2-0.6 wt.% BSA and 10-200 mM, preferably 50-150 mM PDEs inhibitor. The basic medium is Dulbecco's modified eagle medium:nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12) (1:1) medium. The culture medium is prepared by adding specific additive factors to the basic medium and uniformly mixing the materials. The method involves obtaining digestive juice 1, where digestive juice 1 comprises 200-1000 ng/ml hydrocortistone and 200-1000 U/ml collagenase enzyme type IV, digesting the lung cancer tissue at 37 degrees C and 220 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 2-3 hours, adding Hanks' balanced salt solution (HBSS) to stop the digestion, and centrifuging to obtain a digested cell pellet. The method involves obtaining digestive juice 2, where the digestive juice 2 comprises 2-20 mg/ml dispase II and 0.2-2 mg/ml DNase, digesting the once-digested cell pellet at 37 degrees C and 220 rpm for 15-20 minutes, adding HBSS to terminate the digestion, and centrifuging to obtain the second digested cell pellets. The method further involves resuspending the cell pellet obtained from the two digestions with HBSS, passing through 100 mu m cell strainer, and centrifuging to collect the cell pellet.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-B04D2; B04-F02A; B04-H06; B04-J03A; B04-L05A; B04-L05C; B04-N02; B04-N06; B11-A04A; D05-A02C; D05-A04B; D05-H01; D05-H08B

AU YANG L
   ZHU H
   LUO X
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST (UYSW-C)
GA 202147406R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a flue gas input pipe connected with an additive generator. A medium blocking coupling pulse corona discharge device is connected by a connecting pipe. The medium blocking coupling pulse corona discharge device comprises a quartz reactor, a discharge needle beam and a metal sleeve surrounding an outer side wall of quartz reactor. The discharge needle beam is fixed in the center part of quartz reactor. A tip part of the discharge needle beam is located at a middle position of the metal sleeve.
   USE - Flue gas desulfurization and denitration system in field of atmospheric pollution control technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The system performs high efficient desulfurization and denitration method without catalyst at normal pressure excluding the adverse effect of oxygen, carbon dioxide and water in the actual flue gas to normal temperature and pressure (NTP) method denitration, has high desulfurizing efficiency and is not easy to cause secondary pollution so that the nitric oxide and sulfur dioxide are efficiently removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using flue gas desulfurization and denitration system.

PT P
PN IN201921042182-A; IN382221-B
TI Solid waste-based texturizing filler used for paint/powder-based coating formulation, comprises grounded and pulverized biocide fortified emulsion polymer skin-based texturizing filler, and ground at less than glass transition temperature.
AU JAWALE M
   BAJORIA M
   MATHURE P
   PARMAR R
AE ASIAN PAINTS LTD (ASPA-Non-standard)
   ASIAN PAINTS LTD (ASPA-Non-standard)
GA 202143759S
AB    NOVELTY - Solid waste-based texturizing filler comprises grounded and pulverized biocide fortified emulsion polymer skin-based texturizing filler and ground at temperatures below the glass transition temperature of the emulsion polymer.
   USE - Solid waste-based texturizing filler used for paint/powder-based coating formulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid waste-based texturizing filler is usable and reutilizable and good replacement for inorganic fillers and texturizing agent. The filler has low overall density, improved functional properties and cost effective with enhanced performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of solid waste-based texturizing filler, which involves (i) providing emulsion polymer skin based solid waste and decontaminating with bio-pack fortified water to obtain washed emulsion skin, (ii) drying the washed emulsion skin at room temperature for removal of moisture, (iii) cryogenic grinding of dried emulsion skin by dry carbon dioxide followed by sieving for desired particle size generation, or (iii) cryogenic grinding of dried emulsion skin by liquid nitrogen followed by sieving for desired particle size generation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anti-caking agents comprise pyrogenic silica, finer calcium carbonate, finer dolomite, finer barium sulfate. Preferred Property: The anti caking agents have size 5-25 microns.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The color fillers comprising Red oxide 105M (RTM: yellowish iron oxide power), Yellow oxide 3920 sudafast blue 2684(RTM: blue pigment), Hostaperm green GNX (RTM: green pigment stable to aromatic solvents), Carbon Black Pearl 120 (RTM: black pigment) and titanium dioxide R 902. The flow leveling additive comprising Resiflow P-67 (RTM: flow and leveling agent) and Resiflow PV-88 (RTM: flow control agent) comprising polyacrylic flow leveling additives on silica carrier comprising 60-70% active content. The resin comprising glass transition temperature greater than 50 degrees C to avoid lump formation at room temperature and preferably polyester resin-based on 2-methyl-1,3-propanediol and purified terephthalic acid or ethylene glycol, trimethylolpropane and purified terephthalic acid and Ketonic resin SRK 100 (RTM: used in paper coating, printing). Preferred Process: The method involves decontaminating emulsion skin by bio-pack fortified water with 40% emulsion skin, 60% water and 0.2% bactericide are mixed for 5-10 minutes using high speed disperser (HSD) followed by filtration to remove water, drying of the washed emulsion skin for complete removal of moisture, grinding the cryogenic using dry carbon dioxide and sieving for desired particle size of filler comprising 80 wt.% dry emulsion skin, 5% anti-caking agent preferably marble powder 500 mesh size and 20 wt.% dry carbon dioxide to fillers of different sizes, or grinding cryogenic using liquid nitrogen and sieving for desired particle size of filler comprising 80 wt.% dry emulsion skin, 5 wt.% marble powder 500# mesh size, and 20 wt.% liquid nitrogen to attain usable fillers having different sizes. The reutilizable solid waste-based texturizing filler is sourced from waste emulsion attached to a internal reactor wall and a stirrer and filtration residue collected in a filtration device as grit particles followed by skin formed in storage tank, polymerization waste from emulsion skin of acrylic or styrene acrylic polymers of emulsion polymerization process obtained in a water media that is converted into usable filler by subjecting to treatment with water containing biocide free solvents being aqueous-based system is environmentally-friendly. Preferred Composition: The emulsion skin filler is 90-96 wt.% (less than 18 mesh size) is combined with 1-4 wt.% color filler, 0.3% flow leveling additive, 5-8 wt.% polyester followed by extruding through twin screw extruder, and grinding and sieving the extruded material to attain color filler of desired particle sizes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The emulsion polymer skin is cryogenically ground comprises dry ice and/or liquid nitrogen comprising acrylic, styrene-acrylic emulsion polymer. The biocide fortified emulsion polymer skin-based texturizing filler in powder form comprising 95 wt.% gel content, preferably cross-linked gel content and 5-25 wt.% thermoplastic non-gel content. The cryogenically ground and pulverized biocide fortified emulsion polymer skin-based texturizing filler comprising 1-10 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% dry ground pulverized emulsion skin powder (DGPES) for low cost powder coating and aqueous-based decorative/architectural paint formulations have appearance and mechanical performance comparable to standard formulations. The DGPES comprises 5 wt.% (18/30) as texturizing filler in architectural coating formulations is comparable to existing silica/dolomite filer. The DGPES as color texturing fillers/agent comprises 90-97 wt.% dry ground pulverized emulsion skin powder (DGPES) greater than 18 mesh, 1-4 wt.% color filler, 0.1- 0.3% flow leveling additive, and 5-8% resin. Preferred Property: The thermoplastic non-gel content has particle size 10 microns to 5 mm, preferably 300-400 microns (passing 100%through 0.85 mm sieve). Preferred Conditions: The cryogenically ground and pulverized biocide fortified emulsion polymer skin-based texturizing filler is cryogenically grounded with fineness (less than 20 mesh) as compared to filler ground by normal ice comprises similar quantity of ground material.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); ; T01 (Digital Computers)

PT P
PN RU2746569-C1
TI METHOD FOR WEANING FROM ARTIFICIAL VENTILATION OF LUNG PATIENTS WITH SPINAL CORD INJURY AT THE LEVEL OF THE NECK SPINE WITH SEVERITY OF SPINAL CORD INJURY ASIA A AND ASIA B.
AU STATSENKO I A
   LEBEDEVA M N
   PALMASH A V
   PERVUKHIN S A
   VASILEV S V
AE NOVOSIBIRSK TRAUMATOLOGY & ORTHOPEDICS (NOVO-Non-standard)
GA 2021433042
AB    NOVELTY - Invention can be used for weaning from mechanical ventilation (ALV) in the postoperative period of patients with spinal cord injury at the level of the cervical spine with the severity of spinal cord injury ASIA A and ASIA B. For this, the method of ultrasound imaging in the mode of independent breathing determines the thickness and mobility of the diaphragm. Gas exchange parameters are determined: partial pressure of oxygen (pO2), partial pressure of carbon dioxide (pCO2), acid-base state (pH) and oxygenation index (OI). The thickness of the diaphragm is determined at rest. The mobility of the diaphragm is determined at rest and during forced breathing. Ventilation is stopped when the preoperative values of the thickness and mobility of the diaphragm are reached or exceeded, as well as if pO2 is greater than or equal to 136 mmHg, pCO2 is greater than or equal to 36 mmHg, pH is 7.43, OI is greater than or equal to 365.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to determine the time point for transferring these patients to spontaneous breathing.
1 cl, 2 ex

PT P
PN CN112645976-A
TI Preparing methylchlorosilane-based organosilicon            using chlorine-based chemical vapor deposition crystal            film growth process tail gas full temperature            range-pressure swing adsorption comprises prepare            exhaust gas in epitaxial growth process.
AU WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
   TANG J
   CAI Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANCAI YUNJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202143654C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methylchlorosilane-based                organosilicon using chlorine-based chemical vapor                deposition (CVD) crystal film growth process tail                gas full temperature range-pressure swing                adsorption (FTrPSA) comprises taking raw material                gas as methylchlorosilane-based organosilicon as                the source of carbon, silicon, and chlorine, and                adding hydrogen CVD to prepare exhaust gas in the                chlorine-based epitaxial growth process based on                silicon carbide, where the main composition is                hydrogen, hydrogen chloride, methyl chloride and                methane, small and trace amounts of chlorosilane,                methane chloride, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                diatomic carbon or more light hydrocarbon                components, methane chloride, silane, water and                silicon dioxide and silicon/carbon fine particles,                and the pressure is room pressure or low pressure,                and the temperature is room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                methylchlorosilane-based organosilicon using                chlorine-based CVD crystal film growth process tail                gas FTrPSA.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes comprehensive utilization                of exhaust gas, reduces exhaust gas emissions and                makes up for the blank of Silicon carbide cvd                chlorine-based crystal or epitaxial film growth                process tail gas treatment technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises pretreatment including pressurizing                  feed gas and sending to a pretreatment unit                  consisting of a dust collector, a particulate                  filter, and an oil mist trap under the operating                  conditions of 0.2-0.3 MPa pressure and room                  temperature, which successively removes dust,                  particles, oil mist, part of perchlorosilane,                  perchloralkanes and high hydrocarbon impurities,                  and making the purified raw material gas formed                  to enter the next process-medium temperature                  pressure swing adsorption, and cleaning and                  returning silicon powder fine particles collected                  from the particulate filter to the subsequent                  direct synthesis process for recycling. The                  medium temperature pressure swing adsorption                  comprises exchanging purified raw material gas                  from the pretreatment with heat to 60-160 degrees                  C, pressurizing to 0.6-0.8 MPa and entering the                  medium temperature pressure swing adsorption                  process consisting of more than 4 adsorption                  towers, where the adsorption temperature is                  60-160 degrees C, and the adsorption pressure is                  0.6-0.8 MPa and the desorption uses vacuum to                  flow out the intermediate gas in the                  non-adsorption phase from the top of the                  adsorption tower in the adsorption state, and                  directly enters the next process after heat and                  cold exchange-medium and room temperature                  pressure swing adsorption concentration, and                  taking adsorption phase desorption gas flowing                  out from the bottom of the adsorption tower in                  the desorption vacuum state as the concentrated                  gas directly into the subsequent chlorosilane                  spray absorption process. The medium and room                  temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration comprises making intermediate gas                  from the medium-temperature PSA process to                  undergo cold and heat exchange to 20-60 degrees                  C, and entering the medium-room temperature PSA                  concentration process composed of more than 4                  adsorption towers, where the adsorption                  temperature is 20-60 degrees C, and the                  adsorption pressure is 0.6-0.8 MPa, and the                  desorption adopts vacuum, and discharging                  non-adsorption phase of the adsorption waste gas                  from the top of the adsorption tower in the                  adsorption state, entering the washing and                  adsorption purification, which can be used as a                  fuel gas or as a raw material gas for hydrogen                  production for pressure swing adsorption hydrogen                  extraction, and taking adsorption phase                  desorption gas flowing out from the bottom of the                  adsorption tower in the desorption vacuum state                  as the concentrated intermediate gas and directly                  entering the next process-direct synthesis. The                  direct synthesis comprises making concentrated                  intermediate gas from the medium and room                  temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration process and methyl chloride or                  anhydrous hydrochloric acid which is in the                  subsequent process according to a certain                  proportion to enter the fluidized bed reactor                  loaded with silicon powder or freshly                  supplemented silicon powder collected in the                  pretreatment process with a suitable ratio and                  the catalyst solid copper powder or copper salt                  particles to directly synthesize methyl                  chlorosilane at 250-300 degrees C and 0.5-0.8                  MPa, passing gas-solid mixture from the fluidized                  bed through the cyclone separator to remove most                  of the dust and entering to the next working                  procedure, rectifying crude monomer of                  methylchlorosilane-based organosilicon which                  flows out from the top, and outputting slurry                  filtered from the bottom of the washing tower or                  if is solid slag waste, or further utilizing as a                  raw material for the production of                  methylchlorosilane organosilicone. The crude                  monomer rectification comprises making crude                  monomer from the direct synthesis process to                  enter the crude monomer rectification separation                  process consisting mainly of the high-boiler                  removal tower, the low-boiler removal tower and                  the binary rectification tower, and distilling                  product from the bottom of the high-boiler                  removal tower, or as waste, sending to the                  incinerator and washing, and subjecting to                  harmless treatment to meet the discharge                  standards, or inputting into azeotropic and                  high-boiling distillation towers to further                  recycle low-chlorine high-boiling substances for                  use as chlorosilane absorbents in the subsequent                  process, and sending high-chlorine high-boiling                  substances to the incinerator as waste and washed                  to be harmless and discharging, making the                  components distilled from the top of the                  high-boiler removal tower to enter the low-boiler                  removal tower, where the top distillate is the                  low-boiler fraction or can be directly used as                  the reaction gas and mixing concentrated                  intermediate gas from the medium and room                  temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration process in proportion or adjusting                  ratio before entering the direct synthesis                  process, or entering the subsequent hydrochloric                  acid refining process to further recycle                  hydrochloric acid and methyl chloride, and making                  distillate from the bottom of the low-boiler                  column to enter the binary rectification tower to                  obtain mono methyl chlorosilane and                  dimethylchlorosilane products (monomer) from the                  top and bottom of the tower, respectively, which                  can be used as the precursor of                  methylchlorosilane organosilicone to be recycled                  into the chlorine-based CVD process. The                  chlorosilane spray absorption comprises                  pressurizing concentrated gas from the medium                  temperature pressure swing adsorption process to                  0.3-0.8 MPa, and subjecting to cold and heat                  exchange to 80-200 degrees C, entering from the                  bottom of the chlorosilane spray absorption                  tower, taking mixed liquid containing mainly                  chlorosilane and small amount of anhydrous                  hydrochloric acid liquid as an absorbent,                  spraying from the top of the spray absorption                  tower to perform reverse mass transfer exchange                  with the concentrated gas, where the                  chlorosilane/hydrochloric acid-enriched absorbing                  liquid flows out from the bottom of the                  chlorosilane spray absorption tower and enters                  the next process-multi-stage                  evaporation/compression/condensation, and                  simultaneously small amount of residual                  particles, high chlorosilane, and high methyl                  chloride flow out from the bottom of the tower                  and outputting high-hydrocarbon impurity for                  environmental protection treatment or collection                  of residual particles for reuse, where                  non-condensable gas 1 flows out from the top of                  the absorption tower, and compressing formed                  non-condensable gas 2, condensing, gas-liquid                  separating and returning to the medium                  temperature pressure swing adsorption process to                  further recycle the components, and separating                  the liquid formed from the gas-liquid separation,                  or returning to the chlorosilane spray absorption                  process as a reflux, or mixing with the                  absorption liquid formed in the chlorosilane                  spray absorption process, and entering the next                  process-multi-stage                  evaporation/compression/condensation. The                  multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation                  comprises mixing absorption liquid from the                  chlorosilane spray absorption process with the                  liquid formed compression, condensation and                  gas-liquid separation, and entering the                  multi-stage evaporation, and entering the                  condenser directly or after being compressed to                  0.3-0.8 MPa to obtain crude hydrochloric acid gas                  in the gas phase, which mainly contains                  hydrochloric acid and methyl chloride, refining                  and rectifying to obtain hydrochloric acid and                  methyl chloride products respectively, returning                  hydrochloric acid or as an absorbent to the                  absorbent in the chlorosilane spray absorption                  process for recycling, or mixing with the                  concentrated intermediate gas from the medium and                  room temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration process to enter the direct                  synthesis process, where the liquid obtained from                  the condenser is crude chlorosilane, which enters                  the next process-the shallow cooling                  rectification in chlorosilane. The shallow                  cooling rectifying in chlorosilane comprises                  making crude chlorosilane liquid from the                  multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation                  process to enter the chlorosilane medium and                  shallow cold rectification process, operating at                  -35 to 10 degrees C under pressure of 0.6-2 MPa,                  directly outputting non-condensable gas 3 flowing                  out from the top of the rectification tower as a                  fuel gas mainly containing diatomic carbon and                  silane, and making chlorosilane liquid flow out                  from the bottom of the rectification tower, or                  mixing with hydrochloric acid liquid as an                  absorbent and returning to the chlorosilane spray                  absorption process for recycling, or as a                  chlorosilane precursor to the chlorine-based CVD                  process for recycling. The medium temperature                  pressure swing adsorption and medium temperature                  pressure swing adsorption concentration processes                  can be combined into a two-stage PSA process,                  i.e. the purified raw gas from the pretreatment                  process, directly or after pressurization to                  0.2-0.4MPa, cold and heat exchange to 80-160                  degrees C, entering the bottom of the first PSA                  adsorption tower (1 stage PSA) composed of                  multiple towers, making intermediate gas of the                  non-adsorption phase flowing out from the top of                  the PSA tower of the first stage to undergo cold                  and heat exchange to 40-70 degrees C, entering                  the second PSA adsorption tower (2-stage PSA)                  composed of multiple towers, pressurizing                  desorbed gas from the bottom of the PSA tower                  (reverse discharge/sequential discharge and                  vacuum) and directly entering the subsequent                  chlorosilane spray absorption process, where the                  adsorption temperature of the second stage PSA is                  40-70 degrees C, and the adsorption pressure is                  0.2-0.4 MPa and the adsorption waste gas flows                  from the top of the second stage PSA tower,                  washing and adsorption purification, which can be                  used as fuel gas, or as raw material gas for                  hydrogen production for pressure swing adsorption                  hydrogen extraction, two-stage PSA desorption,                  additional replacement step of methyl                  chloride/hydrogen chloride mixed gas as                  replacement gas, and reverse discharge/sequential                  release and vacuuming, where adsorption phase gas                  flowing out from the bottom of the adsorption                  tower of the second stage PSA is a concentrated                  intermediate gas and directly entering the direct                  synthesis process to synthesize                  methylchlorosilane-based organosilicon, and the                  amount of replacement gas and the ratio of methyl                  chloride/hydrogen chloride can be adjusted                  according to the ratio of methyl chloride,                  hydrogen chloride, silicon powder and catalyst                  copper-based solid required for the direct                  synthesis reaction. In the method, when the                  concentration of hydrogen chloride, methyl                  chloride, and chlorosilane in the purified raw                  gas is relatively high, making purified raw                  material gas to enter the chlorosilane spray                  absorption process, and the non-condensable gas 1                  to flow out from the top of the absorption tower,                  and directly entering the medium temperature                  pressure swing adsorption process, separating                  intermediate gas escaping from the top of the                  adsorption tower, or freshly supplementing the                  methyl chloride or anhydrous hydrogen chloride in                  the subsequent processes, entering the direct                  synthesis process in a certain proportion, and                  collecting by the pretreatment process with a                  suitable ratio, carrying out direct synthesis                  reaction of methylchlorosilane in a fluidized bed                  reactor of silicon powder or freshly                  supplementing silicon powder and catalyst solid                  copper powder or copper salt particles to save                  the medium and room temperature pressure swing                  adsorption concentration process, returning                  concentrated gas flowing out from the medium                  temperature pressure swing adsorption process to                  the chlorosilane spray absorption process, where                  the absorption liquid enriched in                  chlorosilane/hydrogen chloride flows out from the                  bottom of the absorption tower and enters more                  evaporation/compression/condensation process, and                  simultaneously outputting small amount of                  residual particles, high chlorosilane, high                  methyl chloride and high hydrocarbon impurities                  flowing out from the bottom of the tower for                  environmental protection treatment or collected                  residual particles for recycling. The gas-solid                  mixture flowing out of the fluidized bed reactor                  in the direct synthesis process is passed through                  the vertical cracking set in the catalyst bed of                  the reactor to directly generate water vapor to                  remove the heat generated by the reaction, and                  entered and separated by cyclone, where the                  reactor collects a small amount of catalyst                  particles and silicon powder brought out by the                  reaction gas, and periodically makes up to the                  fluidized bed reactor and the reaction gas                  obtained by the cyclone separator is passed                  through the quench tower to form a crude monomer                  containing methylchlorosilane organosilicone, and                  entered the crude monomer rectification process                  for separation and purification. The crude                  monomer produced by washing or                  quenching/condensing in the direct synthesis                  process contains a small amount of carbon dioxide                  or diatomic carbon, and in the working condition,                  part of the crude monomer needs to be added with                  a decarburization tower before entering the crude                  monomer rectification process, and the organic                  amine is used to wash and absorb, and the crude                  monomer which is decarbonized by washing and                  absorbing enters the crude monomer rectification                  process, and a part of the crude monomer flowing                  out from the washing or quenching/condensation is                  used as the reaction cycle gas, without                  decarburization, and is directly returned to the                  reactor of the direct synthesis process for                  further reaction. In the chlorosilane spray                  absorption process, under the working condition                  of high concentration of hydrogen chloride,                  chlorosilane/chloromethane contained in the                  purified raw material gas, a secondary                  intermediate temperature chlorosilane absorption                  process is added, i.e., the non-condensable gas                  from the chlorosilane spray absorption process,                  after being compressed, condensed and separated                  from the gas and liquid, forms non-condensable                  gas directly or pressurized to 0.2-1 MPa, and                  exchanged between cold and heat to 60-120 degrees                  C, entering from the bottom of the absorption                  tower of the additional secondary chlorosilane                  spray absorption process, and                  chlorosilane/hydrogen chloride mixed liquid is                  taken as the absorbent, sprayed from the top of                  the secondary chlorosilane spray absorption tower                  to the non-condensing gas to undergo reverse mass                  transfer exchange, and the absorption liquid 2                  enriched with chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride                  flows out from the bottom of the absorption                  tower, and mixed with the absorption liquid 1 of                  the medium temperature (primary) chlorosilane                  spray absorption process, and enters the                  subsequent multi-stage                  evaporation/compression/condensation process,                  where non-condensable gas flows out from the top                  of the absorption tower and enters the medium and                  room temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration process to save the medium                  temperature pressure swing adsorption process. In                  the crude monomer rectification process, when the                  crude monomer contains non-condensable gas                  hydrogen and other methylchlorosilane-based                  organosilicon monomers need to be obtained, a                  light separation tower can be added (the product                  is a light boiling component),                  hydrogen-containing tower (the product is                  hydrogen-containing gas), azeotropic tower (the                  product is azeotrope), the trimethyl tower (the                  product is trimethylchlorosilane) and the                  high-boiling tower (the product is high-boiler)                  carry out rectification and separation operations                  to obtain other methylchlorosilane-based                  organosilicon monomer products. In the pressure                  swing adsorption tower in the middle temperature                  pressure swing adsorption and the middle                  temperature pressure swing adsorption                  concentration process, under the operating                  condition that the adsorption pressure is greater                  than or equal to 0.6 MPa, the pressure change                  during the adsorption and desorption cycle                  operation is realized by the program control                  valve and the regulating valve on the pipeline                  connected between the adsorption towers to                  realize the slow average control and prevent the                  system pressure from changing too much, and air                  flow caused by the large air scouring the bed of                  the adsorption tower and the powdering of the                  adsorbent makes the operation of the system                  stable and safe in the process. Preferred                  Components: The raw material gas includes waste                  gas or tail gas containing hydrogen, hydrogen                  chloride, chlorosilane, and methyl                  chloride/methane as the main components produced                  in other semiconductor manufacturing                  processes.

TI Sample analysis system, has sensors for obtaining environment parameter of analysis device and processor for managing test of to-be-tested sample according to environment parameter.
AU YI J
   WANG X
AE SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO MEDICAL ELECTRONICS (MRAY-C)
GA 202137416J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an analysis device provided with a sample component, a reagent component and a measuring component. The sample component bears a to-be-tested sample. The reagent component carries reagent. The measuring component tests the sample to obtain test data. Multiple sensors obtain an environment parameter of the analysis device. A processor manages test of the to-be-tested sample according to the environment parameter. The sensor is provided with a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, an air quality sensor, a water quality sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor and an air pressure sensor.
   USE - Sample analysis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system obtains the environment parameter of the analysis device, fuses the environment parameter to a test system, simplifies work flow, enhances reliability of a detection result and promotes unmanned management effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a sample analysis device test management method; and
   (2) a computer-readable storage medium for storing set of instructions for managing test of a sample analysis device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a sample analysis device test management method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN112457853-A; CN112457853-B
TI Preparing zinc-rich biochar comprises e.g. mixing furfural residue, zinc(II) oxide and water, then storing in sealed container, heating the product, carbonizing, heating, activating and cooling.
AU LIU K
   DONG X
   YU X
   XIAO J
   ZHANG X
AE HIGH & NEW TECHNOLOGY RES CENT SHANDONG (SDAC-C)
GA 2021251708
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zinc-rich biochar comprises (i) mixing furfural residue, zinc(II) oxide and water, then storing it in a sealed container at a temperature of 20-50 degrees C for 120-240 hours, (ii) heating the product obtained in step (i) to 250-450 degrees C, carbonizing for 1~3 hours under nitrogen atmosphere and (iii) heating to 500-700 degrees C, activating it for 0.5-2 hours at a flow rate of carbon dioxide gas of 10-60 ml/minute and then cooling it to obtain final product.
   USE - The zinc-rich biochar is useful in the passivation remediation of heavy metal contaminated soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the reduction and harmless disposal of furfural residue and reduces environmental discharge and pollution; produces biochar, which can fix heavy metal pollutants, has a good repair effect on heavy metal polluted farmland and is an ideal low-cost soil heavy metal pollution passivation repair agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) zinc-rich biochar prepared by above method; and
   (2) use of zinc-rich biochar in the passivation remediation of heavy metal contaminated soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (i) comprises mixing the furfural residue, zinc(II) oxide and water in mass ratio of 30-50:0.25-1.5:50-75. The step (i) comprises sealing and storing at 35 degrees C for 180 hours. The step (ii) comprises heating to 400 degrees C for 2 hours at a heating rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute. The step (iii) comprises heating to 600-650 degrees C at a heating rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute and activating for 1.5 hours at a flow rate of carbon dioxide gas of 30 ml/minute. The heavy metal is lead, cadmium and/or arsenic. Preferred Method: In use method: addition amount of zinc-rich biochar to heavy metal contaminated soil is 90-3000 kg per mu or 0.030-1.0 wt.% of the soil. The use method comprises adding water into zinc-rich biochar until the water content is maintained at 30-50% and then applying to heavy metal contaminated soil.

PN CN112442208-A; CN112442208-B
TI Foaming agent based on cyclohexanone by-product            waste alkali liquor, comprises water, sodium            bicarbonate, organic acid sodium salt and ketone            alcohol organic matter.
AU JI F
   HUANG W
   JIN H
   YANG Z
   HE Z
   ZHAO S
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM IND CO LTD CATALYST (SNPC-C)
GA 202124421C
AB    NOVELTY - Foaming agent based on cyclohexanone                by-product waste alkali liquor is claimed. The                foaming agent is prepared by waste alkali liquor                i.e. a by-product of cyclohexane oxidation method                taking cyclohexanone as raw material; the foaming                agent comprises 40-60 wt.% water, 10-20 wt.% sodium                bicarbonate, 10-20 wt.% organic acid sodium salt                and 5-10 wt.% ketone alcohol organic matter.
   USE - Used as foaming agent based on cyclohexanone                by-product waste alkali liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can effectively recycle the                cyclohexanone waste alkali liquor; realizes                resource recycling; produces foaming agent, which                has excellent foaming effect and closed hole rate                more than 95%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing foaming agent based on                cyclohexanone by-product waste alkali liquor                produced by the cyclohexane oxidation method,                comprising deodorizing, purifying and carbon                dioxide reaction to obtain final product; and
   (2) use method of the foaming agent,                comprising mixing foaming agent with a                polymerization catalyst, a flame retardant, a foam                stabilizer and a polyol ether to form a foaming                composition, adding it into the raw materials of                polyurethane, polyvinyl chloride or                polystyrene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material                  cyclohexanone waste alkali liquor comprises 30-70                  wt.% water, 20-40 wt.% sum of sodium carbonate                  and sodium hydroxide and the sodium adipate,                  sodium valerate, cyclohexanone and cyclohexanol                  (balanced amount). The pH value of the foaming                  agent is 8-9 and the foaming temperature range is                  120-150 degrees C. The foaming agent has a                  viscosity of 100-400 centipoise and a foaming                  volume-mass ratio of 30-50%. In use method: the                  mass ratio of polyurethane, polyvinyl chloride or                  polystyrene raw material to the foaming agent is                  10:1-20:1. Preferred Method: The deodorization                  and purification is to heating the waste                  cyclohexanone alkali liquor in a reaction kettle                  to 80-120 degrees C, stirring for 1-4 hours until                  there is no peculiar smell and adsorbing the tail                  gas and purifying by an adsorption device. The                  carbon dioxide passing reaction is to pass the                  waste cyclohexanone alkali liquor into the                  reactor by bubbling and stirring with carbon                  dioxide, maintaining a pressure of 0.1-2 MPa, a                  reaction temperature of 25-40 degrees C and a                  reaction time of 4-8 hours to produce foaming                  agent. The muse method comprises uniformly                  injecting the foaming composition into a foaming                  mold after stirring, heating to the foaming                  temperature to prepare a foam material with a                  closed cell ratio of greater than 95%.

PN CN112138695-A
TI Preparing Z-type zinc oxide-gold-g-carbon nitride composite film photocatalytic material comprises performing hydro thermal reaction of water, ethylenediamine, zinc flakes, immersing in chloroauric acid aqueous solution and calcining.
AU LI X
   WANG H
   JIANG H
   GAO M
   ZHU Z
   MA C
   HUO P
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202103215K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Z-type zinc oxide-gold-g-carbon nitride composite film photocatalytic material comprises (i) using a mixture of water and ethylenediamine as a solvent, zinc flakes as zinc source and growth substrate, performing hydrothermal reaction, washing with water and alcohol, drying, and calcining to obtain zinc oxide micron needle film material, (ii) immersing the zinc oxide micro-needle thin film material in chloroauric acid aqueous solution, exposing to a UV lamp for a certain period of time, and calcining to obtain binary composite material, and (iii) immersing the zinc oxide-gold micro-needle film in melamine methanol solution, blow drying, and calcining in a muffle furnace at 500 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The film is useful for ternary microneedle thin film composite material with plasmon resonance enhancement effect and for the photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide molecules to carbon monoxide and methane fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The film will not cause waste of resources and the formation of secondary pollution, is a green and environmentally friendly photocatalyst for efficient pollution treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the size of the zinc sheet is 2 cm asterisk 2 cm and V. Water: V-ethylenediamine is 1:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at a temperature of 180 degrees C for 12 hours, drying for 6 hours, and calcining is performed at a temperature of 350 degrees C for 2 hours. In the step (ii), the concentration of the chloroauric acid aqueous solution is cAu=0.0024 micromole/l, the absorption is performed for 2 minutes, the irradiation time of the ultraviolet lamp is 5 minutes and the times of adsorption-reduction is 1-4 times. In the step (ii), the calcining is performed at a temperature of 500 degrees C for 2 hours. In the step (iii), the concentration of the melamine methanol solution is 0.1 mM, the adsorption time is 2 minutes, and the repeated times of the adsorption process is 2-8 times. In the step (iii), the calcining is performed at a temperature of 500 degrees C for 2 hours.

   M=Al, Co, Cr, Fe, Zn, Mn, Ti, Ni, Ga, Sm, Y, or V;
   formula (i)=formulae (i1)-(i4);

TI Regioselective electrochemical synthesis of nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic carboxylic acid includes reacting nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic compound, alkali and metal catalyst with supporting electrolyte solution.
AU YU D
   SUN G
   ZHANG W
   LIAO L
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020C47015
AB    NOVELTY - Regioselective electrochemical synthesis of nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic carboxylic acid includes adding nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic compound, alkali and metal catalyst at mole ratio of 1:0.1-10:0.1-1 into synthesis vessel with electrodes fixed on both sides, adding supporting electrolyte solution under carbon dioxide atmosphere, stirring and dissolving to obtain mixed solution; passing a constant electrolytic current between electrodes and reacting at 10-60 degrees C; adding methyl iodide to reacted mixed solution and reacting continuously at 50-70 degrees C for 1-10 hours to obtain crude product; and purifying to obtain target carboxylated products with different regioselectivities.
   USE - The method is for regioselective electrochemical synthesis of nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of high cost caused by rare earth transition metal catalyst in the existing method and low yield and poor selectivity of the reaction product. The synthesis method has convenient operation, mild reaction condition, wide substrate range, the used raw material is cheap and easy to obtain; and has good industrial application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali is cesium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, lithium carbonate, sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, lithium tert-butoxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium tert-butoxide, bicyclic guanidine, sodium pivalate, cesium pivalate or cesium fluoride. The metal catalyst is nickel acetylacetonate, palladium acetylacetonate, iron trichloride, aluminum trichloride, cobalt acetylacetonate, copper trifluoromethanesulfonate, copper bromide, copper acetate, cupric acetylacetonate, nickel trifluoromethanesulfonate, scandium triflate or ytterbium trifluorosulfonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic compound is substituted pyridine, substituted pyrazine, substituted pyrimidine, substituted imidazole, substituted pyrazole, substituted pyrrole, substituted quinoline, aryl pyridine, aryl pyrazine, aryl imidazole or aryl pyrrole. The supporting electrolyte solution includes solute comprising tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium iodide, tetrabutylammonium hexafluorophosphate, tetrabutylammonium acetate, tetrabutylammonium p-toluenesulfonate, tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate, lithium bromide, lithium chloride, sodium iodide or 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide; and solvent comprising N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, acetonitrile or diethylene glycol dimethyl ether.

PN CN112029913-A
TI Agent useful for clarifying and decolorizing sugar juice i.e. sugarcane mixed juice, brown sugar remelting syrup and raw sugar remelting syrup, comprises magnesium carbonate.
AU LI L
   CHENG H
   LI W
   KONG H
   HUANG W
   LI Y
   FENG J
AE UNIV GUANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYGN-C)
GA 2020C5340A
AB    NOVELTY - Agent comprises magnesium carbonate.
   USE - The agent is useful for clarifying and decolorizing sugar juice. The sugar juice is chosen from sugarcane mixed juice, brown sugar remelting syrup and raw sugar remelting syrup (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The agent has strong adsorption performance of magnesium hydroxide and calcium carbonate, and achieves a satisfactory decolorization effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of agent for clarifying and decolorizing sugar juice by adding 0.1-0.3 g magnesium carbonate with respect to 100 ml sugar juice at 30-60 degrees C, adjusting pH value to 10.5-11.5 with lime milk, stirring and reacting at 30-60 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, adding anionic polyacrylamide, stirring quickly for 1-3 minutes, switching to slow stirring for 2-10 minutes, maintaining for 25-35 minutes, taking supernatant liquid, passing in carbon dioxide (CO2) to adjust the pH to 8-8.5, adding anionic polyacrylamide, flocculating, settling, and taking supernatant after the flocculation and sedimentation are stable to obtain a clean juice, where 0.05-0.20 ml of 2 g/l anionic polyacrylamide is added with respect to 100 ml sugar juice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The magnesium carbonate is a petal-like or rod-like structure of magnesium carbonate. Preferred Method: The magnesium carbonate having petal-like structure is prepared by using sodium carbonate and magnesium chloride substance in a ratio of 1:0.9-1.1, by taking 0.9-1.1 mol/l sodium carbonate solution and 0.9-1.1 mol/l magnesium chloride solution, mixing to obtain a mixed solution, stirring for 15-25 minutes, maintaining for aging for 1.5-2.5 hours, filtering and washing to obtain magnesium carbonate, and drying at 45-55 degrees C for 9-11 hours. The rapid stirring is carried out at a speed of 300-350 revolutions per minute (rpm). The slow stirring is carried out at a speed of 20-40 rpm.

PN CN111933911-A
TI High dispersion degree silica-nitrogen-doped porous carbon cathode material useful for lithium ion batteries prepared by reacting cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, magnesium carbonate hydroxide and ethyl orthosilicate.
AU LIU D
AE LIU D (LIUD-Individual)
GA 2020B5776L
AB    NOVELTY - High dispersion degree silica-nitrogen-doped porous carbon cathode material is claimed. The cathode material is prepared by (i) adding cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, magnesium carbonate hydroxide and ethyl orthosilicate into the mixed solvent of concentrated ammonia, distilled water and ethanol, reacting for 4-8 hours at room temperature, filtering, washing, and drying, placing the solid product in the ammonium chloride solution in a atmosphere reactor, passing carbon dioxide gas, carrying out the etching reaction for 30-60 minutes, placing the etching precursor product in atmosphere furnace, blowing in air, heating at 500-550 degrees C, and heat preservation treating for 2-4 hours to prepare bird's nest-shaped multilayer silica hollow microspheres.
   USE - The carbon cathode material is useful for lithium ion batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode material: has complex layered structure and unique hollow structure, a high specific surface area, and rich active sites for lithium removal/intercalation; buffers the stress generated in the process of lithium removal/lithium insertion; and reduces the phenomenon of silica volume expansion leading to matrix powdering and shedding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (ii) adding bird's nest-type multilayer silica hollow microspheres and isophorone diisocyanate into toluene solvent, uniformly ultrasonic dispersing, filling the atmosphere reactor with nitrogen, heating at 65-85 degrees C, reacting for 10-20 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to prepare isocyanated silica hollow microspheres, (iii) adding the isocyanated silica hollow microspheres and 2-hydroxyethyl 2-bromoisobutyrate into toluene solvent, and uniformly ultrasonic dispersing, placing it in an atmosphere reactor and blowing with nitrogen, heating at 30-50 degrees C, reacting for 6-24 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to prepare functionalized bromo-silica macroinitiator, (iv) adding styrene, acrylamide, functionalized bromo-silica macroinitiator, 2,2'-bipyridine and cuprous bromide into toluene solvent, and pass freezing treating and vacuum thawing treating, placing in an atmosphere reactor and blown into nitrogen, heating at 45-65 degrees C, reacting for 6-12 hours, distilling under reduced pressure, washing and drying to prepare hollow silica microspheres grafted with polystyrene in situ, (v) adding silica hollow microspheres into the carbon tetrachloride solvent to graft polystyrene in situ, stirring well and adding aluminum chloride, performing super cross-linking reaction for 24-48 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to prepare silica hollow microspheres grafted super cross-linked polystyrene, (vi) placing the silica hollow microspheres grafted with polystyrene in situ in an atmosphere furnace, blowing with nitrogen, heating at 600-700 degrees C, and calcining for 2-4 hours to prepare nitrogen-doped carbon-coated silica, and (vii) mixing nitrogen-doped carbon-coated silica and potassium hydroxide uniformly, placing in an atmosphere furnace, heating at 600-650 degrees C, and calcining for 2-3 hours to prepare final product.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN111747437-A
TI Preparation of ultra-small calcium carbonate powder for processing artificial stone slab, involves crushing calcium carbide slag, adding e.g. stearic acid, mixing, adding e.g. calcium hydroxide melt, stirring, carbonizing, and grinding.
AU MIN C
AE YAAN ZHENGXING WHITE MARBLE CO LTD (YAAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A2423E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultra-small calcium carbonate powder involves primary-crushing calcium carbide slag to primary particle size, washing with micro-acid solution, mixing treated calcium carbide slag with distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:2, adding an impurity removing agent, stirring, filtering, introducing carbon dioxide, heating, drying, crushing the dried calcium carbide slag with primary particle size, then grinding, collecting ultra-small calcium carbonate powder in a cloth bag by airflow through a winnowing equipment, adding the ultra-small calcium carbonate powder to a reactor, then adding stearic acid and carbon tetrachloride, stirring at 120-160 degrees C for 20 minutes, cooling to 30-40 degrees C, adding calcium hydroxide melt and crystal form control agent, stirring, carbonizing, grinding obtained powder crystals, screening calcium carbonate crystal powder with a particle size of less than 0.1 micron, and packaging for storage.
   USE - Preparation of ultra-small calcium carbonate powder used for processing artificial stone slab.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of ultra-small calcium carbonate powder by simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The primary crushing is performed using a hammer crushing device. The distilled water is mixed with the impurity removing agent, and the primary crushed carbide slag is circulated and washed by circulating the water pump. The heating and drying treatment by introducing carbon dioxide is performed by introducing carbon dioxide gas into a drying reactor under the pressure of 100 pa, circulating carbon dioxide through an air pump and a circulation pipe. An adsorption filter plug is installed to absorb water. The wind direction of the winnowing equipment flows from bottom to top, and the air volume of the winnowing equipment is 2400-3000 m3/minute. Preferred Property: The primary particle size of calcium carbide slag is 260-300 mesh. Preferred Composition: The crystal control agent is chosen from sulfate, phosphate and sodium metaphosphate. The impurity removing agent is chosen from calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN CN111659380-A
TI Preparing high specific surface area silica-carbonized bacterial cellulose preferential oxidation catalyst comprises e.g. preparing bacterial cellulose membrane, preparing bacterial cellulose silica sol solution, calcining and carbonizing.
AU ZUO H
AE ZUO H (ZUOH-Individual)
GA 202092241P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high specific surface area silica-carbonized bacterial cellulose preferential oxidation catalyst comprises (i) preparing bacterial cellulose membrane by culture medium-autoclaving-inoculation-fermentation-washing and activation, (ii) preparing bacterial cellulose silica sol solution, (iii) preparing three-dimensional network polystyrene template, and filling the bacterial cellulose silica sol solution into the ethylene template to obtain silica-bacterial cellulose-polystyrene precursor, (iv) calcining and carbonizing with high temperature under nitrogen gas to obtain silica- carbonized bacterial cellulose, and (v) impregnating ruthenium with equal volume to obtain ruthenium/silica-carbonized bacterial cellulose monolithic block catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high specific surface area silica-carbonized bacterial cellulose preferential oxidation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has monolithic structure, high compressive strength, strong thermal conductivity, adjustable shape, and strong applicability; utilizes three-dimensional multi-stage porous structure as catalyst carrier which having very high specific surface area of 1200-1500 m2/g; and utilizes ruthenium nanoparticles are highly dispersed on the surface of the carrier, which has extremely high catalytic activity, and excellent water and carbon dioxide resistance and stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) is performed by (a) dissolving 45 g/l glucose, 1.5 g/l citric acid, 4.5 g/l yeast powder, 5.5 g/l peptone, and 0.75 g/l sodium hydrogen phosphate in deionized water, and adjusting the pH to 5 with sodium hydroxide to obtain culture medium, (b) sterilizing in a high-temperature steam sterilizer at 115 degrees C for 40 minutes, and irradiating it with a sterilization lamp to reduce to room temperature, (c) inoculating Acetobacter xylinum by pipette, where the volume of the bacterial solution accounts for 1/10 of the volume of the medium, (d) fermenting in an incubator at 30 degrees C for 6-8 days until the medium is significantly reduced, (e) washing with deionized water for many times, washing with 1 wt.% sodium hydroxide at 70 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours to obtain white bacterial cellulose membrane, washing with deionized water again to neutrality, and crushing using a homogenizer to obtain bacterial cellulose slurry deionized aqueous solution. The bacterial cellulose silica sol solution in step (2) is prepared by stirring and dissolving certain amount of P123 surfactant in deionized water at 50 degrees oC, adding bacterial cellulose slurry deionized aqueous solution, adding 5-20 g ethyl orthosilicate to obtain white translucent bacterial cellulose silica sol solution. The process of three-dimensional network polystyrene template in step (iii) is performed by washing styrene and divinylbenzene reagents with sodium hydroxide and deionized water respectively, taking 0.81 g Span 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) and 0.093g azobisisobutyronitrile into a three-necked flask, adding 9 ml styrene and 2.78 ml divinylbenzene, stirring vigorously for 0.5 hours in a water bath at 30 degrees C to make it evenly dispersed, dropping 68 ml deionized water into the three-necked flask by an infusion needle, continue to stirring for 3 hours, pouring it into a sealable mold, placing it in a 60oC blast and drying for 12 hours, open the mold and continue drying for overnight (24 hours), ultrasonically washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol, and drying for 24 hours to obtain block polystyrene template. The filling process in step (iii) is performed by filling the prepared polystyrene template with the white translucent bacterial cellulose silica sol solution under the condition of pumping vacuum until no bubbles emerge from the template, drying it in a 60 degrees C oven, and repeating the filling and drying for 5 times to obtain silica-bacterial cellulose-polystyrene precursor. The calcination and carbonization with high temperature is performed by calcining the obtained silica-bacterial cellulose-polystyrene precursor in a low-temperature nitrogen tube furnace at 550 degrees C for 7 hours to remove the polystyrene template and carbonizing the bacterial cellulose film at a heating rate of 1.5 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: In step (v), the amount of impregnated ruthenium in an equal volume is 1-3 wt.%, and after loading, vacuum freeze-drying is used to remove water to obtain ruthenium/silica- carbonized bacterial cellulose monolithic block catalyst. The ruthenium precursor liquid is ruthenium(III) nitrosyl nitrate, and the time for the equal volume immersion is 24-36 hours. The ruthenium/silica- carbonized bacterial cellulose monolithic block catalyst is activated and reduced in advance with 5 vol.% hydrogen/nitrogen at 300oC for 2 hours before the activity test.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN111608741-A; CN111608741-B
TI Generator waste heat recycling organic Rankine            cycle system, has steam injection cooling system for            pumping gaseous working medium in working medium            circulation system, where steam injection cooling            system reduces condensation temperature.
AU LI Y
   YE S
   ZHUANG X
   HUANG W
   YU Y
   CHEN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 202087197P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a working medium circulating                system connected with a generator. A generator                waste heat recycling system divides a working                medium in the working medium circulating system.                The generator waste heat recovery system passes the                working medium in the working medium circulation                system into the generator to absorb waste heat when                the generator is working. A steam injection cooling                system boosts pressure of the working medium that                absorbs waste heat of the generator. The steam                injection cooling system reduces cooling pressure.                The steam injection cooling system pumps gaseous                working medium in the working medium circulation                system. The steam injection cooling system reduces                condensation temperature, where the working medium                is pentafluoropropane, dichloro-trifluoroethane,                carbon dioxide, ammonia or helium.
   USE - Generator waste heat recycling organic Rankine                cycle (ORC) system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has simple structure, and                eliminates need to increase the working medium,                recovers waste heat of the generator, increases                output power of an expander and improves energy                utilization efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                generator waste heat recycling ORC system.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide/polyethylene oxide complex                aerogel comprises (1) dissolving polyethylene oxide                in 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone to obtain solution A, (2)                dissolving 4-aminodiphenyl ether in                1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinone, stirring, adding                3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride,                stirring until the reactant becomes transparent and                forming solution B, (3) dissolving the                cross-linking agent                1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene in 12 ml                1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone to obtain a solution C, (4)                pouring solution C into solution B and stirring at                room temperature to obtain a transparent solution                BC, (5) mixing and stirring solution BC and                solution A to obtain a mixed solution, adding                acetic anhydride and pyridine, stirring and pouring                into the mold, (6) aging the gel in a sealed                environment, soaking in acetone solution, and (7)                drying by supercritical carbon dioxide drying                method, drying the sample in a vacuum drying oven                to remove residual solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                polyimide/polyethylene oxide complex aerogel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                polyimide/polyethylene oxide complex aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1),                  dissolving 4 g polyethylene oxide in 80 ml                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone to obtain a solution A,                  where the value of each component content used to                  express the proportional relationship between the                  content of each component, and the numerical                  value of each component content and process                  parameter represents the range of 15% above and                  below the numerical value. In the step (2),                  dissolving 1.58 g 4-aminodiphenyl ether in 66 ml                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, stirring, adding 2.395 g                  of 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride,                  stirring until the reaction becomes transparent                  to obtain solution B. In the step (3), dissolving                  0.07 g cross-linking agent                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene in 12 ml                  1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone to obtain a solution C. In                  the step (5), mixing solution BC and solution A                  in the volume ratio of 20:0, and adding 1.55 ml                  acetic anhydride and 1.30 ml pyridine. In the                  step (5), mixing solution BC and the solution A                  mixed in a volume ratio of 18:2, and adding 1.40                  ml acetic anhydride and 1.20 ml pyridine. In the                  step (5), mixing the solution BC and the solution                  A in a volume ratio of 16:4, and adding 1.25 ml                  acetic anhydride and 1.05 ml pyridine. In the                  step (5), mixing the solution BC and the solution                  A in a volume ratio of 15:5, and adding 1.15 ml                  acetic anhydride and 1 ml pyridine. In the step                  (6), the soaking with acetone solution includes                  soaking in 75% 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone in acetone,                  soaking 50% 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone in acetone,                  soaking 25% 1-methyl sodium 2-pyrrolidinone in                  acetone, and then soaking in pure acetone.

PN CN111286062-A; CN111286062-B
TI Composite material useful for fruit and vegetable            packaging bags, comprises e.g. waterproof and            moisture-permeable layer, water-absorbing layer, and            antibacterial layer.
AU TAO T
   TAO Y
AE SICHUAN TIANXING INTELLIGENT PACKAGING (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2020564825
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material comprises waterproof and                moisture-permeable layer (1) for making fruit and                vegetable packaging bags, coating water-absorbing                layer (2) on the inner wall of the waterproof and                moisture-permeable layer, and coating antibacterial                layer (3) on the water-absorbing layer, where the                waterproof and moisture-permeable layer includes,                2-20 pts. wt. polyacrylonitrile, 15-30 pts. wt.                polyurethane, and 50-83 pts. wt.                N,N-dimethylformamide.
   USE - The composite material is useful for fruit and                vegetable packaging bags (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: can absorb water when there is                too much water mist in fruits and vegetables                through the water-absorbing layer; maintains the                packaging bag dry; avoids moisture in the packaging                bag to help bacterial growth; maintains the                packaging bag dry; allows air to pass through;                maintains low oxygen and high carbon dioxide                atmosphere in the packaging bag; inhibits the                growth of bacteria in the packaging bag; ensures                that fruits and vegetables can have a longer shelf                life; and reduces the risk of deterioration of                fruits and vegetables.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of composite material.
   Waterproof and moisture-permeable layer                (1)
   Coating water-absorbing layer (2)
   Coating Antibacterial layer (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method comprises, (i)                  taking 2-20 wt.% polyacrylonitrile particles, and                  15-30 wt.% polyurethane particles, (ii) mixing                  polyacrylonitrile particles and polyurethane                  particles, (iii) dividing the mixed                  polyacrylonitrile particles and polyurethane                  particles into 3-5 batches, and placing the first                  batch of the mixture into 50-83 wt.%                  N,N-dimethylformamide solvent, stirring at 200                  revolutions per minute with a temperature of 80                  degrees C until the polyacrylonitrile and                  polyurethane particles are mixes in                  N,N-dimethylformamide solvent, (iv) repeating the                  step (iii) until all batches of the mixture are                  added and mixed uniformly, and (v) extruding the                  mixed solvent on a blown film extruder to blow                  out the film to obtain finished product of                  waterproof and moisture-permeable layer.                  Preferred Components: The material for the                  waterproof and moisture-permeable layer is                  polyurethane mixed with hydrophobic                  polyacrylonitrile, and the thickness of                  waterproof and moisture-permeable layer is 10-160                  mu m. The water-absorbing layer includes 40-50                  wt.% water absorbent, 10-20 wt.% starch and                  de-ionized water (balance amount). The water                  absorbent is sodium hexametaphosphate and/or                  crosslinked carboxymethyl cellulose with high                  degree of substitution, and the thickness of the                  water-absorbing layer is 1-20 mu m. The                  antibacterial layer is mixture of antibacterial                  agent and antibacterial carrier, and the                  thickness of antibacterial layer is 1-20 mu m.                  The antibacterial agent is nisin and/or                  antibacterial peptide, and the antibacterial                  carrier is a mixture of polysaccharide and                  plasticizer. The polysaccharide is dextrin,                  chitosan, or K-carrageenan oligosaccharide, and                  the plasticizer is glycerol, sorbitol, or propyl                  gallate.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The drug includes an                  effective dose of pentylbenzene derivative (I).                  Preferred Conditions: The medicine is an oral                  preparation or an injection preparation.
