The injection preparation is selected                  from injections or powder injections. The carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction conditions are                  pressure of extraction kettle is 20-30 megapascal                  (MPa), temperature is 35-60 degrees C, pressure                  of separation kettle I is 8-11 MPa, temperature                  of separation kettle I is 35-65 degrees C,                  pressure of separation kettle II is 3-6MPa,                  temperature of separation kettle II is 30-40                  degrees C. The amount of ethanol used in ethanol                  extraction is 15-25% of the inflorescence weight                  of hemp plants, and the extraction time is 30-60                  minutes. The method for preparing terpene phenol                  includes the crude extract is fully dissolved                  with petroleum ether. The normal phase silica gel                  column chromatography accordingly uses 100%                  petroleum ether, petroleum ether: ethyl acetate                  is 100: 1, petroleum ether: ethyl acetate is 10:                  1, ethyl acetate 100% as the eluent gradient                  elution. The first-stage reverse-phase silica gel                  column chromatography uses methanol solution as                  the eluent for gradient elution, preferably                  50-100 vol.% methanol solution. The second-stage                  reverse phase silica gel column chromatography                  uses methanol solution as the eluent for gradient                  elution, preferably 60-80% (v/v) methanol                  solution.

TI Continuous production of furfural dicarboxylic acid by furfural includes dissolving furfural as raw material in solvent as raw material solution, storing raw material solution, discharging reaction product and condensing.
AU QIAN C
   JIN H
   ZHOU S
   RUAN J
   WANG S
   CHEN X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG QUZHOU RES INST (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202047914X
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production of furfural dicarboxylic acid by furfural comprises dissolving 1 kg furfural as raw material in 9.5-10.5 L solvent as raw material solution, storing raw material solution in raw material tank, using raw material solution, carbon dioxide and oxygen as mixed raw materials to enter reactor from top of constant temperature fixed bed reactor for reaction, fixing 50-100 g transition metal supported catalyst in constant temperature fixed bed reactor, reacting at 120-140 degrees c under atmospheric pressure, discharging reaction product from bottom of constant temperature fixed bed reactor, heating in vacuum distillation tower, discharging furan dicarboxylic acid from the bottom of vacuum distillation column, discharging gas from the top of vacuum distillation column to recovery tank, condensing, circulating back to the top of constant temperature fixed bed reactor by pump, sending to reactor together with mixed raw materials and reacting for 10-15 hours.
   USE - The method is for continuous production of furfural dicarboxylic acid by furfural.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process and high yield and is environment friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The transition metal supported catalyst is prepared by dissolving soluble salt of transition metal in water to obtain salt solution A, dispersing molecular sieve in water, adding salt solution, mixing for 3-6 hours, standing for 1-2 hours and roasting precipitate at 400-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours. Preferred Components: The weight ratio of transition metal in soluble salt to molecular sieve is 1-1.6:100. The transition metal is nickel (Ni), copper (Cu), rhodium (Rh) and/or palladium (Pd). The molecular sieve is 4A, X, Y, Zeolite Socony Mobil 5 (ZSM-5), aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide and/or zirconium dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is glycol dimethyl ether, dioxane and/or acetonitrile.

PN CN111115673-A
TI Alkali residue component utilization method involves mixing alkali residue with water, water washing and separating to form liquid phase and solid, crystallizing liquid phase to form sodium chloride, and e.g. heat-treating solid phase.
AU JIA Z
   GUO Q
AE HEBEI CAS TONGCHUANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202041416Y
AB    NOVELTY - Alkali residue component utilization method involves mixing alkali residue with water, repeating water washing and solid-liquid separation to obtain liquid phase (A) and solid phase (A), concentrating and crystallizing liquid phase (A) to obtain sodium chloride and calcium chloride, heat-treating solid phase to obtain calcined powder, mixing calcined powder with 20 %mass ammonium chloride solution for leaching (L1), separating to obtain solid precipitate and liquid phase (B), adding dispersant to liquid phase (B), introducing carbon dioxide gas to perform carbonization to obtain calcium carbonate, mixing solid precipitate with 10-20 %mass ammonium sulfate solution, heating, leaching (L2) until solid-liquid separation is carried out after distillation of aqueous ammonia to obtain solid phase (B) containing silica and liquid phase (C), mixing liquid phase (C) and 10 %mass ammonium carbonate solution, precipitating to form magnesium carbonate and calcining to form magnesium oxide.
   USE - Alkali residue component utilization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and environmentally-friendly, and effectively utilize and recover all components of alkali residue and reduces energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The water washing process involves washing alkali residue with solid-liquid ratio of 1:2-5 for 2-5 times to obtain solid phase and filtrate, washing filtrate with 3-5 times until the each time density of the solution reaches 1.25 g/mL or more, concentrating and crystallizing. Preferred Condition: The heat treatment is carried out at 850 degrees C for 1 hour. The leaching (L1) time is 5-20 minutes. The volume of obtained aqueous ammonia after distillation is 20-80 mL. The precipitation temperature is 65 plus minus 3 degrees C. The calcination is carried out at 600-1500 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Component: The dispersant is sodium hexametaphosphate. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of calcium oxide in calcined powder and ammonium chloride in ammonium chloride solution is 1:2-3. The mass ratio of calcined powder and water in ammonium chloride solution is 1:5-20. The amount of dispersant added to liquid phase (B) is 0-3 %mass. The mass ratio of solid precipitate and ammonium sulfate solution is 1:15-25.

PN CN111097407-A
TI Preparing platinum/aluminum oxide catalyst by dissolving hexachloroplatinic acid hexahydrate and triethylamine in propylene carbonate, reducing temperature, stirring, impregnating aluminum oxide, stirring and adsorbing, drying and calcining.
AU YAN X
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202040431G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a platinum/aluminum oxide catalyst involves (i) dissolving hexachloroplatinic acid hexahydrate and triethylamine in propylene carbonate, reducing temperature to 10-40 degrees C under a hydrogen pressure of 1-4 MPa and stirring and reacting for 0.5-6 h to prepare a platinum nano-sol, where the ratio of the amount of hexachloroplatinic acid hexahydrate to triethylamine is 1:(0-20) and (ii) directly impregnating aluminum oxide in the platinum nano-sol, so that the theoretical load of platinum is 0.1 wt.%, stirring and adsorbing, leaving still and drying and calcining for 4 hours at 350-400 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a platinum/aluminum oxide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can obtain supported nano platinum/aluminum oxide catalyst with high dispersion degree which has particle size distribution of the platinum nanoparticles of 2-3 nm, a relatively narrow particle size distribution, small platinum nanoparticles with higher catalytic activity and no agglomeration. The catalyst completely converts toluene into water and carbon dioxide at 220 degrees C under low amount of noble metal loading (0.1% loading) without other by-products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adding 1-10 ml/mg, preferably 3.3 ml/mg propylene carbonate based on the mass of platinum in hexachloroplatinic acid hexahydrate. The step (i) involves adopting the hydrogen pressure of 4 MPa, where the time of the reduction reaction is 3 hours and the reduction temperature is 10 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110921830-A
TI Treating rural domestic sewage involves introducing sewage into water tank, performing rough filtration on sewage to remove large particles of debris, crushing large particles of debris, and then pouring into fermentation tank.
AU SHEN Y
   LV W
   SHEN G
AE ZHEJIANG TONGCHENG ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOG (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202025916A
AB    NOVELTY - Treating rural domestic sewage involves introducing sewage into a water tank, performing rough filtration on the sewage to remove large particles of debris, crushing the large particles of debris, and then pouring into the fermentation tank, crushing and transporting the large particles of debris in the sewage to avoid residues, vacuum the fermentation tank with an air pump, or filing with carbon dioxide, and adding anaerobic fermentation degradation agent to the organic matter in the sewage to degrade it, using air pump to continuously fill the sewage with fresh air, and introducing aerobic fermentation degrading agent to degrade the organic matter in the sewage, exporting and analyzing the sewage in the fermentation tank, and then after the comparison, exporting the sewage, exporting and analyzing the gas produced by fermentation in the fermentation tank, and after comparison, exporting the gas.
   USE - Method for treating rural domestic sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat rural domestic sewage effectively converts the solid-liquid separation, promotes the speed of internal fermentation, improves the recycling rate of sewage processing in cost-effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condiitons: The large particles of debris are further deodorized before being crushed, and the deodorization process is carried out per cubic meter, added 10-20 grams of deodorant to the rice material and mixed for 5-10 days. The deodorant is a microbial carrier loaded with mixed bacteria of denitrifying microbial flora and EM flora, and the microbial carrier is vermiculite, sawdust and clay are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:2-5:0.1-0.5, then added with aluminum-chromium phosphate binder and pressed into granules. Preferred Process: The organic matter is degraded by anaerobic fermentation, the pH needs to be adjusted, the pH is adjusted between 6-7, the conductivity EC value is 3.0 ms. The anaerobic fermentation is degraded, the fermentation tank is provided with biological fillers and high-efficiency microorganisms, and the suspended sludge is attached to the fillers during the ascent On the surface, microorganisms grow and propagate on the filler, further improved the treatment efficiency. The biological filler is one or more of four types of river bottom mud, soil, biomass ash, and commercially available ash. The high-efficiency microorganisms are anaerobic microorganisms are yeasts and actinomycetes. The aerobic fermentation is degraded, a foaming agent and a fertilizer starter are added to the fermentation tank. The foaming agent includes 3-5 pts. wt. No. 2 oil, 1-3 pts. wt. isobutyl sodium xanthate, and 1-3 pts. wt. blooming. The fertilizer starter is prepared by weighing 10-80 pts. wt. fermentation industry waste residues, 5-30 pts. wt. straw, 5-30 pts. wt. cake residue, 10-30 pts. wt. bran or rice bran, 1.5-2 pts. wt. urea, 0.5-1 sodium chloride, 1-30 pts. wt. glucose and / or 1 to 30 servings of , 1-5 pts. wt. strains of each of Azotobacter chroococcum, Bacillus megaterium, Bacillus licheniformis, Lactobacillus plantarum and Saccharomycopsis fibuligera, mixing materials except the microbial strains in the above raw materials, sterilize them, adding water until the water content of the materials is 40-60 wt.%. The microbial strains in the raw material formula are fermented in aeration, stirred during the fermentation process, naturally heated, and fermented at 30-40 degrees C for 1-3 days to obtain a fertilizer starter. The method involves shortening or crushing straw to a length of l-2 cm, and the method of sterilization is steam sterilize, added water until the moisture content of the material is 50%, dried the fertilizer starter obtained at 90-99 degrees C until the moisture content is below 20, and then bag and seal to store. The fertilizer starter is added with fumaric acid, hopol and ethyl palmitate, fumaric acid, hopol and palmitic acid, where the ratio of ethyl ester is 2:1:3, and the mixture of fumaric acid, hop alcohol and ethyl palmitate is 0.05 kg per unit kilogram of fertilizer.

TI Manufacture of porous carbon material support with vertically-aligned carbon nanotubes for e.g. electrode, involves depositing ceramic sublayer on support, and producing carbon nanotubes on support by catalytic chemical vapor deposition.
AU AUBERT P

   UNIV PARIS CY CERGY (UYPA-Non-standard)
   ATOMIC ENERGY & ALTERNATIVE ENERGY COMMI (COMS-C)
GA 2020175951
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of porous carbon material support with vertically-aligned carbon nanotubes involves depositing a ceramic sublayer on the support, and producing carbon nanotubes vertically aligned on the support by catalytic chemical vapor deposition by injecting a carbon source in a direction perpendicular to a plane of the support and at a pressure of less than 8.104 Pa (800 mbar).
   USE - Manufacture of porous carbon material support with vertically-aligned carbon nanotubes used for preparing electrode e.g. positive/negative electrode of device for storing and restoring electricity such as supercapacitor and battery, electrode for photovoltaic device, material for storage of carbon dioxide, and electrode for electrochemical sensor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces porous carbon material support with vertically-aligned carbon nanotubes which is capable of producing electrode having improved performance in terms of capacitance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of electrode, which involves providing the support, and electrochemically depositing a polymer matrix on the carbon nanotubes from an electrolytic solution comprising at least one precursor monomer of the matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The ceramic is an oxide ceramic, preferably silicon oxide of formula: SiOx (I).x = 0-2 (excluding 0).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carbon material support is in the form of carbon fibers or carbon foam. The carbon source is co-injected with a catalytic source. Preferred Process: Preparation of electrode further involves subjecting the vertically-aligned carbon nanotubes to an oxidizing treatment (or pretreatment) prior to electrochemical deposition, and rinsing and optionally drying. Electrochemical deposition is performed by cyclic method, pulsed or continuous galvanostatic method and/or pulsed or continuous potentiostatic method. Preferred Conditions: Injection of carbon source and catalytic source is carried out at a pressure 103 Pa (10 mbar) to 7.5x 104 Pa (750 mbar), preferably 3.104 Pa (300 mbar) to 6.104 Pa (600 mbar). Deposition of ceramic sublayer is carried out in a pre-treatment chamber. Catalytic chemical vapor deposition is carried out in a reaction chamber.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN WO2020018759-A1; US2021259992-A1
TI Nanoporous starch aerogel e.g. wheat starch aerogel used for e.g. treating or reducing inflammatory disease or disorder, is impregnated with curcumin.
AU CIFTCI O N
AE NUTECH VENTURES (NUTE-Non-standard)
   NUTECH VENTURES (NUTE-Non-standard)
GA 202009035G
AB    NOVELTY - A nanoporous starch aerogel is impregnated with curcumin.
   USE - Nanoporous starch aerogel is used for treating or reducing inflammatory disease or disorder chosen from cancer and inflammatory bowel disease in subject (all claimed). The nanoporous aerogel is also useful for treating diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, neurodegenerative diseases and gastrointestinal irritation, and for preparing high nutraceutical value food and wellness-improving supplement in the powder or capsule form.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoporous starch aerogel increases water solubility and bioavailability of the curcumin, preferably provide food grade curcumin with reduced crystallinity and bioaccessibility, thus making them available for preparation of high nutraceutical value food, and has enhanced efficacy in human health and cost-benefit ratio of water-insoluble bioactives, thus providing low-cost of handling, storage and transportation of bioactives, and can avert toxic chemicals and environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) formation of nanoporous starch aerogel impregnated with a low-crystallinity curcumin, which involves exchanging water in a starch hydrogel with ethanolic curcumin solution to form an alcogel, and supercritical carbon dioxide drying the alcogel; and
   (2) usage method of curcumin nanoparticles for reducing inflammation and treating inflammatory disease or disorder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethanolic curcumin solution comprises 30-100 (volume/volume)% ethanol, and has a curcumin concentration of 4.4 plus minus 0.2 mg/mL at room temperature and at 17.2 plus minus 0.3 mg/mL 60 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The starch hydrogel is produced by gelatinizing a starch at 80-140 degrees C to form a three-dimensional starch hydrogel. Solvent exchanging water in the starch hydrogel is performed by immersing the starch hydrogel in the ethanolic curcumin solution. Supercritical carbon dioxide drying of the alcogel is supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. Preferred Composition: The starch hydrogel comprises 10% starch e.g. wheat starch.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Cytostatic; Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Antidiabetic; Cardiovascular-Gen.; Neuroprotective. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PT P
PN CN110563015-A; CN110563015-B
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate for food, by carrying out digestive reaction between calcium oxide and water, acidifying calcium hydroxide pulp, carbonizing calcium hydroxide refined slurry, aging, and filtering calcium carbonate slurry.
AU XU K
   XU H
   DONG D
   DONG L
   HAN X
   CHEN Z
   XU Z
AE HEBEI LIXIN CHEM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6677L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate for food, involves (a) carrying out digestive reaction between calcium oxide and water, and aging to obtain calcium hydroxide pulp, (b) adding hydrochloric acid to the calcium hydroxide raw pulp to acidify, adding calcium hydroxide dispersion to adjust a pH to 11-12, precipitating, and filtering to obtain calcium hydroxide refined pulp, (c) purifying a kiln gas to obtain a reaction gas containing carbon dioxide, (d) adding the calcium hydroxide refined slurry to a reaction kettle, passing the reaction gas through a carbonization reaction under stirring conditions, when the pH of the reaction system is 8-9, adding an amino acid chelating agent and polycarboxylate, when the pH value of the reaction system is 7-7.5, and aging to obtain a calcium carbonate slurry, and (e) filtering the calcium carbonate slurry, washing, and drying to obtain the calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light calcium carbonate for food.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared light calcium carbonate for food meets the quality standard of GB 1886.214-2016.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves introducing the reaction gas from the bottom of the reaction kettle, and introducing 3-8 %mass amino acid chelating agent and 0.6-2 %mass sodium polycarboxylate to the reaction kettle through the gas flow of the reaction gas. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the temperature of water in the digestion reaction is 70-80 degrees C, and the digestion reaction time is 3-5 hours. The aging is carried out at 75-85 degrees C for 10-14 hours. In step (d), the pressure in the reaction kettle is 0.1-0.15 MPa, and the temperature is 30-35 degrees C, and the aging time is 4-6 hours. The stirring speed is 400-600 rpm. In step (e), the drying temperature is 120-150 degrees C, time is 3-4 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of calcium oxide to water is 1:(8-10). The concentration of the calcium hydroxide raw pulp is 15-20 %mass. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 4-6 mol/l. The molar ratio of the hydrochloric acid to the calcium oxide in the step (a) is 2.1-2.5. The concentration of the calcium hydroxide dispersion is 8-10 %mass. The reaction gas comprises 30-40 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amino acid chelating agent comprises aspartic acid, lysine, methionine or histidine.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

   USE - Fuel composition used as fuel for reducing polluting and/or toxic exhaust emissions containing particulate emissions, polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxides, increasing power gain and reducing consumption in internal combustion engine e.g. diesel engine, non-road motor engines, stationary engines and engines of industrial devices (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel composition effectively reduces emission of harmful substances from engines. The composition has excellent storage stability and cold resistance, and low sensitivity to oxidation and bacterial development.

The composition increases power gain compared to reference fuel by 0.4% or more, and reduces consumption as compared to reference fuel by 0.7% or more. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon fraction comprises a hydrocarbon compound having 9-17 carbon atoms, preferably 11-14 carbon atoms. The composition further contains anti-static additive(s).

AE WUHAN DIASCIE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5164T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a blood culture device. The blood culture bottle device comprises a culture bottle and a connector which is coaxially arranged with the culture bottle mouth. The connector is provided with a sampling channel along the central axis. The sampling channel is provided with a sealing support block, and the upper and lower ends of the sealing support block are provided with butyl rubber. A sealing chamber is formed between the upper and lower butyl rubber and the middle channel of the sealing support block, and a liquid medium is filled in the culture bottle. The connector and the culture bottle neck adopt concentric circle structure to solve the problem of air leakage that may occur when eccentric structure is used. The concentric circle structure can greatly improve the positioning accuracy of the manipulator in the process of seed transfer. Carbon dioxide induction and sampling are the same outlet, which simplifies the difficulty of mold making and improves the loading accuracy and reliability of connectors.

PN CN110526733-A
TI Preparing light ceramsite for backfilling soil comprises excavating the light ceramsite made of silt soil in underground space, fly ash and straw ash, spraying sodium aluminate solution and performing high temperature roasting.
AU GAO H
   XIA S
   ZHANG J
   GE X
   HAN A
   XU Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   NANJING JIANGBEI NEW AREA CENT DEV CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2983Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light ceramsite for backfilling soil comprises excavating the light ceramsite made of silt soil in underground space, fly ash and straw ash and made into pellets by a granulator, spraying sodium aluminate solution and weak organic acid solution, and performing two-stage high temperature roasting, where the mass ratio of the silt soil, fly ash, and straw ash is: 60-85%:12-30%:3-10%, and evenly mixing, and water-solid ratio is 5-20%, where the mass concentration of sodium aluminate solution and weak organic acid solution are both 1-10%, the first-stage calcining temperature is 400-600 degrees C for 30-300s, or the concentration of carbon dioxide decomposed by the combustion to 20-50% of the estimated total and the secondary calcining temperature is 1000-1150 degrees C for 5-20 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light ceramsite for backfilling soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramsite has low density and high strength, reduces load on underlying soil, reduces settlement and lateral earth pressure on retaining structure and enhances stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The moisture content of the silt soil is 29.7-42.8%, severity is 17.3-18.8 kN/m3, porosity is 0.936-1.198, plastic limit index is 7-15.3, liquid limit index is 0.82-3.05, compression coefficient at 100-200kPa is 0.29-14/MPa and the compression modulus is 2.97-7.9 MPa. The weak organic acid comprises citric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, citrate, hydroxy ethylidene diphosphonic acid, maleic acid, adipic acid and/or fumaric acid. The cementing material comprises cement, lime, gypsum and/or polymer. The single-point point load strength of the ceramsite made by the calcining is 4.8-5.4 MPa, the compressive coefficient of loose ceramic aggregate at 100-200 kPa is 0.134-0.148/MPa and the peak compressive strength of cemented ceramsite backfill soil with 10% cement addition is 0.75 MPa. The particle diameter of the ceramsite is 0-32 mm and the particle bulk density in the range of 0.3-1.2 g/cm3. Preferred Method: The straw ash is obtained by taking wheat, rice, corn, potato, cotton, sugarcane, oil crops after mature and threshing, adding into the carbonization equipment and performing centralized carbonization treatment, and decomposing into carbon and other products by heating at 650 degrees C in the absence of air. The method specifically comprises pre-preparation steps (i) taking the light ceramsite formed from light ceramsite, cementitious material and water in a mass ratio of 59-82%:6-40%:1-12% mixing and stirring to form cemented ceramsite backfill soil, (ii) mixing the silt soil generated during the excavation of the underground space, where the mass ratio of silt soil: fly ash: straw ash is 60-85%:12-30%: 3-10% and water-solid ratio is 5-20%, (iii) uniformly forming the slurry, extruding with a ball making machine, rolling and forming into a ball, and successively spraying a 1-10% sodium aluminate solution and weak organic acid solution, preheating with a multi-channel burner at a first-stage temperature of 400-600 degrees C for 30-300 seconds, or when the carbon dioxide concentration of the combustible decomposes to 20-50% of the estimated total, then performing the second-stage calcining at 1000-1150?OC for 5-20 minutes to obtain ceramsite, (iv) cooling the ceramsite with a rotary cooler, (v) taking a vibratory screening machine and screening the ceramsite, where the side of the vibrating square hole screen is 4 mm, 8 mm, 12 mm, 16 mm, 20 mm, 24 mm, 28 mm and 32 mm, sieving and storing in a warehouse, and (vi) if cemented ceramsite is required to backfill the soil, the prepared ceramsite, cementitious material and water are mass ratio of 59-82%, 6-40% and 1-12% mixing and stirring well. The construction steps are as follows (a) transporting the lightweight ceramsite or cemented ceramsite backfill soil prepared in the pre-preparation step into the backfill site by lorry or tanker, unloading the lorry directly, pouring the tanker by compressed air using air pressure, and the mixer is used to transport the cemented ceramsite backfill soil, and transporting into the backfill site before initial setting, (b) laying the drainage pipe facilities at the bottom of the base with a gradient of 3-6%, and taking a fine mesh filter to block the drainage facilities near the ceramsite side to avoid blockage of the pipeline, (c) laying a layer of geotextile on the pre-filled site, and filling the ceramsite backfill layer by layer, where the thickness of each layer is 1 m and the thickness of each layer of small projects and backfill retaining walls and abutments not greater than 0.6 m, (d) for each layer of filling, taking a roller compactor or a vibrating plate compactor to vibrate and compact at least 3 times, where the compaction pressure not greater than 50 kPa until the volume of ceramsite backfill soil is reduced to 10 wt.% or the height is reduced by 0.1 m, (e) after the ceramsite backfill is completed, and laying a layer of geotextile on top of the ceramsite backfill to isolate the upper cover, (f) depending on the thickness of the laying, and the top of the backfill soil layer can be anti-floated with stones, and (g) performing the construction of the base layer and structural layer of the upper pavement.

AU WANG X
   LENG Y
   ZHANG Y
   HU X
   ZHOU J
   WANG S
   LV Q
AE UNIV WUHAN POLYTECHNIC (UWHP-C)
GA 201995631F
AB    NOVELTY - A leisure snack product containing plant oil comprises 1100-1300 pts. wt. wheat flour, 40-60 pts. wt. short chain inulin, 16-20 pts. wt. hyssop oil embedded substance, 38-62 pts. wt. edible salt, 5-7 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 163.2-190.4 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 28.8-33.6 pts. wt. sesame oil, 24-28 pts. wt. Dasiphora davurica extract, and 330-390 pts. wt. water. The short-chain inulin is jerusalem artichoke extracted by water, filtered, concentrated in vacuum, and the precipitate obtained after evaporating of short chain inulin; the hyssop oil embedding substance is extracted by ethanol solution and supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extract, and mixed with gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution, filtered, and dried to obtain the embedded substance; and the Dasiphora davurica extract is mixed D. davurica leaf and cellulase hydrolysis, and the filtrate is concentrated in vacuum.
   USE - A leisure snack product containing plant oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The leisure snack product has reduced hardening degree during long-term storage, increased oil capacity, and is healthy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The leisure snack product containing plant oil comprises 1150-1250 pts. wt. wheat flour, 45-55 pts. wt. short chain inulin, 17-19 pts. wt. hyssop oil embedded substance, 44-56 pts. wt. edible salt, 5.5-6.5 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 170-183.6 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 30-32.4 pts. wt. sesame oil, 25-27 pts. wt. Dasiphora davurica extract, and 345-375 pts. wt. water. The descending value of wheat flour is 150-400, lactic acid solvent retention of wheat flour is 90-140%, and ash content of flour is 0.45-0.78%. The ratio of material to liquid of Jerusalem artichoke to water is 1 g:8-12 mL, Jerusalem artichoke and active carbon volume ratio is 1:1-2; and crude stevioside and water ratio is 1 g:6-10 mL. Ratio of the amount of hyssop oil with 45-55% ethanol is 1 g:30-40 ml. Weight ratio of cellulase is 100:0.08-0.12. Preferred Process: The short-chain inulin prepared by (1-1) mixing the Jerusalem artichoke extract, water and activated carbon at 70-90 degrees C in a sealed container, filtering, concentrating in vacuum to obtain crude stevioside; and (1-2) dissolving crude stevioside to water, adding excess 25-35%, removing the supernatant and drying the precipitate to obtain short-chain inulin. The hyssop oil embedding substance is prepared (2-1) mixing dried hyssop with 45-55% ethanol at 50-60 degrees C, extracting obtained hyssop oil with supercritical CO2 extraction, (2-2) using the micro-capsule technology, fully mixing hyssop oil and excess gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution, cooling, filtering and drying. The Dasiphora davurica extract is prepared by mixing D. davurica leaf with cellulase to hydrolyze at 75-85 degrees C for 90-110 minutes, filtering and taking the filtrate, and vacuum concentrating. The method includes (3-1) mixing embedded substance hyssop oil, short chain inulin, and wheat flour with brine, extruding and curing to obtain semi-finished product, (3-2) mixing semi-finished product and rapeseed oil, balm Dasiphora davurica extract where the brine is a mixture of edible salt, glycerine and water. Preferred Parameters: The pressure of supercritical CO2 extraction is 12-18 MPa, temperature is 35-45 degrees C, extracting time is 10-20 minutes. Hydrolyzing time is 50-70 minutes.

TI Producing diatomic microalgae used for extracting oil, by cultivating microalgae culture in vertical photobioreactor, circulating microalgae culture, harvesting microalgae, adding culture media, separating and draining microalgae biomass.
AU MELIANA Y
   DUNIANI Z
   PEBRIANI S
   PRAYITNO J
   RIFAI A
   HANIF M
   SANTOSO A D
   OKTIVIA R
   SUSANTI J P
AE BADAN PENGKAJIAN DAN PENERAPAN TEKNOLOGI (BADA-Non-standard)
GA 2020303293
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing diatomic microalgae involves (a) cultivating microalgae culture in at least one vertical photobioreactor which has an inlet at top of the column and an outlet at bottom of the column, (b) circulating microalgae culture in photobioreactor preventing deposition of biomass at bottom of the column by injecting air or carbon dioxide enriched air from the bottom of the column, and removing microalgae culture at bottom of the column and reinserting through the top of the column at intervals, (c) cultivating microalgae culture and harvesting the microalgae in the harvesting tank periodically, (d) adding culture media into the photobioreactor to keep the volume of microalgae culture in the photobioreactor steady after harvesting, (e) subjecting the microalgae culture in the harvest tank to gravity to obtain microalgae biomass deposited at bottom of the tank, and (f) draining the microalgae biomass that settles at the bottom of the harvest tank into a vessel.
   USE - The method is useful for producing diatomic microalgae which is used for extracting oil contained in diatom microalgae (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for extracting oil contained in diatom microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The flow of microalgae biomass that settles at bottom of the harvesting tank into the vessel is based on the principle of the related vessel due to the pressure caused by the difference in surface level of the microalgae culture in the harvest tank with the entrance of the biomass into these vessels. The harvesting is carried out every day after cultivating microalgae culture for 5 days. The method involves removing part of biomass from the harvesting tank through top of the tank at specified time intervals and releasing a part of the supernatant into the photobioreactor as additional culture media. The method further involves (g) introducing solvent in the extraction vessel, (h) breaking the microalgae cell wall with a probe-type ultrasonicator and by stirring to extract the oil contained in the microalgae, and (i) separating the oil contained in the extracted mixture from water, solvents and microalgae residues. Preferred Device: The photobioreactor is one or more arranged in parallel. The vessel is the extraction vessel. Preferred Composition: The microalgae biomass feed comprises 5% or more water.

PN CN109437195-A
TI Preparing bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon used in e.g. separation of mixed gases, involves processing bamboo, slowly drying bamboo block, controlling moisture content, carbonizing, reacting with superheated steam, cooling.
AU DU S
   LI W
   WU L
AE ZHEJIANG JIZHU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019249180
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon, involves (1) processing bamboo into small pieces of 5-50 mm, and maintaining the length of any side at not more than 50 mm, (2) slowly drying the bamboo block at a temperature of 50-150 degrees C, controlling the moisture content of the bamboo at 30% after drying, (3) carbonizing at 150-450 degrees C for 60-90 minutes under condition of insulating air, (4) transferring the charred bamboo block into the activation furnace, reacting bamboo block with superheated steam at 700-800 degrees C in an activation furnace for 90-110 minutes, (5) cooling, crushing and sieving the cooled bamboo block with particle size of 2.5-4.5 mm.
   USE - Preparing bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon used in separation of mixed gases or purification of gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon with high pressure swing adsorption activity, carbon dioxide adsorption amount of 65-80%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The bamboo is selected from bamboo poles, bamboo head or bamboo shoot.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon has loading density of 350-450 g/l, and intensity of above 96%. Preferred Composition: The bamboo pressure swing adsorption activated carbon has carbon dioxide adsorption amount of 65-80%, and ash of 5-10%.

PN CN109020002-A
TI Coal chemical demineralization treatment by subjecting pretreated water to secondary reverse osmosis treatment, filtering, subjecting to electrodeionization treatment, and returning back polar water and concentrated water to first step.
AU YUE P
   SUN S
AE SHANGHAI YUANMAI ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4628E
AB    NOVELTY - A coal chemical demineralization treatment comprises subjecting pretreated water to secondary reverse osmosis treatment until conductivity of produced water does not exceed 20 mu S/cm, filtering to remove carbon dioxide and total organic carbon until total carbon content is not greater than 2 mg/L, conductivity is 1-20 mu S/cm and pH is 6-8, subjecting to electrodeionization treatment at inlet water temperature of 25-35 degrees C and inlet water pressure of 0.15-0.7 MPa to obtain polar water, concentrated water having conductivity of less than 0.2 mu S/cm and high purity water having silica content of below 20 mu g/L and hardness close to zero, and returning back polar water and concentrated water to first step to adjust conductivity to less than 0.2 mu S/cm.
   USE - Method for coal chemical demineralization treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, does not consume acid and alkali, produces stable effluent water quality which meets the requirements of coal chemical boiler, and has no hazardous waste liquid discharge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The electrodeionization treatment is done at influent conductivity of 2-20 mu S/cm, pH of 6-8, inlet water temperature of 25-35 degrees C and pressure of 0.15-0.5 MPa. The filtration is done until calcium carbonate content is not greater than 1 ppm, total organic carbon content is not greater than 0.5 ppm, chloride content is not greater than 0.01 ppm, ozone content is not greater than 0.01 ppm, heavy metal content is not greater than 0.01 ppm, active silicon content is not greater than 0.2 ppm, carbon dioxide content is not greater than 2 ppm and oil-free.

PN CN108896703-A
TI Air data processing method, involves terminating content information processing operation when first time point is equal to second time point, and determining variable assignment value if first time point is not equal to second time point.
AU PING B
AE NANJING SIDAJIE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201897943L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves transmitting information of content to an air treatment device. Mapping relationship is established between the content information and a time point. Variable assignment process is performed in the air treatment device. A time point fitting value is determined. The time point fitting value is added with a standby fitting value to obtain an average value. A target point fitting value is determined. Time intervals between two time points are compared. Content information processing operation is terminated if the first time point is equal to the second time point. A variable assignment value is determined if the first time point is not equal to the second time point. The content is included with formaldehyde, carbon dioxide, proximate matter 2.5, ammonia, total volatile organic compounds and combustible gas.
   USE - Air data processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding message transmission difficulty between the air treatment device and a server, reducing time delay variation, time point fitting error and air data processing cost, and increasing exponential fitting accuracy.

PN CN108816193-A; CN108816193-B
TI Preparing super microporous molasses-based carbon material comprises e.g. uniformly mixing carbon precursor, molasses and catalyst, placing in hydrothermal kettle, hydrothermally reacting, placing in activation device and activating.
AU LI Z
   JIANG T
   ZHOU X
   XIA Q
   XIAO J
   WANG X
   LIANG Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018920363
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing super microporous molasses-based carbon material comprises (i) uniformly mixing carbon precursor, molasses, sodium tetraborate, dicyandiamide and catalyst, placing into hydrothermal reaction kettle, performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain carbon precursor, where the catalyst is a cleavage structure of dicyandiamide in the carbon precursor and the temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 190-210 degrees C, and (ii) mixing carbon precursor and activator, placing it in an activation device for stepwise temperature-programming activation and treating to obtain a super-high-microporous molasses-based carbon material, where the activation specifically performed at heating to 300-500 degrees C for heat activation, then heating to 500-690 degrees C and performing heat preservation and reactivation.
   USE - The material is useful in adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high proportion of ultra-microporous pore and under normal pressure, especially under low pressure conditions the high proportion of pore volume reaches 68.97%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing super microporous molasses-based carbon material comprising the steps mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii) the time of the heat preservation and activation is 1-3 hours, and the time of the heat-reactivation activation is 2-4 hours and in the step (i) the mass ratio of molasses to dicyandiamide is (6-10): (0.1-1). In the step (i) the hydrothermal reaction time is performed for 10-14 hours and in the step (ii) the mass ratio of carbon precursor to activator is 1: (1-4). In the step (ii), heating to 300-500 degrees C, at the rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute and heating at rate of 2.5-7 degrees C/minutes and in the step (i) the mass ratio of molasses to water is (6-10)g: (30-50) ml. The step (ii) further comprises pickling the activated product, washing with many times with water and drying. Preferred Components: In the step (i) the mass ratio of the dicyandiamide to sodium tetraborate is (0.1-1): (0.1-1) and the mass ratio of the molasses to catalyst is (6-10): (0.01-0.04). In the step (i) the catalyst is nickel nitrate, cobalt nitrate and/or zinc nitrate and in the step (ii) the activator is potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, zinc chloride, and phosphoric acid.

TI Preparing sparkling wine useful for nourishing stomach by drying medlar, red dates and Chrysanthemum, crushing, mixing, soaking in water, extracting material, centrifuging, inoculating yeast, fermenting material and filtering product.
AU QIAO Z
   DONG S
   LI T
   SUN Y
   MA Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 201891889E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sparkling wine involves (a) drying medlar, red dates and Chrysanthemum, crushing, mixing uniformly, adding water to soak mixed powder, mixing and extracting material and (b) centrifuging extract to remove residue, adding white sugar to adjust sugar content and citric acid to adjust acidity, inoculating yeast, fermenting at constant temperature fermentation until the fermentation broth is clarified, the bacteria accumulate in bottom of tank and the residual sugar amount reaches to 6-8 g/l, lowering the temperature to 4-5 degrees C every 12 hours and fermenting for more than 30 days and filtering the fermented material to remove yeast and bacteria.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sparkling wine, which is useful for nourishing stomach, regulating blood fat, promoting body fluid and memory and resisting fatigue.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared sparkling wine is rich in nutrients and has an alcohol content of 3.56-4.67 vol.%, residual sugar content of 4.23-5.31 g/l, total acid content of 2.24-3.17 g/l, and carbon dioxide content of 0.12-0.14 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of dried medlar, red dates and Chrysanthemum is 70-80:10-20:5-10. The amount of white sugar is 68-85 g/l, and citric acid is 5.2-7.2 g/l. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves selecting dried sorghum, dried red dates, and dried Chrysanthemum, removing moldy fruit, black fruit and incomplete grains with more than 50% damage, crushing using vibrating drug superfine pulverizer, adding 8-10 times water to mixed powder, soaking for 10-12 hours, extracting at 60-65 degrees C for 24-26 hours and extracting at 100 degrees C for 25-35 minutes. The step (b) involves hermetically cooling the fermenter to 25-30 degrees C, when the yeast is inoculated, inoculating 2-2.5% activated yeast, fermenting at 16-18 degrees C and 0.12-0.14 MPa for 7-9 days, providing sufficient oxygen for 24-48 hours and performing anaerobic fermentation, filtering mixture through freezing filter having a pore size of 0.45 mu m, filling and storing at 4 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Antilipemic; Nootropic; CNS-Gen.; Muscular-Gen.

GA 201892059U

   USE - The facial mask is useful for whitening and beautifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask has stable quality, and good absorption effect; and is suitable for hemiplegia patient. The method: is simple and suitable for large scale production; and utilizes widely available raw material.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The nettle essential oil is obtained by steam distillation or supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction. The bat dung is obtained by collecting bat dung with gauze, washing until there is no visible impurity, and grinding. The rhizoma gastrodiae capsule is prepared by (i) providing rhizoma gastrodiae decoction, adding acrylic resin and water having weight 2-4 times of rhizoma gastrodiae decoction, heating, refluxing for 2-4 hours, filtering, extracting for 1-3 times, combining filtrate, concentrating filtrate to a relative density of 1.1-1.2 (at 50-60 degrees C) to obtain thick paste, drying into dry paste, and crushing by 80 meshes sieve, and (ii) mixing crushed dry paste with diluents, adding lactate ethanol solution, granulating, drying, and filling in capsule. The amount of acrylic resin is 0.5-1.5% weight of rhizoma gastrodiae decoction. The diluent comprises starch, acrylic resin and microcrystalline cellulose in the mass ratio of 1:0.5:1. The mass ratio of the acrylic resin to the rhizoma gastrodiae dry powder is 1:20-40. The lactate ethanol solution comprises lactic acid, ethanol, and water in the ratio of 2:60:38.

TI Preparation of spherical cluster-shaped light            calcium carbonate involves carrying out digestion            reaction of quicklime and water to obtain lime slurry,            aging, sieving and diluting lime slurry to obtain            calcium hydroxide suspension.
AU MAN Z
   ZHU Y
   MO G
   LI Y
   YANG A
AE GUANGXI HESHAN HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018927532
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of spherical cluster-shaped                light calcium carbonate involves calcining                quicklime at 1000-1300&#176; C for 3-6 hours,                carrying out digestion reaction of quicklime and                water to obtain lime slurry, aging, passing through                150 mesh sieve, diluting lime slurry to solid                content of 10-13% to obtain suspension of calcium                hydroxide, adding crystal form regulator (I) and                crystal form regulator (II) to the calcium                hydroxide suspension, stirring and mixing uniformly                to obtain mixed suspension, introducing carbon                dioxide gas into mixed suspension for carbonation                reaction, and stopping reaction when the pH of                slurry is 7.0-7.5, dehydrating the slurry after                carbonation reaction, drying, pulverizing, and                sieving to obtain spherical cluster calcium                carbonate product. The crystal form regulator (I)                is triethanolamine lauryl phosphate, dodecyl                phosphate and cetyl glucoside. The crystal form                regulator (II) is sodium N-lauroylamine, sodium                N-lauroylglutamate, and polysorbate.
   USE - Preparation of spherical cluster-shaped light                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of spherical                cluster-shaped light calcium carbonate with uniform                morphology, high dispersibility, and wide                application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  quicklime and water in digestion reaction is                  1:(6-8). The mass ratio of crystal form regulator                  (I) and the crystal form regulator (II) is 2:1.                  The crystal form regulator (I) and the crystal                  form regulator (II) are formulated into a                  solution having a mass concentration of 5%. The                  total amount of two crystal form regulator                  solutions is 1-5% of mass of calcium hydroxide                  suspension. The introduced amount of carbon                  dioxide is 0.6-0.8 l/minute. Preferred                  Conditions: The digestion reaction speed is                  100-150 rpm at room temperature, and the aging                  time is &#8805; 24 hours. The stirring and                  mixing speed is 500-800 r/min and the stirring                  and mixing time is 20-30 minutes. The carbonation                  reaction temperature is 30-50&#176; C and                  speed is 500-800 rpm.

   ADVANTAGE - The milk beverage has improved flavor, reduces dosage of skim milk powder, reduces sedimentation condition of product, increases stability of product, contains whey fermented concentrated liquid abundant with micropeptides and amino acid which can be quickly digested and absorbed by human body, and supplements motion energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the milk beverage comprising adding skim milk, white granulated sugar and soluble soybean polysaccharide to water at 40-50 degrees C, dissolving, cooling to less than 20 degrees C, adding whey fermented concentrated liquid and pure fruit juice, sterilizing and cooling to 4-5 degrees C, filling carbon dioxide and performing reperfusion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Lactobacillus is Lactobacillus bulgaricus, Streptococcus thermophilus, Lactobacillus helveticus and/or Lactobacillus paracasei.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The milk beverage comprises 85 pts. wt. water, 0.1 pt. wt. whey fermentation concentrated liquid, 1 pt. wt. skim milk powder, 3 pts. wt. high-fructose syrup, 6 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 0.5 pts. wt. pure fruit juice, and 0.2 pt. wt. soluble soybean polysaccharide. The whey fermented concentrated liquid comprises 40-60 pts. wt., preferably 50 pts. wt. water; 10-25 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. whey protein powder; 0.001-0.05 pt. wt., preferably 0.001 pt. wt. Lactobacillus; 20-40 pts. wt., preferably 34 pts. wt. sweet substance; 0.1-0.6 pt. wt., preferably 0.3 pt. wt. food stabilizer; and 0.1-0.2 pt. wt., preferably 0.2 pt. wt. essence, prepared by adding whey protein into water, stirring at 45-55 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, adjusting pH to 6.5-7.5, adding neutral protease, hydrolyzing for 2-3 hours to hydrolysis rate of 2-10%, pasteurizing, cooling to 37 degrees C, adding Lactobacillus, fermenting at 30-40 degrees C for 15-60 hours until pH is 3.7-4.0, adding sweet substance, food stabilizer and essence, mixing at 100-120 degrees C, ultra-high temperature sterilizing for 5-15 seconds, cooling to 85-90 degrees C, hot-filling, cooling to room temperature and clarifying by ceramic membrane. The whey is concentrated whey, demineralized whey and/or whey isolated protein powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sweet substance is white granulated sugar, high-fructose syrup and/or concentrated fruit juice. The food stabilizer is sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, xanthan gum, gellan gum, pectin and/or soybean polysaccharide.
   ACTIVITY - Hypotensive.

   USE - Manufacture of electrode used as                oxygen-evolving electrocatalyst in water splitting                method (all claimed) for producing hydrogen fuel,                and oxygen in hospital and spacecraft. Can also be                used in solar water splitting, metal-air batteries,                and carbon dioxide reduction for producing carbon                fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides electrode with                high intrinsic catalytic activity and                electrochemical active surface area, improved                current density, excellent electroconductivity and                electrochemical durability in alkaline electrolyte,                and efficient charge transfer, and is suitable for                large scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) electrode, which comprises the substrate                and the metal-based phosphate catalyst; and
   (2) method of electrocatalytic water                splitting, which involves uniformly distributing                the metal-based phosphate catalyst on the                conductive substrate to form oxygen-evolving                electrocatalyst, and utilizing the oxygen-evolving                electrocatalyst as the electrode for oxygen                evolution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phosphorus source                  is chosen from sodium hypophosphite monohydrate,                  ammonium hypophosphite, phosphorus, and other                  phosphorus-containing materials. The metal                  nitrate is chosen from iron nitrate, cobalt                  nitrate, nickel nitrate, manganese nitrate, and                  copper nitrate, preferably iron(III) nitrate. The                  method uses a metal nitrate and/or a metal                  nitrate precursor, for forming the metal nitrate                  solution. The metal nitrate or metal nitrate                  precursor comprises iron(III) nitrate                  nonahydrate, iron(II) nitrate, cobalt nitrate,                  nickel nitrate, manganese nitrate, and copper                  nitrate. The metal-based phosphate catalyst                  comprises ferrous metaphosphate, cobalt                  metaphosphate, nickel metaphosphate and/or                  manganous metaphosphate, preferably ferrous                  metaphosphate. The electrode comprises ferrous                  metaphosphate/nickel phosphide/nickel foam. The                  substrate comprises one or more metallic foams or                  carbon cloth papers. The metallic foam comprises                  nickel, copper, iron, cobalt, and/or titanium,                  preferably nickel. Preferred Composition: The                  metal-based phosphate catalyst is loaded in an                  amount of 2-15 mg/cm2. Preferred Properties: When                  electrocatalytic water splitting method is                  operated in 1M alkaline solution, the                  oxygen-evolving electrocatalyst yields a current                  density of at least 300 mA/cm2 at an                  overpotential of 265 mV or less, exhibits a                  durability for at least 10000 cycles, and/or is                  operable for at least 20 hours at 300                  mA/cm2.

PN CN108358223-A
TI Treating of boron-containing magnesium residue, involves preparing magnesium residue, performing solid-liquid separation, pyrolyzing heavy magnesium water, filtering, calcining, adjusting pH of boron-containing filtrate, cooling, drying.
AU LIU D
   ZHANG S
   LI L
AE ZIBO MEISHENG CHEM CO LTD (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 201863792L
AB    NOVELTY - The method for treating boron-containing magnesium residue, involves (1) preparing magnesium residue as slurry, introducing process gas-containing carbon dioxide, performing carbonization reaction, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and heavy magnesium water, (2) pyrolyzing heavy magnesium water to obtain magnesium carbonate slurry, filtering to obtain filter cake (I) and filtrate (I), calcining filter cake (I) to obtain light magnesium oxide, (3) adding strong inorganic acid to filter residue until the pH is 4-6, maintaining temperature at 50-70 degrees C to obtain liquid, adjusting the boric acid content in the liquid to 130-150 g/L, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain boron-containing filtrate, (4) adjusting the pH of boron-containing filtrate to 1-2, cooling, filtering to obtain mother liquor and filter cake (II) and drying filter cake (II) to obtain boric acid product.
   USE - Method for treating boron-containing magnesium residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple treatment of boron-containing magnesium residue with low energy consumption, high separation of boron and magnesium, low equipment requirements, small investment and high returns.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The magnesium residue in step (1) is prepared using filtrate obtained in step (2). The process gas adopts gas generated by calcining in step (2). The content of boric acid in step (3) is adjusted using the mother liquid in the step (4). The solid-liquid separation in step (1) is carried out using a filter screen with air permeability of 18 L/m2.second. The step (S3) and step (S4) involves adjusting pH using hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or nitric acid. In step (S3), the solid-liquid separation is hot filtration using a plate and frame filter press or a centrifuge. Preferred Composition: The content of magnesium hydroxide in the slurry in step (1) is 1-2 wt.%. The volume content of carbon dioxide in the process gas is greater than or equal to 30 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: The step (1) comprises carbonizing at 15-35 degrees C and 0.08-0.55 MPa for 1.5-3 hours. The pyrolyzing is carried out at 30-40 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The step (S2) comprises calcining at 880-950 degrees C. The step (S2) comprises drying filter cake (I) at 250-300 degrees C for 0.8-1.2 hours to obtain basic magnesium carbonate.

   USE - The hybrid mesoporous silica gel is useful as                adsorbent material for selectively adsorbing                rubidium ion and/or caesium ion from the salt lake                brine (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing hybrid mesoporous silica gel, comprising                (1) adding Prussian blue and/or analogues and                tetraethyl orthosilicate sol and/or suspension and                tetraethyl orthosilicate miscible in water-soluble                organic solvent, adding strong acid, stirring the                reaction solution under the protection of inert                gas, heating to 50-90 degrees C to react for 1-5                hours, (2) adding porogen into the step (1)                reaction solution, stirring uniformly, adjusting                the pH value to neutral, allowing to stand for                producing silica gel, and (3) lyophilizing the                silica gel produced in step (2) or drying it with                supercritical carbon dioxide, washing to remove the                porogen, and drying again to obtain the hybrid                mesoporous silica gel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Prussian blue                  analog is a transition metal ferrocyanide,                  preferably copper potassium ferrocyanide, zinc                  potassium ferrocyanide, cobalt-potassium                  ferrocyanide and/or nickel potassium                  ferrocyanide. The Prussian blue and/or its analog                  nanoparticles particle diameter is 1-100 nm,                  preferably 1-20 nm. The water-soluble organic                  solvent is ethanol, methanol, acetone,                  acetonitrile, and 1,4-dioxane, the strong acid is                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or nitric acid,                  and the inert gas is nitrogen or argon. The                  transition metal salt is iron salt, copper salt,                  cobalt salt, nickel salt or zinc salt. The volume                  ratio of Prussian blue and/or its analog sol to                  tetraethyl orthosilicate in the step (1) is                  7:10-7:30, and the amount of strong acid added                  control the molar ratio of hydrogen ions to                  tetraethyl orthosilicate in the range of                  1:100-1:500. The porogen is                  N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, polyol                  or water soluble polymer, where the polyol is                  ethylene glycol or glycerol, the water soluble                  high polymer is polyvinyl alcohol, poly                  N-vinylpyrrolidone or polyethylene glycol. The                  mass ratio of the porogen to tetraethyl                  orthosilicate in the step (2) is 1:1 and 1:10. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises adding 4-carboxybenzotriazole and tetraethylene pentamine into xylene, stirring the reaction at 70-80 degrees C for 5-10 minutes under microwave conditions, then adding long carbon chain alkene carboxylic acid, refluxing the reaction at 120-140 degrees C for 30-40 minutes and then heating to 160-180 degrees C for 20-30 minutes under reduced pressure, cooling, removing xylene by rotary evaporation to obtain final product; where the long carbon chain alkene carboxylic acid is of formula (R-C(=O)OH). Preferred Components: The molar ratio between 4-carboxybenzotriazole, tetraethylenepentamine and long carbon chain alkene carboxylic acid is 1:2-2.5:1. Preferred Method: : The microwave power is 100-600 W.
   Preferred Definitions:

AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas and utilizing coupled liquefied natural gas (LNG) decompression cycle comprises exchanging heat with carbon dioxide rich carbon dioxide capture solution in carbon dioxide analysis module, absorbing heat, resolving carbon dioxide to obtain carbon dioxide poor capture solution, analyzing absorbed carbon dioxide at second preset temperature, refining carbon dioxide in first intercooler which has been gasified with LNG to obtain dry ice, depressurizing compressed natural gas, cooling carbon dioxide capture liquid with poor carbon dioxide in second intercooler after the compressed natural gas is depressurized and cooled, mixing raw flue gas with carbon dioxide capture liquid carbon in dioxide capture module and absorbing carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas and utilizing coupled liquefied natural gas decompression cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system comprising carbon dioxide analysis module, carbon dioxide capture module, first intercooling device and second intercooling device. The flue gas outlet of carbon dioxide analysis module is connected with the flue gas inlet of carbon dioxide capture module. The carbon dioxide outlet of carbon dioxide analysis module is connected with first intercooler. The gas outlet of first intercooler is connected with the gas inlet of second intercooler. The liquid outlet of carbon dioxide analysis module is connected with liquid inlet of second intercooler. The liquid outlet of second intercooler is connected with the liquid inlet of carbon dioxide capture module.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide capture solution is methyl diethanolamine (MDEA), ethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), diisopropylamine (DIPA), MDEA modified by carboxyl and/or carbonyl, MEA modified by carboxyl and/or carbonyl, DEA modified by carboxyl and/or carbonyl, DIPA modified by carboxyl and/or carbonyl. The mass concentration range of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide rich carbon dioxide capture solution is 4-6 wt.%. The mass concentration range of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide poor carbon dioxide capture solution is 0.02-0.5%.

TI Carbon dioxide separation involves bringing slurry comprising carbon dioxide solid absorbent and dispersion medium into contact with gas containing carbon dioxide to absorb carbon dioxide.
AU HARA Y
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 202037598R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide separation involves bringing slurry comprising carbon dioxide solid absorbent and dispersion medium into contact with a gas containing carbon dioxide to absorb the carbon dioxide, where the slurry is heated to desorb the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for separating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates the carbon dioxide in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner by releasing carbon dioxide at low temperatures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide solid absorbent is a polyethylene polyamine supported on a solid carrier. The solid carrier is selected from silica, alumina, titania, zirconia, silica-alumina, zeolite, clay, coal ash, and activated carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersion medium is water or an organic substance. The organic substances are alcohols such as methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol, ethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, glycerin, monoethanolamine, N-alkylated monoethanolamine, N,N-dialkylated monoethanolamine or diethanolamine. The N-alkylated diethanolamine is selected from amines such as triethanolamine, ethers such as ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, methoxyethanol, and tetrahydrofuran.

PN US10464872-B1; WO2020028067-A1; CN112888767-A
TI Generating methanol synthesis feed gas from non-gaseous carbonaceous material and hydromethanation catalyst, comprises e.g. reacting feedstock in reactor to give by-product char and methane-raw product gas, and withdrawing stream of char.
AU SIRDESHPANDE A
   ROBINSON E
   RAMAN P K
AE GREATPOINT ENERGY INC (GRAT-C)
   SURE CHAMPION INVESTMENT LTD (SURE-Non-standard)
GA 2019936181
AB    NOVELTY - Generating methanol synthesis feed gas from non-gaseous carbonaceous material and hydromethanation catalyst, comprises: (a) preparing carbonaceous feedstock from the material; (b) introducing the feedstock, hydromethanation catalyst, high-pressure, superheated steam, oxygen into reactor; (c) reacting the feedstock in reactor to give by-product char and methane-enriched raw product gas; (d) withdrawing stream of by-product char from reactor as by-product char stream; (e) (1) removing portion of entrained fines from methane-enriched raw product gas to form recovered primary fines stream and fines-depleted methane-enriched raw product gas stream and (2) removing residual entrained fines from gas stream to form recovered secondary fines stream and fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas; (f) cooling product gas to give steam and cooled stream; (g) removing particulates from cooled stream to generate particle-depleted cooled gas; and (h) generating methanol synthesis feed gas.
   USE - The process is useful for generating methanol synthesis feed gas from non-gaseous carbonaceous material and hydromethanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: combines catalytic gasification with an autothermal reformer unit to produce a methanol synthesis feed gas with an optimal module number for methanol synthesis feed gas production and produces products e.g. methanol and dimethyl ether from the methanol synthesis feed gas; and reduces oxygen and byproduct carbon dioxide and increases methanol yield per unit mass of solid carbonaceous feedstock as compared to conventional oxygen blown gasification methods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating a methanol synthesis feed gas from a non-gaseous carbonaceous material and a hydromethanation catalyst, comprises: (a) preparing a carbonaceous feedstock from the non-gaseous carbonaceous material; (b) introducing the carbonaceous feedstock, a hydromethanation catalyst, high-pressure, superheated steam, oxygen into a hydromethanation reactor; (c) reacting the carbonaceous feedstock in the hydromethanation reactor at 800-1500 degrees F (427-816 degrees C) and an operating pressure of at least 250 psig (1825 kPa), to produce a by-product char and a methane-enriched raw product gas comprising methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, steam, ammonia, heat energy and entrained fines; (d) withdrawing a stream of the by-product char from the hydromethanation reactor as the by-product char stream, where the by-product char stream comprises a carbon content and entrained hydromethanation catalyst; (e) (1) removing a substantial portion of the entrained fines from the methane-enriched raw product gas to form a recovered primary fines stream and a fines-depleted methane-enriched raw product gas stream comprising methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide, steam, ammonia, heat energy, and residual entrained fines, and (2) removing residual entrained fines from the fines-depleted methane-enriched raw product gas stream to form a recovered secondary fines stream and a fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas; (f) cooling the fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas to generate steam and a cooled stream; (g) removing additional particulates from the cooled stream to generate a particle-depleted cooled gas; and (h) generating the methanol synthesis feed gas having a module number of 1.75-2 by (i) using autothermal reforming by the steps of (A) further cooling the particle-depleted cooled gas to generate steam, then recovering the ammonia present in the cooled effluent in an ammonia recovery system to generate an ammonia-depleted effluent, (B1) removing a substantial portion of the carbon dioxide and a substantial portion of the hydrogen sulfide from the ammonia-depleted effluent in an acid gas removal unit to produce a sweetened gas, where the sweetened gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane, and has greater than 25% methane on a dry, carbon dioxide-free basis, (C1) mixing the sweetened gas with high-pressure, superheated steam and heating the mixture to produce a product stream, (D1) mixing the product stream with oxygen and feeding the mixture into an autothermal reformer unit to form an effluent gas, and (E1) cooling the effluent gas to generate steam and a methanol synthesis feed gas, where the methanol synthesis feed gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, or (ii) using cryogenic separation and co-producing substitute natural gas by the steps of (A1) forming a hydrogen-enriched raw product gas by (I) introducing a portion of the particle-depleted cooled gas into a water-gas shift reactor and steam shifting at least a portion of the carbon monoxide to generate an effluent gas and (II) combining the remaining portion of the particle-depleted cooled gas with the effluent gas generated by steam shifting to form hydrogen-enriched raw product gas, where the part A1 is carried out only when the module number of the methanol synthesis feed gas produced in part E is less than 1.75, (B2) cooling the particle-depleted cooled gas, or the hydrogen-enriched raw product gas, if present, to generate steam and a cooled effluent, (C2) recovering the ammonia present in the cooled effluent in an ammonia recovery system to generate an ammonia-depleted effluent, (D2) removing a substantial portion of the carbon dioxide and a substantial portion of the hydrogen sulfide from the ammonia-depleted effluent in acid gas removal unit to produce a sweetened gas and (E2) feeding the sweetened gas into a cryogenic separator and separating the gas into a substitute natural gas stream and a methanol synthesis feed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydromethanation catalyst comprises an alkali metal. The alkali metal is potassium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises: generating methanol from the methanol synthesis feed gas; treating all or a portion of the by-product char stream in a catalyst recovery unit comprising a quench tank and a quench medium, the treatment comprising quenching the by-product char stream with the quench medium to extract a portion of the entrained catalyst to generate a carbon- and catalyst-depleted char and liberated hydromethanation catalyst, withdrawing a stream of carbon- and catalyst-depleted char from the catalyst recovery unit as the carbon- and catalyst-depleted char stream and withdrawing a stream of liberated hydromethanation catalyst from the catalyst recovery unit as the recovered hydromethanation catalyst stream; feeding at least a portion of the recovered secondary fines stream to the catalyst recovery unit; converting the methanol synthesis feed gas to dimethyl ether; and converting the methanol to dimethyl ether. The process has a steam demand and a power demand that are met by internal energy integration so that the process requires no net import of steam or power. The hydromethanation catalyst comprises at least a portion of the recovered hydromethanation catalyst stream. The methanol synthesis feed gas is generated by the autothermal reforming process of step (h), part (i). The product stream produced in step (h), part (i), has a temperature of at least 1000 degrees F (538 degrees C). The methanol synthesis feed gas is generated by the cryogenic separation and co-production process of step (h), part (ii).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
