PN JP2019133938-A; JP6739563-B2
TI Fuel cell system comprises fuel cell that produces electric power using fuel gas or anode off gas, and separation membrane that separates carbon dioxide and water vapor from anode off gas having unreacted fuel gas discharged from fuel cell.
AU TAKU S
   NAKAMURA K
   DOKO T
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 201968654H
AB    NOVELTY - A fuel cell system (10) comprises 1st fuel cell (11) which produces electric power using fuel gas, separation membrane (16) which separates greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O) from anode off gas containing unreacted fuel gas discharged from 1st fuel cell, 2nd fuel cell (12) which produces electric power using anode off gas from which greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O is separated, source gas used as fuel gas used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, cathode gas containing oxygen used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, anode off gas discharged from 2nd fuel cell and 1st fuel cell, and distribution channel which distributes cathode off gas discharged from cathode off gas or 2nd fuel cell supplied to 2nd fuel cell. Permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )1(PCO2/PH2)) of separation membrane and permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )2(PH2O/PH2)) of separation membrane are greater than or equal to 30.
   USE - A fuel cell system.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell system is excellent in system-wide generating efficiency by making the ratio of greater than or equal to 1 permeability of carbon dioxide in separation membrane and water vapor, and permeability of hydrogen into appropriate range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fuel cell system (10) comprises 1st fuel cell (11) which produces electric power using fuel gas, separation membrane (16) which separates greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O) from anode off gas containing unreacted fuel gas discharged from 1st fuel cell, 2nd fuel cell (12) which produces electric power using anode off gas from which greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O is separated, source gas used as fuel gas used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, cathode gas containing oxygen used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, anode off gas discharged from 2nd fuel cell and 1st fuel cell, and distribution channel which distributes cathode off gas discharged from cathode off gas or 2nd fuel cell supplied to 2nd fuel cell. Permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )1(PCO2/PH2)) of separation membrane and permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )2(PH2O/PH2)) of separation membrane are greater than or equal to 30. Generating efficiency improvement effect of the system is greatly affected by greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O permeating the separation membrane than generating efficiency fall of system by hydrogen permeating the separation membrane. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) fuel cell system comprising 1st fuel cell which produces electric power using fuel gas, separation membrane which separates greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O) from anode off gas containing unreacted fuel gas discharged from 1st fuel cell, 2nd fuel cell which produces electric power using anode off gas from which greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O is separated, source gas used as fuel gas used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, cathode gas containing oxygen used for electric power generation of 1st fuel cell, anode off gas discharged from 2nd fuel cell and 1st fuel cell, and distribution channel which distributes cathode off gas discharged from cathode off gas or 2nd fuel cell supplied to 2nd fuel cell in which permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )1(PCO2/PH2)) of separation membrane is greater than or equal to 30;
   (2) fuel cell system comprising fuel cell which produces electric power using fuel gas, separation membrane which separates greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O) from anode off gas containing unreacted fuel gas discharged from 1st fuel cell, off-gas circulation path which supplies anode off gas from separation membrane and greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O is separated to fuel cell, and distribution channel which distributes source gas used as fuel gas for electric power generation of fuel cell, cathode gas containing oxygen used for electric power generation of fuel cell, or cathode off gas discharged from fuel cell in which permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )1(PCO2/PH2)) of separation membrane and permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )2(PH2O/PH2)) of separation membrane are greater than or equal to 30 and generating efficiency improvement effect of the system is greatly affected by greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O permeating the separation membrane than generating efficiency fall of system by hydrogen permeating the separation membrane; and
   (3) fuel cell system comprising fuel cell which produces electric power using fuel gas, separation membrane which separates greater than or equal to 1 of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O) from anode off gas containing unreacted fuel gas discharged from 1st fuel cell, off-gas circulation path which supplies anode off gas from separation membrane and greater than or equal to 1 of CO2 and H2O is separated to fuel cell, and distribution channel which distributes source gas used as fuel gas for electric power generation of fuel cell, cathode gas containing oxygen used for electric power generation of fuel cell, or cathode off gas discharged from fuel cell in which permeability coefficient ratio (( alpha )1(PCO2/PH2)) of separation membrane is greater than or equal to 30.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the fuel cell system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Fuel cell system (10)
   1st fuel cell (11)
   2nd fuel cell (12)
   Modifier (14)
   Separation membrane (16)

TI Efficient strategy for mitigating global warming            for preventing elevation of atmospheric carbon dioxide            concentrations and rising of sea levels, involves using            metal sheets and iron sheets, in which dimensions            change optionally, but slope remains same.
AU SUNORI S K
   MITTAL A
   JOSHI M
   JUNEJA P K
AE GRAPHIC ERA (GRAP-Non-standard)
   UNIV ERA HILL GRAPHIC (UYER-Non-standard)
GA 2022A99231
AB    NOVELTY - Efficient strategy for mitigating global                warming involves using metal sheets and iron                sheets, in which the dimensions change optionally,                but the slope remains the same.
   USE - Efficient strategy for mitigating global                warming for preventing elevation of atmospheric                carbon dioxide concentrations and rising of sea                levels.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficient strategy enables mitigation of                global warming using metals such as iron having                excellent thermal conductivity, by reducing the                level of carbon dioxide, chlorofluorocarbon, and                other pollutants causing the global climate change,                while increasing the availability of renewable                energy, by economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: Efficient strategy for                  mitigating global warming further involves                  placing a thin iron sheet of 4-5 mm at the sea                  shore, and placing the metal sheet at a distance                  of 2 inches above the ground, such that the rate                  of evaporation increases, when the sun rays fall                  on the metal sheet. Preferred Component: A cooler                  portion of the ground having a temperature of                  20&#176; C is provided at the sea                  shore.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal sheet does                  not allow the transfer of any heat to the                  ground.

PT P
PN WO2022120486-A1
TI Manufacture of biocarbon used for replacing            metallurgical coal, involves providing biomass,            converting biomass to biocarbon and contacting            biocarbon with supercritical carbon dioxide to form            treated biocarbon.
AU YAN N
   TANGUY N
AE CHAR BIOCARBON INC (CHAR-Non-standard)
GA 202281127T
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of biocarbon involves providing a                biomass, converting the biomass to a biocarbon and                contacting the biocarbon with supercritical carbon                dioxide to form a treated biocarbon.
   USE - Manufacture of biocarbon (claimed) used for                replacing metallurgical coal in steelmaking                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of biocarbon                with reduced alkali metal such as potassium,                sodium, calcium and magnesium and phosphorus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of                separating unwanted components from biocarbon,                which involves contacting the biocarbon with                supercritical carbon dioxide, and separating the                bicarbon from unwanted components to obtain treated                biocarbon with reduced unwanted components.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                manufacture of biocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The conversion of the                  biomass to the biocarbon is performed by                  pyrolyzing the biomass. The biomass is dried                  prior to pyrolyzing. The biocarbon is contacted                  with supercritical carbon dioxide and at least                  one chelating agent ad further contacted with a                  co-solvent and/or modifier to form a treated                  biocarbon. Preferred Conditions: The contacting                  process of biocarbon with supercritical carbon                  dioxide is performed at 140-220 degrees C and                  pressure of 1400-1700 psi for 60-300 minutes. The                  pyrolyzing is performed at 400-900 degrees C for                  20 minutes to 1 hour. Preferred Components: The                  biomass comprises agricultural residue, wood                  residue, forest residue, anaerobic or acidogenic                  digestate, animal waste and/or human waste. The                  weight of the alkali metal, alkaline earth metal,                  and/or phosphorus in the treated biocarbon is                  reduced compared to the biocarbon, preferably                  calcium, sodium, potassium, magnesium,                  phosphorus, and/or sulfur and/or ash content of                  the treated biocarbon is reduced compared to the                  biocarbon. The unwanted components comprises                  alkali metal, alkaline earth metal, and/or                  phosphorus, preferably calcium, sodium,                  potassium, magnesium, phosphorus, and/or sulfur                  and ash. Preferred Properties: The biomass has                  energy content of at least 26 mJ/kg, preferably                  at least 36 mJ/kg and alkali metal and phosphorus                  content of less than 250 mg/MJ, preferably 160                  mg/MJ.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one chelating                  agent is an ether, preferably polyethylene glycol                  dimethyl ether or crown ether. The cosolvent is                  methanol.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN110817839-A; CN110817839-B
TI Reducing carbon dioxide to porous carbon material useful in the electrode material, comprises heat-treating metal containing magnesium, stirring the resulting product in hydrogen chloride solution and purifying and drying resulting mixture.
AU XING Z
   MA Y
   FENG X
   LI A
   ZHONG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL (USCN-C)
GA 202018155D
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide to porous carbon material useful in the electrode material, comprises heat-treating a metal containing magnesium in an atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, stirring the resulting product in hydrogen chloride solution and purifying and drying resulting mixture.
   USE - The material is useful in the electrode material (claimed) and for preparing microbial fuel cell electrode and high power electrochemical capacitor electrodes.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has specific surface area of 1800-2000 m2/g, electrical conductivity of 1000-1100 S/m, capacitance retention rate of 0.60-0.65 g/cm3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal containing the magnesium is magnesium, zinc and/or copper, and certainly contains magnesium. The molar ratio of the other metals and magnesium is 0-10:1 and is not zero. The atmosphere containing carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide or a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in a ratio of 1:1-5:1. The flow rate of each atmosphere in the carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere is independently 20-120 SCCM. The molar concentration of the hydrogen chloride is 0.1-3 M and the stirring time is 1-12 hours. Preferred Method: The heat treatment is performed at 500-1100 degrees C, preferably 680 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours, preferably 6-12 hours.

PN CN108558800-A
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid comprises            reacting the furoformate, the molten salt and the            catalyst.
AU ZHOU G
   WANG R
   JIANG M
   WANG G
   ZHANG Q
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2018779825
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid comprises                (i) reacting the furoformate, the molten salt and                the catalyst under the condition of carbon dioxide                gas to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid, where the                melting point of the melting salt is less than 400                degrees C and the catalyst comprises a metal salt                catalyst and/or an organic base catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molten salt                  includes a single salt or a mixed salt. The                  molten salt is a sodium salt, or a mixed salt of                  a sodium salt and a potassium salt. The molten                  salt includes a mixed molten salt of sodium                  nitrate and potassium nitrate, a mixed molten                  salt of sodium nitrate and potassium nitrite, a                  mixed molten salt of sodium nitrate and sodium                  nitrite, a mixed molten salt of sodium nitrite                  and potassium nitrate, a mixture molten salt of                  sodium nitrite and potassium nitrite, , a mixed                  molten salt of potassium acetate and sodium                  acetate, a mixed molten salt of potassium acetate                  and potassium formate, a mixed molten salt of                  potassium acetate and sodium formate, a mixed                  molten salt of sodium acetate and potassium                  formate, a mixed molten salt of sodium acetate                  and sodium formate, a mixed molten salt of                  potassium formate and sodium formate, a mixed                  molten salt of sodium hydrogencarbonate and                  potassium hydrogencarbonate, a mixed molten salt                  of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide                  and/or a molten sodium nitrite and molten sodium                  formate, where the mass ratio of the mixed molten                  salt is (5-95):(95-5). The molten salt also                  includes other fusible salts and/or non-meltable                  salts, the cation of the metal salt catalyst                  includes potassium ion, sodium ion, lithium ion                  and/or alkaline earth metal ion, the anion of the                  metal salt catalyst includes a carbonate ion, a                  phosphate ion, a hydroxide ion and/or a                  hydrogencarbonate ion, the organic base catalyst                  comprises 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene,                  methylamine, urea, ethylamine, ethanolamine,                  ethylenediamine, dimethylamine, trimethylamine,                  triethylamine, propylamine, Isopropylamine,                  1,3-propanediamine, 1,2-propylenediamine,                  tripropylamine, triethanolamine, butylamine,                  isobutylamine, tert-butylamine, hexylamine,                  octamine, cyclohexylamine, triethylenediamine,                  tetramethylethylenediamine, aniline or its                  derivatives, benzylamine or its derivatives,                  pyridine or its derivatives, pyrimidine,                  4-dimethylaminopyridine, potassium t-butoxide,                  sodium hydride, sodium ethoxide and/or sodium                  methoxide and the furoate comprises potassium                  salt of furancarboxylate and/or sodium                  carbofurate. The molar ratio of the furoate to                  the catalyst is 1:(0.5-50). The ratio of the                  total mass of the furoate and the catalyst to the                  mass of the molten salt is 1:(0.5-100). Preferred                  Method: The reaction time is 1-24 hours, the                  reaction pressure is 0.1-25 MPa and the                  temperature of the reaction is 220-400 degrees C.                  The reaction further comprises a post-treatment                  step and an acidification step. The specific step                  of the post-treatment comprises adding water to                  the reaction system after the reaction, removing                  the insoluble matter in the reaction system and                  performing decolorization, where the acid for                  acidification includes hydrochloric acid,                  sulfuric acid, acetic acid, phosphoric acid,                  and/or dilute nitric acid and the acidified pH is                  less than 3. The acidifying further includes a                  post-treatment step, where the post-treatment                  includes separation, drying and/or                  recrystallization. The furoate is obtained by                  neutralization reaction of furancarboxylic acid,                  a basic compound and water, where the basic                  compound includes potassium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium carbonate and/or sodium                  carbonate.

PN CN108299154-A
TI Direct hydrothermal catalytic hydrogenation of xylitol for producing diol, involves adding xylitol, water and catalyst to reactor, and hydrogenating in hydrogen-containing atmosphere.
AU LI S
   KONG L
   MIAO G
   TAN Z
   LIU C
   SUN Y
   ZAN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 201859072P
AB    NOVELTY - Direct hydrothermal catalytic hydrogenation of xylitol, involves adding xylitol, water and a catalyst to a reactor, and hydrogenating in a hydrogen-containing atmosphere.
   USE - Direct hydrothermal catalytic hydrogenation of xylitol for producing 2-5C diol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses xylitol, which is easy to obtain, and enables efficient carbon dioxide bio-fixation and chemical conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The reactor is a batch reactor or a continuous reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises copper-nickel-metal supported catalyst. The carrier comprises zinc oxide, magnesium oxide, lanthanum oxide, ceria, and/or zirconia. The loading amount of copper and nickel is between 10% and 90%. The amount of carrier is between 90% and 10%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The xylitol/water ratio is between 50 g/100 ml and 1 g/100 ml. The mass ratio of xylitol and catalyst is between 1 and 5. Preferred Conditions: The hydrogenation pressure is between 1 MPa and 8 MPa and the temperature is between 423 K and 573 K. The reaction time is between 30 minutes and 360 minutes. Nitrogen gas is added as a carrier. The partial pressure of nitrogen gas is between 0 MPa and 7 MPa. The partial pressure of hydrogen is not less than 1 MPa.

AB    NOVELTY - Preventing and treating spontaneous combustion                of coal gangue involves determining the coal gangue                mountain of high temperature heat source level                range and heat source temperature distribution map,                grading the high temperature pre-warning value t                according to the coal gangue hill heat source                temperature, where the first stage is coal gangue                hill internal temperature reaches 200-400degrees                Celsius, gangue hill spontaneous combustion,                analyzing the harmful gas, belonging to the high                temperature risk band, the second stage is coal                gangue hill inner temperature is 100-200degrees                Celsius, portion of coal gangue with self-ignition                risk, belonging to the middle-high temperature risk                belt, the third level is coal gangue hill inner                temperature is 60-100degrees Celsius, belonging to                the low high temperature risk band, the waste rock                hill spontaneous combustion drilling integrated                device is ready, performing the liquid nitrogen of                the liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for preventing and treating spontaneous                combustion of coal gangue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects and eliminates the high                temperature heat source in the coal gangue hill,                thus improving the safety of the hill. The liquid                nitrogen of the drill bit has cooling effect, so                that the working period is shortened, the cost is                reduced, the device safety an elevated level, the                reliability is enhanced, and the damage to the                environment is reduced.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using an insulating layer                of insulating material to separate a power supply                and a drain plate. The gate is used for adjusting                an electric field. A signal is sent from a source                to the drain. An ion concentration change of ion                concentration in an ion sensitive field                field-effect transistor gate is changed. A voltage                of the gate is adjusted. The voltage of a gate is                different from a common field effect of metal. An                insulating sheet is directly connected to a process                solution. The solution is coupled to an                electrically conductive "counter electrode and a                reference electrode. The ion is detected by using                an ion-sensitive field effect transistor                semiconductor sensor to detect pH value. The pH                value is converted into carbon dioxide partial                pressure value by an amplifying circuit and                algorithm. The carbon dioxide full pressure value                of the process is realized through the                process.
   USE - Method for realizing percutaneous oxygen                partial pressure and carbon dioxide partial                pressure by using semiconductor in a chemical and                biological field, for detection and measurement of                different gases to identify, detect and measure                compounds within different gases.

   WANG G
AE GUANGZHOU WEIQING CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202218907C
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous polyurethane adhesive resin comprises 100-200 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer, 20-80 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 1-10 pts. wt. chain extension crosslinking agent, 10-30 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender, 0.01-1 pt. wt. organometallic catalysts, 200-400 pt. wt. deionized water, and 5-20 pt. wt. salt-forming agent.
   USE - Used as aqueous polyurethane adhesive resin.
   ADVANTAGE - Aqueous polyurethane adhesive resin: has stronger film-forming adhesiveness, soft, toughness, low viscosity and excellent stability. The method: has short production reaction time, low reaction temperature, simple reaction process, low energy consumption and low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for (i) adding carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer into reaction kettle, dehydrating at 100degrees Celsius for 0.8-2 hours to obtain anhydrous carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer, (ii) adding anhydrous carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer into the reaction kettle, heating to 70degrees Celsius, adding hydrophilic chain extender, acetone into reaction kettle, stirring for 10-15 minutes, then adding diisocyanate, stirring for 10-14 minutes, adding organic metal catalyst, raising the temperature to 80-85degrees Celsius, stirring, reacting for 2 hours, adding chain extension cross-linking agent, acetone, and stirring for 2-4 hours, (iii) cooling obtained solution in step (ii) to 60degrees Celsius, adding salt-forming agent and acetone, stirring, reacting, adding deionized water while stirring at high speed and (iv) finally cooling reacted solution obtained in step (iii) to 50-5degrees Celsius to recover acetone in vacuum, then cooling to room temperature, filtering with 200 mesh filter screen to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: Aqueous polyurethane adhesive resin comprises 100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer preferably polyether diols, 50 pts. wt. diisocyanate preferably diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 1.6 pts. wt. chain extension crosslinking agent preferably 1,6-hexanediol, 12 pts. wt. hydrophilic chain extender preferably dimethylol propionic acid, 0.018 pt. wt. organometallic catalysts preferably dibutyltin dilaurate, 320 pt. wt. deionized water, and 6 pt. wt. salt-forming agent.Preferred Components: The molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer is 1000-4000 g/mol. The hydroxy functionality of the carbon dioxide-polyol copolymer is 2-4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diisocyanate is methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate, liquified methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, polymerized isocyanates, hexamethylene diisocyanate and/or hydrogenated methylene diphenyl diisocyanate. The chain extension crosslinking agent is ethylene glycol, butanediol, diethylene glycol, dipropylene glycol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-propanediol, 1 4-bis(hydroxymethyl)cyclohexane, di-n-butylamine, ethylenediamine, trimethyl hexanediamine, tetraethylenepentamine. The hydrophilic chain extender is dimethylolpropionic acid, dimethylol butyric acid, 1 4-butanediol-2-sulfonate sodium salt and/or 1,2-propanediol-3-sodium sulfonate salt. The organometallic catalyst is stannous octoate, dibutyltin dilaurate, stannous isooctanoate and organobismuth. The salt-forming agent triethylamine, dimethylethanolamine and/or diethanolamine.

TI Optimal placement method of sensors for classrooms, involves constructing intelligent ventilation monitoring system, setting specific ventilation system, and realizing effective discharge of carbon dioxide in public classroom.
AU CAO S
   XI C
   FENG Z
   REN C
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202123088K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out clustering analysis on basic database and expanded database by adopting a fuzzy clustering algorithm, so that pollution clustering information in the ventilation environment is quickly obtained. The optimal sensor arrangement strategy-indoor pollution monitoring is developed to obtain comprehensive indoor pollution distribution information. The reference information is provided for decisions such as evaluation and control of the ventilation system. The number of sensors and the setting position in combination with the volume and the layout of the actual chamber are determined according to the number of clusters set in the clustering algorithm. The intelligent ventilation monitoring system is constructed. The specific ventilation system is set, and effective discharge of carbon dioxide in the public classroom is realized.
   USE - Optimal placement method of sensors for classrooms.
   ADVANTAGE - The storage cost and time cost of numerical calculation based on a low-dimensional linear data processing method can be effectively reduced. The concentration clustering of carbon dioxide in a public classroom can be realized based on the unsupervised machine learning clustering algorithm, and reasonable monitoring point deployment scheme is determined. The indoor ventilation system can be quickly controlled real-time for the carbon dioxide concentration and position in the public classroom, and the carbon dioxide concentration in the public classroom can be rapidly reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ventilation monitoring system for optimal placement method of sensors in classroom.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the optimal placement method of sensors for classrooms. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Polymer dope used for preparing polymer membrane            for treating natural gas stream comprising carbon            dioxide and methane, comprises polysulfone polymer            dispersed in solvent composition comprising organic            solvent and non-solvent additive.
AU SHAFAWI A
   KADIRKHAN F
   HALIM M H M
   MUSTAPA W N F W
AE PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 202290145H
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer dope comprises a polysulfone (PS)                polymer substantially dispersed in a solvent                composition comprising at least one organic solvent                and at least one non-solvent additive. The PS                polymer comprises 27-75 wt.% organic solvent and                non-solvent additive are based on the total weight                of the polymer dope. The organic solvent is a                mixture comprising tetrahydrofuran (THF) and                dimethylacetamide (DMaC).
   USE - Polymer dope used for preparing polymer                membrane for treating natural gas stream comprising                carbon dioxide and methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer dope provides polymer membrane                exhibiting superior flux and selectivity for carbon                dioxide under pressure conditions of greater than 1                MPa, highly permeable to gases, exhibiting carbon                dioxide permeability of at least 15-20 GPU when                measured under standard conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of polymer membrane, which                involves (a) contacting the polymer dope with a                coagulant bath, and (b) passing a bore fluid                concentrically through the polymer dope during step                (a) to define an internal lumen therethrough. The                coagulant bath is chosen to remove the solvent from                the polymer dope to thereby form a nascent polymer                membrane;
   (2) membrane, which comprises at least 27 wt.%                polysulfone polymer (PS) and an organic solvent                mixture comprising THF and DMaC; and
   (3) use of hollow fiber, asymmetric membrane                comprising the polysulfone polymer (PS) and the                organic solvent mixture comprising THF and DMaC for                treatment of a natural gas stream comprising carbon                dioxide and methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bore fluid is                  chosen from water and an alkali salt solution.                  The coagulant bath is water. Preferred                  Composition: The bore fluid comprises 10 wt.%                  potassium acetate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nonsolvent                  additive is a polar compound. The polar                  nonsolvent additive is chosen from water and                  aliphatic or branched, optionally substituted                  1-4C alcohol, preferably ethanol. The asymmetric                  membrane is a hollow fiber membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer dope                  comprises at least 27-60 wt.%, preferably 28-60                  wt.% polysulfone (PS) polymer, 30-65 wt.% mixture                  of THF and DMaC, and 5-15 wt.% ethanol. The                  asymmetric membrane comprises 27-60 wt.% polymer                  PS. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves passing the polymer dope and bore fluid                  through an air gap prior to contacting the                  coagulant bath, and contacting the nascent                  polymer membrane with a water bath (B2).                  Preferred Property: The air gap is 5-70 cm in                  length.

TI Preparing safe storage method for sweet potato scale, involves performing indoor disinfection treatment on storage room before storage, and spraying storage room with carbendazim and screening sweetened sweet potato.
AU WANG L
   LI J
   WANG H
AE TIANJIN FENGHUA YULONG AGRIC DEV CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201953883H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing safe storage method involves performing indoor disinfection treatment on the storage room before storage, and spraying the storage room with 50 wt.% carbendazim 1000 times solution or 50 wt.% sulfur suspension agent 800 times solution, screening the sweetened sweet potato after harvesting, and removing the potato pieces that are broken, sick, frozen, exposed to wormholes, and have serious wounds. The sweet potato is packaged and then sterilized 50 wt.% is used. The carbendazim suspension is soaked for 12-18 minutes in 400 times, and it is air-dried in the air-drying chamber. The storage room is adjusted the concentration of carbon dioxide in the storage chamber through the carbon dioxide transfer device. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the storage chamber is 45-56 wt.%.
   USE - Method for preparing safe storage method for sweet potato scale.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare safe storage method which is avoids direct blowing to the sweet potato surface and cooperating with carbon dioxide, ensures that the oxygen in the storage chamber can maintain the normal physiological activities of the potato chips, reduces mildew and soft rot, delays the loss of nutrients, prolongs the storage time of the sweet potato and increases the commodity rate of sweet potato.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The frequency of use of the carbon dioxide transfer device in the summer is once every four hours, each time the machine is turned on 10 minutes, every 8 hours in the winter, every time 15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The storage chamber has a specification height of 1.8 meter and a length and a width of 2.5 meter. The sweet potato after the packing is sterilized and replaced with methyl sulfur having a mass concentration of 25-30 wt.%. The humidifier is used to humidify the storage chamber. The bottom layer of the storagechamber with a straw.

   USE - The system is useful for capturing CO2 in a CO2-containing gas (claimed).

The system further comprises: a cooling section connected between a liquid outlet of a first CO2 absorption tower and a liquid inlet of an n-th CO2 absorption tower in the n number of CO2 absorption towers; a mixer connected between the liquid outlet of the CO2 absorption towers and a liquid inlet of the stripper and used for mixing a rich solvent from the CO2 absorption towers and a water; a condensation section (148) connected to a gas outlet of the stripper; a second flash drum connected to the condensation section; a heat exchange section connected between the liquid outlet of the CO2 absorption towers and a liquid inlet of the stripper via a first flow path and connected between the liquid outlet of the first flash drum and the reflow liquid inlet of the CO2 absorption towers via a second flow path; and a cooling section connected between the heat exchange section and the reflow liquid inlet of the CO2 absorption towers. A liquid outlet of the first flash drum is connected to a reflow liquid inlet of the CO2 absorption towers. Preferred Method: The method further comprises: cooling the rich solvent from a first CO2 absorption tower and returning the cooled rich solvent to an n-th CO2 absorption tower; mixing the rich solvent and a water; condensing the CO2 stripping gas to form a condensed CO2 stripping gas; performing a second flash treatment on the condensed CO2 stripping gas using a second flash drum to form a CO2 flash gas; performing a heat exchange treatment on the rich solvent and the lean solvent after the first flash treatment in a heat exchange section; and cooling the lean solvent after the heat exchange treatment. The liquid after the first flash treatment comprises the lean solvent.

TI Method for performing chromatographic separation with valve, involves merging flow of compressible fluid based chromatography solvent with elution from trap column to generate diluted elution by merging flow of carbon dioxide with elution.
AU FOGWILL M O
   FRANCOIS I
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 201859399E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves modulating a portion of a flowstream to a trap column (24) to retain analyte from the flowstream on the trap column. A flow of a modifier i.e. compressible fluid-based chromatography modifier, is provided through the trap column to generate an elution comprising the analyte. Flow of a compressible fluid based chromatography solvent is merged with the elution from the trap column to generate a diluted elution by merging a flow of carbon dioxide with the elution from the trap column. The diluted elution is provided to a compressible fluid-based chromatography column (14), where the analyte is focused at a head of the compressible fluid-based chromatography column.
   USE - Method for performing a chromatographic separation with a valve.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables desorbing analytes on the trap column, while the downstream addition of the carbon dioxide at the flow combiner so as to reduce elution strength of the fluid flow so as to provide increased mass transfer resulting in high chromatographic efficiency at high mobile phase flow rates, thus preventing focusing at the chromatographic column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for performing a chromatographic separation with a valve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon dioxide-based compressible fluid-based chromatography system for performing a chromatographic separation.
   Mobile phase pump (12)
   Compressible fluid-based chromatography column (14)
   Valve (22)
   Trap column (24)
   Carbon dioxide-based system (30)

PN CN107902686-A
TI Preparing intelligent ecological resource-light calcium carbonate by carbonization method comprises e.g. generating lime and carbon dioxide by calcining raw materials e.g. limestone and adding digested lime water to generate lime milk.
AU CHENG Y
AE HUNAN HENGWANG CALCIUM CARBONATE CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2018301071
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing intelligent ecological resource-light calcium carbonate by carbonization method comprises e.g. generating lime and carbon dioxide by calcining raw materials e.g. limestone, adding digested lime water to generate lime milk, introducing carbon dioxide, carbide lime milk to generate calcium carbonate precipitate, at last, dehydrating the calcium carbonate precipitate to obtain light calcium carbonate, drying and crushing, where in the calcining production process, feeding the calciner automatically into calcining furnace by lift way, and swing out of the reclaimer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing intelligent ecological resource-light calcium carbonate by carbonization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; and realizes intelligent ecological resource utilization for producing light calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing intelligent ecological resource-light calcium carbonate by carbonization method comprises generating lime and carbon dioxide by calcining raw materials e.g. limestone, adding digested lime water to generate lime milk, then introducing carbon dioxide, carbide lime milk to generate calcium carbonate precipitate, at last, dehydrating the calcium carbonate precipitate to obtain light calcium carbonate, drying and crushing, where in the calcining production process, feeding the calciner automatically into calcining furnace by lift way, swing out of the reclaimer, in dehydration and drying process, the chaff-fired furnace is used for hot air drying and use of multi-layer circulation for the comprehensive utilization of hot gas waste heat, the smoke generated from the combustion of the chaff is deposited without pollution, and intelligent ecological resources are used to produce light calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The automatic feeding of the calciner is the installation of a lift or a belt conveyor next to the calciner, lift has a barrel, broken limestone and coal are conveyed to the barrel through conveyor belts, when the cartridge is full, automatically raised to the top of the calciner, loading the calciner from the top or directly by the belt conveyor directly for the calciner. The lower calcining and discharging of the calcining furnace is swinging conveying device arranged at the discharging port at the lower part of the calcining furnace, and the pendulum conveying device outputs the slaked lime at the lower discharging port of the calcining furnace to the conveying belt through the swinging conveying pipe and sending to the next process. In the dehydrating and drying process, the hot air is used to obtain the hot air to dry the rice husk combustion furnace in the digestion workshop and the drying workshop, the husk combustion provides hot air or hot gas. The chaff combustion furnace uses the chaff as a raw material, and adopts an automatic conveying method to convey the chaff to the boiler, and at the same time controls the feeding of the chaff according to the require of the temperature, and controls the temperature of the hot air or hot gas provided by the chaff combustion furnace. The comprehensive utilization of the residual heat of the hot gas using the multi-layer circulation method is that after the filter cake (wet material water content is less than or equal to 35%) from the centrifuge is broken by the beater, the belt conveyor is used to feed the drum dryer, in the drying process, drying is performed by a multi-layer circulating drying apparatus, and the dried hot air is dried by the preheating generated by the chaff combustion furnace. The non-polluting emission of the flue gas produced by the combustion of the chaff is set in a flue gas settling device in the flue gas channel of the husk combustion furnace, and flue gas generated by the husk combustion is treated by the flue gas settling device, emissions after reaching the standard. The carbonization method comprises crushing limestone mined in quarry at crushing site, breaking into limestone with a grain size of 75-150 mm, blending limestone and anthracite with a particle size of 20-40 mm at a blending ratio of 100:12-100:15, placing in the bucket, transporting to the lift next to the calciner, pouring into the ingredients conveyor belt, from the conveyor belt to the lift hopper, starting the lift to raise the hopper filled with ingredients to the top of the calciner, pouring into the calciner, conveying calcined limestone through a swinging conveyor at the lower outlet of the calciner, in a vibrating manner, sending out to the conveyor belt through the discharging cylinder, then into the digestive plant for digestion, where the ratio of lime to water is 1:4 .8, concentration of lime milk obtained by digestion is approximately 20 degrees Be , mixing with cold water to 30 degrees Be , passing through the grate, the coarse slag is precipitating and refining with a three-stage sieve and a hydro-cyclone separator and adjusting to 12 degrees Be, then the refined milk is sending to the carbonization tower for carbonization reaction with the kiln gas to obtain calcium carbonate suspension, calcium carbonate suspension from the carbonation tower is sending to the slurry tank for concentration by gravity sedimentation, after the thickened slurry is sending to the high tank, filtering by the upper suspension centrifuge, filtered filtrate enters the sedimentation tank for gravity sedimentation to recover the calcium carbonate, added calcium carbonate is returned to the slurry pool, clear liquid discharged during settling returns to the dust-removal tower of the spray dust collector screen to clean the kiln gas together with the clear liquid discharged when the thickened liquid is collected, after the filter cake from the centrifuge is broken by a beater, using a belt conveyor into a multi-layer dryer for drying, dry material from the dryer having water content of less than or equal to 0.4%, removing iron from the iron remover, adding into the high-speed centrifugal crusher for smashing, taking powder from the crusher, 99.995% passing over 125 mu m sieve, 99.7% passing over 45 mu m sieve, that is, light calcium carbonate, enter the cycle, most of the coarser light calcium carbonate cyclones come out of the bottom of the cyclone and are placing for storage, small part of the finer calcium carbonate comes out of the top of the cyclone collector, enters the bag dust collector, and is also storing as a product package.

The carbon                dioxide catalytic conversion device is provided                with product outlet, proton exchange membrane,                negative electrode, balance port, positive                electrode and first feeding port. The proton                exchange membrane is used for transferring hydrogen                ions between cathode and anode. The methanol                membrane separation device is provided with                methanol separation membrane, second feeding port,                steam outlet and unpenetrated outlet.
   USE - Intermittent carbon dioxide capture and                conversion coupling device.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An intermittent carbon dioxide capture and                conversion coupling device comprises carbon dioxide                trap (1), carbon dioxide desorption device (2),                carbon dioxide catalytic conversion device (8) and                methanol membrane separation device (9). The                proton exchange membrane is used to transfer                hydrogen ions between cathode and anode. The                methanol membrane separation device is provided                with second feeding port, methanol separation                membrane, unpenetrated outlet and steam outlet. The                methanol separation membrane is used for separating                methanol aqueous solution. The rich liquid outlet                of carbon dioxide trap is connected with rich                liquid inlet of carbon dioxide desorption device                through rich liquid transmission pipeline. The                alkali liquid outlet of carbon dioxide desorption                device is connected with first feeding port and                carbon dioxide catalytic conversion device by first                pipeline (11). The product outlet of carbon dioxide                catalytic conversion device is connected with                second feeding port of methanol membrane separation                device through product conveying pipeline. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation method                of device.

PN EP4011850-A1; WO2022123435-A1
TI Production of clinker from cement raw meal involves using electrical energy to decarbonize raw meal instead of burning fossil fuels.
AU BUCHER E
   BRUSCO G
   BLUM R
   STOFFEL B
   WEIHRAUCH M
AE HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
GA 2022803073
AB    NOVELTY - Production of clinker from cement raw meal involves (a) preheating cement raw meal in a preheater string (1) comprising preheater stages, (b) pre-calcining preheated raw meal in a pre-calciner (7) to obtain a pre-calcined product, and (c) introducing the pre-calcined product into a rotary kiln (2) for calcining the pre-calcined product to obtain cement clinker. A partial flow of at least partly preheated raw meal is diverted from the preheater string, introduced into a calcination device and at least partially decarbonated in the calcination device in order to obtain an at least partially decarbonated product and carbon dioxide. The calcination device is heated by electrical energy, and at least partially decarbonated product is fed into the rotary kiln and the carbon dioxide is drawn off from the calcination device.
   USE - Production of clinker e.g. Portland cement clinker used in cement industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables an efficient use of electrical energy for the production of cement, and reduces the use of fossil fuels and other alternative fuels which are traditionally used in the cement process. This enables the reduction of the carbon dioxide footprint of the production clinker, as electricity has a lower carbon-di-oxide footprint than fossil or alternative fuels. The exhaust gas from the calcination device, where no fuel is burnt, is pure carbon dioxide, so that the process does without the requirement to separate carbon dioxide from flue gasses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for producing clinker.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating cement production process.
   Preheater string (1)
   Rotary kiln (2)
   Cyclone suspension-type preheater stages (3,4,5,6)
   Pre-calciner (7)
   Devider (8)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing oxalate by strengthening carbon                dioxide (CO2) electroreduction with ionic liquid                electrolyte containing nitrile group and phenolic                hydroxyl group, involves preparing ionic liquid                electrolyte by compounding bifunctional ionic                liquid of formula (I) with quaternary phosphine or                quaternary ammonium as the cation and                p-hydroxybenzonitrile and its derivatives as the                anion, with an aprotic solvent, mainly through the                double-site synergistic coupling between the                nitrile group and phenolic hydroxyl group on the                aromatic ring anion and CO2, strengthening the                dissolution and activation of CO2molecules, and                promoting the electrochemical reduction of CO2 into                oxalate. TI Method of non-invasively using breathing metrics to determine oxygen status of patient, involves using first and second oxygen measurements, and carbon dioxide measurement to determine partial pressure of oxygen in arterial blood.
AU LEE Y
   LEE S
AE MEDIPINES CORP (MEDI-Non-standard)
GA 202250785U
AB    NOVELTY - The method (300) involves obtaining an oxygen measurement from exhaled breath of a patient. The second oxygen measurement of the patient is obtained. A carbon dioxide measurement is obtained from the patient. The partial pressure of oxygen in arterial blood is determined using the oxygen measurements and the carbon dioxide measurements. A blood oxygen saturation monitor is used to obtain the second oxygen measurement. The carbon dioxide and oxygen measurements are obtained as an end-tidal value. A temperature measurement is used as an additional factor to determine partial pressure of oxygen in arterial blood. An alarm signal is rendered based upon the determined oxygen deficit. A practitioner evaluates the oxygen status by concurrently displaying pressure of oxygen and partial pressure of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for non-invasively using breathing metrics to determine an oxygen status of a patient with a pulmonary disease and lung disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring gas exchange efficiency or inefficiency, which is well tolerated by the patient and can be easily repeated. The method is non-invasive, comfortable for the patient, requires a technically skilled person, has occasional complications, and is inexpensive.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of method for non-invasively using breathing metrics to determine an oxygen status of a patient with a pulmonary disease and lung disease.
   Breathing metrics using method (300)
   Instructing patient to breath normally through the breathing tube for a period of time (302)
   Measuring oxygen saturation with pulse oximeter (316)
   Measuring end tidal values from exhaled gases (320)
   Obtaining related physiological variables (322)

The method relieves the problem of land resource waste and ecological environment pollution caused by large stacking of metallurgical slag to a certain extent, and reduces the discharge of carbon dioxide, promotes the realization of carbon neutralization. The resource utilization of the steel slag and carbon dioxide can finally realize the economic benefit growth.

TI Preparing modified jute fiber reinforced bio-based epoxy resin composite material, involves placing jute fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, mixing bio-based epoxy resin, curing agent, active release agent, toughening agent and emulsifying agent, drying, solidifying and forming.
AU ZHAO C
   LI J
   WU Q
   YE S
   ZHENG F
   CAI T
AE UNIV LIMING VOCATIONAL (UYLI-Non-standard)
   SHISHI ZHONGFANGXUE CLOTHING & ACCESSORI (SHIS-Non-standard)
GA 202245698D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified jute fiber reinforced bio-based epoxy resin composite material, comprises (a) placing jute fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, passing in carbon dioxide with biological enzyme treatment solution, and adjusting temperature, pressure, time and carbon dioxide flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain modified jute fiber, and weaving to obtain modified jute fabric and drying; (b) mixing 70-90 pts. wt. bio-based epoxy resin, 5-9 pts. wt. curing agent, 4-6 pts. wt. active release agent, 1-3 pts. wt. toughening agent and 0.3-2 pts. wt. emulsifying agent, uniformly stirring at room temperature; and (c) coating mold cavity with mold release agent, drying, injecting bio-based epoxy resin liquid into the mold, covering with release agent, obtaining the modified jute fabric and placing in mold, pouring remaining bio-based epoxy resin into the mold without spilling, drying, placing at room temperature, solidifying and forming.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified jute fiber reinforced bio-based epoxy resin composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is green and pollution-free, conducive to improving environmental problems and reducing consumption of resources, has the characteristics of being biodegradable, improves the bond fastness between jute fiber and bio-based epoxy resin, and synergistically enhances the tensile, flexural and impact properties of the composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the biological enzyme dosage is 3-5% (owf), and comprises hemicellulose, pectinase and lignin peroxidase in a ratio of 1-3:1:1-4, which is dissolved in water, and the pH of the treatment solution is adjusted to 5.0-6.0 with 1% hydrochloric acid. The bio-based epoxy resin is gallic acid epoxy resin or eugenol epoxy resin. The curing agent comprises 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole, 2-phenylimidazole and/or 3-diethylaminopropylamine. The active release agent comprises methacrylic acid-beta-hydroxyethyl ester, 1,6-hexanediol diacrylate, 2-phenoxyethyl acrylate, and/or tripropylene glycol diacrylate. The toughening agent is a polyether compound or nano calcium carbonate. The emulsifying agent is isomeric alcohol polyoxyethylene ether.Preferred Method: In the step (a), the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 40-60degrees Celsius, the pressure is 10-20 MPa, the time is 0.5-1.5 hours, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-40 g/minute. In the step (a), the modified jute fabric size is 60x60 mm, the drying is carried out at 50-80degrees Celsius in oven, In the step (c), the size of the mold is 65x65x5 mm, and the drying is carried out in an oven at 60-80degrees Celsius, and the curing time at room temperature is 20-30 hours.

   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing metal                oxide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides novel heterogeneous                catalyst using supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction which can provides increased rate,                conversion, or selectivity to selective activation                reactions bonds inherent in reactants compared to                existing catalyst preparation method; provides                methodology that can control the redox properties,                and distribution/number/intensity of Bronsted acid                sites, Lewis acid sites, surface labile oxygen's,                oxygen vacancies to for selective activation                reaction; advantages provided by supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction described above/below, it                is preferable to prepare intermediate product in                which precursor of catalyst crystalline grains is                precipitated onto a support; utilizes supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction may have remarkably                improved reactivity and durability; improves                dispersity of active sites in pores or the support                surface; and improves rate and performance and                exhibited improved resistance to poisons in low                temperature range and has improved life span.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                metal oxide catalyst comprising an active site                containing at least one type of metal oxide and a                support onto which the active site is loaded, where                the metal oxide is an oxide of a metal comprising                transition metals (atomic number 21-29, 39-47,                72-79, or 104-108), lanthanide (atomic number                57-71), post-transition metals (atomic number 13,                30-31, 48-50, 80-84, and 112), and/or metalloids                (atomic number 14, 32-33, 51-52, and 85) in the                periodic table.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises:                  precipitating a precursor of metal oxide catalyst                  crystalline grains onto a surface of a support                  after dissolving the precursor of the metal oxide                  catalyst crystalline grains in a synthetic                  solvent; drying a catalyst crystalline grain                  precursor-support intermediate product using                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; and                  calcining the dried catalyst crystalline grain                  precursor-support intermediate product to                  synthesize a metal oxide catalyst. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is                  performed at 50-150degrees Celsius for 0.1-24                  hours, at a flow rate of 10-5-105 m/minute, and                  at a carbon dioxide pressure of 75-150 atm. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extracted by                  the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                  weakens an interaction between the support and                  the synthetic solvent.Preferred Components: The active site is                  porous and has a diameter of 0.1 nm to 500                  microm. The active site has a composition of                  10-4-50 wt.% based on 100 parts by weight of the                  support. The support contains element comprsing                  alkaline earth metals (atomic number 4, 12, 20,                  38, 56, and 88), transition metals (atomic number                  21-29, 39-47, and 72-79, or 104-108), lanthanide                  (atomic number 57-71), post-transition metals                  (atomic number 13, 30-31, 48-50, 80-84, and 112),                  and metalloids (atomic number 14, 32-33, 51-52,                  and 85) in the periodic table, and/or carbon (C).                  The support has microporosity, mesoporisity,                  macroporosity, or hierarchical porosity. The                  support contains at least one oxide of the                  element.

TI Composite film structure used in gas adsorption            filter, comprises metal oxide layer formed on support            and metal-organic framework thin film formed on layer,            and metal atom is shared between metal oxide of metal            oxide layer and metal-organic structure of thin film at            interface.
AU OOE H
AE MURATA MFG CO LTD (MURA-C)
GA 2021F0830F
AB    NOVELTY - A composite film structure (10) comprises a                metal oxide layer (2) formed on a support (1) and a                metal-organic framework thin film (3) formed on the                metal oxide layer. At the interface between the                metal oxide (20) of the metal oxide layer and the                metal-organic structure of the metal-organic                framework thin film, a metal atom is shared by the                metal oxide and a metal-organic structure.
   USE - Composite film structure used in gas                adsorption filter for gas removing device (all                claimed) and gas or odor sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite film structure improves                excellent reliability and gas adsorption of the                sensor and the gas adsorption filter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) gas adsorption filter, which comprises the                composite film structure; and
   (2) gas removing device, which comprises the                gas adsorption filter. The gas is carbon dioxide                gas. The removal of carbon dioxide gas in the room                to outside involves (i) blowing indoor air onto the                gas adsorption filter to adsorb carbon dioxide gas,                (ii) releasing adsorbed carbon dioxide gas by                blowing warm air onto the gas adsorption filter or                heating the gas adsorption filter, and (iii)                discharging the released carbon dioxide gas to the                outside of the room.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional                view of the composite film structure.
   Support (1)
   Metal oxide layer (2)
   Metal-organic framework thin film (3)
   Composite film structure (10)
   Metal oxide (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide layer                  comprises a modified layer at least on its                  surface, in which the metal oxide constituting                  the metal oxide layer is modified by the                  metal-organic structure constituting the                  metal-organic framework thin film. In the                  modified layer, the metal-organic framework thin                  film is formed on the surface of the metal oxide                  and the metal-organic framework is formed by                  utilizing the metal atoms of the metal oxide. The                  metal-organic framework thin film is (M1) a thin                  film produced by applying heat and pressure while                  immersing the metal oxide layer in a solution                  containing organic molecules constituting the                  metal-organic structure or (M2) manufactured by                  immersing the metal oxide layer in a solution                  containing the metal-organic structure, drying,                  and heat-treating to mix the interface between                  the metal oxide and the metal-organic structure.                  The metal oxide layer is a porous layer or a                  porous plating layer. The metal oxide layer                  comprises one or more metal oxides chosen from                  zinc oxide, copper oxide, nickel oxide, iron                  oxide, indium oxide, and aluminum oxide. The                  metal-organic framework thin film is a porous                  film based on a coordinate bond between an                  organic molecule and a metal atom containing a                  metal atom derived from the metal oxide. The                  support is a crystal vibrator or piezoelectric                  ceramics. The gas is carbon dioxide gas.                  Preferred Properties: The metal oxide layer has                  thickness of 1-10 mu m. The metal-organic                  framework thin film has thickness of 10-100                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic molecule                  comprises one or more chosen from azole-based                  organic molecules, cyanide-based organic                  molecules and carboxylic acid-based organic                  molecules, preferably azole-based organic                  molecule or a cyanide-based organic molecule. The                  azole-based organic molecule is chosen from                  imidazole, benzimidazole, triazole and purine.                  The amine compound is supported on the                  metal-organic framework thin film.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amine compound is                  an amino group-containing polymer having weight                  average molecular weight of 1000 or more.

PN CN113719843-A; CN113719843-B
TI Method for recycling low-concentration gas            resource, involves generating carbon dioxide, hydrogen            peroxide and hydrogen oxide with low concentration, and            finally realizing complete conversion of low content            gas.
AU KANG W
   SONG C
   DI C
   SONG Z
   LIU F
   ZHAO J
   YANG L
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021E5834T
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling low-concentration gas resource                involves introducing fuel gas into a reactor. The                reduction reaction is fixed with an oxidation state                oxygen carrier in a reactor. An oxidized oxygen                carrier is reduced after the fuel gas is oxidized.                The low concentration gas is introduced to a                reactor of an oxidation reaction stage, where the                oxidation reaction is performed with a                reduction-state oxygen-carrying body. A reduction                state oxygen-carrying body is oxidized into an                oxidized state oxygen-carrying body. Carbon                dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, and hydrogen oxide with                low concentrations are generated. The complete                conversion of the low content gas is finally                realized.
   USE - Method for recycling low-concentration gas                resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing low concentration                gas with concentration about 0.25-1.5% by using                chemical chain combustion technology, increasing                system combustion efficiency, improving extraction                utilization rate of low-concentration gas, and                obviously reducing gas emptying amount. The method                enables effectively utilizing low- concentration                gas for a long time, and realizing greenhouse                effect, great economic benefit and                environment-friendly benefit. The method enables                directly generating carbon dioxide and water by the                fuel gas, which is convenient for separation of the                later carbon dioxide. The method enables easily                capturing carbon dioxide under the dilution of                nitrogen. The fuel in the method is not directly                contact with the air and the oxygen carrier in the                reactor adopts iron-based
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Nitrogen gas cylinder (1)
   Methane gas bottle (2)
   Air bottle (3)
   Carbon monoxide gas bottle (4)
   Flow switch (6)
   Gas distribution box (8)
   Needle valve (9)
   Vertical tubular furnace (10)
   Flue gas analyzer (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The oxygen carrier in                  the first step is a transition metal-based oxide,                  a multi-metal composite oxygen carrier or an                  oxygen carrier with perovskite structure, where                  the transition metal is iron, cobalt, copper,                  nickel or manganese. The oxygen carrier in the                  first step is 50% hematite/50%aluminum oxide. The                  reactor is a fluidized bed reactor, a fixed bed                  reactor, a moving bed reactor, a trickle bed                  reactor and a bubbling bed reactor.

AB    NOVELTY - Dinuclear imidazole polyionic liquid material,                prepared by ternary copolymerization of dinuclear                imidazole ionic liquid monomer and crosslinking                agent, is claimed. The crosslinking agent comprises                divinylbenzene and N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, and                the mass content of the dinuclear imidazole ionic                liquid monomer is 20-40%.
   USE - The dinuclear imidazole polyionic liquid                material is useful in carbon dioxide adsorption and                catalyzing cycloaddition reaction (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dinuclear imidazole polyionic liquid                material: has a porous structure and rich active                groups (ether bond, amide bond), adjustable                structure, stable properties, easy to recycle and                reuse, reduces the cost, and shows a good                application prospect in the efficient use of carbon                resources; exhibits high carbon dioxide adsorption                performance; can catalytically convert carbon                dioxide into cyclic carbonate in a relatively short                period of time thus realizes the efficient                utilization of carbon dioxide; and preparation                method utilizes simple equipment and mild                conditions and is convenient to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the dinuclear imidazole polyionic liquid                material, comprising (i) adding vinyl imidazole,                halogenated hydrocarbon or ether into the solvent,                reacting under inert gas atmosphere at 60-90                degrees C for 24-48 hours, extracting, filtering,                washing, and drying to obtain the dinuclear                imidazole ionic liquid monomer; and (ii) adding the                dinuclear imidazole ionic liquid monomer,                divinylbenzene, N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide and                initiator into the solvent and reacting under an                inert gas atmosphere at 50-80 degrees C for 12-24                hours, centrifuging, washing, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogenated                  hydrocarbon or ether described in step (i) is                  1,2-dibromoethane, 1,2-dichloroethane,                  1,4-dibromobutane, 1,4-dichlorobutane, bis                  2-bromoethyl ether or bis(2-chloroethyl)ether. GA 2020198819
AB    NOVELTY - The anti-icing system for the safe emission of supercritical carbon dioxide power generation systems has a buffer unit that determines its number of stages depending on the total pressure of the system, and the higher the total pressure, the higher the number. The pipelines that need to emit carbon dioxide working fluid are connected to the inlet of the primary buffer pressure reducing valve in emergency or accident conditions. PN CN110687792-A; CN110687792-B
TI Method for controlling anti-smoke disturbance            fuzzy for Carbon dioxide collecting system after            chemical adsorption combustion, involves designing            fuzzy PI controller for lean-capture rate loop and            variable gain ratio controller.
AU WU X
   XI H
   LI Y
   SHEN J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020073365
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting lean liquid flow                and extraction steam flow as input parameters.                Carbon capture rate and reboiler temperature are                selected as output parameters, where the lean                liquid flow is selected to control Carbon dioxide                capture rate, the extraction steam flow is selected                to control reboiler temperature. A transfer                function model of an object is identified based on                experimental data. Feedforward compensation                decoupling is performed. PID parameters are set. A                fuzzy PI controller is designed for lean-capture                rate loop under condition of satisfying input                constraints. A variable gain ratio controller is                designed based on the relationship between the flue                gas flow and the lean liquid flow and the                extraction steam flow under different steady-state                conditions.
   USE - Method for controlling anti-smoke disturbance                fuzzy for Carbon dioxide collecting system after                chemical adsorption combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables introducing the fuzzy                controller on basis of PI control, adjusting PI                parameters online so as to quickly track the Carbon                dioxide capture rate by a controlled object,                designing a variable gain proportional controller                according to measurable disturbance of flue gas                flow rate and relative changes of the flue gas flow                rate and the lean liquid and the extraction steam                flow rate under different capture rates, and                improving system ability to resist flue gas                disturbances in case of large-scale system                changes.

PN DE102018205571-A1; WO2019185328-A1; EP3728698-A1; US2021002776-A1; EP3728698-B1; ES2908010-T3
TI Gas diffusion electrode useful in electrolysis plant system for electrolytic conversion of carbon dioxide, comprises a first layer containing electrically conductive fabrics and a second layer containing catalytically active particles.
AU LANDES H
   RELLER C
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 2019831178
AB    NOVELTY - Gas diffusion electrode (2) comprises at least two layers including a first layer (4) and a second layer (6). The first layer comprises electrically conductive fabrics (8) which are partially embedded in a hydrophobic polymer matrix (10) and the second layer has an open porosity comprising catalytically active particles having thickness of 1-50 mu m.
   USE - The electrode is useful in electrolysis plant system for electrolytic conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode has extended durability, excellent efficiency, selectivity and track resistance of 0.001 ~o; prevents penetration of liquid electrolyte into first layer, excretion of salts from electrolyte in matrix, wetting of silver fibers with electrolyte in cooperation with hydrophobic property of matrix material and unwanted water; reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide; avoids electrochemical reaction; and is hydrophobic, un-reactive and harmless; requires less electronic power supply.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrolysis plant system for electrolytic conversion of carbon dioxide, comprising the gas diffusion electrode; and
   (2) operating electrolysis plant, comprising introducing carbon dioxide-containing educt into a gas space, connecting the educt to the gas diffusion electrode, and diffusing through a first layer the gas diffusion electrode after which it strikes and is reduced to an interface of the first layer and second layer of the gas diffusion electrode, where the second layer is a porous layer impregnated by a liquid electrolyte.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the gas diffusion electrode.
   Gas diffusion electrode (2)
   First layer (4)
   Second layer (6)
   Electrically conductive fabric (8)
   Hydrophobic polymer matrix (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The electrolysis plant system comprises an electrolysis plant having a gas space and an electrolyte chamber, where the second layer of the gas diffusion electrode is directed towards the electrolyte chamber. The electrolysis plant system further comprises a contact web electrically connected to the gas diffusion electrode.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fabrics comprise silver, titanium, nickel and/or carbon-containing fibers. The polymer matrix comprises polytetrafluoroethylene. The polymer matrix is designed to be hydrophobic and has wetting angle to water of more than 90 degrees . The first layer has thickness of 50-1000 mu m, preferably 100-400 mu m. The polymer matrix has porosity of 95% and diameter of 0.1-2 mu , preferably 0.6-0.9 mu . The catalytically active particles of the second layer have diameter of 0.05-1 mu m, preferably 0.1-0.5 mu m. The second layer comprises particles of catalytically active silver particles. The particles of the second layer comprises hydrophilic binder having wetting angle to water of less than 90 degrees . The 95% pores of the second layer have diameter of 0.1-5 mu m, preferably 0.1-1 mu m.
