PT P
PN EP3421662-A1; WO2019002553-A1; EP3645786-A1; EP3645786-B1; ES2894609-T3
TI Separating lignin from lignocellulosic material            having lignin and cellulose, comprises e.g. mixing            lignocellulosic material with carbon dioxide-triggered            solvent to dissolve lignin and cellulose is not            dissolved, and separating cellulose.
AU OCHOA GOMEZ J R
   BLANCO PEREZ N
   LORENZO IBARRETA L
AE FUNDACION TECNALIA RES & INNOVATION (TCNA-C)
   FUNDACION TECNALIA RES & INNOVATION (TCNA-C)
GA 201900455V
AB    NOVELTY - Separating lignin from a lignocellulosic                material comprising lignin and cellulose, in order                to obtain cellulose free or free of lignin, where                the cellulose comprises less than or equal to 1 of                lignin, comprises: (a) preparing a carbon                dioxide-triggered switchable solvent by mixing                water, a water-insoluble amine which is a secondary                amine and/or a tertiary amine, and carbon dioxide                until a single aqueous phase is formed; (b) mixing                the lignocellulosic material with the carbon                dioxide-triggered switchable solvent obtained in                step (a), thus dissolving the lignin in the solvent                and the cellulose is not dissolved; and (c)                separating the cellulose.
   USE - The process is useful for separating lignin                from lignocellulosic material comprising lignin and                cellulose.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes the solvent that is                simple, efficient and inexpensive for lignin                removal from a lignocellulosic material; does not                require the use of anti-solvents or                energy-intensive steps, thus avoiding the use of                volatile and dangerous compounds; allows to perform                lignin extractions with complete selectivity and an                unbeatable yield of 100%; separates lignin from                cellulose by solid-liquid extraction using carbon                dioxide-triggered switchable solvents as extraction                solvents as compared to conventional processes;                allows the extraction to be performed at room                temperature, thus saving large quantities of                energy, and does not require large volumes of                liquid that would hinder the scalability of the                process; allows the complete recycling of the                water-insoluble amines with low energy consumption,                with no need of using inorganic salts of costly                recovery; and is simple with use of inexpensive                compounds and low energetic cost to provide lignin                with extraordinary yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The amine added in step                  (a) comprises 20-50 wt.%, relative to the total                  quantity of amine and water before bubbling                  carbon dioxide. The mixing of step (a) is carried                  out by bubbling the carbon dioxide through the                  water and the amine at a pressure of ambient                  pressure to 1 MPa, while stirring. The mixing of                  step (a) is carried out by bubbling the carbon                  dioxide through the water and the amine at room                  temperature, while stirring. The step (b) is                  performed at room temperature, while stirring.                  The process: further comprises (d) removing the                  carbon dioxide from a solution of lignin in the                  carbon dioxide-triggered switchable solvent                  resulting from the separation of the cellulose in                  step (c), thus triggering the separation of the                  water and the amine into two distinct phases, and                  the precipitation of the lignin, and (e)                  separating the precipitated lignin; and using the                  mixture of amine and water resulting from step                  (e) for preparing the carbon dioxide-triggered                  switchable solvent of step (a). The step (d) is                  performed by heating the solution at 50-100                  degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C. The step                  (d) is performed either under vacuum and/or by                  bubbling an inert gas through the solution.                  Preferred Components: The water-insoluble amine                  comprises substituted amine compounds                  (R2-N(R1)-R3) (I). The amine comprises                  N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine,                  N-methylcyclohexylamine (both preferred),                  N,N-diethylcyclohexylamine or                  N-ethylcyclohexylamine. The inert gas comprises                  nitrogen, argon or air.R1 = 5-6C cycloalkyl;R2 = 1-5C alkyl, preferably 1-2C alkyl;                  andR3 = H or 1-5C alkyl, preferably H or 1-2C                  alkyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

AB    NOVELTY - The loop (100) has a gas storage reservoir                (110) for storing a refrigerant. A pressure relief                flow passage (120) connects the gas storage                reservoir and a refrigeration system and discharges                the refrigerant in the refrigeration system into                the gas storage reservoir. A recovery flow passage                (130) is provided with a driving apparatus, where                the recovery flow passage recovers the refrigerant                in the gas storage reservoir into the refrigeration                system under drive of the driving apparatus. The                pressure relief flow passage connects the gas                storage reservoir and a flow passage between an                evaporator (240) and a compressor (210) of the                refrigeration system.
   USE - Pressure relief and recovery loop for use in a                carbon dioxide refrigeration system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The loop avoids refrigerant overpressure                phenomenon such that refrigerant needs to be                constantly refilled is solved and considerations to                maintenance cost and system operation stability are                balanced with environmental-friendly. The recovery                flow passage connects the gas storage reservoir and                a low-pressure side flow passage of the so that                discharged carbon dioxide refrigerant is recovered                into the refrigeration loop after a system is                recovered to a normal pressure state, thus avoiding                influence on refrigeration capacity of the system                due to insufficiency of the refrigerant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide refrigeration                system
   (2) a method for controlling a carbon dioxide                refrigeration system.

PN CN108444948-A; CN208224084-U
TI Differential absorption laser radar system for measuring atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration, has upper computer which transmits transmission monitoring digital signal and echo digital signal after acquisition of acquisition card.
AU HONG G
   ZHANG H
   LIU H
AE SHANGHAI TECH PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CATP-C)
GA 201868098M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fiber amplifier (8) which power-amplifies the output laser coupled by the fiber combiner (7). The power amplified laser is divided into 1% energy and 99% energy two-way laser through the third fiber splitter (17), in which 1% energy laser is input into the first photodetector (11), and 99% of the energy is emitted through the collimating mirror (9). The telescope (10) receives the echo light signal. The output of the telescope is coupled to the input of the second photodetector (12). The acquisition card (13) collects the emission monitoring analog signal at the output of the first photodetector and the echo analog signal at the output of the second photodetector. The upper computer (14) transmits the transmission monitoring digital signal and the echo digital signal after the acquisition of the acquisition card through the network port. The upper computer processes the data to obtain the distance between the carbon dioxide concentration and the air column.
   USE - Differential absorption laser radar system for measuring atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts chopping acousto-optic modulation method, ensures waveform stability and simplifies the system, and simultaneously detects carbon dioxide concentration and distance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for differential absorption laser radar method for measuring atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the differential absorption laser radar system.
   Fiber combiner (7)
   Fiber amplifier (8)
   Collimating mirror (9)
   Telescope (10)
   First photodetector (11)
   Second photodetector (12)
   Acquisition card (13)
   Upper computer (14)

PT P
PN CN111252766-A
TI Polarizing of high-efficiency carbon dioxide raw            material gas comprises heating carbon dioxide, flowing            heated carbon dioxide through solid oxide, hydrolyzing            of carbon dioxide and cooling hydrolyzed carbon dioxide            to normal temperature.
AU WANG H
AE HUADONG ENERGY ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HUAD-Non-standard)
GA 202053681U
AB    NOVELTY - Polarizing of high-efficiency carbon dioxide                raw material gas comprises (i) heating the carbon                dioxide at 120-140 degrees C; (ii) flowing the                heated carbon dioxide through the solid oxide;                (iii) hydrolyzing of carbon dioxide in presence of                a hydrolysis catalyst; (iv) cooling the hydrolyzed                carbon dioxide to normal temperature; (v)                performing fine desulfurization of carbon dioxide                at room temperature to obtain carbon dioxide with                total sulfur content of less than or equal to 0.1                ppm.
   USE - The method is useful for polarizing of                high-efficiency carbon dioxide raw material                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces sulfur content in carbon                dioxide; and allows carbon dioxide to meet food                grade requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii),                  hydrolyzing of carbon dioxide in presence of                  hydrolysis catalyst is gamma -aluminum oxide with                  ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate and potassium                  carbonate as active ingredients with a molar                  ratio of 0.3-0.5, controlling at 1.5-2.5 Mpa with                  flow rate of 1500-1800 m3/hour. In the step (ii),                  flowing heated carbon dioxide through solid oxide                  is copper oxide particles. In the step (v), the                  fine polarizing is carried out using ferric oxide                  particles are filled in the pores of the                  honeycomb activated carbon block.

PT P
PN FR3053325-A1; FR3053325-B1
TI Debottlenecking hydrogen generating plant, comprises module for generating synthesis gas by reforming from light hydrocarbons, optionally shift module, and pressure swing adsorption-hydrogen unit for purification of hydrogen.
AU ALLIDIERES L
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2018010774
AB    NOVELTY - Debottlenecking hydrogen generating plant, comprises: module for generating synthesis gas by reforming from light hydrocarbons; optionally shift module for enriching hydrogen and carbon dioxide by conversion of carbon monoxide contained in synthesis gas with water vapor; and pressure swing adsorption-hydrogen (PSA-H2) unit for purification of hydrogen and production of high pressure gas stream of ultra pure hydrogen with associated production of low-pressure gas residual (PSA waste) whose two major constituents are carbon dioxide and hydrogen having PSA waste is sent to electrochemical cell.
   USE - The method is useful for debottlenecking a hydrogen generating plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides hydrogen having an optimized hydrogen yield and hydrogen purities compatible with the International organization for standardization standard relating to the purity of hydrogen for fuel cells and minimizing the investment required to achieve hydrogen and increase the recovery efficiency of the hydrogen produced; utilizes the PSA-H2 unit that is simple for purification, thus reducing cost of the process; utilizes the electrochemical cell that is fed by a low pressure gas and the PSA waste is used as it is produced by the PSA-H2 without prior compression by supplementing the PSA feed with the gaseous complement of hydrogen from the EHS cell and the hydrogen content of the PSA feed gas is significantly increased and the yield and productivity of the PSA are also significantly improved; interrupts the operation of the electrochemical cell so as to optimize the electrical consumption of the plant; increases the hydrogen production of the plant in proportions much greater than those which can be reached by the conventional means of debottlenecking, exhibits no modifications to the plant requiring heavy work; preserves the hydrogen gas produced; utilizes the plant that adopts the solution during construction, production of very high purity hydrogen with a maximum hydrogen yield, over-sizing of other equipment is avoided; and recovers the second fluid produced by the EHS cell that is poor in hydrogen and rich in carbon dioxide in order to produce carbon dioxide if it is recoverable or to carry out a carbon dioxide capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Debottlenecking a hydrogen generating plant, comprises: a module for generating a synthesis gas by reforming from light hydrocarbons; optionally a shift module for enriching hydrogen and carbon dioxide by conversion of the carbon monoxide contained in the synthesis gas with water vapor; and pressure swing adsorption-hydrogen (PSA-H2) unit for the purification of hydrogen and the production of a high pressure gas stream of ultra pure hydrogen with associated production of a low-pressure gas residual (PSA waste) whose two major constituents are carbon dioxide and hydrogen having all or part of the PSA waste is sent to an electrochemical cell which separates from hydrogen and a waste (EHS cell waste) depleted of hydrogen from the PSA waste, where the hydrogen is recovered to form an additional hydrogen stream which is compressed to a pressure of 8-25 bar and sent wholly or partly to the inlet of the PSA unit so as to increase the hydrogen production of the plant while maintaining the purity of hydrogen unchanged produced by the PSA. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) hydrogen production process comprising (a) generation by reforming a synthesis gas from a light hydrocarbon feedstock, (b) optional enrichment of the synthesis gas with hydrogen and carbon dioxide by conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, (c) purification of enriched synthesis gas for the production of a high pressure gas stream of ultra pure hydrogen by PSA-H2 with production associated with a low pressure gas PSA waste whose two major constituents are carbon dioxide and hydrogen, (d) supplying an electrochemical cell (EHS cell) with all or part of the low pressure PSA waste for recovering additional hydrogen from the PSA waste and a hydrogen-depleted residual (cell waste), (e) compression of the additional hydrogen recovered to a pressure of 8-25 bar, and (f) recycling all or part of the additional hydrogen recovered compressed in the process upstream of the PSA unit to supply the PSA so as to increase the production yield of very high hydrogen purity of the installation; and
   (2) plant for producing hydrogen from a light hydrocarbon feed stream having an optimized yield, comprising a module for generating by reforming a synthesis gas from the hydrocarbon feed stream light, an optional module for the conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide for hydrogen and carbon dioxide enrichment of synthesis gas, the PSA-H2 unit for purifying the hydrogen contained in the synthesis gas with the production of a high-pressure leaving gas stream of ultra-pure hydrogen and associated production of a low-pressure leaving gas PSA residual, both of which main constituents are carbon dioxide and hydrogen, an electrochemical cell (EHS cell) capable of being fed by the low-pressure gas PSA waste and capable of separating hydrogen present in the PSA residual from the other constituents so as to produce a flow of hydrogen and a waste gas depleted in hydrogen (EHS cell waste), a means of compressing the flow of hydrogen separated by the EHS cell, a treatment means and / or a means of using the EHS cell waste, and a means for outputting, driving and feeding the different flows used.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The compression of the additional hydrogen recovered is provided at least in part by the electrochemical cell. At least a portion of the additional hydrogen recovered at the output of the electrochemical cell is used for the desulfurization of the hydrocarbon feed prior to reforming. All or part of the hydrogen-depleted waste leaving the electrochemical cell (EHS cell waste) is recovered to produce carbon dioxide. At least a portion of the hydrogen depleted waste leaving the electrochemical cell (EHS cell waste) is used as a reforming fuel. In case of production of excess hydrogen, the operation of the electrochemical cell is interrupted so as to optimize the power consumption of the installation and the waste gas of the PSA then find its use prior to debottlenecking or the installation can return to its original mode of operation. Preferred Components: The electrochemical cell is at least partly to ensure the compression of the additional hydrogen recovered. The plant further comprises: a means for compressing and transferring at least a portion of the additional hydrogen recovered at the outlet of the electrochemical cell to a desulfurization module of the light hydrocarbon feedstock; and a means for using the hydrogen depleted waste leaving the electrochemical cell (EHS cell waste) as fuel for reforming and/or for producing dioxide of carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (e) of compression of the additional hydrogen recovered is carried out at least in part by the electrochemical cell. At least a portion of the additional hydrogen recovered at the outlet of the electrochemical cell is used to desulfurize the light hydrocarbon feedstock prior to step (a). All or part of the hydrogen depleted waste leaving the electrochemical cell (EHS cell waste) is recovered to produce carbon dioxide. At least a portion of the hydrogen depleted waste leaving the electrochemical cell (EHS cell waste) is used as a reforming fuel.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The phage                Riemerella anatipestifer phage WXLRAP15 is                deposited in the China Center for Type Culture                Collection on November 23, 2021 with deposition                number is CCTCC NO: M20211477.
   USE - The R. anatipestifer phage is useful in                preventing and treating R. anatipestifer disease                caused by R. anatipestifer
   ADVANTAGE - The phage Riemerella anatipestifer phage has a                high fermentation titer, good tolerance to                temperature and pH, and wide lysis spectrum, which                can lyse 6 serotypes of R. anatipestifer, with                lysis rate of 75.5% for R. It lyses R.                anatipestifer quickly and maintains lysis for a                long time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separating and purifying the R. anatipestifer phage                comprising (i) collecting total of 50 water                samples, fecal samples and soil samples from a duck                farm in Hubei Province, simple pretreatment of the                samples, taking the supernatant after                centrifugation at 5000 revolutions per minute (rpm)                for 10 minutes and sterilizing by filtration,                evenly mixing filtrate with the same volume of                2&#215;TSB liquid medium (containing 10%                newborn bovine serum) and 1 mL of R. anatipestifer                (107cfu/mL) in logarithmic phase, incubating                overnight at 37&#176;C in a 5% carbon dioxide                incubator to enrich for phage, (ii) centrifuging                the sample enrichment solution at 5000 rpm for 10                minutes, taking the supernatant and sterilizing it                through a 0.22 &#956;m microporous membrane to                obtain a filtrate containing bacteriophage, (iii)                taking filtrate 100uL and mixing evenly with its                host R. anatipestifer bacteria liquid 500uL,                allowing to stand for 15 minutes and making it                completely combine with the receptor on bacterial                surface, (iv) adding above mixture to 7 ml of TSB                semi-solid agar medium (containing 5% newborn calf                serum) cooling to 50&#176;C, Immediately after                mixing, spreading on the solidified TSA plate, and                solidifying the agar, incubating upside down in a                5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37&#176;C for                12-16 hours to observe the growth of plaques, and                (v) on a double-layered plate that forms plaques,                picking large and translucent phage plaques with a                sterile pipette tip, desorb them in 1 mL of SM                solution by shaking, and sterilize them through a                0.22 &#956;m microporous filter to obtain a                phage filtrate, inoculating into 5mL TSB liquid                medium (containing 5% newborn bovine serum), adding                100uL of the corresponding host R. anatipestifer                bacteria solution and mixing well, incubating                overnight at 37&#176;C in a 5% carbon dioxide                incubator, centrifuging at 5000 rpm for 10 minutes                to take the supernatant and filter it with a                bacterial filter, using the double-layer plate                method to observe the plaque morphology, repeating                the operation 3-5 times, obtaining plaques with the                same shape and size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The bacteriophage was                  isolated from R. anatipestifer strain WXLRA15 as                  a host, and it was a long-tailed bacteriophage                  with a plaque size of 1 mm, the full length of                  the bacteriophage genome was 47956 bp, the G+C                  content was 34.9%, and there were 83 ORFs in                  total .

PN WO2022002884-A1; TW202212298-A
TI Producing ethylene by contacting ethane and oxygen with oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst in reactor tubes, contacting resulting mixture with catalyst, and subjecting stream of e.g. ethylene and acetylene to acetylene hydrogenation.
AU SCHOONEBEEK R J
   VAN ROSSUM G
   BOS A N R
   ESPOSITO CASSIBBA I D
   CHRISTIANSEN M A
   ESPOSITO C I D
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202202606F
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene is produced by (a) supplying ethane and oxygen to 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone which is formed by multiple reactor tubes containing catalyst bed containing oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst which is a mixed metal oxide catalyst; (b) contacting ethane and oxygen with catalyst in the multiple reactor tubes; (c) mixing multiple effluent streams, resulting in a mixture comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, unconverted oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and acetylene; (d) supplying mixture from step (c) to 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone containing catalyst bed containing oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst which is a mixed metal oxide catalyst; (e) contacting mixture with catalyst; (f) separating at least a portion of stream resulting from step (e); and (g) subjecting at least a portion of stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, and acetylene to acetylene hydrogenation, resulting in a stream comprising ethylene and unconverted ethane.
   USE - Production of ethylene by oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed metal oxide catalyst in the 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone does contain tellurium, because the conversion of oxygen in that 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone can be kept relatively low, as further conversion will be effected in the downstream, 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone any way, so that any non-oxidizing environment and any subsequent tellurium loss may be prevented, and as a result, a stable catalyst performance for the selective, tellurium-containing catalyst may be ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ethylene is produced by (a) supplying ethane and oxygen to 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone which is formed by multiple reactor tubes containing catalyst bed containing oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst which is a mixed metal oxide catalyst; (b) contacting ethane and oxygen with catalyst in the multiple reactor tubes in 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone, resulting in multiple effluent streams, where multiple reactor tubes are cooled by coolant; (c) mixing at least a portion of multiple effluent streams, resulting in a mixture comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, unconverted oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and acetylene; (d) supplying at least a portion of mixture from step (c) to 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone containing catalyst bed containing oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst which is a mixed metal oxide catalyst; (e) contacting at least a portion of the mixture from step (c) with catalyst in 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone, resulting in stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and acetylene; (f) separating at least a portion of stream resulting from step (e) into a stream comprising carbon monoxide and a stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, and acetylene; and (g) subjecting at least a portion of stream comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane, and acetylene resulting from step (f) to acetylene hydrogenation, resulting in a stream comprising ethylene and unconverted ethane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Catalyst in the 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone is a mixed metal oxide catalyst containing tellurium. Catalyst in the 1st oxidative dehydrogenation zone contains molybdenum, vanadium, niobium, and tellurium. Catalyst in the 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone is a mixed metal oxide catalyst which does not contain tellurium. Catalyst in the 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zone contains molybdenum, vanadium, and niobium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The 1st and 2nd oxidative dehydrogenation zones are comprised within the same reactor vessel or within separate reactor vessels arranged in series. In step (b), 80-99% of the oxygen supplied to step (a) is converted. In steps (b) and (e) in combination, 99-100% of the oxygen supplied to step (a) and any additional oxygen supplied to step (d) is converted. Preferred Compositions: Mixture resulting from step (c) comprises 1000 parts per million by volume (ppmv) to 10 vol.% unconverted oxygen. Mxture resulting from step (e) comprises no or substantially no unconverted oxygen or at most less than 1000 ppmv unconverted oxygen.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN SE202030081-A1; SE543928-C2
TI Preparation of ikaite crystals in carbon capture and storage, involves preparing aqueous solution with preset total inorganic carbon content, divalent magnesium ion content, divalent calcium ion content and divalent sodium ion content, adjusting pH of solution, and adjusting temperature of solution.
AU TOLLEFSEN E
   SKELTON A
AE SKELTON A (SKEL-Individual)
   TOLLEFSEN E (TOLL-Individual)
GA 2021B5590L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ikaite crystals involves preparing (10) an aqueous solution having a total inorganic carbon content of 10 mmol/kg or more, a divalent magnesium ion content of 5 mmol/kg or more, a divalent calcium ion content of 2 mmol/kg or more, and a divalent sodium ion content of 50 mmol/kg or more, adjusting (20) the pH of the aqueous solution to 9.1-10.4, and adjusting (30) the temperature of the aqueous solution to more than 15 degrees C to facilitate formation of ikaite crystals in the aqueous solution.
   USE - Preparation of ikaite crystals in carbon capture and storage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method rapidly converts unstable ikaite into stable calcite with high yield, and effectively and permanently stores carbon dioxide with no risk of leakage back to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ikaite (CaCO3.6H2O) crystals involves preparing an aqueous solution having a total inorganic carbon content of 10 mmol/kg or more, a divalent magnesium ion content of 5 mmol/kg or more, a divalent calcium ion content of 2 mmol/kg or more, and a divalent sodium ion content of 50 mmol/kg or more, adjusting the pH of the aqueous solution to 9.1-10.4, and adjusting the temperature of the aqueous solution to more than 15 degrees C to facilitate formation of ikaite crystals in the aqueous solution. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for carbon capture and storage, which involves preparing ikaite crystals. The aqueous solution having total inorganic carbon content of 10 mmol/kg or more is prepared by dissolving (15) carbon dioxide in aqueous solution; and
   (2) system for preparing ikaite crystals, which comprises a compartment (c1) for holding an aqueous solution (s1) having total inorganic carbon content of 10 mmol/kg or more and divalent sodium ion content of 50 mmol/kg or more, a compartment (c2) for holding an aqueous solution (s2) having divalent magnesium ion content of 5 mmol/kg or more and divalent calcium ion content of 2 mmol/kg or more, and a compartment (c3) for receiving a portion of the solution (s1) and a portion of the solution (s2) to form an aqueous solution (s3) having a pH of 9.1-10.4 and a temperature of more than 15 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the preparation of ikaite crystals.
   Preparation of aqueous solution (10)
   Dissolution of carbon dioxide in aqueous solution (15)
   Adjustment of pH of aqueous solution (20)
   Adjustment of temperature of aqueous solution (30)
   Removal of ikaite crystals (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Composition: The system further comprises an arrangement for conducting gaseous carbon dioxide into the solution (s1), such that the carbon dioxide is dissolved in the solution (s1).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The temperature of the solution is 16-50 degrees C, 16-45 degrees C, 16-40 degrees C, 16-35 degrees C, 16-30 degrees C, 16-25 degrees C, 16-20 degrees C, 18-50 degrees C, 18-45 degrees C, 18-40 degrees C, 18-35 degrees C, 18-30 degrees C, 18-25 degrees C, 18-20 degrees C, 20-50 degrees C, 20-45 degrees C, 20-40 degrees C, 20-35 degrees C, 20-30 degrees C, 20-25 degrees C, 25-50 degrees C, 25-45 degrees C, 25-40 degrees C, 25-35 degrees C, 25-30 degrees C, 30-50 degrees C, 30-45 degrees C, 30-40 degrees C, 30-35 degrees C, 35-50 degrees C, 35-45 degrees C, 35-40 degrees C, 40-50 degrees C, 40-45 degrees C, or 45-50 degrees C. The preparation of ikaite crystals further involves removing (40) obtained ikaite crystals and/or calcite crystals obtained using the ikaite crystals from the solution. Preferred Property: The pH of the solution is 9.1-10.2, 9.1-10, 9.1-9.8, 9.1-9.6, 9.1-9.4, 9.1-9.2, 9.2-10.4, 9.2-10.2, 9.2-10, 9.2-9.8, 9.2-9.6, 9.2-9.4, 9.4-10.4, 9.4-10.2, 9.4-10, 9.4-9.8, 9.4-9.6, 9.6-10.4, 9.6-10.2, 9.6-10, 9.6-9.8, 9.8-10.4, 9.8-10.2, 9.8-10, 10-10.4, 10-10.2, or 10.2-10.4. Preferred Composition: The solution has a total inorganic carbon content of 10-100 mmol/kg, preferably 10-15 mmol/kg, divalent magnesium ion content of 5-100 mmol/kg, preferably 95-100 mmol/kg, divalent calcium ion content of 2-50 mmol/kg, preferably 45-50 mmol/kg, and divalent sodium ion content of 50-500 mmol/kg, preferably 50-60 mmol/kg.

PT P
PN CN111335294-A; CN111335294-B
TI Preparing pre-carbonized modified composite pile involves starting pile mixer on set pile position to penetration state until spiral drill bit spins and penetrates to set depth, and starting pile mixer to lifting state.
AU CUI C
   YU C
   YI N
   ZHAO J
   XIN Y
   LIANG Z
   WANG B
AE UNIV DALIAN MARITIME (UYDM-C)
GA 202061117J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a pre-carbonized modified composite pile involves starting the pile mixer on the set pile position to the penetration state until the spiral drill bit spins and penetrates to the set depth, and starting the pile mixer to the lifting state, and spraying the curing agent while the lifting of spiral drill bit. The pile mixing machine repeatedly drills down and lifts the drill to make the soil and curing agent mix uniformly to form a mixed soil. The drill bit is withdrawn from the pile hole, the pile hole is closed and carbon dioxide gas with a temperature higher than 50 degrees C are passed into the pile hole. The mixed soil is pre-carbonized and modified to obtain modified soil. The modified soil is taken out, crushed outside the pile hole, mixed with a curing agent, and the moisture content is adjusted to obtain a mixed modified soil. The hole of the pile is filled with the mixed modified soil layer and compacted.
   USE - Method for preparing a pre-carbonized modified composite pile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the engineering performance of the soil, and significantly improves the strength of the pile, and ensures fast pile formation speed and low project cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a pre-carbonized modified composite pile involves starting the pile mixer on the set pile position to the penetration state until the spiral drill bit spins and penetrates to the set depth, and starting the pile mixer to the lifting state, and spraying the curing agent while the lifting of spiral drill bit. The pile mixing machine repeatedly drills down and lifts the drill to make the soil and curing agent mix uniformly to form a mixed soil. The drill bit is withdrawn from the pile hole, the pile hole is closed and carbon dioxide gas with a temperature higher than 50 degrees C are passed into the pile hole. The mixed soil is pre-carbonized and modified to obtain modified soil. The modified soil is taken out, crushed outside the pile hole, mixed with a curing agent, and the moisture content is adjusted to obtain a mixed modified soil. The hole of the pile is filled with the mixed modified soil layer and compacted. The holes are drilled in the rammed modified soil piles, and drilled into ventilating steel pipes. The pile hole is closed, and the carbon dioxide is injected into the pile hole through a ventilated steel pipe. A gravel pile is made in the ventilating steel pipe, and the gravel pile is combined with the carbonized mixed modified soil to form a pile.

TI System used to make base stock from light gas stream contains oxidation reactor, separation system, catalytic reactor, removal system, oligomerization reactor, distillation column, hydro-processing reactor and product distillation column.
AU CAO G
   DAKKA J M
   HO S C
   WEISS B M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202017458R
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: oxidation reactor to form raw ethylene stream from light gas stream; separation system to remove water from the raw ethylene stream; catalytic reactor to oxidize carbon monoxide in the raw ethylene stream; removal system to remove carbon dioxide from the raw ethylene stream; oligomerization reactor to oligomerize the raw ethylene stream to form oligomer stream; distillation column to separate the oligomer stream into light olefinic stream or recover light alpha -olefin stream, separate intermediate olefinic stream from the higher molecular weight stream and send the intermediate olefinic stream to dimerization reactor or alkylation reactor, where the distillation column is to recover light alpha -olefin, intermediate olefinic stream, and heavy olefinic stream; hydro-processing reactor configured to hydro-process the heavy olefinic stream to form a hydro-processed stream; and product distillation column configured to separate the hydro-processed stream to form base stock.
   USE - The system is useful for manufacturing a base stock from a light gas stream (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises: an oxidation reactor configured to form a raw ethylene stream from the light gas stream; a separation system configured to remove water from the raw ethylene stream; a catalytic reactor configured to oxidize carbon monoxide in the raw ethylene stream; a removal system configured to remove carbon dioxide from the raw ethylene stream; an oligomerization reactor configured to oligomerize the raw ethylene stream to form an oligomer stream; a distillation column configured to separate the oligomer stream into a light olefinic stream or recover a light alpha -olefin stream, separate an intermediate olefinic stream from the higher molecular weight stream and send the intermediate olefinic stream to a dimerization reactor or an alkylation reactor, where the distillation column is configured to recover a light alpha -olefin, an intermediate olefinic stream, and a heavy olefinic stream; a hydro-processing reactor configured to hydro-process the heavy olefinic stream to form a hydro-processed stream; and a product distillation column configured to separate the hydro-processed stream to form the base stock. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for manufacturing a base stock from a light gas stream comprising oxidizing the light gas stream to form an impure ethylene mixture, removing water from the impure ethylene mixture, oxidizing carbon monoxide in the impure ethylene mixture, separating carbon dioxide from the impure ethylene mixture; oligomerizing the impure ethylene mixture to form a raw oligomer stream; distilling a light olefinic stream from the raw oligomer stream and recovering a light alpha olefin from the light olefinic stream; distilling a heavy olefinic stream from the raw oligomer stream; hydro-processing the heavy olefinic stream to form a hydro-processed stream; and distilling the hydro-processed stream to form the base stock.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The oxidation reactor is configured to perform: an oxidative coupling of methane. The oxidation reactor is configured to perform an oxidative dehydration of ethane. The removal system comprises an amine column. The system comprises: a dimerization reactor configured to dimerize the intermediate olefinic stream and return a dimerized stream to the distillation column; an alkylation reactor configured to alkylate the intermediate olefinic stream and provide an alkylated stream to an alkylation distillation column, where the alkylation distillation column is configured to separate an unreacted olefin stream from the alkylated stream and return the unreacted olefin stream to the alkylation reactor. The oligomerization reactor is configured to use a homogenous catalyst. The homogenous catalyst comprises an iron(II) pyridine-bis-imine catalyst is of formula (I).R1n = CH3 and/or F.The hydro-processing reactor comprises a demetallation unit. The hydro-processing reactor comprises a hydrocracking unit or a hydroisomerization unit. The product distillation column is configured to separate the hydro-processed stream into a distillate stream comprising naphtha, a heavy neutral stream, a medium neutral stream and a light neutral stream. Preferred Method: The method comprises: distilling an intermediate olefinic stream from the raw oligomer stream; dimerizing the intermediate olefinic stream to form a dimerized stream and distilling the dimerized stream with the raw oligomer stream; alkylating the intermediate olefinic stream to form an alkylated stream, distilling the alkylated stream to form a lights stream and a heavies stream, combining the lights stream with the intermediate olefinic stream to form a combined stream and alkylating the combined stream; hydro-processing the heavies stream, where oligomerizing the impure ethylene mixture comprises contacting the impure ethylene mixture with a homogenous catalyst comprising an iron(II) pyridine-bis-imine (I); and separating an unreacted ethylene stream from the impure ethylene mixture, and oligomerizing the unreacted ethylene stream with the impure ethylene mixture, where hydro-processing the heavy olefinic stream comprises hydrocracking the heavy olefinic stream and hydro-processing the heavy olefinic stream comprises hydroisomerizing the heavy olefinic stream. The step of distilling the hydro-processed stream comprises: separating a distillate stream, a naphtha stream, or both from the hydro-processed stream; and forming a heavy neutral oil stock stream, a medium neutral oil stock stream and/or a light neutral oil stock stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE ZHEJIANG WANSHOUKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020040519
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting O-acetyl grape mannan from Dendrobium candidum, involves selecting leaves and stems of disease-free D.candidum, soaking D.candidum in water, drying D.candidum at 30-100 degrees C until the mass content of water in the leaves and stems is less than 3%, crushing dried leaves and stems to a particle size of 20-120 meshes to obtain leaf powder of D.candidum, mixing leaf powder of D.candidum with the immobilized enzyme to obtain an extract, where the mass ratio of the powder of the D.candidum and the immobilized enzyme is 0.1-100:1, and the immobilized enzyme is cellulase, xylanase, pectinase, hemicellulase and snail enzyme, adding the D.candidum extract into the extraction kettle, extracting the mixture by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, passing the extraction mixture through reduced pressure separation kettle to perform a two-stage separation.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting grape mannan which is used for preparing O-acetyl grape mannan in deciduous tooth mesenchymal stem cell culture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective to perform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting O-acetyl grape mannan from Dendrobium candidum, involves selecting leaves and stems of disease-free D.candidum, soaking D.candidum in water, drying D.candidum at 30-100 degrees C until the mass content of water in the leaves and stems is less than 3%, crushing dried leaves and stems to a particle size of 20-120 meshes to obtain leaf powder of D.candidum, mixing leaf powder of D.candidum with the immobilized enzyme to obtain an extract, where the mass ratio of the powder of the D.candidum and the immobilized enzyme is 0.1-100:1, and the immobilized enzyme is cellulase, xylanase, pectinase, hemicellulase and snail enzyme, adding the D.candidum extract into the extraction kettle, extracting the mixture by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the extraction conditions are as follows: the extraction pressure is 15-50 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-75 degrees C, the supercritical carbon dioxide flow is 5-45 kg/hour and extraction time is 0.5-3 hours, passing the extraction mixture through reduced pressure separation kettle to perform a two-stage separation, where the first-stage reduced pressure separation pressure is 5-10 MPa, the temperature is 40-50 degrees C, and the second-stage reduced pressure separation pressure is 5-6 MPa, and the temperature is 35-50 degrees C, and obtaining polysaccharide extract of D.candidum. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of D.candidum O-acetyl grape mannan, which is for preparing O-acetyl grape mannan in deciduous tooth mesenchymal stem cell culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding entrainer and an extractant during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, where the mass ratio of entrainer and an extractant is 1:1.4-20. The entrainer is chosen from methanol, ethanol and acetone. The entrainer is prepared by dissolving an organic solvent in a buffer solution having a pH of 4-7 and 50-90% organic solvent solution, where the organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, and acetone. The mass ratio of leaf powder and immobilized enzyme of D.candidum is 10-50:1.

PN CN108910838-A
TI Recovery of aluminum by-product from potassium feldspar and phosphogypsum involves grinding potassium feldspar and additive, calcining, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, treating liquid using carbon dioxide, and stirring residue and urea.
AU LI J
   SU X
   XIE Y
   LU F
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
   CHEN X
   GAO G
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018984434
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of aluminum by-product from potassium feldspar and phosphogypsum involves pulverizing potassium-feldspar, mixing with phosphogypsum, additives, and 14-22 wt.% modifier to obtain raw meal, roasting raw meal in a kiln to obtain clinker, grinding the clinker, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, adding carbon dioxide to the liquid until white precipitate is no longer generated, filtering off the white precipitate, washing and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide, calcining to obtain alumina, performing flotation of the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation, calcining the floated sulfide, transferring the generated flue gas to a sulfuric acid production system to prepare sulfuric acid, adding remaining residue obtained after floatation, to a blender, adding urea, monoammonium phosphate, boric acid, magnesium sulfate, ferric sulfate, manganese sulfate, zinc sulfate, sodium selenite, and humic acid, stirring, air-drying, and granulating to obtain special fertilizer.
   USE - Recovery of aluminum by-product from potassium feldspar and phosphogypsum, and producing special fertilizer for eggplant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum for overall utilization, improves added-value of potassium feldspar and phosphogypsum, and provides fertilizer which does not cause damage to acidity and alkalinity of eggplant planting soil, and recovers aluminum with high yield, and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of aluminum by-product from potassium feldspar and phosphogypsum involves pulverizing potassium-feldspar to 140-200 mesh, mixing with phosphogypsum, additives, and 14-22 wt.% modifier to obtain raw meal, transferring raw meal to a kiln, roasting to obtain clinker, grinding the clinker, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, adding carbon dioxide to the liquid until white precipitate is no longer generated, filtering off the white precipitate, washing and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide, calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain alumina, performing flotation of the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation, calcining the floated sulfide, transferring the generated flue gas to a sulfuric acid production system to prepare sulfuric acid, adding 100-120 pts. wt. remaining residue obtained after floatation, to a blender, adding 80-100 pts. wt. urea, 50-70 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. boric acid, 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.05-0.1 pt. wt. ferric sulfate, 0.02-0.04 pt. wt. manganese sulfate, 0.04-0.1 pt. wt. zinc sulfate, 0.4-0.8 pt. wt. sodium selenite, and 10-20 pts. wt. humic acid, stirring, air-drying, and granulating in a granulator to obtain special fertilizer. The weight ratio of phosphogypsum, and potassium feldspar in raw meal is 0.8-2:1. The additive ratio of the additive according to molecular ratio of sum of sodium oxide, alumina, and ferric oxide contained in the raw meal, is 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln, or industrial vertical kiln.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The potassium feldspar comprises 10-18 %mass potassium oxide, 16-20 %mass alumina, and 60-70 %mass silica. The liquid-solid volume ratio during dissolution is 4-8:1. The fertilizer preferably comprises 110 pts. wt. residue, 90 pts. wt. urea, 60 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 7.5 pts. wt. boric acid, 0.25 pt. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.075 pt. wt. ferric sulfate, 0.03 pt. wt. manganese sulfate, 0.07 pt. wt. zinc sulfate, 0.6 pt. wt. sodium selenite, and 15 pts. wt. humic acid. Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate, or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon, or coal gangue. Preferred Conditions: The raw meal is roasted at 800-1300 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The aluminum hydroxide is calcined at 900-1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The stirring time is 10-20 minutes. Preferred Process: The floated sulfide is placed in 40-44% oxygen-rich environment, and calcined at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The flue gas generated by the calcination is catalyzed using vanadium pentoxide, and then absorbed using concentrated sulfuric acid to prepare sulfuric acid.

TI Culturing Pleurotus nebrodensis strain comprises cutting the branches, soaking the treated wood segment in the nutrient solution, smashing the soaked wood piece, cooling the test tube and placing in carbon dioxide incubator.
AU LI X
   LIU X
   ZHAO J
   LI C
   GAO X
   WANG Z
AE SHANDONG HENGFA EDIBLE FUNGUS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG FUHE FLORA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG KECHUANG EDIBLE MUSHROOM IND (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018884668
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing Pleurotus nebrodensis strain comprises (i) cutting the branches with similar diameters into multiple wood segments of uniform length and performing roughening process transversely and/or longitudinally along the surface of the wood segment by a ring cutter to form a groove or a pit on the surface of the wood segment to increase the fixing area, (ii) loading the treated wood segment into the soaking container, adding the pre-prepared nutrient solution to submerge the wood segment, (iii) smashing the soaked wood piece in a pre-conditioned hot medium and inserting into a test tube, sealing the test tube and placing in a sterilizing boiler, and sterilizing at 125-130 degrees C for 30-45 minutes, (iv) cooling the test tube to room temperature in an inoculation chamber and inoculating the wood segment under sterile conditions, (v) placing the test tube in an incubator and regulating the temperature to 21-25 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration to 3000-3500 parts/million.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing Pleurotus nebrodensis strain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and ensures the healthy growth and rapid propagation of strain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing Pleurotus nebrodensis strain, comprising (i) cutting the branches with similar diameters into multiple wood segments of uniform length and performing roughening process transversely and/or longitudinally along the surface of the wood segment by a ring cutter to form a groove or a pit on the surface of the wood segment to increase the fixing area, (ii) loading the treated wood segment into the soaking container, adding the pre-prepared nutrient solution to submerge the wood segment, finally soaking in a vacuum environment for 2-3 hours, evacuating the wood section for every 30 minutes during the vacuum soaking process to accelerate the absorption of the nutrient solution by the wood section, where the nutrient solution is obtained by mixing 850 ml water, 50 g bean dregs, 15 g bark, 18 g trehalose, 3.5 g urea, 3 g monopotassium phosphate, 2 g dipotassium hydrogen phosphate and 2 g magnesium sulfate, heating to 85 degrees C for 30 minutes and filtering, (iii) smashing the soaked wood piece in a pre-conditioned hot medium and inserting into a test tube, sealing the test tube and placing in a sterilizing boiler, and sterilizing at 125-130 degrees C for 30-45 minutes, (iv) cooling the test tube to room temperature in an inoculation chamber and inoculating the wood segment under sterile conditions, (v) placing the test tube in an incubator and regulating the temperature to 21-25 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration to 3000-3500 parts/million.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The culture medium comprises 32-40 pts. wt. dreg, 10-15 pt. wt. bark, 5-7 pts. wt. bran, 4-6.5 pts. wt. bean dregs, 3-5 pts. wt. corn flour, 2-3 pts. wt. agar,1-2 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium superphosphate, 1-1.5 pts. wt. trehalose, 0.5-1 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. urea and water (remaining amount). The culture medium is in the form of paste. The temperature required for the low temperature stimulation is 3-6 degrees C less than the culture temperature. Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises entirely immersing the wood segment in the culture medium to form a medium layer film on the outer surface of the wood segment. The step (iv) comprises stimulating the growing hyphae at a low temperature during the culturing process and converting the hyphae from vegetative growth to reproductive growth, filling the strain on the wood segment with bag and preparing the shoot.

TI Porous carbonaceous particles for manufacturing            carbonaceous monolith used for extracting carbon            dioxide from gas composition comprise pores including            micropores and macropores, where particles have average            diameter within micrometer range.
AU FINSY V
   DUBOIS E P
   VANDERVEKEN Y
   CHAPOTOT A
   VANDERVECKEN Y
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   UNIV VRIJE BRUSSELS (ULBR-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   UNIV VRIJE BRUSSELS (ULBR-C)
GA 201845188R
AB    NOVELTY - Porous carbonaceous particles (I) comprise                pores including micropores and macropores, where                the particles have average diameter of 15-100 mu m,                determined by laser diffraction.
   USE - Porous carbonaceous particles for                manufacturing carbonaceous monolith used for                extracting carbon dioxide from gas composition by                selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide gas                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The particles exhibit microporosity for                improved selective adsorption of gas and                macroporosity for enhanced mass transfer of gas                and/or gaseous mixture comprising the gas. The                monolith manufactured from the particles (III)                allows the machining of low wall thickness grooved                monolith having good mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) size reduction of porous carbonaceous                particles (II) having average diameter of 150-800                mu m, which involves cryogenically freezing the                particles (II), and grinding frozen particles to                obtain particles of reduced size, which are                preferably the particles (I);
   (2) porous particles (III) comprising greater                than or equal to 1 vinylidene chloride polymer                having a melting point, where particles (III) have                average diameter of 20-140 mu m, a span of less                than or equal to 2, and macropore volume measured                by mercury (Hg) porosimetry of greater than or                equal to 0.05 cm3/g;
   (3) size reduction of porous particles (IV)                having macropores, comprising greater than or equal                to 1 vinylidene chloride polymer having a melting                point and average diameter of 170-800 mu m, which                involves cryogenically freezing particles (IV) of                vinylidene chloride polymer, and grinding frozen                particles (IV) to obtain particles of reduced size,                where obtained porous particles comprising                vinylidene chloride polymer are preferably the                particles (III);
   (4) manufacture of porous particles having                macropores and comprising vinylidene chloride                polymer having a melting point, which involves                free-radical suspension polymerization of greater                than or equal to 1 monomer comprising vinylidene                chloride in the presence of 0.13-1.5 wt.%                cellulosic dispersing agent based on the total                weight of the monomer, where manufactured porous                particles comprising vinylidene chloride polymer                are preferably the particles (III);
   (5) manufacture of porous carbonaceous                particles which involves causing the pyrolysis of                vinylidene chloride polymer comprised in porous                particles (III);
   (6) manufacture of porous carbonaceous                particles which involves manufacturing porous                particles and causing the pyrolysis of the                vinylidene chloride polymer comprised in the                so-manufactured porous particles, where porous                carbonaceous particles are preferably the particles                (I);
   (7) manufacture of porous carbonaceous                monolith which involves (i) preparing precursor                material comprising the particles (III), (ii)                forming shaped body (S) comprising aggregates of                particles (III), (iii) introducing the shaped body                in a furnace, and (iv) causing the pyrolysis of                vinylidene chloride polymer in the furnace until                the porous carbonaceous monolith is obtained;
   (8) shaped body (S) comprising aggregates of                particles (III) and having macropores; and
   (9) porous carbonaceous monolith comprising                aggregates of carbonaceous particles (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The particles (I) have                  a span of less than or equal to 2.5, preferably                  less than or equal to 1.5; a macropore volume,                  measured by Hg porosimetry, of greater than or                  equal to 0.05 cm3/g; and a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                  (BET) surface area of greater than or equal to                  800 m2/g. Porous carbonaceous monolith has a                  total macropore volume measured by Hg porosimetry                  of greater than or equal to 0.12 cm3/g and BET                  surface area of greater than or equal to 800                  m2/g. Porous carbonaceous monolith has a                  honeycomb structure. Preferred Processes: In the                  monolith manufacturing process, step (ii)                  includes forming shaped body, by concurrently                  applying to the precursor material, previously                  introduced in a mold, at a pressure (P) of 10-300                  bars, preferably 10-150 bars, when vinylidene                  chloride polymer is a homopolymer, and at 10-150                  bars when vinylidene chloride polymer is a                  copolymer, and temperature (T1) ranging from                  T1,min=20 degrees C to T1,max=Tm-50 degrees C,                  where Tm is melting point of vinylidene chloride                  polymer. Step (iv) includes (iva) bringing the                  temperature of shaped body up to a temperature                  (T2) strictly above T2,min=Tm-50 degrees C and                  strictly below T2,max=Tm; (ivb) maintaining the                  shaped body, at the temperature T2 under inert                  gas flow, to cause the pyrolysis of the                  vinylidene chloride polymer and the formation of                  an infusible char; (ivc) bringing the temperature                  of the infusible char up to a temperature (T3)                  strictly above T3,min=Tm and strictly below                  T3,max=1300 degrees C; and (ivd) maintaining the                  infusible char, at the temperature T3 under inert                  gas flow, to cause the pyrolysis of the                  vinylidene chloride polymer, obtaining the porous                  carbonaceous monolith structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: Vinylidene chloride                  polymer is a homopolymer.

PT P
PN CN107581517-A
TI Preparing ultra-high pressure low sodium salt pickled duck product, by preparing pickling liquid by dissolving salt in distilled water, performing ultra-high pressure pickling of duck and spreading spices extract, and baking product.
AU CHEN Z
AE ANHUI KANGLETAI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201807690C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing ultra-high pressure low sodium salt pickled duck product, involves (a) preparing pickling liquid by dissolving salt in distilled water to obtain salt solution, and adding potassium chloride, white sugar, cooking wine, and yellow rice wine, cooking mixture, cooling to room temperature and filtering cooled product, (b) performing ultra-high pressure pickling by pickling duck body using pickling liquid, and (c) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of e.g. cinnamon, and spreading spices extract on duck body, and baking and cooling product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-high pressure low sodium salt pickled duck product which is used for providing antioxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares product which has anticorrosion effects, and color protection effect, reduces negative effects of ultra-high pressure treatment, replaces sodium salt by potassium chloride, improves tenderness of muscle and reduces negative impact of calcium chloride on meat quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing ultra-high pressure low sodium salt pickled duck product, involves (a) preparing pickling liquid by dissolving 18-27 pts. wt. salt in distilled water to obtain 1.5-2.5% salt solution, and adding 3-9 pts. wt. potassium chloride, 4-9 pts. wt. white sugar, 13-25 pts. wt. cooking wine, 15-27 pts. wt. yellow rice wine, 2-3 pts. wt. Illicium verum, 2-3 pts. wt. pepper and 3-5 pts. wt. ginger, cooking mixture for 20-30 minutes, cooling to room temperature and filtering cooled product, (b) performing ultra-high pressure pickling by washing pre-cooled duck body, and adding into pickling liquid in a ratio of 1:1, performing ultra-high pressure treatment, and removing duck body after soaked in pickling liquid, and pickling for 6-13 hours, (c) washing clove, cinnamon and pepper, crushing and sieving using a sieve of less than 20 mesh size, and adding into extraction kettle, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 0.5-2 hours, slowly releasing carbon dioxide from kettle, reducing pressure to atmospheric pressure and collecting the product with ethanol, (d) placing pickled duck body into hot water at 30-40 degrees C, rinsing for 2-3 times, washing using clean water, drying washed product, spreading the spices extract obtained in steps (c) on inner and outer surface of dried ducks, and hanging into ventilation for air-drying, and (e) adjusting the oven temperature to 28-32 degrees C, placing the duck body into oven at 50-55 degrees C for 1-2 hours, until the moisture content reaches 38-42%, cooling duck body, and vacuum-packaging product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves removing hair and internal organs of duck body, washing using water, adding cold water at 8-13 degrees C, precooling for 30-40 minutes, placing in cold water at 0-5 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, centrifuging and drying cooled product. The ultra-high pressure pickling is performed at 200-300 MPa for 10-20 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed at 35-45 degrees C, 30-35 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-25 l/hour.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-H01P; D03-H01T2C; D03-H02D; D03-L

PT P
PN WO2020120305-A1; TW202021937-A; IN202147021309-A; CN113166010-A; KR2021102243-A; EP3894382-A1; BR112021009797-A2; US2022024841-A1; EA39838-B1
TI Preparing ethylene glycol from ethylene by            converting ethylene to ethylene oxide, absorbing            ethylene oxide in aqueous absorbent, converting aqueous            ethylene oxide stream to ethylene carbonate stream,            hydrolyzing, and dehydrating.
AU VAN KRUCHTEN E M G
   BOONS P J G
   VAN KRUCHTEN E M G A
   KRUCHTEN E M G
   VAN K E M G
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2020539908
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene glycol is prepared from ethylene by                (i) converting ethylene in the presence of oxygen,                epoxidation catalyst, and chloride moderator to                ethylene oxide in an ethylene oxide reactor; (ii)                absorbing ethylene oxide in aqueous absorbent and                then stripping absorbent to provide aqueous                ethylene oxide stream; (iii) converting aqueous                ethylene oxide stream in the presence of catalyst                and carbon dioxide in a carboxylation reactor to                ethylene carbonate stream; (iv) converting ethylene                carbonate stream in the presence of catalyst in                hydrolysis reactor to ethylene glycol stream and                carbon dioxide stream; (v) removing water from                ethylene glycol stream in dehydration column to                form dehydrated ethylene glycol stream and waste                water stream; and (vi) purifying dehydrated                ethylene glycol stream in glycol distillation                column to form purified ethylene glycol product                stream and catalyst recycle stream. In step (iii)                and/or step (iv), the catalyst is an iodide                catalyst or a bromide catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of ethylene glycol from                ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to add additional                iodide or bromide catalyst or to add substantially                less additional iodide or bromide catalyst, where                at the same time, the undesired organic chloride                compounds can be removed, and the recovery of                ethylene glycol can be maximized. Contact between                2-chloroethanol from the resulting condensate                stream and catalyst from the ethylene glycol stream                is advantageously avoided, preventing formation of                inorganic chloride (e.g. potassium chloride).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ethylene glycol is prepared from ethylene by                (i) converting ethylene in the presence of oxygen,                epoxidation catalyst, and chloride moderator to                ethylene oxide in an ethylene oxide reactor; (ii)                absorbing ethylene oxide in aqueous absorbent and                then stripping absorbent to provide aqueous                ethylene oxide stream; (iii) converting aqueous                ethylene oxide stream in the presence of catalyst                and carbon dioxide in a carboxylation reactor to                ethylene carbonate stream; (iv) converting ethylene                carbonate stream in the presence of catalyst in                hydrolysis reactor to ethylene glycol stream and                carbon dioxide stream; (v) removing water from                ethylene glycol stream in dehydration column to                form dehydrated ethylene glycol stream and waste                water stream; and (vi) purifying dehydrated                ethylene glycol stream in glycol distillation                column to form purified ethylene glycol product                stream and catalyst recycle stream. In step (iii)                and/or step (iv), the catalyst is an iodide                catalyst or a bromide catalyst. Carbon dioxide                stream resulting from step (iv) comprises carbon                dioxide, water, 2-chloroethanol, and ethylene                glycol and additionally comprises 2-iodoethanol or                2-bromoethanol and is condensed in a condenser to                form a recycle carbon dioxide stream and a                condensate stream; and carbon dioxide stream                resulting from step (iv), or the condensate stream,                or the waste water stream resulting from step (v)                is contacted with an alkali metal containing basic                compound to convert 2-iodoethanol or 2-bromoethanol                into alkali metal iodide or alkali metal bromide,                and the resulting mixture comprising water,                2-chloroethanol, and ethylene glycol and                additionally comprising alkali metal iodide or                alkali metal bromide is dehydrated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Temperature during                  contacting the carbon dioxide stream resulting                  from step (iv), or the condensate stream, or the                  waste water stream resulting from step (v) with                  the alkali metal containing basic compound is                  20-200 degrees C, preferably 50-150 degrees C.                  Time period of contacting the carbon dioxide                  stream resulting from step (iv), or the                  condensate stream, or the waste water stream                  resulting from step (v) with the alkali metal                  containing basic compound is 0.1 s to 5 hours,                  preferably 1-30 minutes. Preferred Processes:                  Carbon dioxide stream resulting from step (iv) is                  contacted in the condenser with an alkali metal                  containing basic compound, and condensate stream                  comprising water, 2-chloroethanol, and ethylene                  glycol and additionally comprising alkali metal                  iodide or alkali metal bromide is dehydrated.                  Condensate stream is dehydrated in step (v) and                  is fed to the dehydration column at a position                  which is above the position at which the ethylene                  glycol stream is fed. Ethylene glycol stream                  comprises alkali metal containing basic compound,                  and condensate stream comprises water,                  2-chloroethanol, and ethylene glycol and                  additionally comprises 2-iodoethanol or                  2-bromoethanol and is contacted with the ethylene                  glycol stream, and the resulting ethylene glycol                  stream comprising water, 2-chloroethanol, and                  ethylene glycol and additionally comprising                  alkali metal iodide or alkali metal bromide is                  fed to and dehydrated in step (v). Condensate                  stream comprises water, 2-chloroethanol, and                  ethylene glycol and additionally comprises                  2-iodoethanol or 2-bromoethanol and is contacted                  with an alkali metal containing basic compound,                  and the resulting condensate stream comprising                  water, 2-chloroethanol and ethylene glycol and                  additionally comprising alkali metal iodide or                  alkali metal bromide is dehydrated. Resulting                  condensate stream is dehydrated in step (v) and                  is fed to the dehydration column at a position                  which is above the position at which the ethylene                  glycol stream is fed.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E10-E04B; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-E01; E11-F02B; E11-F06; E11-G03; E11-G05; N01-A; N07-C01; N07-D02B; N07-D07; N07-F06A; N07-F07

PN CN115264979-A
TI Pressurizing-type carbon dioxide refrigeration            system, has vortex compressor whose inlet is connected            with evaporator, where outlet of vortex compressor is            connected with low-temperature compressor and reservoir            is connected with intermediate temperature            compressor.
AU FANG C
   XIE W
   DAI X
AE BEIJER REF WUXI CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022D8222S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a vortex expansion device (8)                provided with a vortex expander (81) and a vortex                compressor (82) that are coaxially arranged, where                an inlet of the vortex expander is connected with a                gas cooler (4) and an outlet of the vortex expander                is connected with a reservoir (5). An inlet of the                vortex compressor is connected with an evaporator                (7). An outlet of the vortex compressor is                connected with a low-temperature compressor (1).                The reservoir is connected with an intermediate                temperature compressor (3).
   USE - Pressurizing-type carbon dioxide refrigeration                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system recovers a part of pressure work of                a transcritical carbon dioxide refrigeration system                without increasing additional energy consumption to                pre-compress return steam of the evaporator, thus                reducing energy consumption of the compressor, and                improving energy efficiency of the refrigeration                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                pressurizing-type carbon dioxide refrigeration                system.
   1Low-temperature compressor
   2Sequentially exhaust cooler
   3Intermediate temperature compressor
   4Gas cooler
   5Reservoir
   6Sequentially expansion valve
   7Evaporator
   8Vortex expansion device
   81Vortex expander
   82Vortex compressor

PT P
PN KR2441493-B1
TI Production of dry ice used as refrigerant,            involves supplying liquefied carbon dioxide gas to            pelletizer, producing pellets, producing dry ice by            putting pellets in reformer, and packaging dry ice in            box made of film containing polypropylene resin.
AU MIN K K
AE INFINITE TRADING CO LTD (INFI-Non-standard)
GA 2022B5904M
AB    NOVELTY - Production of dry ice involves storing                liquefied carbon dioxide, supplying the liquefied                carbon dioxide gas to the pelletizer, producing                pellets using the pelletizer, producing dry ice                comprising shapes by putting the pellets in a                reformer, and packaging the dry ice by moving                through a conveyor. The pelletizer comprises a                discharge unit for discharging the cooled liquefied                carbon dioxide gas. The packaging step is packaged                in a box using a packaging device. The box further                comprises a film attached to the inside and                outside.    USE - Production of dry ice used as                refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently manufactures dry ice at                a high speed because the pellets that escape to the                outside are minimized.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The pelletizer                  comprises a gas inlet receiving the stored                  liquefied carbon dioxide gas, a gas discharge                  unit for discharging the liquefied carbon dioxide                  gas that has not been cooled, and a cooling unit                  for cooling the liquefied carbon dioxide gas. The                  discharge portion is formed in a mesh shape to                  produce pellets of the liquefied carbon dioxide                  gas after the cooling has been completed. The                  collection unit comprises a collection hole and                  collection wings installed on both sides of the                  collection hole, and the collection wings are                  formed at an obtuse angle with the collection                  hole.

AB    NOVELTY - Non-noble metal coordination polymer material                based on 1,1'-ferrocene dicarboxylic acid and                cerium (III) of chemical formula (I), is new
   USE - The coordination polymer material is useful in                the field of photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coordination polymer material exhibits                excellent carbon dioxide reduction performance                under visible light irradiation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-noble metal coordination polymer material                based on 1,1'-ferrocene dicarboxylic acid and                cerium (III) of chemical formula (Ce2(fcd)3 H2O)                H2O (I), monoclinic, and P21/c space group, is new.                The unit cell volume is V = 3506.171 A3. The unit                cell parameters are
   a = 14.99500 A;
   b = 10.86300 A;
   c = 21.52500 A;
   alpha = 90degrees;
   beta = 90.3100degrees;
   gamma = 90degrees;
   Z = 4; and
   fcd = dianion (Fe(C5H4COO)2)2- formed by the                removal of two protons from 1,1'-ferrocene                dicarboxylic acid.
