   (1) a method for preparing coordination                polymer material; and
   (2) a use method of coordination polymer                material comprising efficiently reducing carbon                dioxide to generate carbon monoxide (CO), formic                acid and hydrogen under visible light irradiation                in the presence of sacrificial agent and                photosensitizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  coordination polymer material comprises mixing                  1,1'-ferrocene dicarboxylic acid, cerium salt and                  alkali source in the inner tank of the                  hydrothermal reaction kettle according to the                  molar ratio of 1.5:1:0-1.5, adding 10 ml                  deionized water, stirring to make it evenly                  mixed, equipping the hydrothermal reaction                  kettle, transferring to the reaction furnace,                  heating to 110degrees Celsius for 1-2 days, then                  slowly cooling to room temperature, and washing                  alternately with water and ethanol to obtain a                  non-noble metal coordination polymer material                  based on 1,1'-ferrocene dicarboxylic acid and                  cerium (III).Preferred Components: The alkali source is                  lithium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or                  triethanolamine. PT P
PN WO2021252966-A1; US2021386029-A1
TI Supplying algae cultivation fluid with nutrients e.g. carbon dioxide and nitrogen, involves culturing algae in channel having bottom surface, opposing side walls coupled to bottom surface and algae cultivation fluid and applying bore waves.
AU HAZLEBECK D A
   RICKMAN W
   HAZLEBECK P
AE GLOBAL ALGAE TECHNOLOGY LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
   GLOBAL ALGAE TECHNOLOGY LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
GA 2021E34353
AB    NOVELTY - Supplying an algae involves culturing algae in channel having a bottom surface, opposing side walls coupled to the bottom surface, and an algae cultivation fluid disposed in the at least one channel and applying bore waves through the algae cultivation fluid at a bore wave frequency sufficient to disrupt an air-liquid interface of the algae cultivation fluid to induce direct absorption of atmospheric carbon dioxide or atmospheric nitrogen from air into the algae cultivation fluid, where a majority of the carbon or nitrogen in the algae is from the atmospheric carbon dioxide or atmospheric nitrogen.
   USE - Method for supplying algae cultivation fluid with nutrients e.g. carbon dioxide and nitrogen, directly from atmosphere or air in an algae farm, but not limited to blue-green algae and cyanobacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces operational costs and environmental impacts, as well as provides greater flexibility in locating algae farms increased biomass productivity, reduces energy, increased response and recovery to perturbations, and reduced impact of perturbation on the biomass productivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Condition: The bottom surface of the channel is slope less than 0.5%. The method further involves passing the bore waves through one or more air-liquid mixing device configured within the at least one channel. The at least one channel involves from one air-liquid mixing device for every 300 ft2 of surface of the at least one channel to one air-liquid mixing device for every 400,000 ft2 of surface of the at least one channel. The bore wave frequency, intensity, or a combination is adjusted to obtain a minimum bicarbonate concentration in the algae cultivation fluid from 1-150 mM. The equivalent bicarbonate concentration of sodium ions in the algae cultivation fluid is 10-500 mM. The equivalent bicarbonate concentration of sodium ions in the algae cultivation fluid and the bore wave frequency are selected to maintain a difference between a maximum and minimum pH during day light hours of less than 0.8 pH units. The bore wave frequency, intensity, or a combination is adjusted to maintain a pH in the algae cultivation fluid of less than 11 by displacing a gate in a bore wave generator. The method further involves displacing the gate at a frequency from 10-300 seconds to apply the bore waves through the algae cultivation fluid, measuring at least one process parameter and adjusting the bore wave frequency, intensity, or a combination based on the at least one parameter or rate of change of the at least one parameter. Preferred Components: The air-liquid mixing devices are powered by the flow of the bore wave. The rate of air-liquid mixing is adjusted during the cultivation to reduce the energy consumption. The solar energy is used to power the one or more air-liquid mixing device, and where a rate of air-liquid mixing is greater during times of higher solar radiation relative to times of lower solar radiation. The air-liquid mixing device generates air bubbles in the algae cultivation fluid. The bubble generation rate is increased when the bore wave passes the air-liquid mixing device, and is decreased during a period in between the bore waves. The parameter is selected from a pH, a dissolved oxygen content, a bicarbonate concentration, a nitrogen concentration, solar intensity, algae growth rate, turbidity, optical density, and temperature.

AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2021934581
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a piece of software that calculates the expected carbon dioxide emission resulting from per unit mass of load for a particular flight based on historic data from load sheets of flights. The notification is sent to the traveler informing them of the carbon dioxide emission resulting from per unit mass of load for that flight. The load sheet data is retrieved for flight to estimate the actual carbon dioxide emission resulting from per unit mass of load for the journey just completed.
   USE - System for reducing carbon emissions from air travel through direct traveler interactions to minimize aircraft payload.
   ADVANTAGE - The system relies on the notification of travelers about the carbon impact of per unit mass luggage load for their flight. The notifications are aimed at influencing passengers to develop a habit of packing luggage more conservatively thus reducing luggage load and hence lower fuel burn.

PT P
PN CN113201761-A
TI Preparing formic acid by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by microbial production involves taking iron-reducing reducing bacteria as anode electron donor, taking solution containing the high-purity carbon dioxide gas.
AU LI P
   WANG L
   YANG J
   XUE M
   ZHU G
   JIA Y
   LIU Z
   ZHAO C
AE UNIV XIAN (UYXI-Non-standard)
GA 202195267Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing formic acid by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) by microbial production involves taking the iron-reducing reducing bacteria as the anode electron donor, taking the solution containing the high-purity CO2 gas as the cathode substrate, putting the anode substrate into the anode electrode carbon felt and placing the cathode substrate carbon felt the cathode electrode. The two electrodes are connected by the lead, under the 30-50 degrees C, the electron generated by the anode is supplied carbon felt the cathode electrode through the lead. The carbon felt of the cathode electrode is electrically catalyzed and reduced the CO2 hydrogenation reaction of formic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing the formic acid fuel cell prepared by the microorganism electrogenesis catalytic reduction CO2 is conducted in biofuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses microorganism self-generating electricity, without extra consumption of electric energy to realize the catalytic reduction of CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a biofuel cell is used for preparing formic acid by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) by microbial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The suspension of the iron-reducing reducing bacteria comprises the following steps: 20-40 millimoles/liter (mmol/L) of PIPES buffer solution, 10 milliliters (mL) of iron-reducing bacteria suspension, and 5 mL of electron donor. The concentration of the iron reducing bacteria suspension is (1.5x 109)-(3x 109) cells/mL. The concentration of the electron donor is 1 mole (mol)/L. The solution containing high-purity CO2 gas is as follows: 80 mL, 0.5-1.0 mol/L of potassium carbonate (KHCO3) or sodium carbonate (NaHCO3) solution of 99.99 % high pure CO2 gas. Preferred Components: The electron donor is one of lactic acid, sodium lactate, acetic acid, citric acid, glucose. The lead is titanium wire lead. The biofuel cell comprises a chamber, both of which are sealed, where the cathode chamber is internally provided with cathode substrate. The anode chamber is internally provided with anode substrate, and a proton exchange membrane is arranged between the cathode chamber and the anode chamber. The anode chamber and the cathode chamber are provided with the carbon felt electrode. The two of the electrode carbon felt connected with the titanium wire. The volume of the cathode chamber is equal to that of the anode chamber. Preferred Process: The material of the electrode carbon felt prepared according to the following steps: The (3-6)x (3-6) centimeter square (cm2) carbon felt is cut, cleaned under the 90-120 degrees C, the volume fraction of the nitric acid (HNO3) solution with the volume fraction of 10-20 vol.% is used to carbon felt for 5-8 hours, the distilled water is used to wash until the cleaning liquid is colorless and transparent. The carbon felt after cleaning treatment is in the 90-120 degrees C solution with a volume fraction of 10 vol.%, boiled for 3-5 hours, the surface carbon felt is washed in distilled water, dried to constant weight to obtain the electrode material carbon felt. The 0.2-0.5 grams (g) of metal in metal oxide catalyst is loaded on the cathode electrode of the carbon felt, the Nafion is mixed with isopropanol according to the volume ratio of 1:20 to obtain a mixed solution, the metal in metal oxide catalyst is mixed into the mixed solution, ultrasonically mixed and uniformly coated on the cathode carbon felt the electrode material. The metal in metal oxide catalyst is core-shell structure, which is one of iron (Fe) in ferric oxide (Fe2O3) nanowire, cobalt (Co) in cobalt tetraoxide (Co3O4), nickel (Ni) in nickel oxide (NiO). The metal in metal oxide catalyst is prepared according to the following steps: the Sodium borohydride (NaBH4) is dissolved in distilled water to prepare NaBH4 solution. The metal salt is dissolved in distilled water to prepare a metal salt solution. The NaBH4 solution is dripped into the metal salt solution and continuously stirred, after finishing dripping, standing until the bubble disappears, then filtered and washed by water, after washing with absolute ethyl alcohol, the infrared drying is used under the argon atmosphere, where the metal salt is ferric chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3x 6H2O), cobalt nitrate hexahydrate (Co(NO3)2x 6H2O) or nickel nitrate hexahydrate (Ni(NO3)2x 6H2O), when the metal salt is FeCl3x 6H2O, the prepared metal in metal oxide catalyst is Fe @Fe2O3 nanowire, when the metal salt is Co(NO3) 2 asterisk 6H2O, the prepared metal in metal oxide catalyst is Co in Co3O4. The metal salt is Ni(NO3)2x 6H2O, the prepared metal in metal oxide catalyst is Ni is NiO. The mass ratio of NaBH4 solution to FeCl3x 6H2O, Co(NO3)x 6H2O, Ni(NO3)2x 6H2O is 2:1.

PN CN112850775-A
TI Recycling carbon-ammonia in production of rare            earth oxide by stripping and distilling ammonia from            generated ammonia nitrogen wastewater, carbonizing            aqueous ammonia and generated carbon dioxide, and            precipitating ammonium bicarbonate.
AU GUO J
   GUO Y
   CHEN D
   LAI L
   ZHOU J
   LAI Y
   CHEN H
   WANG M
   ZHANG J
   WU Y
   HU X
AE GANZHOU NONFERROUS METALLURGY INST (GANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202161826N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling carbon-ammonia in the                production process of rare earth oxide, involves:                performing rare earth oxide production process                including performing organic phase saponification,                extraction, hydrochloric acid stripping,                precipitation and calcination, where the                saponification agent for saponification is aqueous                ammonia, and the precipitating agent for                precipitation is an aqueous ammonia bicarbonate                solution; and generating ammonia nitrogen                wastewater during the extraction and precipitation                process, and generating carbon dioxide during the                precipitation and calcination process, and the                method comprises: stripping and distilling ammonia                from the ammonia nitrogen wastewater to obtain                aqueous ammonia; and mixing the aqueous ammonia,                water, and the carbon dioxide for carbonization to                obtain an ammonium bicarbonate solution, and                returning the obtained ammonium bicarbonate                solution to the precipitation process for use as a                precipitant.
   USE - The method is used for recycling                carbon-ammonia in the production process of rare                earth oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the treatment of ammonia                nitrogen wastewater, reduces carbon dioxide                emissions, reduces the consumption of ammonium                bicarbonate, realizes the closed-loop utilization                of carbon ammonia resources, and reduces the                preparation cost of rare earth oxides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process of stripping                  and distilling ammonia includes adjusting the pH                  value of the ammonia nitrogen wastewater to                  11-12, preheating and sending it to an ammonia                  distilling tower for stripping and distilling                  ammonia. The carbonization process includes                  passing carbon dioxide and aqueous ammonia into                  the bottom of the carbonization tower, filling                  the top of the carbonization tower with water,                  contacting aqueous ammonia, carbon dioxide and                  water in the carbonization tower for                  carbonization to obtain the carbonization liquid,                  returning the carbonization liquid to the top of                  the carbonization tower to continue contact                  carbonization after cooling down, and discharging                  the carbonization liquid from the carbonization                  tower until the concentration of ammonium                  bicarbonate in the carbonization liquid is                  1.2-2.2 mol/l. Preferred Conditions: The pressure                  of the stripping and distilling ammonia is                  0.03-0.06 MPa, and the temperature is 80-100                  degrees C. The ammonia stripping and distilling                  is carried out in an ammonia distillation tower.                  The preheating temperature is 70-80 degrees C.                  The carbon dioxide generated during the                  calcination process is sequentially de-dusted and                  cooled, and then mixed with aqueous ammonia for                  carbonization. The carbonization is performed in                  a carbonization tower. The carbonization                  temperature is 20-40 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The regulator for adjusting the pH                  value of the ammonia nitrogen wastewater is one                  or more of sodium hydroxide solution, potassium                  hydroxide solution, calcium oxide solution and                  calcium hydroxide solution.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

   USE - The method is useful for producing renewable natural gas based on scenery biogas resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves wind and light intermittent renewable power consumption and low carbon in electricity-gas interconnection system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution includes potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The biogas purification device comprises a series of desulfurization equipment, dehydration equipment and filtration equipment, where biogas enters the desulfurization equipment for desulfurization. The dehydration equipment is an adsorption bed on which a desiccant is laid. The dehydrated biogas is filtered through the filtration equipment to remove entrained impurities. The desulfurization equipment is a dry iron oxide desulfurization tower. The desiccant is silica gel and/or alumina desiccant. The biogas purification device uses a pressure water washing method to purify the biogas. The electrolyzed water device uses alkaline solution as an electrolyte, and water is electrolyzed during the electrolysis process to produce hydrogen and oxygen. In the electric gas production device, carbon dioxide and hydrogen react to form methane under condition that the catalyst is heated.

PT P
PN CN110280294-A; WO2021008196-A1
TI Catalyst used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises nitrogen-doped carbon nano-fiber composite iron sulfide particles.
AU WANG X
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING EAST CHINA NORMAL PHOTOELE (UYZH-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL INT ACAD OPTOELE (USCN-C)
GA 201984190V
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises nitrogen-doped carbon nano-fiber composite iron sulfide particles.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high electrocatalytic activity and selectivity for carbon dioxide reduction, improves the energy efficiency for carbon dioxide utilization, is prepared by simple and environmentally-friendly process, and has high yield and wide application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) taking polyacrylonitrile and ferric nitrate in N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring for 12-24 hours, taking uniform solution, and preparing fiber membrane precursor using electrostatic spinning method, (ii) placing the fiber film precursor prepared in a tubular furnace, calcining under a mixed gas atmosphere consisting of argon and hydrogen, and cooling the furnace after the calcination is completed to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber composite iron particles, and (3) mixing and grinding the elemental sulfur powder and the nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber composite iron particles prepared in the step (2) to obtain mixed powder, placing the mixed powder in the tubular furnace, calcining under an argon atmosphere, and cooling with the furnace after the calcination is completed to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber composite iron sulfide particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The step (i) comprises 1-2 g polyacrylonitrile, 1-2 g ferric nitrate and 10-20 ml N,N-dimethylformamide. The amount of elemental sulfur powder in step (iii) is 0.5-1 g, and nitrogen-doped carbon nano-fiber composite iron particles are 0.1-0.5 g. Preferred Conditions: The electrostatic spinning method is carried out by sucking the prepared uniform solution into a syringe, installing a needle head with model number of 20, discharging air bubbles, setting the voltage to 10-20 kV, flow speed to 0.02-0.08 mm/minute, spinning distance at 13-20 cm, and humidity of 20-50% at room temperature, and spinning after 12-24 hours to obtain fiber membrane precursor. The gas flow velocity of mixed gas composed of argon and hydrogen in step (ii) is 100-200 ml/minute, where the volume ratio of the argon gas and hydrogen gas is (5-10):1. The calcination process is carried out by heating at 500-1000 degrees C at a speed of 1-5 degrees CoC/minute, and maintaining the temperature for 2-5 hours. The calcination in step (iii) involves heating at 500-600 degrees C at a speed of 1-5 degrees C/minute, and heat-preserving for 1-2 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a defrosting loop including an                ultra cooled electromagnetic three-way valve that                is installed at an outlet of a gas cooler. PN CN109249113-A
TI Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method comprises using vertical or horizontal welding method comprising orderly welding under layer, filling layer and cover layer, and welding flux cored wire with deoxidizer component.
AU ZHANG Z
AE XUZHOU ZHONGZHOU POWER TRANSMISSION (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019108791
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method comprises using vertical or horizontal welding method comprising orderly welding under layer, filling layer and cover layer, and welding flux cored wire with deoxidizer component; taking groove, blanking, machining, performing carbon dioxide gas welding method, ultrasonic welding, and performing non-destructive testing. The vertical welding method comprises bottom layer welding comprises using current of 170 A and voltage of 21 V; filling layer welding comprises using current of 240 A and voltage of 26 V; and cover layer comprises using current of 180 A and voltage of 25 V. The horizontal welding method comprises bottom layer using current of 180 A and voltage of 24 V; filling layer welding comprises current of 280 A and voltage of 26 V; and cover layer welding comprises current of 180 A and voltage of 24 V.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, does not need additional heating or cooling device, or apparatus, labor efficient, low-cost, improved welding efficiency, and solves oxidation problem in the welding process.

PN CN107560087-A; WO2019051893-A1
TI Method for controlling air conditioner of            household system, involves outputting fresh air            exchange instruction or air purifying instruction based            on detected outdoor air quality to execute            corresponding operation.
AU LI W
   OU Z
   LIU J
AE GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
   MIDEA GROUP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
   GD MIDEA AIR-CONDITIONING EQUIP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
   MIDEA GROUP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
GA 2018047244
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves monitoring a change in                indoor carbon dioxide concentration of each area in                a room. An outdoor air quality is detected when the                indoor carbon dioxide concentration at any area is                greater than a preset threshold value corresponding                to a current time point. A fresh air exchange                instruction or air purifying instruction is output                based on the detected outdoor air quality to                execute a corresponding operation. The indoor                Carbon dioxide concentration of each region in the                room is preset the pre-set threshold for different                time periods.
   USE - Method for controlling an air conditioner of a                household system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving an indoor air                quality so as to avoid harm to human health by high                concentration of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                storage medium comprising a set of instructions for                controlling an air conditioner of a household                system.

PT P
PN CN113388913-A; CN113388913-B
TI Preparing polyacrylonitrile antibacterial            ultrafine fiber useful in preparing medical and health            protection products, comprises e.g. preparing high melt            index ultra-fine fibers, adding initiator for reaction            and electrostatic spinning.
AU TAN L
   QIN L
   LI S
   XIE Z
   DENG L
   MO H
   CHEN W
   LI Y
AE UNIV HUBEI MINZU (UNHN-C)
GA 2021A8597J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyacrylonitrile antibacterial                ultrafine fiber comprises (i) preparing high melt                index polyacrylonitrile based on supercritical                carbon dioxide combines 75-95 mol.% acrylonitrile                monomer, uniformly mixing 1-20 mol.% second monomer                and 1-10 mol.% third monomer to obtain a mixed                monomer, taking 5-30 wt.% of the mixed monomers of                the total mixed monomers, adding the initiator into                the supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave for                reaction, and reacting, and (ii) preparing                antibacterial polyacrylonitrile ultra-fine fibers,                uniformly mixing the PAN resin powder and the flow                dispersing agent, adding into the melt differential                electrostatic spinning feed device for                electrostatic spinning to obtain ultra-fine fibers,                adjusting spinning temperature of 200-280 degrees                C, spinning voltage of 40-100 KV, spinning distance                of 5-30 cm, micro extruder of 5-200                revolutions/minute, air flow rate of 5-100                m/seconds, and receiving roller speed 50-2000                revolutions/minute.
   USE - The ultrafine fiber is useful in preparing                medical and health protection products, preferably                the medical and health protection products are                masks (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ultrafine fiber is suitable in chemical                synthesis and electrostatic spinning technology                field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyacrylonitrile antibacterial                ultrafine fiber comprises (i) preparing high melt                index polyacrylonitrile based on supercritical                carbon dioxide combines 75-95 mol.% acrylonitrile                monomer, uniformly mixing 1-20 mol.% second monomer                and 1-10 mol.% third monomer to obtain a mixed                monomer, taking 5-30 wt.% of the mixed monomers of                the total mixed monomers, adding the initiator into                the supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave for                reaction, reacting for 1-2 hours, injecting the                remaining mixed monomer into the reactor, reacting                for 1-5 hours to obtain polyacrylonitrile resin,                where the second monomer is allyl trimethyl                ammonium chloride, methacryloxyethyl trimethyl                ammonium chloride, methacryloxy ethyl dimethyl                benzyl ammonium chloride, or dimethyl diallyl                ammonium chloride monomers, the third monomer is                allyl mercaptan, divinylbenzene,                3-mercaptopropionyloxyethyl methacrylate, or                isopentenyl mercaptan, and the initiator is                ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate,                azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptonitrile, or                sodium persulfate, and (ii) preparing antibacterial                polyacrylonitrile ultra-fine fibers, uniformly                mixing the PAN resin powder and the flow dispersing                agent, adding into the melt differential                electrostatic spinning feed device for                electrostatic spinning to obtain ultra-fine fibers,                adjusting spinning temperature of 200-280 degrees                C, spinning voltage of 40-100 KV, spinning distance                of 5-30 cm, micro extruder of 5-200                revolutions/minute, air flow rate of 5-100                m/seconds, and receiving roller speed 50-2000                revolutions/minute, where the flow dispersion aid                is CR76 modifier, Arsen 7008, HyPer C100,                polyethylene glycol, glycerol, ionic liquid, or                acetyl tributyl citrate, and the amount of the                initiator and the flow dispersing aid is 0.5-5% and                1-20%, respectively. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for polyacrylonitrile antibacterial                ultrafine fiber prepared by the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  reaction is carried out at 75-100 degrees C with                  pressure of 7.5-8.5 MPa and speed of 50-200                  revolutions/minute. In the step (ii), the mass                  ratio of PAN resin powder and flow dispersion aid                  is 85-95: 5-15 or 85-90: 10-15, and the spinning                  is carried out at 200-280 degrees C or 220-280                  degrees C with spinning voltage of 45-65 KV,                  spinning distance of 10-30 cm, micro extruder                  speed of 20-40 revolutions/minute, air flow rate                  of 60-100 m/seconds, and receiving roller speed                  of 600-1000 revolutions/minute when the second                  monomer is allyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, the                  third monomer is allyl mercaptan and the molar                  ratio of acrylonitrile/allyltrimethylammonium                  chloride/allyl mercaptan is 75-95: 3-15: 2-10.                  The mass ratio of PAN resin powder and flow                  dispersing aid is 85-95: 5-15 or 85-92: 8-15 and                  the spinning is carried out at 230-250 degrees C,                  spinning voltage of 75-100 KV, spinning distance                  of 10-25 cm, micro extruder speed of 5-35                  revolutions/minute, air flow rate of 30-100                  m/second, and receiving roll speed 500-2000                  revolutions/minute when the second monomer is                  methacryloxyethyl trimethyl ammonium chloride,                  and the third monomer is isopentenyl mercaptan                  and the molar ratio of                  acrylonitrile/methacryloxyethyltrimethylammonium                  chloride/isopentenyl mercaptan is 80-95: 4-15:                  1-5 or 80-90: 8-15: 2-5; the mass ratio of PAN                  resin powder and flow dispersion aid is 85-95:                  5-15 or 85-92: 8-15 and the spinning is carried                  out at 230-250 degrees C, spinning voltage of                  75-100 KV, spinning distance of 10-25 cm, micro                  extruder speed of 10-35 revolutions/minute, air                  flow rate of 40-90m/s and receiving roll speed of                  400-1800 revolutions/minute when the second                  monomer is methacryloxyethyl trimethylammonium                  chloride, and the third monomer is                  3-mercaptopropionyloxyethyl methacrylate, the                  molar ratio of acrylonitrile/methacryloxyethyl                  trimethylammonium                  chloride/-3-mercaptopropionyloxyethyl                  methacrylate 85-95: 3-12: 2-5 or 85-90: 8-12: 2-5                  or 85-87: 8-12: 3-5.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); A85 (Electrical applications.); ; P21 (Wearing apparel (A41, A42).)

PT P
PN CN112881589-A
TI Detecting carbonate ions in lithium hydroxide            monohydrate involves adding de-carbon dioxide pure            water, selecting test program, adding sodium carbonate            solution, adding hydrochloric acid standard solution,            and entering dynamic titration.
AU HU J
   JIA G
   PENG R
   ZHANG L
   XU P
   HE D
   SONG X
   JIN X
   LIU Y
AE JIANGXI YUNWEI NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021643106
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting the content of carbonate ions in                lithium hydroxide monohydrate involves weighing a                sample of lithium hydroxide monohydrate into a                sample cup, adding de-carbon dioxide pure water to                dissolve, placing the sample cup on the fixed                position of the sample rack of the autosampler,                selecting the preset test program, clicking to                start running, adding sodium carbonate solution to                the additive titration unit, stirring, adding the                hydrochloric acid standard solution to the main                titration unit, stirring, and entering dynamic                titration by applying to an automatic                potentiometric titrator. According to titration                curve and the preset calculation formula, carbonate                content is calculated by carbonate content in the                lithium hydroxide monohydrate sample, concentration                of the hydrochloric acid standard solution as the                main titrant, concentration of the additive reagent                sodium carbonate solution, volume of the main                titrant hydrochloric acid and weight of the lithium                hydroxide monohydrate sample.
   USE - Detecting the content of carbonate ions in                lithium hydroxide monohydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively detects the content of                carbonate in high-hydroxy substances quickly and                accurately in batches.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting the content of carbonate ions in                lithium hydroxide monohydrate involves weighing a                sample of lithium hydroxide monohydrate into a                sample cup, adding de-carbon dioxide pure water to                dissolve, placing the sample cup on the fixed                position of the sample rack of the autosampler,                entering the corresponding sample information on                the computer end of the automatic potentiometric                titrator, selecting the preset test program,                clicking to start running, adding sodium carbonate                solution to the additive titration unit, stirring                for the preset time (T1), adding the hydrochloric                acid standard solution to the main titration unit,                stirring for the preset time (T2), and entering                dynamic titration until the end of the titration by                applying to an automatic potentiometric titrator.                The automatic potentiometric titrator contains a                main titration unit and the additive titration                unit. According to the titration curve and the                preset calculation formula, carbonate content is                calculated by carbon dioxide content in the lithium                hydroxide monohydrate sample, concentration of the                hydrochloric acid standard solution as the main                titrant, concentration of the additive reagent                sodium carbonate solution, volume of the main                titrant hydrochloric acid and weight of the lithium                hydroxide monohydrate sample using a formula of: (                omega ) = ((1/2x 1/1000x C1x (V2-V1)-C2x 2/1000)x                60/M)x 100% (I), where ( omega ) is the carbonate                content in the lithium hydroxide monohydrate                sample, C1 (mol/L) is the concentration of the                hydrochloric acid standard solution as the main                titrant, C2 (mol/L) is the concentration of the                auxiliary reagent sodium carbonate solution, V1                (ml) is the volume of the main titrant hydrochloric                acid consumed when the jump point (P1) is reached,                V2 (ml) is the volume of the main titrant                hydrochloric acid consumed when the jump point (P2)                is reached and M (g) is the weight of the lithium                hydroxide monohydrate sample.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC S03-E09D; S03-E14B; T01-G02A2D; T01-L02B; T01-S03

PN CN112138076-A
TI Fumigating liquid used for treating liver and kidney yin deficiency type xerophthalmia comprises ethanol and active components extracted by supercritical fluid extraction from e.g. prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, medlar and yam.
AU WENG Y
   LOUHE M
   HAN S
   MENG S
   QIU S
   LIU Y
   XIONG Y
   WANG Y
   LI R
   DUZHENG Y
   LI A
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG CHINESE MEDICAL (UZTC-C)
GA 202103255P
AB    NOVELTY - Fumigating liquid comprises ethanol and active components extracted by supercritical fluid extraction (SFE) extraction method from the following traditional Chinese medicinal materials: 1-50 pts. wt. prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, 1-50 pts. wt. medlar, 1-50 pts. wt. yam, 1-50 pts. wt. dogwood, 1-50 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 1-50 pts. wt. cortex moutan, 1-50 pts. wt. rhizoma alismatis, 1-50 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 1-50 pts. wt. butterflybush flower and 1-50 pts. wt. radix bupleuri.
   USE - The fumigating liquid is used for treating liver and kidney yin deficiency type xerophthalmia (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fumigating liquid avoids the pain of oral administration of the patient, prevents oxidation and dissipation of the heat sensitive substances, keeps the effective components of the traditional Chinese medicine materials, does not have residual toxic and harmful solvent substance, since the whole preparation process does not use toxic and harmful organic solvent, preventing the presence of harmful substance in the extracting process and pollution to the environment, ensures 100% pure natural property, and can be directly contacted with the eyes through the fumigation instrument.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the fumigating liquid by supercritical fluid extraction comprising respectively crushing the raw materials, weighing, mixing uniformly, and putting the mixture into supercritical extraction kettle; using carbon dioxide as medium for supercritical fluid extraction for 1.5-5 hours, where the extracting kettle has pressure of 20-50 MPa and temperature of 15-60 degrees C and separating kettle has pressure of 6-15 MPa and temperature of 20-45 degrees C, and obtaining first extracting liquid from the separating kettle; using carbon dioxide and entrainer to mix as medium for supercritical fluid extraction for 1.5-5 hours, where the extracting kettle has pressure of 20-50 MPa and temperature of 15-60 degrees C and separating kettle has pressure of 6-15 MPa and temperature of 20-45 degrees C, and obtaining second extracting liquid from the separating kettle; using carbon dioxide and entrainer to mix as medium to carry out supercritical fluid extraction for 1.5-5 hours, where the extracting kettle has pressure of 20-50 MPa and temperature of 15-60 degrees C, and the separating kettle has pressure of 6-15 MPa and temperature of 20-45 degrees C, and obtaining third extracting liquid from the separating kettle; using carbon dioxide and entrainer to mix as medium for supercritical fluid extraction for 1.5-5 hours, where the extracting kettle has pressure of 20-50 MPa and temperature of 15-60 degrees C, and the separating kettle has pressure of 6-15 MPa and temperature of 20-45 degrees C, and obtaining the fourth extracting liquid from the separating kettle; and uniformly shaking and mixing the first extracting liquid, second extracting liquid, third extracting liquid and fourth extracting liquid, and obtaining mixed liquid as fumigation liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the third step, the entrainer is 20% ethanol having a flow rate of 0.38 ml/minute. In the fourth step, the entrainer is 80% ethanol having a flow rate of 0.48 ml/minute. In the fifth step, the entrainer is 95% ethanol having a flow rate of 0.42 ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The traditional Chinese medicinal materials are 10 pts. wt. prepared rhizome of Rehmannia, 10 pts. wt. medlar, 12 pts. wt. yam, 10 pts. wt. dogwood, 12 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 10 pts. wt. cortex moutan, 6 pts. wt. rhizoma alismatis, 6 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 6 pts. wt. butterflybush flower and 9 pts. wt. radix bupleuri.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic; Nephrotropic; Immunosuppressive.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PT P
PN CN109444274-A
TI Removing carbon dioxide and carbonate ion in high concentration sodium hydroxide eluent by pouring ultra-pure water into water-bath magnetic stirrer, stirring, degassing ultra-pure water, heating and cooling and adding calcium hydroxide.
AU ZHANG C
   YANG C
   ZHAI X
   LIU X
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV NANJING INFORMATION SCI & TECHNOLOG (UNAI-C)
GA 201924741V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing carbon dioxide and carbonate ion in a high concentration sodium hydroxide eluent involves (i) preparing a two-necked flask with a certain volume, pouring ultra-pure water into a water-bath magnetic stirrer, placing a clean stirrer in it and continuously stirring the mixture during the process, (ii) connecting one port of the two-necked flask to a three-way valve, inserting the lower end of the three-way valve into a bottle opening and connecting the upper end with helium gas and middle port to a air suction device, (iii) connecting the other end of the two-necked flask to a bend drying pipe, covering the bottom end of the drying tube with a layer of absorbent cotton to prevent calcium hydroxide from being sucked in, plugging a sufficient amount of calcium hydroxide to the upper end of the absorbent cotton and covering a layer of absorbent cotton over calcium hydroxide to prevent calcium hydroxide from being sprayed out and (iv) degassing the ultra-pure water.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide and carbonate ion in a high concentration sodium hydroxide eluent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the effect of separating sugar and sugar alcohol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for removing carbon dioxide and carbonate ion in a high concentration sodium hydroxide eluent involves (i) preparing a two-necked flask with a certain volume, pouring ultra-pure water into a water-bath magnetic stirrer, placing a clean stirrer in it and continuously stirring the mixture during the process, (ii) connecting one port of the two-necked flask to a three-way valve, inserting the lower end of the three-way valve into a bottle opening and connecting the upper end with helium gas and middle port to a air suction device, (iii) connecting the other end of the two-necked flask to a bend drying pipe, covering the bottom end of the drying tube with a layer of absorbent cotton to prevent calcium hydroxide from being sucked in, plugging a sufficient amount of calcium hydroxide to the upper end of the absorbent cotton and covering a layer of absorbent cotton over calcium hydroxide to prevent calcium hydroxide from being sprayed out, (iv) degassing the ultra-pure water using a suction device, continuously pumping for 30 minutes, opening the upper channel and lower channel of the three-way valve, replacing the helium into the flask and repeating the steps thrice, (v) turning on the temperature control device of the water bath magnetic stirrer, heating the ultra-pure water to boil and cooling to room temperature and (vi) adding a quantitative amount of calcium hydroxide to the cooled ultra-pure water, reacting for a period time and filtering the ultra-pure water using 0.25 mu m filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (vi) involves adding 0.001 g calcium hydroxide per 1 l ultra-pure water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves preparing two-necked flask and carrying the above-mentioned operation, taking one of the two-necked flask and adding sodium hydroxide to prepare an eluent and taking another two-necked flask for washing water. The two-necked flask is a 2 l two-necked flask.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (vi), the concentration of carbonate in ultrapure water is 0.09-0.9 mg/l.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; E34-D01; J04-X; S03-E09C

PN CN114814066-A; CN217717643-U
TI Integrated system for online monitoring food grade            carbon dioxide, has control area installed with air            inlet valve and pre-processing unit, where left side of            analysis cabinet is provided with gas connecting            pipeline inlet.
AU HUANG X
   HE B
   WANG F
   YANG K
AE SHANGHAI FANWEI INSTR EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1292Y
AB    NOVELTY - The integrated system comprises an analysis                cabinet. A pre-processing unit is installed in the                analysis cabinet, analyzer combination unit, and                exhaust unit. The right side of the analysis                cabinet is provided with an explosion-proof air                conditioner and a control unit. The upper part of                the analysis cabinet is a positive pressure sealing                area, and the lower part is a control area. A                pressure sensor, a temperature sensor, a bypass                throttle valve, a pressure relief valve, and an                analyzer combined unit are installed in the                positive pressure sealing area. An intake valve and                a pre-processing unit are installed in the control                area. The left side of the analysis cabinet is                provided with a gas connection pipeline                inlet.
   USE - Integrated system for online monitoring food                grade carbon dioxide used in carbonated beverage,                tobacco shred puffing, and food fresh-keeping                field. Can also be used in gas analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The analysis system can finish each node                analysis and finished product gas impurity full                analysis in food-grade carbon dioxide purification                process, ensuring the quality of food grade carbon                dioxide product gas. Compared with the traditional                analysis method, the integration degree is higher,                and real-time analysis in the production field, the                data can be directly uploaded to the control                centre. The system uses the electric heating                pressure reducing valve as the sample adjusting                valve, ensuring that the sample gas entering the                analysis cabinet end has stronger timeliness. It                eliminates the problem that the analysis meter has                resistance to influence the detection result                because of the sample outlet end. The tail gas                emptying pipeline uses the stainless steel pipeline                with inner diameter of 25 mm, it can allow the                sample bypass flow set larger, and improves the                pipeline replacement speed of the measuring point                to the analyzing cabinet end

PN CN114058442-A
TI Processing edible chicken oil comprises e.g. selecting fresh chicken suet, obtaining chicken suet particles as raw material after crushing, crushing raw material by ultrasonic cell disruptor, and packing raw material into extractive rectification column for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction.
AU MI W
   GUO H
AE TAIAN JINGUANHONG OIL PRODN IND CO (TAIA-Non-standard)
GA 2022368063
AB    NOVELTY - Processing edible chicken oil comprises (1) selecting fresh chicken suet without rot, and obtaining chicken suet particles with particle diameter of 0.1-1 cm as raw material A after crushing, (2) crushing the raw material A by an ultrasonic cell disruptor for 1-10 minutes to obtain the raw material B, (3) packing the raw material B into the extractive rectification column for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, (4) adding the extraction product C into the decolorization tower, adding decolorizing agent, and filtering after allowing to stand for 17-23 minutes, (5) adding the filtered grease to the vacuum deodorization tower, (6) adding natural antioxidants to the deodorized oil and mixing to obtain product D, and (7) filtering the product D with a filter bag with a pore size not greater than 5 microns.
   USE - The method is useful for processing edible chicken oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not use any solvent in the whole process; is simple in operation; has no organic solvent residue; can avoid oxidative rancidity of oil; has high oil extraction rate; and the obtained chicken oil has less phosphorus and low color.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing edible chicken oil comprises (1) selecting fresh chicken suet without rot, and obtaining chicken suet particles with particle diameter of 0.1-1 cm as raw material A after crushing, (2) crushing the raw material A by an ultrasonic cell disruptor for 1-10 minutes to obtain the raw material B, (3) packing the raw material B into the extractive rectification column for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, where the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid flows through the packing layer from bottom to top from the bottom of the column, carrying out the extraction product into the separation kettle from the exhaust valve at the top of the tower, and obtaining the extraction product C after separation by the separation kettle, where the extraction pressure in the extractive distillation tower is 15-50 MPa, and the extraction temperature is 55-70degrees Celsius, (4) adding the extraction product C into the decolorization tower, heating the oil to 110plus minus10degrees Celsius, adding decolorizing agent, and filtering after allowing to stand for 17-23 minutes, (5) adding the filtered grease to the vacuum deodorization tower, and reducing the temperature of the vacuum deodorized grease to 60-80degrees Celsius through a heat exchanger by a pump, (6) adding natural antioxidants to the deodorized oil and mixing to obtain product D, and (7) filtering the product D with a filter bag with a pore size not greater than 5 microns, where the filtration pressure is 0.01-0.3 MPa, obtaining edible chicken oil, vacuum-packing and placing into storage after passing the inspection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the chicken suet pretreatment includes selecting fresh chicken suet without rot, drying the chicken suet after cleaning, crushing into 7-10 cm block raw materials by a crusher, and then processing the block raw materials into chicken suet particles with a particle size of 0.1-1cm by a meat grinder. The ultrasonic frequency used in the ultrasonic treatment in the step (2) is 10-30 KHz, and the ultrasonic power is 10-100 W. In the step (3), the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction time is 30-200 minutes, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 100-150 ml/minutes. In the step (3), the packing amount of the raw material B in the extractive distillation column is 30-50% of the column volume. In the step (3), the packing in the extractive distillation column adopts Raschig ring or Pall ring packing, and the Raschig ring is theta-shaped or cross-shaped. In the step (4), the decolorizing agent is neutral or acidic clay, and the amount of the clay is 0.1-0.5%. In the step (5), the vacuum deodorization temperature is 104plus minus2degrees Celsius, the deodorization time is 20-40 minutes, and the vacuum degree is not greater than -0.090 MPa. In the step (6), natural antioxidant is vitamin E, rosemary essential oil, or tea polyphenol, and the added amount of the natural antioxidant is less than 0.05 g/kg. In the step (7), the packaging of the final product adopts a sealed, oxygen-isolated, and light-proof container.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A03; D10-A02

PN WO2021055186-A1
TI Making synthesis gas comprises e.g. reacting fuel and oxidant in combustion zone within reverse flow reactor under combustion conditions to produce flue gas, introducing anode input stream and introducing cathode input stream.
AU PRENTICE K M
   KISS G
   HAN L
   SUTTON C R
   SKOULIDAS A
   HARANDI M
   ELSEN H A
   BARCKHOLTZ T A
   ZHANG L
   ONEAL E J
   REINER V M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2021297415
AB    NOVELTY - Making a synthesis gas, comprises: reacting a fuel and an oxidant in a combustion zone within a reverse flow reactor under combustion conditions to produce a flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and to heat a conversion zone within the reverse flow reactor to an average conversion zone temperature, where the conversion zone comprises a reforming catalyst; introducing an anode input stream comprising hydrogen gas and/or a reformable fuel into anodes of many molten carbonate fuel cells; introducing a cathode input stream comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen gas into cathodes of the molten carbonate fuel cells; operating the molten carbonate fuel cells to generate electricity, a cathode exhaust and an anode exhaust; and exposing a reforming input stream comprising a reformable fuel and at least a portion of the anode exhaust to the reforming catalyst.
   USE - The process is useful for making a synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making a synthesis gas, comprises: reacting a fuel and an oxidant in a combustion zone within a reverse flow reactor under combustion conditions to produce a flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and to heat a conversion zone within the reverse flow reactor to an average conversion zone temperature, where the conversion zone comprises a reforming catalyst; introducing an anode input stream comprising hydrogen gas and/or a reformable fuel into anodes of many molten carbonate fuel cells; introducing a cathode input stream comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen gas into cathodes of the molten carbonate fuel cells; operating the molten carbonate fuel cells to generate electricity, a cathode exhaust and an anode exhaust; and exposing a reforming input stream comprising a reformable fuel and at least a portion of the anode exhaust to the reforming catalyst under reforming conditions comprising the average conversion zone temperature in the conversion zone within the reverse flow reactor to produce a synthesis gas having a molar ratio of hydrogen gas to carbon monoxide of less than or equal to 2.5:1, where the at least a portion of the anode exhaust makes up greater than or equal to 10 vol.% the reforming input stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for making a synthesis gas, comprising a reverse flow reactor comprising a first inlet, a second inlet, a first outlet, a second outlet and a combustion zone and a conversion zone disposed within the reverse flow reactor, where the conversion zone comprises a catalyst disposed in it, and many molten carbonate fuel cells each comprising a cathode inlet, a cathode, a cathode outlet, an anode inlet, an anode and an anode outlet, where the second inlet is in fluid communication with the conversion zone within the reverse flow reactor, and at least one anode outlet of the carbonate fuel cells is in fluid communication with the second inlet of the reverse flow reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, less than 1 vol.% the flue gas is recycled to the combustion zone. The process further comprises compressing at least a portion of the anode exhaust to produce a compressed anode exhaust, where the at least a portion of the anode exhaust exposed to the reforming catalyst under the reforming conditions comprises at least a portion of the compressed anode exhaust. Preferred Components: The at least a portion of the anode exhaust makes up greater than or equal to 30 vol.% of the reforming input stream. The flue gas further comprises oxygen gas, where the cathode input stream comprises at least a portion of the flue gas. The flue gas comprises greater than or equal to 4 vol.% oxygen gas. The flue gas contributes greater than or equal to 50 vol.% o of a total content of oxygen gas in the cathode input stream. The cathode input stream comprises a greater content of oxgyen than carbon dioxide. The synthesis gas comprises a molar ratio of hydrogen gas to carbon monoxide of less than or equal to 2.3:1. Upon exiting the combustion zone, the flue gas is at a pressure of less than 35 kPag or 35 kPag to 15 MPag. The compressed anode exhaust is at a pressure of 0.7-2.8 MPag, preferably less than 2.2 MPag. The reforming catalyst comprises nickel, cobalt, iron, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, platinum, iridium, copper, silver, gold, zirconium, chromium, titanium, vanadium and/or tungsten.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110643574-A
TI Preparing feeder cell useful for rapid culturing            of tumor infiltrating lymphocyte comprises diluting            peripheral blood with phosphate buffered saline,            culturing, adding mitomycin C, resuspending,            incubating, collecting cells and centrifuging.
AU XIAO Z
   ZHAO Y
   YU J
   WEN Q
   LI J
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST MEDICAL (USWM-C)
GA 202004038Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing feeder cell comprises collecting                peripheral blood of patients with non-small cell                lung cancer, diluting with phosphate buffered                saline, culturing, counting the separated                lymphocytes, adjusting the cell density, adding                mitomycin C, centrifuging, discarding the                supernatant, resuspending and washing the cells                with X-VIVO (RTM: Serum-free hematopoietic cell)                culture medium, resuspending the cells by adding                the culture medium to obtain feeder cells, adding                tumor infiltrating lymphocyte culture medium,                adding fresh culture medium, centrifuging, then                resuspending and washing the cells with culture                medium to obtain pre-cultured tumor infiltrating                lymphocytes, centrifuging the feeder cells and                pre-cultured tumor infiltrating lymphocytes                separately, resuspending and washing in culture                medium, adding interleukin-2 and cluster of                differentiation 3/cluster of differentiation 28                antibodies, then adding in carbon dioxide                incubator, and adding fresh culture medium.
   USE - The feeder cell is useful for rapid culturing                of tumor infiltrating lymphocyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The feeder cell: has great efficiency compared                to the feeder cells produced by gamma -ray                irradiation to expand tumor infiltrating                lymphocytes. The method: can greatly reduce the                cost; is simple and is easy to implement; and can                be widely popularized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing feeder cell comprises (a1)                collecting peripheral blood of patients with                non-small cell lung cancer, diluting with phosphate                buffered saline, adding lymphocyte separation                solution for performing lymphocyte separation, and                culturing in serum-free X-VIVO (RTM: Serum-free                hematopoietic cell) culture medium in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, (a2) culturing                for overnight, counting the separated lymphocytes,                adjusting the cell density to 1.5x 106 cells/ml,                adding mitomycin C for 1.5-2 hours, centrifuging,                discarding the supernatant, resuspending and                washing the cells with X-VIVO (RTM: Serum-free                hematopoietic cell) culture medium, centrifuging                again, discarding the supernatant, and resuspending                the cells by adding the culture medium to obtain                feeder cells, (b) washing the clinically collected                non-small cell lung cancer tissues with sterile                phosphate buffer solution, cutting into 1-2 mm3                tissue pieces, then adding tumor infiltrating                lymphocyte culture medium, placing in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 2-3 days for                incubating, discarding the tissue block, continuing                culturing the tumor infiltrating lymphocytes,                adding fresh culture medium for every 2-3 days,                culturing for 10-14 days, collecting cells,                centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, then                resuspending and washing the cells with culture                medium to obtain pre-cultured tumor infiltrating                lymphocytes, and (c) centrifuging the feeder cells                and pre-cultured tumor infiltrating lymphocytes                separately, resuspending and washing twice in                culture medium, then adding AIM-V (RTM: Serum free                cell culture medium)/tumor infiltrating lymphocytes                medium, adding interleukin-2 and cluster of                differentiation 3/cluster of differentiation 28                antibodies at the same time, then adding them in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for                culturing, and adding fresh culture medium for                every 2-3 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The phosphate buffer                  and patient's autologous peripheral blood are                  diluted in a volume ratio of 1:1-1.2. The X-VIVO                  (RTM: Serum-free hematopoietic cell) medium is                  also supplemented with 40 ng/ml interleukin-2 and                  200 ng/ml cluster of differentiation 3/cluster of                  differentiation 28 antibodies. The final                  concentration of mitomycin C is 50 mu g/ml. The                  tumor infiltrating lymphocyte medium comprises                  1640 medium, 10 wt.% human A/B serum, 1 wt.%                  penicillin/streptomycin solution, 10 mM L                  glutamine, and 10 mM 2-mercaptoethanol. The AIM-V                  (RTM: Serum free cell culture medium)/tumor                  infiltrating lymphocytes medium is further                  supplemented with 5000 U/ml interleukin-2 and 200                  ng/ml cluster of differentiation 3/cluster of                  differentiation 28 antibodies. Preferred Method:                  The step (c) comprises adding AIM-V (RTM: Serum                  free cell culture medium)/tumor infiltrating                  lymphocytes medium in a ratio of 200-230:1 cell                  number of feeder cells, and pre-culturing tumor                  infiltrating lymphocytes. The AIM-V (RTM: Serum                  free cell culture medium)/tumor infiltrating                  lymphocytes medium is prepared by using AIM-V                  (RTM: Serum free cell culture medium) and tumor                  infiltrating lymphocyte medium in a volume ratio                  of 1:1. The step (c) comprises performing cell                  passage in a ratio of 1:1 after the cell density                  reaches to 2x 106 cells/ml, and at the same time                  performing cell counting for 1-2 times a                  week.

PN EP3524574-A1
TI Use of catalyst material containing iron single            atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix prepared from            iron(II)-doped zeolitic imidazole framework for            electroreduction system used for electroreduction of            carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU HU X
   GU J
AE GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
GA 2019703138
AB    NOVELTY - Use of catalyst material containing iron (Fe)                single atom on nitrogen (N) doped carbon matrix                prepared from Fe(II)-doped zeolitic imidazole                framework (ZIF) is claimed for electroreduction of                carbon dioxide (CO2) to carbon monoxide (CO).
   USE - Utilization of catalyst material containing                iron (Fe) single atom on nitrogen (N) doped carbon                matrix prepared from Fe(II)-doped zeolitic                imidazole framework for electroreduction system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst material has high catalytic                activity allowing increasing carbon dioxide (CO2)                electroreduction rates, while maintaining a high                selectivity to carbon monoxide (CO) formation,                production cost that allows a large scale use, is                stable over time of use, and is made of abundant                metals that allows a large scale use. The method is                cost-effective and prepares catalyst from readily                available chemical reagents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparing a catalyst material which                involves (a) providing iron(II)-doped zeolitic                imidazole framework (ZIF) in dried form under inert                atmosphere; (b) subjecting Fe(II)-doped ZIF to                pyrolysis under inert atmosphere for 2-24 hours,                preferably 3 hours; and (c) collecting the obtained                pyrolysis product as a catalyst material;
   (2) preparing Fe(II)-doped ZIF material which                involves (i) providing an imidazole or imidazole                derivative precursor; (ii) dissolving the imidazole                or imidazole derivative precursor in degassed polar                solvent, in a reductive atmosphere to prevent the                oxidation of iron(II) cations to obtain a solution                A; (iii) dissolving iron(II) precursor and zinc                (Zn) salt in degassed polar solvent to obtain a                solution B; (iv) adding solution B into solution A;                (v) collecting and washing the precipitate; and                (vi) leaving the washed precipitate drying at room                temperature to obtain a Fe(II)-doped zeolitic                imidazole framework (ZIF) in the form of dry                powder, where steps (ii)-(vi) are conducted under                stirring in an inert atmosphere;
   (3) catalyst material comprising iron (Fe)                single atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix;
   (4) electroreduction system for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2)                comprising a catalyst material prepared from                Fe(II)-doped zeolitic imidazole framework                (ZIF);
   (5) kit for preparation of catalyst material                comprising greater than or equal to 1 container                comprising Fe(II)-doped zeolitic imidazole                framework (ZIF) in dry form and under inert                atmosphere; and
   (6) kit for preparation of Fe(II)-doped                zeolitic imidazole framework (ZIF), comprising in                separate compartments or containers: (a) imidazole                or imidazole derivate precursor; (b) iron (II)                precursor such as iron(II) chloride (FeCl2) or                iron(II) acetate (Fe(CH3COO)2); (c) zinc salt such                as zinc nitrate (Zn(NO3)2) or zinc chloride (ZnCl2)                and (d) optionally a reductive agent to prevent                oxidation of the iron(II) ions such as iodide                derivative such as potassium, sodium or ammonium                iodide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the                process of obtaining catalyst material comprising                iron single atoms on nitrogen doped carbon                matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pyrolysis under step                  (b) is carried out at 800-950 degrees C and rate                  of 3-10 degrees C/minute. Solution B is added                  into solution A dropwise under stirring for 1                  hour under step (iv). Reacting mixture from step                  (iv) is kept under stirring under inert                  atmosphere for one day after the completion of                  the addition of solution B. The precipitate is                  collected under step (v) by ambient-pressure                  filtration or centrifugation under inert                  atmosphere. The catalyst material is obtained                  from the pyrolysis of Fe(II)doped-ZIF-8.                  Preferred Components: The iron(II) precursor is                  iron(II) chloride (FeCl2). The inert atmosphere                  is provided by nitrogen gas (N2) flow.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The imidazole                  precursor is 2-methyl imidazole.

PN WO2019046283-A1; US2019135625-A1; KR2020045484-A; EP3675996-A1; JP2020532479-W; EP3675996-A4
TI System used for sorbent enhanced reformation for production of hydrogen, consists of reforming unit, sorbent unit, separation units, calciner unit and return line.
AU FITZSIMMONS M
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201922945G
AB    NOVELTY - A system (110) consists of a reforming unit (120) containing a bed of methane reforming catalyst to process a feed containing methane and steam (212) to produce a reformer product stream containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The sorbent unit (130) is operatively connected to the unit (120) to process the reformer product stream. A separation unit (170) is operatively connected to the unit (130) to process the sorbent unit product stream to separate hydrogen from the used sorbent. A calciner unit (160) is operatively connected to the unit (170) to calcine at least a portion of the used sorbent to form a calciner product stream containing regenerated sorbent material and residual gases. A separation unit (170) is operatively connected to the unit (160) to process the calciner product stream to separate the regenerated sorbent material from the residual gases and a return line is operatively connected to the unit (170) (B) to return regenerated sorbent material to the unit (130).
   USE - System is used for sorbent enhanced reformation for production of hydrogen (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The separated hydrogen is high purity hydrogen having higher purity than product produced through steam-methane reformation. Sorbent enhanced reformation is cost effective from waste heat standpoint and calcination takes place at lower temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system consists of a reforming unit containing a bed of methane reforming catalyst to process a feed containing methane and steam to produce a reformer product stream containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide. A sorber unit consists of a bed of carbon dioxide sorbent material and is operatively connected to the reforming unit to process the reformer product stream with the sorbent material absorbing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the reformer product stream to produce the sorber unit product stream containing 3/4 and used sorbent. A separation unit (A) is operatively connected to the sorber unit to process product stream to separate hydrogen from the used sorbent. A calciner unit is operatively connected to the unit (A) to calcine at least a portion of the used sorbent to form a calciner product stream containing regenerated sorbent material and residual gases. Another separation unit (B) is operatively connected to the unit calciner unit to process the calciner product stream to separate the regenerated sorbent material from the residual gases and a return line is operatively connected to the separation unit (B) to return regenerated sorbent material to the sorber unit. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for sorbent enhanced reformation of methane to form hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the system.
   Process system (110)
   Input stream (112)
   Reforming catalyst bed unit (120)
   Streams (122,132,146,152,162,172,174)
   Sorbent unit (130)
   Filtration unit (140)
   Filtrate stream (142)
   Scavenging process unit (150)
   Calciner unit (160)
   Separation unit (170)
   Feed stream containing methane and steam (212)

PN CN108531541-A
TI Detecting thyroid hormone interference-inducing activity in an environment comprises e.g. taking 96-hole plate, inoculating rat pituitary tumor GH3 cell, incubating, discarding supernatant, drawing standard dose effect curve, and comparing.
AU LIU Y
   LI J
   WEN Y
   YI H
   CUI K
   ZHANG B
   LIN J
AE MEP SOUTH CHINA ENVIRONMENTAL SCI INST (HNES-C)
GA 201875290M
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting thyroid hormone interference-inducing activity in environment comprises e.g. (i) taking 96-hole plate, preparing cell suspension, inoculating, and incubating, (ii) taking 96-hole plate of the step (i) after the cell adheres to the wall, discarding supernatant, adding phenol-free red F12 medium to each centrifugal tube, adding sample dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide, taking dimethyl sulfoxide as reference, adding in 96-hole plate, and exposing to carbon dioxide, (iii) taking 96-hole plate obtained in the step (ii), removing the supernatant, adding 3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-Yl)-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide (MTT) solution to each hole, forming reaction product formazan crystals, pipetting supernatant, and dissolving reduced product, (iv) detecting the optical density value of the reaction liquid of the sample to be tested, and (v) drawing standard dose effect curve, comparing optical density value of sample with standard curve to obtain sample having T3 toxicity equivalent.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting thyroid hormone interference-inducing activity in an environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is fast, simple, and economical; saves time and labor; and rapidly screens environmental samples and suspected thyroid hormone interferers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting thyroid hormone interference-inducing activity in an environment comprises (i) taking 96-hole plate, selecting well grown rat pituitary tumor GH3 cell to prepare 5/asterisk104 cells/ml cell suspension, inoculating 200 mu /hole, and incubating at 37 degrees C for 2-3 days in a culture box containing 5% carbon dioxide, (ii) taking 96-hole plate of the step (i) after the cell adheres to the wall, discarding the supernatant, taking a centrifugal tube, adding 990 mu l phenol-free red F12 medium to each centrifugal tube, adding 10 mu l sample dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide, taking another centrifuge tube, adding 10 mu l dimethyl sulfoxide as reference, adding 200 mu l in 96-hole plate, and exposing to carbon dioxide in a culture tank for 96 hours, (iii) taking 96-hole plate obtained in the step (ii) 4 hours before the end of reaction, removing the supernatant, adding 50 mu l 3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-Yl)-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide (MTT) solution to each hole, incubating in carbon dioxide culture box for 4 hours, forming reaction product formazan crystals, carefully pipetting supernatant, adding 150 mu l dimethyl sulfoxide to stop the reaction, and fully dissolving the reduced product by shaking at room temperature in a 96-well plate shaker, (iv) detecting the optical density value of the reaction liquid of the sample to be tested by enzymatic detector, and (v) taking time as abscissa, detecting the optical density value of the reaction liquid of different concentrations of T3, drawing standard dose effect curve, comparing the optical density value of the sample with standard curve to obtain T3 toxicity equivalent of the sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The inoculation of rat pituitary tumor GH3 cells in the step (i) specifically comprises taking pituitary GH3 cells subcultured to logarithmic growth phase, removing culture liquid, adding Dulbecco's modified eagle media (DMEM) trypsin and EDTA to digest, digesting, adding fetal calf serum to stop digesting, blowing GH3 cells off the wall, adding the medium to aseptic culture bottle, adding culture medium for adjusting cell concentration, mixing uniformly, using blood ball counting plate to count, and inoculating into 96-hole plate. The rat pituitary tumor GH3 cells is cultured by (a) adding 10-50% serum and 8% dimethyl sulfoxide to F10 medium to obtain cryopreservation solution, mixing rat pituitary tumor GH3 cells in cryopreservation solution, mixing uniformly, adding in a cryotube, centrifuging, pipetting supernatant, adding freezing liquid and evenly beating, placing in a freezing and storing box, setting the temperature gradient to -80 degrees C for more than 3 hours, and transferring into a liquid nitrogen storage tank, (b) quickly taking out the cryotube containing frozen cells from the liquid nitrogen tank, melting in warm water bath, centrifuging, pipetting supernatant, adding 10 ml F10 medium containing 15% fetal calf serum and 5% horse serum, spreading uniformly, and incubating in a incubator at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide, and (c) taking out the cell culture bottle after 24 hours, pipetting the culture medium with an bended pipette, washing thrice using phosphate-buffered saline buffer solution, digesting with 0.05% DMEM trypsin and 2.5% EDTA at 37 degrees C for 1 minute, adding F10 medium containing 15% fetal bovine serum and 5% horse serum, rinsing the culture medium repeatedly and gently beating the cell layer on the bottle wall until all the cells are washed down, mixing and pipetting the cell suspension into a new culture flask, mixing and pipetting the cell suspension into a new culture flask, adding new F10 medium containing 15% fetal bovine serum and 5% horse serum to the cell bottle, and incubating at 37 degrees C in an incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The fetal calf serum is 2 ml fresh fetal bovine serum. The wavelength of enzymatic detector to detect optical density value is 490 nm.
