TI Synthesizing farnesyl acetone, comprises heating nerolidol with aluminum oxide, dropping methyl acetoacetate, reacting, distilling generated methanol, collecting generated carbon dioxide gas, and filtering and separating aluminum oxide.
AU TAN L
   LIU J
   LIU H
   WANG F
   LIU X
   ZHANG J
AE JIANGSU HONGBANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D8563S
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing farnesyl acetone, comprises mixing nerolidol with aluminum oxide, heating, dropping methyl acetoacetate, finishing dropping, preserving temperature and reacting, distilling generated methanol in the reaction process, collecting generated carbon dioxide gas, finishing the reaction, filtering and separating aluminum oxide, and vacuum distilling filtrate to collect product farnesyl acetone.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing farnesyl acetone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high reaction yield, separates the catalyst by filtering and repeatedly using, avoids the washing of the reaction material, does not generate waste water, does not generate metal ion-containing waste, avoids the pollution to the environment, and recycles the generated byproduct methanol and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the nerolidol, methyl acetoacetate and aluminum oxide is 1:0.99-1.29:0.04-0.044.Preferred Method: The method comprises increasing the temperature to 160-180degrees Celsius and adding methyl acetoacetate dropwise. The temperature of the heat preservation reaction is 160-180degrees Celsius. The separated aluminum oxide is recycled and reused. The pre-distillation sleeve for vacuum distilling is used for feeding for next time.

PN US2020283348-A1
TI Producing biofertilizer involves inoculating photobioreactor (PBR) with inoculant, supplying carbon dioxide to PBR for reproducing inoculated cyanobacteria and incorporating reproduced cyanobacteria in biofertilizer.
AU FIATO R A
   BISIO A
AE ACCELERGY CORP (ACCE-Non-standard)
GA 202087945B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) a biofertilizer comprises (a) inoculating a photobioreactor (PBR) with inoculant including a nitrogen fixing cyanobacteria naturally occurring in the type of soil to which the biofertilizer is to be applied, (b) supplying carbon dioxide to the PBR for reproducing the inoculated cyanobacteria and (c) incorporating the reproduced cyanobacteria in the biofertilizer.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a biofertilizer. No biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (M2) a biofertilizer comprising (a1) reproducing in a PBR with the use of byproduct carbon dioxide, nitrogen fixing cyanobacteria naturally occurring in the type of soil to which the biofertilizer is to be applied and (b1) incorporating the cyanobacteria in the biofertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The inoculant further includes at least one additional microorganism comprising free-living nitrogen-fixing heterotrophic bacteria, actinomycetes, photosynthetic bacteria, mycorrhizal and/or lichenizing fungi. The nitrogen-fixing heterotrophic bacteria comprises Azobacteriaceae or Frankiaceae comprising Azotobacter, Frankia or Arthrobacter. The photosynthetic bacteria comprise Rhodospirillales comprising Rhodospirillum, Rhodopseudomonas or Rhodobacter. The mycorrhizal fungi belong to the Glomerales and the lichenizing fungi comprises at least one of Collema, Peltigera, Psora, Heppia or Fulgensia. The inoculant further includes rhizobacteria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The inoculant is obtained from the soil to which the biofertilizer is to be applied. The method (M1) further comprises transforming the biofertilizer into a dormant state by spray drying, refractance window drying, solar drying, air drying or freeze drying. The biofertilizer is applied in combination with an additive comprising fibrous, cellulosic mulch material, polymeric tackifiers, clays and/or geotextiles. The cyanobacteria is obtained from the top centimeter of the soil to which the biofertilizer is to be applied. The other photosynthetic microorganisms are reproduced in the PBR together with the cyanobacteria. The other photosynthetic microorganisms include rhizobacteria
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The PBR is a closed PBR.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The xeroprotectant additives comprises at least one of sorbitol, mannitol, sucrose, sorbitan monostereate, dimethyl sulfoxide, methanol, beta -carotene or beta -mercaptoethanol are used to increase post drying viability.
   ACTIVITY - Biofertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2020120759-A1; BE1026873-B1; BE1026871-B1; EP3894361-A1; US2022055932-A1; JP2022513451-W
TI Performing in-line mineralization and carbonation of demineralized water, by circulating demineralized water in channel immobilized with enzymes, introducing carbon dioxide into channel, and introducing solid mineral into circulating water.
AU TOB P
AE AQVITA SRL (AQVI-Non-standard)
   AQVITA SRL (AQVI-Non-standard)
   AQVITA SRL (AQVI-Non-standard)
GA 2020539850
AB    NOVELTY - Process for performing in-line water mineralization involves (a) circulating a demineralized water in a channel in which enzymes are immobilized to catalyze reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form bicarbonate, (b) introducing carbon dioxide into the channel, and (c) introducing a predetermined amount of solid mineral into the circulating water.
   USE - The process is useful for performing in-line mineralization and carbonation of demineralized water.
   ADVANTAGE - The process optimizes dissolution of the minerals to mineralize the water in-line, without stopping the circulation of the water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for performing remineralization of demineralized water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves (i) injecting a mineral powder, and (ii) circulating the water in the channel through a mineral column. The method involves selecting nature and amount of enzyme, and amount of carbon dioxide to obtain a predetermined pH value. The demineralized water is circulated through a mineral column comprising mixed balls of minerals, where the enzymes are grafted on balls. The carbon dioxide is introduced by bubbling, where carbon dioxide is bubbled against the flow of water. The water is circulated through a filter selectively retaining organic substances arranged downstream of the enzymes. Preferred Component: The mineral comprises calcium and/or magnesium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecule: The enzyme is carbonic anhydrase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Apparatus: The channel is permeable to carbon dioxide over at least part of its length and in which the carbon dioxide is introduced by applying a pressure of carbon dioxide to the outer wall of the channel.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PN WO2020102724-A1; CA3120081-A1; US2022002203-A1
TI Formation of cured cementitious material e.g.            precast concrete article, involves contacting            cementitious material comprising calcium silicate            precursor with water and carbon dioxide and forming            crystalline calcium silicate hydrate.
AU CLARENS A F
   PLATTENBERGER D A
AE UNIV VIRGINIA PATENT FOUND (UVIR-C)
   CLARENS A F (CLAR-Individual)
   PLATTENBERGER D A (PLAT-Individual)
   UNIV VIRGINIA PATENT FOUND (UVIR-C)
GA 202043926W
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of cured cementitious materials                involves contacting a cementitious material                comprising calcium silicate precursor with water                and carbon dioxide under conditions effective to                form crystalline calcium silicate hydrates within                the cementitious material and hardening the                cementitious material.
   USE - Formation of cured cementitious materials such                as precast concrete article (claimed) used for                buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the economical and                efficient formation of cured cementitious materials                having excellent strength, durability, chemical                stability, low permeability, reduced maintenance                and replacement process, lower processing                temperature and reduced carbon dioxide emissions,                and eliminates the requirement of virgin materials                by using industrial waste source such as calcium                and silicon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The cementitious                  material is mixed with water and immersed in                  aqueous solution of carbon dioxide. The cured                  cementitious material is prepared by mixing                  limestone and fumed silica and calcining the                  mixture to form the synthetic pseudowollastonite,                  reacting a calcia-rich calcium silicate such as                  rankinite and/or hatruite, with silica to form                  the synthetic pseudowollastonite, or reacting an                  industrial waste material, such as coal ash,                  and/or slag from iron processing, to form the                  synthetic pseudowollastonite. The cured                  cementitious material comprises 1 wt.% or more,                  preferably 1-5 wt.% carbon, based on the total                  weight of the cured cementitious material and                  further comprises an aggregate. Preferred                  Properties: The cured cementitious material has                  compressive strength of 1450 psi or more,                  preferably 1450-7500 psi, or a compressive                  strength of 2500-7500 psi, as measured using the                  standard method according to ASTM C 109/C                  109M-16a or ASTM C39/C39M-18. The cured                  cementitious material is 20% less permeable to                  chloride ion penetration than ordinary Portland                  cement, as measured using the standard method                  according to ASTM C 1202-19. The cured                  cementitious material exhibits 5% or less                  reduction in compressive strength following                  immersion in an aqueous solution having a pH of 5                  for 90 days, and 10 % or less reduction in                  following immersion in aqueous solution having a                  pH of 5 for 7 days.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water and aqueous                  solution of carbon dioxide further comprises                  alkali agent such as sodium hydroxide. The                  calcium silicate precursor comprises a discrete                  calcium silicate phase that exhibits congruent                  dissolution in water. The discrete calcium                  silicate phase comprises pseudowollastonite                  comprising synthetic pseudowollastonite. The                  molar ratio of calcium and silicon in calcium                  silicate precursor is 0.90-1.10. The crystalline                  calcium silicate hydrates comprise k-phase,                  nekoite, truscottite, gyrolite, tobermorite,                  xonotlite, afwillite, jaffeite, scawtite,                  spurrite, magadiite Preferred Property: The water                  and aqueous solution of carbon dioxide have pH of                  7.5-12, preferably 8-11.

AU YANG S

AB    NOVELTY - Beverage comprises Chrysanthemum and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The Chrysanthemum beverage is useful for reducing fever, improving eyesight, providing detoxification and antioxidant effects.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has sweet taste, is light yellow in color, rich in flavor, and simple to prepare, and convenient to drink, has easily available raw materials, does not cause toxic side effects and is suitable for both young and old people.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing beverage, which involves (a) preparing Chrysanthemum liquid with final content of Chrysanthemum of 0.5-2 weight/volume%, (b) adding flavoring agent, preferably rock sugar, (c) filling 2-4 g/l carbon dioxide gas into the above Chrysanthemum liquid, leaving and aseptically packing product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The Chrysanthemum is white Chrysanthemum and/or yellow Chrysanthemum and/or C.morifolium. The Chrysanthemum liquid is prepared by (a) washing and draining Chrysanthemum, soaking in clean drinking water at 80-100 degrees C, repeating the process for 2-3 times, collecting the filtrate each time through filtration, mixing the filtrate, adding a flavoring agent, preferably rock sugar, diluting, until the volume reaches 0.5-2 weight/volume%, or (b) washing, removing impurities, drying and soaking Chrysanthemum in purified water at 30-40 degrees C and pH of 3-4, homogenizing using colloid mill, adding flavoring agent, preferably rock sugar, and diluting to 0.5-2 weight/volume%.
   ACTIVITY - Antipyretic; Ophthalmological; Antidote.

PT P
PN WO2018081308-A1
TI Manufacture of concrete product for construction            applications, involves extracting calcium as            portlandite, forming slurry into structural component,            and exposing component to carbon dioxide in which            portlandite is converted to limestone.
AU WANG B
   PILON L G
   NEITHALATH N
   SANT G
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 2018341373
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of concrete product involves                extracting calcium from reclaimed solids as                portlandite, forming a cementitious slurry                including the portlandite, shaping the cementitious                slurry into a structural component, and exposing                the structural component to carbon dioxide. The                portlandite in the structural component is                converted to limestone to form the concrete                product.
   USE - Manufacture of concrete product (claimed) e.g.                upcycled carbon dioxide-negative concrete product                used in construction applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                manufacture of concrete product having excellent                functionality, utility, mechanical properties, and                durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for concrete                product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                upcycled concrete manufacturing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Process: The extracting process                  involves exposing the reclaimed solids to a                  leaching solution in which calcium is extracted                  as calcium ions, extracting the calcium further                  involves concentrating the calcium ions by                  capacitive concentration, and inducing                  precipitation of the calcium ions as the                  portlandite. The precipitation of the calcium                  ions involves heating at above 20 degrees C by                  applying heat sourced from a flue gas stream. The                  manufacturing process involves forming the                  cementitious slurry by combining fly ash with the                  portlandite, shaping the cementitious slurry by                  casting, extruding, molding, pressing, or                  three-dimensional printing of the slurry, and                  exposing the structural component to a carbon                  dioxide by heating at above 20 degrees C.                  Preferred Composition: The reclaimed solids                  include at least one of iron slag or steel slag.                  The carbon dioxide which is sourced from the flue                  gas stream has a carbon dioxide content of 3                  volume/volume% or more. The concrete product                  comprises a binder including limestone,                  aggregates dispersed in the binder, and further                  comprises leached slag granules and fly ash                  dispersed in the binder. The concrete product                  comprises 5 wt.% or more limestone. Preferred                  Property: The concrete product has a flexural                  strength of 2 MPa or more.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Adsorbing carbon dioxide using activated carbon, comprises adding carboxymethyl starch based film to potassium hydroxide to form activated carbon, leaving mixture to impregnate, and drying obtained mixture by carbonizing, washing and drying.
AU MICHALKIEWICZ B
   SERAFIN J
   CZECH Z
   ANTOSIK A K
   WILPISZEWSKA K
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201833137M
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption of carbon dioxide using activated carbon as an adsorbent which is run under steady state conditions at a constant temperature using a volumetric technique, comprises: obtaining activated carbon by adding a carboxymethyl starch based film to an aqueous potassium hydroxide solution in a weight ratio of 1:0.25-5; leaving the mixture for 0-24 hours to impregnate; and then drying the obtained mixture by carbonizing at a temperature of 400-1000 degrees C in an inert gas chemically by washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for adsorption of carbon dioxide using activated carbon.

PN JP2019011491-A; JP6940756-B2
TI Electrode for carbon dioxide comprises conductor, conductive carbon portion which contains conductive carbon on conductor, carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion, and metal particle which can reduce carbon dioxide.
AU MANABE T
   IMANAKA Y
   ANAZAWA T
   AMADA H
AE FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
GA 201909674G
AB    NOVELTY - An electrode (1) for carbon dioxide comprises conductor (2), conductive carbon portion (3) which contains conductive carbon on conductor, carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion (4) containing carbon-dioxide adsorption agent, and metal particle which can reduce carbon dioxide and exists between conductive carbon portion and carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion.
   USE - An electrode for carbon dioxide used for carbon-dioxide reducing device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electroconductivity of the conductor is improved by the conductive carbon portion. Furthermore, because the cathode side is not immersed in electrolyte solution, dissolution of carbon dioxide to electrolyte solution and dissolution of a product to the electrolyte solution can be suppressed. Because the contact with electrolyte solution and the carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion is protected by proton permeable film, the electrolyte solution can be prevented from approaching into carbon-dioxide adsorption agent. Supply of the carbon dioxide to a reaction zone and the product from a reaction zone are efficiently recoverable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of electrode for carbon dioxide reduction which involves applying conductive carbon and forming conductive carbon portion on a conductor, arranging metal particle which can reduce carbon dioxide on conductive carbon portion, and arranging carbon-dioxide adsorption agent and forming carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion on the conductive carbon portion to which the metal particle is arranged; and
   (2) carbon-dioxide reducing device (12B) comprising electrode for carbon dioxide reduction as an electrode of a cathode side.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional diagram of a carbon-dioxide reducing device.
   Electrode for carbon dioxide (1)
   Conductor (2)
   Conductive carbon portion (3)
   Carbon-dioxide adsorption agent portion (4)
   Carbon-dioxide reducing device (12B)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Conductive carbon contains single wall carbon nanotube, multiwall carbon nanotube, nanographene, and/or carbon paper. Metal particle is selected from copper, nickel, cobalt, iron, and/or ruthenium. Electrode has ligand in which carbon-dioxide adsorption agent can coordinate to a metal and the metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Electrode has functional group in which the conductive carbon can coordinate to the metal particle. The ligand has functional group selected from carboxy, hydroxyl, amino, imino, thiol, and/or oxime.

PN CN112554974-A; CN112554974-B
TI Distributed carbon dioxide power generation system has carbon dioxide working fluid at outlet is gas-liquid mixture and enters carbon dioxide absorption pool, and carbon dioxide working fluid recovered and reused through recovery system.
AU ZHANG D
   WANG Y
   XIE Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021367762
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide power generation system and a carbon dioxide recovery system. The carbon dioxide power generation system is provided with a steel cylinder (1), a carbon dioxide turbine (2), a generator (3) and a carbon dioxide absorption pools (4). The carbon dioxide turbine is coaxially connected with the generator. The carbon dioxide working medium drives the carbon dioxide turbine and the generator to rotate during the expansion process by generating electric energy. The outlet pressure of the carbon dioxide turbine, and the outlet temperature depends on the isentropic efficiency of the turbine. The carbon dioxide working fluid at the outlet is a gas-liquid mixture and enters the carbon dioxide absorption pool. The carbon dioxide working fluid is recovered and reused through the carbon dioxide recovery system.
   USE - Distributed carbon dioxide power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The power generation system provides no compression process, and simultaneously adopts a distributed arrangement method to conveniently adjust the power according to the requirements of back-end users. The system provides fewer components, low cost, convenience in design, control, operation and maintenance, high reliability, capability of effectively reducing emission and meet the requirement of environmental protection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a distributed carbon dioxide power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Steel cylinder (1)
   Carbon dioxide turbine (2)
   Generator (3)
   Carbon dioxide absorption pool (4)

AE UNIV EINDHOVEN TECH (UYEV-C)
GA 202207573J
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide and producing carbon monoxide, comprises: (i) providing a carbon dioxide containing gas flow; (ii) adsorbing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide containing gas flow on a sorbent; (iii) applying plasma conditions on the carbon dioxide adsorbed sorbent to allow for desorption of carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide adsorbed sorbent and conversion to carbon monoxide; and (iv) collecting carbon monoxide from the gas flow of the step (iii).
   USE - The process is useful for capturing carbon dioxide and producing carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes: plasma-based carbon dioxide dissociation for syngas production; and syngas for producing hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for carbon dioxide capture and production of carbon monoxide, comprising at least two reactors connected in parallel or series, where at least one reactor is configured for adsorbing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide containing gas flow on a sorbent and at least one reactor is configured for desorption of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide adsorbed sorbent and conversion to carbon monoxide, and the reactors include means for applying plasma conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gas flow of the step (iii) is again subjected to the step (ii) for adsorbing unreacted carbon dioxide. In the step (i), air is used as carbon dioxide containing gas flow. The steps (ii) and (iii) are: carried out in parallel for continuous capture and conversion of carbon dioxide; and carried out in series for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide with recycle of unreacted carbon dioxide. The step (iii) is carried out in presence of hydrogen gas for the production of syngas, where the ratio of hydrogen gas and carbon monoxide is 1:1 to 6:1. The hydrogen gas is produced from electrolysis. The plasma conditions applied include a frequency of 50 kHz to 1 MHz and a discharge power of 10 W to 2 kW. Preferred Components: The sorbent comprises hydrotalcites, zeolites, activated carbon, solid supported amines and/or solid supported metal organic frameworks. The shape of the sorbent comprises pellets, spheres or three-dimensional printed structures to optimize the plasma discharge and the adsorption capacity and minimize the pressure drop.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Device for pathogen control system comprises body            having internal wall defining internal chamber, and            layer of two-dimensional material on internal wall in            which layer of two-dimensional material has edge            protruding from internal wall.
AU KRUGLICK E
AE XINOVA LLC (XINO-Non-standard)
   XINOVA LLC (XINO-Non-standard)
   XINOVA LLC (XINO-Non-standard)
GA 2019179431
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises body having greater than or                equal to 1 internal wall (104) defining an internal                chamber (106); and greater than or equal to 1 layer                (108) of a two-dimensional material on a portion of                greater than or equal to 1 internal wall, where                greater than or equal to 1 layer of two-dimensional                material has greater than or equal to 1 edge                protruding from greater than or equal to 1 internal                wall.
   USE - A device for pathogen control system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pressurized carbon dioxide may enter into                the pathogen through the perforations formed from                the graphene edges. This allows for enhanced                pathogen control because the perforations may exert                a first degree of control and the pressurized                carbon dioxide may exert a second degree of                control. The combination of perforations and                pressurized carbon dioxide may provide superior                control over perforations alone and/or pressurized                carbon dioxide alone. The graphene-coated chamber                may receive the fluid suspected of including                pathogens along with the pressurized carbon dioxide                as a mixture or receive the fluid and pressurized                carbon dioxide separately. The graphene-coated                chamber having the fluid suspected of including                pathogens and pressurized carbon dioxide may be                operated in a manner that encourages any pathogens                to contact the graphene edges to increase                perforations in the pathogens, which in turn                increases the amount of pressurized carbon dioxide                in the pathogens.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) pathogen control system comprising the                device, greater than or equal to 1 pressure source                operably coupled with inlet (110) of internal                chamber, and depressurization vessel operably                coupled with outlet (122) of internal                chamber;
   (2) pathogen control system comprising device                comprising body having greater than or equal to 1                internal wall defining internal chamber having                greater than or equal to 1 chamber inlet and                greater than or equal to 1 chamber outlet with                greater than or equal to 1 internal wall between                greater than or equal to 1 chamber inlet and                greater than or equal to 1 chamber outlet, and                greater than or equal to 1 layer of two-dimensional                material on a portion of greater than or equal to 1                internal wall, greater than or equal to 1 layer of                two-dimensional material having greater than or                equal to 1 flake with greater than or equal to 1                edge protruding from greater than or equal to 1                internal wall, greater than or equal to 1 pressure                source operably coupled with greater than or equal                to 1 chamber inlet, and depressurization vessel                operably coupled with chamber outlet;
   (3) controlling pathogens which involves                providing device or system and flowing a                combination of fluid and pressurized carbon dioxide                or processing fluid through internal chamber until                greater than or equal to 1 pathogen is                controlled;
   (4) controlling pathogens which involves                providing graphene-coated chamber comprising body                having greater than or equal to 1 internal wall                defining internal chamber having greater than or                equal to 1 chamber inlet and greater than or equal                to 1 chamber outlet with greater than or equal to 1                internal wall between greater than or equal to 1                chamber inlet and greater than or equal to 1                chamber outlet, and graphene layer on a portion of                greater than or equal to 1 internal wall, where                graphene layer has greater than or equal to 1                graphene flake with greater than or equal to 1                graphene edge protruding from greater than or equal                to 1 internal wall, and flowing a combination of                fluid and pressurized carbon dioxide or processing                fluid through internal chamber until greater than                or equal to 1 pathogen is controlled;
   (5) processing a fluid which involves                providing the device or system and flowing a                combination of a fluid and pressurized carbon                dioxide or processing fluid through the internal                chamber; and
   (6) processing a fluid which involves                providing graphene-coated chamber comprising body                having greater than or equal to 1 internal wall                defining internal chamber having greater than or                equal to 1 chamber inlet and greater than or equal                to 1 chamber outlet with greater than or equal to 1                internal wall between greater than or equal to 1                chamber inlet and greater than or equal to 1                chamber outlet, and graphene layer on a portion of                greater than or equal to 1 internal wall, where                graphene layer has greater than or equal to 1                graphene flake with greater than or equal to 1                graphene edge protruding from greater than or equal                to 1 internal wall, and flowing a combination of a                fluid and pressurized carbon dioxide or processing                fluid through the internal chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                graphene-coated chamber.

   Internal chamber (106)

The                  device further comprises a fluid in the internal                  chamber with the pressurized carbon dioxide or                  processing fluid. Preferred                  Components: The device further comprises pathogen                  in the internal chamber. The pathogen includes a                  microorganism. PN US2021236989-A1; US11446606-B2
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide from gaseous source of            carbon dioxide involves contacting aqueous capture            ammonia with gaseous source of carbon dioxide under            conditions sufficient to produce aqueous ammonium            carbonate.
AU BEWERNITZ M
   SCHNEIDER J
   CONSTANTZ B R
AE BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 202191062X
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering carbon dioxide from gaseous                source of carbon dioxide involves contacting                aqueous capture ammonia with gaseous source of                carbon dioxide under conditions sufficient to                produce aqueous ammonium carbonate, combining                cation source and aqueous ammonium carbonate under                conditions sufficient to produce carbon dioxide                sequestering carbonate and aqueous ammonium salt.                The aqueous capture ammonia is regenerated from                aqueous ammonium salt to sequester carbon dioxide                from the gaseous source of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for sequestering carbon dioxide from                gaseous source of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to sequester carbon dioxide                from gaseous source of carbon dioxide that improves                reflectivity without substantially reducing their                color.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous ammonium                  carbonate comprises both ammonium carbonate and                  ammonium bicarbonate. The cation source comprises                  alkaline earth metal cation. The cation source is                  source of divalent cations. The divalent cations                  comprises alkaline earth metal cations. The                  building material comprises aggregate. The seed                  structure is porous, permeable aggregate material                  that is in-filled by the solid carbon dioxide                  sequestering carbonate to produce less porous,                  denser solid aggregate as compared to the seed                  structure. The in-filled aggregate is in-filled                  on the outer margin to a larger extent than in                  the inner portion. The building material                  comprises roofing granules. Preferred Conditions:                  The aqueous capture ammonia comprises ammonia at                  concentration ranging from 4.0-20.0 M. The                  gaseous source of carbon dioxide is                  multi-component gaseous stream. The gaseous                  source of carbon dioxide is flue gas. The flue                  gas is obtained from industrial source. The                  gaseous source of carbon dioxide is contacted                  with the aqueous capture ammonia using membrane                  contactor. The aqueous ammonium carbonate                  comprises ammonium carbonate and ammonium                  bicarbonate. The divalent alkaline earth metal                  cations are selected from Ca^2^+ and Mg^2^+, and                  combinations. Preferred Process: The method                  involves contacting the regenerated aqueous                  capture ammonia with gaseous source of carbon                  dioxide under conditions sufficient to produce                  aqueous ammonium carbonate. The method involves                  introducing the cation source into a flowing                  aqueous aqueous ammonium carbonate under                  conditions sufficient such that non-slurry solid                  carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate is produced                  in the flowing aqueous aqueous ammonium                  carbonate. The method involves producing solid                  carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate in                  association with a seed structure.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN114017994-A
TI Purifying carbon dioxide by alcohol exhaust gas            involves pre-cooling the exhaust gas before            compression, compressing the pre-cooled exhaust gas            before compressing the gas-liquid separation process,            then the exhaust gas into the compressor.
AU LEI Y
   YAN X
   JIN X
AE CHONGQING RUIXIN GASES CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202228646D
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying carbon dioxide by alcohol exhaust                gas involves pre-cooling the exhaust gas before                compression, then compressing the pre-cooled                exhaust gas before compressing the gas-liquid                separation process, then the exhaust gas into the                compressor, the exhaust gas compressed to 2.5-2.9                megapascal, sending the compressed exhaust gas into                the desulfurizing tower, compressing and                pre-cooling the exhaust gas, then compressing the                pre-cooled exhaust gas and separating the gas and                liquid, recycling the separated light wine, the                separated gas enters into the cold box. The cold                box is provided with a first rectifying tower, a                second rectifying tower and a cold circulating                system, after compressing the gas-liquid separating                process, the separated gas firstly enters the first                rectifying tower for rectifying after rectifying by                the first rectifying tower, the high boiling point                impurity in the exhaust gas is gathered at the                tower kettle of the first rectifying tower.
   USE - Method for purifying carbon dioxide by alcohol                exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process adopts double-tower rectifying and                refrigerating combined process, the heavy component                higher than the carbon dioxide boiling point is                separated and removed by means of rectifying in the                first rectifying tower, improving the purity of the                CO2. The middle part of the second rectification                tower is designed with the product outlet, which                can realize the exchange of the industrial grade                and food grade product and any proportion                switching.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying carbon dioxide by alcohol exhaust                gas involves pre-cooling the exhaust gas before                compression, then compressing the pre-cooled                exhaust gas before compressing the gas-liquid                separation process, then the exhaust gas into the                compressor, the exhaust gas compressed to 2.5-2.9                megapascal, sending the compressed exhaust gas into                the desulfurizing tower, compressing and                pre-cooling the exhaust gas, then compressing the                pre-cooled exhaust gas and separating the gas and                liquid, recycling the separated light wine, the                separated gas enters into the cold box. The cold                box is provided with a first rectifying tower, a                second rectifying tower and a cold circulating                system, after compressing the gas-liquid separating                process, the separated gas firstly enters the first                rectifying tower for rectifying after rectifying by                the first rectifying tower, the high boiling point                impurity in the exhaust gas is gathered at the                tower kettle of the first rectifying tower, the                carbon dioxide and the low boiling point gas are                gathered at the tower top of the first rectifying                tower, the gas collected at the top of the first                rectifying tower enters the second rectifying tower                after passing through the cold circulating system,                the secondary rectifying is carried out in the                second rectifying tower, after rectifying by the                second rectifying tower to obtain food-level liquid                carbon dioxide in the tower kettle of the second                rectifying tower, passes the food-grade liquid                dioxide through the subcooler, then compressed into                the food grade carbon dioxide storage tank to store                by the throttle pressure reducing valve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The separated gas                  after the gas-liquid separation after being                  compressed is in front of the cold box, and then                  the gas is passed through the parking dryer. The                  middle portion of the second rectifying tower is                  provided with a product collecting outlet which                  is connected with the industrial carbon dioxide                  storage tank. The waste material generated after                  the first rectifying tower is removed and the                  waste material after being delight by the second                  rectifying tower are all connected to the drain                  pipeline.

TI Refining aminopyrine crude product by dissolving            aminopyrine, adding aminopyrine water solution, mixing            refined crude material, ethanol and active carbon,            heating to reflux, and filtering with membrane to form            filtrate, crystallizing and performing solid-liquid            separation.
AU SONG Z
   LIN L
   GUO C
   QU R
   WANG L
AE SHANDONG XINHUA PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022C20420
AB    NOVELTY - Process for refining aminopyrine crude product                involves (S1) using subcritical carbon dioxide in a                subcritical reactor to wash the aminopyrine crude                product, and washing the residual extraction agent                in the crude product with subcritical carbon                dioxide, (S2) after subcritical washing, and                dissolving aminopyrine in hot water at                99-111&#8451; to obtain an aminopyrine water                solution, adding the aminopyrine water solution to                a centrifugal machine under the condition of heat                preservation for high-speed centrifugation,                collecting supernatant, and concentrating, cooling,                crystallizing and performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain a refined crude material, and                (S3) mixing the refined crude material, ethanol and                active carbon, heating to reflux, and filtering                with membrane to obtain filtrate, cooling,                crystallizing, and performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain refined product.
   USE - The process is used for refining aminopyrine                crude product.
   ADVANTAGE - The process for refining aminopyrine crude                product solves the problem that the aminopyrine                crude product material is indigestible due to the                relatively large output of analgin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: in step (S2), the                  using amount of the hot water is 1.9-1.1 times of                  the mass of the aminopyrine crude product. In                  step (S3), the mass ratio of the refined crude                  material, ethanol and activated carbon is                  1:(1.7-1.9):(1.11-1.12).Preferred Method: In step (S2), the                  centrifugal machine is continuous high-speed                  tubular centrifugal machine, and the rotating                  speed of the centrifugal machine is 25111-31111                  rpm. In step (S3), the filtering membrane used in                  the membrane filtration is ceramic membrane, and                  the pore size of the ceramic membrane is 1.15-1.1                  &#181;m. In step (S2), the heat preservation                  condition is to maintain the aminopyrine water                  solution at 91-99&#8451;. In step (S2), the                  crystallization temperature is 65-71&#8451;                  and the temperature is cooled down to                  11&#8451; for solid-liquid separation. In                  step (S3), the heating and refluxing condition                  comprises the temperature is heating at                  79-92&#8451;, and the reflux decoloring time                  is 2-3 hours. In step (S3), the crystallization                  temperature is 51-61&#8451; and the                  temperature is cooled down to 11&#8451; for                  solid-liquid separation.Preferred Condition: In step (S1), the                  subcritical carbon dioxide washing reaction                  condition comprises the temperature is less than                  314 K, and the pressure is less than                  7.39&#215;116 Pa.

GA 2022838698
AB    NOVELTY - Production of calcium carbonate involves                absorbing carbon dioxide in a sodium hydroxide                aqueous solution having a concentration of 5-21% to                obtain a sodium carbonate aqueous solution (S1)                having a concentration of 4-24%, hydrating by                reacting calcium oxide with a sodium hydroxide                aqueous solution (S2) having a concentration of                less than 0-6% to obtain lime milk which is a                suspension of calcium hydroxide having a BET                specific surface area of 5-40 m2/g, and carbonating                by adding solution (S1) to the lime milk and                reacting at 9-25 degrees C to obtain calcite                crystalline calcium carbonate having a fine                particle shape with a BET specific surface area of                30-90 m2/g. The initial concentration of the lime                milk is 1-6%.
   USE - Production of calcium carbonate. Uses include                but are not limited to filler for sealing material,                adhesive, rubber composition, paper, pigment for                paper coating, and pigment for coating material and                ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces calcium                carbonate with low environmental load.

PN CN114289058-A
TI Regeneration of metal oxide catalyst supported by aluminum nitride, comprises e.g. using hydrogen to regenerate catalyst, rinsing under pure benzene solvent, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, filtering catalyst, placing in aqueous ammonium bifluoride solution and drying.
AU CAO W
   CAO H
   FENG M
   LIU S
   BIAN X
   WANG M
   ZHENG J
   LI J
   DING D
   LIU Z
   WANG R
   WEN D
   MA D
   FAN L
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202255202R
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration of the metal oxide catalyst supported by aluminum nitride, comprises (1) using hydrogen to regenerate the catalyst, (2) rinsing the catalyst under pure benzene solvent, (3) performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction using catalyst, (4) adding silica sol to the catalyst, and stirring for a period of time at room temperature, (5) drying the above-mentioned sample, (6) placing the dried sample in water at room temperature for ultrasonic treatment, (7) filtering the catalyst out from the above-mentioned solution, and placing in an aqueous ammonium bifluoride solution and stirring for a period of time, and (8) filtering the catalyst out of the above solution, washing with pure water, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for regeneration of metal oxide catalyst supported by aluminum nitride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be carried out at low temperature, avoids the influence of the high temperature regeneration to the catalyst structure, reduces the carbon deposition of complex remove on the catalyst, and improves the activity of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the hydrogen adopts the mixed gas of hydrogen and argon, and the mass fraction of hydrogen in the mixed gas is 10-100%. The hydrogen regeneration pressure is 0.5-5MPa, the regeneration temperature is 200-350degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 1-4 hours; preferably, the mass space velocity of hydrogen is 0.3-3/h. In step (2), the mass space velocity of benzene is 0.5-5/h. In the step (2), the temperature is 64-100degrees Celsius, the pressure is 1-3.6 barA, and the rinsing time is 5-10 hours. In the step (3), the method of cyclic extraction is adopted, the temperature is 35-45degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 30-40MPa. In the step (4), the silica sol is nano-silica sol, the quality of the silica sol is 5-10 times of the catalyst quality, and the stirring time is 5-10 hours. In the step (5), drying is carried out at 150-200degrees Celsius for 6-12 hours. In step (6), the frequency of ultrasonic is 19-59KHz, and ultrasonic time is 6-12 hours. In the step (7), the mass fraction of ammonium bifluoride is 20-40%, and the mass of the ammonium bifluoride aqueous solution is 5-10 times the mass of the catalyst. In the step (8), the filtered catalyst is dried at 110-150degrees Celsius for 6-15 hours. The catalyzer is the catalyzer that is used for catechol and methanol to synthesize guaiacol. The catalyst is an aluminum nitride supported metal oxide catalyst. The metal is iron, zinc, manganese, cerium, and/or lanthanum, preferably iron oxide, zinc oxide, and lanthanum oxide.

PN RO135583-A0
TI Propeller has blast that passes through some pipes to pipe submerged at surface of water in which obliquely sectioned pipe in order to unidirectionally direct explosion blast which instantaneously produces vaporization of water and solubilization of carbon dioxide.
AU PASCU N
AE PASCU N (PASC-Individual)
GA 202247568L
AB    NOVELTY - The propeller has two horizontal tubular chambers that are arranged for explosion of fuel gas and air mixtures, in which the compressed gases are introduced through a supply pipe by the opening of a valve. A cam compresses a spring when a shaft brings the cam over the valve due to the rotation by a gear motor. The valve opens after closing the valve. The compressed air is introduced from a pipe followed by the ignition of the mixture by a spark plug through a perforated side wall when explosion causes the movement of a movable wall which compresses spring and controls the closing of the valve. The blast passes through some pipes to pipe submerged at the surface of the water in which obliquely sectioned pipe in order to unidirectionally direct the explosion blast which instantaneously produces the vaporization of water and solubilization of carbon dioxide while increasing pressure through a nozzle is provided. The pressurized water jet produces mechanical work.
   USE - Propeller.

PT P
PN CN113493392-A
TI Preparing glycine (1-14C) comprises reacting anhydrous nitromethane and metal or metal organic reagent to form nitromethane salt solution in anhydrous ether solvent, reacting with carbon dioxide-14 to obtain nitroacetic acid (1-14C) crude product, and performing reduction reaction with hydrogen.
AU LI G
   CAI D
AE WUXI BEITA PHARMATECH CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C04249
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glycine (1-14C), comprises (a) reacting anhydrous nitromethane and metal or metal organic reagent under a protection of inert gas to form nitromethane salt solution in anhydrous ether solvent, reacting with carbon dioxide-14 (14CO2) to obtain nitroacetic acid (1-14C) crude product; where the 14CO2 is obtained by acidolysis of barium carbonate (14C), and (b) performing reduction reaction of nitroacetic acid (1-14C) crude product with hydrogen under an action of palladium carbon catalyst to obtain glycine (1-14C).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing glycine (1-14C).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short synthesis steps, mild and controllable reaction conditions; does not require high temperature and high pressure severe conditions; does not require the use of highly corrosive, highly toxic and other hazardous chemicals; is easy to synthesize in small amounts and economical; and has high 14C utilization rate. The prepared glycine (1-14C) product: has high purity, good condition and small decrease in specific activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the metal is lithium, magnesium or sodium, and the metal organic reagent is lithium diisopropylamide (LDA) or n-butyllithium. In the step (a), the anhydrous ether solvent is absolute anhydrous ether or absolute anhydrous tetrahydrofuran (THF). In the step (a), the mol ratio of the barium carbonate (14C), anhydrous nitromethane and metal or metal organic reagent is 1:1-1.2:1-20. In the step (a), the acid used for acidolysis is concentrated sulfuric acid. In the step (b), the solvent used in the reduction reaction is a mixed solution of water and acetic acid or methanol hydrochloric acid solution.Preferred Method: The post-treatment process in step (a) comprises after the reaction is completed, adding diluted hydrochloric acid extraction reaction, and adjusting the pH to 0-3 (acidic), filtering to remove the precipitate, and spin drying the solvent to obtain nitroacetic acid (1-14C) crude product. The post-treatment process in step (b) comprises after the reaction is completed, filtering to remove palladium carbon catalyst, vacuum drying the solvent to obtain glycine (1-14C) acetate crude product, dissolving the crude product in absolute ethyl alcohol, using triethylamine ethanol solution to adjust pH to 4.5-7.5, allowing to stand at low temperature for 2-48 hours, separating out glycine (1-14C) solid, and filtering at low temperature to obtain the glycine (1-14C) pure product. The step (b) comprises performing reduction reaction at room temperature for 2-24 hours.

PN CN113322376-A
TI Recycling valuable metal from waste lithium ion            battery active material comprises microwave irradiating            lithium ion battery waste and eutectic solvent mixed            solution, performing electrochemical deposition and            introducing carbon dioxide gas.
AU XU Z
   LIU Z
   JIANG F
   LU Z
   YANG F
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 2021A4117P
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling valuable metal from waste lithium                ion battery active material comprises (i)                pre-processing the waste lithium ion battery to                obtain lithium ion battery waste; (ii) mixing                choline chloride and methanesulfonic acid or                p-toluenesulfonic acid, heating and stirring to                obtain low eutectic solvent; (iii) mixing the                lithium ion battery waste and eutectic solvent,                placing the mixed solution in the sealed container,                and performing microwave irradiation to obtain the                leached product; (iv) filtering the leaching                product and washing with water to obtain leaching                residue and leaching solution; (v) performing                electrochemical deposition to the leaching solution                to obtain the valuable metal and the solution                containing lithium; and (vi) introducing carbon                dioxide gas into the lithium-containing solution to                obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling valuable                metal from waste lithium ion battery active                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally-friendly;                consumes low energy; does not produce toxic                chemical residues; has high efficiency and provides                a fresh idea for the upgrade of recycling schemes                in the field of battery recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The pre-processing                  comprises discharging, disassembling, crushing                  and sieving to obtain the waste lithium ion                  battery anode and cathode mixed waste material                  and the pre-processing is discharging,                  disassembling, crushing, sieving and flotation                  and obtaining waste lithium ion battery anode                  waste. The microwave irradiation time is 3-20                  minutes and the reaction temperature is 100-200                  degrees C. The temperature of the electrochemical                  deposition is 40-80 degrees C and the voltage                  range is -1 to -1.5V. The volumetric flow rate of                  the carbon dioxide gas is 50-150 ml/min, the                  temperature is 80-95 degrees C, and the reaction                  time is 0.5-12 hours. Preferred Components: The                  anode material of the waste lithium ion battery                  comprises lithium cobaltate, lithium nickelate,                  lithium manganate and/or Lithium Nickel Cobalt                  Manganese Oxide. The cathode material of the                  waste lithium ion battery is graphite carbon. The                  waste lithium ion battery waste and the eutectic                  solvent are mixed according to a solid-to-liquid                  ratio of 2.95-14.75 mg/liter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mol ratio of                  choline chloride to methanesulfonic acid or                  p-benzosulfonic acid is 1:2 and the heating                  temperature is 80-120 degrees C.

AU HU Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2021811805
AB    NOVELTY - Composite strengthening method involves                coating the surface of the component uniformly with                a carbon source, using carbon dioxide laser to emit                laser beam and continuously irradiating the surface                of the carbon source to convert into a graphene or                activated carbon layer, laying a constraining layer                on top of the carbon layer. The femtosecond laser                is used to emit pulsed laser beams, and the heating                laser is used to emit continuous laser beams to                ensure that the centers of the two laser spots                overlap and are irradiated on the surface of the                carbon layer, moved the 3D numerical control mobile                platform or laser to the next strengthening                position to carry out laser shock composite                strengthening operations.
   USE - Composite strengthening method used for                laser-induced synthesis of nano-diamonds.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite strengthening method induces and                generates nano-diamond particles on the outer                surface of the damageable component, realizes                direct reinforcement of high-hardness diamond,                generates grain tissue gradient distribution along                the thickness direction and improves the strength                of the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  coating layer of carbon source on the surface of                  the member to be strengthened, where the uniform                  coating method includes brush coating method,                  spray coating method, dip coating method, blade                  coating method, roll coating method, and                  injection method. The purpose is to realize the                  efficient and uniform coating of the carbon                  source on the surface of vulnerable components                  before carbon dioxide laser irradiation, a                  heating platform should be used to heat and                  solidify the carbon source. The heating                  temperature of 200 degrees C and the heating time                  for 15 minutes. The constrained layer is laid on                  the graphene, and the constrained layer includes                  BK-7 glass or quartz glass. The heating laser is                  used to emit a continuous laser beam, and the                  heating laser is a heating laser with a                  wavelength higher than 400 nanometer, and the                  laser is used to provide an auxiliary heat                  source. Preferred Components: The process                  parameters of the femtosecond pulsed laser                  include voltage, current, laser frequency, laser                  energy, laser wavelength, laser pulse width, and                  spot size. The process parameters of                  semiconductor lasers include laser action                  distance and laser spot size. The process                  parameters of the femtosecond pulsed laser                  include voltage, current, laser frequency, laser                  energy, laser wavelength, laser pulse width, and                  spot size.

PN CN112708647-A
TI Pretreating maize straw, by crushing corn stalks,            drying, adding corn stalk powder, pretreating,            filtering, drying washed filter residue, adding acid            xylanase to trisodium citrate buffer solution, and            hydrolyzing mixture.
AU XU J
   GU S
   WU H
   ZUO Z
   WEI W
   HU S
   ZHANG K
   XU X
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 202151470F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for pretreating maize straw with                supercritical carbon dioxide coupled with sodium                hydroxide to improve efficiency of enzymatic                hydrolysis and conversion of xylose, involves                crushing corn stalks, washing with water, drying to                obtain corn stalk powder, adding corn stalk powder                to the alkali solution, uniformly stirring the                mixture to obtain mixture, pretreating the mixture                under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions for                50-70 minutes, where the pretreatment temperature                is 35-70 degrees C, filtering the pretreated                mixture through non-woven fabrics to obtain                filtrate and filter residue, washing the filter                residue with deionized water, drying the washed                filter residue to remove water, adding dried filter                residue into the trisodium citrate buffer solution,                adjusting the pH of the trisodium citrate buffer                solution to 5, adding acid xylanase to the                trisodium citrate buffer solution, and                enzymatically hydrolyzing the mixture for 18-24                hours to obtain xylose.
   USE - The method is useful for pretreating maize                straw (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective to                perform, saves energy consumption, and retains                hemicellulose to the extent to ensure efficiency of                enzymatic hydrolysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The particle size of                  maize straw powder is 30-50 meshes sieve. The                  alkaline solution is 1 wt.% sodium hydroxide                  solution. The pretreatment time of supercritical                  carbon dioxide is 60 minutes, and the treatment                  temperature is 35 degrees C. Preferred Method:                  The method involves placing the washed filter                  residue in an oven at 45 degrees C for 12 hours.                  The method involves adding 50 g maize straw                  powder to 500 ml alkali solution, after mixing                  uniformly, and obtaining mixture. The method                  involves adding 2 g dried filter residue into 100                  ml citric acid trisodium buffer.

PT P
PN CN112645802-A
TI Preparing cannabidiol broad-spectrum oil for effectively removing tetrahydrocannabinol comprises e.g. crushing and drying industrial hemp mosaic material, and storing, and fully dissolving crude extract in winterization solvent.
AU TANG R
   BA Y
   LIU B
   TANG X
   FU Q
   LIU H
AE YUNNAN KUNCHUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (CSHI-C)
GA 202143614G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cannabidiol broad-spectrum oil for effectively removing tetrahydrocannabinol comprises (i) crushing and drying industrial hemp mosaic material, and storing for use after the pretreatment is completed, (ii) sending pretreated raw materials to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system, (iii) fully dissolving the crude extract in the winterization solvent, and initial filtering, preserving heat and winterizing, filtering by centrifugal separation, (iv) taking above-mentioned crude extract, fully dissolving in a decarboxylation solvent for decarboxylation treatment until the carboxylic acid molecules are decarboxylated for releasing carbon dioxide, (v) performing short-range molecular distillation for enriching the decarboxylation-treated extract, and collecting light components, and(vi) using the light components, molecularly distilling are deeply separating and purifying, and removing tetrahydrocannabinol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cannabidiol broad-spectrum oil for effectively removing tetrahydrocannabinol (THC).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to control; reduces THC content; achieves automation and intelligence; reduces human interference; stably controls the quality and uniformity of products; improves production efficiency and product yield; reduces raw material and energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), in the process for pretreatment of the industrial hemp mosaic material, where the pulverized material reaches 5-20 meshes, the drying temperature is 100-200 degrees C, and the moisture content after drying is less than 8%. In the step (ii), in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, the extraction temperature is 35-80 degrees C, extraction time is 1.5-3 hours, and the extraction pressure is 20-40 MPa. In the step (iii), the solid-liquid ratio of the crude extract and the winterization solvent is 1:5-1:20, the winterization temperature is -100 degrees C to -20 degrees C, and the maintaining time is 10-24 hours. In the step (iv), the decarboxylation temperature is 65 degrees C to -105 degrees C. In the step (v), the short-path molecular distillation is divided into three stages, and the first stage adopts a thin film evaporator, second and third stage adopt short-path evaporator, short-path molecular distillation temperature is 120-200 degrees C, and the vacuum degree is 0.001-0.1 mbar.

PN CN111747808-A
TI Hydrocarbons production involves contacting            methane-rich gas with oxygen-rich gas to form feed gas,            and performing oxidative coupling reaction to obtain            carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ethylene, ethane,            carbon product gas.
AU LIU G
   WANG Z
   SUN L
   SHENG Z
   NIE Y
   LI S
   DING L
   ZHAO B
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A1142Q
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbons production involves contacting                methane-rich gas with oxygen-rich gas to form feed                gas, and performing oxidative coupling reaction to                obtain carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, ethylene,                ethane, carbon product gas. The reaction is                completed in one or multiple sets of fluidized bed                reactors connected in series, where each set of                reactors has a heat insulation structure arranged                inside the reactor, and one or multiple sets of                catalyst separation measures arranged near the                reactor outlet. The oxidative coupling reaction                output material enters the catalyst separation                measure for separation, where the separated product                gas heats the raw material gas for the first time.                The separated catalyst is directly reused to the                reactor without the regeneration cycle, and is used                for the raw gas at the same time.
   USE - Method for producing hydrocarbons by using                fluidization technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the hydrocarbons in a                simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                the characteristics of strong exothermic heat of                methane oxidation, large heat capacity and high                heat exchange rate of fluidized bed reactors, and                can realize stable oxidative coupling reaction                under strong exothermic conditions.

PN CN111704700-A; CN111704700-B
TI Preparing hydrogel capable of absorbing anionic            dyes and metal ions involves dissolving            diethylaminoethyl methacrylate,            N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, polyether compound, acid,            initiator, and water under stirring ti get mixed            solution.
AU GUO Z
   ZHANG Y
   XIONG L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA JIAOTONG (UYEJ-C)
GA 2020968279
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogel capable of absorbing                anionic dyes and metal ions involves dissolving                5-10 wt.% diethylaminoethyl methacrylate, 0.1-0.5                wt.% N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, 0.5-1 wt.%                polyether compound, 1-5 wt.% acid, 0.1-0.2 wt.%                initiator, and 10-30 wt.% water under stirring to                get a mixed solution in a certain proportion to get                mixed solution. The toluene is slowly and dropwise                added to the mixed solution, then adding a                polymerization accelerator, and polymerizing at a                certain reaction temperature, and after                polymerization, neutralizing to neutral with                alkaline solution to form a hydrogel that absorbs                anionic dyes and metal ions in response to                expansion by carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrogel capable of                absorbing anionic dyes and metal ions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to preparing hydrogel                capable of absorbing anionic dyes and metal ions,                which has a high adsorption amount to the anionic                dye and metal ion and release rate is higher than                85%, allows to release absorbed dye in the alkaline                environment, and use of carbon dioxide has great                potential application value in the field of                environment waste water treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Componenets: The polyether                  compound is polyoxyethylene or polyoxypropylene                  block copolymer. The acid is selected from                  hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid. The initiator                  is selected from water-soluble initiator                  potassium persulfate or ammonium persulfate.                  Preferred conditions: The amount of toluene is                  60-80 wt.%. The polymerization accelerator is                  tetramethylethylenediamine, and its dosage is                  0.01-0.05 wt.%. The polymerization temperature is                  40-60 degrees C, and polymerization time is 18                  hours. The alkaline solution is 0.5 wt.% sodium                  hydroxide solution. The swelling and adsorbing                  hydrogel of anionic dyes and metal ions is filled                  with carbon dioxide to achieve gel swell, and                  adsorb anionic dyes and metal ions during the                  process of gel swelling.

PN CN211295689-U
TI Radio frequency protection circuit for radio frequency carbon dioxide laser, has radio frequency detection circuit connected with pin of photoelectric coupler through triode, where triode outputs low level signal to pin of coupler.
AU TANG J
   TANG Y
AE NANJING CRD LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020822386
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a radio frequency protection circuit for radio frequency carbon dioxide laser, the radio frequency protection circuit comprises a radio frequency detection circuit, the radio frequency detection circuit is connected with the 7 pin of the photoelectric coupler (U5) through the second triode (Q2); when the radio frequency detection circuit receives the laser fault signal, it will output a signal with large reflection to the second triode (Q2), the second triode (Q2) will output a low level signal to the 7 pin of the photoelectric coupler (U5), the photoelectric coupler (U5) stops working. The utility model detects whether the laser is in the fault state through the radio frequency detection circuit, when identifying the fault, the radio frequency detection circuit outputs a enable signal to the 7 pin of the photoelectric coupler, the photoelectric coupler stops working in time, so that the laser is paused to work; after the fault is removed, restarting the laser, effectively preventing the laser forced operation under the fault state, prolonging the service life of the laser.

GA 2020103129
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing anesthetic water for anesthetizing fish and shellfish involves adjusting the concentration of salt in water according to an anesthetic subject, and adjusting the concentration of carbon dioxide to produce an anesthetic effect.
   USE - The method is useful for producing anesthetic water for anesthetizing fish and shellfish (claimed), calming before transporting live fish, and sedating fish and shellfish.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides excellent anesthetizing effect on fish and shellfish at an early stage when transporting live fish over a long distance, calming before transporting live fish and sedating for vaccination, weighing, gear cutting, and cage storage before shipping, and thus contributes to an increase in transportation volume per time, shortens anesthetizing time until the onset of anesthesia, and reduces burden on fish and shellfish once landed for vaccination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for anesthetizing fish and shellfish, which involves providing anesthetic water obtained by the method to fish and shellfish for sedation before being stored in a live fish transport container or during cultivation, and for calming maintenance at the time of transport of fish and shellfishes in accommodation to a live fish transport container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves adjusting the salt concentration to approach osmotic pressure of a body fluid of an anesthetized subject before anesthesia. The method involves adjusting the concentration of carbon dioxide by mixing seawater and carbonated freshwater obtained by carbonated freshwater. The method involves adjusting the concentration of carbon dioxide by mixing carbonated seawater obtained by carbonated seawater with fresh water. The method involves adjusting the carbon dioxide concentration by mixing a carbonated seawater obtained by carbonated seawater and a carbonated freshwater obtained by carbonated freshwater. The method involves carbonating the mixed water in the water tank in which seawater and freshwater are mixed, and adjusting the carbon dioxide concentration. The carbonation is performed by pressurized dissolution of carbon dioxide by a carbonator.
   ACTIVITY - Anesthetic; Sedative. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110538215-A
TI Extracting sterols from pecan husk comprises e.g. crushing the fresh pecan husks and soaking in water, loading it into sieve blue and immersing in liquid nitrogen, taking out and placing at room temperature and repeating the process.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 2019A4431N
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting sterols from pecan husk comprises (i) crushing the fresh pecan husks and soaking in water, loading it into sieve blue and immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, taking out and placing at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, immersing the sieve blue as whole in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, and repeating the process for 2-3 times, (ii) drying the pecan husk at low temperature, pulverizing, and passing through 5-30 meshes sieve to obtain pecan husk powder, (iii) mixing the pecan husk powder with solvent and placing into the extraction kettle, passing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, controlling the temperature of the extraction kettle to 40-50 degrees C, and the extraction pressure is 30-45Mpa, (iv) flowing out the carbon dioxide containing extract from extraction kettle, entering the separation kettle for separation under reduced pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting sterols from pecan husk.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the loss of sterol during extraction process, improves the quality and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), placing the pecan husk powder into the extraction kettle and mixing with 0.01-0.08 times the weight of the biological enzymes thoroughly and allowing it to stand for 30-60 minutes, where the biological enzyme is beta -glucosidase, pectinase and cellulase. In the step (ii), drying the pecan husks under low temperature vacuum, where the process is by staggered pile of pecan husks in vacuum drying box, control the vacuum degree to 0.009-0.36Mpa and the vacuum drying temperature to 40-52 degrees C. The pecan palm shell is dried to a moisture content of 5% or less. In the step (iv), the pressure of the separation kettle is 7-9Mp and the temperature is 25-30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The solvent is low boiling point solvent, preferably, the solvent is acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol, and/or dichloromethane.
