TI Cementitious material made of hydraulic cement-free ground granulated blast-furnace slags capable of reducing carbon dioxide emissions and applied to precast products.
AU FAN C
   CHAO S
AE FAN C (FANC-Individual)
GA 201992259K
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention is a cementitious material made of hydraulic cement-free ground granulated blast-furnace slags, the cementitious material is prepared by mixing ground granulated blast-furnace slag powder and quicklime, wherein the main chemical composition of the ground granulated blast-furnace slag powder comprises CaO and SiO2, and must meet the standard of CNS12549; the main chemical composition of quicklime is CaO, and the content must be 90% or higher. After a hydration reaction is generated by mixing the cementitious material made of hydraulic cement-free ground granulated blast-furnace slags with water, a good cementation and hardening effect is thus produced. The cementitious material is used to completely replace the traditional cement, and as a cementitious material for concrete or mortar, in addition to reducing the amount of cement used to reduce carbon dioxide emissions, also achieving the purpose of recycling the industrial wastes to the maximum. Further, this creation has a property of short curing time, which is very suitable for being used in precast products, and can increase the frequency of use of precast products.

PN DE102017012059-A1
TI Method for producing regenerative energy by buoyancy forces in water using specific properties of carbon dioxide in e.g. hydroelectric power plants, involves heating liquefied gas in transcritical region to create buoyancy of container.
AU SEUFFERHELD J E
AE SEUFFERHELD J E (SEUF-Individual)
GA 201958739P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directly or indirectly combining left-handed camot process of a refrigeration cycle represented in Mollier pressure-enthalpy diagram with right-handed camot process. Liquefied gas is contained in a container (1) by pressurizing a geodesic water column. Pressure of the liquefied gas is isochorically increased. The liquefied gas is heated and evaporated in a transcritical region to create buoyancy of the container. Expanded liquefied gas is regenerated in a closed circuit after the buoyancy is carried out. The left and right-handed camot processes are utilized to directly or indirectly generate buoyancy force.
   USE - Method for producing regenerative energy by buoyancy forces in water using specific properties of carbon dioxide in hydroelectric power plants, offshore platforms and deep boreholes or taverns.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring better continuous power generation and energy storage function to guarantee safe and environmental friendly power supply in networks in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a buoyancy tank in an open condition.

   Piston (2)

PN CN109678697-A
TI Recycling of fatty acid surface treatment agent by using calcium carbonate comprises performing surface treating, adding weak acid, stirring, subjecting to pressure filtration, introducing into precipitation tank and discharging.
AU ZHAN Q
   ZHU Y
   LAN Q
   WU L
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201940715U
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of fatty acid surface treatment agent by using calcium carbonate comprises (i) performing surface treatment of calcium carbonate slurry containing fatty acid salt surface treatment agent in a surface processing tank at less than or equal to 45 degrees C, (ii) adding weak acid and/or carbon dioxide to the processing tank, stirring, adjusting the pH of the slurry to 6.8-8.5, subjecting to pressure filtration to separate the calcium carbonate product and filtering the wastewater and (iii) introducing into a precipitation tank, allowing to stand for 2-4 hours, and discharging the supernatant liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling of fatty acid surface treatment agent by using calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes a weak acid or carbon dioxide to convert a sodium potassium salt of a fatty acid having a high solubility into a fatty acid having a low solubility, which adheres to a calcium carbonate product as a coating agent; reduces the pollutant discharge of the filtering of wastewater; and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises cooling to 20-30 degrees C. The step (ii) comprises controlling the reaction for 15-30 minutes. Preferred Components: The weak acid is phosphoric acid or citric acid. The concentration of carbon dioxide is 30-40%. The fatty acid salt is sodium fatty acid or potassium fatty acid.

   USE - Preparation method of foamed nylon beads for preparing foamed nylon composite material (claimed) used in shoe, automobile, cushioning packaging material, and sports protective gear.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of foamed nylon beads with excellent mechanical property, alkali resistance, reducing agent resistance, mildew proof effect, mothproof effect, low-temperature resistance and soundproof effect, and lightweight.

Preferred Composition: The content of nylon in the nylon beads to-be-foamed is 30-70 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: In the step (2), the pressure is 7.38-30 MPa, the temperature is 32-90 degrees C, and the pressure holding penetration time is 0.5-10 hours. In the step (2), the foaming temperature is 90-150 degrees C and the foaming time is 10-90 seconds. In the step (2) of preparing foamed nylon composite material, the temperature of the foamed nylon composite material is 110-190 degrees C, the pressure is 0.07-0.45 MPa, and the time is 2-5 minutes. Preferred Properties: In the step (2), the foaming rate of the nylon beads to-be-foamed is 4-11 times. The density of the nylon beads to-be-foamed is 0.08-0.3 g/cm3.

PN CN109289140-A
TI Jet type automatic fire extinguisher, has hollow metal shell whose wall surface is provided with multiple orifices, where hollow metal shell is filled with sodium bicarbonate powder, and jet hole whose outer part is covered with latex films.
AU LV H
AE LV H (LVHH-Individual)
GA 2019147958
AB    NOVELTY - The extinguisher has a hollow metal shell whose wall surface is provided with multiple orifices. The hollow metal shell is filled with sodium bicarbonate powder. An outer part of a gas jet hole is covered with latex films. A holding cavity is filled with high water absorbing resin powder, active Carbon powder, ultra-absorbent resin, active Carbon powder and Sodium bicarbonate powder, where 2-5 wt.% of gold nano-rods and 10-20 wt.% nanometer titanium dioxide and mass ratio of the stabilizer to sodium hydrogencarbonate powder is 1: (100- 900).
   USE - Jet type automatic fire extinguisher.
   ADVANTAGE - The extinguisher can generate carbon dioxide gas after sodium hydrogen carbonate is heated so as to eject the carbon dioxide gas through the gas jet hole, thus reducing oxygen concentration at a fire site and achieving effect of controlling fire and ensuring high heat transfer efficiency of the metal shell and sensitivity of reaction at time of fire.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a jet type automatic fire extinguisher.

PT P
PN CN109168975-A
TI Method for cultivating Ganoderma lucidum, involves            preparing culture medium followed by sterilizing,            inoculating with Ganoderma lucidum strain, bagging,            culturing to obtain stipe, transferring to greenhouse            and harvesting.
AU LI J
AE HUBEI WUZHITANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2019084978
AB    NOVELTY - A Ganoderma lucidum cultivation method                involves preparing culture medium, packing to                obtain G.lucidum culture medium, sterilizing the                culture medium at 0.1-3 MPa and 100-105 degrees C                for 2-4 hours, cooling, adjusting water content to                63-70 wt.% using sterile water, sealing the cooled                culture medium, inoculating with G.lucidum strain,                bagging the medium, transferring the bag to dark                room for cultivation, controlling temperature in                the dark room to 26-28 degrees C and humidity to                70-80%, maintaining light intensity at 200-900 lx                and concentration of carbon dioxide to 500-700                parts per million, obtaining stipe, transferring                the bag with the stipe to greenhouse, opening the                bag, controlling temperature to 22-28 degrees C and                humidity to 80-90% in the greenhouse, maintaining                light intensity at 3000-6500 lx, adjusting humidity                to 90-95% and carbon dioxide concentration to                600-1000 parts per million and harvesting                G.lucidum.
   USE - Method for cultivating G.lucidum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating G.lucidum by                effectively simulating growth of G.lucidumunder                natural conditions and providing suitable growth                environment for growth of G.lucidum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture medium                  (0.5-0.6 kg) comprises 100 pts. wt. wood chips,                  3-9 pts. wt. soybean hull, 6-10 pts. wt.                  fermented bran, 2-4 pts. wt. soybean meal, 2-4                  pts. wt. humate, 6-14 pts. wt. fish bone meal,                  11-24 pts. wt. pine needle powder, 2-4 pts. wt.                  mushroom residue.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN CN108893775-A; CN108893775-B
TI Preparing high-purity calcium carbonate whisker            involves screening and grinding calcium carbonate ore            obtained from mining area and calcining and decomposing            into calcium oxide at high temperature, adding calcium            oxide to deionized water.
AU WANG Z
   LI Q
   YIN C
   DAI Z
   DU P
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   ZHEJIANG ANLIAN MINING CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   WUYUAN SHENGLI PLASTICS CO LTD (WUYU-Non-standard)
GA 2018971445
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity calcium carbonate                whisker involves screening and grinding the calcium                carbonate ore obtained from the mining area and                calcining and decomposing into calcium oxide at a                high temperature. The calcium oxide is added to                deionized water to fully digest to obtain calcium                hydroxide suspension, magnesium chloride is used as                a crystal form controlling agent, dissolved in                water, and atomized by digested calcium hydroxide                suspension and added to the magnesium chloride                solution, and the mixture is uniformly stirred to                obtain a mixed liquid. The carbon dioxide gas is                introduced into the mixed liquid through an                aeration device, and the carbonization reaction is                carried out by stirring until the pH of the                solution is lowered to 7 and the reaction is                stopped. The precipitateis subjected to suction                filtration, washed, and dried to obtain high-purity                calcium carbonate whiskers.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity calcium                carbonate whisker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity                calcium carbonate whisker in cost effective manner                which saves great extent of carbon dioxide                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The average particle                  diameter of the calcium carbonate ore after the                  screening and grinding is 1.29 to 4.24 mu m. The                  amount of magnesium chloride in the third step is                  such that molar ratio of magnesium to calcium                  i.e., CMg/CCa is 1.5 and 2.5. The aeration device                  comprises quartz bubble stone, porous ceramic, or                  porous brick, and the pore diameter is not more                  than 5 millimeter. The aeration device is placed                  at the bottom of the mixed liquid. Preferred                  Condition: The calcium oxide is added to                  deionized water at 80 to 100 degrees C for a                  digestion time of at least 1 hours. The stirring                  rate in the fourth step is 100 and 200                  revolutions/minute.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

The solution is adjusted to 1 l in 1640 sterile medium. The amount of active peptide in the basic medium is 0.3%.

AU HOU L
   YANG H
   LI Y
   LIU Y
   LIU G
   WANG Y
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
GA 201853362J
AB    NOVELTY - A flexible metal organic frame material (I) is new. The compound (I) contains cobalt-tetrazole-nuclear cluster unit, forms layered structure by coordination extension of tzba, and has adjacent layers connected by 2-bpy to form dual three-dimensional frame.
   USE - Flexible metal organic frame material used for adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide and methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has simple preparation technique, mild reaction condition, easily obtained raw material and good adsorption selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flexible metal organic frame material of formula (Co3(tzba)2(bpy)3(F)2).DMF.C2H5OH.2H2O (I) is new. The compound (I) contains cobalt-tetrazole-nuclear cluster unit, forms layered structure by coordination extension of tzba, and has adjacent layers connected by 2-bpy to form dual three-dimensional frame.
   tzba=4-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)benzoic acid negative divalent anionic ligand;
   bpy=4,4'-bipyridyl;
   DMF=N,N-dimethylformamide; and
   tzba and 2-bpy=group of formulae (i)-(ii).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is prepared by mixing 120-140 pts. mol N,N-dimethyl formamide, 320-360 pts. mol ethanol and 2110-2330 pts. mol water to form mixed liquid, adding 57 pts. mol fluoroboric acid to form mixed solvent, adding 0.9-1.1 pts. mol cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, 0.9-1.1 pts. mol 4,4'-bipyridyl and 0.75-0.85 pts. mol 4-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)benzoic acid, stirring for 15-30 minutes to form mixture, placing into high-pressure reaction kettle, sealing and heating to 110-120 degrees C, keeping for 48-72 hours, cooling to room at speed of 0.1-0.2 degrees C/minute to obtain reaction solution, filtering to obtain filtrate, washing with ethanol to obtain pink-shaped crystal to obtain initial sample, exchanging with methanol for 40-60 hours, and vacuum heating at 150-160 degrees C for 4-6 hours.

A carbon dioxide (CO2) collection unit (13) collects CO2 in integrated combustion exhaust gas from which exhaust heat collected by the integrated exhaust heat recovery boiler.
   USE - Waste gas processing apparatus for gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen oxide in integrated combustion exhaust gas is removed efficiently, without providing waste-heat-recovery unit in secondary waste gas flow path, so that reduction of increase of installation cost is attained.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the waste gas processing apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Waste gas processing apparatus (1)

   CO2 collection unit (13)

PT P
PN EP3339251-A1; PT109803-A1; EP3339251-B1
TI Treating wastewater from ammonium nitrate/fuel oil- and emulsion-type explosive industries by adding hydrated lime to to-be-treated wastewater and carbonating with atmospheric carbon dioxide by exposure of supernatant to atmosphere.
AU NUNES DE CARVALHO M F
   ARAUJO ALMEIDA M A
   DA CRUZ GODINHO RIBAU TEIXEIRA
   DA SILVA PRAZERES A R
   CHAVES H M I
   SIMAO MADEIRA L M
   DE CARVALHO M J N
   DA CRUZ GODINHO M M
   TEIXEIRA R
   TOMAS CHAVES H M I
AE POLITECNICO BEJA INST (POLI-Non-standard)
   INST POLITECNICO BEJA (POLI-Non-standard)
GA 201849904H
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater from ammonium nitrate/fuel oil (ANFO)-type and emulsion-type explosive industries is treated by (a) chemical precipitation by adding 1-20 g hydrated lime (A) per 1 L to-be-treated wastewater (B) while stirring until solution reaches alkaline pH of 9-12.5; and (b) carbonation with atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO2) by exposure of supernatant (C) to atmosphere, in the absence or presence of precipitate (D) obtained in step (a), for up to about 20 days.
   USE - Treatment of wastewater from ammonium nitrate/fuel oil (ANFO)-type and emulsion-type explosive industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The treatment process reduces the organic content of the effluent from the explosive industries and simultaneously produces by-products with value-added. In addition, the process enables the capture and collection of compounds present in the effluent obtained and their subsequent reuse, both in the explosive industries and in other industries where they are required.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the wastewater treatment process.
   Hydrated lime (A)
   To-be-treated wastewater (B)
   Supernatant (C)
   Precipitate or sludge (D)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: Step (b) is performed by exposure of supernatant (C) to the atmosphere, in the presence of precipitate (B) for up to about 20 days. Step (a) is performed by adding 5-10 g, preferably 6-8 g hydrated lime (A) for each liter of wastewater of explosives (B). Step (a) is performed under stirring conditions of 300-400 rpm. Step (b) is performed in an open container with volume/area ratio of 0.26-0.37 m3/m2 of surface area. Step (b) is performed for 5-15 days, preferably 10-12 days. The method further comprises an ammonia capture and collection system for the ammonia generated in the process itself.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

TI Photo-acoustic gas analyzer for determining            content of e.g. carbon dioxide in ambient air, has            sensor for detecting physical dimension, which            indicates reciprocal effect degree between radiation            and to-be-analyzed gas.
AU DEHE A
   GLACER C
   ONARAN G
   TUMPOLD D
   GLAENZER C
   DEU A
AE INFINEON TECHNOLOGIES AG (INFN-C)
   INFINEON TECHNOLOGIES AG (INFN-C)
   INFINEON TECHNOLOGIES AG (INFN-C)
GA 201846781A
AB    NOVELTY - The analyzer (100) has a gas chamber (102) for                receiving to-be-analyzed gas. A radiation source                (104) emits an electromagnetic radiation into the                gas chamber, where the electromagnetic radiation is                adapted to receive gas molecules contained in the                to-be-analyzed gas, which is received in the gas                chamber. A collimator (112) collimates the                electromagnetic radiation, which is emitted by the                radiation source. A sensor (106) detects physical                dimension, which indicates reciprocal effect degree                between the electromagnetic radiation emitted by                the radiation source and the to-be-analyzed                gas.
   USE - Photo-acoustic gas analyzer for determining                content of gas such as carbon dioxide and/or toxic                gases, in ambient air.
   ADVANTAGE - The electromagnetic radiation is adapted to                receive the gas molecules contained in the                to-be-analyzed gas, so that concentration of the                gas molecules can be increased. The analyzer can be                structured in a simple manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                photo-acoustic gas analyzer.
   Photo-acoustic gas analyzer (100)
   Gas chamber (102)
   Radiation source (104)
   Sensor (106)
   Collimator (112)

TI Domestic appliance for facilitating production and dispensing of carbonated beer, has carbonation unit provided with diluent inlet and pressurized gas inlet, where carbonated diluent and beverage concentrate are mixed.
AU PEIRSMAN D
   VANDEKERCKHOVE S
AE ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
GA 2018442257
AB    NOVELTY - The appliance (1) has a carbonation unit provided with a diluent inlet and a pressurized gas inlet, where carbonated diluent and beverage concentrate are mixed, and foam height of final constituted beverage is 6 millimeter and foam half life is greater than 15 seconds. The carbonation unit generates gaseous bubbles. A flow rate controller regulates a flow rate through a liquid line such that the flow rate controller regulates residence time of liquid to maintain gas dissolved in the liquid. A diluent pressure control unit controls pressure on a diluent at the diluent inlet.
   USE - Domestic appliance for facilitating production and dispensing of carbonated beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The appliance reduces radial distance between a sparger surface and an internal carbonator wall to increase annular velocity of water leading to efficient distribution of carbon dioxide within the water, thus improving dissolution of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbonation unit is an in-line carbonation unit (4).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a carbonation unit integrated in an appliance.

PT P
PN CN114797910-A
TI Preparation of hetero-junction nanosheet with            two-dimensional ultra-thin structure used in            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves            adding bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate and            polyvinylpyrrolidone to mannitol solution, injecting            sodium bromide, heating, cooling, washing and            drying.
AU TANG J
   ZHU X
   GUAN M
   GONG X
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 2022A2129E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hetero-junction nanosheet with                two-dimensional ultra-thin structure involves                preparing mannitol solution under magnetic stirring                at room temperature, adding bismuth(III) nitrate                pentahydrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone to mannitol                solution, injecting a specific amount of sodium                bromide while stirring, adjusting the pH value of                the reaction system by sodium hydroxide solution to                obtain mixture, putting the mixture into                polytetrafluoroethylene stainless steel autoclave,                heating, naturally cooling, centrifuging, washing                and vacuum drying to obtain bismuth oxide/bismuth                oxybromide nanosheet material.
   USE - Preparation of hetero-junction nanosheet with                two-dimensional ultra-thin structure used in                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide with high efficiency and                selectivity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, controllable                experimental conditions and good reproducibility.                The method uses polyvinylpyrrolidone which control                the ultra-thin two-dimensional structure. The high                specific surface area exposed by the                two-dimensional ultra-thin structure, and the                matching of the energy band structures of the two                semiconductors, the recombination of photogenerated                electrons and holes can be effectively                reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  mannitol solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/L. The addition                  amount of mannitol solution is 25-40 mL.Preferred Condition: The pH of the reaction                  system adjusted to 4,5,6 and 7. The heating                  process is carried out at 160-180&#8451; for                  3-4 hours. The centrifugal rotating speed is                  10000 rpm/minute. The washing process is carried                  out for three times using deionized water and                  ethanol. The vacuum drying is carried out at                  60&#8451; for 2 hours.

The titanium dioxide complex is doped with a metal. PN TW748776-B1; TW202223108-A
TI Recovery of vanadium from            nickel/iron/calcium/vanadium-containing concentrate            involves mixing non-magnetic phase with            sodium-containing alkaline compound, oxidizing mixture,            soaking roasted product in water, and dissolving            vanadium in water.
AU SUN Y
   TSAI M
   LIU Y
AE HONG JING METAL CORP (HONG-Non-standard)
GA 202234075B
AB    NOVELTY - A method for recovering vanadium from a                nickel/iron/calcium/vanadium-containing concentrate                is used to solve the problem of the conventional                method for recovering vanadium being not suitable                for the nickel/iron/calcium/vanadium-containing                concentrate. The method includes providing the                nickel/iron/calcium/vanadium-containing concentrate                with a particle size ranging from 100 mesh to 250                mesh. Magnetic separated the                nickel/iron/calcium/vanadium-containing concentrate                to obtain a non-magnetic phase. The non-magnetic                phase is mixed with a sodium-containing alkaline                compound in a weight ration being 1: 0.4 to obtain                a mixture. The mixture is then oxidizing roasted at                900 for 3-4 hours to obtain a roasted product and                carbon dioxide (CO2(g)). The roasted product is                soaked in water of 80 with introduction of carbon                dioxide (CO2(g)) obtained from the oxidizing                roasting reaction. Vanadium in the roasted product                is dissolved in the water to form a pregmamt                solution of vanadium including sodium vanadate                (V).

PN BR102020005994-A2
TI Biogas purifying using magnetic double layer hydroxides as adsorbents involves contacting between magnetic HDL (A1) and biogas (B), whose biogas contains methane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, in fixed bed or under agitation.
AU BAIL A
   ZIGLIOLI E
   BARALDI I J
   FRARE L M
   MOISES M P
   MELLO GIONA R
AE UNIV PARANA TECNOLOGICA FEDERAL (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 202209869N
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas purifying using magnetic double layer hydroxides as adsorbents involves contacting between magnetic HDL (A1) and biogas (B), in the proportion of 2,000 (two thousand) milliliters of biogas for each gram of HDL up to 10,000 (ten thousand) milliliters of biogas for each gram of HDL, whose biogas contains methane (from 60-80 vol.%), carbon dioxide (from 8-40 vol.%) and hydrogen sulfide (from 50-7000 ppm), in fixed bed or under agitation and variable temperature between 50-200 degrees C, decreasing in system pressure (between 0.50-0.10 mBar) with the use of a vacuum pump, establishing chemical equilibrium (between 1-24 hours) and removal of the aliquot for chromatographic analysis (C) and classification as biomethane (E), regenerating adsorbent (D) by heat treatment in a drying oven at a temperature between 105-120 degrees C (A2) to be reused in the biogas purification process (R1) and reusing gasses removed from the adsorbent (F).
   USE - Biogas purifying using magnetic double layer hydroxides as adsorbents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require sophisticated infrastructure, in simple manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The HDLs(A1) are formed by a magnetic core of magnetite or maghemite and a lamellartypic double hydroxide coating. The HDLs (A1) can be formed by magnesium and aluminum in the 2:1 ratio, magnesium and aluminum in the ratios 2:1, 3:1 and 4:1, prepared in the presence of the surfactant cetyltrimethyl ammonium bromide (CTAB) and hydroxides double magnetic lamellar lithium and aluminum and bismuth and aluminum, both in a 2:1 ratio.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

The first gas is argon, and the second gas is carbon dioxide. The ratio of carbon dioxide in the first and second gases is 10-20%. A skimmer (15) is provided for selecting the gas clusters.

PT P
PN CN113289664-A; CN113289664-B
TI Preparing Schottky junction photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, involves reacting aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide comprising thiocyanuric acid with aqueous solution of cadmium salt.
AU DAI K
   LI X
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A0382Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Schottky junction Sn/Cd3(C3N3S3)2 photocatalyst involves adding thiocyanuric acid to the aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and dissolving to form first solution. Cadmium (Cd) salt is added to deionized water to form a second solution under vigorous stirring. The first solution is added to the second solution and aged for 10-24 hours under mild stirring. The dispersed precipitate is washed, centrifuged, and dried to obtain the octahedral Cd3(C3N3S3)2. The octahedral Cd3(C3N3S3)2 is dispersed into the sodium borohydride solution. Tin (Sn) salt is added to the dispersion, stirred for a period of time, centrifuged, washed and dried to obtain the precipitate of octahedral Sn/Cd3(C3N3S3)2 Schottky junction photocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing Schottky junction Sn/Cd3(C3N3S3)2 photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst preparation method is simple and cost effective. The product show high photocatalytic performance and strong circulation stability to reach the important function of carbon neutralization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a Schottky junction Sn/Cd3(C3N3S3)2 photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cadmium salt is selected from one or more of cadmium chloride, cadmium nitrate, cadmium acetate, cadmium sulfate, cadmium carbonate or cadmium iodide. The solvent used in the sodium borohydride solution is selected deionized water or absolute ethanol. The tin salt is selected from sodium stannate, stannous acetate, stannous sulfate, stannous chloride and tin tetrachloride. The solvent used for washing is absolute ethanol or deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of sodium hydroxide in first solution is 0.1-2g/l, and the concentration of thiocyanuric acid is 0.2-4g/l. The concentration of cadmium salt in second solution is 0.1-5 g/l. The drying is vacuum drying or freeze drying. The molar ratio of octahedral Cd3(C3N3S3)2. The molar ratio of sodium borohydride to tin salt is 1:5-12:0.01-0.1.

PT P
PN CN113217081-A
TI Controllable combustion gas removing method for high gas, and low permeability coal seam, involves selecting high gas, and low air permeability coal seam that is planned to be mined, and taking coal layer sample, measuring gas volume.
AU LIANG Y
   LI X
   CHEN Q
   HU Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202193950M
AB    NOVELTY - The controllable combustion gas removing method involves selecting a high gas, and low air permeability coal seam that is planned to be mined, and taking a coal layer sample. The gas volume is measured in the coal layer, and equidistant ground wells are drilled on ground of the coal bed. The oxygen is injected from the ground well to the coal layer, and the well head of the ground well is temporarily plugged after injecting so as to fully mix the gas with oxygen. The alkaline aqueous solution is injected into the coal layer by the ground well, and combustion product carbon dioxide is dissolved.
   USE - Controllable combustion gas removing method for a high gas, and low permeability coal seam.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas is converted into the carbon dioxide gas easy to be dissolved in an alkaline aqueous solution by the controllable combustion, so as to reach the purpose of eliminating the coal bed gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the controllable combustion gas removing method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Phellinus igniarius fruiting body extract used for            treating chronic renal failure, prepared by carrying            out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of            Phellinus igniarius using entrainer comprising            anhydrous ethanol and ethyl acetate mixed solution, and            vacuum rotary evaporating.
AU CHEN A
   LIU E
   WU Y
   SHAO Y
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 202181487A
AB    NOVELTY - Extract of Phellinus igniarius fruiting body,                is prepared by (i) cleaning P.igniarius fruiting                body, drying, crushing and sieving with 60-100 mesh                sieve, (ii) carrying out supercritical extraction                of 100 g P.igniarius fruiting body at carbon                dioxide flow rate of 10-20 L/hour using entrainer                comprising anhydrous ethanol and ethyl acetate                mixed solution, and extracting for 4-10 hours at                extraction temperature of 30-40 degrees C, and                pressure of 15-25 MPa and (iii) after extracting,                vacuum rotary evaporating residual ethanol and                ethyl acetate to obtain the P.igniarius fruiting                body extract.
   USE - The P.igniarius fruiting body extract is used                in preparing medicine for treating chronic renal                failure (claimed), enhancing immunity, and                promoting recovery of kidney function, kidney-yin                deficiency caused by chronic renal failure,                dizziness and tinnitus, soft waist and knee, night                sweat, spermatorrhea and other symptoms.
   ADVANTAGE - The P.igniarius fruiting body extract has                improved curative effect, has no toxicity and side                effect and is safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: In the step (i), the                  drying temperature is 55-65 degrees C and the                  drying time is 1-3 hours. In the step (i),                  sieving uses 80 mesh sieve. In the step (ii), the                  extracting time is 6 hours. In the step (ii), the                  extraction temperature is 36 degrees C and the                  pressure is 20 MPa. Preferred Composition: In the                  step (ii), the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15                  L/hour. In the step (ii), the entrainer is mixed                  with absolute ethanol and ethyl acetate solution                  in a volume ratio of 7:3.
   ACTIVITY - Nephrotropic; Immunostimulant;                Antiinfertility; Auditory.

PN CN112941553-A
TI Preparing three-dimensional nano-tip copper            electrode useful for reduction of electro-catalysis            carbon dioxide, involves e.g. electrically polishing            under action of constant potential, and washing            residual polishing solution with pure water.
AU ZHAO J
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 202171157D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing three-dimensional nano-tip copper                electrode comprises (i) using phosphoric acid as                electro-polishing solution, graphite rod as counter                electrode and copper foil as working electrode,                electrically polishing for 1-3 minutes under the                action of 4 V constant potential, washing the                residual polishing solution with ultra-pure water,                immediately using nitrogen to blow the electrode                for use, (ii) taking 13-18 ml 0.02 M copper sulfate                and 2-4 ml 0.1 M potassium dihydrogen phosphate                solution, stirring to make the solution mixed                uniformly, and (iii) taking the mixed solution of                the step (ii) as the electro-deposition solution,                taking the electro-polishing copper foil as the                working electrode, the platinum sheet is the                counter electrode, silver/silver chloride as the                reference electrode, and depositing for 600-1000                seconds, under the potential of -0.6 V to -0.8                Vvs.
   USE - The electrode is useful for reduction of                electro-catalysis carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode has better stability and                catalytic activity. The method has simple                technique, convenient operation and easy scale                preparation.

PN CN112875739-A; CN112875739-B
TI Preparing active light calcium carbonate, involves            grinding limestone, adding calcium oxide powder to hot            water for digestion, passing calcium hydroxide            suspension and compressed gas using fluid atomization            nozzles, dispersing and drying.
AU CHEN X
   CHEN Z
AE CHANGXING XIEYUAN CHEM AUXILIARY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202164846M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing active light calcium                carbonate, involves (a) grinding limestone to                0.2-0.5 mm, performing negative pressure                calcinations process at calcination temperature of                750-780 degrees C for 50-75 minutes, and                introducing oxygen in calcination process to obtain                calcium oxide powder, (b) crushing calcium oxide                powder to 10-50 mu m, adding calcium oxide powder                to hot water for digestion, adding water for                diluting, adding small amount of cerium oxide and                yttrium oxide, and uniformly stirring at high speed                to obtain calcium hydroxide suspension with                concentration of 0.1-0.3 %mass, (c) passing calcium                hydroxide suspension and carbon dioxide compressed                gas using two fluid atomization nozzles, dispersing                calcium hydroxide suspension in micron-scale                droplets by high-pressure carbon dioxide,                performing carbonization reaction, generating light                calcium carbonate, and rapidly drying light calcium                carbonate powder by hot air flow to obtain light                calcium carbonate powder.
   USE - Method for preparing active light calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the oil absorption                performance and whiteness of light calcium                carbonate, and is simple and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the                  negative pressure condition is-100 to-50 kPa. In                  step (c), the carbon dioxide gas flow rate is                  25-35 l/minute. Preferred Composition: In step                  (b), the addition amount of cerium oxide is                  0.08-0.12%, and the addition amount of yttrium                  oxide is 0.01-0.02% based on total addition                  amount of calcium oxide. The mass ratio of cerium                  oxide and yttrium oxide is (6-10):1.

PT P
PN CN112768714-A
TI Preparing a hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electrocatalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon useful in fuel cell has in-situ oxidizing and polymerizing poly-dopamine and absorbing transition metal ion cobalt ion and solidifying.
AU NIE Y
   GUO W
   DENG M
   YANG R
   ZHANG S
   WANG H
AE UNIV CHONGQING NORMAL (UNCQ-C)
GA 202152590B
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises firstly in-situ oxidizing and polymerizing a layer of poly-dopamine on the surface of the platinum/carbon catalyst, absorbing transition metal ion carbon dioxide, using inorganic salt solution to re-crystallize, solidifying, encapsulating to obtain a salt-encapsulated and solidified sample, driving the salt-encapsulated and solidified sample at 600-1000 degrees C, reducing the carbon dioxide ions into carbon atoms by the reduction of the nitrogen-doped carbon formed by the polydopamine carbonization, inducing the Kirkendall migration, finishing the reaction, cooling the product and stirring in the sulfuric acid aqueuous solution to obtain the nitrogen-doped carbon-coated hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electro-catalyst.
   USE - The hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electrocatalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon is useful in fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electro-catalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon has complex preparation and good product stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electrocatalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) preparing In-situ synthesis of platinum/carbon catalyst modified with polydopamine, namely platinum/carbon @ polydopamine including adding platinum/carbon catalyst to the mixture of absolute ethanol and distilled water, stir ultrasonically to obtain solution A, adding dopamine to the tris- hydrochloride buffer, stirring ultrasonically to obtain solution B, slowly dropping solution B into solution A under stirring conditions, continuing the reaction under stirring conditions, drying the product, grinding to obtain platinum/carbon @ polydopamine, (b) performing metal cobalt salt adsorption and inorganic salt solution recrystallization solidification package including mixing the platinum/carbon @ polydopamine prepared in step (a) with the cobalt precursor, stirring for 5-20 hours to obtain a thick liquid, adding the inorganic salt powder, continuously stirring for 2-6 hours, adding the supersaturated inorganic salt solution, rotary evaporating the dry water to obtain a solid powder, packing the solid powder into a high-temperature container , compacting, covering with inorganic salt powder, slowly adding supersaturated inorganic salt solution to make the powder surface wet, drying, repeating the steps of wetting and drying the above-mentioned covering inorganic salt powder dropwise with supersaturated inorganic salt solution, repeating 2-8 times and placing it in a vacuum drying oven to dry to obtain a salt-sealed solidified sample and (c) preparing hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electrocatalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon including placing the salt-sealed solidified sample prepared in step (b) in a tube furnace, heating to 600-1000 degrees C under protective gas for 1-4 hours, cooling the product, stirring, reacting the product in an aqueous sulfuric acid solution, centrifuging, washing, drying and grinding to obtain a hollow platinum-cobalt alloy nano-particle electrocatalyst coated with nitrogen-doped carbon. In the step (a) the ultrasonic stirring time is 1-30 minutes. The reaction temperature of solution B and solution A is controlled at 0-30 degrees C. The step (a) comprises slowly dropping the solution B into the solution A within 5-60 minutes and reacting for 10-48 hours. In the step (a) the preparation of the hydrochloride buffer comprises dissolving 0.6 g tris hydroxymethyl amino methane in 100 ml water. In the step (b) the rotary evaporation temperature and the drying temperature are both 40-80 degrees C after adding the supersaturated inorganic salt solution. In the step (b) the inorganic salt powder is ground and then used. The high temperature resistant container is a crucible. The vacuum degree of the vacuum drying oven is 0.1 MPa. The drying process is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The step (b) comprises mixing, stirring the platinum/carbon @ polydopamine and the cobal precursor to obtain a thick liquid, where the mass ratio of the platinum/carbon @ polydopamine to cobalt is 1:0.5-10. The step (b) comprises adding 2-20 ml supersaturated inorganic salt solution and rotary evaporating dry water at 40-80 degrees C to obtain a solid powder, packing the solid powder into a high temperature resistant container, compacting, spreading 0.1-1g ground inorganic salt powder on the top, slowly adding 1-5 drops of supersaturated inorganic salt and wetting the surface of the powder by salt solution. The method comprises reacting the product in the sulfuric acid aqueous solution at 60-90 degrees C and stirring the product in the sulfuric acid aqueous solution for 4-10 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (a) the ratio of platinum/carbon catalyst, absolute ethanol and distilled water is: 1g:20-100ml:20-100ml. The mass ratio of platinum/carbon catalyst, dopamine and tris- hydrochloride buffer is 1:0.1-2:0.1-2. In the step (a) the amount of absolute ethanol and distilled water are equal. In the step (a) the dosages of dopamine and tris- hydrochloride buffer are equal. In the step (b) the amount of the inorganic salt powder in the thick liquid is 1-4 g. : In the step (c) the protective gas is nitrogen and/or argon. The metal ion carbon dioxide is derived from a compound containing carbon dioxide ion, and the compound containing carbon dioxide ion comprises cobalt nitrate, cobalt sulfate and/or cobalt chloride. The cobalt precursor comprises cobalt nitrate, cobalt sulfate and/or cobalt chloride and/or the inorganic salt is sodium chloride and/or potassium chloride. The concentration of the sulfuric acid aqueous solution is 0.01-2mol/l.

PN KR2245897-B1
TI Nanofiber membrane used for lithium-ion battery, comprises nanofibers formed in structure and electrospinning liquid containing single molecule, which is starting material of porous polymer formed by growing single molecule on nanofibers.
AU DOO K
   LEE J Y
   SUNG L H
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202144420V
AB    NOVELTY - A nanofiber membrane comprises an electrospinning liquid containing a single molecule, which is a starting material of a porous polymer that selectively permeates oxygen in the atmosphere and suppresses the permeation of moisture and carbon dioxide gas, is electrospinned to form a single axis or grid, and nanofibers formed in a structure. The porous polymer formed by growing the single molecule on the surfaces of the nanofibers.
   USE - Nanofiber membrane used for lithium-ion battery for electric vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly nanofiber membrane has excellent porosity and chemical blocking ability to suppress the inflow of gases such as moisture, contains oxygen-friendly porous polymers with high density to ensures maximum oxygen permeation in the atmosphere and inhibits the penetration of impurities such as moisture, prevents penetration of airborne impurities, enables the lithium-air battery to be driven even under an actual atmosphere, reduces environmental pollution, and prevents global warming by reducing the generation of environmental pollution and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of nanofiber membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The single molecule comprises 1,3,5-triphenylbenzene, 1,2,3-triphenylbenzene, 1,3,5-tris(4-bromophenyl)benzene, 1,3,5-tris(4-iodophenyl)benzene, and 1,3,5-tris(2-methylphenyl)benzene. The solvent contained in the electrospinning solution comprises formic acid, acetic acid, phosphoric acid, sulfuric acid, m-cresol, trifluoroacetic anhydride/dichloromethane, water, N-methylmorpholine N-oxide, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, methyl isobutyl ketone, methyl ethyl ketone, aliphatic hydroxyl group m-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol, isopropyl alcohol, methyl alcohol, ethanol, hexane as an aliphatic compound, tetrachloroethylene, propylene glycol as acetone, glycol group, diethylene glycol, ethylene glycol, trichloroethylene as a halogen compound group, dichloromethane, toluene, aromatic compounds, xylene, cyclohexanone as an alicyclic compound group, cyclohexane and ester group as n-butyl acetate, ethyl acetate, aliphatic ether group as butyl cellosolve, acetic acid 2-ethoxyethanol, 2-ethoxyethanol, amide as dimethylformamide, and dimethylacetamide. The solvent used for washing comprises n-butanol, isopropanol, n-propanol, ethanol, methanol, and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous polymer contains intrinsic pores with a single molecule as a unit structure. The electrospinning solution comprises single molecule and polymer. The polymer comprises poly-( epsilon )-(caprolactone), chitosan, polyamide, poly-1-lactic acid, polylactic acid-glycolic acid copolymer, polyanhydride, polyacrylic acid, poly-n-isopropyl acrylamide, polyvinylidene fluoride, poly(vinylidene fluoride-co-hexa fluoropropylene), perfluoropolymer, PVC, polyvinylidene chloride, polyethylene glycol dialkylether, polyethylene glycol dialkylester, poly(oxymethylene-oligo-oxyethylene), polypropylene oxide, polyvinyl acetate, poly(vinylpyrrolidone-vinylacetate), polystyrene, polyacrylonitrile, polymethylmethacrylate, polyamide, polyimide, poly(meta-phenylene isophthalamide), polysulfone, polyetherketone, polyetherimide, polyethylene terephthalate, polyethylene naphthalate, polyester, poly(1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethylene), polyphosphazene, polyurethane, cellulose acetate, and their copolymers. The porous polymer synthesis solution comprises 1,3,5-triphenylbenzene, 1,2,3-triphenylbenzene, 1,3,5-tris(4-bromophenyl)benzene, 1,3,5-tris(4-iodophenyl)benzene, 1,3,5-tris(2-methylphenyl)benzene, Lewis acid as a catalyst for accelerating the Diels alder reaction, and complex synthetic solution containing dichloromethane and chloroform as a solvent. Preferred Properties: The pores have size of 0.5-10 nm, and specific surface area of 1000-2500 cm2/g. The nanofibers have diameter of 50 nm-5 mu m, preferably 10 nm-25 mu m, and 40-90% porosity. The nanofiber membrane has thickness of 5-100 mu m, and area of 1-900 cm2. Preferred Composition: The electrospinning solution comprises 1-5 wt.% single molecule and 5-20 wt.% polymer.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); E13 (Heterocyclics.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN CN112453048-A
TI Performing electric in-situ repairing of chromium-contaminated soil involves adjusting moisture content of chromium-contaminated soil, passing carbon dioxide gas into chromium-contaminated soil, and providing anaerobic environment.
AU WU Q
   LI X
   FAN D
AE TIANJIN CEMENT IND DESIGN & RES INST (CHNB-C)
   TIANJIN ZHONGCAI ENG RES CENT CO LTD (CHNB-C)
GA 202125161Q
AB    NOVELTY - Performing an electric in-situ repairing of chromium-contaminated soil involves adjusting moisture content of chromium-contaminated soil to more than 10% so that pore water between electrodes is electrically connected, passing carbon dioxide gas into chromium-contaminated soil, providing anaerobic environment, and reducing biodegradation of sodium gluconate. The sodium gluconate solution is added to electromotive repairing cathode well of contaminated soil, and direct current is applied to two electrodes placed in contaminated soil area to electrically repair contaminated soil in situ. The gluconate ions migrate to anode under the action of an electric field, and forms ligand with trivalent chromium-containing cation during the migration process, trivalent chromium is separated from insoluble sediments or surface of soil particles, and chromium is removed from chromium contaminated soil. The hydrogen peroxide is added to repaired soil to remove excess sodium gluconate.
   USE - Method for performing an electric in-situ repairing of chromium-contaminated soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables repairing of chromium contaminated soil with fast repairing speed, high chromium pollution removing rate, and has no secondary pollution.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

AU REN Z
   XU W
AE HENAN AIMU INTELLIGENT EQUIP CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9145C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using first detector and a second detector. The corresponding target gas parameters in the environment is detected in real time by first detector and sent to the controller. The second detector is acted as a calibration sensor. The parameters of the corresponding target gas in the current environment are collected, and the collected parameters are sent to controller. The controller is provided to compare the current measurement information sent by first detector with measurement information sent by second detector. The judgment is performed whether to calibrate the measured value of first detector. The accuracy of the parameters of corresponding target gas collected by first detector is ensured. The first detector is turned off and second detector is turned on when calibration time interval is less than minimum time interval allowed by preset calibration. The second detector is used as a normal working sensor, so that the controller is provided to alarm.
   USE - Method for automatically calibrating carbon dioxide detection accuracy of livestock and poultry house.
   ADVANTAGE - The calibration accuracy is accurate, so that errors in the control of air quality are avoided.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing radix angelicae pubescentis traditional Chinese medicine prescription granule involves taking radix angelicae pubescentis medicinal material powder, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide, and preparing radix angelicae pubescentis extract and medicine slag. The excipient is used to absorb the radix angelicae pubescentis extract, and a radix angelicae pubescentis extract powder is prepared. The water is added into the medicine slag, and extracted. The obtained extracting liquid is concentrated, the auxiliary material is added, and the radix angelicae pubescentis dry extract powder is prepared. The radix angelicae pubescentis extract powder is prepared and the radix angelicae pubescent is dry extract powder. The radix angelicae pubescentis traditional Chinese medicine prescription granule is prepared. The excipient is selected from one or more of silicon dioxide, magnesium aluminum silicate and kaolin, and the grain diameter of the excipient is 1-200 mu m.
   USE - Method for preparing a radix angelicae pubescentis traditional Chinese medicine prescription granule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a radix angelicae pubescentis traditional Chinese medicine prescription granule realizes the extraction and retention of all the ingredients of the Chinese herbal medicine and increases the content of effective ingredients.

The auxiliary material is selected from one or more of dextrin, silicon dioxide, magnesium aluminum silicate, kaolin, magnesium stearate and talc. The dextrin is selected from one or two of maltodextrin and beta -cyclodextrin. Preferred Compositions: The excipient is a mixture of silicon dioxide, magnesium aluminum silicate and kaolin with a mass ratio of 1:(0.5-2):(0.5-2). The excipient is a mixture of silicon dioxide, magnesium aluminum silicate and kaolin with a mass ratio of 1:(0.8-1.2): (0.8-1.2). The weight ratio of the excipient to the radix angelicae pubescentis extract is (0.5-1.0): 1. The weight ratio of the medicine dregs to water is 1:(8-20). Preferred Conditions: The process parameters of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction includes the extraction pressure is 15-35 megapascal (MPa), the extraction temperature is 35-75 degrees C, and the extraction time is 0.5-6 hours. The process parameters of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction further includes the separation pressure is 5-10 MPa, the separation temperature is 30-70 degrees C, and the separation time is 0.5-6 hours. The extraction time is 0.5-2.0 hours. The particle size of the auxiliary material is 1-400 mu m.

PN CN111424055-A
TI Anaerobically converting of sodium alginate production wastewater into methane and organic acids by suspending anaerobic residue with medium, centrifuging, inoculating into anaerobic reactor, adding culture medium and cultivating.
AU ZHANG F
   ZENG J
   DAI K
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 202069403F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for anaerobically converting of sodium alginate production wastewater into methane and organic acids, by (i) taking anaerobic residue, suspending and shaking with the medium to evenly mix, centrifuging to remove the supernatant, repeating the above operation several times, (ii) inoculating the anaerobic residue obtained in step (i) into an anaerobic reactor, adding culture medium, sodium alginate and mannitol, passing in excess nitrogen and carbon dioxide gas mixture, sealing, continuously cultivating for 60-80 days at 20-25 degrees C to enrich the sodium alginate hydrolyzed flora, and (iii) inoculating 50-250 ml/l sodium alginate hydrolyzed bacteria colony enriched in step (ii) in an anaerobic reactor, using sodium alginate production wastewater as a substrate and passing in excess nitrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas, sealing and incubating the anaerobic reactor at 20-25 degrees C for 5-15 days to obtain metabolites of methane and organic acids.
   USE - Method for anaerobic conversion of sodium alginate production wastewater into methane and organic acids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily converts sodium alginate production wastewater into methane and organic acids, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves deriving the anaerobic residue from anaerobic reactor for processing excess residue. The method involves adjusting the pH of the medium to 7-7.8 using acid or alkali. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the rotation speed of the centrifugation is 8500-10000 rpm, and the centrifugation time is 3-7 minutes. Preferred Components: The culture medium comprises 350-750 mg/l ammonium chloride, 180-220 mg/l monopotassium phosphate, 10-15 mg/l sodium sulfate, 20-30 mg/l calcium chloride, 50-80 mg/l magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.8-1.5 mg/l manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, 1.2-1.8 mg/l cobalt(II) chlorate dihydrate, 3-3.5 mg/l ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.5-1.0 mg/l aluminum chloride, 0.1-0.5 mg/l sodium molybdate dihydrate, 0.2-0.5 mg/l boric acid, 0.5-1.5 mg/l nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate, 1-2 mg/l copper(II) chloride and 3-5 mg/l zinc sulfate monohydrate. The organic acid comprises acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid and/or caproic acid. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the sodium alginate and mannitol in the reaction system is 1-5 g/l. The volume ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 70-85%:15-25%.

GA 202055102K

PN KR2020064196-A; KR2175925-B1
TI Indoor air quality (IAQ) monitoring system in indoor spaces with Internet of things (IOT)-based indoor gardens, has control unit which calculates comfort index from temperature sensing value and humidity sensing value of sensor unit.
AU JIN H
   JEONG K M
   AHNSEONMIN
   JUNG-HOON L
AE UNIV PYEONGTAEK IND COOP AGENCY (UYPY-Non-standard)
GA 2020503065
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sensor unit (100) which is configured for detecting temperature, humidity, fine dust, and CO2 in the indoor space where the indoor garden is installed. A control unit (200) calculates a comfort index from the temperature sensing value and the humidity sensing value of the sensor unit, calculates an IAQ index from the fine dust detection value and the carbon dioxide (CO2) sensing value from the sensor unit, and then calculates an IAQ green index from the comfort index and the IAQ index. An output unit (300) outputs air quality information of the indoor space according to the IAQ green index calculated by the control unit.
   USE - IAQ monitoring system in indoor spaces with IOT-based indoor gardens.
   ADVANTAGE - The system maximizes the effect of integrated management and improvement of fine dust, carbon dioxide, which is an indicator of indoor ventilation, and temperature and humidity, which are standards for indoor comfort.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an IAQ monitoring system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Sensor unit (100)

TI Growing biopolymer material by maintaining closed incubation chamber with predetermined environment of e.g. humidity, temperature, and oxygen, and incubating growth media to digest nutritive substrate and produce mycelium biopolymer.
AU KAPLAN-BIE J H
   GREETHAM L E
   BONESTEEL I T
   MCINTYRE G R
AE ECOVATIVE DESIGN LLC (ECOV-Non-standard)
GA 202039705C
AB    NOVELTY - Growing a biopolymer material involves providing containers, each the container defining a cavity containing a growth media comprised of nutritive substrate and a fungus; placing the containers in a closed incubation chamber; maintaining the closed incubation chamber with a predetermined environment of humidity, temperature, carbon dioxide and oxygen sufficient to produce a mycelium biopolymer while preventing full differentiation of the fungus into a mushroom; directing flows of air containing a high carbon dioxide content through the incubation chamber for passage over the growth media in each the container; and incubating the growth media in each the container for a period of time sufficient for the fungus to digest the nutritive substrate and produce a mycelium biopolymer consisting entirely of fungal mycelium in each the container.
   USE - The method is useful for growing a biopolymer material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods can create a biomaterial of increased homogeneity, strength and density. The use of airflow allows for growth from the full surface of the growth container and helps to improve the homogeneity and uniformity of the tissue grown.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: Alternatively, growing a biopolymer material involves providing containers, each the container defining a cavity containing a growth media comprised of nutritive substrate and a fungus; placing the containers in a closed incubation chamber; maintaining the closed incubation chamber with a predetermined environment of humidity, temperature, carbon dioxide and oxygen; distributing a mist through the incubation chamber for passage over the growth media in each the container; and incubating the growth media in each the container for a period of time sufficient for the fungus to digest the nutritive substrate and produce a mycelium biopolymer consisting entirely of fungal mycelium without substantial morphological variation in each the container. The flows of air are directed into the closed incubation chamber laterally of the containers. The flows of air are directed into the closed incubation chamber perpendicularly of the containers, where the containers are stacked within the incubation chamber in vertically spaced apart rows. The flows of air are directed into the closed incubation chamber laterally of the containers; the flows of air are directed into the closed incubation chamber perpendicularly of the containers; the flows of air are pulsed during the step of incubating; or the flows of air contain a carbon dioxide content of 5-7 vol.%. Aerial hypha grow out of each the container during the step of incubating and the mist is distributed at regulated amounts and/or distribution of solute onto a top surface of the aerial hypha to achieve a predetermined material density and material homogeneity. Preferred Condition: The environment is maintained at 99% relative humidity (RH), 5% carbon dioxide (CO2), and a fluctuating temperature of 85-90 degrees F during the step of incubating. Preferred Components: The mist includes moisture and a solute. The solute is a mineral.

PT P
PN KR2020034641-A
TI Sensor for detecting e.g., hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, aromatic compounds, volatile organic compound, biomolecules, bacteria, or acetone, comprises an array of one or more nanostructures containing a sensing material.
AU JUNG H T
   JUNG W B
   JO S Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202028508H
AB    NOVELTY - A sensor comprises an array of greater than or equal to 1 nanostructures containing a sensing material.
   USE - A sensor for detecting hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), water vapor, oxygen (O2), nitrogen (N2), aromatic compounds, and volatile organic compound (VOC), and for detecting blood, biomolecules, bacteria, acetone, or alcohols (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanostructure is manufactured by a simple process and low cost by applying an ion impact phenomenon through physical ion etching, and has various line widths and shapes, and at the same time, ultra-fine nano particles having a line width of up to 10 nm. The resulting sensor has excellent sensitivity and fast response/recovery speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of sensor which involves (a) depositing sensing material on the substrate on which a 1st pre-pattern is formed, (b) re-depositing the sensing material on the side of the 1st pre-pattern through an ion etching process to form 1st nano-pattern, and (c) removing the 1st pre-pattern through an ion etching process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Shape of the nanostructure is selected from line pattern, lattice shape, curved shape, cylindrical shape, square column shape, inverted cone shape, rectangular parallelepiped shape, top shape, cup shape, and/or U shape, preferably lamellar shape or nanoporous cylinder shape. Nanostructures are arranged to be inclined at 30-90 degrees with respect to the substrate, or folded at an upper portion with respect to the substrate at 30-90 degrees . Particle size of sensing material is less than or equal to 100 nm. Sensing material has particle interface gap of less than or equal to 50 nm, aspect ratio of nanostructure is greater than or equal to 1, and line width is less than or equal to 50 nm. When nanostructure is sensing hydrogen gas or hydrogen sulfide gas, Delta R/Ra is greater than or equal to 1%, response time is less than or equal to 100 s, and recovery time is less than or equal to 200 s. Preferred Components: Sensing material comprises metal, metal oxide, metal sulfide, or polymer. Preferred Processes: The method includes (d) forming 2nd pre-pattern by varying angle with 1st nano-pattern on the substrate on which 1st nano-pattern is formed and depositing sensing material on the substrate; (e) re-depositing the sensing material on the side of the 2nd pre-pattern through an ion etching process to form 2nd nano-pattern; and (f) removing the 2nd pre-pattern through ion etching. The method includes asymmetrically etching one side of the pre-pattern formed on the substrate at an angle of 30-50 degrees for 1-30 minutes to adjust the inclination angle of the pre-pattern, removing the portion where the pre-pattern is not formed, exposing the substrate, depositing a sensing material on the pre-pattern with the inclination angle adjusted, and removing the prepattern, obtaining a nanostructure inclined by 30-90 degrees with respect to the substrate. The method involves asymmetrically etching one side of the pre-pattern formed on the substrate at an angle of 50-80 degrees for 1-30 minutes to adjust the inclination angle of the pre-pattern, removing portion where the pre-pattern is not formed, exposing the substrate, depositing a sensing material on the pre-pattern with the inclination angle adjusted, and removing the pre-pattern to obtain a nanostructure in which the upper portion of the substrate is folded at 30-90 degrees . In the deposition of the sensing material, the order of deposition, the thickness of deposition, or the number of depositions is controlled, or the angle of the ion etching process after deposition or the time of the ion etching process is controlled, allowing to change the component, content ratio, or form of the redeposited sensing material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Sensing material is gold (Au), silver (Ag), copper (Cu), aluminum (Al), nickel (Ni), platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), tin (Sn), molybdenum (Mo), titanium (Ti), chromium (Cr), manganese (Mn), iron (Fe), cobalt (Co), zinc (Zn), indium (In), tungsten (W), iridium (Ir), and/or silicon (Si). Sensing material is Au-Cu, Au-Pt, Au-Ni, Au-Ag, Au-Pd, Pd-Ag, Ni-Sn, Mo-Ni, Au-Al, Au-Sn, Au-Mo, Au-Ti, Au-Cr, Au-Mn, Au-Fe, Au-Co, Au-Zn, Au-In, Au-W, Au-Ir, Au-Si, Ag-Cu, Ag- Al, Ag-Ni, Ag-Pt, Ag-Pd, Ag-Sn, Ag-Mo, Ag-Ti, Ag-Cr, Ag-Mn, Ag-Fe, Ag-Zn, Ag-In, Ag-W, Bi-metal materials, Ag-Ir, Ag-Si, Au-Ag-Cu, Au-Cu-Pt, Au-Ag-Pt, Au-Ag-Pd, Au-Cu-Pd, Ag-Cu-Pt, and/or Ag-Cu-Pd. Sensing material is Pd(1-a)/Aua, Pd(1-a)/Pta, or Pd(1-bc)/Aub/Nic. Sensing material is SnO2(1-d)/WO3d, SnO2(1-d)/CuOd, SnO2(1-ef)/WO3e/Auf, and/or SnO2(1-ef)/WO3e/Ptf. Sensing material senses a gas or a liquid.a, b, c, d, e, f=0-0.5; andb+c, e+f=0-1.Preferred Processes: Sensing material is Pd/Au/Ni, deposited on the substrate in the order of Au-Ni-Pd, by adjusting the deposition thickness to 5-10 nm, including a layered structure of the ternary material to form the 1st nano-pattern. Sensing material is Pd/Au/Ni, deposited on the substrate in the order of Au-Ni-Pd, by adjusting the deposition thickness to less than or equal to 5 nm, including a uniformly mixed shape of the ternary material to form 1st nano-pattern. Sensing material is Pd/Au/Ni, and Au-Ni-Pd-Ni-Au is deposited on the substrate in order, by adjusting the deposition thickness to 5-10 nm, including a core-shell shape to form 1st nano-pattern containing the ternary material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Processes: The 1st pre-pattern is formed by removing a portion of polymer of the block copolymer through an ion etching process after forming a block copolymer pattern on the substrate. Preferred Compositions: Block copolymer is polystyrene-b-poly(methyl methacrylate) (PS-b-PMMA), where amount of PS-b-PMMA is 270-280 kg/mol, and 1st pre-pattern is a lamellar shape; or amount of PS-b-PMMA is 65-140 kg/mol, and 1st pre-pattern is a nanoporous cylinder shape.

PT P
PN CN110551571-A
TI Extracting clove volatile oil, by crushing clove, soaking in water, adding soaked material into extractor, sealing, carrying out instantaneous pressure relief, extracting using extraction kettle and purifying volatile oil by diethyl ether.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 2019A5785N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting clove volatile oil, involves (a) crushing clove by a crusher for 24-80 mesh, soaking in water for 24 hours in a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:(7-9), (b) adding the soaked material into an ultrahigh-pressure extractor, sealing at normal temperature, increasing the pressure at 200-400 degrees C and carrying out instantaneous pressure relief, (c) placing the treated material into an extraction kettle of a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor and extracting at a temperature of 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours, at a pressure of 20-30 kg/hour, and the flow rate of the extraction agent carbon dioxide of 15-25 kg/hour to obtain the clove volatile oil with fragrance from the extraction kettle, and (d) purifying the volatile oil by diethyl ether and removing the residual water to obtain pure clove volatile oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting clove volatile oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to operate and environmentally-friendly, provides sufficient color and fragrance retention, and has low agricultural residues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction kettle is carried out at a temperature of 25-30 degrees C at and the pressure of 10-20 MPa. The step of purification is carried out by extracting ethyl ether and water in a volume ratio of 3:1 for 3 times and concentrating and recovering ethyl ether under reduced pressure.

TI Composition used as heat transfer fluid as            substituent of near-azeotropic mixture of            difluoromethane and pentafluoroethane in heat transfer            composition for heat transfer installation, comprises            1,1,2-trifluoroethylene and carbon dioxide.
AU RACHED W
AE ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
   ARKEMA FRANCE (AQOR-C)
GA 2019A20916
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises                1,1,2-trifluoroethylene and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Composition is used as heat transfer fluid as                substituent of R-410A-type refrigerant in heat                transfer composition for heat transfer installation                e.g. refrigeration, freezing and Rankine cycles,                preferably automobile air conditioning, and                centralized stationary air conditioner (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is environmentally-friendly                and nontoxic, and has excellent thermodynamic                properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) heat transfer composition, which comprises                the composition as a heat transfer fluid, and one                or more additives;
   (2) heat transfer installation, which                comprises a vapor compression circuit containing                the composition as the heat transfer agent, or the                heat transfer composition;
   (3) method for heating or cooling a fluid or a                component using vapor compression circuit, which                involves subjecting the heat transfer fluid to                compression, condensation, and expansion; and
   (4) method for reducing environmental impact                of heat transfer installation, which involves                replacing the initial heat transfer fluid with a                final heat transfer fluid. The final heat transfer                fluid has a global warming potential lower than                that of initial heat transfer fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises one or more additional                  compounds chosen from ammonia and optionally                  halogenated alkanes and alkenes, preferably                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, pentafluoroethane,                  difluoromethane, 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, ammonia,                  1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane, propane,                  propylene, 1,1,1-trifluoroethane,                  1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene,                  1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluorobut-2-ene,                  1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane,                  1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethane, and/or                  1,1-difluoroethane. The composition comprises                  5-80 wt.%, preferably 15-60 wt.%                  1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, and 15 wt.% or more,                  preferably 35 wt.% or more carbon dioxide, and                  1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane and/or                  pentafluoroethane in total. The composition                  preferably comprises a mixture of 40-70 wt.%                  1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, 5-30 wt.% carbon                  dioxide, and 5-30 wt.% pentafluoroethane, or                  55-70 wt.% 1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, 5-30 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, and 5-35 wt.% pentafluoroethane,                  or 5-70 wt.% 1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, 5-35 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, and 5-60 wt.% pentafluoroethane,                  or 5-55 wt.% 1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, 5-35 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, and 5-25 wt.% pentafluoroethane,                  or 5-65 wt.% 1,1,2-trifluoroethylene, 5-30 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, 5-30 wt.% pentafluoroethane, 5-10                  wt.% 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, and 5-60 wt.%                  difluoromethane. Preferred Properties: The                  composition is nonflammable. The composition has                  a global warming potential of 1000 or less,                  preferably 150 or less. Preferred Component: The                  additive is lubricant, nanoparticles, stabilizer,                  surfactant, tracer agent, fluorescent agent,                  odorant, and/or solubilizing agent. The initial                  heat transfer fluid is near-azeotropic mixture of                  difluoromethane and pentafluoroethane                  (R-410A-type refrigerant).
