PN CN110304644-A; CN110304644-B
TI Preparation of high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite for e.g. reforming catalysts, involves diluting sodium aluminate solution, reacting sodium aluminate solution with carbon dioxide, and filtering pseudo-boehmite slurry.
AU ZHAO Y
   FAN H
   ZHOU F
   YAO Y
AE CHALCO SHANDONG CO LTD (CNAL-C)
   CHALCO SHANDONG CO LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 2019961654
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite involves (1) performing pre-decomposition of sodium aluminate solution, controlling aluminum oxide loss to 5-10%, and filtering to obtain high purity sodium aluminate solution, (2) diluting high-purity sodium aluminate solution and reacting with carbon dioxide to obtain high purity pseudo-boehmite slurry, filtering, and washing high purity pseudo-boehmite slurry to obtain high purity boehmite wet filter cake, (3) and adding high purity pseudo-boehmite wet filter cake to water, stirring the slurry, adding high carbon alcohol additive, reacting at a temperature of 100-200 degrees C, and heat preserving at a pressure of 0.5-1 MPa for 2-24 hours, lowering temperature, discharging, filtering, washing, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite used for preparing petroleum refining catalysts, reforming catalysts and automobile exhaust gas purification catalysts, production of tail gas, catalysis and filling technical gap in existing catalysts (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite having excellent crystal form, easy control of large specific surface area, low production cost, and excellent product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The sodium aluminate solution in step (1) is prepared by reacting aluminum hydroxide and alkali solution at a temperature of 105-120 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The high purity sodium aluminate solution is reacted with 30-50% carbon dioxide after dilution at an initial reaction temperature of 20-30 degrees C and final reaction temperature of 25-50 degrees C for 30-80 minutes. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is controlled at 1.0-5.0 m3/hour. The aluminum oxide residue in product reacted with carbon dioxide after diluting high purity sodium aluminate solution is controlled to be 3-9 g/L. The step (2) involves washing high purity pseudo-boehmite slurry with high purity water at 80-100 degrees C until wet filter cake has pH of 7.0-7.5. Preferred Composition: The diluted high purity sodium aluminate solution has aluminum oxide content of 40-60 g/L. Preferred Property: The high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite prepared is a network or lock-like multinuclear complex. Preferred Component: The high purity and high viscosity pseudo-boehmite is of formula (I)
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The high carbon alcohol additive is added in an amount of 0.5-2%. Preferred Component: The high carbon alcohol additive is urea.

   USE - Nitrogen-doped porous organic skeleton solid alkali material used for absorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-doped porous organic skeleton solid alkali material has low preparation cost, simple process, and adsorption amount of 57.0 247.7 mg and realizes rapid desorption and circulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing nitrogen-doped porous organic skeleton solid alkali material, which involves:
   (A) dispersing polymerization initiator in a solvent;
   (B) adding crosslinking monomer and alkaline monomer after microwave irradiation treatment at microwave irradiation power;
   (C) cooling the catalyst at 0 degrees C; and
   (D) adding crosslinking catalyst to complete reflux crosslinking treatment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic nitrogen adsorption isotherm of the prepared solid alkali material. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The p-chloromethylstyrene is selected as a crosslinking monomer, p-aminostyrene is a basic monomer, and dichloromethane/ dichloroethane is solvent, ferric chloride is polymerization initiator, and titanium tetrachloride is crosslinking catalyst. The nanoporous organic skeleton solid alkali material is obtained by microwave polymerization, reflux crosslinking and spray drying. Preferred Conditions: The crosslinking monomer and the basic monomer are provided in a molar ratio of 0.5-5.0:1. The monomer and the solvent are provided in a volume ratio of 0.02-0.1:1. The polymerization initiator and the crosslinking monomer are provided in a mass ratio of 0.008-0.06:1. The crosslinking catalyst and the crosslinking monomer are provided in a volume ratio of 1-10:1.

PT P
PN RU2688533-C1
TI LTDR PLANT FOR INTEGRATED GAS PREPARATION AND PRODUCTION OF LNG AND ITS OPERATION METHOD.
AU KUROCHKIN A V
AE KUROCHKIN A V (KURO-Individual)
GA 2019461575
AB    NOVELTY - Group of inventions relates to low-temperature condensation plants and can be used in gas industry. Plant for low-temperature dephlegmation with rectification LTDR includes an inlet separator, a low-temperature condensation unit in a dephlegmator and a demethanizer, recuperative heat exchangers and reducing devices, debutanisation units, carbon dioxide purification units and a refrigerating machine. During the operation of the plant crude gas is cooled in a recuperative heat exchanger and separated in an inlet separator into a condensate and a gas which, after cooling, is fed into a dephlegmator. Circulating phlegm is removed from the middle part of the demethanizer, which is returned to the demethanizer after being heated by the moist gas. Pre-treated gas is removed from the top of demethanizer after heating in recuperative heat exchanger, and demethanised condensate is removed from bottom, which is directed to debutanisation unit for separation into products. LNG is withdrawn from demethanizer middle part to be removed after purification from carbon dioxide and cooling in refrigerating machine.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.

PN CN109734105-A; CN109734105-B
TI Method for self-enriching and utilization of titanium gypsum cyclically converted metal used for producing e.g. titanium,, involves mixing titanium gypsum with carbon dioxide, crystallizing obtained ammonium sulfate solution, and calcining.
AU FENG Y
   WU H
   LI H
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2019451899
AB    NOVELTY - Method for self-enriching and monolithic utilization of titanium gypsum cyclically converted metal involves (i) mixing titanium gypsum with carbon dioxide and aqueous ammonia and subjecting to a heating reaction, (ii) subjecting the obtained mixture to solid-liquid separation to obtain a solid phase (s1) and an ammonium sulfate solution, (iii) evaporating and crystallizing ammonium sulfate solution to obtain ammonium sulfate, (iv) calcining solid phase (s1) at a high temperature to obtain a solid phase (s2), and (v) returning the solid phase (s1) obtained in the step (2) and the solid phase (s2) obtained by calcining in the step (4) to the titanium white industrial chain to neutralize the acidic wastewater and obtaining metal self-enriched titanium gypsum and returning to step (i).
   USE - Method for self-enriching and monolithic utilization of titanium gypsum cyclically converted metal used for producing titanium, iron and calcium used as ingredient in iron ore sinter, or additive in iron or titanium-iron alloy smelting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of self-enriching metals such as iron, titanium and aluminum in the wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (i), the titanium gypsum is produced by using sulfuric acid method to produce titanium white powder. The waste residue in acidic wastewater generated in step (i) is neutralized by adding lime to acidic wastewater. The step (i) involves mixing titanium gypsum and aqueous ammonia, and adding the obtained mixture to the closed reactor, and passing carbon dioxide having a partial pressure of 0.1-1.8 MPa through the reaction of titanium gypsum and ammonia. The liquid-solid ratio in the step (i) is 3:1-10:1. The step (i) id carried out at a reaction temperature of 20-80 degrees C for 1-5 hours, and at stirring rotation speed of 300-800 rpm. The calcination step is carried out at a temperature of 800-1200 degrees C for 0.2-2 hours. The step (v) involves neutralizing wastewater by using the solid phase (s1) to neutralize the pH of the wastewater to 3-5, and using the solid phase (s2) to neutralize the pH of the wastewater to 6-8. After the end of the cycle, when the solid phase (s1) is used as the iron ore sinter ore, the whole iron grade is more than 20% and the titanium dioxide content is less than 10%. Preferred Composition: The titanium gypsum comprises one or more of iron, titanium, aluminum, magnesium and silicon elements. The ratio of content of calcium sulfate in the titanium gypsum with respect to substance to be added in the step (i) is 1:2-1:6. In the step (iv), the solid phase (s1) is high active calcium carbonate, and the solid phase (s2) is calcium oxide. Preferred Properties: The purity of ammonium sulfate in the step (iii) is more than 99%.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN IN201741032485-A
TI Making precipitated silica used in e.g. paints involves reacting rice husk ash with sodium hydroxide and then reacting with carbon dioxide to obtain silica and sodium carbonate solution, and evaporating solution to recover sodium carbonate.
AU JOGHEE R
AE MILLTEC MACHINERY LTD (MILL-Non-standard)
GA 201929091H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing precipitated silica from rice husk comprises (a) reacting rice husk ash with sodium hydroxide to obtain sodium silicate solution, (b) reacting the sodium silicate solution with carbon dioxide to obtain precipitated silica and sodium carbonate solution, and (c) evaporating the sodium carbonate solution to recover sodium carbonate by removing water.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing precipitated silica from rice husk (claimed), where the silica is useful in rubbers and plastics, paints, healthcare, cosmetics, toothpastes, catalyst, coatings, carrier agent, insulation, stabilizing and desiccants.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides silica which is substantially free from any metallic impurities and is obtained in high purity and yield; efficiently recovers sodium carbonate which has varied industrial uses e.g. raw materials in filtration industry and detergent manufacturing respectively; eliminates the expensive effluent treatment plant and also cuts down the plant operation cost; does not involve introduction of another raw material e.g. lime to regenerate sodium hydroxide; and is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for silica substantially free from any metallic impurities as obtainable by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is bubbled into the sodium silicate solution at 0.5-1 kg/cm2 and 85-90 degrees C. The silica obtained in the step (b) is subjected to further steps of purification and drying. The evaporation step (c) involves four stages, where the temperature varies from 60-100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of silica in rice husk to sodium hydroxide is 1:0.5-1:0.75.
   EXAMPLE - Good quality rice husk ash (RHA) that was well burnt (700-750 degrees ) boiler outlet RHA was reacted with 1.5 N sodium hydroxide solution at 90 degrees C to 3 hours. After reaction the sodium silicate solution was separated using plate and frame filter press or notch filter or automated vertical pressure filter. Diluted solution that was 5-6% silica content and 1:3 ratio sodium silicate solution and carbon dioxide was bubbled in to the sodium silicate solution at an atmospheric pressure of 0.5-1 kg/cm2 into the precipitator reactor at 85-90 degrees C for an hour to yield precipitated silica and sodium carbonate. The water insoluble precipitated silica, was separated by filtration and was washed free from other metallic impurities, dried and used as final product. Washed and neutralized silica cake was further dried using spray dryer or spin flash dryer or rotary vacuum paddle dryer. Sodium carbonate solution obtained was evaporated to obtain free sodium carbonate. Multiple evaporator system to evaporate water was present in sodium carbonate solution. This system was also called as zero liquid discharge plant. Multiple evaporator system will concentrate the solution up to 4-50% of concentration. Further concentrate using crystallizer produced sodium carbonate powder.

PN CN108440488-A; CN108440488-B
TI Organic porous copolymer-based polyfunctional bionic heterogeneous catalyst in coupling reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide, is obtained by copolymerizing alkene-based metal porphyrin and alkenyl-containing quaternary phosphonium salt.
AU DING Y
   WANG Y
   WANG W
   YAN L
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018682018
AB    NOVELTY - An organic porous copolymer-based polyfunctional bionic heterogeneous catalyst is obtained by copolymerizing an alkene-based metal porphyrin and an alkenyl-containing quaternary phosphonium salt monomer, in molar ratio of 1:1-1:50.
   USE - Organic porous copolymer-based polyfunctional bionic heterogeneous catalyst in coupling reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic porous copolymer-based polyfunctional bionic heterogeneous catalyst has high activity and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of heterogeneous catalyst, which involves mixing an alkene-functionalized porphyrin monomer, an organic solvent (s1), an organic base, Lewis acid and metal precursor, reacting at room temperature for 6-24 hours to obtain alkene-functionalized metalloporphyrin, reacting the product with alkene-containing quaternary phosphonium salt and optionally crosslinking agent in an organic solvent (s2) at 273-473 K, adding the mixed solution to a synthetic autoclave, reacting at 323-473 K under inert atmosphere (argon), and performing solvothermal polymerization for 1-100 hours (preferably, 20-80 hours) in presence of a radical initiator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal is chosen from magnesium, cobalt, zinc, aluminum and chromium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkenyl functional group is terminal alpha -olefin. The alkylated metal porphyrin is preferably a metal porphyrin containing a tetrastyryl group. The catalyst is of formula (I). The alkenyl-containing quaternary phosphonium salt is chosen from methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)ruthenium chloride, methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)cesium bromide, methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)cesium iodide, ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)ruthenium chloride, ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)cesium bromide, and ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)cesium iodide. The organic solvent (s1) is chosen from dichloromethane, chloroform, benzene, toluene, methanol, ethanol, and tetrahydrofuran. The organic base is chosen from triethylamine, triethylenediamine, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5,4,0)undec-7-ene and 4-dimethylaminopyridine. The metal precursor is magnesium bromide diethyl ether, zinc acetate hydrate, cobalt chloride, aluminum chloride and/or chromium chloride. The organic solvent (s2) is chosen from benzene, toluene, methanol, ethanol, tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane and chloroform. The crosslinking agent is chosen from styrene, ethylene, propylene, divinylbenzene and 1,3,5-triacetylenylbenzene. The radical initiator is chosen from cyclohexanone peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, t-butyl hydroperoxide, azobisisobutyronitrile, and azobisisoheptanenitrile.M = Mg, Zn, Co, Al or Cr.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The organic copolymer carrier has multi-stage pore structure, specific surface area of 100-3000 m2/g, pore volume of 0.1-5 cm3/g and pore diameter of 0.2-50 nm.

The vinyl group-containing halomethyl compound is p-chloromethylstyrene, allyl chloride, p-bromomethylstyrene, allyl bromide or allyl iodide. The nitrogen-containing compound is imidazole, benzimidazole, pyridine, bipyridyl, trimethylamine, triethylamine, triethanolamine, tributylamine, triamylamine, trihexylamine, triheptylamine and/or trioctylamine. The comonomer is vinyl compound such as styrene, divinylbenzene, vinylpyridine, and vinylnaphthalene. The molar ratio of vinyl group-containing halomethyl compound and nitrogen-containing compound is 0.1-20, preferably 1. The molar ratio of vinyl group-containing halomethyl compound and comonomer is 0.1-20, preferably 1.
   USE - Preparation of quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid polymer used as carbon dioxide cycloaddition catalyst, catalyst support, solvent-resistant nanofiltration separation membrane, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas storage material (all claimed) and battery material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid polymer with excellent thermal stability and chemical stability, and adjustable pore size and specific surface area, by simple process.

The molar ratio of vinyl group-containing halomethyl compound and free radical initiator is 0.01-10. The organic solvent used for polymerization is methanol, ethanol, dichloromethane, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, benzene, toluene, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or protic or aprotic solvent such as carboxamide and N,N-dimethyl sulfoxide.

PN CN107754560-A
TI Gas processing system comprises e.g. absorption unit for contacting feed gas with non-aqueous solution to remove acid gas and water vapor from feed gas to produce rich liquid and product gas and non-aqueous solution comprises amines.
AU CHEN W
   GAO J
   HU L
   WANG D
   ZHANG J
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
GA 2018203522
AB    NOVELTY - Gas processing system comprises absorption unit for contacting feed gas with a non-aqueous solution to simultaneously remove acid gas and water vapor from the feed gas to produce rich liquid and product gas. The non-aqueous solution comprises mixture of amines and glycols. The acid gas comprises carbon dioxide (CO2). The regeneration unit is for removing acid gas and water absorbed in rich liquid to generate barren liquor and recovering liquor to the absorption unit .
   USE - Used as gas processing system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing gas, comprising contacting feed gas with a non-aqueous solution to simultaneously remove acid gas and water vapor from feed gas to produce a rich liquid and product gas, where non-aqueous solution includes mixture of amine and glycol and acid gas includes carbon dioxide (CO2), removing acid gas and water absorbed in rich solution to produce lean liquid and recycling lean liquid to the absorption unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the gas processing system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acidic gas comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen sulfide (H2S) where, concentration of CO2 in feed gas supplied to absorbing unit is less than 15%. The non-aqueous solution comprises a mixture of amine, glycol and absorption enhancer. The amine comprises primary amine, secondary amine, mixture of primary amine and secondary amine, mixture of primary amine and tertiary amine, secondary amine and tertiary amine compound, mixture of primary amine, secondary amine and tertiary amine or amino organosilicone non-aqueous solution. The glycol comprises monoethylene glycol (MEG), diethylene glycol (DEG) and/or triethylene glycol (TEG). The concentration of CO2 in feed gas fed to coarse fractionation device is in the range of 15-80%. The acid gas does not include sulfur dioxide (SO2). Preferred Method: The gas processing method further comprises arranging stirring tank to strip water absorbed in rich liquid by drying gas or removing water absorbed by rich liquid by heating and removing acid gas absorbed by rich liquid by heating to generate lean liquid and stirring the mixture of lean liquid and rich liquid. The method comprises configuring regeneration tower to strip absorbed water in rich liquid by dry gas or to heat regeneration tower with a reboiler to remove water absorbed by rich liquid and using reboiler to heat regeneration tower to remove acid gas absorbed in rich liquid thus producing lean liquid and controlling regeneration tower so its operating temperature is higher than dew point temperature of the water vapor at that location.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The non-aqueous solution comprises 30-90% amine. The regeneration unit comprises a stirred tank configured to use dry gas to strip water absorbed in rich liquid or to remove absorbed water in rich liquid by heating and heating stirring tank to remove acid gas absorbed in rich liquid to generate lean liquid and stirring tank also used to stir the mixture of lean liquid and rich liquid. The regeneration device comprises regeneration tower configured to dry gas for stripping rich liquid absorbed in water or heating reboiler to remove water absorbed in the rich liquid of regeneration tower further heated by reboiler to remove acidic gas absorbed in rich liquid to create barren liquor, where regeneration tower in each of the working temperature is higher than dew-point temperature of water vapor of the place. The gas processing system comprises flash tank which is used for reducing the pressure and for stripping at least a portion of feed gas from rich liquid to produce flash gas. The molecular sieve is for removing vapor from the product gas. The flash gas is used for combustion to heat molecular sieve or heating heater and stirring tank or heating reboiler in regeneration tower or recycling to the absorption unit. The refrigeration unit is used to refrigerate to product gas to produce a liquefied product gas and storage tank for storing liquefied product gas. The molecular sieve is coupled between absorbing unit and refrigeration unit for removing water vapor in the product gas. The evaporation gas of storage tank is used for combustion to heat molecular sieve or heating heater and stirring tank or reboiler to heat regeneration tower or recycling to absorption unit. The coarse separation device is coupled with absorption unit and located upstream of the absorption unit. The roughing device is used to remove at least a portion of the CO2 from the feed gas.

   USE - Manufacture of electrolyte membrane supported reducing electrode used for reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the electrolyte membrane is immersed in the reducing agent solution before immersing in the metal salt solution, the formation of the reducing electrode inside the electrolyte membrane when the reducing electrode is manufactured and when the reduction reaction is stopped, is suppressed. As a result, the oxidation of the reducing electrode can be suppressed and the efficiency of the carbon dioxide reduction reaction can be improved.

Preferred Component: The reducing agent solution is a polar compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The electrolyte membrane is a solid polymer membrane that conducts a cation or an anion.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   (2) preparing methanol by carbon dioxide thermal catalytic hydrogenation comprises carrying out reaction in a fixed-bed reactor, where the conditions for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol are a feed gas pressure of 3-6 MPa, preferably 5MPa, the reaction temperature is 160-260degrees Celsius, preferably 200degrees Celsius, airspeed is 4000-30000/ hour, preferably 12000/ hour and raw material gas n(H2): n(CO2) volume ratio is 1-4, preferably 3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the preparation to obtain ZnZrOx/MoS2, X represents the number of moles corresponding to the content of O atoms required by the molar content of metal atoms under the corresponding metal valence, where the valences corresponding to zinc and zirconium elements are respectively +2 valence and +4 valence, and the valence corresponding to O is -2 valence respectively, in ZnZrOx, nZn represents the amount of zinc element, nZr represents the amount of substance of the zirconium element.The molar ratio of zinc and molybdenum in t prepared ZnZrOx/MoS2 catalyst is 1:1-1:3, preferably 1:2 . The preparation process of molybdenum di sulfate comprises adding 0.070-0.89 g, preferably 0 0.420g ammonium molybdate, 0.127-1.614 g, preferably 0.762 g thiourea into 10-60 ml, preferably 30 ml water, ultrasonicating for 10-60 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, moving it into a hydrothermal kettle, and reacting at 140-220degrees Celsius, preferably 180degrees Celsius for 8-14 hours, preferably 12 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing, and drying. The specific preparation process of the ZnZrOx/MoS catalyst comprises adding 0.010-0.612 g, preferably 0.297 g zinc nitrate and 10-60 ml, preferably 20-40 ml zirconium nitrate and 30 ml water, dissolving, adding molybdenum sulfide powder according to the molar ratio of zinc and molybdenum, ultrasonically stirring, immersing and evaporating to dryness and then transfering to a muffle furnace. The method comprises after adding MoS2 powder, the power of ultrasonic treatment is 250-400W, the working frequency is 30-80 KHz, stirring time is carried out for 0.5-5 hours, preferably 1 hour, moving into a water bath, immersing and evaporating to dryness, where water bath temperature is 60-90degrees Celsius, preferably 90degrees Celsius, cooling to 20-40degrees Celsius, transfering to muffle furnace calcination after grinding, in normal pressure air atmosphere, where the temperature of calcination is 300-700degrees Celsius preferably 550degrees Celsius for 2-8 hours, preferably 4 hours, and the heating rate from room temperature to calcination temperature is 2-10degrees Celsius/minute, preferably 2 3degrees Celsius/minute and cooling to 20-40degrees Celsius.

AE MODERN PROCESS EQUIP INC (MODE-Non-standard)
GA 202198911A
AB    NOVELTY - Coffee degassing method involves flowing a diffusion gas into an one branch of a degassing tree in a coffee bin containing roasted coffee, where the flowing diffuses a carbon dioxide from the roasted coffee and moves the carbon dioxide and the diffusion gas through the branch and a trunk of the degassing tree toward a top end of the bin, where the branch is in the roasted coffee and the branch is open to the roasted coffee, the branch is connected to the trunk and extends from an exterior of the trunk, and, exhausting the carbon dioxide and the diffusion gas from the bin.

TI Direct reduction of iron ore to sponge iron            involves successively passing iron ore through            reduction zone for reducing iron ore to sponge iron,            and cooling zone for cooling sponge iron by cooling gas            comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU WEINBERG M
   SCHUBERT D
AE THYSSENKRUPP STEEL EURO AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP STEEL EURO AG (THYS-C)
GA 202153661K
AB    NOVELTY - Direct reduction of iron ore to sponge iron                involves successively passing the iron ore through                a reduction zone (13) for reducing the iron ore to                sponge iron, and a cooling zone (15) for cooling                the sponge iron. The iron ore in the reduction zone                is traversed by a reducing gas. The sponge iron is                traversed by a cooling gas flowing through the                cooling zone. The cooling gas comprises hydrogen                and carbon dioxide in a mol ratio of greater than                1.8. The ratio of carbon dioxide is greater than 20                mol%.
   USE - Direct reduction of iron ore to sponge                iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly due to                reduced consumption of greenhouse gas e.g. carbon                dioxide and fossil fuels, occur no side reactions                due to usage of hydrogen instead of carbon                monoxide, and ensures efficient reaction between                gases and solids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cooling                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a shaft                furnace.
   Shaft furnace (11)
   Reduction zone (13)
   Cooling zone (15)
   Inlets (17,21)
   Outlet (23)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cooling gas undergoes                  Bosch reaction in presence of the sponge iron as                  a catalyst, so that carbon is deposited on the                  sponge iron. The deposited carbon combines with                  iron of the sponge iron to form iron carbide.                  Preferred Composition: The cooled sponge iron                  contains 0.5-4.5 wt.% carbon. The reducing gas                  contains more than 75 mol%, preferably more than                  85 mol% hydrogen, and less than 5 mol%,                  preferably less than 2 mol% hydrocarbons.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cooling gas                  further contains less than 5 mol%, preferably                  less than 2 mol% hydrocarbons.

PN CN112574036-A; CN112574036-B
TI Preparation of dimethyl carbonate used in chemical            industry, involves passing in carbon dioxide to make            reaction device reach set pressure, cooling temperature            of reaction device, discharging gas and separating            dimethyl carbonate.
AU XU K
   ZHANG X
   YIN X
   WU X
   LU J
   LIU S
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DEPU NEW MATERIAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY              CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021372599
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dimethyl carbonate, involves                (1) adding methanol and catalyst to the closed                reaction device, passing in carbon dioxide for                purging, passing in carbon dioxide to make the                reaction device reach the set pressure and                conducting the reaction under heating and (2)                cooling the temperature of the reaction device,                discharging the gas in the device and separating                dimethyl carbonate after the material is discharged                and after the reaction is completed. The catalyst                is prepared by (i) adding a coupling agent to the                calcium carbonate suspension, stirring evenly,                adding copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite,                stirring evenly to obtain a composite suspension                for use, (ii) immersing the cotton in the composite                suspension, soaking, taking the cotton out and                drying to obtain the catalyst precursor for use and                (iii) putting the catalyst precursor in a                protective atmosphere for thermal decomposition and                sintering in air.
   USE - Preparation of dimethyl carbonate used in                chemical industry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts a catalyst with a special                structure to catalyze carbon dioxide and methanol                to prepare dimethyl carbonate, which effectively                improves the reaction efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of calcium                  carbonate powder and deionized water is 1 g: 5-12                  ml. In step (i), the mass ratio of calcium                  carbonate and copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite                  is (55-70):(30-4). Preferred Process: The calcium                  carbonate suspension is prepared by                  ultrasonically stirring calcium carbonate powder                  and deionized water. In step (2), the dimethyl                  carbonate product in the reaction product is                  separated by rectification. Preferred Components:                  In step (iii), the protective atmosphere includes                  any one of nitrogen and argon. Preferred                  Conditions: In step (ii), the drying temperature                  is 60-85 degrees C, and the drying time is 2-4                  hours. In step (iii), the temperature of the                  thermal decomposition is 520-650 degrees C, and                  the time is 2-5 hours. In step (iii), the                  sintering temperature is 600-780 degrees C and                  the time is 1.5-3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (1), the                  addition ratio of methanol and catalyst is                  110-140 ml: 0.5-1.7 g. Preferred Conditions: In                  step (1), the pressure is 1.5-5 MPa and the                  heating temperature is 95-120 degrees C.                  Preferred Component: In the step (i), the                  coupling agent includes silane coupling                  agent.

The catalyst is applied to the reaction of CO2 hydrogenation to prepare ethylene, propylene, and butene, and the reaction conditions are, dihydrogen/carbon dioxide (H2/CO2) is 1-4, temperature is 260-400 degrees C, and pressure is 1-3 megapascal. The catalyst is based on the SrFexCoyO3- delta catalyst precursor. The urea solution is added to the matrix, and after it is in a slurry state, porous carbon is added to mixed, and after drying again, calcined under nitrogen protection at 350 degrees C for 2 hours and ground to obtain the target catalyst.
   USE - Catalyst used for CO2 hydrogenation to directly produce low-carbon olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can effectively inhibit the secondary hydrogenation reaction of primary olefins, and regulate the distribution of products with high added value, especially the acquisition of low-carbon olefins is about 45%, and preparation method used by the catalyst is simple, environmentally friendly, convenient and controllable, has good repeatability, is environmentally friendly, has a long reaction life, and has potential economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The urea concentration is 1-30 mol/L. The porous carbon doping content accounts for 0.5-50 wt.% of the total weight of the catalyst. Preferred Components: The catalyst is optimized and modified by elements of potassium (K), manganese (Mn), zinc (Zn), sodium (Na), and magnesium (Mg).

PT P
PN CN111389369-A
TI Synthesizing metal organic framework mesoporous material useful in adsorption of carbon dioxide, and absorption of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. adding zinc acetate dihydrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone, fully stirring and drying.
AU ZHONG M
   XIE Y
   ZENG D
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2020668048
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing metal organic framework mesoporous material comprises (i) adding zinc acetate dihydrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone to the analytically pure absolute ethanol solution, fully stirring and uniformly mixing, (ii) dissolving 2-methylimidazole in absolute ethanol, magnetically stirring for 30 minutes to make it completely dissolved and the solution becomes clear and transparent, (iii) mixing the two solutions obtained in steps (i) and (ii), stirring at 50-90 degrees C for a period of time until the two solutions are fully mixed, then transferring to a polytetrafluoroethylene stainless steel reactor, fully reacting at 120 degrees C for 5 hours, and then naturally cooling to room temperature, separating the white solid material, washing and drying to obtain Zn-ZIF material, and(iv) placing the Zn-ZIF material in a tube furnace under the protection of inert gas nitrogen (N2), roasting in a tube furnace at 550-850 degrees C for 5-9 hours to obtain metal organic framework mesoporous material.
   USE - The mesoporous material is useful in adsorption of carbon dioxide, and absorption of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal organic framework mesoporous material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass fraction ratios of the zinc acetate dihydrate solution and the 2-methylimidazole solution are 20%, 40%, and 60%, respectively. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the stirring process is carried out for 24 hours. In the step (iv), the heating rate is 5 degrees C/minute. In the step (iii), the solvothermal reaction environment is polytetrafluoroethylene stainless steel reactor. The solvothermal reaction time is 3-6 hours. The solvothermal reaction temperature is 100-200 degrees C.

PN WO2020043985-A1; FR3085370-A1; FR3085370-B1; CN112689610-A; EP3844102-A1
TI Producing synthesis gas used for forming e.g. methanol, by supplying gas stream containing carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon, generating plasma cone from arc plasma torch, mixing gas stream with plasma cone, and propagating mixture.
AU ROBERT-ARNOUIL J
AE EUROPLASMA (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROPLASMA (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROPLASMA NV (FRFR-C)
   EUROPLASMA (EURO-Non-standard)
GA 202019376X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a synthesis gas by treating a gas stream containing carbon dioxide and one or more hydrocarbons, involves (a) supplying a gas stream containing 10-50 vol.% carbon dioxide and 10-50 vol.% one or more hydrocarbons, (b) generating a plasma cone from a non-transferred arc plasma torch, (c) mixing the gas stream with the plasma cone downstream from the plasma torch, and (d) propagating the mixture in an elongate reactor in order to convert the gas stream into a synthesis gas.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing synthesis gas, and treating synthesis gas for forming hydrogen gas, methanol and ammonia (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces synthesis gas in a simple, reliable and cost-effective manner, enables to treat large gas volumes, is capable of accepting gas streams having wide fluctuations in quality and quantity, reduces emissions of carbon dioxide, and is suitable for thermal power stations and industrial installations such as steel and cement industries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for treating a synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) separating the to-be-treated gas stream into a first gas stream and a second gas stream, (b) generating a plasma cone from a non-transferred arc plasma torch using at least the second gas stream as plasma gas, (c) mixing the first gas stream with the plasma cone downstream of the plasma torch, and (d) propagating the mixture in an elongated reactor to ensure the conversion of the first gas stream and the plasma cone into a synthesis gas. The plasma gas supplying the plasma torch comprises the only second gas stream. When the plasma torch is supplied with the plasma gas to generate the plasma cone, the plasma gas is at a temperature above the dew point of heavy hydrocarbons such as tar, contained in the second gas stream. The plasma gas is at a temperature of 20-150 degrees C. The gas stream is supplied at a pressure higher than atmospheric pressure, and the plasma gas supplying the non-transferred arc plasma torch is at a supply pressure (Pr-alim), which is possible to generate a plasma cone at least equal to the operating pressure of the reactor. The method involves mounting the plasma torch on an introduction enclosure, receiving the gas stream or the first gas stream at at least one inlet unit of the introduction enclosure, where the input is being placed downstream of the plasma torch, and introducing the gas stream or the first gas stream so that it at least partially meets the plasma dart to ensure its mixing with the plasma dart, where the torch has a main axis, and the dart having an axis of propagation substantially collinear with the main axis of the torch. The method involves supplying the to-be-treated gas stream at a temperature above ambient temperature, where the to-be-treated gas stream comprises carbon dioxide and methane. The method involves introducing the gas stream or the first gas stream in a direction separate from the propagation axis to establish a zone of turbulent mixing between the plasma dart and the gas stream or first gas stream. The method involves introducing the gas stream or the first gas stream into an introduction enclosure in communication with the reactor, and introducing at least one fluid for adjusting the composition of the mixture into the introduction enclosure or into the reactor, such as water vapor.

TI Formation of porous metal-organic framework for            use in capture of acidic gas, involves reacting            precursor metal-organic framework comprising metal            ions, each coordinated with auxiliary ligand to perform            post-synthetic ligand exchange.
AU WADE C
   BIEN C
   CAI Z
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 2019A6203S
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of porous metal-organic framework                (MOF) comprising metal-hydroxide moieties for use                in capture of acidic gas, involves providing a                precursor MOF comprising several metal ions each                coordinated with an auxiliary ligand and backbone                ligand(s) comprising a coordination moiety (a) and                a coordination moiety (b), and reacting the                precursor MOF under conditions appropriate to                perform post-synthetic ligand exchange of the                auxiliary ligand for a hydroxide ligand.
   USE - Formation of porous metal-organic framework                for use in capture of acidic gas comprising carbon                dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide and                acetylene from gas mixture (all claimed), in gas                separation applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical formation of                porous metal-organic framework having improved                chemical stability and thermal stability, and                excellent performance for low pressure carbon                dioxide capture via carbon dioxide/bicarbonate                fixation mechanism in which cooperative                inter-cluster hydrogen bonding interactions enhance                carbon dioxide capture performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for capture                of acidic gas from gas mixture, which involves                contacting the gas mixture with the porous                MOF.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal ions                  comprise manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper                  and/or zinc.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The formation of porous                  MOF comprising metal-hydroxide moieties involves                  providing precursor MOF, preferably aluminum                  hydroxide-6-(5-carboxypyridin-2-yl)pyridine-3-carboxylic                  acid (bpydc) (Al(OH)-(bpydc)) and zirconium oxide                  hydroxide-6-(5-carboxypyridin-2-yl)pyridine-3-carboxylic                  acid (Zr6O4(OH)4(bpydc)6), reacting the precursor                  MOF under conditions appropriate to perform                  post-synthetic metal insertion, for immobilizing                  metal-auxiliary ligand (MX'2) species at one or                  more bipyridine sites within the MOF, and                  performing post-synthetic ligand exchange of the                  auxiliary ligands for hydroxide ligands. The                  post-synthetic ligand exchange includes direct                  reaction with hydroxide, reaction with water and                  an organic base, basic ligand exchange and                  hydrolysis, and/or bicarbonate exchange followed                  by thermolysis. The direct reaction with                  hydroxide involves contacting the precursor MOF                  with a hydroxide source, such as sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium                  hydroxide, cesium hydroxide and/or                  tetraalkylammonium hydroxide. The reaction with                  water and organic base involves contacting the                  precursor MOF with an aqueous solution comprising                  an organic base, such as 2,6-lutidine,                  trialkylamine and/or                  1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene. The basic                  ligand exchange and hydrolysis involves                  contacting the precursor MOF with a nucleophile,                  such as alkoxide or thiolate, and hydrolyzing, or                  contacting the precursor MOF with formate,                  heating the precursor MOF to drive off carbon                  dioxide, and hydrolyzing. The capture of acidic                  gas from gas mixture further involves thermally                  regenerating the MOF by heating the MOF to a                  temperature of 80-160 degrees C.M = metal ion;andX' = auxiliary ligand.Preferred Composition: In MOF,                  metal-hydroxide moieties are present at a                  metal-hydroxide site density of 1.5-7.5 mmol g 1.                  The moiety (a) and the moiety (b) comprise                  carboxylate moieties. The backbone ligand is                  6-(5-carboxypyridin-2-yl)pyridine-3-carboxylic                  acid of formula (I), and the moiety (a) comprises                  a heterocyclic moiety, and the moiety (b)                  comprises a carboxylate moiety. The backbone                  ligand is 4-(4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)benzoic acid                  of formula (II), and the moiety (a) and the                  moiety (b) comprise heterocyclic moieties. The                  heterocyclic moieties comprise triazole moieties.                  The backbone ligand comprises 4H-1,2,4-triazole                  ligands of formula (III), preferably chosen from                  ligands of formulae (III-1)-(III-4), and                  4,5-dihydro-1H-1,2,3-triazole ligands of formula                  (IV), preferably chosen from ligands of formulae                  (IV-1)-(IV-8). The MOF comprises metal-hydroxide                  moieties comprising secondary building units, and                  Kuratowski cluster-based secondary building                  units. If MOF comprises metal-hydroxide moieties,                  the metal-metal distance measured between metal                  centers of neighboring secondary building units                  by X-ray crystallography is 5-15 Angstrom ,                  preferably 5-12 Angstrom . The MOF comprising                  metal-hydroxide moieties comprises CFA-l-,                  MFU-4-, or MFU-4l-type framework. The auxiliary                  ligand comprises a halide (e.g. chlorine, iodine                  and bromine), aliphatic and aromatic carboxylate                  (e.g. acetate, propionate, butanoate and                  benzoate), triflate, nitrate, sulfate, hydrogen                  sulfate, phosphate, hydrogen phosphate,                  dihydrogen phosphate, perchlorate, azide,                  cyanide, nitrite, thiocyanate, hydrogen, 1-4C                  alkyl, 1-4C alkoxy, 1-4C alkylthio, and a solvent                  molecule. The gas mixture comprises 5000 ppm or                  less acidic gas.n = 1-3;X = bonds formed between ring A' and ring                  B', preferably phenyl, adamantyl, or                  azaadamantyl;andC' = bonds formed between ring A' and ring                  B'.

PT P
PN US2018221862-A1; US10668462-B2
TI Production of multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to methanol, involves mixing e.g. titanium alkoxide, inorganic acid, and surfactant, ultrasonically processing mixture, drying to form powder, and heating.
AU BAEISSA E S
AE UNIV KING ABDULAZIZ (UYKI-Non-standard)
GA 201865915C
AB    NOVELTY - Production of multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst involves mixing a titanium alkoxide, an alcohol, water, an inorganic acid, and a surfactant to form a mixture (a), ultrasonically processing the mixture to produce an ultrasonically processed mixture, drying the ultrasonically processed mixture to form a powder, and heating the powder at 400 degrees C or more to form a multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst comprising anatase, brookite, and rutile phases.
   USE - Production of multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst used for forming platinum-doped multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst used in reduction of carbon dioxide into methanol (all claimed). Can also be used for conversion of gas-phase carbon dioxide, and reduction of aqueous metal ions, such as chromium(VI) or nickel(II) ions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of the multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst which can be recovered and reused, and excellent photocatalytic activity for at least 4 reaction cycles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for reducing carbon dioxide into methanol, involves mixing the photocatalyst with a carbonate solution to form a dispersed photocatalyst mixture, and irradiating the dispersed photocatalyst mixture with light to produce methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The platinum-doped multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst comprises anatase, brookite, and rutile phases. The dispersed photocatalyst mixture comprises carbonate and/or bicarbonate at a total concentration of 30-150 mM. The platinum-doped multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst comprises 0.5-3 wt.% platinum. Preferred Properties: The platinum-doped multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst has band gap energy of 2.10-2.45 eV and a surface area of 70-90 m2/g. The titanium alkoxide has concentration of 0.5-2 M in the mixture (a). The alcohol has concentration of 1-5 M in the mixture (a). The inorganic acid has concentration of 0.05-1 mM in the mixture (a). The surfactant has concentration of 3.5-5.2 mM in the mixture (a). The multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst has band gap energy of 2.46-2.70 eV and surface area of 91-105 m2/g. The platinum precursor has concentration of 0.5-5 mM in the mixture (b). The multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst has concentration of 5-50 g/L. The multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst has concentration of 0.2-5 g/L in the dispersed photocatalyst mixture. Preferred Process: The method further involves mixing the multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst with a platinum precursor and an alcohol to form a mixture (b), irradiating the mixture (b) with an ultraviolet radiation to produce an irradiated mixture, and drying the irradiated mixture to form a platinum-doped multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst. The method for reducing carbon dioxide into methanol further involves recovering the multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst after the irradiating to obtain a recovered multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst, and reusing the recovered multiphasic titanium dioxide photocatalyst, which maintains photocatalytic activity for at least 4 reaction cycles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium alkoxide is titanium isopropoxide. The surfactant is alkylamine comprising 6-20 carbon atoms per molecule, preferably hexadecylamine. The methanol is produced at a rate of 0.2-1 mu mol/hour per gram of photocatalyst.

PN US2018142581-A1; WO2018097450-A1; KR2018058325-A; KR1947877-B1; US10371015-B2
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide generation system for parallel recuperative type, has recuperator unit including set of recuperators exchanging heat between working fluid passing through turbines and working fluid passing through compressor.
AU JEONG C R
   KANG S G
   HWANG J H
   BAK B G
   LEE E C
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 2018412141
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor compressing a working fluid. A heat exchanger unit is supplied heat from an external heat source to heat the working fluid. A set of turbines is driven by the working fluid. A recuperator unit includes a set of recuperators exchanging heat between the working fluid passing through the turbines and the working fluid passing through the compressor to cool the working fluid passing through the turbines and installed in parallel. A pre-cooler cools the working fluid primarily cooled by the recuperator unit and supplies the pre-cooled working fluid to the compressor.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide generation system for a parallel recuperative type.
   ADVANTAGE - The system distributes flow rate to maintain a mass balance of the entire system while maintaining the temperature difference between the high and low temperature portions of each heat exchanger constant by utilizing physical properties according to the pressure of the working fluid in each operation region.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a supercritical carbon dioxide generation system.
   Turbine (1)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide (2)
   Outlet temperature (3)
   Outlet temperature (5)
   Flow Rate (9)

PN KR2022128563-A
TI Producing biosuccinic acid by performing anaerobic            fermentation by adding sugar to first reaction tank,            inoculating hydrogen-producing anaerobic            microorganisms, collecting hydrogen and carbon dioxide            and producing succinic acid using succinic            acid-producing microorganism.
AU KIMSANGHYUN
   JEONG J H
   AMRADINARESHKUMA
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 2022C1777U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing biosuccinic acid involves                (a) performing anaerobic fermentation by adding                sugar to first reaction tank and inoculating                hydrogen-producing anaerobic microorganism, (b)                after step (a), collecting hydrogen and carbon                dioxide produced through anaerobic fermentation                from first reactor, and transferring to second                reactor and (c) after step (b), producing succinic                acid using a succinic acid-producing microorganism                in the secondary reactor to which hydrogen and                carbon dioxide are supplied.
   USE - The method is useful for producing biosuccinic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces succinic acid at high                yield with increased metabolic efficiency and                improved high rate of succinic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The sugar is glucose. The                  hydrogen-producing anaerobic microorganism is any                  one chosen from Clostridium butylicum,                  Clostridium tyrobutyricum, Clostridium                  acetobutylicum, Clostridium fridicarnisand                  Clostridium vincentii. The succinic                  acid-producing microorganism is any one chosen                  from Actinobacillus succinogenes,                  Anaerobiospirillum succiniciproducens, Mannheimia                  succiniciproducensand Basfia succiniciproducens.                  In step (c), magnesium carbonate is additionally                  added to second reaction tank.

TI Composition useful in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or carbohydrates, comprises nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds and/or metal elements, elemental metal as auxiliary catalyst and elemental metal as reactant.
AU CAO X

AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
   UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 202066801X
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds and/or metal elements, elemental metal as auxiliary catalyst, another elemental metal as reactant; and the elemental metal as reactant is more active than elemental metal as auxiliary catalyst.
   USE - The composition is useful in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or carbohydrates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has no light needed during reaction and no heating required, product is mostly gas and no need of solvent separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of composition in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide or carbohydrates comprising mixing substrate with composition and reacting to obtain carbon monoxide and/or methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound is imidazole, 1-methylimidazole, 1-ethylimidazole, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole tetrafluoroborate, 4-methylimidazole, 1-allylimidazole, 2-methylimidazole, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole bromide, 1-benzylimidazole, histamine, 1-butylimidazole, (1-imidazolyl)acetonitrile, 1,2-dimethylimidazole, 1-acetylimidazole and/or 1,2,4-triazole. The elemental metals are of two types. The elemental metal as auxiliary catalyst is tin, copper, silver, nickel, cadmium, cobalt and lead. The elemental metal as reactant is zinc, iron, aluminum, manganese, magnesium, nickel and tin. The solvent is water, methanol or ethanol. The source of carbon dioxide is pure carbon dioxide or exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound solid in solvent at room temperature.

PT P
PN CN110787789-A
TI Catalyst for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises ceria rod as carrier, and copper and/or palladium as active component.
AU LIU X
   JIANG F
   XU Y
   LIU B
   WANG S
   WANG L
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2020154873
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises ceria rod as carrier, and copper and/or palladium as active component.
   USE - Catalyst is used for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity and methanol selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which involves dissolving a soluble cerium salt in deionized water, adding sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring, hydrothermally reacting, centrifuging, separating and washing the obtained solid, drying, and roasting to obtain ceria rod carrier, dissolving active component precursor and additive precursor in a solvent to form a solution, immersing the carrier in the solution, drying, and roasting; and
   (2) preparation of methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide using the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst further comprises additive(s) chosen from potassium, calcium, magnesium, niobium, zinc, zirconium, silver, gold, platinum, nickel, chromium, iron, cobalt, titanium, lanthanum, cadmium and gallium. The soluble cerium salt is cerium nitrate, ceric ammonium sulfate, cerium trifluoromethanesulfonate, cerium chloride, cerium sulfate, cerium carbonate, and/or cerium acetate. The additive precursor is a nitrate, a carbonate, a sulfate, and/or an acetate. The active component precursor is copper nitrate, copper phosphate, copper sulfate, copper acetate, copper acetate, copper citrate, copper thiocyanate, copper acetylacetonate, copper chloride, palladium nitrate, palladium acetate, palladium acetylacetonate, and/or palladium chloride. Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 50-99.9 wt.% carrier, 0.1-50 wt.% active component, and 0-20 wt.% additive. Preferred Properties: The ceria rod has a length of 10-1000 nm and a diameter of 2-100 nm. Preferred Method: Prior to use, the catalyst is reduced in an atmosphere of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, or hydrogen/carbon monoxide mixture at 150-600 degrees C for 1-8 hours at an airspeed of 800-30000 mL/g/hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Carbon dioxide hydrogenation is carried out at a temperature of 180-300 degrees C, a pressure of 1-7 MPa, and an airspeed of 800-30000 mL/g/hour in a fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor, using carbon dioxide/hydrogen, carbon monoxide/hydrogen, or carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide/hydrogen as raw material gas.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Annulus protection fluid comprises corrosion inhibitor, surfactant, scale inhibitor and diesel.
AU HE M
   HE Z
   ZHOU P
   JIANG Y
   LIU N
   DONG J
   TANG Z
   QIU X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2018553341
AB    NOVELTY - Annulus protection fluid comprises 2.5-7 wt.% corrosion inhibitor, 1-5 wt.% surfactant, 1-3 wt.% scale inhibitor and diesel (balance amount).
   USE - The annulus protection fluid is useful in carbon dioxide flooding injection well (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The annulus protection fluid: has excellent low temperature stability; reduces carbon dioxide injection well pipe column corrosion rate; and is suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing annulus protection fluid comprising (i) dissolving modified imidazoline derivative and alkylphenol ethoxylate phosphate in diesel oil until under normal temperature and pressure, (ii) adding hexamethylenetetramine and diethylenetriamine penta methylene phosphate into the solution obtained in the step (i) under stirring and mixing to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of annulus protection fluid in carbon dioxide flooding injection well.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The corrosion inhibitor is modified imidazoline derivative and/or hexamethylenetetramine, the surfactant is alkylphenol ethoxylate phosphate and/or polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene alkyl phenol ether, and the scale inhibitor is diethylene triamine penta (methylene phosphate) and/or amino trimethylene phosphonic acid. The corrosion inhibitor is modified imidazoline derivative and/or hexamethylenetetramine, the mass fraction of the modified imidazoline derivative is 0.5-2%, the mass fraction of hexamethylenetetramine is 2-5%, the surfactant is alkylphenol ethoxylate phosphate, and the scale inhibitor is diethylene triamine penta methylene phosphate. The modified imidazoline derivative is phosphate imidazoline, mercaptobenzothiazole imidazoline or thiourea imidazoline benzoate. The amount of single well is 7-15 tons. Preferred Composition: The annulus protection fluid comprises 2 wt.% modified imidazoline derivative, 1 wt.% alkylphenol ethoxylate phosphate, 5 wt.% hexamethylenetetramine, 3 wt.% diethylenetriamine penta methylene phosphate and 89 wt.% diesel, preferably 0.5 wt.% modified imidazoline derivative, 5 wt.% alkylphenol ethoxylate phosphate, 3 wt.% hexamethylenetetramine, 2 wt.% diethylenetriamine penta methylene phosphate and 89.5 wt.% diesel.

The carbon dioxide content tester comprises bracket, an absorber, a measuring tube and two tap. one end of the absorber is fixedly connected with a first plug, and the other end is fixedly connected with siphon, siphon is upwards fixedly connected to the second plug, the second plug connected by a hose placed vertically is set with graduations of the measuring tube. TI Potato storing method, involves cleaning potato            cellar, spraying cellar wall with formalin solution,            judging whether measured value of carbon dioxide is            less than threshold value, and closing ventilation            system.
AU YU X
AE FOSHAN GAOMING YUNDA MACHINERY TECHNOLOG (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018205803
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cleaning potato cellar. A                cellar wall is sprayed with formalin solution. An                opening and a vent hole formed on a cellar door are                sealed. A pit part is provided with multiple                temperature sensors. The temperature sensors are                located on a cold air outlet when measured                temperature is greater than four degree Celsius. A                cold air blowing state of each temperature sensor                is controlled. A cellar carbon dioxide detector is                arranged with a meter. Judgment is made to check                whether a measured value of carbon dioxide is less                than a threshold value. A ventilation system is                closed.
   USE - Potato storing method.
DC P12 (Harvesting (A01D, F).)

PN JP2019217430-A
TI Carbon dioxide absorbing material of carbon dioxide absorbing device, absorbs carbon dioxide that is contained in gas from gas containing moisture and carbon dioxide, and includes lithium sodium silicate.
AU NAKA T
   KOJI H
   YAMADA H
   YANASE I
AE SHARP KK (SHAF-C)
   UNIV SAITAMA (SAIT-C)
GA 2019A8086X
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide absorbing material (10) absorbs carbon dioxide that is contained in the gas from a gas containing moisture and carbon dioxide, and includes lithium sodium silicate.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbing material of carbon dioxide absorbing device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorption rate can be improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide absorbing device has a supply unit that supplies a gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of 0.1% or more and a humidity of 35% or more relative humidity to the carbon dioxide absorbing material. A concentration unit increases the carbon dioxide concentration of the gas taken in by the carbon dioxide absorbing device. The concentration unit supplies gas after increasing the carbon dioxide concentration to the carbon dioxide absorbing material. A concentration detecting unit detects the carbon dioxide concentration of the gas taken in by the carbon dioxide absorbing device. A concentration control unit controls the concentration unit based on a detection result of the concentration detection unit. A humidity detecting unit detects humidity of gas taken in by the carbon dioxide absorbing device. A humidification control unit controls the humidification unit based on a detection result of the humidity detection unit. The humidification control unit increases the humidity of the gas taken in by the carbon dioxide absorbing device in the humidifying unit. A supply route switching unit switches a supply route for supplying the gas to the carbon dioxide absorbing material and a non-supply route for preventing the gas from being supplied to the carbon dioxide absorbing material. A supply switching control unit controls the supply path switching unit based on a detection result of the concentration detection unit and a detection result of the humidity detection unit. A heating control unit controls a temperature in the heating unit; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide absorption method, involves supplying the gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of 0.1% or more and a humidity of 35% or more to the carbon dioxide absorbing material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the configuration of a main portion of a carbon dioxide absorbing device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide absorbing device (1)
   Carbon dioxide absorbing material (10)
   Concentration unit (11)
   Humidifying unit (12)
   Gas (G0)

PN CN114922705-A
TI Shunt repressing supercritical carbon dioxide            circulating system used in power generation field, has            external heat source which is set between cold side of            high pressure secondary heat regenerator and high            pressure turbine set and between the three-way valve            and middle pressure turbine set.
AU WU J
   GAO W
   BAI W
   QIAO Y
   ZHANG T
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2022B0501F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a middle pressure turbine set                (7) whose exhaust side is sequentially connected                with the hot side of the high pressure primary heat                regenerator (2a) and the hot side of the medium                pressure heat regenerator (6). The exhaust side                sequentially the high pressure turbine set (4) is                connected with the hot side of the high pressure                secondary heat regenerator (2b) and the three-way                valve (12). The input end of the shunt repressing                loop is connected with the hot side outlet of the                medium pressure regenerator, and the output end is                respectively connected with input end of the high                pressure compressor (1) and the medium pressure                compressor (5). An external heat source (3) is set                between cold side of the high pressure secondary                heat regenerator and the high pressure turbine set                and between the three-way valve and middle pressure                turbine set.
   USE - Shunt repressing supercritical carbon dioxide                circulating system used in power generation                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The shunt repressing supercritical carbon                dioxide circulating system controls the parallel                design to make the high pressure compressor and the                medium pressure compressor to operate at a lower                temperature slightly higher than the liquid phase                temperature, improves the working efficiency of the                medium-pressure compressor, and improves working                ability of medium pressure turbine machine set. The                shunt repressing supercritical carbon dioxide                circulating system solves the problem that the                supercritical carbon dioxide unit operation                process, back pressure fluctuation caused by                disturbance to the system; so as to solve the limit                of the series cycle for the total flow rate. The                three-way valve directly recycles waste heat                working medium after high pressure two-stage heat                regenerator heat exchange in operation, which                realizes uniform and coordination of the system.                The external heat source is set between the cold                side and the hot side of the hot pressure secondary                heat regenerators, which stabilizes the pressure of                the internal pressure system, and gradually reduces                the load of the high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a shunt                repressing supercritical carbon dioxide circulating                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                shunt repressing supercritical carbon dioxide                circulating system.
   1High pressure compressor
   2aHigh pressure primary heat                regenerator
   2bHigh pressure secondary heat                regenerator
   3External heat source
   4High pressure turbine set
   5Medium pressure compressor
   6Medium pressure heat regenerator
   7Middle pressure turbine set
   12Three-way valve
