PN WO2022169363-A1; NL2027501-B1
TI Extracting organic acid from mixture comprises            passing eluent along sorbent to provide eluate            comprising solvent comprising acetone or alcohol,            cation and separating organic acid from eluate.
AU CABRERA C
   CAPARROS-SALVADOR F
   LEIROS J P C
   CABRERA-RODRIGUEZ C I
AE GREENCOVERY BV (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A15481
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting an organic acid from a mixture,                comprises: an extraction stage comprising providing                the mixture to a sorbent; an elution stage                comprising passing an eluent along the sorbent to                provide an eluate, where the eluent comprises a                solvent comprising acetone or alcohol, and the                eluent comprising at least 0.1 M of hydroxy, and at                least 0.1 M of an cation comprising sodium,                potassium ion, calcium ion, magnesium and ammonium                ion; a precipitation stage comprising providing                carbon dioxide to the eluate, the carbon dioxide                and the cation form a carbonate, the carbonate                precipitates; and a separation stage comprising                separating the organic acid from the eluate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting an organic                acid from a mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides benefit that organic                acids may be recovered from a mixture efficiently                while forming little waste product, preferably                organic acids with a low pKa and/or a low                solubility in alcohol may be recovered efficiently;                facilitates recovering specific acid, especially                via the choice of sorbent; utilizes eluent may                enable recovering acids with a low pKa or poor                solubility in alcohol, especially while also                regenerating the sorbent; and utilizes carbon                dioxide exposure may enable efficiently removing                ion and the hydroxy from the eluate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   ystem for recovery of an organic acid from a                mixture comprising inlet, sorbent container, eluent                supply, a separation unit, a carbon dioxide supply,                where the eluent supply is configured to provide an                eluent to the sorbent container, the separation                unit is configured to receive an eluate from the                sorbent container, the sorbent container is                configured to host a sorbent, and the system has an                operational mode, the inlet is configured to                provide the mixture to the sorbent, the eluent                supply is configured to provide the eluent to the                sorbent to provide an eluate to the separation                unit, the eluent comprises 30-90 wt.% solvent and                the eluent, the carbon dioxide supply is configured                to provide carbon dioxide to the separation unit,                the carbon dioxide and the cation form a carbonate,                the carbonate precipitates, the separation unit is                configured to separate the organic acid from the                eluate;
   computer program product comprising                instructions for execution on a control system                functionally coupled to a system, where the                instructions, when executed by the control system,                cause the system to carry out the method; and
   data carrier, carrying thereupon program                instructions which, when executed by a control                system functionally coupled to a system, cause the                system to carry out the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separation stage                  comprises providing carbon dioxide to the eluate                  to reduce the pH of the eluate to a precipitation                  point of the organic acid. The organic acid                  comprises at least two organic acid compounds and                  the separation stage comprises providing carbon                  dioxide to the eluate to successively reduce the                  eluate to the precipitation points of the organic                  acid compounds to separate the organic acid                  compounds. The separation unit comprises a first                  separation unit and a second separation unit,                  where the first separation unit is configured to                  receive an eluate from the sorbent, where in the                  operational mode, the carbon dioxide supply is                  configured to provide carbon dioxide to the first                  separation unit, where the first separation unit                  is configured to separate the precipitated                  carbonate and remaining eluate, and to provide                  the eluate to the second separation unit, the                  second separation unit is configured to separate                  the organic acid from the eluate. The operational                  mode, the carbon dioxide supply is configured to                  provide carbon dioxide to the second separation                  unit to reduce the pH of the eluate to a                  precipitation point of the organic acid.                  Preferred Components: The organic acid comprises                  an amino acid. The organic acid has a pKa                  &#8805; 4. The sorbent comprises a weak anion                  exchange resin, a strong anion exchange resin, or                  an adsorbent. The solvent comprises acetone,                  methanol, ethanol, and ethylene glycol, and the                  eluent comprises 30-90 wt.% solvent. The eluent                  comprises 10-50 wt.% water, and at least 40 wt.%                  solvent. The precipitation stage comprises                  providing the carbon dioxide at a temperature of                  5-40&#176; C. The precipitation stage                  comprises separating the eluate and the                  precipitated carbonate. The cation comprises                  sodium ion. The mixture is a waste stream.
   EXAMPLE - Column packed with 6.6 ml of a weak anion                exchange resin, one was performed with a solution                containing 20 g/l of lactic acid and the other one                with a solution with 20 g/l of caproic acid. The                total volume of solution passed through the column                during adsorption was 30 ml, which corresponds to a                total lactic acid/caproic acid mass of 0.6 g fed to                the column. The total outlet from adsorption and                desorption was collected and afterwards analyzed in                an HPLC to quantify the amount of lactate and                caproate present in the fractions. A total of 48.1                mg lactate was adsorbed per ml of the resin bed,                whereas a total of 38.3 mg of caproate was adsorbed                per ml of the resin bed. After washing the column                with MiliQ(RTM: Ultrapure water system) water until                conductivity was stable, an eluent was passed along                the sorbent to provide an acid-containing eluate.                The eluent comprised a solvent having 90 wt.%                ethanol and 10 wt.% water, and the eluent comprised                1M of potassium hydroxide. A total of 18.5 mg of                lactate per ml of resin bed were desorbed, while                (essentially) all adsorbed caproate was desorbed.                In the precipitation stage, desorption samples were                sparged with carbon dioxide, and the organic acids                were quantified again the precipitation stage.                There was (essentially) no reduction of lactate and                caproate concentration in each solution.

PT P
PN WO2021087605-A1; CA3159579-A1; IN202227031585-A; CN114929643-A; KR2022123635-A; EP4054990-A1
TI Mechanochemically carboxylated fly ash used as            partial replacement for asphalt or cement in concrete,            and for increasing compressive strength of concrete,            has specific Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area and            carbon dioxide content.
AU SINHA A
   SINHAPURB
AE CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   SINHA A (SINH-Individual)
   CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202151127F
AB    NOVELTY - Mechanochemically carboxylated fly ash has a                Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of less                than 50 m2/g and a carbon dioxide content of more                than 1 wt.%, where the carbon dioxide content is                determined as the mass loss above 120 degrees C                measured by thermogravimetric analysis/mass                spectrometry (TGA-MS) employing a temperature                trajectory where the temperature is increased from                room temperature to 800 degrees C at a rate of 10                degrees C/minute and decreased to room temperature                at a rate of 15 degrees C/minute.
   USE - The fly ash is useful in a composition for                preparing a concrete, as filler, preferably as a                filler in a material chosen from asphalt, cement                and/or polymers, as partial replacement for asphalt                or cement in concrete, and to increase the                compressive strength of concrete, improve the                durability of concrete, or improve the durability                of concrete by reducing chloride permeability                and/or porosity (all claimed) for building                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The fly ash provides affordable filler                technology which can combine both the carbon                dioxide emission reduction achieved by reduced                cement production and the carbon dioxide emission                reduction achieved by carbon capture technology and                which does not detrimentally affect the properties                of concrete. The fly ash provides improved fillers                for cement or asphalt binder which are cheap to                produce, are produced using carbon dioxide storage                technology, and improve the properties of the                resulting concrete, such as the compressive                strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing the                mechanochemically carboxylated fly ash, comprising                (a) providing a solid feedstock comprising or                consisting of fly ash, wherein the solid feedstock                is a particulate material which has a BET surface                area of more than 0.01 m2/g and a D50 within the                range of 0.1-5000 pm, (b) providing an oxidizing                gas comprising carbon dioxide, (c) introducing the                solid feedstock and the oxidizing gas into a                mechanical agitation unit, and (d) subjecting the                material of the solid feedstock to a mechanical                agitation operation in the presence of the                oxidizing gas in the mechanical agitation unit at                an oxidizing gas pressure of more than 1 atmosphere                to obtain the mechanochemically carboxylated fly                ash;
   (2) a composition comprising mechanochemically                carboxylated fly ash and further material chosen                from asphalt, cement and/or, polymer, preferably                Portland cement; and
   (3) a concrete obtainable by a method for                preparing concrete, comprising (i) providing a                composition, where the further material is asphalt                or concrete, (ii) providing a construction                aggregate, and (iii) contacting, preferably mixing                the composition with the construction                aggregate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fly ash comprises                  30-50 wt.% silicon dioxide content, 10-20 wt.%                  aluminum oxide content, and/or 10-40 wt.% ferric                  oxide content. The solid feedstock has a combined                  silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide and ferric oxide                  content of more than 60 wt.%, preferably more                  than 75 wt.%. The oxidizing gas provided in step                  (b) comprises more than 90 mol.% carbon dioxide,                  preferably more than 95 mol.% carbon dioxide. The                  weight ratio of the mechanochemically                  carboxylated fly ash and the further material is                  within the range of 1:9-2:1, preferably 1:6-5:6.                  Preferred Properties: The fly ash has                  characteristics of a D10 within the range of                  0.005-5 pm, preferably 0.1 -0.5 pm, a D50 within                  the range of 0.5-50 pm, preferably 1-10 pm, a D90                  within the range of 5-200 pm, preferably 30-50                  pm, and/or a D(4:3) within the range of 1-100 pm,                  preferably 10-25 pm. Preferred Conditions: The                  solid feedstock comprises or consists of fly ash                  which meets the ASTM C618 requirements, most                  preferably fly ash which meets the class F ASTM                  C618 requirements. The step (d) is performed at a                  pressure of more than 3 atmospheres, preferably                  more than 6 atmospheres at less than 100 degrees                  C, preferably 30 degrees C for at least 1 hour,                  preferably at least 8 hours. The mechanochemical                  agitation operation of step (d) comprises mixing,                  stirring (low-speed stirring or high-speed                  stirring), shearing (high-torque shearing),                  shaking, blending, a fluidized bed or                  ultrasonication, preferably mixing, stirring                  (low-speed stirring or high-speed stirring),                  shearing (high-torque shearing) or                  ultrasonication. The step (d) is performed in the                  presence of a catalyst such as transition metal                  oxide catalyst, preferably transition metal                  dioxide catalyst chosen from iron oxides, cobalt                  oxides, ruthenium oxides and/or titanium                  oxides.

PN WO2021021827-A1; US2021033499-A1; TW202109006-A; US11441974-B2
TI Detecting surface particles of chamber components            based on use of carbon dioxide, specifically, stream of            solid carbon dioxide particles and/or liquid carbon            dioxide droplets using apparatus, involves directing            stream.
AU WANG C
   ZUO Z
   KE C
   SUH S
AE APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
GA 202112982V
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting surface particles of chamber                components based on use of carbon dioxide (CO2),                specifically, stream of solid CO2 particles and/or                liquid CO2 droplets involves directing, from a                distribution unit, a stream comprising a solid CO2                particles or CO2 droplets towards an article. The                article comprises multiple surface particles. The                stream comprises solid CO2 particles or CO2                droplets causes a portion of multiple surface                particles on the article to dislodge from a surface                of the article. A surface of a substrate is                collected having a pre-determined initial state                comprising initial surface particles on the surface                of the substrate, some of the portion of multiple                surface particles dislodged from the surface of the                article.
   USE - Method for detecting surface particles of                chamber components based on use of carbon dioxide,                specifically, stream of solid carbon dioxide                particles and/or liquid carbon dioxide droplets                using apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provide accurate surface particle                metrology, understanding and control of surface                particles in the semiconductor industry, provide a                composition of the surface particles, and are                likely to introduce cross contamination from the                liquid used to analyze the surface particles,                making it difficult to obtain accurate data                regarding the surface particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The surface of the substrate is analyzed after                performing the collecting, and size, morphology,                chemical composition, particle number                concentration, or particle size distribution of the                portion of multiple surface particles that are                dislodged from the surface of the article and                collected on the surface of the substrate are                determined. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus (400) comprising a distribution unit                configured to generate a CO2 stream (410)                comprising solid CO2 particles or CO2 droplets;                controller configured to direct the stream towards                an article (402) comprising multiple surface                particles, where the stream causes a portion of                multiple surface particles on the article to                dislodge from a surface of the article; and                substrate having a pre-determined initial state                comprising surface particles on a surface of the                substrate, where a surface of the substrate is to                collect the portion of multiple surface particles                dislodged from the surface of the article, and                where the surface of the substrate is to be                analyzed to determine size, morphology, chemical                composition, particle number concentration, or                particle size distribution of the portion of                multiple surface particles that were dislodged from                the surface of the article and collected on the                surface of the substrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a                particle detection chamber
   Apparatus (400)
   Article (402)
   Spray Nozzle (408)
   CO2 stream (410)
   Aerosol Sampling Component (420)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method further                  involves determining information about size,                  morphology, chemical composition, particle number                  concentration, or particle size distribution of a                  remainder of multiple surface particles on the                  article or determining size, morphology, chemical                  composition, particle number concentration, or                  particle size distribution of all detectable                  surface particles on the substrate; and                  subtracting out a size, morphology, chemical                  composition, particle number concentration, or                  particle size distribution of pre-determined                  particles included in the pre-determined initial                  state, where a remainder of the size, the                  morphology, chemical composition, particle number                  concentration, or particle size distribution of                  all detectable surface particles on the substrate                  represents data for the portion of multiple                  surface particles that were dislodged from the                  surface of the article and collected on the                  surface of the substrate. A laminar flow is                  generated around the article to transport the                  portion of multiple surface particles dislodged                  from the surface of the article to the substrate,                  and then reducing, by one or more particle charge                  neutralizers, a charge of the portion of multiple                  surface particles dislodged from the surface of                  the article such to facilitate transport to the                  substrate. The step of directing the stream                  comprising solid CO2 particles or CO2 droplets                  towards the article involves forming a first                  layer of solid CO2 on the surface of the article,                  where the first layer of solid CO2 causes the                  portion of multiple surface particles to be                  dislodged from the surface of the article, and                  where a portion of the dislodged surface                  particles are to collect on the surface of the                  article; increasing a temperature of the article                  to facilitate sublimation of the first layer of                  solid CO2; and forming a second layer of solid                  CO2 on the surface of the article, where the                  second layer of solid CO2 causes the portion of                  the dislodged surface particles to be dislodged                  from the surface of the article. The step of                  directing the stream comprising solid CO2                  particles or CO2 droplets towards the article                  flowing liquid CO2 into the distribution unit,                  where the liquid CO2 is converted into solid CO2                  particles or CO2 droplets upon exiting the                  distribution unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107991842-A; CN107991842-B
TI Use of polycarbonate formed by carrying out alternating copolymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide(s), used in semiconductor processing application.
AU WU G
   LU X
   ZHANG Y
   WANG K
   ZHANG R
   DAI Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201837601X
AB    NOVELTY - Use of polycarbonate as electron beam photoresist material, is claimed. The polycarbonate is formed by carrying out alternating copolymerization of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide(s).
   USE - Use of polycarbonate used in semiconductor processing application.
   ADVANTAGE - The polycarbonate has high sensitivity, resolution and contrast.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkylene oxide is epoxy cyclohexane, propylene oxide, limonene oxide, 2-vinylcyclohexene and/or styrene oxide. The solvent of electron beam resist solution is propylene glycol monomethyl ether acetate or methyl ethyl ketone. When the electron beam photoresist is positive photoresist, the developer is mixture of good solvent and poor solvent. The good solvent is methyl isobutyl ketone, methyl ethyl ketone, propylene glycol monomethyl ether acetate, cyclopentanone, xylene and/or amyl acetate. The poor solvent is isopropanol. When electron beam photoresist is negative photoresist, then the developer is methyl isobutyl ketone, methyl ethyl ketone, propylene glycol monomethyl ether acetate, cyclopentanone, xylene, and/or amyl acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polycarbonate has number average molecular weight of 10-100 kDa, carbonate unit content of 95-100%, and molecular weight distribution of 1-1.2. Preferred Component: Polycarbonate is chosen from 8 compounds e.g. compounds of formulae (I)-(V). Preferred Process: Polycarbonate is made into 0.1-15 %mass electron beam resist solution. The electron beam photoresist solution is spin-coated on the surface of a substrate and film is baked. The resultant material is subjected to specific area exposure using high-energy electron beams and developed using a developer to form a pattern.

PN WO2019163968-A1; CN111712461-A; EP3757064-A1; US2021009428-A1; JP2020501071-X; EP3757064-A4
TI System used for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises            reduction device to reduce carbon dioxide and transport            path to transport carbon dioxide by recycling energy            and exhaust heat in transport route.
AU RASIKA D A
   SHINMEI K
   MIYAMA T
   DASANAYAKE A R
   DASANAYAKE ALUTHGE R
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2019747657
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction system comprises a                reduction device that reduces carbon dioxide and a                transport path that transports carbon dioxide to                the reduction device. The carbon dioxide is heated                by at least one of recycling energy and exhaust                heat in the transport route.
   USE - System used for reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The reduction system enables efficient                reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reduction                of carbon dioxide, which involves heating carbon                dioxide by at least one of recycling energy and                exhaust heat, and reducing heated carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The carbon dioxide                  reduction system further comprises a carbon                  dioxide separator through which the exhaust gas                  containing carbon dioxide in the transport path                  is separated, a combustion furnace, where the                  exhaust heat is heat generated and transport                  route is a circulation route for circulating the                  carbon dioxide so as to be heated by the heat                  generated in the combustion furnace, heat                  exchanger, a heat exchanger that cools the                  exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide generated                  in the combustion furnace and the circulation                  path sends carbon dioxide to the heat exchanger.                  The combustion chamber contains a gas discharge                  path, in which the combustion article is burned,                  and the heat exchanger attached to the gas                  discharge path. Carbon dioxide is reduced to                  carbon monoxide and water by a reverse shift                  reaction in the reduction device. The reduction                  device is a chemical-looping-type reaction                  device. Preferred Method: The reduction of carbon                  dioxide further involves separating carbon                  dioxide from exhaust gas containing carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The recycling energy                  is chosen from solar power generation, wind power                  generation, hydroelectric power generation, wave                  power generation, tidal power generation, biomass                  power generation, solar heat, geothermal power                  generation and underground heat.

PN CN113912098-A
TI Carbonizing nano calcium carbonate involves putting calcium hydroxide into the stirring tank A that pure water is housed,stirring,if the transparency of the liquid in the stirring tank A is lower,adding pure water to the mixing tank A extends to the outside of the air the top end of the air injecti.
AU WEI X
   WEI L
AE WEI L (WEIL-Individual)
GA 202215153H
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonizing nano calcium carbonate involves putting calcium hydroxide into the stirring tank A that pure water is housed, stirring for 10-15min, if the transparency of the liquid in the stirring tank A is lower, adding pure water to the mixing tank A, stirring again and test the transparency of the liquid in the mixing tank A, repeating the operation until the liquid in the stirring tank A is clear, and the clear solution in the stirring tank A is introduced into the stirring tank B. The alkyl glycosides, sodium polyacrylate and lauric acid are added to stirring tank B, and stirred for 20-30 min to obtain a modified calcium hydroxide solution. The carbonization operation uses the air pump A to pump the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank into the reaction tank A, so that the reaction tank A is filled with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for carbonizing nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to carbonize nano calcium carbonate, which has improved particle size qualification rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbonizing nano calcium carbonate involves putting calcium hydroxide into the stirring tank A that pure water is housed, stirring for 10-15min, if the transparency of the liquid in the stirring tank A is lower, adding pure water to the mixing tank A, stirring again and test the transparency of the liquid in the mixing tank A, repeating the operation until the liquid in the stirring tank A is clear, and the clear solution in the stirring tank A is introduced into the stirring tank B. The alkyl glycosides, sodium polyacrylate and lauric acid are added to stirring tank B, and stirred for 20-30 min to obtain a modified calcium hydroxide solution. The carbonization operation uses the air pump A to pump the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank into the reaction tank A, so that the reaction tank A is filled with carbon dioxide. The modified calcium hydroxide solution is introduced into the bubble machine, and the modified calcium hydroxide bubbles are sprayed into the reaction tank A through the bubble machine. The carbon dioxide in the reaction tank A contacts the bubble film wall of the modified calcium hydroxide bubbles, and after the carbonization reaction, modified ultrafine calcium carbonate and water are generated. The modified ultrafine calcium carbonate and water in the reaction tank A are intermittently introduced into the ultrafiltration machine through the slurry pump, and the ultrafiltration machine sieves and filters the modified ultrafine calcium carbonate to obtain nano-scale calcium carbonate and large calcium carbonate. The nanoscale calcium carbonate is put into the stirring tank C, add guar gum, vitamin D, lactic acid bacteria, cellulolytic bacteria and Ageratum extract to the stirring tank C, stirring for 15-20 min to obtain synthetic nano-calcium carbonate, and drying the synthetic nano-calcium carbonate by a vacuum freeze dryer. A vacuum bagging machine is used to bag dry synthetic nano-calcium carbonate to obtain a finished product of nano-calcium carbonate, and put the nano-calcium carbonate into a refrigerator for low temperature storage. The large particle calcium carbonate is dried and put into the reactor for high temperature calcination, and the large particle calcium carbonate is decomposed and reacted to generate calcium oxide and carbon dioxide. After the carbon dioxide generated in the reaction process is cooled, it is passed into the carbon dioxide storage tank, and the calcium oxide generated by the reaction is put into the reaction tank B. The pure water is added to obtain a calcium hydroxide solution, and the calcium hydroxide solution is introduced into the stirring tank A.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The raw material preparation and the carbonization process are all carried out in the reaction chamber, and air is pumped into the reaction tank C containing sodium hydroxide solution by air pump B, eliminate carbon dioxide in the air, pass the carbon dioxide-removed air into the reaction chamber. The pH value of the solution in the reaction tank C is detected by a pH meter, when the pH value in the reaction tank C is low, portion of the liquid in the reaction tank C is discharged, and then the sodium hydroxide solution is supplemented into the reaction tank C through the lye pump. The gas in the reaction chamber is processed by an air purifier to eliminate solid particulate matter in the air. The transparency of the liquid in the stirring tank A is detected by a turbidimeter after each stirring in the stirring tank A. The pore size of the ultrafiltration membrane of the ultrafiltration machine is 0.1 mum. The refrigerating temperature of the refrigerating chamber is 2-6degreesC. The calcination temperature of the reaction kettle is 890-910degreesC.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

TI Preparing 3 D gel carrier comprises adding cross-linking agent BIS, anti-cell adhesion component A and cell adhesion promoting component B into phosphate buffered saline solution, immersing 3D gel in phosphate buffered saline solution.

   DAI S
   ZHOU F
AE NANJING ENHANCER BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202131649T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3 D gel carrier comprises adding cross-linking agent BIS, the anti-cell adhesion component A and the cell adhesion promoting component B into phosphate buffered saline solution, adding cross-linking initiator under the condition of shaking or stirring, heating the water bath to 50-60 degrees C to completely dissolve to obtain the hydrogel solution, injecting hydrogel solution into transparent mold, controlling the temperature at 20-35 degrees C, performing light with wavelength of 405 nm, curing reaction occurs to obtain 3D gel, immersing 3D gel in phosphate buffered saline solution and sterilizing by ethylene oxide for use, where the anti-cell adhesion component is polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate, acrylamide or polyvinyl alcohol, where the cell adhesion promoting component is gelatin methacryloyl, acrylamido collagen and acrylamido fibrinogen.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 3 D gel carrier for inducing cells to self-organize into multicellular spheres.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: cells exist in a microenvironment with limited adhesion; and simulates physiological function of tumor cell tissue and its environment to the greatest extent, which is closer to the real biological environment and eventually self-organizes to form multicellular spheres.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) multi-cell spheroid culture of colorectal cancer cells LOVO comprising inoculating colorectal cancer cells LOVO at a density of 0.1-0.2x 106 on the surface of the 3D gel carrier, placing in the culture medium, culturing at 37 plus minus 2 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 4-5%, changing culture medium every two days, training for at least 7 days, completing the culture of multi-cell ball structure in the process of preparing the 3D gel carrier, where the mass ratios of polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate, gelatin methacryloyl and cross-linking agent BIS added in hydrogel solution are 10%, 10% and 0.5%; and
   (2) culturing gastric cancer cell SGC7901 multicellular spheres comprising seeding gastric cancer cell SGC7901 on surface of 3D gel carrier at density of 0.1-0.2x 106, placing in the culture medium and cultivate at a temperature of 37 plus minus 2 degrees C and 4-5% carbon dioxide concentration, changing culture medium every two days, culturing for at least 7 days, completing multicellular sphere structure culture, where in the process of preparing the 3D gel carrier, the mass ratios of polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate, gelatin methacryloyl and the cross-linking agent BIS added in the hydrogel solution are 10%, 15%, and 0.5%; and
   (3) culturing ovarian cancer cell SKOV3 multicellular spheres comprising seeding ovarian cancer cell SKOV3 on the surface of the 3D gel carrier at density of 0.1-0.2x 106, placing in a culture solution Cultivate in an environment with a temperature of 37 plus minus 2 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 4-5%, changing culture medium for every two days, culturing for at least 7 days, completing multicellular sphere structure culture, where in process of preparing 3D gel carrier and mass ratios of polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate, gelatin methacryloyl and the cross-linking agent BIS added in the hydrogel solution are 10%, 5% and 0.5% and
   (4) culturing breast cancer cells MCF-7 multicellular spheres comprising seeding breast cancer cells MCF-7 on the surface of the 3D gel carrier at density of 0.1-0.2x 106, placing in the culture medium, culturing at 37 plus minus 2 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 4-5%, changing culture medium every two days, completing multicellular sphere structure culture, where in the process of preparing the 3D gel carrier, the mass ratios of polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate, gelatin methacryloyl, and cross-linking agent BIS added in the hydrogel solution are 10%, 20% and 0.5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crosslinking initiator is LAP.

PN CN111647844-A
TI Performing composite nitriding of corrosion resistance main shaft involves performing thermal treatment, air cooling it, performing heat preservation, quenching it in water, tempering and performing composite nitriding treatment.
AU YIN Z
   YU X
   DONG Y
   LI Q
AE QIQIHAR YINGGE MACHINERY CO LTD (QIQI-Non-standard)
GA 202091648U
AB    NOVELTY - Performing composite nitriding of corrosion resistance main shaft involves performing thermal treatment at a temperature of 880 degrees C for 4 hours, taking out from furnace, and air cooling it, performing heat preservation at a temperature of 870 degrees C for 4 hours, quenching it in water at 18-23 degrees C, tempering it at 580-590 degrees C for 6-8 hours, and then performing composite nitriding treatment. The furnace pressure is kept at 10KPa, temperature is increased to 350 degrees C in 10 minutes, pre-oxidation function is started, furnace pressure is kept at 5KPa, air is blown for 35 minutes, and then pre-oxidation function is turned off. The furnace pressure is kept at 10KPa, heated continuously up to 480 degrees C in 5 minutes, furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, and nitrogen is blown for 4 minutes, and nitrogen concentration is 2m3/hour. The furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, heated continuously up to 570 degrees C in 10 minutes.
   USE - Method for performing composite nitriding of corrosion resistance main shaft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform composite nitriding of corrosion resistance main shaft, which forms composite layer by composite nitriding method, and composite layer has oxide film, bright white layer, diffusion layer and matrix, where oxide film on surface has a good corrosion resistance function, bright white layer has a compact structure, excellent anti-corrosion and wear-resistant function, reduces unqualified rate of 34CrNi3Mo top drive shaft to less than 0.3%, extends service life 2-5 times to number of working hours, which saves million of dollars in economic losses caused by early failure of the shaft material each year.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing composite nitriding of corrosion resistance main shaft involves performing thermal treatment at a temperature of 880 degrees C for 4 hours, taking out from furnace, and air cooling it, performing heat preservation at a temperature of 870 degrees C for 4 hours, quenching it in water at 18-23 degrees C, tempering it at 580-590 degrees C for 6-8 hours, and then performing composite nitriding treatment. The furnace pressure is kept at 10KPa, temperature is increased to 350 degrees C in 10 minutes, pre-oxidation function is started, furnace pressure is kept at 5KPa, air is blown for 35 minutes, and then pre-oxidation function is turned off. The furnace pressure is kept at 10KPa, heated continuously up to 480 degrees C in 5 minutes, furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, and nitrogen is blown for 4 minutes, and nitrogen concentration is 2m3/hour. The furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, heated continuously up to 570 degrees C in 10 minutes, ammonia and carbon dioxide are blown at the same time as temperature rises. The ammonia concentration is 1m3/hour and carbon dioxide concentration is 20m3/hour. The furnace pressure is maintained at 15KPa at a temperature of 570 degrees C, and then ammonia and carbon dioxide are blown for 270 minutes. The ammonia concentration is 2m3/hour and carbon dioxide concentration is 10m3/hour. The furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, nitrogen is blown at same time as the temperature is lowered, where temperature is lowered to 520 degrees C in 1 minute, and nitrogen concentration is 4m3/hour at same time when temperature is lowered, and post-oxidation function is started at 520 degrees C, where furnace pressure is kept at 15KPa, and post-oxidation function is turned off after blowing air for 120 minutes. The furnace pressure is kept at 8KPa, nitrogen is blown for 30 minutes at the same time as the temperature is lowered. The nitrogen concentration to be blown is 4m3/hour and temperature is lowered continuously to 200 degrees C, and then composite nitriding process is completed and discharged.

TI Preparation of impact-resistant flame-retardant material for automobile instrument panel, involves mixing material containing melamine resin to form mixture, reacting with alloy solution to form mixed solution, casting, and polishing.
AU MAO Y
AE LIUZHOU GONGLIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 202059356G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of impact-resistant flame-retardant material involves feeding material containing 180-200 pts. wt. melamine resin, 80-100 pts. wt. fluororubber, and 3-7 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide into roasting machine in batches, controlling the roasting time for 15-25 minutes, crushing, mixing material containing glass fiber, and nano-carbon dioxide into the natural gas furnace to melt, melting at 750-770 degrees C to obtain alloy solution, adding all components except glass fiber, barium sulfate whisker, vanadium, strontium, nano zinc oxide powder and nano carbon dioxide to the reaction kettle for reaction, stirring, mixing at 185-195 degrees C for 1-2 hour at rate of 100-200 rpm to obtain mixture (A), adding the mixture (A) to the alloy solution, reacting to obtain mixed solution (B), pouring the mixed solution (B) into prefabricated mold, casting to obtain preliminary impact-resistant flame-retardant material, cooling, and polishing the surface.
   USE - Preparation of impact-resistant flame-retardant material for automobile instrument panel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and produces flame-retardant material having high overall performance, high flame extinguishing speed, reduced combustion distance, and excellent acid and alkali resistance, corrosion resistance, oxidation resistance, oil resistance and waterproof performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of impact-resistant flame-retardant material involves using 180-200 pts. wt., preferably 190 pts. wt., more preferably 200 pts. wt. melamine resin, 80-100 pts. wt., preferably 90 pts. wt., more preferably 100 pts. wt. fluororubber, 60-80 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt., more preferably 80 pts. wt. polyethylene, 50-60 pts. wt., preferably 55 pts. wt., more preferably 60 pts. wt. polypropylene, 9-13 pts. wt., preferably 11 pts. wt., more preferably 13 pts. wt. nano silica , 8-12 pts. wt., preferably 11 pts. wt., more preferably 12 pts. wt. glass fiber, 7-13 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt., more preferably 13 pts. wt. potassium aluminum sulfate, 4-8 pts. wt., preferably 6 pts. wt., more preferably 8 pts. wt. barium sulfate whiskers, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 3 pts. wt., more preferably 5 pts. wt. vanadium, 11-15 pts. wt., preferably 13 pts. wt., more preferably 15 pts. wt. high-strength carbon fiber, 4-10 pts. wt., preferably 7 pts. wt., more preferably 10 pts. wt. nano zinc oxide powder, 3-7 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt., more preferably 7 pts. wt. strontium, 6-10 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt., more preferably 10 pts. wt. nano silicon carbide, 12-18 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt., more preferably 18 pts. wt. high pigment carbon black, 6-12 pts. wt., preferably 9 pts. wt., more preferably 12 pts. wt. talc powder, and 3-7 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt., more preferably 7 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, feeding into roasting machine in batches, controlling the roasting time for 15-25 minutes, preferably 20 minutes, more preferably 25 minutes based on the humidity of different components, roasting until completely dried, crushing, mixing glass fiber, barium sulfate whisker, vanadium, strontium, nano-zinc oxide powder and nano-carbon dioxide into the natural gas furnace to melt, melting at 750-770 degrees C, preferably 760 degrees C, more preferably 770 degrees C to obtain alloy solution, adding all components except glass fiber, barium sulfate whisker, vanadium, strontium, nano zinc oxide powder and nano carbon dioxide to the reaction kettle for reaction, stirring, mixing at 185-195 degrees C, preferably 190 degrees C, more preferably 195 degrees C for 1-2 hour, preferably 1.5 hours, more preferably 2 hours at rate of 100-200 rpm, preferably 150 rpm, more preferably 200 rpm to obtain mixture (A), adding the mixture (A) to the alloy solution, increasing the temperature to 450-470 degrees C, preferably 460 degrees C, more preferably 470 degrees C to fully react, stirring, maintaining the molten state at constant temperature to obtain mixed solution (B), pouring the mixed solution (B) into prefabricated mold, casting to obtain preliminary impact-resistant flame-retardant material for automotive instrument panels, cooling, and polishing the surface.

PN CN110819444-A
TI Extracting tea oil and tea saponin from Camellia seed, comprises grinding Camellia seed, adding alcohol aqueous solution supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, dealcoholizing, evaporating and recovering.
AU LIU Z
AE LIU Z (LIUZ-Individual)
GA 202018120R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting tea oil and tea saponin from Camellia seed, comprises (i) grinding Camellia seed and hydrolyzing in water (ii) adding an alcohol aqueous solution, immersing under reflux condition, adjusting PH, (iii) filtering and centrifuging to obtain A liquid phase and A seed residue, extracting liquid phase by supercritical carbon dioxide, separating by a separator to obtain crude tea oil and A alcohol aqueous solution, vacuum freeze-drying (iv) adding an alcohol solution to A seed residue, (v) adding 50-60% alcohol solution into wet residue of B extracting, dealcoholizing crude tea oil, extracting C alcohol aqueous solution and the C wet residue were separated by filtration dehydrating to obtain pure natural original camellia seed oil, and recovering, configuring alcohol aqueous solution, water was used as water added to ground Camelliaseed, the alcohol aqueous solution is an ethanol solution, isopropanol, and a polyethanol solution.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea oil and tea saponin from Camellia seed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method oil extraction rate is more than 96%, has 95% recovery rate of tea saponin, is safe and environmentally friendly, and extremely suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tea oil and tea saponin from Camellia seed, comprises (i) grinding Camellia seed under vacuum, where fineness of the powder is 10-35 mesh, adding water, the mass of water is 1.5-4 times (mass of grounded Camellia seeds, and hydrolyzing in water at 60-80 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, (ii) adding an alcohol aqueous solution(water volume), where , the volume concentration of alcohol is 8-20% and temperature is 50-80 degrees C, immersing under reflux condition for 1-3 hours, adjusting PH to 5-6.5, (iii) filtering and centrifuging to obtain A liquid phase and A seed residue, extracting liquid phase by supercritical carbon dioxide, separating by a separator to obtain crude tea oil and A alcohol aqueous solution, crude tea oil is obtained by a existing method, concentrate A alcohol aqueous solution, and vacuum freeze-drying, cold trap temperature less than or equal to -80 degrees C to obtain tea saponin, (iv) adding an alcohol solution to A seed residue, The volume percentage concentration of alcohol is 30-40%, the volume of alcohol solution is 1-4 times (residue of A seed), adjusting pH to 5-6.5. extracting at 60-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours, filtering to obtain B alcohol aqueous solution and B wet residue, (v) adding 50-60% alcohol solution into wet residue of B, where, the amount of alcohol solution is 1-4 times volume of B wet slag, extracting at 60-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours eC alcohol aqueous solution and the C wet residue were separated by filtration, returning alcohol solution C to A wet residue used as alcohol solution for extracting A seed residue and repeating, (vi) B alcohol aqueous solution, performing alcohol precipitation at 60-80 degrees C for 2-5 hours, centrifugal separation to obtain E wet slag and liquid B phase, returning E wet slag to A wet slag and repeat, subjecting B liquid phase to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction as A liquid phase to obtain secondary extraction crude tea oil, alcohol aqueous solution E, dealcoholizing crude tea oil, dehydrating to obtain pure natural original camellia seed oil with a total oil yield of more than 96%, and (vii) alcohol solution E is at 18-14 degrees C, cooling for 2-36 hours under conditions to separate solid phase and B liquid phase, solid phase at 70-90 degrees C, Crude tea saponin is obtained dealcoholization under vacuum conditions of 0.06-0.109 MPa, B liquid phase at 60-110 degrees C, evaporating to remove alcohol and recovering under vacuum condition of 0.07-0.109 MPa, configuring alcohol aqueous solution, water was used as water added to ground Camelliaseed, the alcohol aqueous solution is an ethanol solution, isopropanol, and a polyethanol solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii) and (iii), the base to adjust pH is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, and/or sodium bicarbonate. In the step (i) the amount of water is 1.2-3 times ( mass of Camelliaseed kernels. In the step (i) the amount of water is 2-2.5 times (mass of Camellia seed kernel). Preferred Method: In the step (ii) and (iv), the acid to adjust the pH is citric acid. The alcohol is recovered by evaporation and concentration at 80-110 degrees C and 0.08-0.1 MPa.In the step (iii) and (vi) the centrifugation the rotation speed of centrifuge is 5000-8000 revolutions/minute , and the centrifugation time of the centrifuge is 14-30 minutes. In the step (vii) The E alcohol aqueous solution S7 is cooled at -18-0 degrees C for 2-24 hours, and Camellia saponin is crystallized out.

TI System useful for enriching acid gas for feeding sulfur recovery unit, comprises e.g. acid gas stream, contactor configured to separate acid gas stream, regenerator in fluid communication with contactor and recycle stream.
AU AL-QAHTANI N A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020140434
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: e.g. acid gas stream (106) connector configured to receive an acid gas stream; contactor configured to separate the acid gas stream received from the acid gas stream connector into a carbon dioxide rich stream and a purified acid gas stream; regenerator in fluid communication with the contactor such that the regenerator is configured to separate the purified acid gas stream to create a hydrogen sulfide rich stream (142) and a hydrogen sulfide lean stream (144); and acid gas recycle valve coupled to the recycle stream conduit that opens and closes a fluid pathway from the regenerator to the contactor based on an hydrogen sulfide content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream to direct the hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting the regenerator to combine with the acid gas stream at the acid gas stream connector prior to the combination of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and the acid gas stream entering the contactor.
   USE - The system is useful for enriching acid gas for feeding a sulfur recovery unit (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises: acid gas stream connector configured to receive an acid gas stream; a contactor configured to separate the acid gas stream received from the acid gas stream connector into a carbon dioxide rich stream and a purified acid gas stream, where the acid gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide (H2S), carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrocarbons; a regenerator in fluid communication with the contactor such that the regenerator is configured to separate the purified acid gas stream to create a hydrogen sulfide rich stream and a hydrogen sulfide lean stream, where the hydrogen sulfide rich stream having a concentration of hydrogen sulfide; a recycle stream conduit fluidly coupled between the regenerator to the contactor and configured to supply at least a portion of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream from the regenerator to the contactor; and an acid gas recycle valve coupled to the recycle stream conduit that opens and closes a fluid pathway from the regenerator to the contactor based on an hydrogen sulfide content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream to direct the hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting the regenerator to combine with the acid gas stream at the acid gas stream connector prior to the combination of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and the acid gas stream entering the contactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system of enriching acid gas for feeding a sulfur recovery unit, comprising the acid gas stream connector configured to receive an acid gas stream, the contactor connected to the acid gas stream connector by a conduit, the contactor configured to separate the acid gas stream from the acid gas stream connector into a carbon dioxide rich stream and a purified acid gas stream, the regenerator in fluid communication with the contactor, a recycle valve positioned in another conduit between the regenerator and the acid gas stream connector to direct the hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting the regenerator to combine with the acid gas stream at the acid gas stream connector prior to the combination of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and the acid gas stream entering the contactor and a controller configured to operate recycle valve based on an H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream, where the recycle valve, when in an open state, is configured to separate at least a portion of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting from the regenerator and to feed the portion of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream to the contactor, and the recycle valve, when in an closed state, is configured to purge an entire hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting from the regenerator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view acid gas enrichment system that recycles enriched acid gas to optimize the hydrogen sulfide content in the enriched acid gas.
   System (100)
   Acid gas stream (106)
   Contactor (120)
   Hydrogen sulfide rich stream (142)
   Hydrogen sulfide lean stream (144)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The controller is configured to open the recycle valve when the H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream is less than or equal to 35 wt.%. The controller is configured to close the recycle valve when the H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream is greater than or equal to 75 wt.%. The controller is configured to close the recycle valve when an H2S content in the carbon dioxide rich stream is greater than or equal to 500 ppm. The controller is configured to close the recycle valve when a total mass flow of the recycled enriched gas from the regenerator is 20 wt.% or greater than the acid gas stream to contactor. The acid gas recycle valve is configured to open when the H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream is less than 35 wt.%. The acid gas recycle valve is configured to open when the H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream is greater than or equal to 75 wt.%. The acid gas recycle valve is configured to open when an H2S content in the carbon dioxide rich stream greater than or equal to 500 ppm. The acid gas recycle valve is configured to close when at least one of the H2S content in the hydrogen sulfide rich stream is greater than or equal to 75 wt.%, a threshold mass flow of the recycled enriched gas from the regenerator is 20 wt.% or greater than a mass flow of the acid gas stream to the contactor or an H2S content in the carbon dioxide rich stream is at least 500 ppm. The acid gas recycle valve is configured to open or close based on an operating pressure of the contactor. The controller is configured to determine a residual factor based on a flow rate of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and a flow rate of the carbon dioxide rich stream. The residual factor comprises a comparative measurement that compares flow rates of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and the carbon dioxide rich stream in a predicted model to measured flow rates of the hydrogen sulfide rich stream and the carbon dioxide rich stream.

   USE - Absorber is used in apparatus for separation and recovery of carbon dioxide (all claimed) from waste gas discharged from thermal power station, iron mill and cement plant.

PN WO2020044286-A1; EP3844135-A1; EP3844135-B1
TI Making methanol involves introducing syngas            reactor feed stream from feed gas source having            hydrogen gas and carbon oxides, separating methanol and            water, providing water gas shift unit and introducing            part of water gas shift product stream.
AU PANT A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   ENI SPA (ENIE-C)
GA 202019372P
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol comprises: introducing                syngas reactor feed stream from feed gas source                having hydrogen gas and carbon oxides into methanol                synthesis reactor to form reactor product stream                having methanol, water and unreacted carbon oxides                and hydrogen gas; separating methanol and water                from the reactor product stream leaving unreacted                gas stream; providing water gas shift (WGS) unit                for converting carbon monoxide and water to WGS                product stream of carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas                and performing operation A1 and optionally                operation B1, where A1 is introducing at least                portion of fresh syngas reactor feed from feed gas                source into WGS unit, and B1 is introducing at                least portion of unreacted gas stream and steam                into WGS unit for converting unreacted carbon                monoxide and water to WGS product stream of carbon                dioxide and hydrogen gas; and introducing at least                portion of WGS product stream from operation A1 and                optionally operation B1 into hydrogen recovery                unit.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the stoichiometric number                of the feed to the methanol reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing methanol comprises: introducing a                syngas reactor feed stream from a feed gas source                comprising hydrogen gas and carbon oxides of carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide into a methanol                synthesis reactor to form a reactor product stream                comprising methanol, water, and unreacted carbon                oxides of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and                hydrogen gas; separating methanol and water from                the reactor product stream leaving an unreacted gas                stream; providing at least one water gas shift                (WGS) unit for converting carbon monoxide and water                to a WGS product stream of carbon dioxide and                hydrogen gas and performing operation A1 and                optionally operation B1, where A1 is introducing at                least a portion of fresh syngas reactor feed from                the feed gas source into the at least one WGS unit                to provide a WGS product stream of carbon dioxide                and hydrogen gas, and B1 is introducing at least a                portion of the unreacted gas stream and steam into                the at least one WGS unit for converting unreacted                carbon monoxide and water to a WGS product stream                of carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas; and introducing                at least a portion of the WGS product stream from                operation A1 and optionally operation B1 into a                hydrogen recovery unit for producing a                hydrogen-rich stream, at least a portion of the                hydrogen-rich stream being recycled and introduced                into the methanol synthesis reactor as part of the                reactor feed stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for a system for the synthesis of                methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed gas source is                  the product of catalytic partial oxidation of                  natural gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN208206922-U; CN108982396-A
TI Carbon-di-oxide infrared gas sensor, has infrared detector for converting optical signal into electric signal by signal amplifying circuit, where analog to digital conversion circuit for obtaining detection signal.
AU GU F
   HUANG Y
   SUN Y
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NANJING INFORMATION SCI & TECHNOLOG (UNAI-C)
GA 2018A11815
AB    (CN208206922-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an infrared CO2 gas sensor comprises connecting the hardware circuit, an infrared light source, a flat cone optical gas chamber and filter with double-channel infrared detector. In addition, the calibration system comprises orderly connected of the gas bottle, a pressure reducing valve, a constant temperature box, a guide tube, a humidifier, a humidity sensor and an infrared CO2 gas sensor; the flat cone optical chamber reduces the cavity length, the sensor cavity; and calibration system uses full-enclosed device, which effectively solves the problem that the temperature and humidity and CO2 concentration caused by interference in the environment, the calibration result more accurate, using the constant-temperature and constant-humidity calibration method solves the humidity problem of error correction and improves the measuring precision and stability; adopts genetic algorithm improvement based on wavelet neural network (GA-WNN) fusion algorithm for the CO2 gas sensor to humidity error compensation and improves the measuring precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide infrared gas sensor calibrating system
   (2) a carbon dioxide infrared gas sensor calibrating system temperature and humidity compensating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a carbon dioxide infrared gas sensor calibrating system temperature and humidity compensating method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
EA    NOVELTY - The sensor has a light source driving circuit for driving an infrared light source to radiate an infrared light to an optical air chamber, a microprocessor, a signal amplifying circuit, an A/D converting circuit and a communicating circuit. The light source driving circuit passes the infrared light source by an infrared detector under controlling process of the microprocessor through the optical air chamber. The infrared detector converts an optical signal into an electric signal by the signal amplifying circuit and the A/D converting circuit to obtain a detecting signal. The A/D converting circuit transmits the detecting signal to the microprocessor by the communicating circuit.
   USE - Carbon dioxide infrared gas sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor has high stability, and realizes genetic algorithm adopting process based on wavelet neural network fusion algorithm for carbon dioxide gas sensor to reduce humidity error compensation, improves measuring precision and calibrating performance and reduces carbon dioxide concentration effect caused by interference in an environment and humidity problem of error correcting process.

PN CN108727683-A
TI Preparing polypropylene composition comprises e.g.            taking the raw materials and mixing in high-speed            mixer, adding component mixture from the first heating            zone of twin-screw extruder and adding supercritical            carbon dioxide fluid.
AU ZUO S
   YUAN X
   QIU D
   WANG J
   XU Z
   FU Y
AE SINOPEC YANGZI PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 201887241P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polypropylene composition comprises                e.g. (i) taking the raw materials and mixing in                high-speed mixer, where the polypropylene                composition com polypropylene, primary antioxidant,                auxiliary antioxidant, halogen scavenger, odor                removing agent, odor chelating agent and talc, and                (ii) adding component mixture of the step (i)                formulation from the first heating zone of                twin-screw extruder and adding supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid from the third heating zone or the                second heating zone to the twin-screw extruder, and                adding the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid from                the third heating zone or the second heating zone                to the twin-screw extruder, then vacuuming off the                die in the extruder die, water cooling the extruded                strip, granulating and drying to obtain final                product, where the mass of the supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid is 2-30% of the total mass of                extrudate, the main screw speed of the extruder is                50-500 revolutions/minute , the extrusion                temperature is 50-240 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                polypropylene composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process and low                production cost; and the product: inhibits thermal                and oxygen degradation of polypropylene during                extrusion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polypropylene composition comprises                (i) taking the raw materials and mixing in a                high-speed mixer, where the polypropylene                composition comprises 100 pts. wt. polypropylene,                0.05-0.15 pts. wt. primary antioxidant, 0.1-0.3                pts. wt. auxiliary antioxidant, 0.05-0.2 pts. wt.                halogen scavenger, 1-5 pts. wt. odor removing                agent, 0.1-1 pts. wt. odor chelating agent and                10-40 pts. wt. talc, the polypropylene is an                isotactic homopolymerized having a melt flow rate                of 0.1-120 g/10 minute or copolymerized                polypropylene pellet and/or powder; the primary                antioxidant is a hindered phenol compound, and (ii)                adding the component mixture of the step (i)                formulation from the first heating zone of the                twin-screw extruder, and adding supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid from the third heating zone or                the second heating zone to the twin-screw extruder,                and adding the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                from the third heating zone or the second heating                zone to the twin-screw extruder, then vacuuming off                the die in the extruder die, water cooling the                extruded strip, granulating and drying to obtain                final product, where the mass of the supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid is 2-30% of the total mass of                extrudate, the main screw speed of the extruder is                50-500 revolutions/minute , the extrusion                temperature is 50-240 degrees C.

PN CN108412613-A
TI Biogas-supercritical carbon dioxide recompression cycle power generation system has biogas compression and delivery device whose inlet is in communication with outlet of filter device and outlet is in communication with inlet of combustor.
AU CHEN H
   XUE Z
   WANG W
   WU T
   FU C
   ZHAO J
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 201866488K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas turbine power generation system, a supercritical carbon dioxide recompression Brayton power cycle power generation system and a biogas production and purification system. An air compressor unit (1) with compressed air outlet is connected to a compressed air inlet of a combustor (2). A burner with outlet is connected to the inlet of a gas turbine (3). An outlet of the gas turbine is connected to the inlet of a heat exchanger (5). The outlet of heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of a supercritical carbon dioxide turbine (11). The outlet of a desulfurization unit (16) is connected to an inlet of a dryer unit (17). The outlet of the dryer unit is in communication with the inlet of a filter device (18). The outlet of the filter device is in communication with the inlet of a biogas compression and delivery device (19). The outlet of the biogas compression and delivery device is in communication with the fuel inlet of a combustor.
   USE - Biogas-supercritical carbon dioxide recompression cycle power generation system used in industrial, agricultural or urban fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The circulation efficiency is reduced, and the supercritical carbon dioxide in the system is reduced. The overall efficiency of the power generation system is improved. The stable power supply is provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The outlet of a pre-cooler (8) is in communication with the inlet of a main compressor (9). The outlet of main compressor is in communication with low temperature side fluid inlet of a low temperature regenerator (7). The low temperature side fluid outlet of low temperature regenerator is combined with outlet of a re-compressor (10) and is connected to the low temperature side fluid inlet of a high temperature regenerator (6). The low temperature side fluid outlet of high temperature regenerator is in communication with inlet of heat exchanger. The outlet of a mixing tank (13) is in communication with the inlet of a fermentation unit (15). The outlet of the fermentation unit is in communication with the inlet of a desulfurization unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the biogas-supercritical carbon dioxide recompression cycle power generation system.
   Air compressor unit (1)
   Combustor (2)
   Gas turbine (3)
   Heat exchanger (5)
   High temperature regenerator (6)
   Low temperature regenerator (7)
   Pre-cooler (8)
   Main compressor (9)
   Re-compressor (10)

GA 201833778S
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises mixing compression system, desulfurization catalytic combustion system, drying and filtering system and a purification system, where hybrid compression system comprising vaporizer, preheater, raw material gas mixing tank, the compressor and the air temperature vaporizer, pass inlet of shell side outlet of the vaporizer is connected with the heater through the pipeline.
   USE - Device used for preparing high purity food grade liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple structure, low production cost, and has high recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises mixing compression system, desulfurization catalytic combustion system, drying and filtering system and a purification system, where hybrid compression system comprising vaporizer, preheater, raw material gas mixing tank, the compressor and the air temperature vaporizer, pass inlet of shell side outlet of the vaporizer is connected with the heater through the pipeline, the tube pass outlet of the preheater tube pass outlet of the air temperature vaporizer are connected with the raw material gas mixing tank. The raw material gas mixing tank is connected with the inlet of the compressor through the pipeline, desulfurizing catalytic combustion system comprises a heat exchanger, an electric heater, a desulfurizing tower and a catalytic reactor. The outlet pipeline of the shell side inlet of the heat exchanger and the compressor is connected with the inlet of the shell side outlet of the heat exchanger is connected with the electric heater through the pipeline is connected with the inlet of desulfurizing tower is connected with the inlet of the electric heater through pipeline outlet of desulphurizing tower with the catalytic reactor through pipeline, tube pass outlet of the catalytic reactor is connected with the heat exchanger through the pipeline. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high-purity food grade liquid carbon dioxide, which involves:
   (A) decompressing liquid industrial-grade carbon dioxide, entering sheller vaporization of the vaporizer, pre-heating vaporized industrial-grade carbon dioxide by the preheater and entering raw material into gas mixing tank;
   (B) entering liquid oxygen into feed gas mixing tank, vaporizing by the air temperature vaporizer, mixing with the industrial-grade carbon dioxide, and compressing mixed raw material gas into a compressor to obtain compressed gas;
   (C) heating compressed gas by a heat exchanger and an electric heater in sequence, entering desulfurization tower to desulfurize, and entering into the catalytic reactor to remove hydrocarbons, aldehydes, alcohols, and fats to obtain catalytic combustion of gas;
   (D) entering catalytic combustion gas into tube of the heat exchanger, entering shell side of the preheater after heat exchange with the compressed gas, and vaporized industrial grade, heat-exchanging carbon dioxide, cooling by the water cooler, drying by the drying tower, filtering through the filter, and removing solid particles or dust to obtain filtered gas;
   (E) entering filtered gas into purification tower through the reboiler's inlet of the reboiler to providing heat for the purification tower, discharging from the reboiler's tube-range outlet, and entering vaporizer through a pipeline, entering first gas-liquid separator after performing heat exchange with the liquid industrial-grade carbon dioxide for gas-liquid separation, and separating gas by the first gas-liquid further liquefied by entering the liquefier, and liquefying; and
   (F) entering product into second gas-liquid separator for gas-liquid separation, separating liquid by the second gas-liquid separator entering first gas-liquid separator, and separating gas by the second gas-liquid separator venting or returning to the feed gas mixing tank, where liquid in the first gas-liquid separator entered packed section of the purification column through the feed pump for purification to obtain food-grade liquid carbon dioxide and non-condensable gas, discharging food grade liquid carbon dioxide through the product outlet of the purification tower, and entering non-condensable gas liquefier through the gas outlet at the top of the purification tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The liquid industrial-grade carbon dioxide is depressurized to 1.0-1.7 MPa, entered vaporizer shell side, and vaporized at -38-23 degrees C, vaporized industrial-grade carbon dioxide is preheated to 40 degrees C by the preheater, it is introduced into the raw gas mixing tank, and the volume content of oxygen in the mixed raw gas is 0.8-2.0%.The pressure of the compressed gas is 2.0-3.5MPa, and temperature is 60-150 degrees C. The compressed air is heated by the heat exchanger and the temperature is increased from 60-150?OC to 200-350?OC, and then heated by the electric heater to 250-450?OC, and the sulfur is introduced into the desulfurization tower remove to 0.1PPm. The temperature of the gas cooled by the water cooler is 40 degrees C, and then the free water in the drying tower is removed to 10 ppm. The temperature of the gas after cooling by the reboiler is -10-5 degrees C. The desulfurization agent in the desulfurization tower is an intermediate temperature zinc oxide desulfurization agent, and temperature application range is 200-400 degrees C, and the pressure is 2.0-3.5 MPa (G) and the desulfurization accuracy is 0.1 ppm. The catalytic reactor is of a fixed bed type, catalyst in the catalytic reactor is a precious metal catalyst of Pd or Pt. The temperature is 250-500 degrees C, and the pressure is 2.0-3.5 MPa. The catalytically combustible substances include alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, alcohols, aldehydes and lipids Preferred Components: The drying agent in the drying tower is selected from molecular sieves or silica gel.

AU SOON L S
   LEE K
   HA S W
   JEON W
AE UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 202117410Y
AB    NOVELTY - The sensing module (100) has a main component (110) which is partitioned into multiple vertical spaces (112,114,116,118) and includes openings (113,115,117) which are formed on a side wall so that each of the vertical spaces communicates with the outside. Multiple carbon dioxide sensors (152,154,156,158) are arranged corresponding to each of the vertical spaces to determine the direction of the carbon dioxide pollution cloud and detect carbon dioxide introduced into the vertical space. A magnetic field sensor (160) outputs a magnetic field signal to define the direction of the vertical spaces partitioned in the main component.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sensing module of carbon dioxide measuring system (claimed), for detecting direction of carbon dioxide contaminated cloud contained in underground soil and groundwater.
   ADVANTAGE - The direction of the carbon dioxide pollution cloud is determined based on the sensing timing, by arranging the carbon dioxide sensors in each of the vertical spaces, as carbon dioxide flows into the vertical spaces through the openings. Since the inflow direction of the carbon dioxide contaminated cloud is known, the extent and distribution of the pollution influence are grasped from the leak point. The reliability of detection of the carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide sensing module is increased in the next cycle by removing carbon dioxide gas from the first to fourth vertical spaces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide measuring system; and
