PN US2021162340-A1
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide from gaseous source of            carbon dioxide involves subjecting initial liquid to            alkali enrichment protocol to produce enhanced            alkalinity liquid and employing the enhanced alkalinity            liquid.
AU BEWERNITZ M
   SCHNEIDER J
   CAMIRE C L
   CONSTANTZ B R
AE BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 202160487U
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering carbon dioxide from gaseous                source of carbon dioxide involves subjecting                initial liquid to alkali enrichment protocol to                produce enhanced alkalinity liquid and employing                the enhanced alkalinity liquid in carbon dioxide                sequestration protocol to sequester carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for sequestering carbon dioxide from                gaseous source of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to sequester carbon dioxide                from gaseous source of carbon dioxide that improves                oil recovery or tertiary recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali enrichment                  protocol is membrane mediated protocol. The                  initial liquid is selected from freshwater,                  seawater, brine water, produced water and waste                  water. The divalent alkaline earth metal cations                  are selected from Ca^2^+ and Mg^2^+, and                  combinations. Preferred Conditions: The membrane                  is cationic membrane, anionic membrane. The                  alkali enrichment protocol comprises an osmotic                  pressure mediated protocol. The gaseous source of                  carbon dioxide is multi-component gaseous stream.                  The cation source is source of divalent cations.                  The divalent cations comprise alkaline earth                  metal cations. The divalent cation source is                  introduced into flowing LCP containing liquid                  under conditions sufficient such that non-slurry                  solid phase carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate                  material is produced in the flowing LCP                  containing liquid. The divalent cation source is                  introduced into LCP containing liquid under                  conditions sufficient such that slurry carbon                  dioxide sequestering carbonate material is                  produced from the LCP containing liquid.                  Preferred Process: The method involves contacting                  the enhanced alkalinity liquid with the gaseous                  source of carbon dioxide under conditions                  sufficient to produce LCP containing liquid. The                  method involves producing the initial liquid by                  contacting source liquid with the gaseous source                  of carbon dioxide The gaseous source of carbon                  dioxide is contacted with liquid under conditions                  such that carbon dioxide is selectively absorbed                  by the carbon dioxide absorbing aqueous medium.                  The carbonate is produced by introducing cation                  source into an LCP containing liquid under                  conditions sufficient to produce the                  carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE HEFEI JIULUO MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201963777M
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium bicarbonate preparation device comprises a dissolution tank (1), liquid storage tank (2), carbon dioxide gas storage tank (3), spiral twist flat tube (4), crystallization tank (5), centrifuge and air flow dryer. The tank is connected to the liquid inlet of the liquid storage tank through the first liquid guiding tube, the spiral twisting flat tube is horizontally arranged, and the feeding port of the spiral twisting flat tube passes through the second liquid guiding tube. The first air guiding pipe is respectively connected with the liquid outlet of the liquid storage tank and the air outlet of the carbon dioxide gas storage tank, and the second liquid guiding tube is provided with an infusion pump, and the first air guiding tube is provided with a gas pump. The liquid outlet of the spiral twisted flat tube is connected to the liquid inlet of the crystallizing tank, and the liquid outlet of the crystallizing tank passes through the fourth liquid guiding liquid.
   USE - Used as sodium bicarbonate preparation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The production device and the operation are simplified, the maintenance is easy, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sodium bicarbonate preparation device comprises a dissolution tank (1), liquid storage tank (2), carbon dioxide gas storage tank (3), spiral twist flat tube (4), crystallization tank (5), centrifuge and air flow dryer. The tank is connected to the liquid inlet of the liquid storage tank through the first liquid guiding tube, the spiral twisting flat tube is horizontally arranged, and the feeding port of the spiral twisting flat tube passes through the second liquid guiding tube. The first air guiding pipe is respectively connected with the liquid outlet of the liquid storage tank and the air outlet of the carbon dioxide gas storage tank, and the second liquid guiding tube is provided with an infusion pump, and the first air guiding tube is provided with a gas pump. The liquid outlet of the spiral twisted flat tube is connected to the liquid inlet of the crystallizing tank through the third liquid guiding tube, and the liquid outlet of the crystallizing tank passes through the fourth liquid guiding liquid. The tube is connected with the centrifuge, and the solids outlet of the centrifuge is in communication with the airflow dryer. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing sodium hydrogencarbonate using the preparation apparatus, comprising (i) adding sodium carbonate solid and purified water to the dissolving tank, controlling the residence time to be 0.1-0.3 hours, where the dissolving tank is kept stirring continuously, and then transferring the dissolved sodium carbonate solution into the liquid storage tank; (ii) introducing the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide gas storage tank into the spiral twisted flat tube through the gas pump, and passing the sodium carbonate solution in the liquid storage tank into the spiral infusion pump, reacting the sodium carbonate solution and the carbon dioxide in the spiral twisted flat tube, settling and crystallizing as the gas flow completely flows into the crystallizing tank; and (iii) transferring the liquid in the crystallization tank to a centrifuge for centrifugation, and transferring the separated solid to a gas flow dryer for drying.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the sodium bicarbonate preparation device.
   Dissolution tank (1)
   Liquid storage tank (2)
   Carbon dioxide gas storage tank (3)
   Spiral twist flat tube (4)
   Crystallization tank (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A stirring mechanism is provided in the dissolution tank, and the stirring temperature of the dissolution tank is 40-60 degrees C. The spiral twisted flat tube is made of general-purpose stainless steel, and the cross-sectional shape of the spiral twisted flat tube is oblate or elliptical. The ratio of the short axis d1 of the spiral twisted flat tube to its long axis d2 is 0.3-0.5, and the pitch of the spiral twisted flat tube is 3-30 with its equivalent diameter. The device further comprises a condenser and a carbon dioxide compressor, the inlet of the condenser passes through the second air guiding tube connected with the air outlet of the crystallization tank, and the air outlet of the condenser connects with the air inlet of the carbon dioxide compressor through the third air guiding tube, and the air outlet of the carbon dioxide compressor passes through the four air ducts, that are connected with the air inlet of the carbon dioxide gas storage tank. Further, the mother liquid recovery tank is included, and the liquid inlet of the mother liquid recovery tank connects with the liquid outlet of the condenser through the fifth liquid guiding tube, and the liquid outlet of the mother liquid recovery tank passes via the sixth liquid guiding tube and linked with the liquid inlet of the dissolution tank. Preferred Method: In (i), the stirring temperature of the dissolution tank (i) is 40-60 degrees C; preferably, the sodium carbonate solution obtained by (i) has a mass percentage of 40-50%. In (ii), the carbon dioxide pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa. In step (iii), the drying temperature of the air flow dryer is from 110-140 degrees C.

   USE - The porphyrin-based multifunctional photocatalytic metal-organic framework material is useful for achieving para-substituted aromatic alcohols.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: makes full use of electrons and holes through coupling strategies in photocatalytic systems; and proves the universality of this reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zirconium-nickel-porphyrin metal-organic frameworks visible light absorbing semiconductor material is not only suitable for the coupling reaction involving benzyl alcohol, and realizing the above-mentioned bifunctional coupling reaction with the para-substituted aromatic alcohol.Preferred Method: The zirconium-nickel-porphyrin metal-organic framework is synthesized by the solvothermal method includes adding zirconium(IV) chloride, benzoic acid and nickel(II) tetracarboxylate porphyrin (Ni-TCPP), ultrasonically dissolving in dimethylformamide, transferring solution to the reactor, heating and reacting, centrifugal separating of The zirconium-nickel-porphyrin metal-organic frameworks, washing and drying in vacuum to obtain purple powdery crystals. The method comprises taking zirconium(IV) chloride, benzoic acid and nickel(II) tetracarboxylate porphyrin in a ratio of 4:50:3, heating in a muffle furnace at 120degreesC for 72 hours, centrifuging at speed of 12000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, washing for three times with ethanol and drying under vacuum at 70degreesC for 12 hours with a yield of 53%. The conduction band potential of the zirconium-nickel-porphyrin metal-organic frameworks (PMOFs) is 1.22 V having sufficient H+ reduction driving force, valence band potential is +1.12V and sufficient oxidizing power is provided for the selective oxidation of benzyl alcohol. The zirconium-nickel-porphyrin metal-organic frameworks having strong absorption in the 200-800 nm band, and the band gap width is 2.34 eV.

PN CN113072691-A
TI New polycyclohexene carbonate copolymer prepared            by e.g. reacting carbon dioxide and            1,2-epoxycyclohexane, then adding macromolecular chain            transfer agent, and performing block            polymerization.
AU HU H
   XU D
   CUI Y
   TANG J
AE SHANGHAI HUAFON NEW MATERIALS RES & DEV (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021849513
AB    NOVELTY - Polycyclohexene carbonate block copolymer (I)                are new.
   USE - Used as polycyclohexene carbonate block                copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem of                deterioration of thermal properties of material                while improving toughness of polycyclohexenyl                carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polycyclohexene carbonate block copolymer of                formula (B1-(A1B1)n) (I) are new.
   n = 1, 3 or 5;
   A1 = biodegradable aliphatic polyester                segment; and
   B1 = polycyclohexenyl carbonate segment.
   Provided that: A1 and B1 are connected by                ether or ester bonds. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises reacting carbon dioxide and                  1,2-epoxycyclohexane, then adding macromolecular                  chain transfer agent for K times, and performing                  block polymerization, where macromolecular chain                  transfer agent is biodegradable aliphatic                  polyester C with double-sided active ends,                  double-sided active ends are hydroxy or carboxy                  groups, and the relationship between K and n is n                  is equal to 2x K-1. Preferred Components: The                  polycyclohexene carbonate copolymer has glass                  transition temperature of 93-105 degrees C,                  impact strength of 25-30 KJ/m2, and breaking                  elongation of 15-30%. The number average                  molecular weight of polycyclohexene carbonate                  copolymer is 35-350 kg/mol; the molecular weight                  distribution index is 1.5-3.5; the ratio between                  contribution of segment A to the number average                  molecular weight and contribution of segment B to                  the molecular weight is 1:1.3-14.4; and the                  contribution of segment A to the number average                  molecular weight is 5-25 kg/mol. The number                  average molecular weight of polycyclohexene                  carbonate copolymer is 50-500 kg/mol, and the                  molecular weight distribution index is 1.5-3.5;                  the ratio between contribution of segment A to                  the number average molecular weight and                  contribution of segment B to the molecular weight                  is 1:1.3-14.4; and the contribution of segment A                  to the number average molecular weight is 15-125                  kg/mol. The biodegradable aliphatic polyester C                  with double-sided active ends is polybutylene                  terephthalate-co-butylene adipate, polybutylene                  adipate, poly DL-lactide, poly- epsilon                  -caprolactone or polypropylene carbonate. The                  solvent is tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane,                  chloroform, dioxolane, dioxane, ethyl acetate,                  acetone or methyl ethyl ketone; the catalyst is                  heterogeneous complex catalyst or catalytic                  system composed of homogeneous complex as main                  catalyst and promoter; the heterogeneous complex                  catalyst includes zinc compound of formula (ZnR2)                  catalyst, zinc dicarboxylic acid catalyst, rare                  earth ternary catalyst and/or double metal                  cyanide catalyst; the zinc dicarboxylic acid                  catalyst is zinc succinate, zinc glutarate, zinc                  adipate or zinc pimelate; the rare earth ternary                  catalyst is yttrium compound                  (Y(P2O4)3)-triisobutylaluminum                  (Al(i-Bu)3)-glycerol, rhenium compound                  (Re(P2O4)3)-triisobutylaluminum (Al(i-Bu)3),                  lanthanum compound                  (Ln(CCl3COO)3)-glycerol-diethylzinc (ZnEt2) or                  niodium compound (Nd(CCl3COO)3)-diethylzinc                  (ZnEt2)-glycerol; the double metal cyanide                  catalyst is zinc-cobalt-double metal cyanide,                  zinc-iron-double metal cyanide,                  iron-cobalt-double metal cyanide or                  nickel-cobalt-double metal cyanide; catalytic                  system composed of homogeneous complexes as main                  catalyst, and co-catalyst, the ratio between                  amount of main catalyst and co-catalyst is                  1:0.5-10; the main catalyst includes zinc                  phenolic catalysts, porphyrin metal complex                  catalysts and/or Schiff alkali metal complex                  catalysts; the co-catalyst includes                  tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium                  bromide, tetrabutylammonium chloride,                  tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate,                  bistriphenylphosphine ammonium chloride,                  bistriphenylphosphine ammonium bromide,                  bistriphenylphosphonium nitroamine (sic),                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene-4-dimethylaminopyridine,                  and/or gamma -chloropropylmethyldimethoxysilane;                  the zinc phenolic catalyst is diethyl                  zinc/ternary phenol complex, beta -diimine zinc                  complex or prolinol zinc complex; the porphyrin                  metal complex catalyst is aluminum porphyrin                  complex, cobalt porphyrin complex or chromium                  porphyrin complex; the Schiff alkali metal                  complex catalyst is aluminum Schiff base complex,                  cobalt Schiff base complex or chromium Schiff                  base complex; the feed mass ratio between                  heterogeneous complex catalyst and                  1,2-epoxycyclohexane is 1:1000-10000; the ratio                  between homogeneous complex and                  1,2-epoxycyclohexane is 1:2000-500000; the mass                  ratio between total feed amount of macromolecular                  chain transfer agent and feed amount of                  1,2-epoxycyclohexane is 1:1-10; and the volume                  ratio between 1,2-epoxycyclohexane and solvent is                  1-5:1. Preferred Method: When K is 1, the                  preparation process of polycyclohexenyl carbonate                  copolymer comprises adding macromolecular chain                  transfer agent, 1,2-epoxycyclohexane, solvent and                  catalyst all at once into autoclave at the                  beginning of the reaction, filling carbon                  dioxide, and copolymerizing into segment B to                  obtain polycyclohexene carbonate copolymer. When                  K is greater than 1, the preparation process of                  polycyclohexenyl carbonate copolymer comprises                  adding certain amount of macromolecular chain                  transfer agent, 1,2-epoxycyclohexane, solvent and                  catalyst at the initial stage of the reaction                  into autoclave, filling carbon dioxide,                  copolymerizing into segment B to form B-A-B                  prepolymer, then continue adding macromolecular                  chain transfer agent into reaction system for K-1                  times until copolymerization reaction is                  completed to obtain final product, where the                  amount of macromolecular chain transfer agent                  added each time is equal, and the interval time                  for continuously adding macromolecular chain                  transfer agent into reaction system is same every                  two adjacent times after B-A-B type prepolymer is                  formed. The pressure in autoclave after filling                  with carbon dioxide is 1-8 MPa; the                  copolymerization reaction is carried out at                  25-150 degrees C for 5-48 hours; the conversion                  rate of 1,2-epoxycyclohexane in the                  copolymerization reaction is greater than or                  equal to 90%; cyclohexenyl carbonate by-product                  content is less than 5 wt.%; and the content of                  carbonate units in the segment B of                  polycyclohexene carbonate copolymer is greater                  than 90%.R = ethyl, butyl, isopropyl, cyclohexyl or                  phenyl.

AU HIROSE T
   IMAGAWA H
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 2020A71157
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst for hydrocarbon synthesis comprises                sodium (Na)-containing cobalt(II) oxide (CoO)                particles and greater than or equal to 1 nickel                (Ni)-containing oxide particles selected from                Ni-containing alumina (Al2O3) particles and                Ni-containing titania (TiO2) particles.
   USE - A catalyst for hydrocarbon synthesis.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Catalyst contains                  70-98 mass% Na-containing CoO particles and 2-30                  mass% Ni-containing oxide particles. Amount of Ni with respect to total                  Ni-containing oxide particles is 2-40                  mass%.

PT P
PN BR102019000768-A2
TI Transport polynuclear type microcapsules used in drilling of oil, and gas, injector wells, and geothermal wells, where gas selected from air, or nitrogen, comprises acid emulsified mineral oil incorporated into oil-based drilling fluids.
AU MAGALHAES DE MENEZES M
   COSTA DA SILVA PINTO J
   TEIXEIRA LOMBA R F
   SITTON N K
   NELE DE SOUZA M
AE UNIV RIO DE JANEIRO (UYRI-Non-standard)
GA 2020773774
AB    NOVELTY - Transport polynuclear type microcapsules comprises 30 wt.% acid emulsified mineral oil, which is incorporated into oil-based drilling fluids.
   USE - Transport polynuclear type microcapsules used in drilling of oil, and gas, injector wells, and geothermal wells, where gas selected from air, nitrogen or carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The transport polynuclear type microcapsules increase the penetration rate of the drill bit (ROP).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The transport polynuclear type microcapsules comprises 0.5-2 wt.% biodegradable polymer, such as encapsulating agent. Preferred Conditions: The acid emulsion is produced in the presence, or not, of a surfatante (emulsifying agent) in concentrations up to 5 wt.%. The biodegradable polymers are biopolymers, or mixtures of biopolymers, solutions of biopolymers and biopolymers containing solvents with a melting point at 200oC. The alginates are derived from extraction of cell walls of brown algae, which are modified by processes of acidification and alkaline. The biopolymer is the melt-forming, shell-forming solid of the microcapsules that is resistant to vegetable, mineral and synthetic oils up to 200 degrees C. The shell-forming biopolymer is resistant to acid/mineral oil emulsion. The meltable solid is a biopolymer or a biopolymer modified by esterification or mixtures of biopolymers, with a melting temperature of 200 degrees C. The strong acid and weak acid to be microencapsulated is an organic acid. The organic acid is selected from sulfonic acids and their chlorinated or fluorinated derivatives containing up to ten carbon atoms, where acids is pure or in solution, in concentrations of 15-50 vol.%. The weak acid to be microencapsulated is a weak organic acid selected from acetic, formic or glycolic acid, pure or in aqueous solution, or mixture of them, in concentrations between 15-50 vol.%. The organic acid is methanesulfonic acid (MSA). The organic acid is alkyl and aryl sulfonic acids and their chlorinated or fluorinated derivatives containing up to ten carbon atoms. The surfactant of the acid/mineral oil emulsion, has a lipophilic-hydrophilic balance (HLB) between 1 and 6 and is soluble in the oil phase. The surfactant is selected from sorbitan esters derived from sorbitol or fatty acids including sorbitan oleate. The microcapsules is obtained via emulsification of the strong acid in mineral oil under mechanical stirring or sonication, in the presence or absence of a proportion of surfactant, and then pouring the emulsion over a solution of sodium alginate with a concentration between 0.5-2 wt.%, where sodium alginate solution is in volumetric excess in relation to the acid/mineral oil emulsion, in volume ratio of 1:5 to 1:10. The acid/oil emulsion mixture and sodium alginate solution are dripped, using a syringe with a needle and syringe pump (extrusion process) and injecting a gas at the end of the syringe, over a solution of barium, calcium, strontium or aluminum salts, with a concentration between 5-20 wt.% and crosslinked for 40 minutes with magnetic stirring. The microcapsules are subjected to a second coating of sodium alginate, poured into sodium alginate solution 0.1-0.3 wt.%, containing 1 wt.% surfactant (with HLB above 8), under magnetic stirring for 15 minutes, then they are treated with a solution containing barium, calcium, strontium or aluminum salts with a concentration between 10-20 wt.% for 15 minutes, under magnetic stirring and finally treated with isopropanol, under agitation, for 15 minutes, subjected to simple filtration, washed with deionized water and dried in an oven at 40 degrees C or room temperature. The microcapsules are obtained via coacervation, using an encapsulator or via emulsion, the dispersed phase having a vegetable oil, mineral or synthetic oil. The microcapsules are solid microcapsules of predominantly spherical shape, where size of the particles obtained is between 0.1-2.5 mm. The microcapsules falling apart when subjected to crushing by the weight of the drill column/drill bit set and the shear caused by the drill bit rotating. The microcapsules injected during the drilling of oil, gas, injectors and geothermal wells, combined with a drilling fluid or carrier, exclusively oil-based, in a proportion of from 1-90 wt.% or 0.5-95 wt.% or 1-85 wt.% in relation to drilling or carrier fluid in accordance with the desired acid concentration in the region in which the drill operates and containing acid in the microcapsule. Preferred Components: The biopolymers are selected from alginates, starch and cellulose derivatives, proteins, fatty acids, acacia gum or chitosan, where alginates are selected from biopolymers, predominantly composed of a mixture of manuronic and guluronic acid residues with a melting point around 70 degrees C and insoluble in synthetic oils, vegetable oils or mineral oils. The microcapsules are fused or non-acid resistant biopolymer, comprising poly lactic acid (PLA) and its isomers, polyglycolic acid (PGA) and its isomers, PLA/PGA copolymers or polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB), all with a melting temperature above 100 degrees C. The strong acid to be microencapsulated is a strong inorganic or organic acid selected from hydrochloric and methanesulfonic acid, pure or in aqueous solution, in concentrations between 15-50 vol.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

TI New phosphine-containing carboxylic acid compounds, prepared by dissolving reaction substrate with a phosphine oxide and a base, adding a photocatalyst under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and then reacting.
AU YU D
   LI J
   FU Q
   BO Z
   YE J
   JU T
   HUANG H
   LIAO L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
   UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2019917476
AB    NOVELTY - Phosphine-containing carboxylic acid compounds (I) are new.
   USE - Used as phosphine-containing carboxylic acid compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Phosphine-containing carboxylic acid compounds of formula (I) are new.
   X = aromatic, substituted aromatic, alkyl or alkoxy;
   A, G = H, hydroxy, ester, alkyl, amido, amino, aryl, substituted aryl, heteroaryl or substituted heteroaryl; and
   T = H or an alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises dissolving reaction substrate with a phosphine oxide and a base, adding a photocatalyst under carbon dioxide atmosphere, reacting at 10-60 degrees C, at a illumination of 1-100 W for 2-24 hours, where amount of phosphine oxide is 1-3 times the amount of the reaction substrate; amount of base is 1-20 times the amount of the reaction substrate; amount of photocatalyst is 0.1-5% the amount of the reaction substrate; and reaction substrate is an amide compound, olefin compound, silicon-alkenyl ether compound or acrylic ester compound. Preferred Components: The phosphine oxide compound is an aryl phosphine oxide compound, phosphonate compound or an alkyl phosphine oxide compound; base is potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate or cesium carbonate. The photocatalyst is 1,2,3,5-Tetrakis(carbazol-9-yl)-4,6-dicyanobenzene, bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl) -5-trifluoromethylpyridine) (1,10-phenanthroline) iridium hexafluorophosphate, bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl) -5-trifluoromethylpyridine) (1,10-phenanthroline) iridium hexafluorophosphate-iridium(III) bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-5-methylpyridine-N,C20)-4,40-di-tert-butyl-2,20-bipyridine hexafluorophosphate or Iridium(III) bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-5-methylpyridine-N,C20)-4,40-di-tert-butyl-2,20-bipyridine hexafluorophosphate. The light source used in the illumination reaction is blue light. The eluent used in the column chromatography is a mixture of dichloromethane and methanol in a volume ratio of 100:1-20:1, or a mixture of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 2:1-1:1; and amount of mixture of dichloromethane-methanol and mixture of petroleum ether-ethyl acetate is 0.1-0.6 vol.% the total volume of glacial acetic acid. Preferred Method: The method comprises reaction is carried out at 20-25 degrees C, at a illumination of 30-35 W for 10-12 hours. The method further comprises quenching the reaction product with acid, extracting with ethyl acetate for at least 5 times, concentrating the extracted organic phase, then purifying the concentrated product by column chromatography.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 69 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed which are e.g. 3-(diphenylphosphoryl)-2-(4-fluoro-3-phenoxyphenyl)propanoic acid (Ia), 3-(diphenylphosphoryl)-2-mesitylpropanoic acid, 3-(diphenylphosphoryl)-2-methyl-2-phenylpropanoic acid, 3-(diphenylphosphoryl)-2-phenylbutanoic acid and 3-(diphenylphosphoryl)-2-(naphthalen-2-yl)propanoic acid.

PT P
PN CN109999793-A; CN109999793-B
TI Titanium dioxide/tungstate photocatalyst used in environment protection field comprises titanium dioxide nanosheet distributed with Lewis base tungstate.
AU JIANG Z
   YUAN Z
   CHEN J
   YANG G
   MIAO W
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 201962869S
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium dioxide (TiO2)/tungstate (WO4) photocatalyst comprises TiO2 nanosheet distributed with Lewis base (WO4)2-.
   USE - The TiO2/WO4 photocatalyst is used in environment protection field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst realizes the TiO2 photon-generated carrier separation, improves the carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption capability, and greatly improves the CO2 TiO2 photoreduction activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the TiO2/WO4 photocatalyst comprising adding water-soluble tungstate in TiO2 nanosheet dispersion synthesized by hydrothermal method to obtain mixed solution, continuously stirring, obtaining solid product, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The thickness of the TiO2 nanosheet is 3-10 nm. The Lewis base (WO4)2- loading amount is 0.3-6% of the amount of TiO2 nanosheet. Preferred Method: The water-soluble tungstate is added at 1-10% of the amount of the TiO2 nanosheet. The water-soluble tungstate comprises sodium tungstate or ammonium tungstate. The TiO2 nanosheet dispersion is prepared by dispersing TiO2 nanosheet in water, and stirring uniformly for 5-30 minutes, where water temperature is controlled at 12-30 degrees C, and the feeding ratio of the TiO2 nanosheet and water is 0.03-0.1 g:50-100 ml.

PN CA2988445-A1
TI Production of aluminum, silicon, their allied            refractory metals and equipment for production of these            elements and on line production of their alloys and            compounds.
AU GHULAM N
AE GHULAM N (GHUL-Individual)
GA 201951496G
AB    NOVELTY - Production of aluminum, silicon, and their                allied refractory metals. Also claimed is the                production of these elements and on line production                of their alloys and compounds.
   USE - Production of aluminum and silicon from                ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is economical, with no slag                formation and carbon dioxide emission.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                reactor.
   Low pressure compressor (1)
   Intercooler (2)
   Turbines (3)
   Drive compressor (4)
   Combustor (5)
   Fuels (6)
   Power turbine (7)
   Gear box (8)
   Incoming hot gases (9)
   Out gases for water (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Raw material used are                  ores which are stoichiometrically adjusted using                  sodium and oxygen or caustic compound in                  refractory reactor or furnace by introducing                  solid charge and gaseous reactants and                  measurement control for production inputs,                  discharging liquid and gaseous product from                  various points at preset rate, temperature and                  pressure. Reactor has output product gases of                  carbon dioxide and water containing nitrogen lead                  to energy recovery equipment and exit gases.                  Liquid slag is introduced to lower reactor for                  further energy recovery and lowering temperature                  for processing. Heat is extracted from high                  temperature slag by graphite heat exchanger and                  heat extracted from gases is joined in main heat                  extraction line. Another discharge line is below                  slag line where the iron liquid is discharged                  from this line and contains all elements melted                  before iron, but water soluble sodium salts from                  metal oxides are not melted with iron. All slag                  is dripped in water trough where water soluble                  sodium slats are separated from water insoluble.                  The process is applied for production of aluminum                  alloy and alloying metal oxide from purified                  alumina and extended process is to obtain                  aluminum or silicon from pure alumina and silica                  ore. Powder alumina is charged through double                  locked hopper, oxygen is metered in hopper and                  then preheated hydrogen, and combined slag                  portion enters to refractor. The exit gas is                  cooled by cold lines from other portion of                  system. Exothermic condition of the reaction is                  aluminum oxide, hydrogen and oxygen gives                  aluminum, water and hydrogen. Hydrogen is                  introduced with electrodes discharge, heat energy                  produced is more than heat formation of aluminum                  oxide. Refractory metal is graphite cooled by                  plate and staves coolers. Alumina is reduced to                  liquid aluminum which falls downward to graphite                  heat exchanger. Exit gas is cooled to 700 degrees                  C by energy recovery system and undergoes shift                  reactions. Blown out particles are retained and                  water is recycled and bleeds out. Oxygen is                  introduced in alumina feed and reaches high                  temperature and undergoes the reaction of                  aluminum oxide oxidation and reduction to give                  aluminum oxide and water at 1600 degrees C. High                  temperature shifting operated at 400-700 degrees                  C and uses chromium or copper promoted iron based                  catalyst. Synthetic gas is added to exit water                  stream after its temperature lowered to 700                  degrees C. Low temperature shift which are                  sensitive to sulfur and chlorine is used to                  remove carbon monoxide below 1% at 300-400                  degrees C and using copper-zinc-aluminum as                  catalyst. Aluminum ore treated with hydrochloric                  acid to give high purity elements and their                  important compounds such as sodium aluminate,                  calcium orthosilicate, magnesium metasilicate,                  and sodium metasilicate on treated with                  hydrochloric acid, silicon tetrachloride and                  aluminum chloride are volatilized and three                  compounds of calcium chloride, magnesium chloride                  and sodium chloride are left behind and are                  separated by its different melting points.                  Silicon oxide and aluminum oxide is mixed by                  reaction of sodium, filtering aluminum at                  660-1100 degrees C. Sodium oxide is filtered out                  leaving silicon. The system can be extended to                  other high melting oxides like titanium oxide,                  chromium oxide and others.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-C01; M25-C02; M25-G01; M25-J

PT P
PN CN110669950-A; CN110669950-B
TI Enhanced leaching of uranium by ground leaching comprises e.g. using air pre-oxidation for oxidizing uranium mineral and sealing upper part of the ore layer, filtering, degreasing, drying, and gas-liquid mixing.
AU LIAO W
   WANG L
   TAN Y
   CHEN X
   WANG P
   DU Z
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2020061656
AB    NOVELTY - Enhanced leaching of uranium by ground leaching comprises e.g. (i) using air pre-oxidation for oxidizing the uranium mineral and sealing the upper part of the ore layer, (i.i) where estimated total pre-oxidation air demand is based on the pore volume V of the upper aquifer, multiplying the ore pressure P and the porosity correction factor gamma - and estimating the total air demand, (i.ii) selecting single injection well that can perform air pre-oxidation, where the minimum injection volume is greater than 0.5 m3/hour, (i.iii) starting air pre-oxidation pumping cycle, air passes air compressor, and air bag, filtering, degreasing, drying, gas-liquid mixing with the leach agent measuring by the electromagnetic flow meter by the gas flow meter, the initial control air injection pressure is above 0.4 MPa, adjusting the injection well valve for controlling the total injection pressure above 0.3 MPa, mixing according to the ratio of injection liquid and injection gas and injecting.
   USE - The method is useful for enhanced leaching of uranium by ground leaching.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: rapidly oxidizes uranium minerals; reduces reagent consumption; and improves leaching strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhanced leaching of uranium by ground leaching comprises (i) using air pre-oxidation for oxidizing the uranium mineral and sealing the upper part of the ore layer, (i.i) where estimated total pre-oxidation air demand is based on the pore volume V of the upper aquifer, multiplying the ore pressure P and the porosity correction factor gamma - and estimating the total air demand, (i.ii) selecting single injection well that can perform air pre-oxidation, where the minimum injection volume is greater than 0.5 m3/hour, and air injection volume is greater than or equal to 0.5 m3/h, (i.iii) starting air pre-oxidation pumping cycle, air passes air compressor, and air bag, filtering, degreasing, drying, gas-liquid mixing with the leach agent measuring by the electromagnetic flow meter by the gas flow meter, the initial control air injection pressure is above 0.4 MPa, adjusting the injection well valve for controlling the total injection pressure above 0.3 MPa, mixing according to the ratio of injection liquid and injection gas 1:1 and injecting, basically unchanging the injection volume of 0.5 hour, increasing the injection volume of 0.1 m3/hour, allowing to stand for 0.5 hour, increasing the gas injection volume by 0.1 m3/hour, so gradually increasing the gas injection volume until the last increase in gas injection, where the injection volume decreased significantly within 0.5 hour, indicating that there is an air blockage, then reducing the last increase in gas injection volume, while maintaining the infusion, allowing to stand until the air blockage is gradually eliminated, then increasing the power of the injection pump, increasing the injection pressure by 0.1-0.2 MPa, then increasing the gas injection volume as described above until the injection pressure increases to 1-1.5 MPa, and maximizing the amount of gas injected at this time, injecting the gas-liquid mixed leaching agent into the ore layer through the injection well, pre-oxidizing the leaching according to the estimated value of total air demand and the air injection volume per hour, estimating the number of days of pre-oxidation, and preparing monitoring, (i.iv) when pre-oxidation is over, when the dissolved oxygen concentration in the leachate reaches 10 mg/l or when obvious bubbles are visible in the leachate, stopping adding air, and ending pre-oxidation, (ii) processing enhanced synergistic leaching, (ii.i) adding sodium dichloroisocyanurate solution pre-dissolved in water to the extractant, and mixing into the injection system, (ii.ii) adding both oxygen and carbon dioxide to the leaching agent in the injection system, mixing, injecting into the ore layer for leaching, adding oxygen concentration according to the volume of leaching agent is 650-1000 mg/l, carbon dioxide concentration is added according to the volume of leaching agent 800-1500 mg/l, (iii) normal leaching, (iii.i) when the concentration of dissolved oxygen in the leaching solution reaches 15 mg/l, stopping adding the sodium dichloroisocyanurate solution, and reducing oxygen concentration to 300-500 mg/l, when the leaching rate of uranium reaches to 65%, reducing the oxygen concentration to 50-200 mg/l until the end of leaching, (iii.ii) when the pH value of the leaching solution drops to 6.5, reducing the concentration of added carbon dioxide to 300-500 mg/l, when the pH of the leachate drops to 6.3, reducing the added carbon dioxide concentration to 100-200 mg/l, (iii.iii) controlling the concentration of bicarbonate in the leachate to 1-1.2 g/l, (iii.iv) when the uranium concentration of the leachate is greater than 10 mg/l, and adsorbing the leachate into the tower, and(iv) circulating and adsorbing tail solution, treating the adsorption tail solution, and adding the sodium dichloroisocyanurate solution, carbon dioxide and oxygen to the ore layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i.iii), if reducing the last increase of gas injection still does not eliminate the air blockage, closing the gas injection valve, and exhausting at the wellhead and eliminating air blockage, then re-injecting according to the last injection volume before the occurrence of air blockage. In the step (i.i), the formula for estimating the total demand for pre-oxidized air is Px Vx gamma x 10 is Px (Hx Sx phi )x gamma x 10, where, P is mineral pressure, MPa, V is the pore volume of the upper aquifer, m3, gamma is Porosity correction coefficient, taking 0.05-0.2 according to the pore size distribution, H is thickness of the upper aquifer, m, S is plane area of pre-oxidized ore body, m2, and phi core porosity,%. In the step (i.iii), the whole process controls the air injection pressure to be more than 0.05 MPa above the injection pressure. The initial injection is based on the ratio of 1 m3 liquid to 1 m3 air in the standard state. In the step (ii.i), the amount of sodium dichloroisocyanurate is added according to 20-100 mg/l of the volume of the extractant. In the step (ii.ii), the injection pressure of oxygen and carbon dioxide is higher than the injection pressure by more than 0.05 MPa. In the step (iii.iii), if the concentration of bicarbonate in the leaching solution is less than 1 g/l, 0.2-0.6 g/l bicarbonate based on the volume of the leaching agent is added to maintain the bicarbonate concentration of 1-1.2 g/l.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-B; M25-F; M25-G25; M25-H; M25-J
IP C22B-003/16; C22B-060/02; E21B-043/28

AE AGITATED SOLUTIONS INC (AGIT-Non-standard)
GA 2022688485
AB    NOVELTY - The catheter (300) has a catheter wall that extending from a proximal end (303) to a distal end (306) having first (308A) and second (308B) distal segments. An inflatable balloon structure (305) is arranged between the distal segments. A first lumen port is arranged at the proximal end and fluidly coupled to a first internal lumen adjacent the catheter wall. A second lumen port is arranged at the proximal end and fluidly coupled to a second internal lumen. A third lumen port is arranged at the proximal end and fluidly coupled to an interior of the inflatable balloon structure by a third internal lumen. The catheter wall includes a material that facilitates diffusion of carbon dioxide from outside the catheter wall to the first interior lumen. A surface of the inflatable balloon structure is configured to facilitate passage of oxygen from inside the second internal lumen, through the surface, to a region outside the inflatable balloon structure.
   USE - Intravascular gas exchange catheter for use in a patient's circulatory system to assist in oxygenating the patient's blood and/or in removing carbon dioxide e.g. bicarbonate form and dissolved gas, from a patient`s blood.
   ADVANTAGE - The device avoids pitfalls of extravascular circuits and employs unique approaches to solve boundary layer problem. The device effectively leverages bioactive carbon dioxide enzymes, flow rates, and sweep gas parameters, and employs membranes folded into fins and arranged radially about a central catheter, thus optimizing carbon dioxide extraction. The catheter can be deployed using widely known Seldinger techniques, and has a sufficiently small form factor to be clinically and commercially viable, and can be temporarily implanted in a patient's circulatory system to assist in oxygenating the patient's blood and/or in removing carbon dioxide from a patients blood. It can assist in resolving hypoxemia and hypercapnia, with each variable being controlled independently.

TI Treating material to achieve reducing surface wettability or fluid transport comprises exposing material to composition having solution of polymer portion of polyfluoroacrylate or copolymer of fluoroacrylate and comonomer and carbon dioxide.
AU ENICK R M
AE UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
   UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
GA 201986359E
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a material to achieve at least one of reducing surface wettability of the material or reducing fluid transport through the material comprises exposing the material to a composition comprising a solution of a polymer portion and carbon dioxide for a period of time, where the polymer portion comprises at least one of a polyfluoroacrylate or a copolymer of a fluoroacrylate and a comonomer, and a pressure of the composition is maintained above the cloud point of the polymer portion at their concentration in the carbon dioxide for the period of time.
   USE - The methods are useful for treating the material to achieve at least one of reducing surface wettability of the material or reducing fluid transport through the material.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods provide remediation of cracks or other transport paths with substantial permeability to natural gas and/or other gases and/or liquids but which are too small to be plugged using rapidly-pumped materials having viscosities that far exceed those of the formation fluids; and provide a significant advance in, e.g. the enhancement of wellbore integrity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system for treating the material to achieve at least one of reducing surface wettability of the material or reducing fluid transport through the material, comprising a container including a solution of polymer portion and carbon dioxide, where the container comprises an outlet from which the composition is injected, and the system further comprises a pressurizing mechanism to inject the solution from the outlet of the container at a pressure above the cloud point of the polymer portion; and
   (2) treating (t2) the material to achieve at least one of reducing surface wettability of the material or reducing fluid transport through the material, comprising exposing the material to a composition comprising a solution of a polymer portion which is oleophobic, hydrophobic or multiphobic and carbon dioxide for a period of time, where a pressure of the composition is maintained above the cloud point of polymer portion at a concentration thereof in the carbon dioxide for the period of time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises an isolating system to limit the flow of the composition to a predetermined volume during injection of the composition into a well bore. The isolating system comprises straddle packer system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method (t1) further comprises isolating the cement to be treated before exposing the cement to the composition. Preferred Composition: The composition is used for conformance control and selectively injected into a high-permeability zone of a subterraneous formation. The composition is used for reducing fluid transport through at least a portion the subterraneous formation subsequent to injection of carbon dioxide for sequestration of the carbon dioxide in the subterraneous formation. Preferred Components: The copolymer comprises up to 50 mole% the comonomer which is an alkyl acrylate, an alkyl methacrylate, styrene, benzyl acrylate, phenyl acrylate, naphthyl acrylate and/or cyclohexyl acrylate lauryl acrylate. The cloud point is not greater than 5000 psi, preferably not greater than 1500 psi. The polymer portion consist of polyfluoroacrylate polymers, where the concentration of polyfluoroacrylate in the carbon dioxide is 0.01-20 wt.%, preferably 0.5-2 wt.%. The material comprises a cement. The cement is present in a well bore used in a hydrocarbon recovery process. The cement is positioned outside a well casing in the well bore. The material is a subterraneous formation. The polymer portion comprises at least one of a polyvinyl acetate, a polydimethyl siloxane, a polyfluoroacrylate, and a copolymer of fluoroacrylate and a comonomer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing carbonated beverage using hydrate comprises e. g. disposing carbonated beverage raw material liquid and pure water, and mixing the added raw material containing sucrose thoroughly.
AU ZHOU S
   LI C
   HU Q
   ZHANG W
   RAO Y
   LV X
   LI E
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2019573915
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonated beverage using hydrate comprises (i) disposing the carbonated beverage raw material liquid and the pure water, mixing the added raw material containing sucrose thoroughly, and removing the precipitated impurity to obtain the carbonated beverage raw material liquid, where the raw material liquid has sucrose mass content of 8-10%; and (ii) pouring the carbonated beverage raw material into the beverage bottle, and pouring the carbon dioxide hydrate slurry and the liquid nitrogen sequentially, and sealing the liquid nitrogen immediately sealed after the addition of the liquid nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonated beverage using hydrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbonated beverage using hydrate preparing device, comprising a carbonated beverage raw material liquid supply system, a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry supply system, and a liquid nitrogen supply system disposed in order from left to right, the carbonated beverage raw material liquid supply system includes a carbonated beverage raw material liquid preparation device connected to the pure reservoir and the additive raw material supply device, an impurity precipitation tank is connected to the carbonated beverage raw material liquid preparation device, a raw material liquid filling device connected to the impurity precipitation tank, the raw material liquid filling device is used for pouring a carbonated beverage raw material liquid after sedimentation and decontamination into a beverage bottle, the carbon dioxide hydrate slurry supply system includes a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry storage tank, a hydrate slurry temporary storage tank connected to a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry storage tank, a hydrate slurry filling device connected to the hydrate slurry temporary storage tank for injecting a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry into the beverage bottle, the liquid nitrogen supply system includes a liquid nitrogen storage tank and a liquid nitrogen temporary storage tank connected to the liquid nitrogen storage tank, a liquid nitrogen filling device connected to the liquid nitrogen temporary storage tank for injecting liquid nitrogen into the beverage bottle, a sealing device for sealing a beverage bottle filled with a carbonated beverage raw material liquid, a hydrate slurry, and liquid nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The ratio of the hydrate slurry to the beverage material solution is 1:24. The carbonated beverage raw material liquid accounts for 96% of the total amount of the beverage. The carbon dioxide hydrate slurry is added in an amount of 4% of the total amount of the beverage. The total amount of beverages accounts for 95% of the volume of the container. The amount of liquid nitrogen added is 1% of the volume of the container. The added raw material also includes citric acid, white sugar, edible essence, and pigment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A conveyor belt having a beverage bottle delivery device at the front and a packaging device at the back. The conveyor belt is used for sequentially feeding the beverage bottles in the beverage bottle conveying device to the raw material liquid filling device, the hydrate slurry filling device, the liquid nitrogen filling device, the sealing device and the packaging device. An electronic flow meter is disposed between the hydrate slurry temporary storage tank and the hydrate slurry filling device and is used to quantitatively inject carbon dioxide hydrate slurry into a beverage bottle. An electronic flow meter is provided between the liquid nitrogen temporary storage tank and the liquid nitrogen filling device for metering liquid nitrogen into the beverage bottle.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN US2018214945-A1; WO2018144895-A1; DE112018000318-T5; CN110267909-A; JP2020507452-W; US10675681-B2; JP6952783-B2; CN110267909-B
TI Nanoparticle used as electrocatalytic component in cathode of electrochemical cell, comprises catalytic core component comprising catalytic material encompassed by shell comprising compound having halogen and/or oxygen atom.
AU CHOU N H
   MCKENNEY R
   BROOKS C
   ZHOU N
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   CHOU N H (CHOU-Individual)
   MCKENNEY R (MCKE-Individual)
   BROOKS C (BROO-Individual)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   CHOU N H (CHOU-Individual)
   MCKENNEY R (MCKE-Individual)
   BROOKS C (BROO-Individual)
GA 2018603214
AB    NOVELTY - Nanoparticle (20) comprises a catalytic core component (21) encompassed by a shell (23), where the catalytic core component comprises at least one catalytic material, and the shell comprises at least one compound having a halogen and/or an oxygen atom.
   USE - The nanoparticle is useful as an electrocatalytic component in an electrode, preferably cathode of an electrochemical cell (all claimed) that is used to electrochemically convert carbon dioxide to e.g. useful fuels (e.g. synthetic fuels) or other products.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoparticle exhibits a single binding mode and thus increases the local concentration of carbon dioxide and/or carbonate ions on its surface of the CSN and/or the amount of time carbon dioxide and/or carbonate ions that are adsorbed and/or chemisorbed on the surface of the CSN when compared to nanoparticles without the shell material, while aiding in increasing reaction product specificity, and overcoming problems associated with poor solubility of carbon dioxide in aqueous electrolytes, and enhancing the catalytic activity of and the specificity of the reaction product produced by the electrochemical cell. The shell: has lower binding energy and thus improves the catalytic activity of the catalytic core component by reducing the amount of energy required by a reaction; protects the catalytic core component from corrosion by an electrolyte solution and/or suppress hydrogen evolution reactions; and also reduces or inhibits carbon deposition and/or metal deposition from impurities in the electrolyte, which can reduce catalytic performance over time. The electrode containing the nanoparticle exhibits higher catalytic activity and/or selectivity due to the above desirable characteristics of the shell material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an electrochemical cell comprising an anode, a cathode and a liquid electrolyte, where the cathode comprises an electrocatalytic component including the nanoparticle , where the catalytic core component is capable of chemically reducing carbon dioxide; and
   (2) an electrode comprising an electrocatalytic component, the electrocatalytic component including a nanoparticle having a catalytic core component encompassed by the shell, where the catalytic core component is capable of chemically reducing carbon dioxide and the shell comprises the compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of a nanoparticle.
   Nanoparticle (20)
   Catalytic core component (21)
   Outer shell (23)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound is a fluoride, an oxide, an oxyfluoride or a hydroxide. The compound further comprises a metal atom, where the metal is lanthanum, calcium, cerium, magnesium or manganese. The catalytic material comprises a metal, and/or metal oxide. The catalytic core component is: solid and comprises a first catalytic material coated by a second catalytic material, where the first catalytic material is a metal and the second catalytic material is a metal oxide; or hollow, preferably hollow sphere comprising a metal oxide. The compound consists of lanthanum trifluoride, lanthanum hydroxide, cerium fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, metal fluoride (MeF3) (sic), lanthanum(III) oxide-fluoride, cerium oxy fluoride, lanthanum oxide or cerium(IV) oxide. The shell comprises more than one compound, and completely or partially covers the surface area of the catalytic core component. The nanoparticle has 25-45%, preferably 1-20% shell coverage. The cell chemically reduces carbon dioxide when the cell is energized.
   EXAMPLE - 2 mmol copper nitrate (Cu(NO3)2.2.5H2O) and 1.87 mmol hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) were dissolved in 75 ml water at room temperature, and 0.5 ml ammonium hydroxide (28-30 wt.% ammonia in water) were added to adjust the pH to 10-11. A solution was prepared containing 3 ml of 50-60% reagent grade hydrazine, 1.87 mmol CTAB and 0.38 mmol citric acid in 75 ml water under argon and mixed for 20 minutes before adding the copper nitrate solution. The reaction mixture was stirred for 1.5 hours to maximize copper nanoparticle growth. The resulting copper nanoparticles (50 nm) were isolated and washed. Then the nanoparticles were redispersed in water with 3 ml of hydrazine under an argon atmosphere following isolation and washing of the copper nanoparticles. To the mixture of water, copper nanoparticles and hydrazine was added a solution of lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate (1 mmol in 15 ml water) and a solution of sodium fluoride (1 mmol in 15 ml water). The reaction mixture was stirred for 10 minutes and then centrifuged to obtain nanoparticles.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN113549713-A
TI Controlling carbon dioxide culture box involves obtaining temperature inside carbon dioxide incubator, obtaining heating power in carbon dioxide culture box, judging that culture box has reached stable state of temperature and power, and determining that culture box reaches system steady state.
AU CHU X
   WANG Q
   XIA Y
   DUAN Z
   CHEN H
AE QINGDAO HAIER BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAITE BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2964S
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling a carbon dioxide culture box involves obtaining temperature inside the carbon dioxide incubator, obtaining heating power in the carbon dioxide culture box, and judging that the carbon dioxide culture box has reached a stable state of temperature, and judging that carbon dioxide culture box has reached a stable state of power. The carbon dioxide culture box reaches a stable state of temperature and carbon dioxide culture box reaches a stable state of power and it is determined that carbon dioxide culture box reaches a system steady state.
   USE - Method for controlling a carbon dioxide culture box.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls carbon dioxide culture box, which avoids setting a temperature sensor outside the culture box, reduces cost and failure rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   a control device for a carbon dioxide culture box comprises processor and memory storing program instructions, where processor is configured to execute control method for carbon dioxide culture box when program instructions are run; and
   a carbon dioxide culture box comprises control device for a carbon dioxide culture box.
   (1)
   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature in carbon dioxide culture box is obtained by detecting temperature in carbon dioxide culture box for multiple times, obtaining N temperature detection values, and taking average value of N temperature detection values as temperature inside the box. The temperature in carbon dioxide culture box is obtained by continuously obtaining the temperature in multiple culture box, judging that carbon dioxide culture box has reached a stable temperature state, determining that the carbon dioxide culture box reaches a stable temperature state when difference between two adjacently acquired temperatures in the chamber is less than or equal to a set temperature difference. The heating power in the carbon dioxide culture box is obtained by detecting heating power in the carbon dioxide culture box multiple times, obtaining N power detection values, and taking average of the N power detection values as heating power. The heating power in carbon dioxide culture box is obtained by continuously obtaining multiple heating powers, judging that carbon dioxide culture box has reached a steady state of power, determining that carbon dioxide culture box reaches a stable power state when difference between two adjacent heating powers is less than or equal to a set power difference. The method further involves obtaining a steady state temperature value in the carbon dioxide culture box after determining that the carbon dioxide culture box reaches a system steady state, where difference between the steady-state temperature value and set temperature is greater than or equal to the preset abnormal value then carbon dioxide culture box is controlled to perform temperature abnormality processing. The carbon dioxide culture box is controlled to perform temperature abnormality processing and when steady-state temperature value is greater than the set temperature then it is determined according to the difference between the steady-state temperature value and set temperature, and temperature increase correction parameter value corresponding to the temperature increase preset interval is acquired based on temperature increase correction parameter value, and carbon dioxide culture box is controlled to perform temperature reduction processing. The carbon dioxide culture box is controlled to perform temperature abnormality processing, and when steady-state temperature value is less than the set temperature then preset abnormal temperature drop zone is determined according to difference between steady-state temperature value and set temperature. The temperature drops correction parameter value corresponding to the preset temperature drop interval is acquired, and carbon dioxide culture box is controlled to perform heating processing based on the cooling correction parameter value.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); T01 (Digital Computers)

PN CN112322426-A
TI Preparing blueberry wine by picking blueberry fruit, soaking in fruit cleaning solution, rinsing, crushing, adding fermented raw wine, performing hydrolysis, adding Angel yeast for fermentation, filtering and mixing with original white wine.
AU XIA L
AE ANHUI DUXIUSHAN BLUEBERRY TECHNOLOGY DEV (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202115450L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing blueberry wine involves (a) picking fully mature blueberry fruits and then selecting and removing the picked fruits to obtain blueberry raw materials, (b) soaking the selected blueberries in a fruit cleaning solution and then rinsing the blueberries with water, (c) crushing the cleaned blueberries in crusher to obtain blueberry puree, then adding fermented raw wine and uniformly mixing, (d) adding pectinase to the mixture, uniformly mixing and allowing to stand for 48 hours, at 18-22 degrees C for enzymatic hydrolysis and clarification, (e) adding supernatant into a fermenter and then adding Angel yeast to the fermenter for fermentation at 22-25 degrees C, (f) adding the fermented upper liquid into an aging tank, then allowing to stand at 10-22 degrees C and (g) filtering the aged blueberry wine through multiple layers of diatomaceous earth to obtain a clear and transparent blueberry wine stock solution and uniformly mixing the blueberry wine stock solution with original white wine.
   USE - The method is used for preparing blueberry wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and convenient to control external environment during blueberry wine preparation, guarantees efficiency and quality of blueberry wine and provides high-quality production of blueberry wine in batches.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for blueberry wine production device, which comprises crushing mixing chamber and fermentation raw wine storage tank. A connecting pipe is connected through the bottom of the crushing mixing chamber and the fermentation raw wine storage tank, and the connecting pipe is provided with a pump body and a stop valve. An enzyme hydrolysis clarification tank is also provided on the side of the crushing mixing chamber away from the fermentation raw wine storage tank. A carbon dioxide storage bottle is also provided on the side of the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank near the crushing and mixing chamber. The top surface of the crushing mixing chamber is connected with the output end of the pump body one through first catheter, and a stop valve two is installed on the first catheter. The enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank is also provided with a fermentation tank and an aging tank on the side away from the carbon dioxide storage bottle. The second liquid guide tube is connected through the fermentation tank and the enzymatic clarification tank, and the second liquid guide tube is equipped with a second pump. The third aging pipe is also connected to the aging tank and the fermentation tank, and the third pipe is provided with a third pump. The upper portion between the aging tank and the fermentation tank, the upper portion between the fermentation tank and the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank, the upper portion between the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank and carbon dioxide storage bottle are connected to the air duct. An installation stand is also provided on the side of the aging pot away from the fermentation tank, and a filter is fixedly connected to the top surface of the installation stand. A fourth catheter is connected between the input end of the filter and the aging irrigation, and a fourth pump is also installed on the fourth catheter. The output end of the filter is also fixedly connected with a liquid outlet pipe, and the liquid outlet pipe is also equipped with a three-way stop valve. The lower ends of the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank, fermentation tank and aging irrigation are all provided with a conditioning greenhouse, and the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank, fermentation tank and aging irrigation is also provided with a driving part on the bottom side. The lower side of the carbon dioxide storage bottle is also provided with a booster pump, and the output end of the booster pump is in a through connection with the bottom surface of the carbon dioxide storage bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves selecting blueberries that are fully mature and has no mold, rot, pests or green fruits. In step (b), the fruit cleaning agent comprises 30% alkyl glucoside, 3.7% polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate, 2.85% citric acid, 1.65% honeysuckle extract and sterile water. The step (g) involves uniformly mixing the blueberry wine stock solution with original white wine in a ratio of 1:2.8. Preferred Device: Thermometers are installed on the front side walls of the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank, the fermentation tank and the aging tank. The output ends of the driving member respectively pass through the bottom surface of the temperature-conditioning chamber, the enzymatic hydrolysis clarification tank, the fermentation tank and the aging tank, and are fixedly connected with agitating paddles. The inner wall of the filter is fixed with multiple layers of diatomaceous earth.

PN CN109266694-A
TI Processing multi-product co-production of ethanol            comprises using of corn, sweet sorghum and straw            including e.g. choosing season, crushing fresh sweet            sorghum straw, juicing, using sweet sorghum juice for            ethanol and crystallizing.
AU FENG C
AE FENG C (FENG-Individual)
GA 2019118795
AB    NOVELTY - Processing multi-product co-production of                ethanol comprises using of corn, sweet sorghum and                straw including e.g. choosing season, crushing                fresh sweet sorghum straw, juicing, using sweet                sorghum juice for ethanol, crystallizing fructose                production and sorghum slag for silage production,                using the corn as a raw material for ethanol                production in the non-season season, crushing the                corn, liquefying and saccharifying according to the                ratio of concentrated fermented water to water,                pulverizing the corn granules, mixing glucose syrup                produced in process of sweet sorghum juice and                crystal fructose and performing liquefaction and                saccharification, adjusting solution a suitable pH                value, adding strain, performing fermentation,                transporting the fermentation material into the                distillation apparatus and dehydrating to obtain                fuel ethanol product, subjecting distiller's waste                liquid at bottom to detection and test, qualifying                for production of DDGS for removing generated                unqualified biogas power.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                multi-product co-production of ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens fermentation time and                increases alcohol yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing multi-product co-production of                ethanol comprises using of corn, sweet sorghum and                straw including (i) choosing season (ie, sweet                sorghum harvesting season, from September to                February of the following year), crushing fresh                sweet sorghum straw, juicing, using sweet sorghum                juice for ethanol, crystallizing fructose                production and sorghum slag for silage production,                using the corn as a raw material for ethanol                production in the non-season season, crushing the                corn, liquefying and saccharifying according to the                ratio of concentrated fermented water to water;                (ii) pulverizing the corn granules, mixing glucose                syrup produced in the process of sweet sorghum                juice and crystal fructose according to the ratio                of material to liquid of 1:2-2.1 and performing                liquefaction and saccharification; (iii) adjusting                the solution a suitable pH value, adding strain,                performing fermentation and controlling the                fermentation temperature to be 30-32 degrees C;                (iv) transporting the fermentation material into                the distillation apparatus and dehydrating to                obtain 99.5% fuel ethanol product; (v) subjecting                the distiller's waste liquid at the bottom to                detection and test (aflatoxin, etc.), qualifying                for production of DDGS for removing generated                unqualified biogas power; (vi) passing the                qualified distiller's waste liquid into the vinasse                flash tank through pressure pump, flashing to                remove part of the moisture and heat and entering                the temporary storage tank; (vii) pumping the                temporary storage tank material into the separation                section to obtain filter residue and clear liquid,                pumping the clear liquid into the evaporation                system and sending the filter residue into the                drying system; (viii) mixing and drying the liquid                and slag materials to obtain DDGS product; (ix)                adjusting the sweet sorghum slag to have a moisture                content of 70-75%; (x) adding the silage agent to                the material, sealing it in a bag or using                traditional silage to compact and storing the                silage; (xi) mixing distiller's waste liquid                (aflatoxin, etc.) which does not meet the DDGS                production standard and mixing with some cattle and                sheep feces and residual straw; (xii) using                anaerobic fermentation technology the anaerobic                fermentation of the mixture obtained in the                previous step, biogas power generation, and                obtained by-products are biogas residue and biogas                slurry; (xiii) mixing biogas residue and biogas                slurry obtained in the previous step with some                cattle and sheep feces and residual straw and                composing the organic fertilizer to be applied to                the farmland; (xiv) recovering the carbon dioxide                gas into the buffer tank after recovering the                entrained ethanol through trap and carbon dioxide                scrubber; (xv) vaccum-extracting carbon dioxide gas                coming out of buffer tank by a compressor and is                adsorbed and purified in the adsorption tower;                (xvi) subjecting to sorption and purification of                carbon dioxide gas into plate liquefier,                liquefaction, directly into the rectification                column and eliminating light components in the                column; and (xvii) obtaining food grade carbon                dioxide from reboiler, taking the liquid product at                the bottom of the reboiler, directly adding to the                finished tank for storage, bottling or                loading.

PN RU2771428-C1
TI METHOD FOR RADIOLUCENT RETROGRADE CHOLANGIOGRAPHY IN PATIENTS WITH ALLERGIC AND ANAPHYLACTIC REACTIONS TO IODINE-BASED CONTRAST AGENTS.
AU ZHDANOV A V
   KORYMASOV E A
   LYSOV N A
   LEZHNEV M A
   BARILKO E V
AE UNIV REAVIZ MEDICAL (UYRE-Non-standard)
GA 202277991J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to medicine, namely, to endoscopy, and can be used for X-ray negative retrograde cholangiography in patients with allergic and anaphylactic reactions to iodine-based contrast agents. An endoscopic catheter or papillotome is inserted immediately before contrast study to the mouth of the large duodenal papilla. Cannulation of the bile duct is performed. Sterile carbon dioxide is then injected in the conditions of an operating room using an insufflator connected to the channel of the endoscopic catheter or papillotome. Up to 150 ml of sterile carbon dioxide is injected into the biliary tract with constant X-ray monitoring.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method eliminates the development of complications associated with introduction of iodine-containing radiopaque contrast agents (RCA) into the bile ducts during endoscopic retrograde cholangiography, due to the use of sterile carbon dioxide as a RCA.
1 cl, 2 ex

TI Organozinc catalyst used for catalyzing reaction            for obtaining polyalkylene carbonate from carbon            dioxide and epoxide, is obtained by reacting zinc            compound with aliphatic carboxylic acid and has            specific water content.
