AE SUMITOMO SEIKA CHEM CO LTD (SEIT-C)
GA 2022876505
AB    NOVELTY - Organozinc catalyst is obtained by reacting a                zinc compound with an aliphatic carboxylic acid and                has a water content of 10-10000 ppm.
   USE - The catalyst is used for catalyzing a reaction                for obtaining a polyalkylene carbonate from carbon                dioxide and epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can control molecular weight of a                polyalkylene carbonate to be produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for controlling molecular weight of a                polyalkylene carbonate to be produced involving                reacting carbon dioxide with an epoxide in the                presence of the catalyst and controlling the                molecular weight of the produced polyalkylene                carbonate using the water content of the catalyst                used as an index.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The catalyst is obtained                  by reacting a zinc compound and an aliphatic                  dicarboxylic acid. The catalyst is obtained by                  reacting a zinc compound, an aliphatic                  dicarboxylic acid, and an aliphatic                  monocarboxylic acid. Preferred Components: The                  aliphatic dicarboxylic acid is an aliphatic                  dicarboxylic acid having 2-15C. The aliphatic                  dicarboxylic acid is an aliphatic dicarboxylic                  acid having 2-15C, and the aliphatic                  monocarboxylic acid is an aliphatic                  monocarboxylic acid having 1-15C. The zinc                  compound is zinc oxide and/or zinc hydroxide. The                  catalyst is obtained by reacting at least zinc                  oxide and glutaric acid.
   EXAMPLE - The examples do not provide sufficient details                of specific components of an organozinc catalyst,                so they do not support claims.

PN CN112516077-A; CN112516077-B
TI Phloroglucinol injection comprises phloroglucinol and its hydrate, trimethylphloroglucinol, hydrochloric acid, sodium chloride and water for injection, where carbon dioxide is introduced into injection during preparation process.
AU YIN L
   YAN Z
   LI J
   XIAO L
   YU B
   CHEN A
AE WEPON PHARM GROUP CO LTD (WEPO-Non-standard)
GA 202134990G
AB    NOVELTY - Phloroglucinol injection comprises phloroglucinol and its hydrate, trimethylphloroglucinol, hydrochloric acid, sodium chloride and water for injection, where carbon dioxide is introduced into the injection during the preparation process.
   USE - Phloroglucinol injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The injection avoids use of antioxidants, reduces pH of solution, improves stability of solution, simplifies formulation process, and has stable content, slow impurity growth and long validity period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the injection, involving (a) adding trimethylphloroglucinol, phloroglucinol dihydrate and sodium chloride to water, and stirring until it is completely dissolved, (b) making up the water and evenly stirring, (c) filtering and sterilizing the obtained solution, and (d) potting and sterilizing to obtain a filtered solution, where carbon dioxide is introduced during steps (a)-(d).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The injection comprises 5-20 mg/ml or 40 mg/4 ml phloroglucinol or its hydrate content, 5-20 mu g/ml or 0.04 mg/4 ml trimethylphloroglucinol content, 5-10 mg/ml or 28 mg/4 ml sodium chloride content, hydrochloric acid, and water for injection. The pH of the solution is 3.6-6, preferably 4-5. The residual oxygen content of the injection does not exceed 3 %, preferably 2.52%. The pH of the solution is 3.6-5. The residual oxygen content of the injection does not exceed 3%. Preferred Method: The method involves adding 80% water for injection at 80 degrees C, adding carbon dioxide to make it saturated in the water, adding trimethylphloroglucinol, phloroglucinol dihydrate and sodium chloride, stirring for 5 minutes, adjusting the pH to 4 with hydrochloric acid, adding water for injection, stirring for 10 minutes, filtering the solution through a 0.22 mu m filter membrane and sealing with carbon dioxide.

   ADVANTAGE - The method improves efficiency of carbon dioxide ring addition reaction, and has high conversion efficiency and mild reaction condition.

The alkylene oxide is preferably one or more of ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, cyclopentane oxide, and epichlorohydrin. The alkylene oxide is chosen from one or more of ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, cyclopentane oxide, and epichlorohydrin.X = cobalt or nickel;andY' = cobalt, iron or nickel.Preferred Composition: The amount of supporting electrolyte tetrabutylammonium perchlorate added per 50 mL electrolyte is 1-2 g. The amount of the catalyst (c1) and the catalyst (c2) added to each 50 mL electrolyte is 50-100 mg respectively. The molar ratio of the catalyst (c1) to the alkylene oxide is 1:5000-50000.

PN CN111111660-A; CN111111660-B
TI Preparing photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide to obtain high value added            products e.g. carbon monoxide comprises e.g. crushing            super-enriching plant, subjecting to sand treatment,            and adding distilled water.
AU HU Z
   HE Q
   PENG Y
   ZHENG N
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2020415102
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing photocatalyst comprises (1) crushing                super-enriching plant, and subjecting to sand                treatment, (2) uniformly mixing to add distilled                water to treated super-enriching plant, and                performing hydrothermal reaction at 120-250 degrees                C for 2-50 hours to obtain reaction product, and                (3) centrifuging obtained product, washing, and                drying to obtain carbon dioxide reduction                photocatalyst.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to obtain high value                added products e.g. carbon monoxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple. The photocatalyst has                wider range of applications in field of energy and                chemical industries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The super-enriching                  plant is Dicranopteris, Elsholtzia haichowensis,                  Phyllanthus urinaria, Scindapsus aureus, Fuchsia                  hybrida, Violaceae, and/or Thlaspi arvense. In                  the step (2), the adding ratio of super-enriching                  plants and distilled water is 0.2-200 g:10-2000                  ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2),                  mixing is carried out for 5-10 minutes. In the                  step (3), centrifugation is carried out at a                  speed of 2000-16000 revolutions/minute for 1-30                  minutes; drying is vacuum drying at 70-90 degrees                  C for 12-36 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (3), washing                  is carried out by alternately washing using                  distilled water and methanol for multiple                  times.

PN CN110776991-A
TI Extracting fatty acids from Dioscorea comprises peeling using alkaline solution, adding phytic acid for color protection, pulping, centrifuging, adding complex enzyme, spray drying, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction.
AU DUAN L
   ZHU Y
   MIAO L
   CHEN S
   WEI H
AE CHINA TOBACCO HUBEI IND LLC (CNTA-C)
   WUHAN HUANGHELOU FLAVOUR SPICES CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 202016285R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting fatty acids from Dioscorea comprises (i) peeling using alkaline solution, (ii) adding 0.1-0.3% phytic acid for color protection under microwave conditions, (iii) pulping and centrifuging, (iv) adding complex enzyme to reduce mucus viscosity, where the complex enzyme includes cellulase, mannanase, and xylanase, and (v) spray drying, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to obtain fatty acid.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting fatty acids fromDioscorea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide in the supercritical state, as an extractant, hence less solvent consumption and high product yield, which is cheap and easily available; provides fatty acid with high purity, and no solvent residue; is simple, environmentally friendly, and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution includes sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution having a concentration of 1-3%. The mass ratio of cellulase, mannanase, and xylanase is 3:2: 1. Preferred Method: The microwave frequency is 500-700 w, and the time is 30-60 seconds. In the step (iv), the enzymolysis temperature is 50 degrees C, and the enzymolysis time is 50-70 minutes. The extraction temperature is 45-50 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 35-40 MPa, and the extraction time is 80-100 minutes.

PT P
PN KR2032948-B1
TI Manufacture of carbonate e.g. sodium bicarbonate, involves mixing sodium hydroxide with industrial wastewater to precipitate and separate metal salts, reacting metal salt with carbon dioxide, and separating and purifying obtained carbonate.
AU PARK J W
   WOO K
   YOO Y
   KIM I
   CHOI E J
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 201987909V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbonate involves (i) precipitating and separating metal salts by mixing sodium hydroxide with industrial wastewater generated in the salt purification process, (ii) reacting the metal salt with carbon dioxide, and (iii) separating and purifying sodium bicarbonate carbonate in the obtained product by adding an aqueous carbon dioxide adsorbed alkanolamine solution to the filtrate from which the metal salt is separated and adding sodium bicarbonate separated filtrate to the industrial wastewater generated in the salt purification process to regenerate sodium hydroxide.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonate e.g. sodium bicarbonate from seawater-based industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of carbonate with improved purity of 90% or more and reuse rate of used raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal salt is magnesium salt or calcium salt. Preferred Process: The metal salt is precipitated and separated under the conditions of pH 11 or less. The step (ii) involves reacting a mixture of metal salt and sodium hydroxide with gaseous carbon dioxide, or mixing metal salt with an aqueous solution of alkanolamine absorbed carbon dioxide. The step (iii) involves regenerating alkanolamine by performing nitrogen treatment of filtrate from which the carbonate is separated at 330-380 K. The carbon dioxide adsorption rate of the regenerated alkanolamine is 70% or more of the carbon dioxide adsorption rate of the alkanolamine before regeneration. The product obtained from step (ii) is filtered and dried for 1-48 hours at 330-380 K to separate and purify the carbonate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

PN CN109786836-A; CN109786836-B
TI Preparation of lithium ion battery involves using electrolyte comprising ethylene sulfate and sodium hexafluorophosphate as additive, in atmosphere of e.g. high-concentration carbon dioxide.
AU LI Z
AE LI Z (LIZZ-Individual)
   JINMINGXIN BEIJING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JINM-Non-standard)
GA 201948037Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lithium ion battery involves using electrolyte comprising ethylene sulfate and sodium hexafluorophosphate as additive. The content of ethylene sulfate is 0.5-3 vol.% with respect to the total content of electrolyte. The concentration of sodium hexafluorophosphate in the electrolyte is 0.001-0.02 mol/L. The lithium ion battery preparation process is performed in an atmosphere of high-concentration carbon dioxide, and in an atmosphere of low-concentration or zero concentration of carbon dioxide, respectively.
   USE - Preparation of lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of lithium ion battery with reduced volume expansion ratio in high temperature environment. The increase in internal resistance of the battery is suppressed by forming stable solid electrolyte interface film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Process: The electrolyte is injected into lithium ion battery, and the lithium ion battery is placed in a sealing device including inert gas containing more than 10 vol.% carbon dioxide. The lithium ion battery is charged, and a constant current charge and discharge cycle is performed several times. The atmosphere in the sealing device is adjusted, to reduce the content of carbon dioxide in the inert gas to less than 3 vol.%. The constant current charge and discharge cycle is performed between the charge cut-off voltage and the discharge cut-off voltage several times.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lithium ion battery comprises a negative electrode containing graphite as an active material. The inert gas is nitrogen or argon.

TI Preparing carbon dioxide enriched agent comprises stirring microalgae, yeast, sugar, amino acid, complexing agent and purified water in reactor, stirring, adding activating agent, water absorbing agent and adsorbent, mixing and stirring.

   PENG G
AE HENAN FENGHUI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201944635F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide enriched agent comprises adding 40-45 pts. wt. microalgae, 10-15 pts. wt. yeast, 10-15 pts. wt. sugar, 8-14 pts. wt. amino acid, 2-4 pts. wt. complexing agent and 100 pts. wt. purified water into the reactor, stirring for 1-3 hours, adding 1-3 pts. wt. activating agent, 5-7 pts. wt. water absorbing agent and 7-12 pts. wt. adsorbent, mixing and stirring for 0.5-1 hour.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide enriched agent.

PN CN109608376-A
TI Supercritical extraction of astaxanthin, comprises e.g. granulating ultrafine powder crushed Haematococcus pluvialis, loading into extraction vessel of supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction device and separately heating and pressurizing.
AU DUAN M
   GE F
   LI H
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201936535E
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extraction of astaxanthin, comprises granulating ultrafine powder crushed Haematococcus pluvialis, loading into an extraction vessel of a supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction device, separately heating and pressurizing under extraction conditions, starting cyclic extraction, maintaining constant temperature and constant pressure to the required extraction time, using supercritical carbon-dioxide as an extracting agent in the extraction process, extracting edible oil as an entraining agent, after extraction is completed, discharging separation kettle and calculating astaxanthin extraction rate.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical extraction of astaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good stability, simple operation, short preparation time, high extraction efficiency, avoiding oxidative degradation of astaxanthin, and the astaxanthin soft capsule prepared by using the extract is safe and non-toxic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The edible oil is soybean oil, linseed oil, Camellia seed oil, walnut oil, grape seed oil, rapeseed oil, olive oil, peanut oil, sesame oil or sunflower oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the extraction vessel is 35-55 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C, the pressure of the extraction vessel is 45-85 MPa, preferably 65 MPa, and the extraction time is 30-120 minutes, preferably 45 minutes. The separation vessel comprises a separation vessel I and a separation vessel II, and the temperature of the separation vessel I and the separation vessel II is 35-55 degrees C, pressure of the separation vessel I is 6-17 MPa, and the pressure of the separation vessel II is less than that of the separation vessel I, after the extraction is completed, the separation vessel 1 is discharged and weighed. The temperature of the separation vessel I is 45-50 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C, and the pressure of the separation vessel 1 is 8-14 MPa, preferably 11 MPa. The temperature of separation vessel II is 40-50 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C and the pressure of the separation vessel II is 4-8 MPa, preferably 4 MPa. In the extraction process, 0.5-2 ml of edible oil is added per gram of Haematococcus pluvialis.
DC E15 (Alicyclics.)
MC E10-E04F; E11-K03; E11-Q01C; E11-W

TI Growing graphene on silicon carbide surface useful for graphene and silicon carbide composite catalyst in carbon dioxide photo catalyst reaction of electrolyzed water, comprises pre-treating the silicon carbide powder.
AU GE R
   CHEN W
   MA C
   BAI X
   ZHANG J
   WANG B
   SONG Y
   LI G
   SUN Y
   WEI W
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 201859086V
AB    NOVELTY - Growing graphene on silicon carbide surface, comprises: (i) pre-treating the silicon carbide powder under reducing atmosphere at 600-1200 degrees C; and (ii) further pre-treating the silicon carbide under inert atmosphere, preserving heat at 1100-1800 degrees C to obtain desired growth on surface of silicon carbide.
   USE - The graphene and silicon carbide composite catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide photo catalyst reaction of electrolyzed water (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) graphene and silicon carbide composite catalyst, prepared by growing graphene on silicon carbide surface; and
   (2) use of graphene and silicon carbide composite catalyst in carbon dioxide photo catalyst reaction of electrolyzed water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of silicon carbide powder is 100-1100 nm. The reducing atmosphere is hydrogen atmosphere. The hydrogen atmosphere further comprises an inert gas, where the volume ratio of hydrogen and inert gas is 5:95-100:0. The inert gas comprises nitrogen, argon, helium, neon, krypton and/or xenon. Preferred Method: In the step (i), pre-treating the silicon carbide powder from room temperature with heating speed of 5-60 degrees C/minute for 0.5-6 hours. In the step (ii), pre-treating the silicon carbide, preserving heat at a rate of 2-50 degrees C/minute for 5 seconds to 2 hours. The use method of graphene and silicon carbide composite catalyst comprises dispersing the prepared catalyst on carbon paper, vacuum oven drying for 3-5 hours, taking out, placing into the reaction device, performing ethanol ultrasonication at 10-30 degrees C under normal pressure with gas flow rate of 10-200 ml/minute, where the electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate solution, the voltage bias is -0.4 to 0V, and the light source is xenon light source.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylene carbonate comprises contacting ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide with catalyst under reaction conditions to obtain final product, where the catalyst is supported composite metal oxide catalyst; the weight content of MO is 1-20%, and the weight content of TO2 is 5-30%, and the weight content of SP is 50-94%.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: is suitable for industrial production; and solves the problem of low activity of heterogeneous catalyst.

The weight ratio between catalyst and ethylene oxide is 0.001-1:1 (preferably 0.005-0.5:1). Preferred Method: The reaction conditions includes: the reaction temperature is 60-200 degrees C (preferably 80-160 degrees C) and the reaction pressure is 0.1-10 MPa (preferably 0.5-8 MPa).

GA 2022D3021G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovering method, involves (A)                desorbing carbon dioxide from a reactant (c) by                irradiating reactant (c) of amine compound (a) and                gas (b) containing carbon dioxide with                electromagnetic waves.
   USE - The carbon dioxide recovering method is used                in a carbon dioxide capture device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of lowering desorption                temperature of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide capture device comprising a storage unit                for storing reactant (c) of amine compound (a) and                gas (b) containing carbon dioxide, and an                electromagnetic wave irradiation unit for                irradiating the reactant (c) with an                electromagnetic wave.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (B) of preparing                  the reactant (c) is further included before the                  step (A), where the step (B) comprises a carbon                  dioxide absorbent containing the amine compound                  (a) and the gas (b) to obtain the reactant (c).                  The amine compound (a) has a molecular weight of                  50-1000. The carbon dioxide maximum dissociation                  temperature of the amine compound (a) is                  150&#176; C or less, measured by the                  following method. The method involves heating                  amine compound (a) which has absorbed carbon                  dioxide from 23-250&#176; C at a temperature                  elevation rate of 10&#176; C/minute, and                  measuring the temperature at which the                  endothermic amount due to the desorption of                  carbon dioxide becomes maximum, and letting the                  temperature be the carbon dioxide maximum                  dissociation temperature. The step (A), the                  surface temperature of the reactant (c) is                  35-120&#176; C. The amine value of the amine                  compound (a) is 500-1500 mgKOH/g. The reactant                  (c) is solid at 23&#176; C. Preferred                  Components: The gas (b) is air. The                  electromagnetic wave is at least one chosen from                  microwaves, UV rays, infrared rays, &#947;                  rays, electron beams, and X-rays.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for regenerating carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising (a) adsorbing carbon dioxide using the carbon dioxide adsorbent, and (b) desorbing the carbon dioxide by heating the carbon dioxide adsorbed carbon dioxide adsorbent.

The graphene nanoribbon has a zigzag structure, and average width of 10-100 Angstrom . The metal has cohesive energy of less than or equal to 2 eV. The metal includes lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, or barium. The carbon dioxide adsorption has capacity of greater than or equal to 3 mmol/g. Preferred Method: In the step (b), the desorbing is carried out by heating at greater than or equal to 350 K.

PN CN113860329-A
TI Chemical energy storage system based on synthetic ammonia for converting solar energy and wind energy into chemical energy of hydrogen by electrolysis, comprises photovoltaic plate and windmill connected with electrolytic tank.
AU YAO M
   LI H
   ZHANG Y
   WU J
   FENG T
   ZHANG C
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202210785A
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical energy storage system based on synthetic ammonia comprises photovoltaic plate (1) and windmill (2) connected with electrolytic tank (3) to supply power to electrolytic tank. A hydrogen outlet of electrolytic tank is connected with inlet of hydrogen storage tank (4), outlet of hydrogen storage tank is connected with hydrogen inlet of a mixer (5). A nitrogen outlet of air separation device (10) is connected with nitrogen inlet of mixer, and outlet of mixer is connected with inlet of compressor (6). An outlet of compressor is connected with tube pass inlet of synthetic ammonia reactor (7), tube pass outlet of synthetic ammonia reactor is communicated with inlet of ammonia separation device (8), and gas outlet of ammonia separation device is connected with inlet of mixer. The liquid outlet of ammonia separation device is connected with inlet of liquid ammonia storage tank (9). The shell-side outlet of synthetic ammonia reactor is communicated with inlet of turbine (11).
   USE - Chemical energy storage system based on synthetic ammonia for converting solar energy and wind energy into chemical energy of hydrogen by electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical energy storage system converts the electric energy into the chemical energy of hydrogen by electrolysis, and further stores the energy in methanol through synthetic ammonia reaction, so that energy density is high, stored the energy for a long time, has wide use, which realizes effective storage of solar energy and wind energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for storing chemical energy based on synthetic ammonia, which involves:
   (1) passing electric power generated by photovoltaic plate and windmill into electrolytic tank, passing hydrogen generated after water electrolysis in electrolytic tank into hydrogen storage tank, sending hydrogen in hydrogen storage tank into mixer, passing air through air separation device, sending separated nitrogen into mixer, mixing hydrogen and nitrogen in mixer in proportion and passing into compressor for pressurization, passing hydrogen and nitrogen into tube pass of synthetic ammonia reactor after pressurization for generating ammonia under the action of iron catalyst in tube pass, which is passed into separation device for separation, sending separated ammonia into liquid ammonia storage tank, and sensing back unreacted hydrogen and nitrogen to the mixer;
   (2) absorbing heat emitted by tube side of synthesis ammonia reactor by carbon dioxide working medium in shell side, where synthesis ammonia reaction is an exothermic reaction, passing carbon dioxide after absorbing heat into turbine to do work, passing carbon dioxide after doing work into hot side of heat regenerator, passing into hot side of pre-cooler after exchanging heat with low-temperature carbon dioxide working medium, exchanging heat with circulating water, cooling, passing into carbon dioxide compressor to be pressurized, passing pressurized carbon dioxide into cold side of heat regenerator, exchanging heat with the high-temperature carbon dioxide working medium, passing into shell side of the synthesis ammonia reactor to absorb heat further, and completing circulation; and
   (3) using turbine to drag the compressor, carbon dioxide compressor and generator to rotate through the shaft couplings.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a chemical energy storage system based on synthetic ammonia. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Photovoltaic plate (1)

   Synthetic ammonia reactor (7)
   Ammonia separation device (8)
   Liquid ammonia storage tank (9)
   Air separation device (10)
   Turbine (11)
   Heat regenerator (12)
   Pre-cooler (13)
   Carbon dioxide compressor (14)
   Generator (15)

The apparatus                further consists of unit for adjusting the outlet                pressure of the expansion unit (V5).
   USE - Liquefaction apparatus of gas stream                containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus is economical, and stably                enables liquefaction of gas stream containing                carbon dioxide with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A liquefaction apparatus of a gas stream (3)                containing 35 mol% or more carbon dioxide consists                of compression units, a condenser, a heat exchange                unit (E3), a phase separator (S1), a phase                separator (S2), an expansion unit (V5), an                expansion unit (V6), a pipe for sending the gas                stream to the inlet or to an intermediate stage of                the compression unit for producing a compressed gas                stream (Cg1), a line for sending the compressed gas                stream to the condenser to form a liquid stream, a                pipe for sending the liquid stream by heat exchange                unit, a pipe for sending the cooled liquid stream                into the heat exchange unit to the expansion unit                (V5) to form expanded fluid, a line for sending the                fluid expanded by the expansion unit to the phase                separator (S1), a line for withdrawing a gas from                the phase separator (S1), a line for sending a                liquid from the phase separator (S1) to the unit                for relaxation, a line for sending a two-phase                fluid from the expansion unit (V6) to the phase                separator (S2) and a line for withdrawing a liquid                product rich in carbon dioxide (21,23) from the                phase separator (S2). The apparatus further                consists of unit for adjusting the outlet pressure                of the expansion unit (V5) as a function of the                composition and/or the flow rate of the gas stream,                such as unit for modifying the opening of a valve                constituting the expansion unit (V5). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for liquefaction                method of gas stream containing 35 mol% or more                carbon dioxide, which involves compressing the gas                stream (3), cooling, liquefying, sub-cooling at                least one portion of the liquefied gas stream (11)                to form a sub-cooled liquid, optionally expanding                the sub-cooled liquid or a liquid derived from the                sub-cooled liquid to form an optionally expanded                current, supplying resultant to phase separator                (S1), expanding liquid (17) from the phase                separator (S1), supplying in biphasic form to phase                separator (S2), removing a liquid (23) as a product                from the phase separator (S2).

Preferred                  Method: When carbon dioxide composition of the                  gas stream (3) is above a threshold, the expanded                  current sent to the phase separator (S1) is                  entirely in liquid form, and when the carbon                  dioxide composition of the gas current is below                  the threshold, the relaxed current sent to the                  phase separator (S1) is two-phase. AU DIEHL J
AE GREEN MILL SUPERCRITICAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREEN MILL SUPERCRITICAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8464K
AB    NOVELTY - The outlet flow controller nut (100) has tubular portion whose first end (102) is provided with connection for a first fluid collection port located in the upper portion of a collection vessel. A second end of the tubular portion is provided with an intake port (110) on the top side of the tubular portion. The intake port on the top side of the tubular portion is provided to cause fluids to flow up and over the nut main portion before the fluids are collected by flowing through the intake port.
   USE - Outlet flow controller nut of collection vessel for extraction apparatus (all claimed) for extracting highly pure compounds for food and drugs.
   ADVANTAGE - The extracted product is consistent and of high quality. The efficient amount of extract is obtained from the plant matter. The extraction efficiency is improved. The extracted product is provided with excellent chemical resistance and high performance in food and pharmaceutical applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The connection is selected from the group consisting of threads, brazing, welding, compression fitting, rivet structures, adhesive, tape, gaskets, and combinations. The tubular portion is provided with a cross-sectional profile selected from the group consisting of a circle, an ellipse, a square, a rectangle, a polygon, an irregular shape having no linear edges, and combinations. The intake port is provided with a profile selected from the group consisting of a circle, an ellipse, a square, a rectangle, a polygon, an irregular shape having no linear edges, and combinations. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) collection vessel, which is comprised of an outlet flow controller nut;
   (2) method of operating extraction apparatus with aid of digital computer, which involves providing digital computer with instructions based on the thermodynamic properties of carbon dioxide. The instructions based on the thermodynamic properties of carbon dioxide are derived from the fundamental equation for the specific Helmholtz free energy. The digital computer is provided with a database that includes the coefficient data usable in the fundamental equation for the specific Helmholtz free energy. The coefficient data is specific to the thermodynamic properties of carbon dioxide. The digital computer is provided to cause the extraction apparatus to provide carbon dioxide in a supercritical state for extraction of compounds from a charge material. The extracted compounds are selectively extracted based on the temperature and pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide which is monitored and adjusted by digital computer at frequent intervals using the instructions and the coefficient data; and
   (3) extraction apparatus for extraction of compounds from charge material, which comprises a digital computer provided with processor and a non-transitory computer readable medium. The non-transitory computer readable medium is provided with instructions based on the thermodynamic properties of carbon dioxide which are derived from the fundamental equation for the specific Helmholtz free energy. The non-transitory computer readable medium is provided with a database that includes coefficient data usable in the fundamental equation for the specific Helmholtz free energy, and which is specific to the thermodynamic properties of carbon dioxide. The extraction apparatus is configured to provide carbon dioxide in a supercritical state for extraction of compounds from a charge material during operation. The extraction apparatus is provided to selectively extract desired compounds from the charge material based on the temperature and pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide which is monitored and adjusted by the digital computer at frequent intervals using the instructions and the coefficient data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side cross-sectional view of the collection vessel.
   Collection vessel (16)
   Outlet flow controller nut (100)
   End (102)
   Intake port (110)

AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC ZHUHAI (GREZ-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Mixed refrigerant comprises                trifluoroiodomethane, fluoroethane and component                (A), where the component (A) comprises isobutane or                carbon dioxide.
   USE - As mixed refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed refrigerant has lower global warming                potential value capable of meeting requirements of                automotive air conditioning refrigerant, achieves                safety requirement, has zero ozone depletion                potential value, and does not cause damage to ozone                layer even after using for long time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed refrigerant                  further comprises component (B). The component                  (B) is chosen from 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene,                  trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene, propane,                  propylene and/or dimethyl ether. Preferred                  Composition: The mixed refrigerant comprises                  14-70 wt.%, preferably 45-61 wt.%                  trifluoroiodomethane, 25-82 wt.%, preferably                  30-47 wt.% fluoroethane and 2-20 wt.%, preferably                  2-9 wt.% component (A). The mixed refrigerant                  comprises 9-66 wt.% trifluoroiodomethane, 4-82                  wt.% fluoroethane, 2-21 wt.% component (A) and                  0-76 wt.% component (B), where the weight                  percentage of the fourth component is not zero.                  Preferred Conditions: The mixed refrigerant                  comprises 48-66 wt.% trifluoroiodomethane, 30-47                  wt.% fluoroethane, 2-5 wt.% isobutane and 2-16                  wt.% component (B), or 49-51 wt.%                  trifluoroiodomethane, 43-47 wt.% fluoroethane,                  2-3 wt.% carbon dioxide and 2-6 wt.% fourth                  component. The mixed refrigerant comprises 49                  wt.% trifluoroiodomethane, 43 wt.% fluoroethane,                  3 wt.% isobutane, and 5 wt.% 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoro                  propylene, (or) 50 wt.% trifluoroiodomethane, 42                  wt.% fluoroethane, 3 wt.% isobutane, and 5 wt.%                  dimethyl ether.

TI Photoelectric hydrogen production energy storage            and cold energy recovery coupled dry ice production            device, has air separation device orderly connected to            heat exchangers through nitrogen pipeline for storing            liquid nitrogen in low-temperature liquid nitrogen            storage tank for recycling.
AU WANG J
   LI L
   YAO L
   PENG X
   TAN F
   HAN Y
AE HANGZHOU OXYGEN PLANT GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
   HANGYANG GROUP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2022356894
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a low-temperature liquid                hydrogen storage tank (5) orderly connected to a                hydrogen-nitrogen heat exchanger (7) and two                hydrogen-carbon dioxide heat exchanger (11, 13). A                low-temperature liquid hydrogen pump (6) is                arranged between the low-temperature liquid                hydrogen storage tank and the hydrogen-nitrogen                heat exchanger. An air separation device (9) is                orderly connected to one of the hydrogen-carbon                dioxide heat exchangers and the hydrogen-nitrogen                heat exchanger through a nitrogen pipeline (10) for                finally storing product liquid nitrogen in a                low-temperature liquid nitrogen storage tank (8)                for recycling.
   USE - Photoelectric hydrogen production energy                storage and cold energy recovery coupled dry ice                production device for use in various industries.                Uses include but are not limited to mold cleaning,                petrochemical industry, printing industry, food                freezing, fire extinguishing and medical                sanitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device solves intermittent photovoltaic                power generation problem, ensures better industrial                tail gas carbon dioxide recovering and utilizing                effect and better low-temperature liquid hydrogen                energy utilization rate and reduces energy                consumption during dry ice preparation, which can                realize intermittent photoelectric storage in a                form of liquid hydrogen, thus optimizing recovered                liquid hydrogen cold capacity for producing liquid                nitrogen and dry ice, and achieving target of                energy saving and emission reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                photoelectric hydrogen production energy storage                and cold energy recovery coupled dry ice production                device using method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                photoelectric hydrogen production energy storage                and cold energy recovery coupled dry ice production                device. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

   Mature hydrogen liquefaction unit (4)
   Low-temperature liquid hydrogen storage tank                (5)
   Low-temperature liquid hydrogen pump                (6)
   Hydrogen-nitrogen heat exchanger (7)
   Low-temperature liquid nitrogen storage tank                (8)
   Air separation device (9)
   Nitrogen pipeline (10)
   Hydrogen-carbon dioxide heat exchangers (11,                13)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (12)
   Low-temperature liquid tank (14)

PT P
PN CN111129632-A; CN111129632-B
TI Recycling method of waste ternary lithium ion battery positive and negative electrode mixed material involves subjecting waste lithium ion battery into discharge treatment, separating, ball milling, filtering, heating and concentrating.
AU TIAN Y
   FU T
   YE L
   CHEN J
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV TSINGHUA RES INST IN SHENZHEN (UTHR-C)
GA 2020418259
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling method of waste ternary lithium ion battery positive and negative electrode mixed material involves subjecting waste lithium ion battery into discharge treatment and physical method, breaking, disassembling and separating mixed powder of lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide positive electrode and silicon-carbon negative electrode, battery shell, copper foil, aluminum foil and diaphragm; ball milling mixed powder of lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide positive electrode and silicon-carbon negative electrode, roasting, recycling carbon dioxide gas in furnace reaction process, adding roasted mixed material into diluted alkali solution, filtering to obtain filtrate compound and silicon-aluminum compound of mixed solution, adding carbon dioxide into filtrate, and separating silicon and aluminum filter liquid; and precipitating carbon dioxide in the solution, heating and concentrating to obtain crystallized lithium carbonate.
   USE - Method for recycling waste ternary lithium ion battery positive and negative electrode mixed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and has low cost, high recovery rate and recyclable resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling method of waste ternary lithium ion battery positive and negative electrode mixed material comprises:
   (1) subjecting waste lithium ion battery into discharge treatment and physical method, breaking, disassembling and separating mixed powder of lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide positive electrode and silicon-carbon negative electrode, battery shell, copper foil, aluminum foil and diaphragm;
   (2) ball milling mixed powder of lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide positive electrode and silicon-carbon negative electrode, roasting, recycling carbon dioxide gas in furnace reaction process, adding roasted mixed material into diluted alkali solution, filtering to obtain filtrate compound and silicon-aluminum compound of mixed solution, adding carbon dioxide into filtrate, and separating silicon and aluminum filter liquid;
   (3) processing obtained residue into acid with to dissolve, leaching residue into lithium, nickel, cobalt, manganese and copper, and filtering to remove graphite and separate positive electrode active material and negative electrode active material by solid cathode material recycling;
   (4) adding sulfide into obtained filtrate, adjusting pH value to remove copper impurity copper, and processing obtained filtrate by co-precipitation method to obtain nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide ternary precursor; and
   (5) precipitating carbon dioxide in the solution, heating and concentrating to obtain crystallized lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises detecting nickel, cobalt and manganese content of filtrate, adding cobalt sulfate, manganese sulfate and nickel sulfate at mole ratio of 2.98-3.02:4.97-5.1:1.98-2.01, and carrying out co-precipitation method to obtain nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide ternary precursor; roasting at 600-900 degrees C under the condition of inert gas for 0.8-1.5 hours, mixing and ball milling with carbonate; adding diluted alkali solution with 0.05-2 mol/L alkali and 50-150 g/L solid-to-liquid ratio of diluted alkali solution, reacting at 50 degrees C for 2-3 hours and adjusting the pH value into 6.5-8.5; adding 0.5-2.5 mol/L inorganic acid or organic acid with 50-100 g/L solid-to-liquid ratio, and reacting at 50-90 degrees C for 2-3 hours; and heating and concentrating at 80-90 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carbonate is sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate and nickel-cobalt lithium manganate at molar ratio of 0.11-0.30. The inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid or carbonic acid. The sulfide is sodium sulfide or ammonium sulfide with 1-2 pH value.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is tartaric acid, oxalic acid, malic acid or citric acid.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-E01B3; L03-E06; L03-J01; L04-A01A; M25-A01; M25-E; M25-G01; M25-G04; M25-G08; M25-G11; M25-G17; M25-G19; X16-B01F1; X16-E; X16-M

PT P
PN CN110511421-A; CN110511421-B
TI Preparing polyolefin microporous foam material            comprises e.g.carrying out grafting reaction using of            epoxy group-containing siloxane with a polyolefin            grafted maleic anhydride to obtain the olefin copolymer            grafted silicon-containing groups.
AU WANG Z
AE ZHEJIANG XINHENGTAI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A1465J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyolefin microporous foam material                comprises (1) carrying out grafting reaction using                of epoxy group-containing siloxane with a                polyolefin grafted maleic anhydride to obtain the                olefin copolymer grafted silicon-containing groups,                (2) banburying and blending by taking copolymer of                polyolefin, polyolefin grafted silicon-containing                groups obtained in step (1) into the Banbury mixer,                banburying and blending to obtain organosilicon                polymers comprises a blend of a continuous phase                structure, (3) pressing sheet using a tablet                prepared in the blend of pressing into sheet and                cutting, (4) supercritical molding foaming the                prepared plate (3) into oven, heating to a foaming                temperature and pre-heating foaming motherboard                into the high temperature mold, introducing carbon                dioxide gas, when the carbon dioxide gas reach                saturation in the sheet, where the quick-release                carbon dioxide gas in the mold.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyolefin                microporous foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good foaming effect, short                production period, high foaming efficiency, has                light weight, high intensity and excellent                mechanical property and service performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyolefin microporous foam material                comprises (1) carrying out grafting reaction using                of epoxy group-containing siloxane with a                polyolefin grafted maleic anhydride to obtain the                olefin copolymer grafted silicon-containing groups,                (2) banburying and blending by taking copolymer of                polyolefin, polyolefin grafted silicon-containing                groups obtained in step (1) into the Banbury mixer,                banburying and blending to obtain organosilicon                polymers comprises a blend of a continuous phase                structure, (3) pressing sheet using a tablet                prepared in the blend of pressing into sheet and                cutting, (4) supercritical molding foaming the                prepared plate (3) into oven, heating to a foaming                temperature and pre-heating foaming motherboard                into the high temperature mold, introducing carbon                dioxide gas, when the carbon dioxide gas reach                saturation in the sheet, where the quick-release                carbon dioxide gas in the mold, the sheet quickly                foaming to form a micropore structure of the                polyolefin foaming material to obtain polyolefin                micro-porous foam material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for polyolefin micro-porous foam                material prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  epoxy-containing organosiloxane,                  polyolefin-grafted maleic anhydride to polyolefin                  is (6-12):(22-32):100. Preferred Method: In step                  (1), the epoxy group-containing organosiloxane is                  a mixture of two kinds of linear epoxy                  group-containing organosiloxanes and bulk epoxy                  group-containing organosiloxanes. In step (1),                  the polyolefin-grafted maleic anhydride is                  mixture of polyethylene-grafted maleic anhydride                  or polypropylene-grafted maleic anhydride, the                  grafting ratios of the polyethylene-grafted                  maleic anhydride and the polypropylene-grafted                  maleic anhydride are both greater than 0.6%, and                  the melt indices are greater than 40. In the step                  (2), the polyolefin is a granular polyolefin, and                  a particle size is 1 mm or less, preferably 500                  plus minus 50 mu m. In step (2), the melt index                  melt flow rate (MFR) of the polyolefin is less                  than 5, preferably 2. In step (2), temperature                  for performing the mixing and blending is 100-160                  degrees C, preferably 120-150 degrees C. In step                  (3), the die temperature of the tablet press is                  160-250 degrees C, preferably 180-230 degrees C,                  the pressure of the tablet press is 1-20 MPa,                  preferably 8-15 MPa. In step (4), the temperature                  of the high-temperature mold is 130-170 degrees                  C, the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the                  mold is 6-20 MPa, preferably 8-15 MPa.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)

PN CN109126744-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-adsorbing microsphere            comprises using polyethyleneimine emulsion as a            polymerization template, polyvinyl alcohol as a porogen            and dialdehyde as a crosslinking agent to carry out            polymerization.
AU HUANG D
   WANG X
   ZHANG W
   LIU J
   ZHANG C
   XU W
   WANG F
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 201904953H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-adsorbing microsphere                comprises using polyethyleneimine emulsion as a                polymerization template, polyvinyl alcohol as a                porogen and dialdehyde as a crosslinking agent to                carry out polymerization to preparing                polyethyleneimine microspheres, which specifically                comprises mixing polyethyleneimine aqueous                solution, liquid paraffin and polyvinyl alcohol                porogen, adding surfactant to form a mixture,                stirring in a high-speed disperser at 3000-3500                revolutions/minutes for 2-3 minutes, taking                polyethyleneimine emulsion, slowly adding                dialdehyde solution under magnetic stirring, adding                at a rate of 0.2 ml/minute in a drop wise manner                for 15 hours, centrifuging to remove supernatant to                obtain polyethyleneimine microsphere, adsorbing                carbon dioxide at a standard atmospheric pressure                of 145.9-525 mg/g, after using polyethyleneimine                microspheres under vacuum conditions, and recycling                at 175-180 degrees C for 10-15 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide-adsorbing microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy; and produces                microsphere, which has higher amino group density                than the polyethyleneimine-loading material, has                stable properties, does not have load layer                shedding problem and is easy to recycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide-adsorbing microsphere                comprises using polyethyleneimine emulsion as a                polymerization template, polyvinyl alcohol as a                porogen and dialdehyde as a crosslinking agent to                carry out polymerization to preparing                polyethyleneimine microspheres, which specifically                comprises (i) preparing polyethyleneimine emulsion                by: mixing 8-10 ml polyethyleneimine aqueous                solution (20%), 10-12 ml liquid paraffin and 1-4 ml                polyvinyl alcohol porogen, adding 0.04-0.06 g/ml                surfactant to form a mixture, and stirring in a                high-speed disperser at 3000-3500                revolutions/minutes for 2-3 minutes, (ii) taking                15-20 m; polyethyleneimine emulsion, slowly adding                10-20 ml of 20-25% dialdehyde solution under the                magnetic stirring, adding at a rate of 0.2                ml/minute in a drop wise manner for 15 hours,                centrifuging to remove supernatant to obtain                polyethyleneimine microsphere, and (iii) adsorbing                carbon dioxide at a standard atmospheric pressure                of 145.9-525 mg/g, after using of polyethyleneimine                microspheres under vacuum conditions, and recycling                at 175-180 degrees C for 10-15 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyethyleneimine                  microspheres having a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                  specific surface area of 38.49-256.3 m2/g,                  average pore diameter of 2.5-5.5 nm, and pore                  volume is 0.06-0.78 cm3/g. The polyethyleneimine                  in the step (i) has a molecular weight of                  20000-50000 g/mol. The polyvinyl alcohol porogen                  comprises polyvinyl alcohol 400, polyvinyl                  alcohol 1000 and polyvinyl alcohol 2000. The                  surfactant comprises a mixture of Span 80 (RTM:                  Sorbitan monooleate) and TWEEN 80 (RTM:                  Polysorbate 80) having a mass ratio of 5-6:1.                  Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  adjusting the pH of the aqueous solution of                  polyethyleneimine to 3.0-4.0 by using                  hydrochloric acid solution.

PN CN108788135-A; CN108788135-B
TI In situ preparation of self-generated graphene/titanium core structure material comprises e.g. mixing spherical titanium powder and magnesium chips, pouring into long strip corundum ark, placing in self-propagating synthesis furnace.
AU YANG J
   CHENG J
   ZHU S
   TAN H
   QIAO Z
   LIU W
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 201892856X
AB    NOVELTY - In situ preparation of self-generated graphene/titanium core structure material comprises e.g. mixing the spherical titanium powder and magnesium chips, pouring into long strip corundum ark; placing the corundum ark in self-propagating synthesis furnace, embedding a spiral molybdenum wire of the same length as the corundum ark in the mixed powder, and connecting the electrodes at both ends; sealing the self-propagating synthesis furnace, introducing carbon dioxide gas, charging and discharging, and finally introducing carbon dioxide gas to 0.1-0.3 MPa; using electric welding machine to provide current, maintaining at 60-80 A for 5-10 seconds, igniting magnesium powder to react with the carbon dioxide gas to form graphene product on the surface of the titanium metal ball; collecting the fluffy target product, crushing, washing in dilute hydrochloric acid for 2-5 hours, repeatedly washing with distilled water, pouring off the upper black suspension, drying.
   USE - The method is useful for in situ preparation of self-generated graphene/titanium core structure material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, needs simple equipment, and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In situ preparation of self-generated graphene/titanium core structure material comprises mixing the spherical titanium powder and magnesium chips, pouring into long strip corundum ark; placing the corundum ark in self-propagating synthesis furnace, embedding a spiral molybdenum wire of the same length as the corundum ark in the mixed powder, and connecting the electrodes at both ends; sealing the self-propagating synthesis furnace, introducing carbon dioxide gas, charging and discharging, and finally introducing carbon dioxide gas to 0.1-0.3 MPa; using electric welding machine to provide current, maintaining at 60-80 A for 5-10 seconds, igniting magnesium powder to react with the carbon dioxide gas to form graphene product on the surface of the titanium metal ball; collecting the fluffy target product, crushing, washing in dilute hydrochloric acid for 2-5 hours, repeatedly washing with distilled water, pouring off the upper black suspension, drying; sieving the dried powder through standard sieve to obtain graphene/titanium composite material having core-shell structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the spherical titanium powder to the magnesium chips is 1.5:1-3:1. The particle size of magnesium chip is 100-200 mesh. The mass fraction of the dilute hydrochloric acid is 5-10%. The standard sieve has mesh number of 75 mesh.

PN CN108270014-A; CN108270014-B
TI Preparing a silica-graphene composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid useful for lithium metal batteries, comprises introducing crystalline flake graphite, freezing, drying, mixing, soaking, heating and grinding.
AU HUANG H
   YU J
   ZHANG W
   XIA Y
   LIANG C
   ZHANG J
   GAN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201857424B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a silica-graphene composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises (i) introducing the crystalline flake graphite by Hummers method and freezing and drying, (ii) mixing ethyl orthosilicate to the graphite oxide, adding the mixture and balls into a ball milling tank, vacuumizing, pumping, (iii) finishing the reaction, cooling to room temperature, adding the carbon dioxide gas into the high pressure ball tank, removing the liquid from the ball milling tank, placing polytetrafluoroethylene into hydrothermal kettle, reacting with de-ionized water, placing the product into 0.1 mol/l diluted hydrochloric acid solution, soaking, filtering and drying, (iv) heating the product to carbonize at the heating speed of 1-20 degrees C /minutes under the protection of nitrogen or argon gas, carbonizing, cooling and grinding to obtain silicon dioxide/graphene composite material.
   USE - The composite material is useful for lithium metal batteries (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good batch, small silica particles, uniform distribution and strong transmission ability of lithium ion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a silica-graphene composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises (i) introducing the crystalline flake graphite by Hummers method to obtain oxidized graphene and then freezing and drying, (ii) mixing the ethyl orthosilicate to the graphite oxide, adding the mixture and balls into a ball milling tank according to a certain proportion into high-pressure, vacuumizing the high-pressure milling, pumping the carbon dioxide into high pressure ball milling, (iii) finishing the reaction, cooling to room temperature, adding the carbon dioxide gas into the high pressure ball tank, removing the liquid from the ball milling tank, placing the polytetrafluoroethylene into hydrothermal kettle, reacting with de-ionized water at 100-200 degrees C for 6-60 hours, placing the product into 0.1 mol/l diluted hydrochloric acid solution, soaking for 3-48 hours, filtering and drying and (iv) heating product to 400-1000 degrees C to carbonize at the heating speed of 1-20 degrees C /minutes under the protection of nitrogen or argon gas, where the heat preservation time is 0.5-12 hours, carbonizing, cooling and grinding to obtain silicon dioxide/graphene composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the purity of flake graphite is no less than chemically pure. In the step (ii), the purity of the ethyl orthosilicate is not less than that of the chemically pure, the mass ratio of the ethyl orthosilicate to the graphene oxide is (0.1-5):1, and the mass ratio of the mixture to the grinding ball is 1: (10-80). In the step (ii), mass ratio of the mixture and milling ball is 1:(40-60). In the step (ii), the reaction conditions into a ball milling tank is as follows: the pressure is 60-150bar, the temperature is 21-70?OC, milling speed is 100-700 revolutions/minutes and the ball milling time is 0.5-48 hours. In the step (iii), the hydrothermal temperature is 130-180 degrees C and the hydrothermal time is 12-24 hours and the diluted hydrochloric acid for 12-16 hours. In the step (iii), the hydro-thermal temperature is 150 degrees C and the hydrothermal time is 24 hours. In the step (iv), the heating rate is 5-10 degrees C/minutes, the carbonization temperature is 400-800 degrees C; preferably 450 degrees C, and carbonizing time is 1-5 hours; preferably 4 hours.

PN CN107988129-A; CN107988129-B
TI Improving growth rate of Spirulina by adjusting gene expression levels of key enzymes, culturing modified Spirulina, testing fixed carbon dioxide rate of Spirulina, and collecting sample with high rate of fixed carbon dioxide.
AU CHENG J
   CEN K
   ZHU J
   LIU J
   WANG Z
   ZHANG Y
   YANG W
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201836627T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving growth rate of Spirulina by improving gene expression levels of key enzymes involves (a) adjusting gene expression levels of four key enzymes chosen from bacteriochlorophyll synthase (bchG), geranylgeranyl diphosphate reductase (bchP), dihydrolipoamide dehydrogenase (DLD), and 1-aminopropane-1-carboxylic acid oxidase (ACO) in Spirulina cells by genetic editing techniques to obtain modified Spirulina, (b) collecting and placing modified Spirulina in a photobioreactor, and purifying and culturing Spirulina using several test tubes, and (c) collecting the culture liquid from each test tube every day, testing fixed carbon dioxide rate of Spirulina, and collecting the sample with the largest value of the fixed carbon dioxide rate as improved Spirulina strain.
   USE - The method is useful for improving growth rate of Spirulina by improving gene expression levels of key enzymes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances chlorophyll synthesis pathway and tricarboxylic acid pathway of the Spirulina, and improves photosynthesis utilization efficiency and energy supply of the Spirulina cells, thus increases the growth rate of Spirulina.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving growth rate of Spirulina by improving gene expression levels of key enzymes involves (a) adjusting gene expression levels of four key enzymes chosen from bacteriochlorophyll synthase (bchG), geranylgeranyl diphosphate reductase (bchP), dihydrolipoamide dehydrogenase (DLD), and 1-aminopropane-1-carboxylic acid oxidase (ACO) in Spirulina cells by genetic editing techniques to obtain modified Spirulina, where the gene expression levels of bchG is increased from 120-140 to 310-350, the gene expression levels of bchP is increased from 80-100 to 630-670, the gene expression levels of is increased from 100-120 to 320-360, and the gene expression levels of is increased from 170-190 to 460-500, (b) collecting and placing modified Spirulina in a photobioreactor, and purifying and culturing Spirulina using several test tubes, and (c) collecting the culture liquid from each test tube every day, testing fixed carbon dioxide rate of Spirulina, and collecting the sample with the largest value of the fixed carbon dioxide rate as improved Spirulina strain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves obtaining 300 ml modified Spirulina, culturing at 27 degrees C with a light intensity of 7000 Lux using standard medium for Spirulina, where the inoculation density of the modified Spirulina is controlled to 0.1 g/l, and the continuous flow of air is controlled to 30 ml/minute. The step (c) involves testing fixed carbon dioxide rate of Spirulina by (i) collecting 10 ml sample each day, centrifuging sample at 7500 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, drying the precipitate at 105 degrees C for 24 hours to constant weight, and weighing and calculating biomass density (DW), (ii) calculating growth rate of Spirulina by using specific equation involving a predetermined parameters such as DW2, and DW1, where the DW2 is t2, the t2 is biomass density (g/l) on day 5, and the DW1 is t1, the t1 is biomass density (g/l) on day 4, and (iii) calculating fixed carbon dioxide rate of Spirulina by using specific equation involving a predetermined parameters such C, where the C is Ct2 and Ct1, the Ct2 is an average weight of carbon elements in the Spirulina biomass, when measured on day 5, and the Ct1 is an average weight of carbon elements in the Spirulina biomass, when measured on day 4.

PN WO2018078661-A1; IN201937013329-A; EP3512804-A1; CN110049944-A; US2020078728-A1; MX2019004646-A1; HK40006482-A0; IN363886-B; CN110049944-B
TI Formation of hydrogen from syngas, for forming            e.g. urea, involves converting e.g. hydrogen cyanide            and carbonyl sulfide into hydrogen sulfide, adding            sulfur to maintain hydrogen sulfide content, and            converting hydrogen sulfide into sulfur.
AU IAQUANIELLO G
   SALLADINI A
   ANTONETTI E
AE PROCESSI INNOVATIVI SRL (PROC-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SRL (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SRL (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SRL (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SRL (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
GA 201834185Y
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of hydrogen from syngas obtained by                carbon-matrix waste gasification involves                pretreating (210) syngas by removing particulates                and bulk metals, chlorine and ammonia compounds                from raw syngas, converting (220) hydrogen cyanide,                carbon monoxide and carbonyl sulfide into nitrogen,                carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in presence of                hydrogen sulfide, removing (230) hydrogen sulfide                from syngas and transforming into elementary                sulfur, and removing carbon dioxide to purify (240)                hydrogen. The content of hydrogen sulfide in the                syngas is controlled by adding sulfur waste.
   USE - Formation of hydrogen from syngas, used for                producing urea (claimed). Can also be used for be                used for producing ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces hydrogen from                syngas obtained by carbon-matrix waste                gasification, without emission of nitrogen and                sulfur.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Formation of hydrogen from syngas obtained by                carbon-matrix waste gasification involves (a)                pretreating syngas by removing particulates and                bulk of metals, chlorine and ammonia compounds from                raw syngas, (b) converting hydrogen cyanide, carbon                monoxide and carbonyl sulfide present in the                pretreated syngas into nitrogen, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen sulfide in presence of hydrogen sulfide,                (c) removing hydrogen sulfide from syngas and                transforming into elementary sulfur, and (d)                removing carbon dioxide via the cryogenic unit or                amine step, and pure hydrogen is produced by a                pressure swing absorption (PSA) unit. The content                of hydrogen sulfide in the syngas is controlled by                adding waste comprising sulfur into the                gasification reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for apparatus for producing pure hydrogen                from syngas, which consists of a pretreatment                section for removing from the particulate and the                bulk of metals, chlorine and ammonia compounds, a                conversion section for converting hydrogen cyanide,                carbon monoxide and carbonyl sulfide into nitrogen,                carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, a removal unit                for removing hydrogen sulfide and transforming it                into sulfur, a purification section for removing                carbon dioxide, and an unit for controlling content                of hydrogen sulfide in syngas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the production of hydrogen from the syngas produced                by wastes gasification.
   Pretreatment of syngas (210)
   Conversion of hydrogen cyanide carbon                monoxide and carbonyl sulfide (220)
   Removal of hydrogen sulfide (230)
   Purification of hydrogen (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus consists                  of an acid scrubbing column, an alkaline                  scrubbing column, a wet electrostatic                  precipitator, a liquid stream concentrator, a                  unit for pumping the liquid stream from one unit                  to the other unit, an unit for compressing syngas                  from wet electrostatic precipitator, an unit for                  dechlorination and demetalization of the                  compressed syngas, a high/low-temperature sour                  shift reactors for converting hydrogen cyanide,                  carbon monoxide and carbonyl sulfide, an                  absorbing unit for removing hydrogen sulfide from                  the syngas and transferring it into the absorbing                  liquor, a regeneration unit for regenerating                  absorbing liquor and transforming hydrogen                  sulfide into sulfur, and a sulfur recovery unit                  for separating sulfur by the liquor and exiting                  sulfur from the unit. The stream exiting the                  absorbing unit enters to a final sulfur removal                  unit to reduce the sulfur content. The apparatus                  further consists of a cryogenic unit for removing                  carbon dioxide, the PSA unit for final                  purification of hydrogen, and a water                  electrolyzer for producing hydrogen required to                  minimize or avoid any carbon dioxide emission.                  The PSA unit is a multibed PSA to produce                  hydrogen with a purity of more than 99.9%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (a) involves                  (a1) acid scrubbing, (a2) alkaline scrubbing of                  syngas, and (a3) treatment with a wet                  electrostatic precipitator and a liquid stream                  concentrator. The liquid streams are arranged in                  liquid stream concentrator where contaminants are                  removed as solid form and water is recycled back.                  The step (b) involves (b1) syngas compression,                  (b2) dechlorination and (b3) demetalization                  procedure, and high/low temperature sour shift                  reaction where carbonyl sulfide reversibly reacts                  with water to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen                  sulfide, carbon monoxide reacts with hydrogen to                  form carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and hydrogen                  cyanide reacts with water to form nitrogen,                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The method involves                  removing hydrogen sulfide to reduce the sulfur                  content of the stream leaving the absorbing                  section. In the cryogenic unit, all carbon                  dioxide in the syngas is removed and stored in                  liquid form. The obtained pure hydrogen is                  reacted with nitrogen to produce ammonia, and                  ammonia is then reacted with carbon dioxide to                  produce urea. The carbon dioxide in the liquid                  form leaving the cryogenic or the amine unit is                  pumped to the urea synthesis reactor. The                  hydrogen is obtained by water electrolysis, and                  excess hydrogen is produced by electrolysis only                  when excess electricity is available from the                  grid. Preferred Conditions: The syngas is                  introduced at a temperature of 50-200 degrees C.                  The hydrogen is produced at a pressure of 15-30                  barg. The syngas is compressed at a pressure of                  20-35 barg in the purification process. Preferred                  Components: The carbon matrix waste is chosen                  from a solid municipal waste, a derived waste                  fuel as refuse-derived fuel, agricultural waste,                  urban and/or industrial sludge, biomass and/or                  solid chemical waste. The waste composition is                  added to maintain the hydrogen sulfide                  concentration in the syngas high enough to carry                  a sour carbon monoxide shift.
