PT P
PN US2019353100-A1; US11473499-B2
TI Operating a system involves using solar assist to            capture carbon dioxide and generate of electricity,            heat, and desalinated water, and system comprising gas            turbine system including air compressor connected to            combustion chamber.
AU MOKHEIMER E M A
   HABIB M A A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201997394V
AB    NOVELTY - Operating a system (100) involves using solar                assist to capture carbon dioxide and generate of                electricity, heat, and desalinated water. The                system comprising a gas turbine system (200)                including air compressor connected to a combustion                chamber, and the combustion chamber connected to                gas turbine, and the gas turbine connected to first                electric generator. The system further comprising a                waste heat recovery boiler (WHRB) system (300)                including one boiler and connected to economizer,                an evaporator, and a super heater. The system                further comprising a combined heat and power (CHP)                generation system (400) including a high pressure                turbine, and intermediate pressure turbine. The                system further comprising one solar powered energy                system, a thermal energy storage system, and a                desalination system, the desalination system                including a nano filtration system, a reverse                osmosis system, and a desalinator, the desalination                system (600) connected to the CHP generation system                and the WHRB system.
   USE - Method for operating a system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to operate a system, that                improving capabilities of industrial scale systems                and methods for desalinating water and generating                electricity during operation in a variety of                conditions and, at the same time, minimizing the                energy input and other resources needed to meet the                stated needs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Operating a system involves using solar assist                to capture carbon dioxide and generate of                electricity, heat, and desalinated water. The                system comprising a gas turbine system including an                air compressor connected to a combustion chamber,                and the combustion chamber connected to a gas                turbine, and the gas turbine connected to a first                electric generator. The system further comprising a                waste heat recovery boiler system including one                boiler and connected to an economizer, an                evaporator, and a super heater. The system further                comprising a combined heat and power generation                system including a high pressure turbine, an                intermediate pressure turbine, and a low pressure                turbine connected to a second electric generator.                The system further comprising one solar powered                energy system, a thermal energy storage system, and                a desalination system, the desalination system                including a nano filtration system, a reverse                osmosis system, and a desalinator, the desalination                system connected to the CHP generation system and                the WHRB system. The system further comprising a                carbon capture system and a multiple of                proportioning valves configured to control fluid                flow through the thermal energy storage system and                the one solar powered energy system where the gas                turbine system is configured to generate                electricity and heat. The WHRB system is connected                to the gas turbine system and is configured to use                exhaust from the gas turbine system to provide heat                and provide steam power to the CHP generation                system. The CHP generation system is configured to                produce electricity and provide heat to the                desalination system to produce desalinated water,                and the one solar powered energy system is                configured to provide thermal energy to two                selected from the gas turbine system. The WHRB                includes an absorber, a stripper, and a heat                exchange circuit for circulating a heat transfer                working fluid. The heat exchange circuit comprising                each of a first heat exchanger configured to heat                the heat transfer working fluid and a reboiler                configured to heat a lean carbon dioxide solvent                output from the stripper using the heated heat                transfer working fluid provided by the first heat                exchanger. The carbon capture system being                connected to the WHRB system and the one solar                powered energy system. The exhaust from the gas                turbine system is provided to the WHRB system to                recapture a portion of waste heat before being                provided to the carbon capture system to separate                carbon dioxide from other gasses of the exhaust. An                output of the WHRB system provides the exhaust,                from the gas turbine system, to an input of the                carbon capture system at the absorber. The one                solar powered energy system is connected to the                WHRB system and is configured to provide heated                heat transfer fluid (HTF) to heat water of the WHRB                system. The one solar powered energy system is                connected to the carbon capture system and is                configured to provide heated HTF to transfer energy                to the thermal energy storage system for storage.                The thermal energy storage system is configured to                decouple time of collection of solar energy using                the one solar powered energy system with time of                using the collected energy to heat the lean carbon                dioxide solvent output from the stripper for                resupply to the stripper. The multiple of                proportioning valves are coupled between the first                heat exchanger of the heat exchange circuit and                each of the one solar powered energy system and the                thermal energy storage system and are                electronically controllable to selectively provide                heated HTF to the first heat exchanger to heat the                heat transfer working fluid according to each of                the following conditions, where heated HTF is                provided to the first heat exchanger from only the                one solar powered energy system. The heated HTF is                provided to the first heat exchanger from only the                thermal energy storage system. The heated HTF is                provided to the first heat exchanger from both the                one solar powered energy system and the thermal                energy storage system. The heated HTF is not                provided from either the one solar powered energy                system or the thermal energy storage system. The                method involves using solar energy to heat                compressed air entering the combustion chamber and                recovering exhaust heat from the exhaust from the                gas turbine system using the with a WHRB system.                The electricity is produced using the second                electric generator. The high pressure turbine, the                intermediate pressure turbine, and the low pressure                turbine of the CHP generation system drives the                second electric generator. The high pressure                turbine, the intermediate pressure turbine, and the                low pressure turbine are respective steam turbines                powered by the WHRB system. The heat energy is                added to one of the WHRB system, the CHP generation                system, the desalination system, and the carbon                capture system using the one solar powered energy                system. The brine is heated in the desalination                system using the CHP generation system and                desalinated seawater using the distillation                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                operating a system.
   System (100)
   Gas turbine system (200)
   Waste heat recovery boiler system                (300)
   Combined heat and power generation system                (400)
   Desalination system (600)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Conditions: The method further                  involves storing heat energy in the thermal                  energy storage system for supplemental use in at                  least one of the WHRB system, the CHP generation                  system, the desalination system, or the carbon                  capture system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111487216-A
TI Carbon dioxide flux inversion method, involves            adopting ensemble four-dimensional variational            assimilation method, and calculating assimilation value            of carbon dioxide flux and assimilation value of carbon            dioxide column concentration.
AU CHEN B
   CHAI X
   ZONG Z
AE BEIJING SATIMAGE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020759065
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (101)                atmospheric greenhouse gas monitor (GMI) data. The                carbon dioxide column concentration inversion is                carried out to obtain the observed value of the                average carbon dioxide column concentration. The                height interpolation is carried out to obtain the                carbon dioxide profile observation value. The                simulated value of the average column concentration                of carbon dioxide is calculated (103). The                simulated value of the average column concentration                of carbon dioxide is used as the observation                operator, and the sum of the carbon dioxide flux in                the emission inventory of the RAMS-CMAQ model is                used as the prior value of the carbon dioxide flux                simulated by the model, and the ensemble                four-dimensional variational assimilation method is                adopted. The assimilation value of carbon dioxide                flux and the assimilation value of carbon dioxide                column concentration are calculated (104).
   USE - Carbon dioxide flux inversion method.
   ADVANTAGE - The application of GMI load is realized to                calculate the carbon dioxide flux.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide flux inversion system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                carbon dioxide flux inversion method. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining GMI data (101)
   Step for coupling terrestrial ecosystem                photosynthesis model and respiration model to                simulate regional atmospheric terrestrial carbon                flux exchange model (102)
   Step for calculating simulated value of                average column concentration of carbon dioxide                (103)
   Step for calculating assimilation value of                carbon dioxide flux and assimilation value of                carbon dioxide column concentration (104)

PN CN110530044-A; CN210718189-U
TI Carbon dioxide efficient refrigeration device, has liquefied carbon dioxide storage box whose lower surface is equipped with temperature sensor, where liquefied carbon dioxide storage tank is equipped with liquid level sensor.
AU XU X
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG J
   LI X
   ZHANG Y
AE SHANDONG SHENZHOU REFRIGERATION EQUIP CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2910D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide refrigerating system (1) equipped with an evaporator (3), a carbon dioxide compressor (4), a filter (5) and a condenser (6). The evaporator, the carbon dioxide compressor, the filter and the condenser are connected with each other through a pipeline. The carbon dioxide refrigerating system and a cooling system (2) are fixed with a controller (7). The condenser is provided with a semi-liquid-gas automatic control condensing device. An outer surface of the condenser is provided with a cooling liquid inlet pipe. A condensing pipe is equipped with multiple pressure detecting pipes. A lower surface of a liquefied carbon dioxide storage box is equipped with a temperature sensor. The liquefied carbon dioxide storage tank is equipped with a liquid level sensor.
   USE - Carbon dioxide efficient refrigeration device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can rapidly and effectively cool carbon dioxide by arranging the spiral coil pipe type condenser pipe in the condenser, arranges the liquefied carbon dioxide storage tank below the condensation pipe and changes condensed carbon dioxide into liquid, so that the condenser directly discharges liquid carbon dioxide and performs the gas-liquid separation in the condenser, saves structure of the refrigeration device, conveys the liquefied carbon dioxide to the upper end of the condensation pipe for spraying through the secondary condensation liquid pump so as to improve cooling efficiency of the carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a carbon dioxide efficient refrigeration device.

   Cooling system (2)
   Evaporator (3)

   Condenser (6)
   Controller (7)

AE SHENZHEN YUANXINGSHENG PACKAGING MATERIALS CO              LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202292278M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing waterproof fully degradable                polyethylene (PE) composite type environmental                protection bag comprises (S1) using 15-45 pt. wt.                polyethylene resin, 20-45 pt. wt. carbon                dioxide-based fully degradable plastic, 10-35 pt.                wt. hemicellulose, 1-5 pt. wt. polylactic acid                particles, 15-30 pt. wt. starch, 2-8 pt. wt.                plasticizer, 1-5 pt. wt. compatible solvent, 1-5                pt. wt. lubricant, and 1-2 pt. wt. toner, (S2)                pre-mixing the raw materials, using polyethylene                resin, carbon dioxide-based fully degradable                plastic and starch as the main raw materials, and                hemicellulose, polylactic acid particles and toner                as auxiliary materials, and plasticizer, phase                solvent and lubricant as additives, and stirring                raw materials, and (S3) preparing the extruded                biodegradable film roll, adding biodegradable                modified mixture into the hopper of extruder,                performing double-layer co-extrusion blown film                treatment to obtain a degradable film, and cooling                degradable film and then winding,
   USE - The method is useful for preparing waterproof                fully degradable polyethylene composite type                environmental protection bag, which is used for                packing various articles.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyethylene composite environmental                protection bag has good waterproof performance,                flexibility, mechanical property, anti-impact                performance and degradation performance; and meets                the environmental protection requirements of                consumers and meets the functional requirements of                consumers for plastic bags; and is convenient to                transport goods in production circulation process.                The method is simple, economical, safe, and                hygienic; has various specifications, wide                application range, quality inspection methods, high                yield and strong competitiveness of the products;                and solves the problem of white pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing waterproof fully degradable                polyethylene (PE) composite type environmental                protection bag comprises (S1) using 15-45 pt. wt.                polyethylene resin, 20-45 pt. wt. carbon                dioxide-based fully degradable plastic, 10-35 pt.                wt. hemicellulose, 1-5 pt. wt. polylactic acid                particles, 15-30 pt. wt. starch, 2-8 pt. wt.                plasticizer, 1-5 pt. wt. compatible solvent, 1-5                pt. wt. lubricant, and 1-2 pt. wt. toner, (S2)                pre-mixing the raw materials, using polyethylene                resin, carbon dioxide-based fully degradable                plastic and starch as the main raw materials, and                hemicellulose, polylactic acid particles and toner                as auxiliary materials, and plasticizer, phase                solvent and lubricant as additives, and stirring                the above-mentioned raw materials to obtain a                biodegradable modified mixture, (S3) preparing the                extruded biodegradable film roll, adding the                biodegradable modified mixture into the hopper of                the extruder, where the temperature of the extruder                is 160&#8451;, performing double-layer                co-extrusion blown film treatment to obtain a                degradable film, and cooling the degradable film                and then winding, (S4) slitting, cutting the                degradable film into films with a width of 10-350mm                by a slitting machine, classifying and rolling                according to different widths in the manufacture of                finished bags of different sizes, (S5) printing the                degradable film with the desired pattern through                the color printing machine, (S6) making bags in                batches, and adding degradable films of different                widths into the bag making machine according to the                size of the eco-friendly bags, heat-sealing by                ultrasonic high-frequency welding or heat-sealing                knife to form an inner bag with three sides melted                and sealing on one side, and finished on a                three-side bag making machine to obtain a finished                eco-friendly bag, and (S7) carrying out quality                inspection, sampling products and testing product                quality to obtain product qualification rate, and                ensure product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (S1), the                  plasticizer is glycerol monostearate. The starch                  is corn starch, potato starch, wheat starch or                  tapioca starch. The phase solvent includes maleic                  anhydride, acrylic acid or maleic anhydride                  grafted polyethylene (PE). The lubricant is                  stearic acid, oleic acid amide, erucic acid amide                  or calcium stearate. In the step S3, the                  thickness of the obtained film product is                  0.02-0.1 mm.Preferred Method: In the step (S2), the                  polyethylene resin and the carbon dioxide-based                  fully degradable plastic are accompanied by                  crushing during stirring, and after the stirring                  is completed, heating the mixture, stirring at a                  constant temperature and a low speed in a mixer                  with a heating temperature of 80-90 &#176;C,                  where the stirring speed is 600-1200                  revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 5-15                  minutes. In the step (S4), the tensile strength                  of the prepared degradable film is 40 N/mm2. The                  elongation at break is 280%. The oxygen                  permeability of the prepared degradable film                  under the conditions of 25&#8451; and 50%RH                  is 3.5cm3/m2.24 h.0.1MPa. In the step (S5), the                  printed patterns include trademark patterns,                  product size specifications, advertising slogans,                  production dates, product introductions,                  degradable signs of the environmental protection                  bag, and degradation time of the environmental                  protection bag. In the step (S7), the quality                  detection step of the environmental protection                  bag includes (SS1) testing the printing quality,                  size and thickness of the environmental                  protection bag, (SS2) testing the tensile                  strength and load-bearing strength of the                  environmental protection bag, (SS3) testing the                  physical leak-proof performance and high                  temperature resistance performance of                  environmental protection bags, and (SS4) testing                  the hygienic performance of environmental                  protection bags.

TI Preparing layered nickel silicate catalyst useful            for catalytic synthesis of e.g. carbon monoxide            comprises adding nickel source into deionized water,            adding silicon source, hydrothermally crystallizing,            filtering, mixing roasted powder with adhesive, heating            dried strip catalyst in air atmosphere.
AU ZHAO Y
   LV J
   LI M
   WANG S
   MA X
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202282801F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing layered nickel silicate catalyst                comprises (a) adding nickel source and urea into                the deionized water, dissolving, adding silicon                source into the solution, stirring to obtain the                suspension, (b) transferring the above prepared                suspension to a hydrothermal crystallization kettle                for crystallization for 10-8 hours, (c) filtering                the above product, washing with deionized water,                (d) heating the dried powder under air atmosphere                and roasting, (e) mixing the roasted powder with                the adhesive to obtain the mixed powder, adding                deionized water to obtain strip catalyst, drying,                adding 5-20 g deionized water in each 10 g mixed                powder, where the adhesive is aluminium phosphate,                magnesium phosphate, calcium phosphate,                pseudoboehmite, aluminium nitrate, lanthanum                acetate and/or magnesium acetate and (f) heating                the dried strip catalyst under the air atmosphere                and obtaining the layered nickel silicate                catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic synthesis                of carbon monoxide and hydrogen using carbon                dioxide and methane as raw materials                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has small size nickel metal                particles, still high activity under harsh                conditions of high space velocity, good stability                and service life and shows good anti-sintering and                anti-carbon properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing layered nickel silicate catalyst                comprises (a) adding nickel source and urea into                the deionized water and dissolving, adding silicon                source into the solution, stirring to obtain the                suspension, where adding to every 20-60 ml of                deionized water, 0.02- 0.06 mol of nickel source,                0.10-0.16 mol of urea and 0.5-12 g of silicon                source are added, the nickel source is nickel                chloride, nickel nitrate hexahydrate or nickel                sulfate and the silicon source is silicon dioxide                or silica sol, (b) transferring the above prepared                suspension to a hydrothermal crystallization kettle                for crystallization for 10-8 hours, where the                crystallization temperature is 80- 240&#8451;,                (c) filtering the product obtained in the last step                and washing with deionized water at                90-110&#8451; and drying for 4-15 hours, (d)                heating the dried powder to 400-800&#8451; at                1-3&#8451;/minute under air atmosphere and                roasting for 2-8 hours, (e) mixing the roasted                powder with the adhesive to obtain the mixed                powder, adding deionized water to obtain strip                catalyst and drying, where the mass ratio is powder                and binder is1-9:1, adding 5-20 deionized water                powder in each 10 g of mixed powder, the adhesive                is aluminium phosphate, magnesium phosphate,                calcium phosphate, pseudoboehmite, aluminium                nitrate, lanthanum acetate and/or magnesium acetate                and (f) heating the dried strip catalyst at                1-3&#8451; /minute to 400-800&#8451; under                the air atmosphere, roasting for 2-8 hours and                obtaining the layered nickel silicate                catalyst.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of the layered nickel silicate catalyst in                catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen                using carbon dioxide and methane as raw materials                comprising mixing the catalyst with quartz sand,                placing into a fixed-bed reactor, feeding the raw                materials methane gas, carbon dioxide and                protective gas nitrogen at 700-900&#8451; and a                space velocity of 96000-240000 ml h-1g-cat-1 to                obtain the product hydrogen and carbon monoxide,                where the ratio of: raw materials methane and                carbon dioxide is 0.5: 3-3: 0.5 and raw gas and                protective gas is 12: 1-1: 8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The preparation method further comprises (g)                  heating the layered nickel silicate catalyst to                  750-850&#8451;, under a pure hydrogen                  atmosphere, and reducing for 1-4 hours. The                  crystallization time is 24 hours and the                  crystallization temperature is 150&#8451;. In                  the step (d) and (f) roasting process: a heating                  rate of 2&#8451;/minute, a roasting                  temperature of 550&#8451;, and an air                  atmosphere roasting time of 4 hours. In the step                  (g), in the reducing process, the reducing                  temperature is 800&#8451;, the pure hydrogen                  atmosphere reducing time is 2 hours and the                  hydrogen flow rate is 30 ml/minute.In the layered nickel silicate catalyst, the                  nickel loading is 25-35%, and the nickel particle                  size is 4-5 nm. In the roasting process, the                  mixing ratio of roasted powder and binder is 5:5,                  and 6-10 g of deionized water is added to every                  10 g of mixed powder.

TI Extraction of carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas involves performing gas desulfurization and phosphorus removal from blast furnace gas containing saturated water and carbon monoxide, and mixing carbon monoxide removal gas with air.
AU YANG H
   NING P
   DA N
   TIAN S
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 2020C4331C
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas involves performing gas desulfurization and phosphorus removal from blast furnace gas containing saturated water, 20-30 wt.% carbon monoxide, 60-70 wt.% nitrogen, 0.5-3 wt.% hydrogen, and 0.5-3 wt.% carbon dioxide, pressurizing the gas at 49 kPa or more, setting group (1) of temperature swing adsorption dehydration, dehydrating raw material gas dew point of -15 degrees C to 25 degrees C to ensure the maximum adsorption capacity of carbon monoxide adsorbent, performing pressure adsorption, at least two adsorption towers in each group, setting group (2) of pressure swing adsorption, the adsorption working pressure of the adsorption tower is -49 kPa to 49 kPa, starting from -49 kPa, continuously sending the dehydrated raw material gas to boosting series adsorption tower at more than 49-35 kPa, the hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide mixed gas comprising less than 0.5 wt.% carbon monoxide from the stage (s2) is called carbon monoxide removal gas.
   USE - Extraction of carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient extraction of carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of carbon monoxide from blast furnace gas involves performing gas desulfurization and phosphorus removal from blast furnace gas containing saturated water, 20-30 wt.% carbon monoxide, 60-70 wt.% nitrogen, 0.5-3 wt.% hydrogen, and 0.5-3 wt.% carbon dioxide, pressurizing the gas at 49 kPa or more, setting group (1) of temperature swing adsorption dehydration, dehydrating raw material gas dew point of -15 degrees C to 25 degrees C to ensure the maximum adsorption capacity of carbon monoxide adsorbent, performing pressure adsorption, at least two adsorption towers in each group, setting group (2) of pressure swing adsorption, the adsorption working pressure of the adsorption tower is -49 kPa to 49 kPa, starting from -49 kPa, continuously sending the dehydrated raw material gas to boosting series adsorption tower at more than 49-35 kPa, the hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide mixed gas comprising less than 0.5 wt.% carbon monoxide from the stage (s2) is called carbon monoxide removal gas, mixing the carbon monoxide removal gas with air and sending to the heater at 1:0.2-0.6, and then sending to temperature swing adsorption regeneration adsorption tower after heating, sending the regeneration gas to the catalytic burner to catalytically combust the carbon monoxide and hydrogen to the combustible gas less than 0.1%, using a combustion tail gas for heating by the heater to remove carbon monoxide gas, and then discharging directly, after completing the adsorption of the stage (s1) of the adsorption tower, discharging the gas in the tower with 15-30 wt.% carbon monoxide which is called the downstream gas and sending to the buffer tank for use, and then using a vacuum pump to pump the stage (s1) of the adsorption tower to extract air which is enriched carbon monoxide gas, reaching the concentration of carbon monoxide gas to 90-98% as enriched gas, after completing the stage (s2) of adsorption tower adsorption, discharging the carbon monoxide in the gas discharged from the tower, mixing with 5% carbon monoxide removal gas in the stage (s2), evacuating the stage (s2), using the buffer tank to flush the adsorption tower along with the air, the maximum outlet pressure is 49 kPa, directly sending to the stage (s1) of pressure swing adsorption tower in adsorption working state, realizing the blast furnace gas carbon monoxide, and there is no almost combustible gas in the exhaust gas.

PN CN111040915-A
TI Making mulberry wine, involves squeezing mulberry, adding sulfite, sterilizing tank, injecting mulberry juice, adding pectinase, clarifying, adding active dry yeast, activating, fermenting, aging, freezing, sterilizing and filtering.
AU HE M
AE HE M (HEMM-Individual)
GA 2020346053
AB    NOVELTY - Method for making mulberry wine, involves (i) squeezing mulberry to obtain mulberry juice, and adding sulfite for juice extraction process, (ii) sterilizing first fermentation tank, filling fermentation tank with carbon dioxide gas to exhaust air, injecting mulberry juice, adding pectinase, sealing tank, clarifying, transferring supernatant to second fermentation tank, adding active dry yeast, activating, adding active dry yeast to supernatant, adding yeast polysaccharide for expansion, fermenting to obtain seed liquid, adding seed liquid to supernatant in second fermentation tank for fermentation, (iii) transferring to aging tank for post-fermentation, (iv) pouring wine to tank to remove lees, and transferring to aging tank for aging, (v) filling aging tank with nitrogen to remove carbon dioxide dissolved in wine, and mixing with bentonite, and pouring to cans, (vi) blending clarified wine, (vii) filtering, clarifying, and aging, and (viii) freezing wine, sterilizing and filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for making mulberry wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the fermentation process, and adjusts the content of methanol and grade alcohol in the mulberry wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for making mulberry wine, involves (i) squeezing mulberry to obtain mulberry juice, and adding sulfite to the mulberry juice for juice extraction process, where the amount of sulfite added is 60-80 parts per million (ppm), (ii) sterilizing first fermentation tank, filling the first fermentation tank with carbon dioxide gas to exhaust the air in the tank, injecting the mulberry juice obtained in step (i), after the juice injection is completed, adding 20-30 ppm pectinase, sealing the first fermentation tank for uniformly mixing, controlling the temperature in fermentation tank at 0-5 degrees C to clarify the mulberry juice, transferring the supernatant from the first fermentation tank to second fermentation tank, adding 200-250 g active dry yeast per ton of supernatant, activating, adding 300 times active dry yeast to the supernatant, adding yeast polysaccharide for expansion, stirring and enhancing aeration, fermenting at 15-18 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain seed liquid, where the added amount of the yeast polysaccharide is 200-250 ppm, adding the seed liquid to the supernatant at 10-13 degrees C in the second fermentation tank for inoculation fermentation, and controlling the inoculation fermentation at 10-13 degrees C, (iii) when the physical and chemical indexes of the inoculated fermentation reach the requirements, transferring to aging tank for post-fermentation, and when the physical and chemical indexes of the inoculated fermentation meet the required standards such the total sugar in the supernatant after inoculation and fermentation is less than or equal to 5 g/l, specific gravity of supernatant after inoculation and fermentation is less than or equal to 0.998, and less than or equal to 5 vol.% alcohol content of supernatant after inoculation and fermentation is less than or equal to 6 vol.%, (iv) one week after the post-fermentation, pouring the wine body to tank to remove lees, and transferring to the aging tank for aging, (v) after the wine in the aging tank is calm, filling the aging tank with nitrogen to remove carbon dioxide dissolved in the wine, and mixing with 700 ppm bentonite for fining, reducing the degree to 15, and pouring to cans to remove the lees, (vi) blending the clarified wine obtained in step (v), (vii) adjusting the wine obtained in step (vi) for one week, filtering, clarifying, and aging, and (viii) subjecting the wine to freezing treatment for one week, after the stability test of the wine is qualified, sterilizing and filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The activation procedure of the active dry yeast in step (ii) is carried out by taking 8-10 times active dry yeast, adjusting the temperature to 35-40 degrees C, adding the required active dry yeast to the sugar syrup, uniformly mixing and leaving still for 15 minutes, agitating for activation, adjusting the activated yeast liquid to 25 degrees C for 5 minutes with the supernatant, and adjusting to 15 degrees C for 5 minutes to complete the activation of active dry yeast, where the sugar content of the sugar syrup is 5%.

PN CN112979606-A
TI Preparing 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative involves reacting oxirane derivative with the carbon dioxide gas under the synergistic catalysis of the phenylboronic acid and tetrabutylammonium bromide to obtain product.
AU DU H
   CHEN J
   FENG X
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 202172145M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative (I) involves reacting oxirane derivative (II) with carbon dioxide gas under the synergistic catalysis of phenylboronic acid and tetrabutylammonium bromide to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has yield reaches 97%, in simple manner under mild condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative of formula (I) involves reacting oxirane derivative of formula (II) with carbon dioxide gas under the synergistic catalysis of phenylboronic acid and tetrabutylammonium bromide to obtain product..
   R1=alkyl group, substituted alkyl group, substituted alkoxy group, phenyl group, ester group, alkenyl group; and
   R2=hydrogen atom, a methyl group or a chloromethyl group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl group in the substituted alkyl group is a 1-10C linear alkyl group. The substituent in the substituted alkyl group is halogen or hydroxy. The alkoxy in the substituted alkoxy group is CH2O. The substituent in the substituted alkoxy group is selected from chain/straight chain alkyl, phenyl, 4-tert-butylphenyl, benzyl, methacryloyl and allyl. The alkenyl group is -CnH2nCH=CH2 alkenyl group, where n is an integer of 0-2. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the phenylboronic acid, tetrabutylammonium bromide and oxirane derivative of formula (II) is 1:1:(40-100)in sequence. The reaction is carried out in an autoclave, and the pressure of carbon dioxide is 10-20 bar. The temperature of the reaction is 60-100 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-12 hours. The method involves purifying the obtained compound represented by 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative of formula (I) or 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative of formula (III) or 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative derivative of formula (IV) by column chromatography, in the column chromatography, the specification of the chromatography column used is 30mm in diameter x 20cm in height. The column packing used is 200-300 mesh silica gel. The eluent is a mixed solution of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate with a volume ratio of 20:1 to a mixed solution of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate with a volume ratio of 1:1. The 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative of formula (III) is prepared by reacting oxirane derivative of formula (V) with carbon dioxide gas under the synergistic catalysis of benzene boric acid and tetrabutylammonium bromide to obtain product. The 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative of formula (IV) is prepared by reacting oxirane derivative of formula (VI) with carbon dioxide gas under the synergistic catalysis of benzene boric acid and tetrabutyl ammonium bromide to obtain product.R=phenyl..
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 22 compounds of 1,3-dioxolan-2-one derivative are specifically claimed, such as,,,,.

PN CN112663323-A; CN112663323-B
TI Preparing MXene electromagnetic shielding fabric            involves dipping pre-treated flexible fabric substrate            uniformly in MXene dispersion, drying, and then            annealing under carbon dioxide conditions.
AU HOU C
   LIU R
   WU Q
   WANG H
   LI Y
   ZHANG Q
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 202150982H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing MXene electromagnetic shielding                fabric involves dipping pre-treated flexible fabric                substrate uniformly in MXene dispersion, drying,                and then annealing under carbon dioxide conditions.                The process parameters of annealing treatment                involves putting the dry fabric in a rapid heating                furnace, in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, heating up                to 60 degrees C at a rate of 10-20 degrees                C/second, preheating for 20-40 minutes, and then                using the same rate to increased to 60-80 degrees                C, processing for 24-36 hours, and maintaining the                heat after rapid heating furnace turned off for 2-4                hours.
   USE - Method for preparing MXene electromagnetic                shielding fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides MXene electromagnetic                shielding fabric, which has good flexibility, water                washing resistance, and electromagnetic shielding                characteristics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: Preparing MXene                  electromagnetic shielding fabric involves using                  the flexible fabric substrate as one of non-woven                  fabric, pure cotton cloth, ketone ammonia fiber                  cloth, pure linen cloth, and bamboo fiber cloth.                  Preferred Processes: The MXene dispersion is                  obtained by adding MAX phase titanium aluminum                  carbide to an etching solution of lithium                  fluoride, hydrochloric acid and ultrapure water,                  stirring the reaction, centrifuging, dispersing                  the obtained precipitate in deionized water,                  centrifuging again, and collecting the                  dispersion.

PT P
PN CN111100108-A; CN111100108-B
TI Removing impurity components in crude lactide, involves crushing coarse lactide, introducing carbon dioxide for extraction and impurity removal, cooling and separating, and performing melt crystallization.
AU SUN Q
   ZHANG T
   GAO D
   WANG P
   ZHU H
   YAO X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202042186W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing impurity components in crude lactide, involves (a) crushing coarse lactide, placing crushed lactide in reactor for sealing and introducing carbon dioxide for performing extraction and impurity removal, and (b) after the extraction process is completed, reducing pressure, cooling and separating to obtain lactide, and performing melt crystallization to obtain the lactide product.
   USE - Method for removing impurity components in crude lactide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts supercritical carbon dioxide extraction instead of distillation process to remove impurities in crude lactide, which can effectively remove impurities and pigments, reduces the side reaction such as coking, oxidation, polymerization and internal racemization of lactide caused by high temperature, and improves the yield and quality of lactide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crude lactide in step (a) is crude lactide prepared by performing polycondensation and depolymerization using L/D-lactate as raw material, where the purity of L/D-lactide is 78-90%, the content of meso-lactide is not less than 3.7%, the content of lactic acid is not less than 3.4%, and the content of dimer and trimer is not less than 1%. The method involves adding low-boiling ester solvent and/or chloroform while introducing carbon dioxide. The method involves adding 1-10% petroleum ether and/or carbon disulfide while adding low-boiling ester solvent and/or chloroform. The step (b) involves lowering pressure and then lowering temperature, and reducing to normal pressure and room temperature. Preferred Property: The diameter of crushed crude lactide in step (a) is 35-100 mesh. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the flow rate of carbon dioxide in reactor is 15-30 g/minute. The step (a) involves extracting at 36-60 degrees C under extraction pressure of 9-15 MPa for 30-150 minutes. The flow rate of low-boiling ester solvent and/or chloroform is 1-10% based on carbon dioxide flow rate. The melt crystallization process in step (b) involves performing cooling crystallization and heating and sweating process, where the cooling rate of cooling crystallization process is controlled at 0.1-2 degrees C/minute, the initial crystallization temperature is carried out at 83.9-92 degrees C, final temperature is 75-90 degrees C, heating rate of sweating process is controlled at 0.1-5 degrees C/minute, and the final temperature of sweating process is 93-95 degrees C. Preferred Components: The low-boiling ester solvent comprises ethyl acetate, methyl acetate, propyl acetate and/or isopropyl acetate.

PT P
PN CN110938846-A; CN110938846-B
TI Preparation of electrode for carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction, involves soaking electrode base material in plating solution after removing impurities, and performing electrochemical deposition under inert atmosphere.
AU QIU Y
   LI X
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG T
   YAO P
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202027527Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode involves soaking electrode base material in plating solution after removing impurities, and performing electrochemical deposition under protection of inert atmosphere and stirring conditions to obtain electrode with micron copper particles grown on surface. The plating solution comprises main salt and additive. The additive comprises additive (A) and additive (B). The additive (A) is chelate compound with divalent copper. The additive (B) is macromolecular polymer.
   USE - Preparation of electrode for carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction for ethylene production (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode improves the coverage of carbon dioxide on the electrode surface and provides a favorable environment for the dimerization reaction and hydrogenation reaction, and has the effect of inhibiting the side reaction of hydrogen evolution, and has high ethylene selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The main salt is a soluble copper salt comprising one of copper sulfate, copper chloride, and copper nitrate. The acid corresponding to the main salt is used to adjust the pH of plating solution. The base material is a carbon-based porous material or flaky copper. The carbon-based porous material is one of carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt. Preferred Composition: The plating solution comprises 0.01-0.5 M, preferably 0.05-0.2 M main salt, 0.1-10 mM, preferably 0.5-5 mM additive (A), and 5-500 ppm, preferably 20-100 ppm additive (B). The acid concentration is 2-18 M. Preferred Property: The pH of the plating solution is 0.2-3. The carbon based material has porosity of not less than 70%. The flaky copper has thickness of not more than 0.5 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The chelate compound is one or more of sodium triaminoacetate, EDTA, tartaric acid, sodium alginate, sodium edetate, and sodium triammonium phosphate. The chelate compound is preferably one or more of amino carboxylic acid, amino carboxylate, hydroxy carboxylate, and organic phosphonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The macromolecular polymer is polyethylene glycol or/and polyvinylpyrrolidone. Preferred Composition: The macromolecular polymer has average molecular weight of 3000-60000.

TI Method for coal classification conversion gasification involves using first heat to heat coal to produce coke and coke oven gas, second heat is used to heat the coke and carbon dioxide to partially gasifies the coke.
AU GAO L
   JIN H
   HONG H
   LI Z
   HE S
   LI S
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 202025889D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using first heat to heat coal to produce coke and coke oven gas. The second heat is used to heat the coke and carbon dioxide to partially gasifies the coke to produce carbon monoxide. The ungasified coke is burned with hot air to generate heat. The heat includes first heat and second heat. The portion of carbon monoxide and water vapor are reacted to generate the carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   USE - Method for coal classification conversion gasification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coal grading and converting gasification device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of method for coal classification conversion gasification. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN KR2019029167-A
TI Softening hard water by dewatering hard water into vessel, supplying carbon dioxide to hard water dewatered in vessel to provide carbonation reaction, and waiting for set time for carbonation reaction to diffuse into hard water in vessel.
AU AHN M K
AE AHN M K (AHNM-Individual)
GA 201927713B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for softening hard water involves (a) dewatering the hard water having a pH equal to or higher than the set value into the vessel, (b) supplying carbon dioxide to the hard water dewatered in the vessel to provide a carbonation reaction, and (c) waiting for a set time for carbonation reaction to diffuse into the hard water dewatered in the vessel, where the step (b) is repeated for several times, and the supply amount of the carbon dioxide is increased in a step wise manner for every time as the number of times of repeating the step (b) is increased.
   USE - The method is useful for softening hard water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves supplying 5-9 g carbon dioxide to 1 l of hard water when the first-order carbonation reaction proceeds, supplying 12-16 g carbon dioxide to the hard water when the second-order carbonation reaction proceeds, and supplying 19-24 g carbon dioxide to the hard water when the third-order carbonation reaction proceeds. The step (b) is performed by using an injection device for injecting carbon dioxide into the container in which the hard water is desiccated, where the injection device comprises a main portion on which the container for desalting the hard water is seated, a gear portion provided in the main portion, connecting the main portion to the main portion in a slant manner, and rotating the main portion in an upright direction to seat on the main portion, a head portion provided in the main portion and for injecting carbon dioxide into a connection portion of the vessel, and a gas tank installed in the main portion and for storing carbon dioxide to supply carbon dioxide to the head portion.

PT P
PN CN108424810-A
TI Preparing peanut oil comprises pressing peeled peanut kernel to obtain peanut oil A and peanut residue, adding ethanol to the peanut residue, stirring, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peanut oil B.
AU OUYANG D
AE ANHUI LAOOUMOFANG FOOD TRADE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018673368
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing peanut oil comprises (i) pressing the peeled peanut kernel to obtain peanut oil A and peanut residue, (ii) adding ethanol to the peanut residue, stirring for 20-30 minutes, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peanut oil B, and (iii) mixing peanut oil A and peanut oil B to obtain mixed oil, and refining the mixed oil to obtain the peanut oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing peanut oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts combined production process of pressing and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; improves purity and oil yield of peanut oil; has substantially no loss of nutrient composition of peanut oil; and produces natural green edible oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 28-32 MPa, the extraction temperature is 45-55 degrees C, the extraction time is 50-80 minutes, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 23-30 l/hour. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction uses a mixture of diethyl ether and methanol as an cosolvent, and the cosolvent has a weight ratio of diethyl ether to methanol of 0.3-0.5:1. The flow rate of the cosolvent is 10-15% of the carbon dioxide flow rate. In the step (ii), the amount of ethanol is 0.5-0.8 times the weight of the peanut slag. In the step (i), the pressing comprises feeding the peeled peanut kernel into a screw press for pressing, the pressing temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the pressing frequency is 4-6 times, and the rotary shaft speed of the screw press is 30-40 revolution/minute (rpm). In the step (i), the moisture content of the peeled peanut kernel is 7-9%. In the step (iii), purifying comprises adding adsorbent to the mixed oil, stirring for 30-40 minutes, and filtering to obtain peanut oil. The raw materials of the adsorbent include diatomaceous earth, montmorillonite, activated carbon, diatomaceous earth, montmorillonite and activated carbon in a weight ratio of 6-8:6-7:1. The adsorbent is used in an amount of 0.8-1.5% by weight of the blended hair oil.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-C01; D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/06; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/10; C11B-001/04

AU LAZHKO A E

GA 2018604414
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to medicine, namely to tissue engineering and regenerative medicine, and is intended to restore various tissue defects. To harden the hydrogels, the hydrogel scaffold is processed in a reactor in a supercritical carbon dioxide environment at a temperature above room temperature, followed by a lowering of the temperature and a gradual decrease in pressure in the reactor to atmospheric pressure. Processing in the reactor is carried out for 1-2hours at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C and a pressure of 5-15MPa. Gradual decrease in pressure of carbon dioxide after treatment is carried out for 0.5-2hours, while the flow rate of carbon dioxide flowing around the scaffold is maintained in the range of 0.05-1mm/s.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Use of the invention makes it possible to increase the strength of the hydrogel scaffold.

PN CN113565684-A; CN215860603-U
TI Generating system for realizing new energy storage by liquefying carbon dioxide, has liquefied carbon dioxide storage tank whose output end is divided into two paths, where one path is directly sent to carbon dioxide gas turbine.
AU XIA L
   HU M
   GAO S
   XIAO J
   WANG Y
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C5834C
AB    NOVELTY - Generating system comprises a carbon dioxide liquefied compressor (2) connected with a generator (1), wind power and photovoltaic power generation through the generator to drive the carbon dioxide gas compressor to liquefy carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is entered into a liquified carbon dioxide storage tank (4) for storage. The output end of the liquefiated carbon dioxide tank is divided into two paths. One path is directly sent to a carbon dioxide gas turbine (7) for working, and the other path is heated by a heat exchanger (6) and sent to the carbon carbon dioxide turbine for working. After the carbon gas is cooled by the heat exchangers, it is cooled and returned to the compressor of carbon dioxide by a circulating pump (9).
   USE - Generating system for realizing new energy storage by liquefying carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can eliminate new energy in large scale, and store the energy, and can continuously and stably output electric power according to need, and output the stable power and frequency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the generating system for realizing new energy storage by liquefying carbon dioxide.
   Generator (1)
   Carbon dioxide liquefied compressor (2)
   Liquified carbon dioxide storage tank (4)
   Heat exchanger (6)
   Carbon dioxide gas turbine (7)
   Circulating pump (9)

PN JP2022131418-A
TI Producing silica by mixing silicon-containing            waste and aqueous solution having sodium hydroxide,            fractionating extract containing silicate and residue            from slurry, and contacting extract with carbon            dioxide-containing gas to crystallize silica            particles.
AU YAMAGUCHI A
   SAKAI T
   WASHIO T
   MARUYA E
AE UBE MITSUBISHI CEMENT CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2022B66582
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing silica, involves (a)                mixing a silicon-containing waste and an aqueous                solution having a sodium hydroxide concentration of                1-24 %mass to prepare a slurry containing a                silicate, (b) fractionating the extract containing                the silicate and the residue from the slurry, and                (c) contacting the extract with a carbon                dioxide-containing gas to crystallize silica                particles.
   USE - The method is used for producing silica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces silica from                silicon-containing waste at low cost and with high                safety, suppresses fluctuations in the content of                the silica component of the raw material,                facilitates the setting of blending conditions and                enables the production of cement clinker with                stable quality, and expands the effective                utilization of silicon-containing waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method (i) for producing cement clinker                comprising mixing a silicon-containing waste and an                aqueous solution having a sodium hydroxide                concentration of 1-24 %mass to prepare a slurry                containing a silicate, fractionating the extract                containing the silicate and the residue from the                slurry to obtain the residue having a sodium oxide                content of 0.01-5 %mass, and using the residue as a                raw material for cement; and
   a method (ii) for producing a cement                composition, comprising blending the cement clinker                obtained by above-mentioned method (i) and                gypsum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves washing the solid phase containing the                  crystallized silica particles after the                  crystallization step. The method involves washing                  the solid phase with acid and water in this                  order. The step (b) involves obtaining a residue                  having a disodium oxide content of 0.01-5 %mass                  from the slurry. The step (a) involves heating                  the slurry to 50-200&#176; C to react the                  silicon-containing waste with the sodium                  hydroxide. The method (ii) involves mixing a                  silicon-containing waste and an aqueous solution                  having a sodium hydroxide concentration of 1-24                  %mass to prepare a slurry containing a silicate,                  fractionating the extract containing the silicate                  and the residue from the slurry to obtain the                  residue having a sodium oxide content of 0.01-5                  %mass, and blending the residue, cement clinker                  and gypsum. Preferred Components: The                  silicon-containing waste comprises coal ash. The                  carbon dioxide-containing gas comprises exhaust                  gas generated in a factory. The residue is used                  as a raw material for cement.
   EXAMPLE - Sodium hydroxide and distilled water were                mixed to prepare a 3.7 %mass sodium hydroxide                aqueous solution. 50 g Coal ash and 550 g aqueous                sodium hydroxide solution having a concentration of                3.7 wt.% were weighed. The components were mixed                with a stirrer at 200 rpm in a vessel adjusted to                80&#176; C and reacted for 1 hour. The slurry                thus obtained was filtered using commercially                available filter paper and a suction filtration                device to separate the extract and the residue                (solid content) to produce silica.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

GA 202277882M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves manufacturing a                space-holder by using a water-soluble salt powder,                stacking the space holder in a container, and                injecting molten aluminum. The manufacturing step                includes dissolving and casting the water soluble                salt powder to manufacture a complex salt and                crushing the complex salt. The water- soluble salt                contains cations of potassium ion or sodium ion and                anions of chlorine ion or carbon dioxide ion. The                space holder has a diameter of 0.1-5 mm. The                stacking step includes positioning a second filter                at a lower portion of the container.
   USE - Method for manufacturing open-cell type of                porous aluminum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacturing an open-cell                type of porous aluminum that has no limitation on a                shape and size of a product and that reduces                production costs by simplifying a process while                increasing productivity compared to the existing                process. The porous aluminum has excellent energy                absorption ability and a weight reduction effect                due to an effect of internal pores and may be                applied to various fields by controlling a shape, a                size, a distribution, and porosity of pores in an                alloy. Due to recent global environmental                conservation and stricter carbon dioxide gas                emission regulations, it is essential to reduce                weight for fuel efficiency in a vehicular industry                field.

TI Preparing porous aza-carbon material supported            cobalt-based catalyst used in catalyzing carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to synthesize higher alcohol, has e.g.            preparing zeolite imidazole ester-8 and zeolite            imidazole ester framework-67 by coating, loading            auxiliary metal and performing calcination.
AU GONG L
   LU W
   ZHAO Z
   ZHU H
   LI Y
   DING Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202276219Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a porous aza-carbon material                supported cobalt-based catalyst comprises (1)                preparing zeolite imidazole ester-8 and zeolite                imidazole ester framework-67 (ZIF-8-ZIF-67) by                coating ZIF-67 on the outer surface of ZIF-8 with                ZIF-8 as the core, (2) loading the auxiliary metal                M on zeolite imidazole ester-8 and zeolite                imidazole ester framework-67 by the method of equal                volume impregnation, where M includes manganese,                copper, molybdenum, calcium, and/or lithium to                obtain M-zeolite imidazole ester-8 and zeolite                imidazole ester framework-67 modified by additives,                and (3) performing calcination of the modified                M-zeolite imidazole ester-8 and zeolite imidazole                ester framework-67 in the presence of an inert                atmosphere to obtain the porous azacarbon                material-supported cobalt-based catalyst                M-cobalt-polymeric carbon nitride (M-Co-PCN).
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalyzing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize higher alcohols                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has more uniform particle                distribution, high loading, high reduction rate,                excellent catalytic performance, high catalyst                activity, high selectivity and good stability of CO                hydrogenation to form high-carbon alcohols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cobalt-based catalyst M-cobalt-polymeric carbon                nitride (M-Co-PCN) supported by porous aza-carbon                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary metal M                  includes manganese, copper, molybdenum, calcium,                  and/or lithium. The soluble auxiliary salts                  include metal M formate, acetate, nitrate,                  chloride, sulfate and/or citrate. The mass ratio                  of methanol and water in the mixed solution is                  1:1-2. The dosage ratio of ZIF-8-ZIF-67 and                  auxiliary soluble salt is 1 g:0.001-0.02 mol. The                  drying temperature is 50-100 &#8451;. The                  higher alcohol is a 2-20 C alcohol.Preferred Method: The ZIF-8-ZIF-67 is                  prepared by (1) dissolving soluble zinc salt and                  2-methylimidazole in a solution mixed with equal                  volumes of methanol and ethanol, ultrasonically                  processing, stirring, centrifuging, washing, and                  drying to obtain ZIF-8 crystals, where the                  soluble zinc salt includes zinc nitrate, zinc                  sulfate and/or zinc acetate, and the molar ratio                  of the soluble zinc salt to 2-methylimidazole is                  1:2-5, (2) dispersing the dried ZIF-8 in a                  solution mixed with equal volumes of methanol and                  ethanol, and ultrasonically processing obtain a                  ZIF-8 suspension, dissolving soluble cobalt salt                  and 2-methylimidazole respectively in methanol                  and ethanol mixed solution of equal volume, then                  adding to ZIF-8 suspension, allowing to stand,                  centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain                  ZIF-8-ZIF-67, where the soluble cobalt salts                  include cobalt formate, cobalt acetate, cobalt                  nitrate, cobalt oxalate, cobalt sulfate, cobalt                  citrate, and/or cobalt malate, and the dosage                  ratio of ZIF-8, soluble cobalt salt and                  2-methylimidazole is 0.5                  g:0.03-0.05mol:0.1-0.25mol. In the method,                  ultrasonically processing for 0.5-2 hours,                  stirring for 20-40 hours, centrifuging at                  centrifugal speed of 7000-13000                  revolutions/minute, washing with methanol 3-5                  times, and drying at 50-100&#8451;. The                  M-ZIF-8-ZIF-67 is prepared by grinding the dried                  ZIF-8-ZIF-67 crystals into powder, dissolving the                  auxiliary salt in a mixed solution of methanol                  and water, immersing the ZIF-8-ZIF-67 in an equal                  volume, and drying to obtain M-ZIF-8-ZIF-67. In                  the step (3), using argon, nitrogen or helium as                  the inert atmosphere gas, and performing                  calcination of the auxiliary agent-ZIF-8-ZIF-67                  at 600-1000&#8451; for 1-3 hours, and cooling                  the material to obtain M-Co-PCN. The catalyst is                  used for preparing high-carbon alcohol by using                  synthesis gas as raw material, where the volume                  ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide (CO) in the                  synthesis gas is 1-3, the reaction temperature is                  200-300&#8451;, and the reaction pressure is                  1-5MPa. The total reaction space velocity is                  20-60 l/h/g-catalyst.

PN CN112979988-A
TI Preparation of epoxidized lignin used for preparing lignocarbonate, involves dissolving lignin in solvent, adding sodium hydroxide and epichlorohydrin, mixing, reacting in microwave reaction synthesizer, and taking reaction product.
AU WEN J
   ZHANG C
   YUAN T
   CAO X
   SHEN X
   MA C
   XU L
AE UNIV BEIJING FORESTRY (UYBF-C)
GA 202171808X
AB    NOVELTY - An epoxidized lignin is prepared by dissolving lignin in a solvent, adding sodium hydroxide and epichlorohydrin, mixing, placing in microwave reaction synthesizer, reacting at 60-80 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, taking reaction product, rotary evaporating to recover solvent, adding hydrochloric acid dropwise to adjust pH value to neutral, adding 2-5 times the volume of water, precipitating, centrifuging, and freeze drying obtained solid.
   USE - The epoxidized lignin is used for preparing lignocarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly, low-cost and suitable industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the lignocarbonate from epoxidized lignin comprising mixing hydrogen bond donor and hydrogen bond acceptor, stirring and reacting for 1 hour at 70-100 degrees C to obtain eutectic solvent, dissolving epoxidized lignin in eutectic solvent-containing solvent system, obtaining mixed system, putting into reaction kettle, filling carbon dioxide to pressure of 1.0-2.0 MPa, releasing pressure, repeating charging and releasing pressure operations twice, charging carbon dioxide to pressure of 1.0-2.0 MPa, reacting at 100-130 degrees C at rate of 500 revolutions/minute for 5 hours, obtaining reaction mixture, placing in cold water to cool down to room temperature, exhausting excess gas, adding dropwise 2-5 degrees C cold water, separating solid precipitate, performing centrifugal separation, concentration upper liquid by rotary evaporation to recover deep eutectic solvents, and freeze drying bottom sediment to obtain lignocarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen bond donor is choline chloride or tetrabutylammonium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is 40-60% dioxane aqueous solution or 40-60% isopropanol aqueous solution. The mass-volume ratio of the lignin to the solvent is 0.005-0.01 g/mL. The molar ratio of lignin, sodium hydroxide and epichlorohydrin is 1:3:(3-5). The molar ratio of hydrogen bond donor to hydrogen bond acceptor is 1:1-1:5. The hydrogen bond acceptor is ethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol or glycerol. The solvent system is a eutectic solvent, and the mass ratio of epoxidized lignin to the solvent system is 1:(15-30). The mass ratio of the epoxidized lignin to the solvent system is 1:20, and the solvent system is mixture of a eutectic solvent, 25 wt.% 1-allyl-3-methylimidazolyl ionic liquid and 1 wt.% Lewis acid cobalt chloride.

GA 2021645671
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of spent cannabis biomass involves                raising the spent cannabis biomass to a temperature                (T1) which is sufficient to remove water and                volatile components from the spent cannabis                biomass, in an oxygen-free atmosphere, raising the                biomass to a temperature (T2) which is sufficient                to reclaim cannabinoids from the spent cannabis                biomass, in which the temperature (T2) is higher                than the temperature (T1), raising temperature of                the spent cannabis biomass to a temperature (T3)                which is higher than the temperature (T2),                injecting carbon dioxide and/or water vapor into                the spent cannabis biomass with temperature (T2),                and transforming the spent cannabis biomass into                activated carbon.
   USE - Treatment of spent cannabis biomass used for                forming activated carbon as purification/adsorption                material for filtering unwanted components in                cannabis extraction industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and enables                treatment of spent cannabis biomass reduces cost of                waste management of cannabis companies, recovers                leftover cannabinoids as an oil during conversion                of biomass to activated carbon, and avoids a waste                stream going directly into landfills by recycling                cannabis biomass from cannabis extraction                process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                spent cannabis biomass treatment apparatus.
   Milling machine (200)
   Milled spent cannabis biomass (204)
   Tray (208)
   Fixed bed reactor (212)
   Lid (216)
   Inlet (220)
   Condenser (224)
   Bypass system (228)
   Exhaust line (232)
   Cold ethanol (236)
   Cooling system (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The spent cannabis                  biomass is heated in a fixed bed reactor (212).                  The method further involves purging the fixed bed                  reactor with nitrogen before heating the spent                  cannabis biomass, while raising the spent biomass                  to the temperature (T1), directing vapor                  evaporated from the spent biomass to bypass the                  cold trap, while raising the spent biomass from                  the temperature (T1) to the temperature (T2),                  passing vapor evaporated from the spent biomass                  through the cold trap, and collecting the                  reclaimed cannabinoids by directing them into                  chilled ethanol (236), with a high surface area                  condenser (224), maintaining the spent cannabis                  biomass at the temperature (T1) until all the                  water is removed from the spent cannabis biomass,                  collecting water and volatile components from the                  spent cannabis biomass using a cold trap, while                  the spent cannabis biomass is below and at the                  temperature (T1), maintaining the spent cannabis                  biomass at the temperature (T2) until all the                  cannabinoids reclaimed from the spent biomass,                  milling the spent cannabis biomass to an average                  particle size less than 2 mm prior to raising the                  spent biomass to the temperature (T1), and                  passing nitrogen gas over the spent biomass                  throughout the process at a flow rate of 100-3000                  mL/minute. The carbon dioxide and/or water vapor                  is injected into the spent cannabis biomass using                  a boiler (244) and a distributor plate                  (252).Preferred Conditions: The spent cannabis                  biomass at the temperature (T3) is maintained for                  0.5-2 hours, preferably 1 hour. The temperature                  (T3) is 800-1000degrees Celsius. The temperature                  (T1) is 100plus minus20degrees Celsius,                  preferably 100degrees Celsius. The temperature                  (T2) is 170-500degrees Celsius, preferably                  500degrees Celsius. The carbon dioxide and/or                  water vapor is injected at a flow rate of                  100-3000 mL/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The fixed bed reactor                  consists of an exhaust line (232) with a                  bypassable cold trap.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing active calcium, comprising (i) measuring raw materials, (ii) adding water to low-frequency resonance instrument, starting low-frequency resonance instrument and allowing to work continuously for 30-45 minutes to obtain active water, (iii) mixing calcium aspartate with active water to obtain active lime milk with concentration of 7-9%, (iv) passing emulsion into spray tower and spraying out in mist form, passing carbon dioxide after scrubbing and dust removal into spray tower to make emulsion contact and react with carbon dioxide to obtain active calcium particles, (v) mixing and stirring TMC-201 titanate coupling agent, stearic acid, lignin, cod liver oil and Plectranthus barbatus extract to obtain activator after concentration, (vi) adding active calcium particles to activator, stirring and mixing, collecting out, separating and drying to obtain active calcium semi-finished product, and (vii) conveying active calcium semi-finished product into granulator to obtain finished product of active calcium.

The preparation of Plectranthus barbatus extract comprises (1) collect 3 kg Plectranthus barbatus and chose its stamens, add to extraction tank, add 6 kg water to boil and continue to decoct for two hours, filter extract and pour it into liquid storage tank, and (2) perform reverse dialysis and concentration on extract in liquid storage tank, concentrate, obtain extract, where the solution density of concentrated solution is 1.12. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least two carbonyl functional groups are each a carbonyl functional group other than an ester group or a carboxylic acid group. Each of the greater than or equal to 2 carbonyl functional groups is a ketone or an aldehyde group. The 1st compound is diacetylbenzene, and the 2nd compound is acetylene. Preferred Processes: The 1st compound is reacted with 2nd compound to form adduct which is subsequently reacted with carbon dioxide to form polycyclocarbonate compound.

Ethylenically unsaturated polycyclocarbonate compound is an ethylenically unsaturated dicyclocarbonate compound. Polycyclocarbonate compound has structure of formula (I).R1, R3, R4=H or organic group;R2=divalent organic group, where greater than or equal to 2 of R1, R2, or R4 of each ring can optionally join to form a cyclic group;n, s=0 or 1, where when s is 0, a double bond is located between C atom to which R3 is attached, and adjacent carbon atom of carbonate ring, and when s is 1, a single bond is present between these 2 C atoms; andt=1, 2, or 3.Preferred Properties: Polycyclocarbonate compound has number average molecular weight of less than or equal to 1500. Ethylenically unsaturated polycyclocarbonate compound has melting point of 133-135 degrees C.
   Preferred Definitions:
   n=1;
   R1=Me;
   R2=divalent aromatic groups;
   R3=H; and
   t=2.

The purification device includes a purification chamber and an isolation plate. The isolation plate is provided with air holes. The purification cavity is provided with a purification air inlet and a purification air outlet. A heating mechanism is provided in the sterilization chamber. A reactant is provided in the oxygen generating chamber. The heating mechanism heats the air in the disinfection chamber, and the reactant reacts with carbon dioxide in the air to generate oxygen. The air returns from the second ventilation duct into the enclosed space to heat and sterilize the air in the enclosed space, and uses carbon dioxide in the air to regenerate oxygen.
