GA 202098257M
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves feeding a particulated                petroleum coke-based material into a first thermal                gasification reactor in a fluidized bed at a                temperature below 1000 degrees Celsius, and a                pressure of 1-20 bar, to obtain a gaseous product,                with carbon conversion above 75%. The gaseous                product from the first reactor is led through a                separating device for the separation of entrained                fines. The portion of the separated entrained fines                is impregnated with an aqueous solution of one or                more compounds that catalyze gasification                reactions. The catalyst impregnated material is fed                into a second reactor, and gasified in a fluidized                bed with steam and/or carbon-dioxide in the                presence of oxygen at 680-800 degrees Celsius, and                1-10 bar. The two product streams of the first and                second reactors are united, and the components are                optionally separated by pressure swing adsorption                or similar methods.
   USE - Process for gasification of petroleum                coke-based feed material for producing hydrogen                rich gaseous mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces the heating energy                consumption in the gasification process of                petroleum coke-based feed materials. By applying                the reactor system including two continuously                operated fluid bed reactors, the overall catalyst                and heating energy demand of petroleum coke-based                feed materials are reduced significantly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the                process for gasification of petroleum coke-based                feed material.

PN CN111167156-A
TI Extraction of full composition of peony seeds by drying peony seeds, crushing, sieving, adding to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, extracting to oil and peony seed meal, and adding peony seed meal with purified water.
AU SONG L
   LIU C
   HUANG W
AE GUANGZHOU ZELI PHARMATECH CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202044994N
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of full composition of peony seeds comprises drying peony seeds, crushing, sieving through 20-30 mesh sieve, adding 5-10 kg finely crushed peony seeds into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and extracting at extraction pressure of 25-35 MPa, extraction temperature of 45-55 degrees C, separation pressure of 6-10 MPa, separation temperature of 40-50 degrees C and gas flow rate of 300-350 m3/hour for 80-120 minutes to obtain peony seed oil and peony seed meal; adding peony seed meal with purified water, mixing, adding to high-pressure differential low-temperature continuous extractor, extracting to obtain the extraction liquid, centrifuging, collecting supernatant, microfiltering centrifugal liquid through ceramic membrane microfilter, and collecting filtrate; and subjecting to vacuum or reverse osmosis membrane concentration.
   USE - The method is for extraction of full composition of peony seeds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to repeat, maximizes the extraction and conversion of all active ingredients in peony seeds into finished products, increases product added value, reduces the amount of organic solvents used, ensures high extraction rate of active ingredients, reduces pollution, produces peony seed oil yield of up to 27.5% with very low peroxide value and high proportion of unsaturated fatty acids, and has extraction rate of protein, polysaccharide and polyphenols of above 80%, extraction rate of paeoniflorin of more than 64%, and low production costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-pressure differential low-temperature continuous extraction is done for 1-3 times at 15-40 degrees C and at 10-40 MPa. The dry grinding is done at 45-55 degrees C for 10-30 minutes until water content is less than 8%. The centrifugation is done at rotation speed of 3500-5000 revolutions/minute. The method further comprises subjecting peony seed meal to high-pressure steam flash explosion at 40-50 degrees C and at 2-6 MPa for 15-35 seconds. Preferred Components: The ratio of purified water and peony seed meal is 1:25-35.

PT P
PN AU2019101312-A4
TI Fruit juice bulk transport method involves conducting tracking of key specification and attributes throughout supply chain by sensors and internet-of-things enabled devices.
AU ASKEW P
AE AUSSIE FOOD TO YOU PTY LTD (AUSS-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2128M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out real-time tracking of key specification and attributes such as temperature, pressure, microbial count, provenance, and nutritional content of perishable organic liquids. The tracking is conducted throughout the supply chain by the sensors and the internet-of-things enabled devices. The method allows the bulk freight of fruit juice for extended period of time up to 24 days. The fruit juice is preserved in the natural form without the need for additives and chemical preservatives. Upon delivery or when the fruit juice or other organic liquid is needed for production, the container is unloaded and carbon dioxide is released from the liquid by some form of agitation, stirring or mixing independent of or in conjunction with negative pressure and the liquid is then processed.
   USE - Fruit juice bulk transport method. Can also be used for transport of other perishable organic liquids such as dairy products, vegetable juices, plant extracts, fungal extracts, flavoring agents, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The technology has exclusive rights to use and extends the shelf life of perishable organic liquids when treated with carbon dioxide in a specified manner. Real-time tracking of key specification and attributes is enabled. Long duration shipping of liquids without refrigeration is enabled, so the cost is reduced. The counterfeiting and dilution are minimized, and provenance is proved and a level of food safety and quality unachievable from non-real time monitoring is achieved.

PN CN110354881-A; CN110354881-B
TI Preparation of carbon nitride-coated metal nanoparticle core-shell structure material for carbon dioxide reduction reaction, by pretreating metal nanoparticles, and constructing organic metal salt coating layer on surface of nanoparticle.
AU ZHUANG L
   LI Z
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2019888942
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a carbon nitride-coated metal nanoparticle core-shell structure material involves: (1) pretreating metal nanoparticles by putting the metal nanoparticles into a sufficient amount of dilute acid, stirring for a period of time, and then centrifuging and washing to obtain metal nanoparticles without oxide on the surface; (2) constructing an organic metal salt coating layer on the surface of the metal nanoparticle by ultrasonically dispersing the surface-free metal nanoparticles obtained in the step (1) in an organic solvent under stirring conditions, adding calculated amounts of organic ligands, fully reacting the ligand and the metal nanoparticles to form a certain thickness of organic salt on the surface of the nanoparticles, then filtering and washing to obtain metal nanoparticles coated with organic metal salt; and (3) roasting the metal nanoparticles coated with the organic metal salt obtained in the step (2) in a roasting atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a carbon nitride-coated metal nanoparticle core-shell structure material, which is useful for carbon dioxide reduction reaction in new energy field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the carbon nitride-coated metal nanoparticle core-shell structure material with high performance, and excellent electrocatalytic activity, stability, selectivity and reproducibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the carbon nitride-coated metal nanoparticle core-shell structure material prepared by using the above preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: In the step (3), the roasting temperature is greater than 250 degrees C. Preferred Components: The metal is copper, silver and/or gold, or an alloy composed of several metals. In the step (3), the roasting atmosphere is a mixed gas composed of a reducing gas and an inert gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent includes acetonitrile, malononitrile and/or tetrahydrofuran. The organic ligand is an organic molecule having a strong electron withdrawing group. The organic ligand is 7,7',8,8'-tetracyanoquinodimethane, 2-fluoro-7,7',8,8'-tetracyanoquinodimethane, 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-7,7',8,8'-tetracyanoquinonedimethane and/or 1,4-bis(dicyanomethylene)cyclohexane. The mass ratio of organic ligands to metal nanoparticles is greater than 1%.

PN CN110028081-A
TI Synthesizing nano-scale multi-stage pore SSZ-13            molecular sieve useful for applying to adsorption of            carbon dioxide and other gases comprises e.g. mixing            sodium hydroxide and deionized water and adding            aluminum and silicon source.
AU TAO Y
   WANG J
   ZHOU L
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201965140S
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing nano-scale multi-stage pore                SSZ-13 molecular sieve comprises (i) mixing sodium                hydroxide, templating agent N,N,N-trimethyl                adamantane ammonium hydroxide and deionized water,                uniformly mixing, adding aluminum source, silicon                source to obtain a synthetic liquid, where the                molar ratio of each component in synthetic liquid                is silicon source/aluminum source is 10-100, sodium                hydroxide/ silicon source is 0.05-0.5,                water/silicon source is 20-50 and N,N,N-trimethyl                adamantane ammonium hydroxide/silicon source is                0.05-0.5, (ii) ultrasonically oscillating the                synthetic liquid in ultrasonic cleaner, (iii)                continuously stirring the ultrasonically                synthesized synthetic liquid, (iv) adding SSZ-13                zeolite molecular sieve as a seed crystal to                synthetic liquid and stirring and (v) transferring                synthetic liquid to reaction vessel, and                synthesizing at 120-200 degrees C for 24-144 hours,                washing the obtained zeolite molecular sieve with                deionized water to neutrality and drying.
   USE - The synthesizing nano-scale multi-stage pore                SSZ-13 molecular sieve is useful for applying to                adsorption of carbon dioxide, and adsorption of                other gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, does not                use a mesoporous template machine, and does not add                an additional chemical reagent. The molecular sieve                shows potential application possibilities in field                of gas adsorption and has important application                prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv) the                  amount of SSZ-13 zeolite molecular sieve added is                  0.05-5 wt.% of the synthetic liquid, and the                  stirring for 10-30 minutes. In the step (ii)                  ultrasonically oscillating for 2-10 minutes and                  ultrasonic power is 40-100%. In the step (iii)                  stirring for 0.5-3 hours at 25-80 degrees C. In                  the step (i) the aluminum is aluminum oxide,                  aluminum hydroxide, aluminum chloride, aluminum                  sulfate, aluminum nitrate, sodium aluminum oxide                  or aluminum isopropoxide. The silicon source is                  white carbon black, silica sol, sodium silicate                  or ethyl orthosilicate.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the magnesium alloy anode material comprising (i) removing oxides and oil stains on the surface of magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots, washing with absolute ethanol, drying to constant weight at 90 degrees C, i.e. pretreated magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots respectively, (ii) removing oxides and oil stains on the surface of the granules, washing with absolute ethanol, drying at 90 degrees C to constant weight, i.e. pretreated granules, (iii) placing the pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot in furnace protected by mixed gas carbon dioxide and heating the sulfur hexafluoride(S-f6) at 700-760 degrees C to melting, (iv) keeping the reaction at 700-760 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, heating at 770-800 degrees C, adding the niobium particles, stirring under protection of carbon dioxide and SF6 to obtain molten material and keeping for 35-60 minutes, (v) cooling the melt to 650-700 degrees C, allowing to stand for 10-20 minutes, casting the steel mold, preheating at 200-300 degrees C, naturally cooling the ingot to room temperature, (6) placing the ingot in high temperature box, heating at 420 degrees C for 24 hours, taking out the water and quenching to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot were cut into small pieces of 100x 30x 20 mm before removing the oxide and oil stain on the surface of the magnesium ingot and the aluminum ingot. The granules are industrial grade purity or more.

TI Method for dimensioning regeneration column of unit for capturing/treating e.g. carbon dioxide, involves placing contactors one above other at point in certain height of column corresponding to clogging rate of contactor.
AU LETTAT A
   RAYNAL L
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 201946802N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining variation of a clogging rate over entire height of a regeneration column. A clogging curve is determined for contactor of a set of contactors ranging for giving the clogging rate of the contactor as a function of superficial velocity of gas, and the first contactor is set up to allow the regeneration column to work at a congestion rate between 80-90% at a bottom portion of the regeneration column. The contactors are placed one above the other at a point in certain height of the regeneration column corresponding to the clogging rate of the contactor between 50-60%. The process of placing of the contactors is repeated to a top portion of the regeneration column.
   USE - Method for dimensioning a regeneration column of a unit for capturing and treating gas e.g. carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen sulfide gas, natural gas or biogas or fumes in post-combustion and refinery acid gas, treatment- unit (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables placing the contactors one above other at the point in certain height of the regeneration column corresponding to the clogging rate of the contactor, thus capturing and treating gas in an easy and cost effective manner.

TI Cellulose/silicon dioxide composite aerogel long            fiber comprises cellulose nanofiber network, and            silicon dioxide gel skeleton, and macroscopic shape            long fiber.
AU SAI H
   FU R
   WANG Y
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIN-C)
   UNIV INNER MONGOLIA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIN-C)
GA 201927115X
AB    NOVELTY - A cellulose/silicon dioxide composite aerogel                long fiber comprises cellulose nanofiber network,                and silicon dioxide gel skeleton, and macroscopic                shape long fiber.
   USE - Cellulose/silicon dioxide composite aerogel                long fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The cellulose silicon dioxide composite                aerogel long fiber has high strength, and excellent                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation cellulose/silicon dioxide composite                aerogel long fiber involves preparing microbial                cellulose matrix with macrofibrous morphology, and                nanofiber network in microscopic structure by                cutting the fermented coconut fruit pieces into                strip pieces, washing with water, and                freeze-drying, mixing silicon dioxide precursor,                ethanol, and deionized water, stirring, adding                acidic catalyst in drops, stirring, hydrolyzing,                adding basic catalyst in drops to obtain silicon                dioxide sol, immersing the cellulose matrix in the                silicon dioxide sol at 20-80 degrees C for 10-50                minutes, removing the cellulose matrix from the                sol, immersing the cellulose matrix in the silicon                dioxide sol at 40-130 degrees C for 1-18 hours in                the atmosphere of ethanol to obtain                cellulose/silicon dioxide composite gel, and                drying. The drying method is one of supercritical                drying, and atmospheric drying. The supercritical                drying replaces the solvent of cellulose/silicon                dioxide composite gel with ethanol, and dried using                supercritical ethanol, or subjecting the                cellulose/silicon dioxide composite gel to solvent                replacement with ethanol, and liquid carbon                dioxide, and dried using supercritical carbon                dioxide. The atmospheric drying treats the                cellulose/silicon dioxide composite gel with                organic reagent in inert solvent, and dried at                normal pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The composite aerogel                  long fiber has tensile strength of 2-20MPa,                  specific surface area of 300-900m2/g, and thermal                  conductivity of 0.018-0.037 W/m.K. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of silicon dioxide                  precursor, deionized water, ethanol, acidic                  catalyst, and basic catalyst is                  1:(1-7):(3-30):(0.001-0.005):(0.001-0.01).                  Preferred Components: The silicon dioxide                  precursor is tetraethyl orthosilicate or methyl                  orthosilicate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The fermented coconut                  fruit piece has thickness of 2-8 mm. The                  fermented coconut fruit strip has width of 2-8mm,                  and washed with water for 3-5 times. Preferred                  Components: The inert solvent is one of n-hexane,                  cyclohexane, n-heptane, dodecane, and petroleum                  ether. The organic reagent is one of                  trimethylchlorosilane, and hexamethyldisilazane.                  The ethanol can be replaced with methanol in the                  preparation of silicon dioxide sol, and drying                  cellulose/silicon dioxide composite gel.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves grinding and dispersing imitating redwood color paste and a natural wood fiber for about 3 hours under boiling condition The wood fiber is filtered and dried to obtain a dyed wood fiber. Acetic anhydride and acidic catalyst are added and dissolved in ultra-critical carbon dioxide and mixed with dyeing wood fiber under pressure of about 10-15 MPa. Pressure is released after reaction process is completed for recovering carbon dioxide and separating precipitate to obtain a non-polar chemical dyed wood fiber. The obtained non-polar chemical dyed wood fiber is dispersed in non-polar dispersion medium organic solvent, mixed with polypropylene matrix and antioxidant and placed into a mixer after completing mixing treatment. The mixed chemical dyed wood fiber is taken out and placed in a flat vulcanizing machine for realizing hot press molding process to obtain hard wood-imitating redwood building material. The non-polar dispersion medium organic solvent is silicon oil.
   USE - Method for preparing natural hard fiber/polypropylene imitating redwood building material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively improving comprehensive using quality of a prepared natural hard fiber/polypropylene imitating redwood product with better use value and better vivid effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a natural hard fiber/polypropylene imitating redwood building material.

TI System for filtering carbon dioxide from biogas, has controller blocking introduction of filtered gas into reactor during first phase and allowing introduction of filtered gas and gas into reactor starting from output during second phase.
AU ARCHETTI M
   GALANTI OCCULTI L
   GALANTI O L
   OCCULTI L G
AE ECOSPRAY TECHNOLOGIES SRL (ECOS-Non-standard)
   ECOSPRAY TECHNOLOGIES SRL (ECOS-Non-standard)
   ECOSPRAY TECHNOLOGIES SRL (ECOS-Non-standard)
   ECOSPRAY TECHNOLOGIES SRL (ECOS-Non-standard)
GA 2019046308
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1000) has a first vacuum generator (1401) generating vacuum in a first vacuum phase at lower pressure than that of vacuum generated by a second vacuum generator (1402) in a second vacuum phase. A first flow controller (1501) is connected at an output (1200) to a first reactor (1301). A second flow controller (1502) is connected at the output to a second reactor (1302). A third flow controller (1503) is connected at the output to a third reactor (1303). Each flow controller blocks introduction of filtered gas into each reactor during the first vacuum phase. Each flow controller allows introduction of the filtered gas and gas into each reactor starting from the output during the second vacuum phase.
   USE - System for filtering gas e.g. carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, from biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces energy consumption associated particularly with the first and second vacuum phases and avoids a loss of gas coming out of the vacuum generator. The system allows the output of the vacuum generator and the vacuum generator to be connected to the input so as to avoid dispersing filtered methane into the environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a filtering system.

PN IN201711020042-A
TI Two-step simultaneous extraction of curcuminoid oleoresins and turmeric essential oil for food application, involves contacting turmeric rhizomes with liquid carbon dioxide, and subjecting de-oiled turmeric powder to liquid extraction.
AU NAIK S N
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY DELHI (IITD-C)
GA 2018A2776M
AB    NOVELTY - Two-step simultaneous extraction of turmeric essential oil and curcuminoid oleoresins from turmeric rhizome involves contacting turmeric rhizomes with liquid carbon dioxide to obtain turmeric essential oil, subjecting resultant de-oiled turmeric powder to pressurized liquid extraction to extract oleoresin using a solvent comprising ethanol and isopropanol in ratio of 4:1-1:4.
   USE - Two-step simultaneous extraction of curcuminoid oleoresins and turmeric essential oil. Uses include but are not limited to food applications as coloring and flavoring agent in soup, sauce, mayonnaise, cereal and vegetable based curries, vegetable oil, meat, and meat products, synthetic color replacer, pickling agent, decorative agent in non-alcoholic beverages, preservative, stabilizers, and in medical application as remedy for several disease e.g. cancer, diabetes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, rapid and environmentally-friendly simultaneous extraction of curcuminoid oleoresins and turmeric essential oil having high quality with reduced solvent consumption and energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) process for crystallization of curcuminoid from turmeric oleoresin, involves dissolving oleoresin extract in 2-20 ml of isopropanol, heating the mixture at 60-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, cooling the heated mixture to room temperature, refrigerating overnight for settling, centrifuging the refrigerated mixture to precipitate the curcuminoid crystal, and washing the residual oleoresin extract with 5-20 ml of hexane 2-3 times; and
   (2) crystallized curcuminoid powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The liquid carbon dioxide contact processing is carried out at temperature of 5-20 degrees C, and pressure of 65-70 bar for 15-60 minutes. The pressurized liquid extraction has solvent flowrate of 1-10 ml/minute, and is carried out at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C, pressure of 80-150 bar for 60-150 minutes. Preferred Properties: The crystallized curcuminoid powder has purity of 90-98%, and antioxidant activity of 40-80% inhibition at a concentration of 100-5000 mu g/mL. Preferred Composition: The crystallized powder contains 250-400 mg GAE/g of total phenolic content, 40-60 mg QE/g of total flavonoid content.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing capsule, comprising providing traditional Chinese medicine components, removing impurities, drying, crushing, sieving with 60 mesh sieve to obtain powder, extracting by using supercritical extracting device, adding the Chinese traditional medicine powder into extracting kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, adding supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction solvent, extracting under 30-50 degrees C extraction temperature and 15-30 MPa extraction pressure for 5-7 hours, heating, reducing and separating, collecting the separated agglomerated product in the kettle, crushing to 100 mesh sieve to obtain extract powder, adding extract powder of each traditional Chinese medicine component into V-shaped asymmetric mixer, stirring at 12 revolutions/minute for 8-10 minutes to obtain mixed powder, filling the mixed powder into the capsule filler according to 0.5 g/capsule, placing the capsule placed under ultraviolet radiation for 20 minute and packaging.
TF ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Thrombolytic; Anticoagulant; Antidepressant.

PN CN107937353-A
TI Culturing fish infectious hematopoietic necrosis virus (IHNV) used for preparing inactivated vaccine, by digesting Chinook salmon embryo cells using trypsin, dispersing cells in culture medium, adding IHNV and cell growth solution.
AU REN Z
   LU Z
   YIN S
   WU H
   JIANG D
   ZHAO H
AE GUANGZHOU QIZHI BIOLOGICAL ENG EQUIP CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201832735A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing fish infectious hematopoietic necrosis virus (IHNV) involves (a) digesting monolayer of Chinook salmon embryo cells using trypsin, shaking and dispersing cells in cell culture medium, adjusting cell concentration, inoculating cells into bioreactor containing microcarrier, and culturing cells under flow of carbon dioxide, and (b) adding vaccine containing IHNV, adding cell growth solution, stirring solution and incubating cells under above-mentioned conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing fish IHNV, which is used for preparing inactivated vaccine against IHNV.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare IHNV with high titer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing fish infectious hematopoietic necrosis virus (IHNV) involves (a) digesting monolayer of Chinook salmon embryo cells using trypsin, shaking and dispersing cells in cell culture medium, adjusting cell concentration, inoculating cells at concentration of 4x 108 cells/ml into bioreactor containing microcarrier, culturing cells at 16-20 degrees C and pH of 7-7.5 under flow of 5% carbon dioxide, maintaining glucose content at not less than 330 mg/dl, dissolved oxygen content at 40-60% and stirring speed at 40-60 revolutions per minute, and (b) when cell density reaches 2x 106 cells/ml, adding vaccine containing IHNV in a volume ratio of 1:1000, adding cell growth solution, stirring solution once every 5 minutes, and incubating cells under above-mentioned conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Device: The bioreactor is automated stirring type bioreactor. Preferred Components: The microcarrier is spherical microcarrier having density of 6 g/l. The trypsin concentration is 0.125%. The cell culture medium comprises 85-92% minimum essential medium, 2-5% glucose, 6-10% fetal bovine serum, 1-3 mg/l choline chloride and 0.2-0.6 mg/l hypoxanthine. The content of IHNV is 107 tissue culture infectious dose50. The cell growth solution comprises 90-95% minimum essential medium, 3-5% newborn calf serum and 2-5% glucose.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Fresh-keeping method of apricot involves soaking apricot in potassium permanganate solution, taking out, drying, placing into a basket paved with paper, and cooling.
AU SONG Z
   REN K
AE FUYANG HONGYE ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOG (FUYA-Non-standard)
GA 201832112B
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-keeping method of apricot comprises selecting 80% mature apricot with no plant diseases, insect pests and mechanical damage; preparing a basket paved with paper to avoid fruit damage; soaking in 0.08-0.12% potassium permanganate solution for 8-12 minutes, taking out, and drying for good sterilizing, cooling and disintegration delaying effects; placing into the basket, and cooling to 10-20 degrees C for 12-24 hours; gas storing at 0-1 degrees C, keeping the air relative humidity at 85-90%, and adjusting carbon dioxide content to 4-6 % and oxygen to 2-4%; and ripening at 18-24 degrees C before selling.
   USE - Method for fresh-keeping apricot.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fresh-keeping method of apricot comprises selecting 80% mature apricot with no plant diseases, insect pests and mechanical damage; preparing a basket paved with paper to avoid fruit damage; soaking in 0.08-0.12% potassium permanganate solution for 8-12 minutes, taking out, and drying for good sterilizing, cooling and disintegration delaying effects; placing into the basket, and cooling to 10-20 degrees C for 12-24 hours; gas storing at 0-1 degrees C, keeping the air relative humidity at 85-90%, and adjusting carbon dioxide content to 4-6 % and oxygen to 2-4%; and ripening at 18-24 degrees C before selling to show a good flavor.

PT P
PN CN107537564-A; CN107537564-B
TI Quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand containing organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst useful in coupling reaction of epoxy compound and carbon dioxide.
AU DING Y
   WANG W
   LI C
   YAN L
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201803586E
AB    NOVELTY - Quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand containing organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst is claimed. The organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst is obtained by copolymerizing organic phosphine ligand containing olefin group and quaternary phosphonium salt containing olefin group, adding resulting copolymer into with or without Lewis acid metal salts, and then metal ion in metal salt forms coordinate bond with phosphine in organic porous copolymer containing quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand to obtain organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in coupling reaction of epoxy compound and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand containing organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst comprising mixing alkene-functionalized organophosphine ligand and alkene-functionalized quaternary phosphonium salt, carrying out solvent thermal polymerization method, initiating organic phosphine ligand by free radical initiator and olefin group in quaternary phosphonium salt, and polymerizing to obtain multi-stage pore structure containing quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic porous copolymer, and adding resulting copolymer into with or without Lewis acid metal salt to obtain heterogeneous catalyst, then dissolving Lewis acidic metal salt in solvent, adding quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic porous copolymer, stirring, exposing phosphonic in Lewis acid metal salt in metal ion and quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic porous copolymer to obtain coordination bonds, and distilling volatile solvent to obtain heterogeneous catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Lewis acid metal salt is zinc, aluminum, silver, gold, copper, calcium, rhodium, iridium, cobalt, magnesium and rare earth metal halide, where halide is MXn, and metal loading of catalyst is 0.01-10 wt.%. The functionalized functional group of olefin group-containing organophosphine ligand and olefin group-containing quaternary phosphonium salt are terminal olefin functional group (preferably vinyl). The olefin group-containing organophosphine ligand is tris(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine, 6,6'-(3,3'-di-tert-butyl-5,5'-divinylbiphenyl-2,2'-diyl)bis(oxy)didibenzo(d,f)(1,3,2)dioxaphosphepine, phenylbis(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine, tris(4-vinylcyclohexyl)phosphine, diphenyl(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine, 2-(bis(4-vinylphenyl)phosphino)-5-vinylpyridine, 6,6'-((4-vinylphenyl)phosphinediyl)bis(3-vinylpyridine), 2-(pyridin-2-yl(4-vinylphenyl)phosphino)-5-vinylpyridine, 2-(phenyl(pyridin-2-yl)phosphino)-5-vinylpyridine, 2-(phenyl(4-vinylphenyl)phosphino)-5-vinylpyridine, 2-(diphenylphosphino)-5-vinylpyridine and (6,6'-divinyl-1,1'-binaphthyl-2,2'-diyl)bis(diphenylphosphine); and structural formula of quaternary phosphonium salt containing alkylene group is ethyltris(4-vinylphenyl)phosphonium of formula (I). The olefin group-containing organophosphine ligand is vinyl group-containing bidentate phosphite organic phosphine ligand and vinyl group-containing triphenylphosphine ligand; and alkylene group-containing quaternary phosphonium salt is methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium chloride, methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium bromide, methyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium iodide, ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium chloride, ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium bromide and ethyl-tris(4-vinylbenzene)phosphonium iodide. In step (i), organic solvent is benzene, toluene, methanol, ethanol, tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane or trichloromethane; crosslinking agent is styrene, ethylene, propylene, divinylbenzene, dimethoxymethane, methylene iodide, para-formaldehyde or 1,3,5-triethynylbenzene; and free radical initiator is cyclohexanone peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide, azobisisobutyronitrile or azobisisobutyronitrile; molar ratio between organic phosphine ligand and quaternary phosphonium salt is 0.01:1-100:1; when adding crosslinking agent, then molar ratio between organic phosphine ligand and cross-linking agent is 0.01:1-10:1; and molar ratio between organic phosphine ligand and free radical initiator is 300:1-10:1; before polymerization in organic porous copolymer, concentration of organic phosphine ligand in organic solvent is 0.01-1000 g/l; and in step (iv), solvent is water, benzene, toluene, tetrahydrofuran, methanol, ethanol, dichloromethane or trichloromethane; Lewis acid metal salt metal element is zinc, aluminum, silver, gold, copper, calcium, rhodium, iridium, cobalt, magnesium and rare earth metal, where zinc salt is ZnX2, aluminum salt is AlX3, silver salt is AgX, gold salt is AuX, calcium salt is CaX2, rhodium salt is RhX3 and iridium salt is IrX3. Preferred Method: The method comprises catalyst is obtained by solvent thermal polymerization method, where catalyst has multi-stage pore structure, specific surface area is 100-3000 m2/g, catalyst also contains large hole, mesopore and micropore, pore volume is 0.1-5 cm3/g, and pore size distribution is 0.2-50 nm. The method comprises (i) adding alkene-functionalized organophosphine ligand and alkene-functionalized quaternary phosphonium salt, reacting at 273-473 K under inert gas atmosphere in organic solvent, then adding free radical initiator into with or without cross-linking agent, mixing, stirring mixture for 0.1-100 hours (preferably 0.1-1 hour), (ii) transferring mixed solution obtained in step (i) into synthesis autoclave, carrying out solvent thermal polymerization method at 273-473 K under an inert gas atmosphere, allowing to stand for 1-100 hours, and carrying out polymerization reaction to obtain quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic copolymer, (iii) evacuating organic copolymer obtained in step (ii) and solvent in vacuum at room temperature to obtain organic copolymer containing phosphine and quaternary phosphonium salt having multi-stage pore structure i.e. quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic porous copolymer, and (iv) adding organic porous copolymer obtained in step (iii) into solvent containing Lewis acid metal salt, reacting at 273-473 K under inert gas atmosphere, stirring for 0.1-100 hours (preferably 0.1-1 hour), and vacuum extracting organic solvent to obtain heterogeneous catalyst, where steps (i)-(iii) or steps (i) -(iv) yields quaternary phosphonium salt-phosphorus ligand organic porous copolymer heterogeneous catalyst.M = metal element;X = Cl, Br or I; andn = 1-3.

PN US2022307732-A1
TI Electrochemical compressor system used in            refrigeration systems, comprises anion conducting layer            having polymer, between electrodes, where polymer            comprises backbone of poly(arylene), poly(phenylene)            and poly(styrene), and conducting layer has specific            concentration gradient of functional groups.
AU BAHAR B
AE FFI IONIX IP INC (FFII-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4659J
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical compressor system comprises:                a) electrochemical compressor (10) containing                anisotropic anion conducting layer (A) which has                anion conducting polymer (B), between and                contacting anode (40) and cathode (60), the polymer                (B) comprises backbone of poly(arylene),                poly(phenylene) and poly(styrene), the anion                conducting layer (30) is composite anion conducting                layer containing support material (33) attached to                polymer (B), the layer (A) has concentration                gradient of functional groups from anode to                cathode, 1.5:1 or more; b) working fluid; and c)                power supply.
The voltage potential at anode, generates                hydroxyl ions and voltage potential at cathode,                consumes hydroxyl ions to form pH gradient which                transfers working fluid containing carbon dioxide,                from anode to cathode. The carbon dioxide is in                equilibrium with bicarbonate ions which are                transported through anion conducting layer, from                anode to cathode, and reformed into carbon dioxide,                at cathode.
   USE - Electrochemical compressor system is used in                refrigeration systems (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The new electrochemical compressor system                containing noiseless, solid-state, modular and                scalable electrochemical compressors, enables                carbon dioxide compression, more efficiently. The                high performance anionic conducting layer of the                compressor, improves the efficiency of the                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrochemical compressor system                comprises:
   a) an electrochemical compressor (10)                containing: i) an anode (40); ii) a cathode (60),                iii) an anisotropic anion conducting layer (A)                which has an anion conducting polymer (B), formed                between and contacting the anode and cathode, the                polymer (B) comprises a backbone selected from                poly(arylene), poly(phenylene) and poly(styrene),                the layer (A) is a composite anion conducting layer                (30) containing a support material (33) attached to                the polymer (B), the anisotropic anion conducting                layer has a concentration gradient of functional                groups from anode to cathode, 1.5:1 or more; b) a                working fluid; and c) a power supply coupled                electrically with anode and cathode, to form a                voltage potential across the anode and cathode. The                voltage potential at the anode, generates hydroxyl                ions and the voltage potential at the cathode,                consumes hydroxyl ions to form a pH gradient which                transfers the working fluid containing carbon                dioxide, from the anode to the cathode. The carbon                dioxide is in equilibrium with bicarbonate ions                which are transported through the anion conducting                layer, from the anode to the cathode, and reformed                into carbon dioxide, at the cathode.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1) refrigeration system which defines a closed                loop containing working fluid. At least a part of                the working fluid, is circulated through the closed                loop. The refrigeration system comprises an                electrochemical compressor system, condenser and                evaporator; and
   2) preparation of composite anion exchange                conducting layer, which involves: a) providing an                anion conducting polymer which has tertiary amine                groups; b) providing a crosslinking agent; c)                providing a porous support material; d) preparing a                polymer solution of anion conducting polymer and                crosslinking agent; e) coating support material                with polymer solution and forming a coated support                material; f) cross-linking the polymer solution;                and g) exposing coated support material to a                trimethylamine solution, to functionalize the                tertiary amine groups to a quaternary                ammonium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of carbon                dioxide electrochemical compressor.
   10Electrochemical compressor
   30Anion conducting layer
   33Support material
   40Anode
   60Cathode
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: 1) The-anisotropic                  anion conducting layer of the electrochemical                  compressor system, comprises a first anion                  conducting layer having a first concentration of                  functional groups and a second anion conducting                  layer having a second concentration of functional                  groups. The first anion conducting layer contacts                  the anode and the second anion conducting layer                  contacts the cathode.2) The working fluid                  contained in the electrochemical compressor                  system, comprises ammonia or carbon dioxide which                  is in equilibrium with bicarbonate ions. The                  bicarbonate ions which are transported from the                  anode to the cathode, through anion conducting                  layer, are reformed into carbon dioxide at the                  cathode.Preferred Properties: The first                  concentration of functional groups of the layer                  (A) present in the electrochemical compressor                  system, is at least 1.5 times greater than the                  second concentration of functional groups.The support material of the layer (A) of                  electrochemical compressor system, has porosity                  55-90%. The thickness of anion conducting layer,                  is 5-50 microns. Preferred Process: The                  preparation of composite anion exchange                  conducting layer, further involves the step of                  exposing the coated support material to high                  temperatures and accelerate crosslinking, or                  exposing the coated support material to infrared                  or ultraviolet radiation to accelerate                  crosslinking.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: 1)The backbone of                  anion conducting polymer is selected from                  poly(arylene), poly(phenylene) and                  poly(styrene).2) The anion conducting polymer of the layer                  (A), comprises quaternary ammonium functional                  groups or phosphonium functional groups,                  preferably quaternary ammonium functional                  groups.3) The anion conducting polymer comprises                  alkyl or a piperidine side chain provided between                  a functional group and backbone of the anion                  conducting polymer.

PN CN113789137-A; CN113789137-B
TI Preparing pre-reaction liquid used in chlorinated rubber adhesive, involves mixing and stirring aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and distilled water for removing carbon dioxide at room temperature.
AU GENG H
   WANG D
   LI W
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 202200135U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pre-reaction liquid involves mixing and stirring aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and distilled water for removing carbon dioxide at room temperature, adjusting the pH of the obtained mixed solution to 7.0-10.0 by using alkali liquor under the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 100-1000 revolution per minute, then reacting for 0.5-3 hours under the protection of nitrogen and the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 500-1500 revolution per minute, placing the obtained mixture in a closed reaction kettle for reacting for 2-24 hours at 70-120degrees Celsius, filtering the obtained mixture, washing the filtrate by using the distilled water for removing the carbon dioxide until the filtrate is neutral, and reserving the filtered jelly for later use. The sodium phenolate and distilled water with carbon dioxide removed at room temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing pre-reaction liquid used in chlorinated rubber adhesive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps to prevent the pre-reaction liquid from producing solid-liquid stratification. The pre-reaction solution is applied to prepare the adhesive of chlorinated rubber to improve the adhesive force, heat resistance and storage stability of the adhesive. The pre-reaction solution is easy to prepare and is suitable for popularization and application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pre-reaction liquid involves mixing and stirring aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and distilled water for removing carbon dioxide at room temperature, adjusting the pH of the obtained mixed solution to 7.0-10.0 by using alkali liquor under the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 100-1000 revolution per minute, then reacting for 0.5-3 hours under the protection of nitrogen and the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 500-1500 revolution per minute, placing the obtained mixture in a closed reaction kettle for reacting for 2-24 hours at 70-120degrees Celsius, filtering the obtained mixture, washing the filtrate by using the distilled water for removing the carbon dioxide until the filtrate is neutral, and reserving the filtered jelly for later use. The sodium phenolate and distilled water with carbon dioxide removed at room temperature, then reacting for 10-48 hours under the protection of nitrogen and stirring at the rotating speed of 200-1200 revolution per minute. The obtained product is filtered, washed with distilled water with carbon dioxide removed, until the filtrate is neutral. The filtered precipitate is dried at 80-115degrees Celsius for 10-48 hours, and ground the dried solid into (300-500)-mesh white hydrotalcite-sodium phenolate composite powder for later use. The hydrotalcite-sodium phenolate composite powder and magnesium oxide are added into a mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and cyclohexane. The reflux reaction is carried out on the obtained mixture for 3-6 hours at the temperature of 45-70degrees Celsius and under the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 200-3000 revolution per minute, and then stand for 12-36 hours to obtain a solution containing the magnesium oxide-hydrotalcite-sodium phenolate composite. The p-tert-butylphenol formaldehyde resin is added into the solution containing the magnesium oxide-hydrotalcite-sodium phenolate compound and carried out reflux reaction on the obtained mixture for 6-10 hours at 25-60degrees Celsius and under the stirring condition of the rotating speed of 200-1200 revolution per minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: The alkali solution is 5-25 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution. The weight ratio of magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and carbon dioxide-removed water is (1-1.06):(0.32-0.43):(15-30). The weight ratio of the jelly, sodium phenolate and distilled water from which carbon dioxide is removed is 1:(0.22-0.46):(5-14). The magnesium oxide, the ethyl acetate and the cyclohexane is (0.5-1.5):(2-3.8):(1.2-2):(20-38). The weight ratio of the solution containing the magnesium oxide-hydrotalcite-sodium phenolate complex and p-tert-butylphenol formaldehyde resin is (24-46):(22-40).

   ZHOU C
AE GUANGZHOU FUDA THERMAL INSULATION MATERI (BOYH-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a primary base (2) and a secondary base (3) which are connected to both sides of a cylinder main portion (1). A striker groove (4) is provided on surface of primary base. A firing pin is movably connected to inner wall of firing pin slot. The surface of carbon dioxide gas pipe (10) is connected with compression block (11), one-way air valve (12), air inlet pipe (13) and air outlet pipe (14). The surface of compression cylinder is connected with the surface of compression block. The surface of the compression block is connected with the surface of the one-way air valve. An air valve is connected to the top surface of the first base through a fixing base. The inner wall of the air valve is provided with two filter element movable holes. The inner wall of the air valve is movably connected with a valve core, and the surface of air valve is connected with a vent pipe. The surface of vent pipe away from the air valve is communicated with the surface of compression cylinder.
   USE - Carbon dioxide pressurizing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The larger pressure is provided through the cylinder main portion and the compression cylinder, when the carbon dioxide is pressurized, so that the utilization rate of the gas pressure of device is increased. The pressure of carbon dioxide is completed without any external power, to achieve the effect of reducing the energy consumption of the pressurizing device. The device is made as environmentally friendly.

The surface of the movable plug is connected with the inner wall of the striker groove through a spring. The surface of the compression cylinder is penetrated to the surface of the primary base and is communicated with the inside of the cylinder block. A small piston (8) is movably connected to the inner wall of the compression cylinder. The end of the small piston away from the compression cylinder is penetrated to the surface of the primary base and extended to the inside of the cylinder block, and is connected with the large piston (9). The surface of the large piston is movably connected with the inner wall of the cylinder main portion. The surface of the compression cylinder away from the cylinder block is connected with a carbon dioxide gas pipe.

TI Preparing high temperature resistant concrete            block using carbon dioxide comprises e. g. taking            coarse aggregate, fine aggregate, common silicate            cement, fly ash and mineral powder, adding coarse            aggregate and mixing.
AU LIN Z

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature resistant concrete                block using carbon dioxide comprises e. g.    USE - The method is useful for preparing high                temperature resistant concrete block using carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the improvement of high                temperature resistance of concrete blocks,                effectively reduces the external damage of concrete                blocks under high temperature, effectively                increases the strength of concrete blocks after                high temperature, and effectively uses the waste                greenhouse gas-carbon dioxide gas and reduces                greenhouse gas emissions and protects the                environment and curbs the greenhouse effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high temperature resistant concrete                block using carbon dioxide comprises (i) taking                coarse aggregate, fine aggregate, common silicate                cement, fly ash and mineral powder as high                temperature resistant concrete blocks, where the                coarse aggregate particle size is 5-10 mm, the fine                aggregate uses continuous grading river sand, each                cubic is 600-800 Kg, the water absorption of fine                aggregate is 0.87%, and the water absorption of                coarse aggregate is 0.21%; (iii) adding coarse aggregate, adding                fine aggregates in sequence, adding gel material                (silicate cement, fly ash and mineral powder),                mixing uniformly, and adding water for 5 minutes;                (iv) mixing the mixture uniformly, pouring the                mixture into the mold of the block machine, where                each mold is about 700 g, the concrete block                machine is used for compression molding, and the                forming pressure is about 7.8 MPa, molding, and                adding all the test pieces into the carbon dioxide                curing box, where the curing temperature is 20 plus                minus 1 degree, relative humidity is 65 plus minus                1%, the carbon dioxide concentration is 20 plus                minus 1%, the natural conservation served as its                control group; and (v) testing samples in a                high-temperature furnace after 28 days of                maintenance, heating to 100 degrees C, 300 degrees                C, 500 degrees C, 600 degrees C and 800 degrees C                at room temperature, where the heating rate is 10                degrees c/minute, and curing the high temperature                resistance of the concrete blocks by carbon dioxide                evaluated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  concrete blocks used as raw material and a part                  of fly ash and mineral powder are used as gel                  material to improve the performance of concrete                  blocks at high temperature and 5-10 mm coarse                  aggregate is used. In the step (iv), the carbon                  dioxide curing method is adopted to improve the                  high temperature resistance of concrete                  blocks.

TI Chemical looping dry reforming of methane, comprises e.g. delivering a metal ferrite oxygen carrier comprising substituted metal ferrites to fuel reactor, delivering reduced metal ferrite to oxidation reactor, and oxidizing reduced carrier.
AU SIRIWARDANE R V
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 202020715M
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical looping dry reforming of methane, comprises: delivering a metal ferrite oxygen carrier comprising substituted metal ferrites (I) to a fuel reactor; delivering a fuel stream to the metal ferrite oxygen carrier in the fuel reactor and maintaining the fuel reactor at a reducing temperature; delivering the reduced metal ferrite to an oxidation reactor; oxidizing the reduced carrier by contacting the reduced carrier and carbon dioxide at an oxidizing temperature, where the oxidizing temperature is sufficient to generate an oxidizing reaction, reactants of the oxidizing reaction comprise some portion of the carbon dioxide, some portion of the M component, and some portion of the (FecOd) component where c is greater than 0 and d is greater than or equal to 0, a product of the oxidizing reaction is carbon monoxide and a re-oxidized carrier, the re-oxidized carrier comprises some portion of the (I); and withdrawing a product stream carbon monoxide from the oxidation reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for chemical looping dry reforming of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the metal ferrite used as a promoter to produce carbon monoxide continuously from coal gasification with carbon dioxide and for dry-reforming of methane and carbon dioxide to produce syngas not diluted with nitrogen and is low cost and environmentally safe as compared to materials previously used for conventional processes; adds metal ferrites to coal separately without impregnating the coal with metal ferrite solutions making a simple process for operation; and utilizes the metal ferrite oxygen carrier having improved durability and reactivity over metal oxides used in the dry reforming of methane, chemical looping dry reforming of fuels e.g. coal and methane and coal gasification with carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical looping dry reforming of methane, comprises: delivering a metal ferrite oxygen carrier comprising substituted metal ferrites of formula (MzFexOy) (I) to a fuel reactor; delivering a fuel stream to the metal ferrite oxygen carrier in the fuel reactor and maintaining the fuel reactor at a reducing temperature, where the reducing temperature is sufficient to reduce some portion of the metal ferrite oxygen carrier and oxidize some portion of the fuel stream, and generating gaseous products containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide in the fuel reactor; delivering the reduced metal ferrite to an oxidation reactor; oxidizing the reduced carrier by contacting the reduced carrier and carbon dioxide at an oxidizing temperature, where the oxidizing temperature is sufficient to generate an oxidizing reaction, reactants of the oxidizing reaction comprise some portion of the carbon dioxide, some portion of the M component, and some portion of the (FecOd) component where c is greater than 0 and d is greater than or equal to 0, a product of the oxidizing reaction is carbon monoxide and a re-oxidized carrier, the re-oxidized carrier comprises some portion of the (I); and withdrawing a product stream carbon monoxide from the oxidation reactor, where the gaseous products comprise the product stream, and at least greater than 50 vol.% of the product stream comprises carbon monoxide.
   x = 0-4, preferably 2-3;
   z = greater than 0, preferably 0-2;
   y = 0-6, preferably 3-5; and
   M = at least one of Mg, Ca, Ba and/or Sr.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (I) comprises at least 30 wt.% metal ferrite oxygen carrier. The metal ferrite oxygen carrier further comprises an inert support, where the inert support carbon comprises 5-70 wt.% metal ferrite oxygen carrier. The inert support comprises at least one of alumina, silica, zirconia, clay, titania and/or monolith. The mixing the fuel stream and the metal ferrite oxygen carrier in the fuel reactor step generates a reduced carrier, where the reduced carrier comprises a M component and a (FecOd) component, where the M component comprises some portion of the M comprising (I), and MO and the FecOd component comprises some portion of the Fe comprising the (I). The (FecOd) comprises iron(o), iron(II) oxide, Iron(II, III) oxide or iron(III) oxide and M comprises metal oxides (MO) or metal carbonates (MCO3).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reducing temperature is 500-1100 degrees C. Oxidization of the reduced carrier occurs in an oxidizing reactor and further comprises transferring the reduced carrier from the fuel reactor to the oxidizing reactor, supplying the carbon dioxide containing gas stream to the oxidizing reactor, thus generating the re-oxidized carrier, producing carbon monoxide, transferring the re-oxidized carrier from the oxidizing reactor to the fuel reactor, and repeating the delivering the metal ferrite oxygen carrier to the fuel reactor, introducing methane or coal to the metal ferrite oxygen carrier in the fuel reactor, and the withdrawing the product stream from both the fuel reactor and the oxidizing reactor. The oxidizing temperature is 500-1100 degrees C. The carbon dioxide gas stream may be provided by carbon dioxide separated from fuel combustion streams with air or fuel chemical looping combustion streams. Preferred Components: The fuel in the fuel reactor is coal, methane or bio mass. The methane comprises greater than 5 vol.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of bismuth-quantum dot-modified bismuth vanadium oxide hollow nanostructure in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide, by using solution containing vanadium source and bismuth source.
AU CHEN L
   ZHAO X
   DUAN Z
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201926056Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth-quantum dot-modified bismuth vanadium oxide hollow nanostructure involves: (1) mixing an aqueous solution containing a vanadium source and an ethylene glycol solution containing a bismuth source, and then performing Solvothermal reaction to obtain hollow cake-shaped bismuth vanadium oxide nanostructure composed of nanosheets; and (2) dispersing bismuth vanadium oxide in water to obtain a bismuth vanadium oxide dispersion, mixing the bismuth vanadium oxide dispersion with a reducing agent solution, followed by performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain a bismuth quantum dot-modified hollow cake-shaped bismuth vanadium oxide composite nanostructure, and forming the structure by in-situ growth of bismuth quantum dots on the surface of bismuth vanadium oxide particles.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a bismuth-quantum dot-modified bismuth vanadium oxide hollow nanostructure, which is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the bismuth-quantum dot-modified bismuth vanadium oxide hollow nanostructure with large specific surface area. The nanostructure provides more active sites for the photocatalytic reaction, and the bismuth quantum dots having a quantum confinement effect, which can directly and effectively reduce carbon dioxide. The bismuth/bismuth vanadium oxide composite photocatalyst utilizes the synergistic effect of bismuth vanadium oxide and bismuth, thus increasing the light response range, improving the separation efficiency of the hole pairs of the photogenerated electrons and simultaneously improving the conversion rate of the photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by utilizing the direct reaction of the bismuth quantum dots and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of bismuth-quantum dot-modified bismuth vanadium oxide hollow nanostructure involves: (1) mixing an aqueous solution containing a vanadium source and an ethylene glycol solution containing a bismuth source, and then performing Solvothermal reaction to obtain hollow cake-shaped bismuth vanadium oxide nanostructure composed of nanosheets; (2) dispersing bismuth vanadium oxide in water to obtain a bismuth vanadium oxide dispersion, mixing the bismuth vanadium oxide dispersion with a reducing agent solution, followed by performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain a bismuth quantum dot-modified hollow cake-shaped bismuth vanadium oxide composite nanostructure, and forming the structure by in-situ growth of bismuth quantum dots on the surface of bismuth vanadium oxide particles; and (3) dispersing the obtained bismuth/bismuth vanadium oxide composite photocatalyst in distilled water, ultrasonically processing, drying a film at 80 degrees C, carrying out photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by gas-solid reaction, placing the photocatalyst film in a cylindrical reactor, adding a small amount of distilled water to provide a saturated water vapor atmosphere, introducing carbon dioxide, and using a 300W xenon lamp as a light source to produce carbon monoxide. The molecular formula of bismuth vanadium oxide is Bi4V2O11. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for reducing carbon dioxide based on a bismuth/bismuth vanadium oxide composite composite photocatalyst, which involves the step (3) as per above se, where the volume ratio of bismuth/bismuth vanadium oxide composite photocatalyst and distilled water is (10-20) mg:10 mu l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (1), the Solvothermal reaction temperature is 120-200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-12 hours. In the step (2), the hydrothermal reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-8 hours. The reaction time in step (3) is 1-3 hours. Preferred Properties: In the step (1), the bismuth vanadium oxide particles have a particle diameter of 0.5-1.5 mu m, a cake shape, and a hollow structure. In the step (2), the size of the bismuth quantum dots in the bismuth/bismuth vanadium oxide composite nanostructure is 1-5 nm. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the bismuth vanadium oxide particles are composed of nanosheets. The molar ratio of vanadium to bismuth in the mixture is 1:1-1:2. The volume ratio of the aqueous solution containing vanadium source to the ethylene glycol solution containing bismuth source is 5:3-5:5. The bismuth source is bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate. The vanadium source is sodium metavanadate. In the step (2), the mass percentage of the bismuth quantum dots is adjusted to 3-20%, the concentration of the bismuth vanadium oxide dispersion is 0.005-0.01 mol/l, and the reducing agent comprises 80 %mass 0.04-0.02 mol/l hydrazine hydrate.

TI Fixing of carbon dioxide used for thermal power            plant, involves contacting mixed solution containing            chlorides of group 2 element and divalent metal            element, and sodium hydroxide with carbon dioxide, and            electrolyzing mixed solution.

   KENJI S
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)

GA 202076584L
AB    NOVELTY - Fixing of carbon dioxide involves contacting                mixed solution containing chloride of the group 2                element and the chloride of the divalent metal                element, and sodium hydroxide with a gas containing                carbon dioxide, electrolyzing the mixed solution                after the contact, to form a mixed solution after                electrolysis, and reusing the mixed solution after                electrolysis as the mixed solution for contacting                process. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide                in the mixed solution is 0.2 N or less.
   USE - Fixing of carbon dioxide used for thermal                power plant for treating combustion exhaust gas                containing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of immobilized carbon dioxide,                which involves fixing carbon dioxide; and
   (2) carbon dioxide fixing device (1), which                comprises carbon dioxide fixative charging unit                capable of charging the carbon dioxide fixative                into the reaction vessel including a reaction                chamber (11) containing a carbon dioxide fixative                and an electrolysis chamber (12), gas-liquid mixing                unit capable of mixing a gas containing carbon                dioxide into the carbon dioxide fixing agent housed                in the reaction chamber, and is inserted into the                reaction chamber, and the insertion end is a water                repellent, and is coated with an electronegative                material, electrolysis chamber includes an anode                chamber and a cathode chamber, transferring liquid                from the reaction chamber to the electrolysis                chamber, and from the electrolysis chamber to the                reaction chamber to react the carbon dioxide                fixative and carbon dioxide, feeding the carbon                dioxide fixative after the reaction from the                reaction chamber to the electrolysis chamber, the                carbon dioxide fixing agent after the reaction is                electrolyzed. The carbon dioxide fixing agent after                the electrolysis can be sent from the electrolytic                chamber to the reaction chamber. The carbon dioxide                fixing agent after the electrolysis can be sent                from the electrolytic chamber to the reaction                chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon                dioxide fixing device.
   Carbon dioxide fixing device (1)
   Reaction container (10)
   Reaction chamber (11)
   Electrolysis chamber (12)
   Partition (13A)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting process                  involves feeding the gas into the mixed liquid,                  and contacting the mixed liquid and the gas. The                  method further involves adjusting the                  concentration to detect the pH of the mixed                  solution, and based on the pH of the                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide is                  maintained to 0.2 N or less. The method involves                  contacting a solution containing sodium hydroxide                  with a gas containing carbon dioxide, diluting                  the concentration of sodium hydroxide to 0.2 N or                  less, adding chloride of a Group 2 element and a                  chloride of a divalent metal element,                  electrolyzing the mixed solution containing                  sodium hydroxide and chloride of the Group 2                  element and the chloride of a divalent metal                  element, and reusing the mixed solution after the                  electrolysis is reused as a solution containing                  the sodium hydroxide. Preferred Apparatus: The                  gas-liquid mixing unit is inserted into the                  reaction chamber, and holes are provided at the                  insertion end, and the gas can be released from                  the holes into the carbon dioxide fixative in the                  reaction chamber. The gas-liquid mixing means                  includes a liquid circulation channel and a pump.                  The liquid circulation flow path includes a                  liquid suction end and a liquid discharge end,                  where the liquid suction end is inserted into the                  electrolytic chamber, and the liquid discharge                  end is inserted into the reaction chamber. The                  carbon dioxide fixative can be sucked from the                  liquid suction end through the pump, and the                  sucked carbon dioxide fixative can be discharged                  from the liquid discharge end. The liquid                  circulation channel further includes a gas-liquid                  mixing component, and the gas-liquid mixing                  component is capable of mixing the gas with the                  liquid flowing through the liquid circulation                  channel. The reaction vessel includes a reaction                  vessel (R1) and a reaction vessel (R2), where the                  reaction vessel (R1) includes the reaction                  chamber, and the reaction vessel (R2) includes                  the electrolysis chamber. Preferred Components:                  The chloride of the group 2 element and the                  chloride of the divalent metal element comprise                  calcium chloride. The concentration of the                  calcium chloride in the mixed solution is 0.05                  mol/L or more. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide in the                  mixed solution is 0.05 N or more. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature of the mixed solution                  is 70 degrees C or more.

AE EXXONMOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2022561180
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing an input fluid                stream to generate an intermediary fluid stream,                and cooling the stream with a heat exchanger to                generate another stream. The latter stream is                expanded to generate a third stream, where the                third stream comprises solid carbon dioxide. The                third stream is separated into a fourth stream and                an output fluid stream that comprises a liquefied                natural gas (LNG) liquid. The fourth stream is                utilized as a cooling fluid stream for another                exchanger, and a fifth stream is generated. A sixth                stream is compressed and cooled within the former                exchanger followed by the latter exchanger.
   USE - Method for liquefying feed gas stream                comprising natural gas and carbon dioxide during                liquefied natural gas generation in hydrocarbon                recovery, refinement, or reservoir management                operation fields to enable production of subsurface                hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of liquefying a feed gas                stream comprising natural gas and carbon dioxide in                an economical and efficient manner.

   Compressor (110)
   Cooler (120)
   Heat exchanger (130)
   Throttle (150)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sixth intermediary                  fluid stream has a carbon dioxide mole fraction                  that is at least 5 times less than a carbon                  dioxide mole fraction of the second intermediary                  fluid stream. The mixed refrigerant loop includes                  a mixed refrigerant comprising nitrogen, methane,                  C2 hydrocarbons; and C4+ hydrocarbons. The first                  output fluid stream further comprises frozen                  carbon dioxide.

PN CN113842930-A
TI Upgrading metal oxide-phosphorus complex catalytic material useful in e.g. fields of light and electro-catalysis carbon dioxide reduction comprises e.g. simply pre-complexing commercially available metal oxide and phosphorus element by mixing, heating, removing oxide layer through slow oxidation.
AU LI J
   XIE Y
   WANG Y
   JUN A
   LI R
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202209834T
AB    NOVELTY - Upgrading metal oxide-phosphorus complex catalytic material comprises (i) simply pre-complexing the commercially available metal oxide and phosphorus element by mixing to obtain complex material, and (ii) placing the complex material in the chosen solvent, heating at 50-200degreesC under mild conditions, removing the oxide layer on the surface of the phosphorus element through slow oxidation, and facilitating contact between two-phase materials, where the solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, acetone, dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, and/or water, with or without added acid or with or without added base.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading metal oxide-phosphorus complex catalytic material, which is useful in the fields of light, electro-catalysis carbon dioxide reduction, carbon dioxide hydrogenation, carbon dioxide polymerization, hydrogen evolution and oxygen evolution (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, practical, clean and environmental friendly; and achieves a great improvement in the catalytic performance of commercially available common materials through.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a metal oxide-phosphorus complex catalytic material prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding acid and hydrochloric acid to the solvent water. The heating is carried out at 60-150degreesC. In the step (i), the traditional mixing for realizing simple pre-complexing comprises manual grinding, ball milling, ultrasonic, stirring and other material mixing technique in the field. The pre-complexing is carried out for 2-30 minutes, which may vary according to the specific situation to achieve initial contact of materials, and has no strict technical index requirements. In the step (ii), the heating under mild conditions is carried out in an airtight container for 8 hours to 3 days, where the volume of the airtight container is 1.5-3 times the volume of the solvent used, washing the obtained product and drying to complete the performance upgrade of the catalyst. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the metal oxide comprises titanium dioxide, copper oxide, tungsten oxide, zinc oxide, bismuth oxide, manganese oxide, iron oxide, chromium oxide, indium oxide, vanadium oxide, and/or cerium oxide. The phosphorus element comprises white phosphorus, yellow phosphorus, red phosphorus, black phosphorus, purple phosphorus, blue phosphorus, and/or scarlet phosphorus. The mass ratio of the commercially available metal oxide and the commercially available phosphorus elemental material is 0.1-10. The ratio of the complex material of the metal oxide and the elemental phosphorus to the solvent is 0.04-1 g:10 ml.
