TI Use of perovskite catalyst comprising lanthanide element, and chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt and/or nickel, and ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium and/or platinum, in catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare formic acid.
AU LI J
   LIAO X
   WANG H
   LI W
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2021A03757
AB    NOVELTY - Use of perovskite catalyst in catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare formic acid, is claimed. The perovskite catalyst is formed of A-site rare earth and B'-site transition metal in a molar ratio of 0.8:1-1:0.8, and comprises 0.01-4 wt.% noble metal. The A-site rare earth comprises a lanthanide element, and B'-site transition metal comprises chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt and/or nickel, and ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium and/or platinum.
   USE - Use of perovskite catalyst in catalyzing carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The perovskite catalyst has high catalytic activity and enables hydrogenation of carbon dioxide under mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the perovskite catalyst, involves adding water to A-site metal nitrate, B'-site metal nitrate, EDTA, and citric acid, dissolving, adjusting the pH to weak alkaline using aqueous ammonia solution, heating and stirring, drying the obtained sol, calcining to obtain a perovskite matrix, reducing the obtained perovskite matrix at 200-450 degrees C for 1-3 hours under a reducing atmosphere, and separating out portion of B'-site metal in-situ.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of A'-site rare earth and B'-site transition metal is preferably 1:0.85-1:1. The perovskite catalyst preferably comprises 0.5-2 wt.% noble metal. The lanthanide is lanthanum or cerium. Preferred Conditions: Heating and stirring in preparation of the perovskite catalyst is 70-120 degrees C. Calcination is carried out at 600-900 degrees C for 4-8 hours by heating at 2-5 degrees C/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide is carried out using sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution as carbon dioxide source, at a hydrogen pressure of 1-4 MPa, a reaction temperature of 50-200 degrees C, and at a stirring speed of 500-1000 rpm, for 5-24 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of total metal cations of A' site and B' site, EDTA, and citric acid is 1:1:1-1:1.5:1.5.

PN CN113278998-A
TI Preparation of biocathode material for reducing carbon dioxide in flue gas, involves dissolving aromatic diazonium salt and sodium nitrite in dilute hydrochloric acid, adopting three-electrode system, using pretreated rigid three-dimensional graphitized material as working electrode and drying.
AU WANG L
   ZHANG J
   LIAO M
   ZHENG P
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202199626U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of biocathode material involves (1) dissolving the aromatic diazonium salt and sodium nitrite in dilute hydrochloric acid at a concentration of 0.5-1 g/L to prepare an electrochemically reduced diazonium salt electrolyte, (2) adopting the three-electrode system according to the electrochemical reduction of the diazonium salt electrolyte, and using the silver-silver chloride electrode as the reference electrode, using the pretreated metal plate with high hydrogen evolution potential as the counter electrode, using the pretreated rigid three-dimensional graphitized material as the working electrode, setting the cathode potential fluctuation interval to -0.5-1 V until the amount of charge in the path is 0.1-0.2 C, washing working electrode in deionized water until the electrolyte is completely removed, and drying naturally at room temperature.
   USE - Preparation of biocathode material for reducing carbon dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple process and mild synthesis conditions, and the prepared bio-cathode material for reducing carbon dioxide in flue gas has good carbon dioxide capture ability and many reaction sites.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesizing method of the dilute hydrochloric acid involves mixing the mass fraction of 30-40 wt.% hydrochloric acid and deionized water at a volume ratio of 1:(20-30) to obtain dilute hydrochloric acid. Preparation of the high hydrogen evolution potential metal plate involves soaking the high hydrogen evolution potential metal plate in a hydrochloric acid solution with a mass fraction of 20-30% for 2-3 hours, washing with deionized water to neutral, soaking in sodium hydroxide solution with a concentration of 35-45 g/L for 2-3 hours, washing with deionized water until is neutral, and air drying naturally at room temperature until the surface is completely dry to obtain a pretreated metal plate with high hydrogen evolution potential. Preparation of the pretreated rigid three-dimensional graphitized material involves ultrasonically washing the rigid three-dimensional graphitized material in absolute ethanol, acetone, and deionized water for 5 minutes, and then heating at 50-70 degrees C, drying for 2-4 hours under the conditions to obtain the treated rigid three-dimensional graphitized material, and the acetone solution is analytically pure concentration.

AU YU Q
   LIU X
   LIU Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202151563N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a composite metal oxide including Ba element, Zr element and other elements, and the other elements are selected from at least one of Zn, Cu, Fe, Co, Ni, and Mn.
   USE - Catalyst for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst uses a composite metal oxide containing a specific type of metal element and a specific crystal phase structure as a catalyst, and can simultaneously obtain a higher CO2 conversion rate and CO selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst, which involves providing a salt solution containing the Ba element, Zr element and other elements;gelling the salt solution under the action of the precipitating agent to form a gel;diluting the gel, and make the diluted gel generate a precipitate under the action of the precipitating agent, and calcining the precipitate to prepare the catalyst; and
   (2) a method for preparing carbon monoxide by catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, which involves mixing a raw material gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen with the catalyst and contacting to generate carbon monoxide and water vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the feed gas is (1-3):1. The molar content of the Ba element being 1 mol, the molar content of the Zr element is 0.8-0.99 mol, preferably 0.9-0.99 mol, and the molar content of the other elements is 0.01-0.2 mol, Preferably it is 0.01-0.1 mole. The composite metal oxide has an ABO3- delta cubic fluorite perovskite structure, where delta refers to the amount of oxygen vacancy defects, and the value of delta is preferably 0.01-0.1. Preferred Process: The procedure of the roasting treatment includes raising the temperature from room temperature to a first intermediate temperature at a first heating rate, and maintaining the first intermediate temperature for 1-5 hours. The temperature is increased from the first intermediate temperature to a second intermediate temperature, and the temperature is maintained at the second intermediate temperature for 1-6 hours. The temperature is increased from the second intermediate temperature to the target temperature and maintained at the target temperature for 1-6 hours.

PN CN112138683-A
TI Constructing multi-interface electron transfer            system for efficient carbon dioxide photo-reduction            comprises e.g. (i) dissolving cadmium(II) chloride,            indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate and L-cysteine in            deionized water, and water washing.
AU LI X
   WANG H
   JIANG H
   GAO M
   ZHU Z
   MA C
   HUO P
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021030750
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing multi-interface electron transfer                system for efficient carbon dioxide photo-reduction                comprises (i) dissolving cadmium(II) chloride,                indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate and L-cysteine in                deionized water after stirring, transferring to a                stainless steel autoclave, and hydrothermally                reacting, washing precipitate with water and                ethanol, and drying to obtain 3D cadmium indium                sulfide micron curds, (ii) adding curd to hydrogen                tetrachloroaurate(III) tetrahydrate aqueous                solution, stirring under water bath, and                transferring to ultraviolet light, stirring and                irradiating, 3D-cadmium indium sulfide/gold binary                hybrid material can be obtained, and (iii)                subjecting 3D-cadmium indium sulfide/gold binary                hybrid material to ultrasonic and constant                temperature hydrothermal stirring treatment in an                aqueous solution with grphene oxide content, and                transferring to reactor, washing and drying to                obtain 3D-cadmium indium sulfide/gold/2DrGO                composite material.
   USE - The constructing multi-interface electron                transfer system useful for efficient carbon dioxide                photo-reduction to photo-catalytic conversion of                carbon dioxide molecules to prepare carbon monoxide                and methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is environmentally friendly and                has micro-flower-type morphology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                multi-interface electron transfer system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mass                  ratio of cadmium chloride, indium chloride, and                  L-cysteine is 0.18332:0.5805:0.4846 g, the                  temperature at which the hydrothermal reaction                  proceeds is 160 degrees C, and the time is 12                  hours. In the step (ii), the ratio of cadmium                  indium sulfide to tetrachloroaurate(III)                  tetrahydrate aqueous solution is 100 mg:1-4 ml,                  where the concentration of tetrachloroaurate(III)                  tetrahydrate aqueous solution is cAu = 0.0024 mu                  mol/l, and the temperature of thermostatic                  hydrothermal stirring is 60 degrees C. In the                  step (2), the stirring time under the heating                  condition of the water bath is 30 minutes, and                  the stirring irradiation time under the                  ultraviolet light condition is 30 minutes. In the                  step (3), the dosage ratio of the 3D cadmium                  indium sulfide/gold binary hybrid material to                  graphene is 200:2-20 mg. In the step (3), the                  reaction temperature in the reaction kettle is                  150 degrees C, and the time is 5 hours.

PN CN112127888-A; CN112127888-B
TI Method for processing top, direct top and previous            top layered coal in coal mining field, involves            dividing rest unit of air return lane and transport            lane into multiple sections, and utilizing cracking            tube to crack air return lane.
AU WANG M
   AN Y
AE SHANXI XINQIAO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202103018X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing multiple                first cracking holes along length direction of                open-cut. A comprehensive mining bracket is                mounted. A first carbon dioxide cracking tube is                mounted in the first cracking hole. An opening and                cutting hole is cracked by using the first carbon                dioxide cracking tube. Multiple second cracking                holes are pre-constructed from a head of an air                return lane along length direction of the return                airway. A second carbon dioxide cracking tube and a                third carbon dioxide cracking tube are used to                crack the air return lane and a transport lane. A                rest unit of the air return lane and the transport                lane are divided into multiple sections until the                crack of the air return lane and the transport lane                is finished.
   USE - Method for processing top, direct top and                previous top layered coal in a coal mining                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding generation of                harmful gas in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                setting mode diagram of a first cracking hole, a                second cracking hole and a third cracking                hole.

PN CN110981168-A
TI Preparing ultra-high quality whispering wall mode micro-rod cavity comprises e.g. clamping the quartz preform with a diameter of millimeter on air bearing to rotate, and adjusting the focus of carbon dioxide laser to surface of the preform.
AU WANG K
   YANG Y
   WANG M
   SHEN Y
   MENG L
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202031666V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-high quality whispering wall mode micro-rod cavity comprises (i) clamping the quartz preform with a diameter of millimeter on the air bearing to rotate, (ii) adjusting the focus of the carbon dioxide laser to the surface of the preform, (iii) increasing the power of the carbon dioxide laser and gradually moving the three-dimensional mobile stage to form an ablated ring after melting one circle, (iv) moving the 3D mobile station to the next position and continue to ablate another ring, (v) increasing the power of the carbon dioxide laser and heating for short time between the two rings, fused quartz will naturally form an ultra-smooth surface under the effect of surface tension to obtain micro-rod cavity with a quality factor of 109 between the two rings.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-high quality whispering wall mode micro-rod cavity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is fast and simple compared with other existing method. The micro rod cavity processed has high stability, ultra-high quality factor, and controllable surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the quartz preform with a diameter of millimeter is a round rod made of quartz with a purity of 99.9999%. In step (i), the rotation speed of the air bearing reaches 500-1000 revolutions per minute. In step (iii), the three-dimensional mobile station has nanometer accuracy, and the carbon dioxide laser power is adjustable from 0-40 W. In step (iii), the power of the carbon dioxide laser is increased, and the three-dimensional mobile stage is gradually moved to form an ablated ring after melting one circle, and it is to adjust the power of the carbon dioxide laser to 20 W and move the air bearing close to the laser focus until the groove is 0.5 mm deep. In step (iv), move the 3D mobile station to the next position and continue to ablate another ring, where the distance between the moving laser focus position and the last melted groove is 0.3-1 mm. In step (v), increasing the power of the carbon dioxide laser is to increase the power of the carbon dioxide laser to 30 W. In step (v), the short time heating between the two rings is to heat the laser focus at the protruding part between the two rings for 30 seconds. In step (v), the micro rod cavity with a quality factor of 109 order between the two rings is 0.5-8 mm in diameter, the distance between the two rings is 0.1-10 mm, and the echo wall with a quality factor of 109 order Mode optical microcavity. In step (v), a micro rod cavity with a quality factor of 109 order can be obtained between the two rings, quality factor detection uses a tapered fiber waveguide to couple the frequency-swept laser into the microrod cavity to test the transmission spectrum, and the Lorenz linear fitting is used to calculate the quality factor for the resonance peak in the transmission spectrum.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a main body for collecting acidic gas contained in exhaust gas after combustion. The main body is provided with an absorption tower (2) to generate absorption by combining liquid exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide and an absorbent with each other. PN CN109717356-A; CN109717356-B
TI Liquid nitrogen combined with liquid carbon dioxide Cordyceps sinensis quick-freezing fresh-keeping method involves washing Cordyceps sinensis with water, ultrasonic cleaning with calcium lactate aqueous solution, and draining.
AU BI H
   WEI L
   XIAO Y
   DU Y
   YANG H
AE NORTHWEST INST PLATEAU BIOLOGY CHINESE A (CAPB-C)
GA 2019429562
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid nitrogen combined with liquid carbon dioxide Cordyceps sinensis quick-freezing fresh-keeping method comprises washing Cordyceps sinensis with water at 0-10 degrees C, ultrasonic cleaning 1-5 times for 5-10 minutes with calcium lactate aqueous solution, draining, adding low temperature protective agent comprising 0.3-0.5 mol/L of propylene glycol, 1-3 mmol/L sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/L vitamin C or or iso-vitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/L phytic acid and 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine, soaking for 2-5 minutes, taking out, spraying acetic acid/citric acid and liquid nitrogen mist, spraying liquid carbon dioxide at - 20 to - 18 degrees C, freezing at - 120 to - 90 degrees C at pH value of 4-6 for 5-10 minutes, spraying preservative comprising 30-100 mg/mL Cordyceps mycelium extract, 50-200 mg/L chitosan oligosaccharide, 100-200 mg/L antibacterial peptide and water, packing, and refrigerating at - 20 to ?10 degrees C.
   USE - Liquid nitrogen combined with liquid carbon dioxide Cordyceps sinensis quick-freezing fresh-keeping method.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high soluble protein content and has low polyphenol oxidase activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration of calcium lactate in calcium lactate solution is 0.1-0.5%. The Cordyceps mycelium extract includes guanine, cordycepin cytidine, hypoxanthine, xanthine and adenosine. The antimicrobial peptide comprises nisin antimicrobial peptide, Streptomyces antimicrobial peptide and Pediococcus antimicrobial peptide. Preferred Methods: The ultrasonic cleaning is carried out at power of 250-1200 W and frequency of 40-120 kHz at 0-10 degrees C. The Cordyceps mycelium extract is prepared by inoculating activated Cordyceps strain into liquid culture medium, fermenting, filtering, obtaining fermentated liquor, mixing with 70-80 wt.% ethanol aqueous solution, centrifuging, taking supernatant, decompressing and distilling, and freeze drying. The water temperature is 0 degrees C. The packing is carried out at oxygen concentration of 55-65% and carbon dioxide concentration of 35-45%.

PN CN108404889-A
TI Preparation of chitosan skeleton carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves reacting chitosan powder, acetic acid solution, ion exchange resin particles, sodium carbonate solution, glutaraldehyde solution, heating, cooling and lyophilizing.
AU SONG J
   ZHU L
   CHEN X
   YAN Y
AE XIAN HONGJUN RUIZE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018660329
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of chitosan skeleton carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves (1) adding chitosan powder to predetermined amount of 2% acetic acid solution and stirring until chitosan is completely dissolved to obtain chitosan solution, (2) immersing ion exchange resin particles in 0.5 mol/L sodium carbonate solution and stirring for 24 hours, rinsing resin particles 3 times with deionized water, and drying resin particles at room temperature, (3) grinding the dried resin particles into resin powder, (4) adding resin powder to chitosan solution and stirring to obtain suspension (A), adding 10 vol.% glutaraldehyde solution to suspension (A), stirring well, transferring into glass bottle and sealing using bottle cap, the volume ratio of glutaraldehyde solution and acetic acid solution in suspension (A) is 1 ml:20 ml, (5) heating glass bottle in drying oven at 60 degrees C for 6 hours, opening glass bottle cap, cooling to -40 degrees C, lyophilizing for 48 hours.
   USE - Preparation of chitosan skeleton carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of chitosan skeleton carbon dioxide adsorbent with uniform pore diameter, high porosity and large specific surface area by simple and economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (1), the mass ratio of chitosan and acetic acid solution is 1 g:40 ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (2), the ion exchange resin is D201 macroporous styrene anion exchange resin. Preferred Properties: In step (3), the resin powder is 160 mesh, 200 mesh or 400 mesh.

PN CN107828507-A
TI Refining tea seed oil comprises e.g. preparing tea seed kernels, extracting primary oil from the oil-tea seed by using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process by two level vacuum extracting and separating and refining primary oil.
AU FENG Z
   LIANG Z
   ZHU L
   ZHU B
AE GUANGZHOU HAOLI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201825097E
AB    NOVELTY - Refining tea seed oil comprises (i) preparing tea seed kernels; (ii) extracting primary oil from the oil-tea seed by using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process in 20-40 MPa at 35-70 degrees C for 1-4 hours by two level vacuum extracting and separating; and (iii) refining primary oil refined by at least two stages of molecular distillation by using the molecular distillation process.
   USE - The method is useful for refining tea seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes a physical process, does not produce materials that pollute the environment, and product does not have any residual solvent, achieves environmental protection, produces high-grade edible oils meets people's demands for health and environmental protection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tea seed oil refining device, comprising a supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, including an extracting kettle are sequentially connected by the pipeline in series and a two-stage separating kettle, separating kettle is equipped with stainless steel silk screen filling which is connected to the super-critical carbon dioxide extraction device connected in series of the molecular distillation device and including at least two-stage molecular distillation column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the two-stage vacuum extraction and separation conditions includes: first-stage pressure is 5-12 MPa, where separation temperature is 35-50 degrees C, where two-stage decompression separation pressure is 4-8 MPa, and the secondary separation temperature is 35-50 degrees C. In the step (iii), the primary oil is scouring to obtain tea seed oil by using three-stage molecular distillation, where the primary molecular distillation pressure is 100-3000 Pa, the distillation temperature is 70-130 degrees C to obtain first light component and first heavy component, subjecting the first heavy component in to the secondary molecular distillation, where the second molecular distillation pressure is 1-50 Pa, and distilling at 120-180 degrees C to obtain two light components and two heavy components, subjecting secondary heavy components in to tertiary molecular distillation, where the tertiary molecular distillation pressure is 0.1-10 Pa, and the tertiary molecular distillation temperature is 130-190 degrees C to obtain the tertiary light component and tertiary heavy component, and tertiary heavy component is tea seed oil. In the step (i), drying the tea seed oil at low temperature and shelling to obtain tea seed kernels. The stainless steel mesh packing is woven into a mesh shape by a stainless steel wire and formed by stacking.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The separating kettle comprises a separation kettle, separating the bottom of the kettle is discharge hole. The middle upper part is provided with an air outlet. The separation kettle is equipped with stainless steel silk screen stuffing is connected with outlet of the feed pipe. The separating kettle body at least 1/2 of space is equipped with stainless steel silk screen padding and the outlet of the feed pipeline embedded in the stainless steel silk screen stuffing or from up to down through stainless steel silk screen padding. The two-stage separating kettle is connected with the molecular distilling device. The upper part in the last grade separating kettle and recycling the carbon dioxide storage tank, recycling carbon dioxide storage tank connected to the high pressure pump, heater and the extraction kettle is connected to form a loop. The molecular distillation device comprises three-stage molecular distillation column.

Sodium carbonate is converted                to sodium bicarbonate to form a carbon dioxide                enriched liquid. The flue gas is entered into the                secondary absorption tower absorption from the                primary absorption tower, to react the sodium                hydroxide solution with residual carbon dioxide in                flue gas in the secondary absorption tower to                produce sodium carbonate solution as the carbon                dioxide lean. The purified flue gas is entered into a                secondary absorption tower to contact the flue gas                with sodium carbonate solution in the absorption                tower and to absorb carbon dioxide by the sodium                carbonate solution. Sodium carbonate is converted                to sodium bicarbonate to form a carbon dioxide                enriched liquid. The flue gas is entered into the                secondary absorption tower absorption from the                primary absorption tower, to react the sodium                hydroxide solution with residual carbon dioxide in                flue gas in the secondary absorption tower to                produce sodium carbonate solution as the carbon                dioxide lean. The secondary flue gas absorption of                the secondary absorption tower is finished after                emptying, followed by entering the flue gas to a                secondary absorption tower for carbon dioxide                barren resorption of carbon dioxide in flue gas,                entering the first carbon dioxide-enriched liquid                in the absorption tower into a carbon dioxide                desorbing system and transferring to a heat                exchanger with carbon dioxide rich flue gas waste                heat, rising the temperature of the carbon dioxide                rich liquid, sodium bicarbonate decomposition                released in the carbon dioxide rich liquid, and the                carbon dioxide rich barren liquor, collecting the                carbon dioxide by a carbon dioxide collecting                system, and passing the carbon dioxide of the                carbon dioxide lean into the sodium hydroxide                regeneration system to generate sodium hydroxide                solution. The regenerated sodium hydroxide solution                is passed into the secondary absorption tower which                continuously absorbs carbon dioxide in the flue                gas.

PN WO2022103098-A1; KR2022064689-A
TI Ground reinforcement material used for reinforcing ground, comprises gas hydrate-soil mixture.
AU CHO G C
   KANG S J
   KANG C W
   KANG S
   HWAN K C
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202267473C
AB    NOVELTY - Ground reinforcement material comprises a gas hydrate-soil mixture.
   USE - The ground reinforcement material is useful for reinforcing ground.
   ADVANTAGE - The ground reinforcement material has almost no environmental hazards and toxicity and can even serve as a geological storage of carbon dioxide on the ground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a composition for a ground reinforcement comprising a gas hydrate raw material and an accelerator; and
   (2) a method of reinforcing ground using the ground reinforcement material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: In the method, the ground reinforcement material is provided by injecting a gas hydrate raw material into the high-depth ground to form a gas hydrate-soil mixture. Preferred Components: The gas hydrate solidifies between the soil particles. The gas comprises methane, carbon dioxide, ethane, propane, isobutane, a mixture of methane and neohexane, or a mixture of methane and cycloheptane. The soil comprises at least one chosen from sand, silty sand, sandy silt, silt, clay, gravel soil, and sandy soil. The gas hydrate raw material comprises methane, carbon dioxide, ethane, propane, isobutane, a mixture of methane and neohexane, or a mixture of methane and cycloheptane. The accelerator comprises sodium dodecyl sulfate and/or graphene. The ground is a seabed ground; underwater ground; or including hot and cold ground. Preferred Composition: In the ground reinforcement material, the saturation of the gas hydrate is greater than or equal to 40%. The gas hydrate-soil mixture comprises 0.1-20 pts. wt. gas hydrate based on 100 pts. wt. soil. The accelerator is included in an amount of 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. based on composition for ground reinforcement. Preferred Properties: In the ground reinforcement material, the gas hydrate reduces the permeability by filling the space between the soil particles. In the method, the injection pressure of the gas hydrate raw material is higher than the pore pressure inside the ground, and is lower than the shear strength of the ground.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The electrode is prepared by adding binder to the solvent, and stirring the mixture at 20-50 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours to form a polymer solution having a mass concentration of 2-40%, adding the active material or the mixture of the active material and the carbon material to the above solution, and stirring at a temperature of 20-50 degrees C for 2-10 hours to prepare a slurry where the solid content is between 5-40 wt. %, pouring the slurry onto a substrate, scraping and coating, and placing in a low temperature environment -200-0 degrees C till the solvent is completely solidified where the coating agent is 0.5-20 mg/cm2, the substrate is aluminum foil, copper foil, carbon paper or carbon felt and vacuum drying the solidified product. Preferred Components: The solvent is water, methanol, ethanol, propanol, isopropanol, dimethylformamide, nitromethylpyrrolidone, dimethylacetamide, organic solvent, and/or methyl sulfoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder is carboxymethyl cellulose-styrene-butadiene rubber, beta -cyclodextrin, polyvinyl alcohol, polyacrylonitrile, polymethyl methacrylate, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polystyrene, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, poly vinyl fluoride, polyethersulfone, and/or polyvinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene copolymer.

PN CN109205937-A
TI Biochemical sludge zero-emission sewage treatment process involves using activated sludge, treating resulting excess sludge via anaerobic and/or aerobic treatment to produce protein and glycogen, and recycling.
AU LIU Y
AE SHANDONG TIANYI WATER ENG CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019089452
AB    NOVELTY - A biochemical sludge zero-emission sewage treatment process comprises using activated sludge, treating resulting excess sludge via anaerobic and/or aerobic treatment to produce protein and glycogen, recycling back to sewage treatment system for repeated circulation, and decomposing into carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen.
   USE - Biochemical sludge zero-emission sewage treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The biochemical sludge zero-emission sewage treatment process comprises:(A) Anaerobic/anoxic/oxic sewage treatment process comprising filtering sewage to remove large impurities, entering into primary settling tank, precipitating, separating and discharging precipitated anaerobic sludge, entering into anaerobic/anoxic/oxic tank, performing anaerobic, facultative, and aerobic degradation, entering into secondary settling tank, separating mud water, discharging sewage, separating excess sludge, performing anaerobic treatment to form protein and glycogen, refluxing, and repeatedly decomposing into carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen;(B) Anoxic/oxic sewage treatment process comprising filtering sewage to remove large impurities, entering into primary settling tank, precipitating, separating and discharging precipitated anaerobic sludge, entering into anoxic/oxic tank, carrying out anoxic and aerobic degradation, entering into secondary settling tank, separating mud water, discharging sewage, separating excess sludge, performing anaerobic treatment to form protein and glycogen, refluxing, and repeatedly decomposing into carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen; and(C) sequencing batch reactor treatment process comprising filtering sewage to remove large impurities, entering into primary settling tank, precipitating, separating and discharging precipitated anaerobic sludge, entering into sequencing batch reactor, homogenizing and settling, biodegrading, entering into secondary settling tank, separating mud water, discharging sewage, separating excess sludge, performing anaerobic treatment to form protein and glycogen, refluxing, and repeatedly decomposing into carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen.

PN WO2022182781-A1
TI Capturing carbon dioxide involves contacting gas            containing carbon dioxide with alkaline solution for            time sufficient for carbon dioxide to dissolve;            concentrating solution; and extracting dissolved carbon            dioxide from concentrated solution.
AU RINBERG A
   BERGMAN A M
   SCHRAG D P
   AZIZ M J
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 2022B2468V
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide comprises: (a)                providing an alkaline solution comprising dissolved                carbon dioxide; (b) concentrating the solution                using reverse osmosis or capacitive deionization,                where the concentrating selectively retains                bicarbonate ions over carbonate ions in the                concentrated solution; and (c) extracting the                carbon dioxide from the solution. The method                further comprises diluting the solution, capturing                carbon dioxide in the diluted solution, and                repeating the above steps with diluted solution.                Carbon dioxide extracted from concentrated solution                is collected. The solution comprises polyprotic                weak base and/or weak acid.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide, where the carbon dioxide is used as an                essential method for responding to anthropogenic                climate change.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides direct air capture of                carbon dioxide using an alkalinity concentration                swing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for capturing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the line plots the dissolved                inorganic carbon concentration in equilibrium with                atmospheric carbon dioxide (p is equal to 0.4 mbar)                as a function of alkalinity.

PN DE102021125784-A1
TI Method for operating steelmill for production of crude steel, involves adjusting energy supply to reduce carbon dioxide footprint value below carbon dioxide footprint threshold if overall carbon dioxide balance value exceeds threshold.
AU JAGER N
   SCHUBERT D
   AHRENHOLD F
   WEINBERG M
   BECKER R
   SUER J
AE THYSSENKRUPP STEEL EURO AG (THYS-C)
GA 2022528025
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining carbon dioxide footprint value for each source material flow, by-product material flow and energy flow considered. The carbon dioxide footprint initial value is determined for each raw material flow and for each energy flow. The carbon dioxide footprint connection value is determined for each by-product material flow. The total carbon dioxide balance value is formed from the carbon dioxide footprint values. The carbon dioxide footprint values and the overall carbon dioxide balance value are continuously updated. The supply of raw materials and/or the energy supply are adjusted to reduce the carbon dioxide footprint value below the carbon dioxide footprint threshold if the overall carbon dioxide balance value exceeds a predetermined carbon dioxide footprint threshold.
   USE - Method for operating steelmill, used during production of crude steel from primary steel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The economic efficiency is improved in steel production, so as to ensure a comprehensive examination of the production process.

PT P
PN IN202031001325-A
TI Chromogenic nanocomposite detector used for detection and quantification of carbon dioxide, comprises nanocomposite carbon dioxide sensor containing mesoporous silica nanoflowers, chromogen, and phenolphthalein molecule.
AU SUBHRA J
   PAL S K
   KUNDU A K
   BHATTACHARYYA D
AE BOSE NAT CENT BASIC SCI S N (BOSE-C)
GA 202014178U
AB    NOVELTY - A chromogenic nanocomposite-based carbon dioxide detector comprises chromogenic nano composite carbon dioxide sensor containing functionalized monodispersed mesoporous silica nanoflowers, a chromogen, and phenolphthalein molecule for carbon dioxide adsorption based sensing and detection.
   USE - Chromogenic nanocomposite-based carbon dioxide detector for detection and quantification of carbon dioxide (claimed) for diagnozing chronic obstructive pulmonary disorder.
   ADVANTAGE - The economical chromogenic nanocomposite-based carbon dioxide detector has increased adsorption capability, carbon dioxide uptake capacity with fast adsorption kinetics and also holds multi-cycles adsorption-desorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of chromogenic nanocomposite-based carbon dioxide detector; and
   (2) measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide, which involves recording absorbance of paper containing chromogenic nanocomposite, taking the paper, inserting in round bottom flask fitted with rubber septum and two adapters, evacuating round bottom flask for 5 minutes ensuring complete removal of ambient carbon dioxide from flask, injecting carbon dioxide gas having unknown concentration, equilibrating for one minute, removing paper from flask, recording spectrum, and providing concentration of carbon dioxide as display.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The colorless amine silica nanoflowers have intense pink color encapsulation of phenolphthalein to adsorb carbon dioxide by visual color change of fading pink color contact with carbon dioxide. The chromogenic nanocomposite has carbon dioxide uptake capacity of 1.15-1.2 mmol g1 for 10-15 minutes. The nanocomposite has mesoporous structure with specific surface area of 165-175 m2 g-1 and average pore size of 3.4 nm. The special amine efficiency is 0.42-0.45 (mmol g-1co2/mmol g-1n). Preferred Component: The chromogenic nanocomposite based optical device has optical spectroscopic. The measuring concentration diffuse reflectance mode or gradual fading of color based on carbon dioxide adsorption and quantification by change of absorbance. The micro spectrometer with operating electronic module, light source containing optical fibers for transmission of light from source and reflected surface containing an excitation fiber (E), collection fiber (C) electronic module is operatively connected and controlled for carried out detection process starting from signal acquisition to display. Preferred Composition: The sensor comprises 0.1 g of silica nanoflowers dispersed in 1.5-2.5 preferably 2.0 mL water and 1% of phenolphthalein in ethanol. The optical device has dynamic range of the CapNanoScope for the detection of carbon dioxide ranging from 0.04-7.0% and sensitivity with lower detection limit of 400 ppm of carbon dioxide (0.04%). Preferred Process: The preparation of chromogenic nanocomposite involves taking silica nanoflowers into a round bottom flask containing toluene, deaearating the reaction mixture under stirring condition, injecting N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl) propyl) ethylenediamine into flask at 55-65 degrees C preferably at 60 degrees C, refluxing the reaction mixture, cooling to room temperature, collecting product through filtration, washing with toluene and ethanol by drying, mixing aqueous solution of phenolphthalein and with the product in glass vial, stirring, aging mixture at room temperature under static condition which the nanocomposite becomes pink in color, centrifuging pink mixture to get phenolphthalein encapsulated chromogenic silica, redispersing in water to get chromogenic nano composites ink for drop casting on white paper to be used as sensor for carbon dioxide. The concentration of carbon dioxide involving chromogenic nanocomposite based carbon dioxide detector comprising recording the absorbance of paper containing chromogenic nano composites, taking out paper, inserting in round bottom flask fitted with rubber septum and two adapters, evacuating the round bottom flask for 5 minutes ensuring complete removal of ambient carbon dioxide from flask, carbon dioxide gas having unknown concentration to sample flasks, equilibrating for one minute, removing paper from flask recording spectrum and providing concentration of carbon dioxide as display.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.    USE - The method is useful for preparing ethylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that present heterogeneous catalyst activity is low.

The alkaline earth metal is magnesium, calcium, strontium or barium. The alkaline earth metal is calcium or barium. In the phosphorus-aluminum oxide carrier APO, the aluminum content in oxygen carrier re-speed by aluminum oxide is 10-80 wt.%; preferably 20-60 wt.% and the content of phosphorus is 20-90 wt.%; preferably 40-80 wt.% to phosphorus pentoxide. TI Carbon dioxide snow-making kit used to charge            carbon dioxide and generate carbon dioxide snow block            in-situ within container, comprises container, internal            meshed conduit volume, snow chamber, meshed conduit and            carbon dioxide snow charger.
AU ZHOU Y
   BURSAC R
   SEVER R R
AE PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
GA 2018030470
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide snow-making kit (1) for                charging carbon dioxide and generating carbon                dioxide snow block in-situ within a container (2),                comprises: container comprising an interior volume                defined into a first region and a second region;                internal meshed conduit volume configured to                receive a meshed conduit (3); snow chamber                surrounding an exterior of the meshed conduit;                meshed conduit situated within an opening (7) of                the container and extending into the internal                meshed conduit volume; and carbon dioxide snow                charger (4).
   USE - The carbon dioxide snow-making kit is useful                for charging carbon dioxide and generating carbon                dioxide snow block in-situ within a                container.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide snow-making kit: utilizes                the transportable container which exhibits                sufficient temperature hold or endurance; and                generates the in-situ carbon dioxide snow block                with improved packing density that can hold the                requisite temperature of the container with                extended cooling effect duration in comparison to                standard dry ice shipping containers containing                carbon dioxide dry ice produced by conventional                techniques.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide snow-making kit (1) for                charging carbon dioxide and generating carbon                dioxide snow block in-situ within a container (2),                comprises: a container comprising an interior                volume defined into a first region and a second                region, where the first region is an internal                meshed conduit volume, and the second region is a                snow chamber into which carbon dioxide snow block                is generated and stored, the interior volume                further surrounded by multiple container walls; the                internal meshed conduit volume configured to                receive a meshed conduit (3); the snow chamber                surrounding an exterior of the meshed conduit and                at least partially encapsulated by the multiple                container walls; the meshed conduit situated within                an opening (7) of the container and extending into                the internal meshed conduit volume, where the                meshed conduit comprises porous openings sufficient                for gas to pass through, but for the carbon dioxide                snow block to remain substantially within the snow                chamber that is external to the meshed tube, the                meshed conduit further includes an internal                passageway for carbon dioxide off gas to exhaust,                the meshed conduit comprises a first end and second                end, and the first end is oriented at the opening                of the container; and a carbon dioxide snow charger                (4) operably or integrally connected to the first                end of the meshed conduit along the opening of the                container, where the carbon dioxide snow charger                comprises a conduit network with many nozzles, the                nozzles are configured to selectively direct carbon                dioxide fluid into the snow chamber while avoiding                the introduction of carbon dioxide fluid into the                internal passageway of the meshed conduit, the snow                charger further comprises at least one opening in                fluid communication with the internal passageway of                the meshed conduit, and the openings are configured                to allow gas to vent and exit the container.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) in-situ charging (m1) carbon dioxide snow                block within a container, comprising (a) supplying                carbon dioxide liquid into a carbon dioxide snow                charger operably or integrally connected to a                meshed conduit that is located within an interior                of the container, (b) introducing the carbon                dioxide liquid into nozzles of a conduit network of                the carbon dioxide snow charger, (c) selectively                directing the carbon dioxide fluid into a snow                chamber of the container that is external to the                meshed conduit, (d) generating in-situ carbon                dioxide snow particles and gas in the snow chamber,                (e) packing the carbon dioxide snow particles to                form a carbon dioxide snow block characterized by a                hollow passageway along the carbon dioxide snow                block, (f) passing the gas through the meshed                conduit, and (g) exhausting the gas along an                internal passageway of the meshed conduit;
   (2) assembling (m2) a carbon dioxide snow                charging system at a site for in-situ generation of                carbon dioxide snow block in a container,                comprising (a1) providing a liquid carbon dioxide                source, (b1) providing an insulated container with                an opening, where the container further comprises                an interior volume defined into a first region and                a second region, the first region is an internal                meshed conduit volume, and the second region is a                snow chamber, (c1) providing a meshed conduit, (d1)                providing a carbon dioxide snow charger comprising                a conduit network having an inlet opening to                receive the liquid carbon dioxide source and a                plurality of nozzles distributed along an edge of                the conduit network, where the carbon dioxide snow                charger further comprises at least one exhaust                opening for gas to exhaust, (e1) operably                connecting or integrally joining a bottom section                of the carbon dioxide snow charger to a top section                of the meshed conduit, (f1) inserting the meshed                conduit through the top opening of the container                into the internal meshed conduit volume, (g1)                securing a top of the carbon dioxide snow charger                to the container at the top opening of the                container, and (h1) operably connecting the inlet                opening of the carbon dioxide snow charger to the                carbon dioxide source;
   (3) a carbon dioxide snow charger system                adapted to produce carbon dioxide snow block,                comprising the meshed conduit, and the carbon                dioxide snow charger operably or integrally                connected to the meshed conduit, where the meshed                conduit comprises porous openings sufficient for                carbon dioxide off-gas to pass through the openings                into an internal passageway of the meshed conduit,                but block entry of particles from the carbon                dioxide snow block into the internal passageway,                and the meshed conduit is characterized by                sufficient rigidity to pack the carbon dioxide snow                block generated external to the meshed                conduit;
   (4) an apparatus configured for storing,                preserving or transporting at least one item;                and
   (5) assembling (m3) a carbon dioxide snow                charging system at a site for in-situ generation of                carbon dioxide snow block in a container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a cross sectional view of                the carbon dioxide snow-making kit.
   Carbon dioxide snow-making kit (1)
   Container (2)
   Meshed conduit (3)
   Snow charger (4)
   Opening of the container (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The snow chamber is                  characterized by an absence of absorbent or                  foam-filled material. The carbon dioxide snow                  charger further comprises a sleeve extending                  around an edge of the conduit network to form a                  seal with the container. The nozzles are oriented                  away from a vertical wall of the meshed conduit                  at an angle of 30-60 degrees relative to the                  vertical wall, where the vertical wall extends                  perpendicular to a horizontal surface of the                  container. The carbon dioxide snow charger                  comprises an aperture of the conduit network,                  where the aperture is configured to receive                  carbon dioxide fluid from a carbon dioxide                  source. The nozzles are located along a periphery                  of the conduit network, where the aperture is                  connected to the nozzles located along ring-like                  structure, and the aperture is configured to be                  operably connected to a liquid carbon dioxide                  source. The first end of the mesh conduit is                  operably connected to the carbon dioxide snow                  charger. The kit further comprises a unique                  identifier on the container that is configured to                  embed container identification information, where                  the container identification information is                  retrievable by reading the unique identifier.                  Preferred Method: The method (m1) further                  comprises: alternating liquid carbon dioxide                  supply with introduction of a gaseous carbon                  dioxide from a carbon dioxide source into the                  snow chamber; loading one or items into the                  container, and transporting the container;                  measuring an environmental temperature that is                  surrounding the container during the step of                  transporting the container; measuring an internal                  temperature of the container during the step of                  transporting the container; estimating a weight                  of the carbon dioxide snow block to be formed in                  the container, prior to starting the in-situ                  charging of the carbon dioxide snow block into                  the container; monitoring container information,                  temperature and/or location of the container                  during the step of transporting the container to                  generate data, and transferring the data to a                  user; monitoring the block carbon dioxide snow                  produced, generating in-situ additional block                  carbon dioxide snow and performing means for                  detecting when the charging is completed; and                  transferring information relating to the in-situ                  charging carbon dioxide snow block for the                  container onto a unique identifier. The means for                  detecting is based on a parameter comprising a                  predefined weight of carbon dioxide snow block,                  pressure in the container, capacitance, duration                  of the in-situ charging, and temperature of the                  container. In method (m1), all of the carbon                  dioxide particles do not pass into or form within                  the interior passageway of the meshed conduit.                  The method (m2) further comprises: configuring                  the carbon dioxide snow charging system to be in                  fluid communication with a ventilation system to                  allow gas to be directed to the ventilation                  system; integrating safety interlock devices into                  the carbon dioxide supply; configuring the carbon                  dioxide charging system to be in electronic                  communication with a programmable logic                  controller to automate the generation of the                  carbon dioxide snow; integrally joining the                  carbon dioxide snow charger with the meshed                  conduit to create a unitary detachable component                  prior to insertion of the meshed tube into the                  internal meshed conduit volume of the container;                  and providing the insulated container with a                  unique identifier that is configured to embed                  container identification information, where the                  container identification information is                  retrievable by reading the unique identifier. The                  method (m3) comprises (a3) providing a carbon                  dioxide source, (b3) providing an insulated                  container with an opening, where the container                  further comprises an interior volume defined into                  a first region and a second region, the first                  region is an internal meshed conduit volume, and                  the second region is a snow chamber, (c3)                  providing a meshed conduit operably connected or                  integrally connected to the insulated container,                  (d3) providing a carbon dioxide snow charger                  comprising a conduit network having an inlet                  opening to receive the carbon dioxide source and                  many nozzles distributed along an edge of the                  conduit, (e3) operably attaching the carbon                  dioxide snow charger to the container, (f3)                  securing a top of the meshed conduit to the                  carbon dioxide snow charger at the top opening of                  the container, and (g3) operably connecting the                  inlet opening of the carbon dioxide snow charger                  to the carbon dioxide source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus                  comprises the container, the internal product                  storage volume defined by the meshed conduit, the                  snow chamber surrounding an exterior of the                  meshed conduit, the carbon dioxide snow occupying                  the snow chamber, and the meshed conduit, where                  the container further comprises multiple                  insulated container walls at least partially                  surrounding the first region and the second                  region, the walls comprise a getter material                  locating within the walls, the getter material                  maintains a vacuum and an insulation level and is                  suitably compatible with the carbon dioxide snow,                  the meshed conduit is permanently or removably                  fixed to the insulated container walls, the snow                  chamber is partially enclosed by the multiple                  vacuum insulated container walls, and the first                  end is oriented towards the opening of the                  transportable container. The conduit network                  comprises multiple inserts of a predetermined                  shape and pattern with nozzles embedded. The                  apparatus further comprises: a mechanical seal                  engaged into the opening of the transportable                  container, thus isolating the snow chamber from                  surroundings of the transportable container; a                  cover secured to the opening of the container and                  a temperature sensor located adjacent to or along                  an interior of the cover; a unique identifier                  that is configured to embed container                  identification information, where the container                  identification information is retrievable by                  reading the unique identifier; and a product                  holder within the meshed conduit. The internal                  volume of the product storage volume is 0.25-25                  l. The product storage holder is capable of                  holding the items for up to 37 days at an average                  temperature of no warmer than -60 degrees C. The                  second end is oriented towards the bottom                  vacuum-insulated wall. The second region extends                  between the vacuum-insulated walls and an outer                  surface of the product storage holder.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D)); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); T01 (Digital Computers)

TI Indoor ventilation system for use in e.g. balcony, of building, has air purifying plant vegetatively arranged on front portion of vertical garden body, and supply/exhaust line for supplying air to room through supply and exhaust hole.
AU KIM J H
AE MTES CO LTD (MTES-Non-standard)
   MTES ONE CO LTD (MTES-Non-standard)
GA 202232866K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a vertical garden body (110) in which several supply and exhaust holes, a grill (111), and a diffuser are provided in the front portion. An air purifying plant (120) are vegetatively arranged on the front portion of the vertical garden body in multiple stages so as to be discharged from the vertical garden body through the supply/exhaust hole or to come into contact with the air introduced into the vertical garden body from the room. An air supply line (130) is connected to the diffuser through the vertical garden body. An exhaust line (140) is connected to the grill through the vertical garden body. A first damper is installed on the air supply line to be positioned at the inlet side of the air conditioner and controlled by an air conditioner controller. The first and fourth dampers are closed and the third damper is controlled by the air conditioner control unit so that it is opened only in an open state.
   USE - Indoor ventilation system for use in e.g. balcony, of building.
   ADVANTAGE - The system performs indoor heating and cooling, ventilation, removal of fine dust from indoor air, carbon dioxide and oxygen in indoor air and controls concentration and humidity of the indoor air so as to significantly reduce burden on purchase cost and installation cost for creating comfortable indoor environment. The system minimizes an installation space by arranging air purifying plants in multiple stages on the front part of the vertical garden body in form of a vertical tower, so that the system can be installed and utilized in a narrow indoor space without any difficulty, and carbon dioxide can be removed by air purifies plants without input of outdoor air, thus rapidly removing carbon dioxide from the inner side air without changing indoor temperature, reducing amount of electrical energy consumed, and creating a comfortable indoor.

PN CN113369345-A
TI Metal plate bending method, involves bending to-be-bent metal plate by rolling type bending device to take out bent metal plate, arranging liquid storage frame with bending wheel and fixing air absorbing cylinder with absorbing pipe.
AU MA J
   YANG Y
AE YANCHENG DEKU ELECTROMECHANICAL TECHNOLO (YANC-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8182G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a to-be-bent metal plate. A bending position and angle of the to-be-bent metal plate are determined. A bending machine is installed on an operation table. The to-be-bent metal plate is placed on the operation table. A rolling type bending device is installed on the bending machine. The to-be-bent metal plate is bent by the rolling type bending device to take out a bent metal plate. A liquid storage frame is arranged between inner walls of hollow type bending wheel and filled with carbon dioxide aqueous solution. An air injection cylinder is fixed with an expansion air bag. An extruding rod is fixed with a diaphragm. An oil discharging pipe is opened with oil outlet holes. An air absorbing cylinder is fixed with absorbing pipes. An air guide pipe is fixed with a waterproof ventilating film. An oil guide pipe is fixed with an extruding valve. A surface of an ultra-fine fiber is coated with an absorbing layer made of magnetic powder materials.
   USE - Metal plate bending method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rolling and bending the metal plate through the bending machine, uniformly stressing the metal plate, preventing the metal plate from being damaged, shaking carbon dioxide aqueous solution in the liquid storage frame in rolling process of the bending wheel, overflowing carbon dioxide gas into the expansion air bag, driving the expansion air bag to the air injection cylinder, pushing the extrusion rod to move upwards for extruding the diaphragm to drive the extrusion valve to open for dropping lubricating oil from the oil outlet hole to the metal plate through the oil guide pipes to reduce rolling friction between the bending wheel and the metal plate, utilizing the absorbing cylinder for absorbing oil and friction generated by friction on the metal plate through the absorbing pipe and collecting the oil by the ultrafine fiber for avoiding influence to an environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a metal plate bending method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The waterproof ventilating film is made of PTFE material. The extruding valve is made of inorganic polymer colloid material.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P52 (Metal punching, working, forging (B21DL).)
MC A04-E08B; A12-H; A12-H02; A12-W12F; P52-D02; P52-T22
IP B21D-005/04; B21D-037/18

PT P
PN US2021146475-A1; WO2021097382-A1
TI Micromachining diamond rotor involves cutting rectangular log of diamond using diamond cutting process, and drilling hole lengthwise through log using pulsed laser with wavelength.
AU GERSHENFELD N
   GRIFFIN R
   FREDIN Z
   BLACKBURN C
   LANGFORD W
   BAHRI S
   BANKS D
   PATIL P
AE MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 2021538030
AB    NOVELTY - Micromachining a diamond rotor involves cutting a rectangular log of diamond using a diamond cutting process, drilling a hole lengthwise through the log using a pulsed laser with wavelength, pulse duration and pulse power sufficient to convert diamond to graphite and subsequently convert the graphite to carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide in ambient air, and rotating the log in the laser beam to micromachine the log into a cylinder.
   USE - Method for micromachining diamond rotor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is used to create high aspect ratio structures and more particularly to a laser micro machining method that uses diamond as a starting material for these structures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following: a method for micromachining a diamond rotor, which involves obtaining a sheet of chemical vapor deposition (CVD) grown diamond;measuring transmission and absorption of the diamond to determine optimum laser wavelength, pulse duration and pulse power;cutting from the sheet a rectangular log of diamond using a laser cutting process;mounting the rectangular log, and using a top-down pulsed laser with adjustable optical focal length, cutting a circular hole longitudinally through the log from a top end to a bottom end;cutting a pocket of predetermined depth z with the laser by converting diamond material in the cut to graphite with the laser, and then oxidizing it to carbon dioxide by heat from the laser using oxygen from ambient air;moving focal length downward a distance z, until a final pocket penetrates the bottom of the log to produce a drilled log;rotating the drilled log, micromachining the log into a cylinder using the laser to shave material from the log as it rotates; and#a method for laser drilling of diamond, which involves measuring a light transmission and absorption spectrum for a chosen diamond material, using the light transmission and absorption spectrum, choosing a laser wavelength, pulse duration and pulse power sufficient to convert the diamond to graphite, drilling a hole with the laser by converting diamond material in the hole to graphite and subsequently oxidizing it to carbon dioxide using oxygen from ambient air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The rotor has a final outside diameter between 0.1 mm and 4 mm and the inside diameter is between 10-90% of the outside diameter. The log has dimensions greater than the diamond rotor being machined. The wavelength is approximately 532 nm. The laser has a penetration depth of approximately 1.1 mm. The diamond log is a chemical vapor deposition (CVD) diamond.

PN CN112250065-A
TI Nitrogen-doped bagasse activated carbon comprises bagasse, sodium carbonate solution, water vapor, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen-containing compounds.
AU LI C
   NONG Q
   LIN L
   XIE J
   MO Z
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG NATIONALITY AUTONOMOUS RE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021111064
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-doped bagasse activated carbon comprises bagasse, sodium carbonate solution, water vapor, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen-containing compounds.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped bagasse activated carbon is used for sugar industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon uses bagasse as raw material to prepare active carbon and doped with nitrogen-containing compound, solves recycling of bagasse, and avoids resource waste and environmental pollution. The bagasse is safe and non-toxic renewable biomass material. The product added value is high. It can generate good social and economic benefit. The activated carbon decoloring ability is strong, and has good stability. The activated carbon has large specific surface area, small aperture, strong adsorption capacity, no device corrosion and no environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the nitrogen-doped bagasse activated carbon comprising washing bagasse, sun drying or oven drying to obtain dried bagasse, crushing, sieving to 2-4 mm, washing with deionized water for 4-6 times, and placing bagasse in drying box at 85-110 degrees C to obtain dried bagasse; placing dried bagasse in muffle furnace for carbonization to obtain carbonized product; mixing carbonized compound and nitrogen-containing compound with mass ratio of 8:1-2:1, adding deionized water, soaking for 9-15 hours, oven drying, placing mixture in tube furnace, carrying out pyrolysis and carbonization in inert atmosphere, cooling to room temperature, washing carbonized sample repeatedly with deionized water for 7-10 times, and drying at 85-120 degrees C to obtain carbonization precursor; activating carbonization precursor with water vapor at 800 degrees C for 12 hours or at 850 degrees C with mixture of 13% carbon dioxide and 87% water vapor for 12 hours, crushing to make product uniform in particle size distribution, using activated carbon for liquid phase adsorption operation, removing surface impurities and drying to complete refining process, carbonizing bagasse at 200 degrees C for 2 hours, and carrying out steam activation at 400 degrees C for 1 hour until specific surface area is 536.5 m2/g; and cooling to room temperature, washing with 2 mol/L hydrochloric acid for 3-4 times, repeatedly washing with deionized water until neutral, and finally placing in drying oven at 90-110 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The nitrogen-containing compound is nitrous acid ester or amide.

AB    NOVELTY - A system (10) consists of fuel cell (11),                off-gas regeneration unit for removing at least a                portion of at least one of water vapor and carbon                dioxide in the off-gas discharged from the fuel                cell and is provided downstream of the fuel cell,                distribution channel for circulating the                regenerated off-gas from the off-gas regeneration                unit and is provided downstream of the off-gas                regeneration unit, and control component (22)                adjusting the reaction constant (Kpa) of the                reaction (A) to 1.22 or more in the regenerated off                gas discharged from the off gas regenerating unit.                The reaction (A) is a reaction between carbon                monoxide and hydrogen to obtain water and carbon.                The reaction constant (Kpa) (I) is calculated using                partial pressure of water P(H2O), partial pressure                of carbon monoxide (P(CO)) and partial pressure of                hydrogen P(H2).
   USE - System.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is capable of suppressing carbon                deposition after off-gas regeneration during an                off-gas regeneration method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system consists of fuel cell, off-gas                regeneration unit for removing at least a portion                of at least one of water vapor and carbon dioxide                in the off-gas discharged from the fuel cell and is                provided downstream of the fuel cell, distribution                channel for circulating the regenerates off-gas                from the off-gas regeneration unit and is provided                downstream of the off-gas regeneration unit, and                control component adjusting the reaction constant                (Kpa) of the reaction (A) to 1.22 or more in the                regenerated off gas discharged from the off gas                regenerating unit. The reaction (A) is a reaction                between carbon monoxide and hydrogen to obtain                water and carbon. The reaction constant (Kpa) is                calculated using partial pressure of water                (P(H2O)), partial pressure of carbon monoxide                (P(CO)) and partial pressure of hydrogen (P(H2)) of                formula: Kpa = P(H2O)/(P(CO)/asteriskP(H2)) (I). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for regeneration of                off gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the fuel cell system.

The reaction (A)                  is a reaction of carbon monoxide to obtain carbon                  dioxide and carbon. The reaction constant (Kpb)                  is calculated using partial pressure of carbon                  dioxide (P(CO2)) and partial pressure of carbon                  monoxide (P(CO)) of formula: Kpb =                  P(CO2)/(P(CO))2 (II).

PN US2019317074-A1
TI Sepsis assessment device for measurement of carbon dioxide and nitric oxide in exhaled breath of patient, determines sepsis risk score based on first signal and second signal and transmits sepsis risk score to input/output interface.
AU ANDERSON C R
   MORRIS R L
   BURKE T W
   ANDERSON C J
AE VAIL SCI LLC (VAIL-Non-standard)
GA 201986307L
AB    NOVELTY - The sepsis assessment device has a fluid input in fluid communication with a respiratory tract of a patient, and the fluid input receives an exhaled breath from the patient. A nitric oxide (NO) measurement device determines a NO concentration in the exhaled breath. A carbon dioxide (CO2) measurement device determines a CO2 concentration in the exhaled breath. A processing element in electrical communicate with the CO2 and NO measurement devices and the input/output interface. The processing element receives a first signal corresponding to the NO concentration. A second signal corresponding to the CO2 concentration is received. A sepsis risk score is determined based on the first signal and the second signal. The sepsis risk score is transmitted to the input/output interface.
   USE - Sepsis assessment device for measurement of carbon dioxide and nitric oxide in exhaled breath of patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The controlled rate allows for accurate identification of the concentration of NO in the fraction of exhaled breath that has been in closest contact with lung tissue affected by sepsis. The slow controlled exhale for sepsis diagnosis use untreated ambient air for inhale without the need to scrub the inhaled air to remove atmospheric NO. The sepsis assessment device record biometric or demographic information as well as information about exhaled NO and CO2 concentration, and sepsis diagnosis, severity and outcomes about patients to the database such information continually improves the accuracy of sepsis risk assessments. The information from the sepsis assessment device is intended to alert clinicians as to whether there is a need to quickly treat the patient for sepsis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for determining the risk of sepsis in a patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for determining the risk of sepsis in a patient.
   Method for determining the risk of sepsis in a patient (200)
   Step for collecting breath sample (202)
   Step for filtering breath sample (204)
   Step for treating breath sample (206)
   Step for regulating breath sample flow rate (208)
