TI Preparation of peach kernel oil lightly fried yellow peach frozen and dry chip by preparing freeze-dried peach meat slices, crushing peach kernel, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting to obtain peach oil, and frying peach meat slices.
AU CHEN X
AE UNIV CHAOHU (CHXY-C)
GA 201969323F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of peach kernel oil lightly fried yellow peach frozen and dry chip includes washing fresh yellow peach fruit with water, draining surface, peeling, removing seeds, cutting peach meat into slices, soaking to citrate-salt solution, taking out, draining, and freeze-drying; peeling off outer shell of peach seeds to obtain peach kernel, slicing, freeze-drying, adding to cryogenic pulverizer, crushing, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peach oil; and vacuum-deep frying step (A) freeze-dried product in peach oil, draining surface oil, and cooling.
   USE - The method is for preparation of peach kernel oil lightly fried yellow peach frozen and dry chip.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is crisp and delicious, and has sweet fruit flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of peach kernel oil lightly fried yellow peach frozen and dry chip comprises washing fresh yellow peach fruit with water, draining surface, peeling, removing seeds, cutting peach meat into slices, soaking to citrate-salt solution at 90-100 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, taking out, draining, and freeze-drying to - 60 degrees C to - 40 degrees C at vacuum gage pressure of - 0.09 MPa to - 0.10 MPa until water content is 20-25%; peeling off outer shell of peach seeds to obtain peach kernel, slicing, freeze-drying to - 60 degrees C to - 40 degrees C at vacuum gage pressure of - 0.09 MPa to - 0.10 MPa until water content is 5-7%, adding to cryogenic pulverizer, crushing to 100-200 mesh powder at - 80 degrees C to - 60 degrees C, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peach oil; and vacuum-deep frying step (A) freeze-dried product in peach oil at 60-80 degrees C and at - 0.08 MPa to ?0.10 MPa for 4-7 minutes, draining surface oil, and cooling to room temperature. The vacuum-deep frying is done in vacuum low-temperature frying oil comprising 25-50 pts. wt. peach oil, 0-50 pts. wt. soybean oil, 0-50 pts. wt. corn oil, 0-15 pts. wt. sunflower oil, and 0-15 pts. wt. camellia oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The peach slices have thickness of 4-8 mm. Preferred Method: The extraction is done by adding peach kernel freeze-dried ultrafine powder to extraction kettle of carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, and extracting twice at 48 plus minus 0.3 degrees C and pressure of 25-30 MPa for 90-120 minutes, and at 53 plus minus 0.3 degrees C and pressure of 25-30 MPa for 60-75 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The citrate-salt solution comprises 0.25-0.30 wt.% citrate and 0.5-1 wt.% salt.

AE CHANGSHA ERDAO NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019545087
AB    NOVELTY - A biogas power generating method comprises mixing potassium carbonate, sodium metaborate tetrahydrate and water, soaking activated volcanic stone powder, and filtering to obtain first treatment agent; mixing N-methyldiethanolamine and sodium metavanadate dihydrate with water, soaking activated volcanic powder powder, and filtering to obtain second treatment agent; mixing gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, ethanol, hydroxyl modified activated carbon and nano-carboxylated polystyrene microsphere, subjecting to silanization reaction, adding 2-bromobutyric acid, reacting, drying, washing toluene to remove microsphere, and drying to obtain third treatment agent; and passes biogas through pretreatment pipeline filled with silica gel, sequentially filling first pipe, second pipe and third pipe with first treatment agent, second treatment agent and third treatment agent, and extracting purified biogas through end pipeline.
   USE - Method for generating biogas power used for generating electricity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes most of the moisture in biogas, adsorbs and removes most of carbon dioxide and small portion of hydrogen sulfide, and adsorbs and removes residual carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide. The biogas has low content of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, and high power generation efficiency for generating electricity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A biogas power generating method comprises mixing potassium carbonate, sodium metaborate tetrahydrate and water to obtain first mixed solution, soaking activated volcanic stone powder, and filtering to obtain first treatment agent; mixing N-methyldiethanolamine and sodium metavanadate dihydrate with water to obtain second mixed solution, soaking activated volcanic powder powder, and filtering to obtain second treatment agent; mixing gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, ethanol, hydroxyl modified activated carbon and nano-carboxylated polystyrene microsphere, subjecting to silanization reaction, adding 2-bromobutyric acid, reacting, drying, washing toluene to remove microsphere, and drying to obtain third treatment agent; and passes biogas through pretreatment pipeline filled with silica gel, sequentially filling first pipe, second pipe and third pipe with first treatment agent, second treatment agent and third treatment agent, and extracting purified biogas through end pipeline. The flow rate of first pretreattment pipeline and end pipeline are 12-15 meters/second (m/s) and 6-8 m/s respectively. The flow rates of first pipe, second pipe and third pipe is 0.5-1 m/s.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of potassium carbonate, sodium metaborate tetrahydrate and water is 20-30:5-8:100. The mass to volume ratio of activated volcanic rock powder and first mixed solution is 1 g:20-30 mL. The mass ratio of N-methyldiethanolamine, sodium metavanadate dihydrate and water is 30-40:10-12:100. The mass to volume ratio of activated volcanic rock powder and second mixed solution is 1 g:20-30 mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, ethanol, hydroxyl modified activated carbon, nano-carboxylated polystyrene microsphere and 2-bromobutyric acid is 1:10-12:0.3-0.6:1-1.2:2-3. Preferred Method: The activated volcanic powder is prepared by slowly adding volcanic stone powder to 5-6 times its weight 30% sulfuric acid aqueous solution while stirring, stirring for 2-3 hours, washing distilled water until neutralized, and microwave treating at 800-1000 W for 5-7 minutes. The silylation reaction is done at 90-100 degrees C for 15-18 hours. The first drying is done at 60-70 degrees C for 5-8 hours. The second drying is done at 50-60 degrees C for 5-8 hours. The method further comprises adding purified biogas to pressure-controlled biogas storage tank.

PN CN109824064-A; CN109824064-B
TI Recovering sodium carbonate from sodium tungstate leachate, comprises e.g. concentrating sodium tungstate leachate by evaporation, cooling, performing freeze crystallization, introducing carbon dioxide gas, separating and removing.
AU HUO G
   PU H
   YI X
   LIU H
   GUO L
   MA S
   CENG L
   ZHU H
   ZENG L
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201950061A
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering sodium carbonate from sodium tungstate leachate, comprises e.g. concentrating sodium tungstate leachate by evaporation to concentration of 130-220 g/l containing tungsten trioxide and greater than 140 g/l containing sodium carbonate to obtain concentrate (I), or concentrating sodium tungstate leachate by evaporation to concentration of 200-300 g/l of tungsten trioxide to obtain concentrate II, (ii) cooling the concentrated liquid (I), performing freeze crystallization, (iii) cooling the concentrated liquid (II) or the above obtained crystallization mother liquid, introducing carbon dioxide gas into neutralization reaction while stirring, performing crystallization by freezing, starting the precipitation of sodium bicarbonate crystals, and separating the filtrate is sodium tungstate solution and removing sodium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering sodium carbonate from sodium tungstate leachate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes improves the production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering sodium carbonate from sodium tungstate leachate, comprises concentrating sodium tungstate leachate (sodium carbonate/tungsten trioxide, having mass concentration ratio is greater than 0.65) by evaporation to concentration of 130-220 g/l containing tungsten trioxide and greater than 140 g/l containing sodium carbonate to obtain concentrate (I), or concentrating sodium tungstate leachate (sodium carbonate/tungsten trioxide, having mass concentration ratio is 0.4-0.65) by evaporation to concentration of 200-300 g/l of tungsten trioxide to obtain concentrate II, (ii) cooling the concentrated liquid (I) to -2 to 10 degrees C, performing freeze crystallization, starting the precipitation of sodium carbonate crystals, continue the stirring, separating the sodium carbonate crystal and the crystallization mother liquid by filtration to obtain sodium carbonate decahydrate crystal and crystallization mother liquid, reducing the mass concentration ratio of the crystallization mother liquid to 0.2-0.4 by sodium carbonate/tungsten trioxide, and (iii) cooling the concentrated liquid (II) or the above obtained crystallization mother liquid to -2 to 10 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas into neutralization reaction while stirring, performing crystallization by freezing, starting the precipitation of sodium bicarbonate crystals, the continue the stirring, filtering the sodium bicarbonate crystals and filtrate by filtration, and separating the filtrate is sodium tungstate solution and removing sodium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), sodium carbonate solid is added as seed crystal during the freeze crystallization, and the amount of the sodium carbonate seed crystal is 2-5 kg/M3. In step (ii), the stirring time is 0.5-2 hours, and the stirring speed is 5-20 revolutions/minute. In step (iii) comprises the pH of the solution falls to 8.8-9.2, the introduction of carbon dioxide is stopped. In step (iii), sodium bicarbonate solid is added as a seed crystal during the freeze crystallization, and the amount of the sodium bicarbonate seed crystal is 5 to 10 kg/M3. In step (iii), the stirring time is 1-2 hours, and the stirring speed is 5-20 revolutions/minute. In steps (ii) and (iii) comprises the temperature of the solution falls below 30 degrees C in the cooling process, an certain amount of crushed ice is added, and the purpose of rapidly reducing the temperature of the solution is achieved by utilizing the dissolution and absorption process of ice, before the crystals are precipitated in the solution, 12.5 kg of ice per cubic meter of solution is used to lower the temperature of the solution by about 1 degrees C, the amount of ice added is calculated based on desired reduced temperature to increase the cooling rate.

PN CN109602065-A; CN109602065-B
TI Method for recovering flavor in reconstituted tobacco leaf concentration process of discharged exhaust gas, involves recording exhaust gas discharged from reconstituted tobacco leaf concentration process as first-stage exhaust gas.
AU LI H
   WANG R
   LI X
   YANG H
   XUE J
   WANG W
   FAN X
AE HENAN CIGARETTE IND TOBACCO THIN SLICE (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2019359696
AB    NOVELTY - A flavor recovering method involves recording the exhaust gas discharged from the reconstituted tobacco leaf concentration process as a first-stage exhaust gas and absorbing the first-stage exhaust gas by the absorption liquid at 15-35 degrees C to discharge the secondary exhaust gas, where the absorption liquid is ethanol and propylene glycol. The absorption liquid obtained after absorption is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and the residual liquid is transferred to the low-temperature flavor storage tank. The secondary exhaust gas is cooled at 0-10 degrees C, the adsorbent is used for adsorption and enrichment of the flavor component, the adsorbent obtained after adsorption is desorbed and the high-concentration aroma is obtained by desorption, after condensation, transferred to the low-temperature flavor storage tank and the liquid in the low-temperature flavor storage tank is the flavor liquid.
   USE - Method for recovering flavor in reconstituted tobacco leaf concentration process of discharged exhaust gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recovering flavor with better effects of preventing loss in the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flavor recovering method involves recording the exhaust gas discharged from the reconstituted tobacco leaf concentration process as a first-stage exhaust gas and absorbing the first-stage exhaust gas by the absorption liquid at 15-35 degrees C to discharge the secondary exhaust gas, where the absorption liquid is ethanol and propylene glycol. The absorption liquid obtained after absorption is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the extraction conditions of supercritical carbon dioxide are extraction pressure of 10-30 MPa, extraction temperature at 35-65 degrees C for 1-3 hours, flow rate is 0.05-0.15 mL/minutes and the residual liquid is transferred to the low-temperature flavor storage tank. The secondary exhaust gas is cooled at 0-10 degrees C, the adsorbent is used for adsorption and enrichment of the flavor component, the adsorbent obtained after adsorption is desorbed and the high-concentration aroma is obtained by desorption, after condensation, transferred to the low-temperature flavor storage tank and the liquid in the low-temperature flavor storage tank is the flavor liquid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a flavor obtained by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is honeycomb activated carbon or molecular sieve adsorbent. The desorption uses an inert gas as a desorption medium. The inert gas is nitrogen. Preferred Method: The method further involves directly added a flavor to the coating liquid, alternatively, using the flavor as a core material solution for preparing the flavor microcapsules, and a mixed solution of soybean protein isolate and cyclodextrin is used as a wall material solution for preparing the flavor microcapsules and stirring, dispersing the uniform emulsion and spray-drying to obtain a fragrance microcapsule and adding the obtained fragrance microcapsule to the coating liquid.

PN CN109401840-A
TI Jasmine oil comprises preset amount of jasmine essential oil, olive oil, tea oil, preservative, and cinnamon oil.
AU 
AE WANG X (WANG-Individual)
GA 201923941R
AB    NOVELTY - A jasmine oil comprises 50-60 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. jasmine essential oil, 10-15 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. olive oil, 15-20 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. tea oil, 1-2 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. preservative, and 5-8 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. cinnamon oil.
   USE - Jasmine oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of jasmine oil, which involves solvent leaching, extracting, concentrating, extracting jasmine with solvent oil of No. 70 to obtain jasmine extract, carrying out supercritical extraction of carbon dioxide to obtain jasmine oil, picking the jasmine flower buds, cool, placing for 3-10 hours, placing into the immersion machine when the buds bloom and release the fragrance, mixing jasmine flowers and No. 70 solvent oil in weight ratio of 1:(8-10), carrying out countercurrent extraction in the extractor, repeating cycles for 5-6 hours, discharging leachate from leaching machine, pumping high-temperature heat-conducting oil into interlayer of leaching machine, heating the solvent remaining in the slag by heat-conducting oil, cooling, separating and recovering to the storage tank cycle at 70-85 degrees C for 90 minutes, filtering the extract and sending to a sealed sandwich pot to heat No. 70 solvent oil at 80-85 degrees C under normal pressure, transferring to a vacuum decompression sandwich pot and concentrating under reduced pressure until the solvent is completely recovered, heating at 60 degrees C, removing the water, making the extract thick and condensed and cooling to make jasmine extract, pumping jasmine extract into the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, heating, extracting aromatic oil at 25-30 degrees C and pressure of 9-13 MPa for 4-6 hours, heating and releasing carbon dioxide gas by separator, entering crude oil loop into cold box circulation application, fractionating separated crude oil to obtain water and residual solvent and impurities through the rectification column to obtain crude jasmine oil, mixing the raw materials according to the distribution ratio of the raw material group of the clean oil. The recovery rate of No. 70 solvent oil from the extract is 80%.

PN WO2019032646-A1; AU2018313795-A1; KR2020060349-A; US2020263605-A1; JP2020530422-W; GB2593865-A; US11415053-B2; GB2593865-B
TI Floating offshore system for carbon neutral            electric power by using oceanic carbon cycle, has power            generating system including capture system connected to            combustion process to transfer carbon dioxide            combustion gases to subsea location.
AU ROSS G
AE ROSS G (ROSS-Individual)
   ROSS G (ROSS-Individual)
   ROSS G (ROSS-Individual)
   ROSS G (ROSS-Individual)
GA 201915367A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an oceanic offshore structure                i.e. floating structure (10), and an electric power                generating system mounted on the structure and                includes an electric power generator, a driver e.g.                gas turbine, powered by combustion process of a                fossil fuel and being connected to the generator,                and a capture system connected to the combustion                process for transferring carbon dioxide combustion                gases from the combustion process to a subsea                location. The capture system includes a conduit                (14) with a first end connected to the driver to                receive the carbon dioxide combustion gases, and a                second end positioned at a subsea location.
   USE - Floating offshore system for carbon neutral                electric power by using oceanic carbon cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables providing carbon-di-oxide                into the surface ocean at 1 km depth to permit                equilibration with the atmosphere within a few                years to decades, so that carbon-di-oxide provided                into ocean basins greater than 3 km in depth delays                equilibration with the atmosphere for more than                hundred years, thus eliminating the atmospheric                concentration transient. The system enables sending                combusted gas generated in the driver or power                section of module to a gas collection system                including a compression station to compress the gas                and transfer it to a conduit or line to a subsea                location at a desired optimum, thus making the                carbon-di-oxide sequestration more efficient in                colder oceanic waters due to the solubility of                carbon-di-oxide in water increases with decreasing                temperature. The structure can be a floating                structure similar to deep-water oil and gas                offshore platforms, or a fixed structure similar to                current, relatively shallow water oil and gas                platforms, thus forming a semi-permanent                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for generating electric power.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                floating offshore system for generating carbon                neutral electric power by using oceanic carbon                cycle.
   Floating structure (10)
   Gas processing/optimization module                (12)
   Conduit (14)
   Pipeline (16)
   Seabed (18)

PN WO2018203024-A1; GB2563818-A; AU2018263371-A1; CA3062116-A1; IN201917044810-A; EP3619405-A1; CN110945213-A; US2020157975-A1; EP3619405-B1; EP3619405-B8; GB2598248-A; US11255223-B2; US2022136413-A1; ES2899633-T3; CN110945213-B; GB2598248-B
TI Power production method for self-contained power            generation system used in e.g. marine vessel involves            operating second nested cycle that operates in Brayton            cycle including cooling in two-stages by first and            second recuperator steps.
AU AKHTAR M S
   M S A
AE CEOX TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
   CEOX TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CEOX-Non-standard)
GA 201887961X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating first and second                nested cycles utilizing carbon dioxide as working                fluid without mixing of working fluid between                nested cycles. The first cycle operates in Brayton                cycle including oxycombustion of hydrocarbons,                preferably liquefied natural gas (LNG), in low                pressure combustion chamber (10), expansion for                power production to provide a first power source,                cooling in a recuperator (14), compression,                reheating by counter-current passage via the                recuperator, and return of working fluid heated by                recuperator back to combustion chamber. The second                cycle operates in Brayton cycle including heating                of working fluid in second cycle by gas/gas heat                exchanger (12), expansion for power generation to                provide a second power source, cooling in                two-stages by first and second recuperator steps,                compression, reheating by counter-current passage                via first recuperator step, and return of working                fluid heated by first recuperator step back to                gas/gas heat exchanger.
   USE - Power production method for self-contained                power generation system (claimed) used in e.g.                marine vessel, isolated oil or gas well drilling                facility, chemical or petrochemical plant or                refinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts a different approach which                gains the high power density and small size                benefits inherent in the use of carbon dioxide as                working fluid, while avoiding the need for                additional heat from an external source. The nested                first and second carbon dioxide cycles achieve net                efficiencies of over 40 percent, which is higher                than for conventional steam or gas turbine systems                while being much lighter and occupying                significantly less space than conventional steam or                gas turbine power generation systems. Obtains                considerable saving in weight and space overall                that is utilized to increase the payload carried,                making operation more profitable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus adapted to provide mechanical/electrical                power in first and second nested cycles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic circuit diagram                of a self-contained power generation system.
   Low pressure combustion chamber (10)
   Gas/gas heat exchanger (12)
   First turbine (13)
   Recuperator (14)
   First compressor (18)

PN CN107841465-A; CN107841465-B
TI Seawater breeding of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises e.g. inoculating algae seeds into culture medium, irradiating, introducing carbon dioxide, culturing, adding seawater, inoculating, adding ethanol extract and seawater and culturing.
AU GUO H
   ZHOU J
AE HAINAN SANYUANXING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2018252443
AB    NOVELTY - Seawater breeding of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises e.g. (i) inoculating algae seeds into the culture medium, irradiating, controlling the environmental temperature, infusing carbon dioxide, stirring, culturing, adding seawater, forming mixed light source, and culturing, and (ii) inoculating cultured algae in new culture medium, adding cycad fern ethanol extract and seawater, irradiating with blue light, violet light and white light of daylight, controlling the environmental temperature, introducing oxygen, stirring, and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for seawater breeding of Haematococcus pluvialis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures 10.56asterisk105 cells/ml cell density, 3.15 mg/ml dry cell weight, 104.12 mg/g astaxanthin content, 3.51asterisk10-2 mu g/piece unicellular astaxanthin content; improves reproductive capacity; and increases accumulation of astaxanthin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Seawater breeding of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises (i) taking culture medium, where the culture medium comprises 3-5 g/l glucose, 0.6-1 g/l potassium nitrate, 0.1-0.3 g/l monosodium phosphate, 0.1-0.2 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 1-2 mg/l iron- ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 0.5-1 mg/l citric acid, 0.3-0.7 g/l pumpkin powder, 0.2-0.4 g/l sweet potato powder and 50-100 ml/l seawater, and the pH of the culture medium is 5-6, (ii) inoculating 17-20% algae seeds into the culture medium, irradiating with red light, green light and blue light of 2500-3000 Lx light intensity, where the photoperiod is 12:12, controlling the environmental temperature to 8-15 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide, continuously stirring under 150-200 revolutions/minute, culturing for 2-3 days, adding 5-15% seawater, forming mixed light source by combining of white light and red light, adjusting the light intensity to 4200-4500 Lx, and continuously culturing for 2-3 days, and (iii) inoculating 10-15% cultured algae in new culture medium, adding cycad fern ethanol extract and 0.2-0.4% seawater, irradiating with blue light, violet light and white light of 5000-7000 Lx light intensity of daylight, controlling the environmental temperature to 20-25 degrees C, introducing oxygen, continuously stirring to 200-300 revolutions/minute, and culturing for 8-10 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the amount of carbon dioxide introduced is 0.5-0.7 l/minute/liter. The red light, green light and blue light in the mixed light source comprise red light. The ratio of green and blue light intensity is 3:1:2. The intensity ratio of white light to red light in a hybrid light source is formed by a combination of white light and red light according to ratio 7:3. In the step (iii), the amount of oxygen introduced is 0.2-0.4 /minute/liter. The intensity ratio of blue light, violet light and white light in the mixed light source is formed by blue light, violet light and white light. The amount of the cycad fern ethanol extract added is 16-27 mg/liter.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN107541336-A
TI Preparing high nutritional content tea oil involves selecting Camellia seed, removing impurities and musty, drying by drying or hot air drying, crushing Camellia seeds, drying and cooling to room temperature by double helix low-temperature.
AU LI B
   LIU R
   WANG Z
AE JIANGXI CHUNYUAN GREEN FOOD CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201804219R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high nutritional content tea oil involves selecting the Camellia seed, removing impurities and musty, drying by drying or hot air drying until the content reaches to 8-10% at constant temperature, where hot air temperature is 60-65 degrees C, crushing Camellia seeds, drying at 80-85 degrees C till the water content reaches to 4-5%, cooling to the room temperature by double helix low-temperature and pressing by blowing film type plate-and frame filter press to remove solid impurities. The obtained product is heated to 75-80 degrees C into ethylene and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for preparing high nutritional content tea oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high nutritional content tea oil maintains immanent flavor and fragrance of Camellia oil and nutrient substance, has squalene, unsaturated fatty acid, high sterol content, specific physical and chemical properties, good effect of preventing and relieving constipation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high nutritional content tea oil involves selecting the Camellia seed, removing impurities and musty, drying by drying or hot air drying until the content reaches to 8-10% at constant temperature, where hot air temperature is 60-65 degrees C, crushing Camellia seeds, drying at 80-85 degrees C till the water content reaches to 4-5%, cooling to the room temperature by double helix low-temperature and pressing by blowing film type plate-and frame filter press to remove solid impurities. The obtained product is heated to 75-80 degrees C into ethylene and carbon dioxide and added 20-30 wt.% tea oil and traditional Chinese medicinal extractive aqueous solution. The obtained product is stirred uniformly, allowed to stand for 2-3 hours, stopped to introduced ethylene and carbon dioxide. The obtained product is heated to 120-125 degrees C, added 5-6 wt.% active carbon under vacuum degree of 95-98 kiloPascal for 20-25 minutes and cooled to 70-75 degrees C and filtered. The obtained product is cooled to 1-2 degrees C for 10-12 hours and filtered to obtain high nutritional content tea oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The traditional Chinese medicine extract comprises goose bowel, bamboo podophylli and shaven. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of ethylene is 200-300ml per minute. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 50-100ml per minute. The 2-5 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine extract is dissolved in the 100 pts. wt. hot water at 75-80 degrees C. Preferred Process: The traditional Chinese medicine extract is obtained by adding the 60-90 pts. wt. goose and 10-20 pts. wt. bamboo podophylli with 200-300 pts. wt. of 0.5-1.5% hydrogen peroxide solution, soaking for 1 days, heating at 90-95 degrees C for 5-6 hours and washing with 600-800 pts. wt. water for 2-3 times. The shaven vegetable 40-50 pts. wt. is added, extracted at 90-95 degrees C for 1-2 hours and filtered. The obtained product is condensed and dried the filtrate to obtain solid powder to obtain traditional Chinese medicine extract.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Circuit board containing cyanide wastewater            treatment device, has first-stage cyanide breaking tank            that oxidizes cyanide-containing wastewater into carbon            dioxide and nitrogen by basic cyanogen breaking            method.
AU XU H
AE XU H (XUHH-Individual)
GA 2021E6954S
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a concentrate tank for                collecting cyanide-containing wastewater. A pH                controller automatically adds alkali to adjustment                tank to adjust PH and re-receive and treat                unqualified wastewater. The upper end of the                first-stage cyanide breaking tank is provided with                an interface connected to the sodium hypochlorite                tank to automatically add sodium hypochlorite to                the first-stage cyanide breaking tank. The                first-stage cyanide breaking tank oxidizes the                cyanide-containing wastewater into carbon dioxide                and nitrogen by the basic cyanogen breaking method.                The upper end of the secondary cyanide breaking                tank is provided with two interfaces respectively                connected to the sodium hypochlorite tank and the                sulfuric acid tank to automatically add sodium                hypochlorite and sulfuric acid to secondary cyanide                breaking tank. The tap water and alkali are input                to treat the waste gas generated by first-stage                cyanide-breaking tank and second-level                cyanogen-breaking tank in waste gas tower.
   USE - Device for treating cyanide-containing                wastewater used in printed circuit board (PCB)                production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyanide breaking reaction is carried out                in sequence to reduce the treatment load of the                biochemical tank and effectively reduce the total                cyanide in the wastewater station and the cyanide                treatment standard pressure according to the                instructions of the liquid level controller in the                adjustment tank. The operation is simple. The                sodium hydroxide is automatically added to the tank                after adjusting the pH value, and the whole                purification process is simple. The wide source of                medicines, low price and low investment in                equipment is achieved, without high-cost equipment                in the whole device. The processing effect is good.                The sodium hypochlorite oxidant is added in batches                to release the cyanide ions in the complex under                alkaline and acidic conditions, and the cyanide                becomes non-toxic carbon dioxide and nitrogen, to                achieve the purpose of removing cyanide through a                two-stage reaction.

TI Portable incubator platform integrated into mobile            phone that provides real time tracking of samples and            data flow, for culturing, reproducing, and            characterizing cells, comprises optical focusing            apparatus, heat pad, and micro-controller.
AU CETIN A E
AE IZMIR BIYOTIP VE GENOM MERKEZI (IZMI-Non-standard)
   IZMIR BIYOTIP & GENOM MERKEZI (IZMI-Non-standard)
   IZMIR BIYOTIP & GENOM MERKEZI (IZMI-Non-standard)
GA 202128113R
AB    NOVELTY - Portable incubator platform integrated into a                mobile phone (1) that provides real time tracking                of samples and data flow, comprises an optical                focusing apparatus (6); a positioning stage in XYZ                directions (4); a glass holder (3); a protective                glass (5); a heat pad (10,14,15); white LED light                source (7,20,21); a temperature-humidity sensor                (16); a carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor (17) that                enables control of the CO2 ratio; a                micro-controller (11) that enables control of LED                light sources, heat pads, temperature-humidity and                CO2 sensors and safety valve (18); power supply                (19) that feeds micro-controller; a monitor (23);                CO2 tank (13) that supplies the CO2 required for                sample; regulator (12) to maintain the incubator                internal atmospheric pressure at a certain level; a                safety valve; a water container (9); and color LED                light source, and a multi-band light emission                filter (22) for one-color (or multi-color)                fluorescent imaging.
   USE - The portable incubator platform is useful for                culturing, reproducing, and characterizing                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - By using a mobile phone, the portable                incubator platform (e.g. having small volume and                low weight) provides real-time tracking of cells                and data flow without the need for an expensive,                sophisticated and bulky computer-aided lens-camera                system, which greatly reduces the cost. With the                incubator platform integrated to the mobile phone,                cells can proliferate in a healthy way, and                reliable data can be produced without being                affected by the changes due to the external                environment. In addition, the small volume of the                incubator system ensures that the environment                required for the cells can be effectively                controlled and distributed homogeneously inside the                incubator.

PT P
PN CN112410225-A
TI Culturing microalgae, by preparing culture solution, filtering, sterilizing, adding treated seawater to culture container, inserting algae to culture solution, controlling light intensity and filtering microalgae.
AU ZHAO Z
   ZHANG B
   TANG Z
   LIU X
   DI S
AE TIANJIN MODERN CHENHUI TECHNOLOGY GROUP (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202127060D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing microalgae, involves washing culture container and tools, performing sterilization treatment, preparing the culture solution, filtering the seawater twice to filter out impurities, sterilizing the filtered seawater for twice, stirring the disinfected seawater to increase the amount of dissolved gas in the seawater, adding the treated seawater to the culture container, adding the nutritional solution to the culture container, mixing the seawater and the nutritional solution, adjusting the salinity of the culture solution to 18-28%, inserting algae into the culture solution, placing the culture container in the incubator, controlling light intensity in the incubator to 4500-12000 Lux, controlling the temperature, regularly filling the culture vessel with carbon dioxide gas, stirring the culture fluid, collecting microalgae, adding the cultivated microalgae and the culture solution into the filter cartridge, and filtering the microalgae.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing microalgae (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids external environment, temperature, illumination, carbon dioxide, pH and salinity control of culture solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing microalgae, involves washing culture container and tools, performing sterilization treatment, preparing the culture solution, filtering the seawater twice to filter out impurities, sterilizing the filtered seawater for twice, stirring the disinfected seawater to increase the amount of dissolved gas in the seawater, adding the treated seawater to the culture container, adding the nutritional solution to the culture container, mixing the seawater and the nutritional solution to obtain the culture solution, adjusting pH of the culture solution to 8-8.9, adjusting the salinity of the culture solution to 18-28%, inserting algae into the culture solution, where the volume ratio of algae species and culture solution is (1-2):(1-5), placing the culture container in the incubator, where the incubator is airtight, controlling light intensity in the incubator to 4500-12000 Lux, controlling the temperature in the incubator at 26-32 degrees C, regularly filling the culture vessel with carbon dioxide gas, stirring the culture fluid, collecting microalgae, adding the cultivated microalgae and the culture solution into the filter cartridge, and filtering the microalgae with the filter cartridge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves washing culture container and tools with soap liquid, and placing the culture liquid and tools in an oven for drying and sterilization after washing process. The temperature in the oven is 120 degrees C, and the disinfection time is 1.5-2 hours. The method involves filtering the seawater by a sand filter tank, and using ceramic filter tank for secondary filtering. The method involves heating the seawater for primary sterilization, and using UV sterilizer for secondary sterilization. The nutritional solution components comprise glucose, potassium nitrate, monopotassium phosphate, iron(II) sulfate, sodium silicate and urea. The method involves stirring culture solution for 4-8 times a day for 1-2 minutes each time. The method involves charging carbon dioxide gas for 2-3 times/day for 1-2 minutes each time.

PN IN202021020210-A
TI Automated space farming apparatus for moisture regeneration using in-situ resource utilization encapsulated in casing, comprises electrical power system connected to electrolysing chamber at first point and fuel cell chamber at second point.
AU MISHRA P
AE MISHRA P (MISH-Individual)
   KADAM G S (KADA-Individual)
GA 202060654S
AB    NOVELTY - Automated space farming apparatus comprises electrical power system for providing electricity connected to electrolyzing chamber at first point and fuel cell chamber at second point. The electrolyzing chamber for carrying out electrolysis is connected to electrical power system at first point, fuel cell chamber at second point and reducing chamber at third point. The fuel cell chamber for regenerating moisture is connected to electrical power system at first point and electrolyzing chamber at second point and soil chamber at third point. The reducing chamber for reduction of carbon dioxide is connected to electrolyzing chamber at first point and reversibly joined incubator at second point. The incubator for microbial assessment is connected to fuel cell chamber at first point, fresh crops output at second point and reversibly joined to reducing chamber at third point.
   USE - Automated space farming apparatus used for moisture regeneration using in-situ resource utilization encapsulated in casing.
   ADVANTAGE - The automated space farming apparatus avoids water transportation losses and use of fibre-reinforced plastic (FRP) material for payload structure, reduces overall weight and launching cost, makes entire system economical and sustainable with increased efficiency, and improves sustainability and reuse rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for regenerating moisture using automated space farming apparatus and in-situ resource utilization, which involves:
   (A) electrolyzing water in electrolyzing chamber using power generated by electrical power system;
   (B) releasing oxygen and hydrogen as result of electrolysis from electrolyzing chamber and feeding to fuel cell chamber;
   (C) feeding hydrogen to reducing chamber;
   (D) providing electricity generated in-situ by fuel cell chamber to electrical power system;
   (E) releasing moisture generated in fuel cell chamber to incubator;
   (F) recycling portion of moisture generated in fuel cell chamber to electrolyzing chamber;
   (G) reducing carbon dioxide gas in reducing chamber to methane and water;
   (H) feeding methane and water to incubator;
   (I) carrying out microbial assessment in incubator;
   (J) removing excess of carbon dioxide and feeding to reducing chamber; and
   (K) providing nutrient rich soil medium to yield fresh crops.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of automated space farming apparatus.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110283012-A
TI Insecticidal organic fertilizer comprises organic fertilizer particles in a core layer and a microcapsule shell layer coated on the core layer comprising bactericide.
AU XU Y
AE XU Y (XUYY-Individual)
GA 201984128M
AB    NOVELTY - Insecticidal organic fertilizer comprises organic fertilizer particles in a core layer and a microcapsule shell layer coated on the core layer. The microcapsule shell layer contains a bactericide. The bactericide has a certain concentration gradient from the inside to the outside along the thickness of the microcapsule shell.
   USE - Used as insecticidal organic fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic fertilizer: slowly releases into the soil; continuously improves soil quality and crop quality; has excellent insecticidal and sterilization effect; and avoids the residue of the bactericide in the soil, which causes pollution to soil and environment and the toxic effect on the next season crop.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the insecticidal organic fertilizer, comprising (i) stirring and uniformly mixing sodium alginate, organic bentonite, polyethylene glycol 600, sodium citrate and deionized water at 40-60 degrees C to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) placing the mixed solution and the organic fertilizer in an autoclave, where the organic fertilizer is added in an amount of 5-10 wt.% of the total mass of the mixed solution, introducing carbon dioxide, raising the temperature to a supercritical state by pressure, and stirring and uniformly mixing the mixed solution under the action of supercritical carbon dioxide to form an organic fertilizer emulsion, where the temperature in the supercritical state is 38-42 degrees C and the pressure is 8-10 MPa, (iii) reducing the outlet operating pressure of the autoclave, and ejecting the organic fertilizer emulsion obtained in the step (ii) together with the supercritical carbon dioxide from the outlet nozzle to obtain an organic fertilizer core layer, and (iv) preparing coating liquid A containing a high concentration of bactericide and coating liquid B containing a low concentration bactericide, spraying the coating liquid A on the outer surface of the core layer through high pressure spray gun at a constant feed rate, spraying the coating liquid B through the high pressure spray gun in a variable speed feeding manner, simultaneously spraying on the outer surface of the core layer with the coating liquid A until the weight of the particle surface increases to 10-20% of the total weight of the organic fertilizer particles, and drying the microcapsule shell layer forming a concentration gradient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The raw material of the organic fertilizer comprises agricultural waste, livestock manure, and river silt, and the organic fertilizer is fermented and rotten by an aerobic composting process. The microcapsule shell layer comprises cellulose, polyethylene glycol 6000, ethanol, deionized water and antiseptic. The antiseptic is kresoxim-methyl, azoxystrobin, methyl 2-phthalimido-3-(3'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate, N-(trichloromethylthio)-cyclohex-4-ene-1,2-dicarimide, tetramethylthiuram disulfide, myclobutanil, triadimefon, Bordeaux mixture, lime sulfur mixture and/or mineral oil. The organic fertilizer particles in the core layer have a particle diameter of 0.5-3 mm. The concentration of the bactericide in the microcapsule shell is gradually increased from the inside to the outside. In the step (i), the mass ratio of sodium alginate, organic bentonite, polyethylene glycol 600, sodium citrate, and deionized water is 30-50:10-15:3-8:1-7:50-100. In the step (iv), the coating liquid A containing high concentration of the bactericide comprises 10-20 pts. wt. cellulose, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol 6000, 15-30 pts. wt. ethanol, 100 pts. wt. deionized water and 1-3 pts. wt. antiseptic. The coating liquid B containing low concentration bactericide comprises 10-20 pts. wt. cellulose, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol 6000, 15-30 pts. wt. ethanol, 100 pts. wt. deionized water and 0.2-0.8 pts. wt. antiseptic.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide; Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Tar-containing gas reforming catalyst comprises            nickel yttrium magnesium aluminum oxide in which            catalyst has crystal phases of nickel magnesium oxide,            nickel aluminate, magnesium aluminate , and yttrium            oxide.
AU NAKAO K
   TAIRA K
   SUZUKI K
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 201982316Q
AB    NOVELTY - A tar-containing gas reforming catalyst                comprises nickel yttrium magnesium aluminum oxide                (I), where catalyst has crystal phases of nickel                magnesium oxide (NiMgO), nickel aluminate                (NiAl2O4), magnesium aluminate (MgAl2O4), and                yttrium oxide (Y2O3).
   USE - A tar-containing gas reforming catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon deposition can be restrained stably,                and tar-containing gas emitted when coal and                biomass are thermally decomposed can be converted                into lighter substances, such as carbon monoxide                and hydrogen. Even if tar-containing gas contains                hydrogen sulfide in high concentration, it is made                to contact with a catalyst as it is, without                desulfurizing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tar-containing gas reforming catalyst                comprises nickel yttrium magnesium aluminum oxide                of formula aNi.bY.cMg.dAl.eO, where catalyst has                crystal phases of NiMgO, NiAl2O4, MgAl2O4, and                Y2O3.
   a+b+c+d=1;
   a=0.01-0.5;
   b=0.01-0.09;
   c=0.35-0.47;
   d=0.35-0.55; and
   e=value from which O and positive element                become electrically neutral.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of tar-containing gas                reforming catalyst which involves generating                deposit by co-precipitation from mixed solution                containing nickel compound, magnesium compound, and                yttrium compound, generating a mixture by adding                alumina powder, water, or alumina sol to the                deposit, and sintering the mixture, or generating                deposit by co-precipitation from mixed solution                containing nickel compound, magnesium compound, and                yttrium compound, drying deposit, calcining,                generating mixture by adding alumina powder, water,                or alumina sol to calcined deposit, and sintering;                and
   (2) reformation of tar-containing gas using                the catalyst which involves contacting                tar-containing gas emitted when carbonaceous raw                material is thermally decomposed with the catalyst                and reforming tar-containing gas by carbon dioxide                in the tar-containing gas and/or water vapor, to                convert into hydrogen and carbon monoxide, or                introducing and mixing water vapor and/or carbon                dioxide which are added to the tar containing gas                to form mixed gas, making mixed gas contact the                catalyst, and reforming tar-containing gas by the                carbon dioxide in the mixed gas, and/or water vapor                to convert into hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Before sintering the                  mixture, the method includes drying and                  pulverizing the mixture, or drying, calcining,                  pulverizing, and molding the mixture.                  Introduction amount of water vapor and/or carbon                  dioxide added to tar-containing gas is adjusted,                  and hydrogen/carbon monoxide rate in the gas                  after reforming tar-containing gas are                  controlled. Preferred Components: Tar-containing                  gas is coke oven gas discharged from a coke oven.                  Tar-containing gas is dry distillation gas                  emitted when wood-type biomass and/or foodstuff                  waste material-type biomass are                  dry-distilled.

AB    NOVELTY - A nano metallic nickel catalyst for carbonic acid methanation comprises nickel as active component, magnesium oxide as structural additive, and monoclinic phase zirconium oxide with large surface area.
   USE - Nano metallic nickel catalyst for carbonic acid methanation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has low nickel content, exhibits excellent low-temperature catalytic performance in carbon dioxide methanation reaction, stable for long-term high-temperature and high-speed operation. The process is simple, and has strong operability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nano metallic nickel catalyst for carbonic acid methanation comprises nickel as active component, magnesium oxide as structural additive, and monoclinic phase zirconium oxide with large surface area. The catalyst of formula: xNi-(MgO)y/ZrO2, where x is 3.1-12.2% metallic nickel, and y is 0.125-1 is a molar ratio of nickel to metal 8-1. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: preparation of nano metallic nickel catalyst, which involves adding nickel ion, magnesium ion, citric acid and ethylene glycol in the molar ratio of 1:0.125-1:1.125-2.4:0.27-0.48, dissolving in deionized water to prepare a mixed solution with a concentration of 2.1-14.0 mol/L, immersing in zirconium oxide carrier and leaving still at room temperature for 2-14 hours, complexing at 60-100 degrees C for 4-16 hours, drying at 300-500 degrees C at a rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute, calcining for 3 hours to obtain a catalyst precursor, charging the obtained precursor in a fixed bed reactor at 200-450 degrees C at 0.1-3.5 MPa, introducing 20-50 mL/minute reduced reaction gas into the reactor, and heating at 450-550 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours; and# catalyst for carbon dioxide methanation reaction involves charging into a fixed bed reactor at a reaction temperature of 200-450 degrees C, at a pressure of 0.1-3.5 MP, and mass space velocity of 15000-60000 mL/(g.hour)-1 in the ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 3.1-12.2 %mass nickel, 0.3-8.4 %mass magnesium oxide, and balance monoclinic phase zirconium oxide. Preferred Component: The nickel ions is nickel nitrate, and the magnesium ion is magnesium nitrate. The reducing reaction gas used in the process is one or a mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and inert gases. Preferred Properties: The monoclinic phase zirconium oxide has a specific surface area of 146-164 m2/g and pore size of 5-20 nm.

PN CN109651485-A
TI Extracting and purifying grape seed protein comprises e.g. taking the grape seed, removing the impurities and crushing, and extracting the grape seed powder by supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the defatted grape seed powder.
AU XIAO M
AE XIAO M (XIAO-Individual)
GA 201939375E
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and purifying grape seed protein comprises e.g. (i) taking the grape seed, removing the impurities and crushing, extracting the grape seed powder by supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the defatted grape seed powder, and then adding the ethanol solution to extract the defatted and dephenolized grape seed powder, (ii) taking delipidated phenolic grape seed powder, adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH, (iii) placing the liquid into the extractor, and extracting to obtain the extract, (iv) centrifuging the extract in a centrifuge to obtain a supernatant, (v) adding hydrogen peroxide solution to the supernatant, and decolorizing the mixture by stirring at 60-70 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, and (vi) adding ammonium sulfate solid and stirring, then separating the precipitate by centrifugation at 5500-6500 rotations/minute (rpm), adding ammonium sulfate as a centrate, and adding until no protein precipitation occurs to obtain protein precipitation.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and purifying grape seed protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: makes complete use of the residual value of the grape seed produced; and produces the product having high nutritional and biological activity value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and purifying grape seed protein comprises (i) taking the grape seed, removing the impurities and crushing, extracting the grape seed powder by supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the defatted grape seed powder, and then adding the ethanol solution to extract the defatted and dephenolized grape seed powder, (ii) taking delipidated phenolic grape seed powder, adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH value to 7.5-11, (iii) placing the liquid into the extractor, and extracting at 50-95 degrees C for 30-155 minutes to obtain the extract, (iv) centrifuging the extract in a centrifuge to obtain a supernatant, (v) adding hydrogen peroxide solution to the supernatant, where the amount of addition is 3-15%, and decolorizing the mixture by stirring at 60-70 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, and (vi) adding ammonium sulfate solid and stirring, then separating the precipitate by centrifugation at 5500-6500 rotations/minute (rpm), adding ammonium sulfate as a centrate, and adding until no protein precipitation occurs to obtain protein precipitation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the crushed grape seed powder passes through a 60 mesh sieve. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the ratio of the degreased and dephenolized grape seed powder and the sodium hydroxide solution is 1:15-1:30. In the step (v), the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide solution is 30%. In the step (vi), the ammonium sulfate saturation is 80%.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing bamboo leaf tea pollen beverage comprising (i) providing half of dry bamboo leaf and pork, mixing, adding 10-20 times of water, boiling, stewing for 20-30 minutes with low flame, removing out, mixing, grinding, drying, crushing to obtain powder, (ii) mixing other half dry bamboo leaf and Chinese yam rhizome, adding 10-20 times of water, boiling on low flame for 10-20 minutes, removing, mixing with sweet potato mud, and sugarcane juice, and filtering to obtain juice, (iii) deodorizing tea pollen, desensitizing and sterilizing, (iv) freezing tea pollen at -50 to -40 degrees C for 10-20 hours, thawing in steam atmosphere at 120-150 degrees C for 1-3 hours, vacuum drying to control the water content at 4-10%, placing in the extraction vessel of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit, controlling carbon dioxide temperature at 30-40 degrees C, controlling the pressure of the extraction kettle at 30-60 MPa for 10 minutes, opening the exhaust valve to quickly vent carbon dioxide, and collecting broken tea pollen, (v) mixing tea pollen, powder, juice, honey, Schisandra liquid extract, glucose, xanthan gum, citric acid, ethyl maltol and water, and filtering by double filter, (vi) homogenizing twice at 15-25 MPa and 35-45 MPa, and (vii) sterilizing and filling to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The deodorization and desensitization comprises placing tea pollen into water (TC water pollen:water = 1:20), stirring evenly, allowing to stand for 2-6 hours, and pouring off the supernatant.

PN CN109371090-A
TI Extracting mealworm protein by drying mealworm, crushing, adding anhydrous ethanol, extracting, performing microwave catalysis, adjusting pH, adding complex enzyme, ultrasonically extracting, filtering and spray-drying solution.
AU HUANG Z
AE ZHEJIANG DINGYI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201921464K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting mealworm protein involves (a) cleaning mealworm to improve impurity, sterilizing, drying and crushing to obtain dry mealworm powder, (b) adding anhydrous ethanol to the dry yellow mealworm powder, adding the mixture into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit with an external microwave generator and extracting, (c) drying the degreased dry mealworm powder, adding sodium hydroxide solution, evenly stirring the mixture and performing microwave catalysis of the mixture in a microwave oven to obtain a crude extract of mealworm protein, (d) adjusting the pH of crude extract of mealworm protein, adding complex enzyme, ultrasonically extracting and inactivating the enzyme to obtain the protein extract and (e) filtering the protein extract and spray-drying the concentrated solution.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting mealworm protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves quality and reduces fat content of mealworm protein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting mealworm protein involves (a) cleaning mealworm to improve impurity, sterilizing, drying and crushing to obtain dry mealworm powder, (b) adding 0.1-0.2% anhydrous ethanol to the dry yellow mealworm powder, adding the mixture into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit with an external microwave generator and extracting at microwave irradiation frequency of 500-1500 MHz, 35-45 degrees C, 30-35 MPa and carbon dioxide fluid flow rate of 20-24 l/hour for 2-3 hours to obtain the degreased dry mealworm powder, (c) drying the degreased dry mealworm powder, adding 0.05-0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, where the dry mealworm powder and sodium hydroxide solution are in a solid-liquid ratio of 1:5-15 g/ml, evenly stirring the mixture and performing microwave catalysis of the mixture in a microwave oven for 60-240 seconds to obtain a crude extract of mealworm protein, (d) adjusting the pH of crude extract of mealworm protein to 7, adding complex enzyme, ultrasonically extracting by heating at 55-65 degrees C and 400-500 W for 30-60 minutes and inactivating the enzyme to obtain the protein extract and (e) filtering the protein extract obtained in the step (d) through a coarse filter, then filtering through ceramic microfiltration membrane, filtering through ceramic ultrafiltration membrane and spray-drying the concentrated solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the moisture content of dry mealworm powder is less than 5%. The step (c) involves performing microwave catalysis at 200-800 W. In step (d), the complex enzyme comprises neutral protease and papain in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:2. The step (d) involves adding the extract into enzyme inactivation device and heating at 75-85 degrees C for 4-8 minutes for enzyme inactivation.

PN WO2018202828-A1
TI Oxidative coupling of methane for producing alkene, involves feeding methane from methanation unit comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and oxidizing agent into reactor comprising oxidative coupling catalyst.
AU MATHIVANAN G
   FAGERSTOLT K
   OUNI T
   DREHER E
AE BOREALIS AG (BORA-C)
GA 201887837R
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) involves feeding methane produced from at least one methanation unit in which at least portion of the hydrogen from a hydrogen stream generated in a propane dehydrogenation process and carbon dioxide react to form methane, and at least one oxidizing agent, preferably oxygen, into at least one OCM reactor comprising at least one OCM catalyst generating 2C or more alkene from methane, and feeding at least a portion of ethane into at least one OCM reactor comprising further at least one cracking unit generating 2C or more alkene from ethane generated in a propane dehydrogenation process.
   USE - Oxidative coupling of methane used for producing 2C or more alkene such as propene, butene, and ethene for producing polyethylene plastics, PVC, ethylene oxide, ethylbenzene, alcohol and other alkanes and alkenes.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is coupled with propane dehydrogenation process in order to reduce feedstock cost and increases overall efficiency of OMC process. Additional hydrogen is added to the methanation unit, yielding methane and reducing the amount of fresh methane fed into the OCM process. The process can reduce ethane to ethene for easy transportation with low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process system for OCM, which comprises at least one OCM reactor comprising at least one OCM catalyst unit for generating 2C or more alkene from methane and at least one cracking unit for generating 2C or more alkene from ethane and arranged downstream of the OCM catalyst unit, at least one methanation unit arranged downstream of the at least one OCM reactor for generating methane from carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide and hydrogen and which is in fluid connection at least one separating unit arranged downstream of a propane dehydrogenation reactor for separating hydrogen from a product mixture comprising propane, propene and other hydrocarbons, at least one de-ethanizer unit generated ethane fed to OCM reactor, preferably to the cracking unit of the OCM reactor, and/ or at least one fluid connection between the at least one OCM reactor and at least one de-ethanizer unit arranged downstream of the propane dehydrogenation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: At least one portion of hydrogen for at least one methanation unit is separated from a stream leaving the propane dehydrogenation reactor comprising light gases, such as hydrogen and methane, and a product mixture comprising propane, propene, and lighter hydrocarbons such as ethane and ethene in at least one separating unit arranged downstream of the one propane dehydrogenation reactor. The light gases are separated from the product mixture in at least one cold box unit as portion of the at least one separating unit arranged downstream from the propane dehydrogenation reactor. Hydrogen is separated from the remaining light gases leaving the cold box in a pressure swing adsorption unit as a further portion of the separating unit(s) and subsequently fed to the methanation unit(s) as portion of the OCM process. The hydrogen stream is fed from propane dehydrogenation process comprising hydrogen and methane. Ethane fed to cracking unit of the OCM reactor, is provided from at least one de-ethanizer unit of a dehydrogenation process. The stream is left from at least one de-ethanizer unit comprising ethane and ethene.

PN CN108751954-A
TI Production of pressure-resistant heat-resistant ceramic material by phosphogypsum and fly ash by mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifiers, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and mixing with e.g. cerium oxide, and sintering.
AU CHEN X
   WANG S
   LI J
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201888573S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of pressure-resistant heat-resistant ceramic material by phosphogypsum and fly ash comprises mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifiers, grinding, and sending to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker; water milling, and separating solid and liquid; subjecting residue to flotation, and separating sulfide; placing sulfide in oxygen-rich environment, calcining, generating flue gas by calcination, catalyzing by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid; adding calcium oxide, stirring at constant temperature, filtering to obtain high-purity sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas until white precipitate is no longer generated, and filtering white precipitate; stirring with hydrochloric acid, washing with water, and calcining to obtain high-purity alumina powder; uniformly mixing with cerium oxide, calcium oxide, chromium oxide, vanadium silicide, cerium carbide and zirconium boride, and ball milling; and sintering mixture.
   USE - Method for producing pressure-resistant heat-resistant ceramic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost, high waste utilization rate and simple technique. The material has good pressure-resistance and heat-resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of pressure-resistant heat-resistant ceramic material by phosphogypsum and fly ash comprises mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifiers, grinding, and sending to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker; water milling at liquid to solid ratio of 4-6:1, and separating solid and liquid; subjecting residue to flotation, and separating sulfide; placing sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, calcining at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, generating flue gas by calcination, catalyzing by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid; adding calcium oxide, stirring at constant temperature, filtering to obtain high-purity sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas until white precipitate is no longer generated, and filtering white precipitate; stirring with 3-4 times with hydrochloric acid, washing with water until filtrate is neutral, and calcining to obtain high-purity alumina powder; uniformly mixing with cerium oxide, calcium oxide, chromium oxide, vanadium silicide, cerium carbide and zirconium boride, and ball milling by ball mill to obtain mixture; and sintering mixture at high temperature to obtain pressure-resistant heat-resistant ceramic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The mass ratio of phosphogypsum and fly ash is 1:1-2.2. The mixed material comprises 20-30 pts. wt. high-purity aluminum oxide powder, 10-15 pts. wt. barium hydroxide, 10-15 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 7-12 pts. wt. chromic oxide, 8-12 pts. wt. vanadium silicide, 3-5 pts. wt. hafnium carbide and 2-4 pts. wt. zirconium boride. Preferred Method: The roasting in first step is done at 1000-1350 degrees C for 1-2 hours.

AU LI J
   ZENG Y
   LU F
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
   CHEN X
   GAO G
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018836515
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing urea-based compound fertilizer involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier and grinding, calcining the material in a kiln to obtain clinker, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to the white precipitate, filtering the white precipitate, washing the filtrate, drying and heating to obtain alumina, separating sulfide from the residue obtained in the solid-liquid separation step by flotation process, placing separated sulfide in oxygen-rich environment and calcining, catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination process by vanadium pentoxide and then absorbing by concentrated sulfuric acid and diluting, reacting concentrated sulfuric acid with urea and water, mixing urea sulfate solution and concentrated sulfuric acid, then adding base fertilizer and spraying on liquid ammonia and granulating in a granulation device, drying granulation material, cooling and sieving.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing urea-based compound fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of urea-based compound fertilizer with high utilization rate of phosphogypsum and red mud waste residue, aluminum recycling rate, purity and quality.The compostion has enviornmental friendly effect
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing urea-based compound fertilizer involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier and grinding, calcining the material in a kiln to obtain clinker, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to the white precipitate, filtering the white precipitate, washing the filtrate, drying and heating to obtain alumina, separating sulfide from the residue obtained in the solid-liquid separation step by flotation process, placing separated sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment and calcining at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination process by vanadium pentoxide and then absorbing by concentrated sulfuric acid and diluting, reacting concentrated sulfuric acid with urea and water at 50-70 degrees C to form urea sulfate solution, mixing urea sulfate solution and concentrated sulfuric acid, then adding base fertilizer and spraying on liquid ammonia and granulating in a granulation device at 60-90 degrees C for 10-20 minutes to form granulation material, drying granulation material at 90-120 degrees C, cooling and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The base fertilizer comprises 5-10 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. diammonium phosphate, 4-8 pts. wt. ammonium chloride, 10-15 pts. wt. ammonium sulfate, 15-20 pts. wt. superphosphate, 10-15 pts. wt. calcium magnesium phosphate fertilizer, 5-8 pts. wt. potassium chloride, 8-12 pts. wt. potassium sulfate and 1-5 pts. wt. potassium nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln.
