PN CN107653016-A; CN107653016-B
TI Method for separating and purifying biogas,            involves pressurizing raw materials, entering into wet            desulfurization tower, adding limestone, absorbing            water and raw material, and removing hydrogen sulfide            gas and carbon dioxide gas in biogas.
AU HUANG Z
   LIU L
   GUO T
   KANG L
   YANG J
AE HEFEI TRANSCENDENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
   ANHUI MINGMO ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201811471S
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating and purifying                biogas, involves pressurizing raw materials by a                booster fan, and then entering into wet                desulfurization tower, continuously adding                limestone into wet desulfurization tower, arranging                several nozzles in wet desulfurization tower to                continuously spray out water, absorbing water and                raw material and removing hydrogen sulfide gas and                carbon dioxide gas in biogas, (ii) subjecting                desulfurized primary gas to dry desulfurization                treatment for further removal of sulfur-containing                gas in the primary gas, and (iii) deoxygenating                desulfurized gas.
   USE - Method for separating and purifying biogas by                combining membrane separation and pressure swing                adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The method deeply and effectively removes the                impurity gas in the biogas and improves the purity                of the purified methane so as to expand the                applicable range of biogas and improve the                combustion efficiency of the biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for separating and purifying biogas                by combining membrane separation and pressure swing                adsorption, involves pressurizing raw materials by                a booster fan, and then entering into wet                desulfurization tower through lower portion of wet                desulfurization tower, continuously adding                limestone into the wet desulfurization tower,                arranging several nozzles in the wet                desulfurization tower to continuously spray out                water, absorbing water and raw material which are                in contact with the biogas, and removing hydrogen                sulfide gas and carbon dioxide gas in the biogas,                (ii) subjecting desulfurized primary gas to dry                desulfurization treatment for further removal of                sulfur-containing gas in the primary gas, (iii)                deoxygenating desulfurized gas through a coke bed                in a deoxygenation reactor at 700-1100 degrees C,                (iv) passing deoxidized gas through an activated                carbon filter and a particulate filter to remove                oil and particulate matter in the gas to obtain a                secondary gas, (v) introducing secondary gas into                the heater for heating to remove the water vapor                and to prevent the water from corroding the                equipment or the membrane and then separating                through the multistage separation membrane, (vi)                deoxygenating the gas obtained by membrane                separation is through deoxygenation reactor, then                cooling deoxygenated gas by a condenser and then                introducing into pressure swing absorber for                purification, and finally obtaining methane gas                with purity of more than 98%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurization                  agent used in dry-type desulfurization treatment                  in step (2) is carbon-based zinc-iron-manganese,                  and is desulfurized at 400-650 degrees C for 3-4                  hours. Preferred Processes: The carbon-based                  zinc-iron-manganese is prepared by mixing zinc                  nitrate, ferric nitrate and manganese nitrate in                  accordance with molar ratio of 1:2:0.4-0.8,                  formulating into solution, adding activated                  carbon, performing titration coprecipitation with                  aqueous ammonia to obtain precursor, then adding                  additive and calcining in nitrogen atmosphere at                  600 degrees C. The deoxidized gas in step (iv) is                  adsorbed by activated carbon filter and then                  cooled by a condenser, and then particulate is                  separated through a particulate filter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The multistage                  separation membrane is prepared by arranging                  several hollow fiber membrane modules in series.                  The hollow fiber membrane module comprises a                  polyimide hollow fiber membrane module,                  polypropylene hollow fiber membrane module,                  polyvinylidene fluoride hollow fiber membrane                  module and/or cellulose acetate hollow fiber                  membrane module.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC A12-W11A; H06-A04; H06-X01; H09-F03

PT P
PN WO2020010456-A1
TI Production of carbon-derived end-products e.g. synthetic fuel, aliphatic hydrocarbon, alcohol, and aromatic hydrocarbon, involves sensing carbon-derived end-product, selecting carbon dioxide source, and converting captured carbon dioxide.
AU BOILY S
   ROSS R
AE GRP AEQUIUM INC (AEQU-Non-standard)
GA 2020067183
AB    NOVELTY - Production of at least one carbon-derived end-product involves (101) sensing a need for the at least one carbon-derived end-product, (103) selecting at least one carbon dioxide source for manufacturing carbon-derived end-product, (105) capturing carbon dioxide from the at least one carbon dioxide source, and (107) converting the captured carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end-product.
   USE - Production of carbon-derived end-products e.g. synthetic fuel, methane, methanol, ethane, ethanol, propane, propanol, benzene, toluene, and xylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables effective and economical production of carbon-derived end-products with high productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production and selling of at least one carbon-derived end-product, which involves sensing the need for the at least one carbon-derived end-product, which depends on at least one of availability and cost of production of carbon dioxide, weather, location, transportation costs and/or requirements and market price of the at least one carbon-derived end-product, capturing carbon dioxide from at least one carbon dioxide source, converting the carbon dioxide into the carbon-derived end-product, and selling the carbon-derived end-product;
   (2) creation of business opportunities for at least one carbon dioxide source that generates carbon dioxide emissions, which involves sensing need for at least one carbon-derived end-product as function of at least one of market price, transportation costs, production costs and quantity needed, sensing at least one carbon dioxide source as a function of availability and cost of production of carbon dioxide and carbon tax paid by at least one carbon dioxide source, matching the need for at least one carbon-derived end-product with availability of carbon dioxide emitted by at least one carbon dioxide source, offering to at least one carbon dioxide source a contract for acquiring and/or acquiring and converting at least a portion of carbon dioxide emitted, contract for selling carbon dioxide emitted, and/or contract for lowering carbon tax rate paid by at least one carbon dioxide source, and offering to at least one entity a contract for capturing and/or converting carbon dioxide emitted by at least one carbon dioxide source into the at least one carbon-derived end-product; and
   (3) managing demand and supply of carbon-derived end-products, which involves sensing a need for at least one carbon-derived end-product, selecting at least one site of production for fulfilling the need as a function of at least one of capacity of production, market price, transportation cost, location, availability of carbon dioxide, carbon tax paid by at least one site of production and weather, capturing carbon dioxide from the at least one site of production, and converting the carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end- product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of producing carbon-derived end-product.
   Sensing need for carbon-derived end-product (101)
   Selecting carbon dioxide source for manufacturing carbon-derived end-product (103)
   Capturing carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide source (105)
   Converting captured carbon dioxide (107)
   Purifying carbon dioxide (109)
   Recovering at least one impurity (111)
   Converting impurity into valuable product (113)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method of creating business opportunities for at least one carbon dioxide source involves sensing customer having need for at least one carbon-derived end-product as a function of at least one of market price, transportation costs, production costs and quantity needed, searching at least one carbon dioxide source that generates carbon dioxide emissions and which is effective for fulfilling at least a portion of carbon dioxide required as a starting material for generating the at least one carbon-derived end-product needed, establishing a commercial contact between the costumer and the at least one carbon dioxide source, optionally installing the system for carbon dioxide capture and/or conversion of carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end-product at the at least one carbon dioxide source, capturing carbon dioxide from the at least one carbon dioxide source, converting the carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end-product, and providing the customer with at least one carbon-derived end-product. The method of managing demand and supply of carbon-derived end-products involves sensing need for at least one carbon-derived end-product by locating at least one customer having the need, searching among a database comprising site of productions capable of fulfilling the need in terms of carbon dioxide amount required to generate the at least one carbon-derived end-product so as to identify and select at least one site of production for fulfilling the need as a function of at least one of capacity of production, market price, transportation cost, quantity needed, location, availability of carbon dioxide, carbon tax paid by at least one site of production, and weather, establishing a commercial link between the customer and the at least one site of production, capturing carbon dioxide from the at least one site of production, converting the carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end-product, and providing the at least one carbon-derived end-product to the customer. The method of managing demand and supply of carbon-derived end-products and reducing carbon dioxide emissions by using the carbon dioxide emissions as starting material involves sensing carbon dioxide sources of carbon dioxide emission, sensing needs in carbon-derived end-products by locating customers having such needs, establishing correlation between customer needs of the carbon-derived end-products and carbon dioxide sources of carbon dioxide emission potentially effective for fulfilling at least a portion of carbon dioxide amount required to generate the at least one carbon-derived end-product needed the customers as a function of at least one capacity, optionally instructing for installation of systems for carbon dioxide capture and/or conversion of carbon dioxide into carbon-derived end- products at carbon dioxide sources, receiving purchase orders from customers concerning carbon-derived end-products, and instructing carbon dioxide sources to capture carbon dioxide emissions to convert the carbon dioxide into the at least one carbon-derived end-product, and providing customers with carbon-derived end-products. The method of managing demand and supply of carbon-derived end-products further involves determining production capacity of at least one carbon dioxide source before capturing the carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide sources, selecting at least one carbon dioxide source to convert the carbon dioxide into the carbon-derived end-product, in which selecting depends on at least one of the availability of carbon dioxide at the least one carbon dioxide source, weather, location, transportation requirements, and government benefits related to greenhouse gas reductions, selecting at a carbon dioxide source (I) to transform the carbon dioxide into a carbon-derived end-product (A), selecting carbon dioxide source (II) to transform the carbon dioxide into a carbon-derived end-product (B), in which the selection of the carbon dioxide sources depend on at least one of availability of carbon dioxide at the carbon dioxide sources, weather, location, transportation requirements, and government benefits related to greenhouse gas reductions. The conversion of carbon dioxide into the carbon-derived end-product is carried out by using metal catalyst such as heterogeneous metal catalyst. The method further involves (109) purifying the carbon dioxide captured so as to separate carbon dioxide from at least one impurity, capturing at least one impurity, converting hydrogen sulfide into sulfuric acid, (113) converting impurity into a valuable product, and sensing need for at least one impurity or at least one valuable product. Preferred Components: At least one impurity comprises sulfur-based product, sulfur oxide or nitrous oxide product, and hydrogen sulfide. The carbon dioxide sources are concentrated source, industrial source, diffused source, and atmospheric carbon dioxide.
